summary: "Changing distributed cache settings prevents Confluence cluster node restart due to a Hazelcast exception"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    If distributed cache sizes are modified, cluster nodes cannot be restarted without shutting down the whole cluster first. This is due to a Hazelcast exception that occurs due to the detected difference between the cache sizes.    This only affects Confluence 7.0.1 Data Center onwards.  {panel}  *Local* or *hybrid* cache size changes do not trigger this issue, only caches marked in the advanced view as *Distributed*.  {panel}  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Start with all nodes in the cluster running   # Modify one of the distributed caches:   ** *Confluence Administration >> General Configuration >> Cache Management >> Show Advanced View* -> Find 'Content Properties' -> Change size from 10000 to 10050.   # Shutdown Confluence on one of the nodes   # Start Confluence on the node that was shutdown. When starting, it will attempt to configure the distributed cache with the new size of 10050 (specified in *cache-settings-overrides.properties* in the shared home directory) however this won't match the existing size that the other nodes are using (10000) and the error below will occur.     h3. Expected Results    Like pre-7.0.1 versions (Hazelcast 3.8.6 and below), Confluence cluster nodes should start with no errors after cache tuning.  h3. Actual Results    From 7.0.1 onwards (Hazelcast 3.11.4 and 3.11.6), after distributed cache sizes are modified, cluster nodes will not restart.  The below exception is thrown in the atlassian-confluence.log file:    The actual 'name' in MapConfig will differ, depending on which cache value was adjusted.  h3. Workaround    Please use one of the following workarounds to temporarily fix the problem:   # Change the modified cache setting back to the previous value   _or_   # Shutdown all cluster nodes, then restart them one by one.   # Adding the JVM parameter '-Dhazelcast.dynamicconfig.ignore.conflicts=true' to all cluster nodes's startup command (in setenv.sh/bat)."""

summary: "Warning on the logs even when the job Clean Temporary Directory runs successfully"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  The Clean Temporary Directory job successfully deletes the files but the logs show a warning. This does not happen when you run the job manually.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Configure a Confluence instance;  # Start Confluence;  # Head to _General Configuration > Scheduled Jobs_;  # Next to Back Up Confluence, select _Run_ (this will generate a .zip file in the <confluence-home>/temp directory);  # Go to <confluence-home>/temp and note that the zip file for the back up is there;  # Wait until the time set for the job to run (4:20 am by default);  (i) Note: Because Confluence backup is set to remain on the folder for 24h, you may have to wait until the next day or the zip won't be deleted. Actual Results  # The zip file is successfully removed from the directory;  # The logs show this warning:      h3. Workaround  Since the job is working, the only workaround, for now, is to ignore the warnings on the logs."""

summary: "Office Excel thread hangs if xls file contains formulas"
description: """h3. Problem  Office Excel (view-xls) macro does not load and its corresponding thread gets stuck if the file contains formulas. Example:      h3. Steps to Reproduce    # Attach the file  [^Test.xls]  to a page  # Add the *Office Excel* macro and select that file  # Save the page    h3. Expected Results    The page and the macro render as expected.    h3. Actual Results    The page does not load anymore and the thread gets stuck infinitely. Only restarting the application will clear up that thread.    h3. Workaround    The file  [^Test-noformulas.xls]  is the same but the """"COUNTIF"""" formulas were removed. The bug cannot be reproduced with that file. So, the options are:    # Edit the file and remove those formulas  # Or, use the File Preview macro instead of the Office Excel one    h3. Notes  Example of hanging thread:      This can result in full unresponsiveness of the instance if the page is visited the same number of times that is defined in maxThreads (48 by default) since they all will get stuck in this operation. Only restarting will clear them up.    It also affects Data Center because the Office Excel macro is not covered by the file conversion sandboxing due to this bug:    * https://jira.<USER>browse/CONFSERVER-59425"""

summary: "As a Confluence administrator I'd like to have an option to see the status of index replication across Confluence nodes"
description: """h3. Problem Definition    As a Confluence administrator, I'd like to have an option to see the status of index replication between Confluence nodes.    h3. Suggested Solution    In Jira, there is the option to [check the status of the index|https://docs.<USER>software/jira/docs/api/REST/8.4.2/#api/2/index/summary-getIndexSummary] by using the {{/rest/api/2/index/summary/}} API call for each node and then compare the results.    It would be helpful to have a similar option in Confluence.    h3. Why this is important    Currently, there is no way for administrators to determine that the index across the nodes is staying in sync.    h3. Workaround  Confluence administrator may rely on a manual validation as described in [this KB|https://confluence.<USER>confkb/how-to-manually-rebuild-content-index-from-scratch-on-confluence-data-center-with-downtime-1019380452.html#HowtomanuallyrebuildcontentindexfromscratchonConfluenceDataCenterwithdowntime-Confirmingcontentindexisworkingonallnodes]."""

summary: "Make a single item in a list (numbered or lettered) into a checklist to enable task assignment."
description: """h3. Suggestion Summary  It would be great if you were able to select a single item on a list to transform it into a checklist box to enable Task Assignments.  It worked in the previous Confluence Editor.    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a numbered list with more than one item (ie, 1. Job 2. Role 3. Blah blah   # Highlight #2 (Role) and click the checklist icon in the toolbar.   # Watch items #1, 2 and 3 turn from numbered list to checklist.    h3. Expected Results    Only item #2 becomes a checklist  h3. Actual Results    Items #1, 2, and 3 become checklists."""
comments: "For more color, we extensively use confluence for notes, and it was a HUGE feature to be able to take notes in list format, and make just one item of the list an action. One benefit, as stated before, is that it allows for easy note reading to have everything in the same line.    The second benefit is time savings. Since I can no longer create a checklist in my notes without causing the whole list to become a checklist,I will not be able to utilize the awesome action item assignment feature. So at the end of the meeting, I have to review all line items that have someones name and a due date (the action), and create an ""action list"" at the bottom where I copy and paste all those actions and turn them into a checklist. This is critical because without the checklist the macro to summarize an individuals actions across the confluence space will not be activated. So basically I have to re-read and copy down my notes twice to enable this great feature."

summary: "No notification regarding Application Link With Same Consumer Keys"
description: """h3. Problem    There is no notification that using the same Consumer Key with multiple application links won't work.    h3. Suggested Solution    _Add a notification or block users from using a Consume key that is already in use with another application link._   h3. Why This Is Important    _When users aren't aware that this is the case, they might create multiple application links for different purposes, using the same consumer key. This results in user frustration and confusion as to why it's not working as expected._   h3. Workaround  {quote}_""""....It is expected that """"Consumer Key""""s will be unique across Application Links. This is because a """"Consumer Key"""" uniquely identifies each """"client"""" to Jira in much the same way that a """"username"""" might be uniquely associated with a user. If they are duplicated/reused then multiple clients are saying that they are the same client and that is what leads to the behaviour observed when updating the Application Links.""""_    _-_Matthew ????? Knox (https://jira.<USER>browse/JRACLOUD-74475)  {quote}"""

summary: "Related Content Macro"
description: """Hello,     I've been using a number of your label and other macros in Confluence, but have just not found the right fit for what I'm looking for in our knowledgebase. On each of our main pages, I'd like to include what I'm calling a """"Related Content"""" macro. Which would essentially take the labels on the current page and show you any number of pages you specify of content also using those labels. I would also love the option to include labels from child pages and to indicate if I want related content shown only from the current space, or all spaces associated with our company/account. The Content by Label macro is inferior for this because as content is added or labels change, you have to manually go into each macro and add or delete labels. There is also no control over the space it uses to do this. It seems like this would avoid the issues around the Most Popular Content/Labels and indexing.    This would be more work, but the other way to look at this would essentially be a knowledgebase search based on either labels or words on the page and restricted to certain spaces with a limit on how many links were provided, but automatically displayed at the bottom of each page that includes this macro. So a generic or """"related"""" search is unnecessary and reduces the time it takes to find content.    Happy to answer questions.     """

description:
"""h3. Issue Summary In Confluence Server, if the user is using the [Excerpt Include macro|https://confluence.doc/excerpt-include-macro-148067.html] in order to retrieve content from a page that has specific characters (like """":""""), the same will display the following result on the copy: *""""Error rendering macro 'excerpt-include': No link could be created for 'specific page: specific character""""* h3. Steps to Reproduce # Create a new page with a colon character in the title (eg: *Page: One*) # Edit *Page: One* and insert an *Excerpt macro* #* *!https://emoji.slack-edge.com/TE8HJA7RS/info_mark/6ac3f9befb556605.png|width=16,height=16!* The Storage Format of the page should look something like: {code:html}


INLINE

This is my excerpt macro


{code} # Create another page with any title (eg: *Page Two*) # In *Page Two*, add an *Excerpt Include* macro and point it to *Page: One* # Use the *Copy* function on *Page Two* and save it as *Page Two Clone* in any space h3. Expected Results The copy should be properly displaying the Excerpt Include content. h3. Actual Results Instead of having the content, the message below is displayed: *""""Error rendering macro 'excerpt-include': No link could be created for 'specific page: specific character""""* h3. Notes * Similar as CONFCLOUD-65349 but for Confluence Server * A video has been recorded with the steps to reproduce. The *excerpt* video can be found on the attachments of this request (steps to reproduce for Confluence v7.6.1) * This seems to be happening with copied pages because the same remove the *space key* from the macro parameter, hence, making the macro to not identify it. * Simply tapping on the excerpt include macro in order to *edit* and then, searching for the page will automatically adjust it. * Pages without specific characters are not affected once the same *does not use* a space key as a reference. h3. Workaround * Instead of using the *Copy* function, try to manually copy the content with the excerpt to a new page. * Change the title of the pages using the affected character"""

summary: "REST call takes lot of time when page start value is higher > 50k"
description: """h3. Problem    When REST call to extract some pages from Confluence is called, if the pagination start page is more than 50k, the system takes close to 8-9 seconds to return back with the response. This response is faster if the start page is lesser.   h3. Environment    .Confluence 6.13.8  h3. Steps to Reproduce    1. Create about 70K pages in Confluence under a single space.    2. Once created, use the following curl command to extract pages, starting from the 50,001    curl  -u username:password -X GET """"[http://localhost:port/confluence/rest/api/space/SPACE_KEY/content/page?limit=20&start=60000|http://localhost:8082/confluence/rest/api/space/HYUN/content/page?limit=20&start=60000]    3. The command takes about 8-9 seconds to return with a response.   4. Try the same command with the start value with about 10000. The pages are returned much more quickly  h3. Expected Results    Both the calls for pages starting from 50k and pages starting from 5k should take similar times.  h3. Actual Results    There is considerable difference between when pages are fetched with start of 5k to that of 50k.    For 5k start, the call takes about 0.8 seconds :    time_connect: 0.001467s   time_appconnect: 0.000000s   time_pretransfer: 0.001512s   time_redirect: 0.000000s   time_starttransfer: 0.873332s   ----------   time_total: 0.889090s    For a start of 50k, the page takes about 9 seconds :    time_connect: 0.001452s   time_appconnect: 0.000000s   time_pretransfer: 0.001492s   time_redirect: 0.000000s   time_starttransfer: 8.886917s   ----------   time_total: 8.971300s  h3. Workaround    No work around at this point.  h3. Notes"""
comments: "REST calls to /rest/api/space/:key/content/:type directly access the database, and results collected are then filtered by Java-level permission checks, taking into account inherited page permissions. This permission check is slow, and cannot currently be avoided for all the preceding n-1 pages of data as we build the results for the nth page. Notably the REST call is entirely stateless, so the pagination result for one query is not reused to build the pagination result for the next."

summary: "Jira issue macro cache never refreshes in Confluence 7.x DC"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    In order to improve performance, Confluence maintains a cache for the Jira issues macro to prevent each macro view from polling Jira for data. The default TTL for this cache is 5 minutes.    From Confluence Data Center 7.0 onwards, the Jira issues macro cache is never refreshed automatically, resulting in stale Jira data being presented to users.   * Confluence DC 6.x is not affected, and no versions of Confluence Server are affected. Only Confluence DC 7.x has this issue.   * Tested with and Chrome 83.0.4103 and Firefox 72.0.1    The following configurations were tested With Jira 8.3:  ||Confluence||Data good or stale||  |6.13.9 DC|OK|  |6.15.8 DC|OK|  |7.0.3 DC|STALE|  |7.2.1 DC|STALE|  |7.3.1 DC|STALE|  |7.4.0 DC|STALE|  |7.5.2 DC|STALE|  |7.6.0 DC|STALE|  |7.3.1 Server|OK|  |7.5.0 Server|OK|  |7.6.1 Server|OK|  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Configure Confluence Data Center   # Setup application link to Jira   # Create page and insert Jira issue macro against a Jira project   # Modify an attribute of one of the issues in the Jira project   # Wait after 5 mins (default Jira issue macro cache TTL : {{<base_url>/admin/jim/cachesettings.action}}) and reload the page in the browser.   # Issue details remain the same, until the 'refresh' button at the bottom of the Jira issue macro is clicked.    Note: Changing the Jira issue macro cache TTL ({{<base_url>/admin/jim/cachesettings.action}}) or setting it to zero has no effect. Stale data will still be displayed.  h3. Expected Results    Confluence should invalidate the cache data (and refresh the cache from Jira) after the Jira issue macro cache TTL (default 5min) has passed.  h3. Actual Results    The cache data is never refreshed and stale data is shown, even on a page refresh/reload.  h3. Workaround   * Workaround #1 and #3 below are instant fixes but the problem will return immediately.   * Workaround #2 presents the most up-to-date Jira data but will incur a performance hit, as every page load will have to contact Jira for the data, however it ensures that the data is not stale.      h4. Workaround 1:    Click the 'refresh' button at the bottom of the Jira issue macro. This relies on the user to perform this action and a new click on 'refresh' will be required every time to update the data.  h4. Workaround 2:    Install the '[Confluence Source Editor|https://marketplace.<USER>apps/1210722/confluence-source-editor?hosting=server&tab=overview]' from Atlassian Marketplace and when editing the page, click the '<>' icon in the top right to edit the properties of the macro and add the line below (as per [Jira Issues Macro|https://confluence.<USER>doc/jira-issues-macro-139380.html#JiraIssuesMacro-Otherwaystoaddthismacro]):    For example:    With this workaround, each Jira macro needs to be edited, as the macro parameters are specific to each occurrence of the macro and are not global settings.  h4. Workaround 3    Flushing various distributed Jira caches in Confluence (eg. Jira metadata aggregate) via {{<base-url>/admin/cache/showStatistics.action#fullView}} will also force the Jira updates to be loaded, but they will then become stale again. This can have other unintended performance impacts and is not recommended as a workaround, merely documented here to capture the behaviour."""

summary: "Allow to configure cluster connection timeout"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  Cluster panic would happen if a node is restarted and it cannot discover the other running node due to a slow network.  It would be very helpful if the cluster connection timeout can be increased.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Restart Node1 while the other Node2 is running  # Node1 fails to discover Node2 due to a slow network connection  # Node1 starts its own cluster and updates the safety number  # Node2 has a Cluster Panic    h3. Expected Results  In a slow network, the network connection timeout can be changed.    h3. Actual Results  No function to change the network connection timeout for the cluster.    h3. Workaround  Currently, there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""
comments: "Work around is that you stop the node that was in panic and started panic  Bring both up one at a time and shut them down before starting next node.  Once that is completed start node that was in panic originally wait till alive and start node that caused the panic.  If issue occurs repeat again but reverse node order in final step"

Expected Results    # Expected to see the charts side by side as we see in the excel file.  # Expected to see the columns with the same width as defined in the excel file.  # Expected to have all the content inside the macro and had the macro display a horizontal scroll bar to see the entire content inside.    h3. Issue Summary  Section macro doesn't scroll the content of Office Excel macro correctly if there are charts in the file occupying several columns    Reproduced with:  * Confluence 6.13.10 and 7.4  * Browsers: Chrome, Firefox, and Safari    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Create a new page in Confluence and attach this excel file:  [^excel_chart_text2.xlsx]   # Save the page and Edit again  # Add a *Section* macro to the page and an *Office Excel* macro inside the section macro  # For the Office Excel macro options make sure you see the attached excel file in the File Name field  # Insert the macro and publish the page    (i) You can use this Storage format:  [^test_section_storage_format]   h3. Also, searching for the user's ID (entire ID or parts of it) seems to produce better search results from the user picker:   !Screen Shot 2020-07-24 at 11.39.32.png|thumbnail!"""

summary: "Configuring the JVM system property page-tree.partial-loading-batch-size displays an extra page (n+1)"

Steps to Reproduce     # Edit your {{setenv.sh}}   # Configure {{page-tree.partial-loading-batch-size}}   # Notice there is always one more page shown than set. Whether its all parent pages or children.    h3. Expected Results  The set number of pages are loaded in the page tree.    h3. Actual Results  One extra page is show in the page tree.  It's not a major change or workaround but is counter intuitive when reading our documentation.      h3. Workaround  Just set your value to 1 lower than required, remember the counter starts at 0.  For 8 pages, you would set it at 7."""

summary: "RSS Feed Macro is not rendering the link part of the RSS properly with some encodings."
description: """h3. Problem    When using the RSS Feed Macro, and the feed contains a link, the links part of the RSS isn't rendering correctly.    In particular, we are seeing this when the syntax *%2F* is found in a link within the RSS feed. Instances of *%2F* are being changed to *%252.* This results in the links not going to the correct targets.        h3. Steps to Reproduce   * Create an RSS Feed that includes a link with the syntax *%2F*   * **Add an *RSS Macro* to the page using the feed   * Click on the link with the aforementioned syntax in it    h3. Expected Results    The link takes you to the page it's connected to, without changing the syntax of the URL. __   h3. Actual Results    The syntax of the URL is changed so that instances of *%2F* are replaced with *%252* and using it doesn't point to the expected resource  h3. Workaround{{ ** }}  h3. Notes    The RSS Macro is found in the <*Confluence-Installation>/confluence/WEB-INF/atlassian-bundled-plugins/Confluence-HTML-Macros.x.x.x.jar* file.    The issue appears to have been introduced in the __ *{{Confluence-HTML-Macros.3.0.2.jar}}* {{and is also present in the *Confluence-HTML-Macros.5.0.0.jar.*}}"""

Issue Summary    When running Confluence 6.x on latest Adopt JDK 8u262 (and Oracle JDK 8u261), Confluence will not start and we can observe the following stack trace in the logs    This is due to an old version of icu4j [https://github.com/unicode-org/icu]    This issue has been fixed on          In order to support running Confluence 6.x on new JDK 8 versions, we will need to upgrade to a newer version of icu4j  h3. Expected Results    Confluence 6.x can start on latest AdoptOpenJDK 8u262  h3. Actual Results    Confluence 6.x fails to start  h3. Workaround    Upgrade to Confluence 7.x"""

summary: "Upgrading from Confluence 6 to 7 can cause Synchrony schema to not update successfully"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    When performing an upgrade to Confluence from 6 to 7, there is a schema change for Synchrony to support new Data Eviction standards. However, under certain circumstances this schema update can fail and the new columns and keys will not be properly created, causing post-upgrade Collaborative Editor issues.  h3. Steps to Reproduce    Perform an upgrade from Confluence 6 to 7  h3. Expected Results   - Synchrony tables will be dropped and have their schema updated to reflect new table columns and keys (documented in brief here: [Editing and creating pages fails on Confluence due to content reconciliation errors - Unknown column 'snapshots0_.contentid' in 'field list'|https://confluence.<USER>confkb/editing-and-creating-pages-fails-on-confluence-due-to-content-reconciliation-errors-unknown-column-snapshots0_-contentid-in-field-list-1004943042.html]    h3. Actual Results   - Collaborative editing will fail after the upgrade   - It seems that the schema update will fail to drop the Synchrony tables (evidenced by the tables having content in them still after the upgrade). The actual rest of the upgrade completes successfully   - In Oracle, you may see the following error message after the failed update to the Synchrony table schema during the upgrade.  Here, we see the Synchrony tables cleanup scheduled job failing:      h3. Workaround   - You can manually drop the Synchrony tables and recreate them using the schema workaround noted in the [Editing and creating pages fails on Confluence due to content reconciliation errors - Unknown column 'snapshots0_.contentid' in 'field list'|https://confluence.<USER>confkb/editing-and-creating-pages-fails-on-confluence-due-to-content-reconciliation-errors-unknown-column-snapshots0_-contentid-in-field-list-1004943042.html].   - Technically, you don't have to drop the tables, but they do need to be empty, so truncating them pre-upgrade will also allow for the upgrade to complete successfully."""

To have to first look these spaces up in the space directory and then adding them to a search filter is not the most intuitive way to perform a filtered space search."""

summary: "Create an XML file cleaner"
description: """h3. Problem Definition    * There are currently some known problems with an XML file from Confluence as the one described in our ['Incorrect string value' error thrown when restoring XML backup in Confluence|https://confluence.<USER>confkb/incorrect-string-value-error-thrown-when-restoring-xml-backup-in-confluence-777009599.html] document, for example. Those types of problems are very time-consuming for our customers.    h3. Suggested Solution    * Create an alternative to clean the XML files of Confluence from known problems with them before attempting to import them in another Confluence instance.    h3. Why this is important     * This can avoid quite a few cases where there is an incorrect data in the XML file itself.    h3. Workaround   * Follow the workarounds of each document available.    h3. Notes  * Other examples of documents in which a script can fix the error identified in the XML file:  ** [Confluence site XML import fails with """"duplicate key"""" error|https://confluence.<USER>confkb/confluence-site-xml-import-fails-with-duplicate-key-error-438993638.html]  ** [Confluence Space import failed with 'Unable to complete import: Error while importing backup: For input string: """"""""'|https://confluence.<USER>confkb/confluence-space-import-failed-with-unable-to-complete-import-error-while-importing-backup-for-input-string-998893140.html]"""
comments: "yeah, that will be intersting" [SEP] "This is a very good idea. While we understand that Atlassian recommends to rely on native DB backups for large instances, it would be great if there is a cleaner file that can fix the issues with the large backup xml file. Atlassian applications are being widely used for several years now. They should consider that the usage/size of the backup files would  have increased (in several 10s of GBs) and inbuilt back up strategy like xml should be more dynamic, intact and the import should be less time consuming. " [SEP] "+1    Last week I did a  Confluence backup xml that ran for 48 hours in a medium size customer, it failed to import due ""An invalid XML character"", only after using the atlassian-xml-cleaner-0.1.jar it worked;"

summary: " Feature Request for enabling JUST the Facebook-type of commenting"
description: """Our customer has the following feature request:    Could the Confluence core be changed in a way that admin can disable inline-commenting only (leaving the Facebook-style commenting on the end of the page still enabled).    Rationale: we are using Confluence as our Wikipedia-type Management System. It is important that the people using the system know that the information from the system is reliable. Therefore we cannot enable the inline -commenting on instructions, because previously it has led to a situation where the users do not know if they should apply the instruction as it was originally published or should they do also the suggestions in inline comments.    We have a different site for the draft versions and both commenting types are enabled there. But we would also enable users into a discussion in our public site using the Facebook-type commenting at the bottom of the page, but at the moment we cannot enable this while inline commenting is disabled. """

summary: "Confluence search fails with HTTP 500"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Hit the Confluence search box on the top left of the screen there is a spinning wheel and then nothing.    Checking the browser logs (F12 or Developer Tools -> Network tab), it's possible to see a HTTP 500 Error:     !Screen Shot 2020-07-17 at 18.37.36.png|thumbnail!     h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Try to search   # It fails    h3. Expected Results    The search works.    h3. Actual Results    The below exception is thrown in the atlassian-confluence.log:        The lines related to {{java.util.regex.Pattern}} go on forever for hundred of pages.    This is most likely caused by https://ecosystem.atlassian.net/browse/PLUGWEB-422    h3. Workaround    Increase the Java parameter {{-Xss}} to a higher value.    This parameter is the responsible to address the recursive calls made on the Java code, exactly what is happening as shown in the Exception above, therefore increase it can solve the issue at some degree.    More details about this parameter can be found in this discussion:    [JVM option -Xss - What does it do exactly?|https://stackoverflow.com/questions/4967885/jvm-option-xss-what-does-it-do-exactly]"""
comments: "Considering that the problem can be fixed by adjusting the stack size (Xss), we will close this issue and document the configuration in our public knowledge base.    We will keep monitoring this issue and re-open it if it becomes frequent.    <USER> Product Manager"

summary: "Consider renaming ""whitelist"" and ""blacklist"" to ""allow-list"" and ""deny-list"""
description: """Problem:  The use of racially charged terms like """"whitelist"""" and """"blacklist"""" connotate goodness and badness to specific colors, which can be exclusionary and offensive.     Solution:  Use non-exclusionary terms instead. I suggest allow-list and deny-list. This change should be made in product as well as in our documentation."""
comments: "Thank you for adding this suggestion [~<EMAIL>].     Atlassian's preferred terms are allowlist/blocklist to replace whitelist/blacklist. summary: "Event creation pop up in Team Calendar doesn't show up fields "
description: """h3. Issue Summary    The event creation pop up in the Team Calendar doesn't show up any fields of the event. The fields are only shown up after toggling the event types.  h4. Steps to reproduce    # Create a Team calendar in a Space   # Add a custom event type in the calendar.   # Save an event using the newly created custom type event.   # Now navigate to a different Space.   # Add a new calendar and make sure it doesn't have the custom event type we created in step 2.   # Try to add any event on the calendar.    h4. Expected Behaviour    * The event should open a pop-up with all the fields associated with it.   !Screenshot 2020-07-16 at 4.57.55 PM.png|thumbnail!       h4. Actual Behaviour   * It looks like while adding the new event, that custom type which was last used event type is being called as the default event type in new calendar. However, the new calendar does not have the custom event type. Therefore, the pop up is incomplete and Confluence just shows the option to use to select an event type and not loading the fields. The fields only appear when we toggle between the event types.   !Screenshot 2020-07-16 at 5.01.06 PM.png|thumbnail!         h3. Workaround    Toggle between the event types to show up the fields.     """
comments: "We're using a confluence instance that has multiple calendars in every different space and they all have custom event types. This bug is pretty annoying for the users that work in multiple spaces." [SEP] "We have Confluence 7.5.2 and it is also happening to us.  But there is a simpler way to reproduce it:   # Create a Team calendar in a Space   # Add one/multiple custom event types in the calendar.   # Disable all the default event types   # Try to add any event on the calendar.    The only/first option is a custom event type and the screen does not show the fields.     "

summary: "Editing a large page in Confluence causes Synchrony timeouts"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    When loading a large page that contains many macros in Confluence 7.2 and above, the performance impact on the browser is substantial enough that Synchrony fails and displays errors such as “The connection timed out. If it happens again, speak to your Confluence admin. You may be using a proxy server that prevents WebSocket connections.”    Using an example XHTML page that contains multiple macros, once the page is loaded using the Chrome browser the page itself is very unresponsive, and simple operations such as scrolling are difficult. When loading the page to edit or when saving edits to text the page freezes and there is a significant delay before actions take place. The same is true for buttons and cursor states.    Looking at the profiling and development tools in Chrome it’s clear that the majority of the time handling the page edit or load operation is spent on scripting and DOM processing. When the operation doesn’t complete in the default timeout set by Synchrony (In some cases 30 seconds) we see that the WebSocket opened by Synchrony has already been closed due to the delay in receiving the page content.    {code}  VM292:1778 WebSocket connection to 'wss://confluence7.2/synchrony/v1/bayeux-sync1' failed: WebSocket is closed before the connection is established.  {code}    This results in the error we see regarding a proxy and whether it supports WebSocket connections.    This behaviour is tested and is not present in the latest version of Confluence *6.15*, the page is easy to navigate and alter when using the same macros and the example page attached.      h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Install and activate the [Live Input Macros|https://marketplace.<USER>apps/1215287/live-input-macros]   # Install the [Confluence Source Editor|https://marketplace.<USER>apps/1210722/confluence-source-editor]  # Using the example XHTML page export attached edit a page using the Confluence Source Editor and paste in the content from the example page {{temp.html}}.  # Attempt to navigate and perform edits on the page    h3. Expected Results  The page should be able to be easily navigated and edited.    h3. Actual Results  The page causes the browser to be unresponsive and there are problems with editing and saving the page. Errors  such as “The connection timed out. If it happens again, speak to your Confluence admin. You may be using a proxy server that prevents WebSocket connections.” are displayed.    The below exception is thrown in the Chrome console.log      h3. Workaround    Matters improve slightly if the page is approximately 1/3 of the size of the attached example, and then saving can be completed - however the page editing experience is still hampered.    """

summary: "Special characters in page titles breaks excerpt macro"
description: """h3. Problem   * *Scenario 1*   If a page title contains a special character such as a colon("""":"""") and is referenced in an _excerpt include macro_ and the page is moved within the hierarchy or to a different space the macro breaks when the page is updated and shows the following error:  {quote}Error rendering macro 'excerpt-include'    No link could be created for '<page name>'.  {quote}   * *Scenario 2*   This also happens when a page title is edited and a special character(colon """":"""") is added. When the page with the _excerpt include macro_ is updated the macro breaks with the same error as above.    h3. Environment   * Confluence 7.4.0   * Confluence 7.5.1    h3. Steps to Reproduce   * *Scenario 1*   ## Create a page with an _excerpt macro_ adding random text and a page title as follows: """"Testing: Phase 1""""(*note the colon*)   ## Create a page with an _include excerpt macro_ and reference the page created in step 1 to display the excerpt   ## Move the page created in step 1 to a different place in the hierarchy or space   ## Edit the page created in step 2 and without making any changes just update the page   * *Scenario 2*   ## Create a page with an _excerpt macro_ adding random text and a page title as follows: """"Testing Phase 1""""   ## Create a page with an _include excerpt macro_ and reference the page created in step 1 to display the excerpt   ## Edit the page created in step one and change the title to """"Testing: Phase 1""""(*note the colon that was added*)   ## Edit the page created in step 2 and without making any changes just update the page    h3. Expected Results    The link to the """"excerpt macro"""" should be correctly updated and the data within displayed without an error.  h3. Actual Results   * The following error is displayed once the page is updated in both scenarios:  {quote}Error rendering macro 'excerpt-include'    No link could be created for '<page name>'.  {quote}    h3. Workaround   * Do not use any special characters in page titles containing _include macros_   * Manually fix the reference in the _excerpt include macro_ if it breaks    h3. Notes   * *Scenario 1*   ** The link is prefixed by the space key and once the page is moved the space key is removed."""

summary: "Confluence Mobile Server App login fails with multiple nodes"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  An error occurs while attempting to log in via SAML SSO from Confluence Server Mobile App (iOS/Android) to a multi-node Confluence Data Center backend.    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Install 2 or more Confluence Data Center instances behind an Oracle SSO and Load Balancer   # Install Confluence Server Mobile App on mobile device (iOS / Android)   # Provide server base URL and valid user credentials    h3. Expected Results  User is granted access and can use Confluence successfully.    h3. Actual Results  Login fails with """"Can't get a secure connection"""" error:     !Screen Shot 2020-07-14 at 14.56.31.png|thumbnail!     h3. Workaround    Redirect all requests from Confluence Server Mobile App to a single Confluence backend node. In order to achieve that, either:  # Disable all nodes in the Load Balancer except one, or  # Add a rule to the Load Balancer that will listen to the request headers {{mobile-app-request: true}} or {{user-agent=AtlassianMobileApp}} and route them to a single node."""

summary: "Custom Fields are missing in Jira Issue Macro after upgrade to Confluence 7.4.1, 7.5.0, 7.6.0"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Since upgrading to Confluence 7.4.1 and 7.5.0, customers are reporting that values of custom fields are not returning and not being displayed  h3. Bug Behaviour    The behaviour of the bug is in such a way that if a user’s Jira Issue Macro has a custom fields to be returned from Jira, these fields are not retuned.   This issue is not observed as at first, but occurs after some time. Some customers, report that it occurs after 1-2 days.  h3. Steps to Reproduce    This is still not clear as the issue does not present it self straight after upgrade. Currently still under investigation.   h3. Expected Results    Requested custom fields are returned from Jira to Confluence and displayed.   h3. Actual Results    Requested custom fields are not returned from Jira after some few days after upgrade, or Confluence restart.   h3. Diagnosis   # Identify what are the are the Custom Fields that are missing from the Jira Issue Macro table. A screenshot from the customer would help.   # Next, determine the request that is being sent to Jira. To do this, enable the following:   ## *On the Jira side*   ### Navigate to *Jira Administration* » *System* » *Logging and profiling*   #### Enable *HTTP Access Logging*   #### Enable *HTTP Dump Logging*   ## *On the Confluence side*   ### Navigate to *Confluence Administration* » *General Configuration* » *Logging and profiling*   **** Add *org.apache.http* to the Logging Box and set to *DEBUG*   **** Head over to an affected page, and refresh the page. Take note of the time (for timestamp) purposes.    ### Could you please create and send me the *Confluence support zip*. You can do so by heading over to _General Administration_ > _Troubleshooting and support tools_ > _Create support zip_. The new logs will include the *org.apache.http* logs we've recently added.    ### Remove the  *org.apache.http* from logging as keeping it enabled will flood the logs.   ## *Back On the Jira side*   ### Could you please create and send me the *Jira support zip*. You can do so by heading over to _System_ > _Troubleshooting and support tools_ > _Create support zip_. The new logs will include the inbound and outbound HTTP logs we've recently enabled.   ### Navigate to *Jira Administration* » *System* » *Logging and profiling*   #### Disable *HTTP Dump Logging*   #### Disable *HTTP Access Logging*    h3. What to look for    Customers who are reporting the issue seems to fall into two categories as this issue manifests differently:    *First category:*   # In the Confluence logs look for the HTTP request that is being sent to Jira that includes the JQL. It will look like something like this:   ##        # Part of the above record is the exact JQL that is sent to JIRA:   ##     Notice that the last few parameter are the custom fields that are being requested. The HTTP request *must* include these parameters. field=customfield_10200&field=customfield_10300&field=customfield_10301     # If the request do not include the custom field parameter when it should, then you may be affected by this issue.   *Second category:*   # In the Confluence logs look for the HTTP request that is being sent to Jira that includes the JQL. It will look like something like this:   ##         # Part of the above record is the exact JQL that is sent to JIRA:   #      Notice the last few fields:       #    ## The above fields seems to be custom fields as a quick check in Jira, these fields are not by default found there.   ## The above GET request seems to indicate that Confluence does send the request back to Jira.   # In the Jira dump logs, we see that Jira does receive the request and the following are the list of fields Jira recognises:   ##        #    ## Project Manager and Organistion fields are among the fields that Jira recognises above, however, they are not fetched in Jira and are not sent back to Confluence.   ## The following are the values that are sent back to Confluence per Jira issue. Notice that the Project Manager and Organisation are not included.   ##            Looking at the two paths the issue manifests, it looks like:   * When custom fields requests are in the format:field=customfield_10200 i.e. custom field ID, Confluence omits the custom field altogether from the request.     * When custom fields requests are in the format: field=custom field name i.e. custom field name, Confluence does send the request but Jira does not return it.    h3. Workaround    Known workarounds include:   * Disabling the Confluence Jira Macro from the System Plugins and enabling it after few minutes. (this workaround doesn’t work 100% of times, sometimes it needs to be done multiple times)   ** *Note:* Disabling this plugin also disables its dependencies, including {{confluence-jira-content}} and {{confluence-software-blueprints}}. These bundled plugins must be manually Enabled again after the Confluence Jira Macro is Enabled.   * Restarting Confluence.   * Downgrade confluence-jira-plugin to 11.0.14 for Confluence 7.4.1   ** Please note that the JAR file is available for download from this bug ticket."""

summary: "If a user has multiple sessions all sessions should be expired on logout"
description: """h3. Improvement description    To better comply with the safety standard [OWSAP OTG-SESS-006|https://kennel209.gitbooks.io/owasp-testing-guide-v4/en/web_application_security_testing/testing_for_logout_functionality_otg-sess-006.html], where it is stated.    {quote}  Session termination is an important part of the session lifecycle. Reducing to a minimum the lifetime of the session tokens decreases the likelihood of a successful session hijacking attack. This can be seen as a control against preventing other attacks like Cross Site Scripting and Cross Site Request Forgery. Such attacks have been known to rely on a user having an authenticated session present. Not having a secure session termination only increases the attack surface for any of these attacks.  {quote}    We should log the user session (JSESSIONID) out in all browsers where it is active.    In Confluence this is currently achieved with a “session timeout”, when this timeout value is reached all sessions and cookies linked to the the session are expired irrespective of the browser used.    However this session is only reasonably triggered after some time (without disturbing user experience too much).    This could be accomplished as described below.    https://stackoverflow.com/questions/35276554/spring-security-how-to-expire-all-sessions-of-a-user    """

summary: "Application Link Status page fails to load due to Case-sensitive checks for Headers"
description: """Note: this is a clone of the original JRASERVER-70602 issue for JIRA. It affects Confluence also, but this ticket will allow to us to track the release and fix.     h3. Issue Summary  Trying to configure a new application link or loading the Application link page to list the status of existing Applinks will show """"NETWORK ERROR"""" in red.    The REST client  {{atlassian-rest-module:3.2.16}} while reading headers (eg. Content-Type) does a _case-sensitive lookup_ for them (headers name should be _case-insensitive_). So when headers are in lower case it can't parse them properly. The problem is triggered by the proxy server, which is converting all HTTP header names to lower-case.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Setup two Atlassian applications behind a proxy  # Setup HAProxy with HTX enabled on one of the application  # Configure Application link between the two applications    h3. Expected Results  The application link can be configured and established    h3. Actual Results  The application link is not configured correctly with """"NETWORK ERROR"""" due to wrong headers.    h3. Workaround  On the proxy side, disable the handling of headers to all lowercase:  * Example HAProxy, set {{no option http-use-htx}}  * Example for Envoy - [header_casing|https://www.envoyproxy.io/docs/envoy/latest/configuration/http/http_conn_man/header_casing]    """
comments: "A fix for this issue is available to Server and Data Center customers in Confluence 7.6.1.    [Upgrade|https://www.<USER>software/confluence/download] now or check out the [Release Notes|https://confluence.<USER>doc/confluence-7-6-release-notes-1004945745.html] to see what other issues are resolved." [SEP] "Is there a plan to fix this bug in the next release of 7.4? I noticed 7.4.2 was in the Fixed Version, but now has been removed. Also the bug fix is not included in 7.4.3." [SEP] "[~<USER>, apologies for that. The 7.4.2 release was withdrawn, and the fix version for this ticket was not updated accordingly. The fix is in 7.4.3." [SEP] "Thanks @<USER>for the quick response. I am able to see it in the 7.4.3 release notes now."

summary: "Http method DELETE is not supported via applink from Confluence to Bitbucket"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    When a rest API is used to via applink to Bitbucket from Confluence, the DELETE method fails with 405 code.  h4. Steps to reproduce    We created a local bitbucket instance with a project titled *TEST* and added the users named *admin* and *syed* respectively. On Confluence, we created a page with the HTML macro and the script to test the GET and DELETE method via REST API.    As you noticed in your environment, the GET method to fetch the project permissions is successful. See the screenshot below:     !GET response.png|thumbnail!     However, the delete errors our with 405 response:     !DELETE response.png|thumbnail!     Now, for further analysis, we tried to test the behavior for the DELETE method with and without the applink proxy. We used _curl_ to eliminate any possible interference from Confluence other than the applinks. We noticed when we test the REST API without the applink proxy, the response is successful. However, when we go via the applink proxy, the response fails with 405. See below:      If you notice closely, we see that the Http method DELETE is not supported when passed via the applink proxy.  h3. Expected Results    The rest call for DELETE via applink should delete the user from a project on Bitbucket.     """

summary: "Purging of old drafts"
description: """In our Confluence system, we have many instances where users will start editing documents, but never completes them (""""publish""""). As a result, there are many """"orphan"""" drafts left around.    This is confusing for users who would like to edit a page where there is an old draft. We had many  situations where users start editing a page with old draft (sometimes > 2 years old, where the original posted already left the company), and are being prompted to accept/reject the draft. In most cases, users do not know how to proceed, worried that the draft may be important.    {color:#505f79}Asking for a feature on the """"Space Tools"""" ... """"Content Tools"""" to purge drafts based on age.{color}    It will be nice to have the following   * Allow the (space) administrator to send notification to all owners of drafts above certain age.   * Be able to setup a policy that will automatically purge old draft, without admin actions, based on configuration parameters (N=max age of drafts).    I believe change will make it significantly easier to handle the above situation."""

A possible workaround would be:   # Create a script to get the new password from the AWS Secrets Manager as described in this page: [How to use AWS Secrets Manager to rotate credentials for all Amazon RDS database types, including Oracle|https://aws.amazon.com/blogs/security/how-to-use-aws-secrets-manager-rotate-credentials-amazon-rds-database-types-oracle/]   # Use the script to update the password retrieve from the AWS to update the database password from Confluence. More details where the Confluence DB password is stored can be found here: [Where does Confluence store the database password?|https://confluence.<USER>confkb/where-does-confluence-store-the-database-password-211060566.html]   # Finally, use the script to restart the Confluence     """

It will be great to have the similar as in JIRA:       *Workaround solution:*  You can still enable the debug logging for the Default login manager package by adding the following line in the file log4j.properties:   {code}  log4j.category.com.atlassian.confluence.security.login.DefaultLoginManager=DEBUG  {code}  The following example trace will be added to the log file atlassian-confluence.log:   """
comments: "Team,     We have a scenario that we need to capture confluence login & logout info for audit and data governance purposes. This is seemless for Jira, however confluence doesn't provide a way to read login information from access log.    The workaround could be more useful if it prints additional info such as IP address, <USER>along with login time and user id. however for logout again we need to rely on access logs, this adds another complexity of getting logout and login info from two different files.    It will be useful to all the customers who want to audit the login/logout info.  Please reconsider this feature."

summary: "Upgrade Tomcat to version 9.0.37"
description: """h3. Issue Summary   * The current version of Tomcat 9.0.33 bundled with Confluence (at least up to Confluence version 7.6) is vulnerable to HTTP/2 Denial of Service CVE-2020-11996    [https://tomcat.apache.org/security-8.html#Fixed_in_Apache_Tomcat_9.0.36]   [http://mail-archives.us.apache.org/mod_mbox/www-announce/202006.mbox/%3Cfd56bc1d-1219-605b-99c7-946bf7bd8ad4%40apache.org%3E]    This vulnerability uses """"(a) specially crafted sequence of HTTP/2 requests"""" to """"trigger high CPU usage for several seconds."""" A large number of these HTTP/2 requests could be used to make an application unresponsive.    h3. Versions Affected:   * Apache Tomcat 10.0.0-M1 to 10.0.0-M5   * Apache Tomcat 9.0.0.M1 to 9.0.35   * Apache Tomcat 8.5.0 to 8.5.55    h3. Versions affected:   - Apache Tomcat 10.0.0-M6 or later   - Apache Tomcat 9.0.36 or later   - Apache Tomcat 8.5.56 or later    h3. Notes  * By default Confluence is configured to use an HTTP/1.1 connector and would not be vulnerable to this CVE    h3. Mitigation  * No workaround is needed to mitigate this vulnerability.   * If your organization determines that you cannot use a version of Tomcat that is affected by CVE-2020-11996 you can manually update the version of Tomcat used by Confluence to an unaffected version (9.0.37) as described in [How to Upgrade The Tomcat Container for Confluence|https://confluence.<USER>confkb/how-to-upgrade-the-tomcat-container-for-confluence-336757062.html]  ** Note: Manually upgrading the version of Tomcat used by Confluence is not supported. If any issues arise from making this change, Atlassian Support would first recommend going back to a supported version of Tomcat."""
comments: "The cited article specifically says this is unsupported since 5.7:   * While no workaround is needed to mitigate this vulnerability, you can manually update the version of Tomcat used by Confluence to an unaffected version (9.0.37) as described in [How to Upgrade The Tomcat Container for Confluence|https://confluence.<USER>confkb/how-to-upgrade-the-tomcat-container-for-confluence-336757062.html]" [SEP] "[~<USER> you are correct that running Confluence using a version of Tomcat that it did not ship with would be unsupported. We're providing the steps to manually upgrade Tomcat as an option for any organization that cannot run software that has a known vulnerability, regardless of whether or not that vulnerability can is exploitable.    As shipped, Confluence does not use HTTP/2 connections and does not require taking any mitigating measures. It is up to each customer to decide if they must use an unsupported version of Tomcat until we release a new version of Confluence to resolve this issue.    I've updated

summary: "In Windows, Confluence can't start manually after an upgrade"
description: """h4. Summary    In Windows, if Confluence is upgraded to 7.x, a subsequent attempt to start Confluence manually will not work.  h4. Steps to reproduce   # Install Confluence 7.0.x using the Windows installer.   ** The setjre.bat file should include the line: *SET """"JRE_HOME=C:*  *Program Files\Atlassian\Confluence\jre*   # Verify basic functionality.   # Download the Confluence 7.5.2 Windows installer.   # Upgrade Confluence 7.0.x to 7.5.2   # If the installer fails to shut down the 7.0.x instance, shut down the Windows service for 7.0.x.   # Continue with upgrade installer.   # If an error displays """"...while attempting to remove the previous installation directory. Some files may be in use..."""", click _Ignore_.   # Installer prompts to Start Confluence and then to launch the browser.   # Confluence is accessible.   # If you stop and start the Windows service that the installer built, Confluence should still start up ok.   # However, if you stop Confluence via the service and then try starting it via the manual start-confluence.bat, the following error is displayed:      h4. Environment    Windows operating system   Upgrading from 7.0.x to 7.5.x  h4. Expected result    Confluence can start manually.  h4. Actual result    After executing <confluence-install>/bin/start-confluence.bat, an error is displayed in the command prompt and Confluence doesn't start.  h4. Workaround   # To get Confluence to start, ensure that the Confluence is not running.   # Next update the <confluence-install>/bin/setenv.bat file so that the _JAVA_HOME_ environment variable is set to either the packaged JRE location or a separate Java instance on the server within. Here's an example showing a set of _JAVA_HOME_ to packaged JRE location and where to insert this line:    # Execute <confluence-install>/bin/start-confluence.bat.  You may still see the messaging, but Confluence will start."""

summary: "As an Admin I'd like to be able to move move a Space Template to the Global Template list"
description: """h3. Problem Definition    Currently Space Templates are available in a specific space only and sometimes     h3. Suggested Solution    Confluence could have an option to move a Space Template, or even all Space Templates for an specific Space, to the Global Templates list.    h3. Why this is important    Creating a template can be very time consuming and sometimes Space Templates might be needed for other Spaces, either because it was created as a Space template by mistake or maybe due to a business decision that template is needed in other Spaces.     It would be very important if Admins had an option to *easily* move a Space Template to the Global Template list.    h3. Workaround    It is possible to simply port over the template content manually into a new global template using the Confluence Source Editor. As mentioned in our [How to migrate a space template to a global template|https://confluence.<USER>confkb/how-to-migrate-a-space-template-to-a-global-template-966038022.html] KB."""

summary: "Creating new page while editing the initial draft of a new page causes orphaned pages"
description: """h3. Problem  After selecting the 'Create' option to create a new page, if the Create button is selected again, while editing the draft of the first new page, the second new page will be created as a child of the first page- however, the first page was never published in the first place.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Select the 'Create' option to create a new page  # While editing the draft of this new page, click the 'Create' button again (don't publish the first page)  # Publish this second page    h3. Expected Results  Either Confluence shouldn't let this second, child page be created if its parent page wasn't created in the first place, or it should create the new child page on a valid path, disregarding the invalid parent page.    h3. Actual Results  This results in the second, child page being orphaned. It will not show on the page tree, but the breadcrumbs at the top of the page will display the correct path, even though the parent page does not exist and can't be reached.  """
comments: "This is a duplicate of CONFSERVER-59887"

Issue Summary    Disable Atlassian Companion module is not possible above Confluence 7.3.   - [Administering the Atlassian Companion App|https://confluence.<USER>conf74/administering-the-atlassian-companion-app-1003129616.html]  {quote}*Disable file editing*   <USER>can remove the edit files option from their Confluence site. summary: "Duplicated content in pages after running Synchrony Hard Data Removal"
description: """h3. Issue summary  With Collaborative Editing On and Synchrony Hard Data Removal Enabled in the Scheduled Jobs, the editor sometimes will randomly repeat the content and causing the content to be duplicated after saving the page.    h3. Steps to replicate the issue  h4. Creating a two pages  # Create a page with title *Page 1*.   # Create a child page with title *Page 2*. This page must contain some text.  # Save *Page 2*    h4. Removing Synchrony Data (Hard)  # Go to http://<base url>/admin/scheduledjobs/viewscheduledjobs.action  # Find on the page *Synchrony Data Removal (Hard)*  # Click *Run* button to trigger the *Hard Data Removal* job.    h4. Reproduce the bug  1. Return to the *Page 1* created earlier  2. (the next step need to be done very quickly)  3. On the left side, in the Page Tree, click *Page 2* link, and press *E* keyboard shortcut repeatedly at the same time until the page opens for editing. This steps to trigger the editor to load slowly.    h3. Expected behavior  Exact content loaded in the Editor.    h3. Actual behavior  Page load for editing, and after a while (few seconds/milliseconds) a duplicate content (text) appears.     h3. Notes  Issue happen randomly for normal editing. Only when the editor took sometime (few seconds) to load. Users who experience slow network connection while loading the editor usually hit into the issue.    h3. Workaround  Issue does not persist after turning *Collaborative Editing mode* to *Off* and disabling *Synchrony Data Removal (Hard)*."""

summary: "Excel Macro edit button only available on the page excel file is attached to"
description: """h3. Problem    When using the Excel Macro, the *Edit* button is only available on the page that the excel sheet is attached to. Other pages using an Excel Macro that is attached to another page no longer include the *Edit* button above the macro.   h3. Environment    Confluence 7.5.0  h3. Steps to Reproduce    * Add an Excel file as an attachment to a page  * Add an Excel Macro to this page using the Excel file.  * Publish the page  * Create a new page   * Add an Excel macro with the *Page Name* set to the previously created page that we added the Excel file to, and set the File Name to the Excel file.  * Publish the page    h3. Expected Results    We expect that when published the Excel Macro on the second page will include an *Edit* button in the upper right corner.   h3. Actual Results    There is no *Edit* button on the second page. However, the *Edit* button can be seen when viewing the Excel Macro on the original page.   h3. Workaround    Enabling the Edit in Office dark feature will restore the missing *Edit* links to the page.   h3. Notes"""

Save the page   # Manage add-ons screen again, enable *confluence-information-plugin*;    *Expected behavior*   The information is displayed back properly.    *Actual Behavior*   (!) {{Unknown macro: 'info'}}  is displayed on the page and the data is lost. summary: "Content entries with a null creationdate fails import to Cloud"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  Currently, Confluence Server allows content to have a null creationDate which is something not supported in Confluence Cloud.  These conditions will make an import from server to Cloud fail.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Export a Space from Confluence Server.  # Import the Space to Confluence Cloud.    h3. Expected Results  Space is imported successfully.    h3. Actual Results  Space import fails and the following error is thrown in the UI:  {code}  com.atlassian.confluence.importexport.ImportExportException: org.springframework.transaction.UnexpectedRollbackException: Transaction rolled back because it has been marked as rollback-only  {code}    h3. Workaround    Find the affected content entries and update them through the database.  To find the entries:  {code:sql}  select contentid,contenttype,content_status from content where creationdate is null  {code}    h3. Suggestion  Add a constraint to the content table so the *creationdate* column can not be null.  """

summary: "Error message shows incorrect value of queued tasks"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Even when reducing the total queue size for Jira Issues Macro, the error message will say that """"there are more than 1000 tasks queued""""    h3. Steps to Reproduce    # To reduce the pool and queue size, edit {{setenv.sh}} and add the below parameters  {code:borderStyle=solid| title= }  CATALINA_OPTS=""""-Dconfluence.jira.issues.executor.queuesize=1 ${CATALINA_OPTS}""""  CATALINA_OPTS=""""-Dconfluence.jira.issues.executor.poolsize=1 ${CATALINA_OPTS}""""        h3. Expected Results    The error message should indicate the correct value of tasks queued.    h3. Actual Results  The error message in the application logs says *JIM Marshaller rejected task because there are more than 1000 tasks queued*, however, we can also see *queued tasks = 1* in the same error.    h3. Workaround    Currently, there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available."""

summary: "As a Confluence Administrator I'd like to monitor the total number of Jira issues macro items in the queue"
description: """h3. Problem Definition    Currently there is no way to monitor the Jira Issue Macro items that are in the queue, when there is an issue we can see an error message indicating pool size, active threads, queued tasks, <USER>:    {code:borderStyle=solid| title= }  2020-06-15 22:34:07,855 ERROR [http-nio-8080-exec-740] [xhtml.view.macro.ViewMacroMarshaller] handleMacroExecutionException Error rendering macro: jira   -- url: /confluence/pages/viewpage.action | traceId: 6c5728dda552200f | userName: <username> | action: viewpage | page: <pageid>  java.util.concurrent.RejectedExecutionException: Task java.util.concurrent.FutureTask@a616946 rejected from java.util.concurrent.ThreadPoolExecutor@3e4f88f6[Running, pool size = 5, active threads = 5, queued tasks = 1000, completed tasks = 1585899]  {code}    But before getting the error we don't have a way to proactively monitor these values.    h3. Suggested Solution    Add an option for live monitoring of the above values (pool size, active threads, queued tasks, and completed tasks) as Confluence MBeans for example, and in that way, we could suggest [Live Monitoring Using the JMX Interface|https://confluence.<USER>doc/live-monitoring-using-the-jmx-interface-150274182.html].      h3. Why this is important    There is currently no other way for Confluence administrators to monitor the pool for Jira Issue Macros and that would be useful for them to resize the values based on concrete information of their environment if needed.    h3. Workaround    n/a"""
comments: "This is a major issue when teams report statuses of projects via Confluence, If the number of Jira issues being displayed via Jira Issue Macros exceed the configuration limits, they time out without any results, which is a poor customer experience. There should be a way to provide visibility into how resources are being used on Jira and Confluence."

summary: "Copy and paste page likes in a comment overwrites the complete comment"
description:
"""h3. Issue Summary When copying the string ‘and XX others like this’ at the bottom of the page, pasting it into your comment and then liking your new comment, the comment changes from the original text to {quote}You like this {quote} It changes every line of text to the one on the quote above. h3. Steps to Reproduce # Start writing a comment # Select the text from the likes footer at the bottom of the page, e.g the String ‘and 12 others like this’ and paste it into your comment # Add some more text to the comment, so it reads approx. {quote} and 12 others like this -> We already have a lot of agreement!! {quote} # Save the comment # Reload the page (so you are able to like the new comment) # Like your new comment h3. Expected Results The comment in regular ‘read’ view is displayed as before. h3. Actual Results The comments content is displayed as {quote} You like this You like this {quote} h3. Workaround Currently, there is no known workaround for this behaviour. A workaround will be added here when available. h3. Notes: * This was not reproduced on Confluence Cloud * When editing the comment, the 'correct' content is displayed. This bug is a display-only defect * When looking at the initial page request, the page source delivered by the server contains the correct comment text * When looking at the comment's storage format, the with classes is included, when copy & pasting from the page This, together with some over-zealous .js / css selectors probably cause the replacing of content in every

tag of the comment."""

It would be good if you could collapse this time periods, similar like you can collapse single columns/rows in Excel. So that long-period projects are shown in a better way."""

summary: "Retrieving Confluence User Last Login Date using REST API for Confluence"
description: """Add the ability to provide the last login details of users in confluence using REST API"""

summary: "Permissions checks performed against the index instead of on the database"
description: """h3. Summary  As a +<USER>   I want +Confluence to perform permissions checks against an index, instead of directly on the database+  So that +I can have a faster performance on this operation, as with large instances, with thousands of pages, spaces and groups, this can take long times.+    h3. Details  Customer with an environment with Directory containing more than 300K users and 100K groups, where some users can be members of more than 5K groups, permissions checks can take a proportional time to be performed.  This request is to consider the option of having permissions indexed with Lucene or other indexing engine and consult against it, to increase performance        """

summary: "Batch e-mail notifications for Pages Shared to Groups"
description: """  h3. Summary  As a +<USER>   I want to +Be able to batch e-mail notifications for pages shared to groups+  So that +I can reduce the load caused by users sharing pages to large groups, or schedule them to be sent only overnight (for example)+    h3. Details  On larger environments, with groups containing lots of users, sending e-mail notifications throughout the day can cause a load on Confluence and e-mail servers, so this request is to include the option to batch these notifications.  Currently, we can only change the batching window for changes and comments on the same page or blog post, so not specifically for content shared:  [Email Notifications|https://confluence.<USER>doc/email-notifications-145162.html?_ga=2.193161316.852101743.1591883066-1701894688.1591110279]    h3. Workaround  You can disable the whole Sharing option: [How Do I Disable Share Button in Confluence|https://confluence.<USER>confkb/how-do-i-disable-share-button-in-confluence-590938985.html]    """

summary: "Disable Sharing page to Groups"
description: """h3. Summary  As a +System Admin+  I want to +Disable the option to Share a page to a group+  So that +I can avoid the load from a user trying to share a page for large groups+    h3. Details  To reduce load on the server caused by e-mail notifications, and reducing spam from Confluence server, there could be a way to disable Sharing content to group.    h3. Workaround  Currently you can only disable the overall Sharing option using instructions on this article:   [How Do I Disable Share Button in Confluence|https://confluence.<USER>confkb/how-do-i-disable-share-button-in-confluence-590938985.html]"""

Expected Results   * E-mail is sent to the users   * No error on the logs expect if one actually occurs    h3. Actual Results   * E-mail is sent to the users   * SQL error is shown on the logs:   ** With Postgres:       ** With Oracle:      h3. Workaround    Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available    Issue was not reproducible in 7.4.0"""

summary: "Add support for Microsoft SQL Data Compression "
description: """h3. Problem Definition    As per our Supported Platforms documentation ([link|https://confluence.<USER>doc/supported-platforms-207488198.html]), we do not officially support [Data Compression with Microsoft SQL Server|https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/sql/relational-databases/data-compression/data-compression?view=sql-server-ver15].    Several clients have asked us to formally support this compression feature."""

summary: "Custom Jira fields with type ""Number Field"" do not appear on Jira Charts macro"
description: """h4. Issue summary   # In Jira, we can create custom fields for issues with different types, like """"List"""", """"Number Field"""", and """"Label"""", each with their own format.  # In Confluence, with the *Jira Chart* macro, when using the *Two Dimensional* chart's *Display Option*, custom *Number Field* from Jira do not appear as selection for the *XAxis* and *YAxis*.  # Other custom fields with type """"List"""" and type """"Label"""" appear fine.  # There is a difference between the text and label however, where for the text custom fields, I could only filter them between null or not null, and could not use the equals sign *=* in my query, but I could do so with labels, querying the exact value like * = 2.4.3* for example.    h4. Expected behavior  # All custom fields should be able to appear in the Jira Charts macro    h4. Current Behavior   # Custom field with type """"Number Field"""" do not appear.    h4. Workaround  # Use type """"Label"""" instead."""

summary: "Granular text selection not appearing on iOS app for Confluence Server"

Issue Summary  # When selecting a text in View mode, the option to select the text we want to highlight is not shown.  # We will need to edit the page first to be able to see the specific text we want to select.  # Issue does not persist on the Android app, or even on iOS Safari.  **  !Droid_text_selection.jpg|thumbnail!   **  !iTextSelect.jpg|thumbnail!     h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Create a page in Confluence with some text.  # Save the page.  # Try to select a text by long pressing on it.    h3. Expected Results  # The option to select the text we want is shown.    h3. Actual Results  # The option to select the text we want is clear\invisible.    h3. Workaround  # Edit the page to copy specific text  # Alternatively, use the Safari browser."""

summary: "Configurable options for Spam Prevention"
description: """h3. Current behavior  * Confluence has an option for Spam Prevention, which, when enabled adds Captchas to several of the actions in Confluence, like adding, attachments, comments, <USER>  * These are not configurable, and when Spam Prevention is enabled, they are enabled for all actions.  ** [Spam Prevention|https://confluence.<USER>doc/configuring-captcha-for-spam-prevention-169970.html]    h3. Suggested behavior  * Make the Spam Prevention page configurable, so that an admin can enable and disable the functionality for certain actions like adding a comment."""

summary: "Create Confluence Questions Topic with Special Character fails"
description: """h3. Summary  Creating a Confluence Questions topic with special characters fails with:    * {color:red}An unknown server error has occurred.{color}    h3. Environment  * Confluence 7.4.0  * Questions for Confluence 2.7.20    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Install [Questions for Confluence|https://marketplace.<USER>plugins/com.atlassian.confluence.plugins.confluence-questions/server/overview] from Marketplace  # Go to the *Topics* tab  Enter a new topic with *Special Characters* in it  #* For example: *test.topic*  # Click *Next* and *Save*     h3. Expected Results    A new topic is created    h3. Actual Results    # An error occurs:       !test.topic.png|thumbnail!     An error in {{atlassian-confluence.log}} occurs:      h3. Root Cause    A Topic follows the validation for a label in which these characters are not permitted:    {code:title=LabelParser.java}      private static final SortedSet<Character> INVALID_LABEL_CHARACTERS = ImmutableSortedSet.of(              ':', ';', ',', '.', ' ', '?', '&', '[', ']', '(', ')', '#', '^', '*', '@', '!', '<', '>'      );  {code}    The label validation error is shown when creating a """"Label"""":     !label_validation_error.png|thumbnail!     It appears the above informative exception when creating a label is not propagated to Confluence Questions add-on when creating a Topic.    h3. Resolution    Change the Topic name so it does not contain one of the above mentioned special characters.  """

summary: "Update JIRA issue from Confluence page"
