Currently, we can use a blueprint (i.e. Product Requirements) in Confluence to create JIRA issues, but it would be good to be able to update the JIRA issue after it's creation."""

summary: "Support Emojis in Source Editor"
description: """h3. Problem  Currently, emojis outside of the built-in emoticons are not supported in Source Editor.     h3. Suggested Solution  The Source Editor app should encode the characters as required and save without error.    h3. Why This Is Important  Users tend to use other emojis than the built-in emoticons when creating content on the page to make their pages visually engaging. When using the Source Editor app to view and edit some content on a page where emojis already exists, an error similar to the following is thrown making it impossible to save the new changes from the Source Editor:    We've also seen issues where sometimes saving a page also is a problem with emojis present on those pages: [How to identify Invalid Characters on a Confluence page|https://confluence.<USER>confkb/how-to-identify-invalid-characters-on-a-confluence-page-911180364.html] and we've suggested removing them altogether to successfully save the page.    h3. Workaround  N/A"""
comments: "A working Source Editor is very useful in manually fixing CONFSERVER-55928 since we'll be using it to generate ""diffs"" to restore images."

summary: "When exporting a Space to XML Confluence doesn't check if temporary attachment folder was properly created"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  When exporting a Space to XML, Confluence copies pages' attachments from the [attachments directory|https://confluence.<USER>doc/hierarchical-file-system-attachment-storage-704578486.html] {{<confluence-home>/attachments/ver003/}} to a temporary directory such as {{<confluence-home>/temp/xmlexport-<date>/attachments/<pageId>/<attachmentID>}}.    If Confluence is unable to create any of the directories in the above structure, the export fails on a subsequent operation without a meaningful entry in the log on why the directory wasn't created.    The reason why the directory wasn't created isn't the problem for this bug, since it could be something on the OS level.  The problem is that Confluence uses a method that doesn't throw an exception if creating a directory fails.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  The steps below is a way to reproduce a problem on which Confluence tries to create a directory but it isn't possible due to a problem on the OS level.  The problem on the OS isn't the goal for this bug. This occurs because of a limitation of ext3 file system on a maximum number of subdirectories, which will be reached during the export.     # Have a Linux server with an ext3 partition with enough space to install Confluence.   # Install a vanilla instance of Confluence making sure {{<confluence-home>}} is on the ext3 file system.    #* This is validated in Confluence 6.13.10 and 7.4.1.    #* Confluence still uses the same code path on 7.5.0.   # Create a sample space.   # On that space, create 35,000 pages with one attachment in each.    #* The following script may be helpful to automate that task.     # Try to export the Space as XML.    h3. Expected Results  The Space export fails because it wasn't able to create a directory to place one of the attachments.  Checking the Confluence logs we would see an exception thrown during the directory creation and a meaningful message to understand why it couldn't create the directory.    h3. Actual Results  The Space export fails because it wasn't able to create a temporary file in the target directory because one of the directories in the expected hierarchy didn't exist.  There's no meaningful entry in the log telling which specific directory wasn't created and why.      This occurs because this portion of the code was implemented using [{{java.io.Files.mkdirs()}} method|https://docs.oracle.com/javase/7/docs/api/java/io/File.html#mkdirs()] which doesn't throw any exception when it fails.  Although this method doesn't throw an exception on failure, it provide an option to check if it went successfully, which is not used within the code.    An option could be to migrate to [{{java.nio.file.Files.createDirectories()}} method|https://docs.oracle.com/javase/8/docs/api/java/nio/file/Files.html#createDirectories-java.nio.file.Path-java.nio.file.attribute.FileAttribute...-].    h3. Workaround    _Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available_"""
comments: "Just curious in which version it will be fixed."

A workaround will be added here when available."""

summary: "Setting a reminder on a Jira Release event type fails when using an Oracle database"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Attempting to set up a notification on a Jira Release Data event type in Team Calendars results in a SQL exception and fails.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Integrate Confluence & Team Calendars with a Jira instance (Bug report written with Confluence 7.4.0 -> Jira 8.5.4 -> Team Calendars )   # Create a Team Calendar in Confluence   # Create a few issues in Jira and also create a Release in Jira.   # Associate the issues with the Release and be sure to set a *date* on the Release Date. This date is what Team Calendars will display.   # From the Confluence calendar, create an Event and select Jira Release Dates and choose the project & it's release from the drop down. Secondlay, try to choose a reminder interval from the drop down menu.    h3. Expected Results    You will be able to set a reminder interval for a Jira Release event type.  h3. Actual Results    You'll receive the following SQL exception and setting up the reminder fails.    h3. Workaround   * Don't set a reminder for a Jira release event and the date of the release will still display correctly.   * Mirror the release date to a custom field and use a different event type to display and notify on the custom date rather than the OOTB release date event type option.   * Issue is not reproduced on Postgres    h3. Notes    We need to validate this fix against all Oracle versions (12c and 19c) and above as supported in the upcoming releases of Confluence [Supported Platforms|https://confluence.<USER>doc/supported-platforms-207488198.html]"""
comments: "Hello. Upvoting. We run a Confluence that serves over 10k people, and some will care."

summary: "Instructional text mode in template keeps when input multibyte character (Japanese or Chinese)"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    When typing a letter or a word in instructional text (or hint) of template, the Instructional text mode will change to normal text mode automatically. The background color of the input place will change from grey to white.    However, if the input is a multibyte character like Japanese or Chinese, the Instructional text mode will be kept, the background will keep as grey.    The reproducing of above issue depends on browser.  ||Browser||Reproduced or not||  |Chrome|yes|  |Firefox|no|  |Edge|yes|  |Safri|yes|  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a *product requirements* template   # Put mouse pointer on any instructional text(hint) area   # Shift input mode to Hiragana (Japanese) mode   # Type 'あいう' in Japanese mode   # All new input will be treated as instructional text.    h3. Expected Results    The new input will be treated as normal text input without grey background.  h3. Actual Results    The new input will be treated as instructional text with grey background.  h3. Workaround    Input in English mode first, make the grey area of instructional text disappear, and then input in Japanese/Chinese mode."""

summary: "Allow User Macro to obtain user's information after Confluence 7.0"
description: """h3. Problem Definition  Since Confluence 7.0.1, there is a change in {{*<Confluence_Installation>/confluence/WEB-INF/classes/velocity.properties*}} that includes a Secure Introspector to disable certain classes' accessibility.     This affected some of the [User Macros|https://confluence.<USER>doc/writing-user-macros-4485.html] created by the customers that is making use of the restricted class.  Here are some examples of the user macro being affected by this change, mostly related to getting user's information:  * https://community.developer.<USER>t/problem-to-get-about-me-information-from-user-details-after-confluence-7-update/35258  * https://community.developer.<USER>t/confluence-user-details-from-macro-in-7-1-1/34543  * https://community.developer.<USER>t/defaultuserdetailsmanager-does-not-work-in-user-macro-confluence-server-7-3-1/35751  * https://community.developer.<USER>t/cant-use-contentpropertymanager-anymore-in-user-macro-since-7-0-3/32956/3    h3. Suggested Solution  There is no alternative way to obtain user information after the change has been implemented. As this change is based on a security measure, it is not recommended to make any custom changes to the {{velocity.properties}}.     Suggesting to have an alternative way to obtain the user information so that the customers can continue to have custom User Macro that is able to pull user's information"""
comments: "Hi All,    I am the original reporter of this issue.     This issue is due to the new security constraints in Confluence 7, and the “.class.forName” is banned by default in velocity.properties (confluence/WEB-INF/classes).    In our company, we have many macros that use bandanaManager to create the document IDs which needs to use this class, but whenever the version goes 7, all macros are failed, that's really not accepted by our users, so the version update plan is stuck by this issue, the worst is that our current version is already out of support.    If you have similar issues, I encourage you to vote on this ticket, hope Atlassian team can provide an alternative solution for that." Steps to Reproduce  # Integrate Confluence with a Mail Server  # Create a test user and ensure that you are watching a space. Additionally, edit the user profile settings to ensure that your mail subscriptions/email settings will ensure that you will be notified for updates.  # Log in as an admin or another test user and create a blog post OR page with no restrictions in the space that your other test user watches so that they will receive an email notification.    h3. Encrypt data on Android:    [https://stackoverflow.com/questions/6043984/sqlite-encryption-for-android]    Encrypt on IOS:    [https://medium.com/@nirmaluchoudhari/database-encryption-using-sqlcipher-in-ios-87b71d2975ae]"""

summary: "Option to hide data from Confluence on multitask screen and other animations"

This image file holds the information from the last visited page and it's stored on the system storage (in case of a missing device, this can cause data to be stolen). It will be good if there is an option to not use the screenshot from the last screen, but rather another image.    The KTZ image is described in here:  https://abrignoni.blogspot.com/2019/09/ios-snapshots-triage-parser-working.html    This is how you can substitute the screenshot like image, by another image, like a logo or something:    https://stackoverflow.com/questions/12589108/ios-hiding-sensitive-information-on-the-screen-when-app-is-backgrounded/12612380#12612380    How to do it in Android as well:    https://stackoverflow.com/questions/28570644/customize-android-app-screen-image-which-is-visible-in-switch-between-apps-scree  """

summary: "calendar events"
description: """Good morning,    I would like to make a suggestion for an improvement to you confluence calendar.    We have a set of several events that are repeated for each release cycle. For example, we have feature code cut-off, create release branch, test, final code cut-off, create release package and release.  You get the idea, these are sequential events and need to be done in order and take a number of days.    Currently we create a separate event for each of these so when a date changes we need to go back and change each event. This is a pain.    It would be nice to have a template something like:    event 1:  start and length in days that automatically compensates for holidays and weekends.  event 2:  event 1 plus XX days and length of days.  event 3:  event 1 or 2 plus XX days and length of days  ...    Then if the release date changes, I can go in and change the date on event 1 and everything else automatically lines up.    Thanks,  <USER>    """

summary: "Creating a Jira Issue from Confluence may be slow depending on the number of existing projects in the Jira instance"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  When Confluence is integrated with Jira, the Confluence user can create Jira issues directly from Confluence as explained in the [product documentation|https://confluence.<USER>doc/use-jira-applications-and-confluence-together-427623543.html#UseJiraapplicationsandConfluencetogether-CreateissuesfrominsideConfluence].    Loading the create issue form, either on viewing or editing a page, may be slow while Confluence tries to get the the avatar for all projects in the target Jira instance, sending a request for each project from the user's browser to Confluence and then to Jira.    If the Jira instance has just a few projects, then it completes quickly, without the user noticing any delay.    If Confluence is connected to a large Jira instance, with thousands of projects, then it may take some seconds (or even minutes) to release the UI so the user can continue the process to create the Jira Issue.  If the Jira instance has 3,000 projects, then 3,000 requests will be created from the browser to Confluence, no matter if the project is not appearing in the screen.    A better design approach would be one of the two options:  # Use paginated requests from the _select_ box.   #* This would decrease the number of requests to Jira to only those projects the user is viewing in the UI.   #* For example, [select2|https://select2.org/data-sources/ajax] has this feature.  # Don't load the project avatar on the form.      Since Confluence would be processing thousands of requests from an user (or multiple users, leaning on how much this functionality is used), depending how fast it can process or drop these requests, there will be a lot of requests queued on Tomcat or on the HTTP server (if using a reverse proxy).  This can give other users the feeling that Confluence is not working or is slow, since new requests will also be queued.    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Install a vanilla instance of Jira.    #* Jira *8.5.3* was used to validate the issue.   # Create 2,000 sample projects.    #* You may use the following shell script to create these projects.    # Install a vanilla instance of Confluence.   #* The issue was validated on Confluence versions *6.13.10*, *7.2.1* and *7.4.0*.  # Create an application link between Confluence and Jira.   #* Although it doesn't change anything on the bug behavior, create the link using _impersonation_ so you don't need to allow authentication between applications.  # Create a sample Space in Confluence.  # Access the home page of the sample Space.  # Highlight a text on the page and click on the _Create Jira Issue_ icon to open the form.  # On the _Create Task_ form click on the _Select project_ box.    h3. Expected Results  Confluence quickly loads the options in the select box.    h3. Actual Results  There will be a delay to have the select box opened and the browser may freeze for some seconds while thousands of requests are created the sent to Confluence.    If browser's developer tools is opened during the process, monitoring the network requests will shows 2,000 (or more depending on the number of projects) requests.   !screenshot-001.png|thumbnail!        The requests are similar to      If there's an access log enabled on front of Jira (either on a reverse proxy or on Tomcat), there will be lots of requests similar to the below:        Some of these requests may fail depending on the load on Confluence or on Jira -- if Confluence and Jira are capable of dealing with all the requests, then these errors won't occur.  The request may fail with an HTTP 500 status and with a {{JiraCommunicationException}} error.  Looking at the Confluence application logs we may find entries similar to the below:      If another user tries to display a page from Confluence while it is still processing the requests for the projects' avatars, then this new request may take a long time to be processed, since it will be queued on the Tomcat or Reverse Proxy side.  Depending on the timeout configuration of the Reverse Proxy, this new request may not even reach the Confluence server.    The same occurs if the user tries to create the Jira issue using the Jira Issues Macro when editing a page.   !screenshot-002.png|thumbnail!              h3. Workaround  _Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available._"""

summary: "Delegated directory fails to authenticate users that are created in Confluence"
description: """h3. Summary    Confluence allows you to create users when the Delegated directory is at the top position. Although that's possible, the user will be treated as an external user while it does not exist in the LDAP.    This will cause problems with the authentication as the user exists in Confluence but not in the LDAP.    This specific behavior is related to [CONFSERVER-22296 - Provide an option to select user directory on adding/deleting users|https://jira.<USER>browse/CONFSERVER-22296], where you cannot choose the directory where the user will be saved.    h3. Environment  * Confluence  * Delegated directory    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Create a Delegated Directory and move it at the top position.  # Create a new user in Confluence.  # The user will be shown as a Delegated User, however, the user does not exist in the LDAP.  # Try to login in Confluence with this user.    h3. Expected Results    * Confluence will create the user in the internal directory.  or  * Confluence will remove the ability to create users if the delegated option is at the top position      h3. Actual Results  * The interface will show that the user is coming from the LDAP, but the user does not exist in the LDAP.  * The authentication will fail.  * The database will show that the user is has a hashed password, but it should be """"nopass"""".      h3.Workaround  There's no workaround for the bug, but to prevent this issue from occurring again, don't create users in Confluence if there's a delegated directory.  """

summary: "Add option to deactivate auto capitalization of 'Atlassian' and 'Jira'"
description: """A lot of us are using Confluence as a tool to document our own Atlassian tool installations.    When we type a file path such as {{/var/log/atlassian}} Confluence (and Jira) will automatically transform this to {{/var/log/Atlassian}}.  The same goes for 'jira' and 'JIRA' becoming Jira.  It prevents us from writing correct documentation.    Words like Confluence, Bamboo, Bitbucket, <USER> do not suffer from this odd feature.    Please:   * Allow admins to deactivate this feature  OR   * Do not autocorrect those words when they are preceded by a non-whitespace character         Related issue: CONFSERVER-25133  This one was closed when Jira was changed to JIRA but the actual problem was never resolved thereofre I created this suggestion."""

summary: "Create bulk Jira issues from Confluence by specifying a common team field"
description: """This came in from a customer where we found a limitation of the Jira Issues macro.    The use case for """"appending to a specific team"""" - this is not only to relating this to a specific team but more a means to group all these together.    Right now we have a table in Confluence that we create Jira issues from (could be user requirements <USER> but there is no way to easily group these together as it may not need to be grouped by an epic but maybe a different items as these may only be part of the stories in an epic but want to still group these together.    In our example there is are numerous steps to rollout a process across a team so this is a template of work in Confluence which we then create per team. When creating these story's in Jira there is no way to note which team this is. So would be great if we could reference a field of information like Team name that can the show up on the created Jira issue, as opposed to a column on a table that needs to be populated for every row.    Instead of specifying an Epic to associate these tickets with or using a column, I'd like to be able to either specify a bulk item arbitrarily or have some more options when creating bulk issues."""

summary: "Text Size is Inconsistent When Printing Page"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    When a user goes to print a Confluence page from their browser, they will randomly get a page with the text rendered in a smaller size. This appears usually when adding comments to a page and then going to print the page.    Specifically, this issue happens when using the browser's own print option and not Confluence's own export options. There are several valid reasons why a user will want to use this method instead of exporting from within Confluence, as currently there is not a method to include comments on a single page export (this option is only shown for whole Space exports) and Confluence will export the page different than what is shown unless the PDF export is customized with CSS/HTML.    h3. Steps to Reproduce    # Create a page with a few paragraphs worth of content and save.  # Add a comment to the page and then immediately press CTRL + P to print the page from the browser.  # Notice that the text is smaller than what is shown on the page.  # Refresh the page and print it again. You will see that the text is back to its normal size.    h3. Expected Results    A page with the correct text size shown on the export.    !normal.png!    h3. Actual Results    The text is rendered in a smaller size.    !small.png!    h3. Workaround    Luckily refreshing the page seems to resolve this issue, however, this can be an extra step that can be tedious in workflows that need to export single pages with comments."""

summary: "Checking inline tasks will cause unpublished tasks to be set incorrectly"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    If inline tasks are being worked on when editing a confluence page, and these published pages have tasks that are being checked or unchecked by others, the wrong task states will be published when the draft is published.    Example video:  [^tasks-checked-incorrectly.mov]     This may be related to another issue observed in [CONFSERVER-59676|https://jira.<USER>browse/CONFSERVER-59676] where drafts do not show the Unpublished Changes warning message when tasks are modified.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  1. Create a page with two or three task elements and publish the page without checking any tasks  2.  Edit the page again and create a new task between the existing ones (but don't publish and stay in edit mode)  3.  Open the same page in a second tab in view mode and check all the tasks  4.  In the first tab, you will now see that the wrong tasks are checked  5.  The then published page will have incorrect task states    h3. Expected Results    When task states are changed by others in published pages the state will be updated or notified in their drafts or edited pages.    h3. Actual Results    Changing task states in published pages will incorrectly overwrite the state of any new tasks that have been added or changed in edited pages.    h3. Workaround    There is currently no workaround        """

summary: "Restrictions : A way to relax a restriction in a descendant page"
description: """h1. Requirement    As the writer of a page I need to provide access to a particular confluence without giving them access to ancestor pages. This is so that they can access the specific page without accessing some sensitive ancestor pages. It is also so that I can put the page in question in the appropriate page in the hierarchy so it can be easily found by normal viewers/editors of that page tree.    h1. Example    Somewhere in a space there is this page subtree:    * Page A - restricted to users X and Y  ** Page B - inherits restrictions from Page A  *** Page C - I want to permit user Z rights to view and edit just this page    h1. Additional notes    When adding a restriction to Page C it is not obvious that the rules on that page implement an AND. That is the user has to appear in both Page A's and Page C's restrictions in order for that person to have access. I thought that the rules were an OR, such that a user appearing in either restriction list would have access. This confused me."""

summary: "Notify watcher option is checked by default when user cookies is deleted or expired"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  If a user uncheck the notify watcher option when editing a page, the option will get checked back if the user cookies is delete or expired in the browser or switched to another browser where new cookies is generated.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  *Case 1*  # Access Confluence via Google Chrome  # Access a Confluence page  # Edit the page  # Uncheck the """"Notify Watcher"""" option  # Save the page  # Clear the Chrome Browser cache and cookies  # Close Chrome  # Open Chrome and access to the same page  # Edit the page  # You will notice that the """"Notify Watcher"""" option is checked  (i) This behavior affect all Chrome, FireFox, and IE 11 Browser    *Case 2*  # Access Confluence via Google Chrome  # Access a Confluence page  # Edit the page  # Uncheck the """"Notify Watcher"""" option  # Save the page  # Access Confluence via FireFox or IE 11 browser  # Access the same page  # Edit the page  # You will notice that the """"Notify Watcher"""" option is checked.  (i) For details, please review the following video   [^Recording #12.mp4]       h3. Expected Results  The Notify Watcher option will remain uncheck    h3. Actual Results  The Notify Watcher option is reset to check when cookies is deleted, expired, or recreated in other browser    h3. Workaround  Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Excerpt-include macro does not display an Edit button for Office Excel macro generated content "
description: """h3. Issue Summary    When an _Excerpt-include_ macro displays content rendered from an _Office Excel_ macro, the content does not include an Edit button.  h3. Environment    Confluence 7.5.0  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a page that includes an _Excerpt_ macro.   ** Within this macro, insert an _Office Excel_ macro that references an Excel file that's attached to the page.   # Next, create another page with an _Excerpt-include_ macro.   ** Configure this macro instance to reference the _Excerpt_ macro created in the previous step.    h3. Expected Results    The Excel content rendered on the page that has the _Excerpt-include_ macro *should display* an *Edit* button located directly above the included Excel spreadsheet.  h3. Actual Results    The Excel content rendered on the page that has the _Excerpt-include_ macro *does not display* an *Edit* button located directly above the included Excel spreadsheet.  h3. Notes    The page that includes the _Excerpt_ macro does display the *Edit* button regardless of Confluence version.  h3. Workaround    The only known workaround for this issue would be to edit the Excel worksheet within the original source page that contains it."""

summary: "Pages are created outside of page hierarchy when triggering create page from draft"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Hierarchy is lost when you create a child page before publishing the """"draft-parent"""" page.  h3. Steps to Reproduce    *Version 1*    * Visit page A   * Press the 'Create' button   * After the new page draft opens, press the 'Create' button _again_   * Give the new page a name 'B' and 'Publish'    *Version 2*    * Visit a page 'A'   * Press shortcut 'c' and _keep the key pressed_.   * Wait until at least 2 page loads did occur   * give the new draft a name 'B' and 'Publish'    h3. Expected Results   * Confluence will not allow you to hit the """"Create"""" button if you haven't published the page.   * New page with hierarchy: A -> B    h3. Actual Results   * On Page Information the parent is listed as """"$webwork.htmlEncode($content.displayTitle)"""".   * Checking internally in the database, the first page(Page A) will be a draft without title, hence causing the """"$webwork.htmlEncode($content.displayTitle)"""".    *Notes*   * After updating the null entries and rebuilding the ancestor table, the problem is still not resolved as it was created a page from a draft.   * The page can be put back into the page hierarchy by simply doing a 'move' on it, and selecting a regular page as the new parent.    h3. Workaround   * There are no workarounds. Inserting the title manually in the database still does not resolve the problem of having a parent page that it is only a draft."""
comments: "There is a query, on how to fnd affected pages among [this community question|https://community.<USER>t5/Confluence-questions/Parent-Page-is-webwork-htmlEncode-content-displayTitle/qaq-p/753760]'s anwers." [SEP] "Workaround: I fixed the missing parent page by moving the page to a new parent"

summary: "Update our Confluence Page Gadget documentation"
description: """h3. Problem statement    According to [this|https://confluence.<USER>doc/confluence-page-gadget-218275791.html] page, we ended support for the Confluence Page gadget in Confluence 7.0.     This, however, does not apply to the Confluence Page Gadget on Jira, as it keeps working normally on Confluence 7.x versions. The documentation is not clear about that, and might give the impression that customers will need to use another gadget to reference Confluence pages on Jira, and this is not accurate.    h3. Suggest resolution    Please make the documentation more clear, as the Confluence page gadget is still available for Jira and is the only option to reference pages from Confluence."""

summary: "Custom Span Tags Cause all Heading on Page to Change"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    When creating a page and formatting specific sections that have a <span> tag seems to cause unwanted effects when trying to switch a single heading, which causes the whole page to also get changed to the same formatting. This can be a major pain point if users copy over information that introduces these span tags into their connect.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Edit a page with several different headings already on there (H1, H3, Pre-formatted).  # Introduce a <span> tag to modify anything within the line.  # Simply put the cursor in middle on line on one of the headings  # Change the heading to another heading, such as H3 to Pre-formatted    h3. Expected Results    Only a single line should change.    h3. Actual Results    The whole page is changing to the same formatting.    h3. Workaround    Currently, the workaround is to remove the <span> tags."""

Steps to Reproduce  # Create table with header and lots of rows.  # Scroll down.   ** As issue is intermittent, if issue not reproducible, refresh the page and scroll again.  h3. Workaround  * Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available  """

summary: "Related Labels macro fetching content from deleted page"
description: """h3. Summary    The Related Labels macro will also bring labels that are no longer active.    h3. summary: "Team Calendar CalDav integration with iOS and macOS Catalina is not functioning correctly and does not reflect selected calendar colour"
description: """h3. Problem    When subscribing with or adding an account from a Team Calendar in macOS Catalina. the macOS calendar app does not contain checkboxes to select these individual calendars. All Caldav calendars from confluence are also displayed with blank colours. Any colour changes made last only a few moments and then revert to blank or unset, the latest on the next start of the application. There is also a similar problem in iOS 13.4 with a slightly different behaviour and all colours change randomly on reload.  h3. Environment    MacOS Catalina   iOS 13.4  h3. Steps to Reproduce   * Create a Team Calendar in Confluence with multiple calendars   * Choose to subscribe to this calendar and either choose your app as Apple Calendar (MacOS) or Apple Calendar (iOS)   * Add a new account or subscribe to the Caldav calendar in MacOS/iOS calendar.   * Open the calendar app    h3. Expected Results    Team Calendars should behave in the same manner as other Caldav calendars in the app  h3. Actual Results    Team Calendar Caldav calendars do not contain checkboxes to select the individual calendars when uncoloured and they are also displayed with blank checkboxes, any attempt to colour them is reset again after a few seconds or when opening the app.  h3. Workaround    No workaround is currently available  h3. Notes    This does not occur in MacOS Mojave   Other Caldav calendar hosts do work correctly, Nextcloud and Owncloud Caldav calendars behave as expected."""

summary: "Jira Issue description contains no formatting when created using Create Multiple Issues From Table in the Confluence Editor"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    While creating multiple Jira issues within Confluence editor from a table, the auto populated description does not retain any spacing.  When viewing the description in the Jira issue, all spacing and much of the formatting has been removed.  h3. Steps to Reproduce     • Create a simple table with at least three rows.     !screenshot-1.png|thumbnail!      • Use the first row as the table headers.  The first one being Summary and second one Description.   • Add """"Test"""" as the summary in both empty cells in the """"Summary"""" column.   • Write two or more paragraphs with a space between or add a simple bulleted list to the empty cells in the column named Description.   • Highlight the entire table and click the """"Create Jira Issue"""" icon in the pop up   • Click """"Create Multiple Issues From Table""""     !screenshot-5.png|thumbnail!      • Select the correct headers for Summary and Description.   • Create the Jira Issues.   • Open the Jira Issues in Jira.  h3. Expected Results    The basic spacing and formatting is retained in the description in the Jira issue.  h3. Actual Results     !screenshot-2.png|thumbnail!  !screenshot-3.png|thumbnail!  !screenshot-4.png|thumbnail!     The Jira issue does not retain any spacing or most formatting in the Description.  This results in Descriptions which are formatted with spacing, bullets, or bold/italic/underlines being jumbled up and at times unreadable.       h3. Workaround    Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""
comments: "I'm using this capability as the only way to automatically and safely translate Confluence table lines into Jira issues (e.g. User Stories). I urge you to fix this bug ASAP so I (as I'm sure many others) will be able to utilize this great feature. " [SEP] "Could you please raise the priority of this ticket. It may affect my team a lot!" summary: "Upgrade Tomcat to the fixed version of CVE-2020-9484"
description: """h3. Problem Definition    A recent Tomcat vulnerability ([CVE-2020-9484|https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2020-9484]) in which an attacker can access the content and names of files on a server when custom PersistenceManager filestores are used was announced that affects the following versions:   10.0.0-M1 to 10.0.0-M4, 9.0.0.M1 to 9.0.34, 8.5.0 to 8.5.54 and 7.0.0 to 7.0.103    Confluence, as shipped, does not use PersistenceManager and is not vulnerable, but can be made so with customizations.    Affected versions of Tomcat include:  * 7.0.0 to 7.0.103  * 8.5.0 to 8.5.54  * 9.0.0.M1 to 9.0.34  * 10.0.0-M1 to 10.0.0-M4     h3. Suggested Solution  Include an unaffected version of Tomcat (9.0.35 and above for Tomcat) in Confluence, """
comments: "For CVE-2020-1938 too." [SEP] "Is Jira v8.7.1  impacted by this vulnerability?" [SEP] "This should be fixed in Jira, too. Jira 8.9.0 is affected by this." [SEP] "This is a bug, not a ""Suggestion.""    Security should take precedence over everything.         As in, this is high criticality, stop what you are doing now, and fix it." [SEP] "Could you confirm which version is fine from this issue?    Both confluence and jira versions we need.               " [SEP] "Hello,     I raised the same support request and was provided the following feedback:  {panel}   *   {quote}Indeed the Tomcat versions that are bundled with Jira 8.7 (and later), and Confluence 7.5 is affected by the CVE that you have mentioned on your case description, as Jira utilizes Tomcat 8.5.42 and Confluence 9.0.33.    *Tomcat versions:*   * 8.7.1 Jira - Apache Tomcat/8.5.42   * 4.7.1 Jira - Apache Tomcat/8.5.42   * 7.5.0 Confluence - Apache Tomcat/9.0.33    For that reason, we have raised two new bug reports on our side, one for Jira

summary: "Additional Synchrony logging "
description: """As a Confluence administrator, it would be helpful to have improved logging round Synchrony:    * In a scenario where Confluence Data Center is managing Synchrony, and the internal proxy is not enabled, Sychrony traffic will be directed through /synchrony first, and failing that, will try /synchrony-proxy and finally confluence-context-path/synchrony-proxy. It wold be useful to know when/why Confluence uses the alternate paths to reach Synchrony.   * Similarly, logging around failed websocket upgrades could help administrators understand why the failover to XHR occurs.  * There's a lot of noise in the Synchrony logs, and so the ability to filter out the noise would be useful. Unfortunately, changing the level of Synchrony logging from DEBUG to WARN (for example) stops all logging, so it can't be done that way.   """
comments: "Hi. Synchrony has logging on various levels, and default (at least in the dev configuration) is INFO, which is not as noisy as DEBUG, but most certainly logs various things. How do you try to change synchrony logging level? I'll take a look at what sets synchrony logging level to DEBUG." [SEP] "Documentation has been updated to include some information on how to change the logging level in both managed and standalone Synchrony, and how to strip the DEBUG prefix from the managed synchrony logs.      See https://confluence.<USER>display/DOC/Configuring+Synchrony"

Workaround   Options for that could be (although both unsupported):  * 3rd party SAML software could provide this kind of information  * Customizing the login screen to record that information    """

summary: "Convert\move a blog post to a regular page"
description: """{panel:bgColor=#e7f4fa}   *NOTE:* This suggestion is for *Confluence Server*. Using *Confluence Cloud*? [See the corresponding suggestion|http://jira.<USER>browse/CONFCLOUD-16142].   {panel}  Use case: User make a page in personal space and wants to convert\move the page into a news item (blog post) together with all attachments    Also want to move a blog post to a page."""
comments: "We started using the ""Blog"" area for a quick notes bucket, thinking we could then distribute those notes to other spaces/regular pages later/as necessary. Imagine my surprise to find that yes, I can move one to another space, but only to that space's ""blog."" I'm hard pressed to find any other good use for the blog for us, so this was looking like a pretty good plan, particularly using some Zapier magic to allow creating the blog pages from Slack. But alas, having them locked into the ""blog,"" is pretty much a deal breaker. MAYBE if the ""Blog"" could be RENAMED, but of course it cannot. Frustrating." [SEP] "Sorry, this Issue have been around for 5+ years with no progress... longer then most users... so save your energy ;)    Copy/Past (Individual segments), is the only way I can to do this." [SEP] "A user on my team had valuable blog content and then left my organization.   I would like to move his blog entries to a more public space.    Instead, I guess I will try to copy/paste and see how it goes."

However,   * The user can view the home space page.   * Anonymous user can view the Space.   * Unlicensed users can view the Space.   * Issue is also seen when using the [Smart Terms for Confluence app|https://marketplace.<USER>apps/1219677/smart-terms-for-confluence-glossary?hosting=server&tab=overview]    h3. summary: "Docx files with Vector Based images fails to upload"
description: """h3. Problem    The upload of Work file (.docx) with a Vector based image never finishes and a thread gets stuck on the backend forever. Depending on the system size, this can cause some performance issues.    The confluence.log shows this thread stuck:      The class/method affect is this one: com.aspose.words.ShapeRenderer    Based on the [documentation|https://apireference.aspose.com/words/java/com.aspose.words/ShapeRenderer] from the Aspose this class is used to convert Vector images into 'real' images.    h3. Environment    6.13.11 and 7.40    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Upload the docs with the vector image   # The upload never ends    h3. Workaround    It's possible to transform the Vector Based Image in Graphical images using many tools. So, the workaround is not use Vector Based Images on the docs to be upload in the Confluence.  h3. Notes"""
comments: "{color:#FF0000}This is a SECURITY ISSUE.{color}         Please increase the priority significantly. The defect impacts platform stability and a few people can easily do a denial of service attack using this defect / vulernability."

summary: "Restoring page from trash in personal space shows not permitted error for users with a space in their username"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    When using the restoretrashitem.action to restore a page, Confluence prompts a not permitted notification to user when an external LDAP is in play.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Installed Confluence v6.15   # Connected to Apache DS (any LDAP server will do)   # Created a (same) username with space in it and synced it to Confluence   !pk-2.png|width=163,height=110!   # This user's personal space has a white space,  #* {code:sql}  select spacename, lowerspacekey, spacetype from spaces where spacename like '%Pat%';  {code}  #* {code:java}  spacename   lowerspacekey spacetype   ----------- ------------- ---------   Pat Doe ~Pat Doe  personal    {code}  # Created a page in personal space  # Deleted it  # Upon restore, got the same error!   !pk-2.png|width=132,height=89!  # The issue didn't happen with other spaces  # Also, I renamed the user to without space,  # The issue still happened - because Space key is still the same. This also narrows down the cause to white space in Space Key       h3. Expected Results  Page restored from trash.   h3. Actual Results  Confluence prompts notification:   *Not permitted*     h3. Workaround  We can use the REST API to restore the page.   # Get the contentid for the deleted page.   #* We can get this from URL when attempting to restore the deleted page from trash  #* {code:java}  http://localhost:8090/pages/restoretrashitem.action?key=~admin&contentId=917510  {code}  # We need to find the version number for the body.   #* We can run this query against the database:  #* {code:java}  select * from content  where contentid=917510;  {code}  #* (i) We need to use the hibernateversion column.   # Run this REST API:  #* {code:java}  PUT http://localhost:8090/rest/api/content/917510    BODY  {  """"version"""":    {""""number"""":2    },   """"status"""": """"current""""  }  {code}    """
comments: "*+Example call with curl+*  {CODE}  curl -u user:pass -H 'Content-Type: application/<USER> -H 'Accept: application/<USER> -d '{ ""id"": ""111"", ""status"": ""current"", ""version"": { ""number"": 2 } }' -X PUT http://localhost:8090/rest/api/content/111 | python -mjson.tool  {CODE}"

summary: "Processing drop-shadow image effect on certain images causes long JVM safepoint pauses"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    When adding an image to a page and applying an image effect, if you choose drop-shadow the {{ImageFilterEffect}} thread will become long-running and cause a JVM pause while it's attempting to go to safepoint for garbage collection or other VM tasks.     Pauses can be an upwards of 35 seconds, which is long enough to cause a node ejection in Data Center environments if the Hazelcast heartbeat is set to the default interval of 30 seconds or less. Other pauses can still be 9-24 seconds, during which the Confluence application would be totally inaccessible.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a new page and upload the *1MB-small-image.png* file (see below), which is under 1MB in size   # Hover your mouse over the inserted image and select """"Properties""""   # Choose the """"Drop Shadow"""" effect and click OK   # You will then observe a long pause while it processes this small image, during which time the JVM itself is paused    A similar long pause can be induced by using very large files also, (must be less than 16MB in size, which is the {{ImageFilterEffect}} plugin's default threshold). See *14MB-large-image.png* as an example.     Only certain small files can induce the long pauses, as many less than 1MB images process in under 3 seconds. This is likely has more to do with the pixel count than it does with the size of the image, as the *3MB-small-image-fast-processing.png* example file (see below) will process faster than the 1MB example file - the difference being the pixel count.   h3. Expected Results    The image will be displayed on the page with the image effect applied to it, and the whole process will complete in 2-3 seconds max.  h3. Actual Results    The JVM will pause for upwards of 9-35 seconds while processing the image effect due to the rush to safepoint being delayed by the {{ImageFilterEffect}} thread - it is suspected that {{ImageFilterEffect}} thread is executing native code while processing images, in which case the thread would ignore the call to safepoint until it's completed processing.    The following safepoint statistics output will be shown in the {{catalina.out}} file:    The following system properties must be added to see the output above:    The {{atlassian-confluence.log}} file will show the following:    The following logging package must be set to DEBUG to see the output above:    h3. Workarounds   # Disable the {{ImageEffectsPlugin}} system app in General Configuration -> Manage Apps -> System apps   # Do not use the """"Drop-Shadow"""" effect    [Limit the impact of Image Effects on Confluence|https://confluence.<USER>confkb/limit-the-impact-of-image-effects-on-confluence-952071975.html] describes a process to place an upper limit of 1MB on the images it will process and 4000x4000 pixels, however the *1MB-small-image.png* file (see below) is an example of a file slightly under 1MB and slightly under 4000x4000 pixels which still has this same problem, so limiting the images by file size or pixel size may not fully circumvent this issue unless you set the limiting threshold very low.   h3. Sample Images  ||14MB file||1MB file||3MB file ||  | * high pixel count   * large size   * is slow processing   !14MB-large-image.png|thumbnail!| * small size   * high pixel count   * is slow processing   !1MB-small-image.png|thumbnail!| * low pixel count   * size is larger than the 1MB slow-processing example   * is fast processing   !3MB-small-image-fast-processing.png|thumbnail!|"""
comments: "A fix for this issue is available to Server and Data Center customers in Confluence 7.7.2  [Upgrade|https://www.<USER>software/confluence/download?utm_source=confluence_bugfix&utm_campaign=confluence_bugfix&utm_medium=email] now or check out the [Release Notes|https://confluence.<USER>doc/confluence-7-7-release-notes-1004960930.html?utm_source=confluence_bugfix&utm_campaign=confluence_bugfix&utm_medium=email] to see what other issues are resolved." Requirement    My requirement is to be able to watch a page tree. That is:     * To receive notifications if that page or any descendant page is modified.  * To receive notifications if a new page is created under that page or any descendant page.    h2. Reason why its important    This is an important feature for me as I'm being swamped by emails when I watch the space that I mostly work with. The space has a few thousand pages. I'm interested in part of that space which contains a couple of thousand pages.     If I watch the space my email inbox is flooded with messages about changes to parts of the space I'm not interested in.    If I don't watch the space then I miss out on new pages being added and edits to existing pages. I could just about add a watch for each of the couple of thousand pages I'm interested in, but I'd still miss out on new pages being adde

summary: "Error rendering macro 'contributors-summary'"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    [Contributors Summary Macro|https://confluence.<USER>doc/contributors-summary-macro-183470224.html] fails to render after renaming a user, if the system still has old """"~favourites"""" style labels present.   When trying to load the macro on a page, you will get:   Error rendering macro 'contributors-summary'   No user could be found with the username _old_user_id_    And com.atlassian.confluence.labels.Label.<init> stack is logged in support logs.    h3. Steps to Reproduce    The system needs to have old favourites labels with """"~username:favourite"""" name, and content labelled by them.   # Create a page and add [Contributors Summary Macro|https://confluence.<USER>doc/contributors-summary-macro-183470224.html] macro to it.   # Take one of the existing users and rename their account (irrelevant of directory type)   # Try to load a page with Contributors Summary Macro in it    h3. Expected Results   * Macro Renders and loads as expected    h3. Actual Results   * Instead of a macro, the following error is displayed:   Error rendering macro 'contributors-summary    Below exception is thrown in the atlassian-confluence.log file:    h3. Workaround    h4. Workaround 1   You can try creating and then disabling a user account with an old username, this would allow for the macro to render    h4. Workaround 2   You can delete old favourites label from the content and delete the label itself   (!) *If you are unsure how to deal with the database, contact your DBA. Make sure to have the database backed up completely before going further. These SQL commands were tested in some environments and they worked as intended, however, it might not work in specific cases and newer versions of confluence as new constraints as changes may be done to the confluence database structure. As such, a database backup is mandatory in case something goes wrong and you need to revert.*   # Check if there are legacy favourites labels still present in the system   You should see one of the labels containing a username for the non-existent user     # Check if any content is still tagged with old favourites labels    If you see any output, it means there are pages still labelled as old user favourites, those pages would not appear under users favourites as those are linked to a label that maps to nobody and using old favourites system, on newer Confluence versions favourites label has no username in the name field and is simply called """"favourite"""" and maps to the user by the owner key value.   # Shutdown Confluence   # Take complete Confluence DB and File system backups and test on staging system first   # Run the following queries to delete old favourites labels from _label_content_ table:     # Then this query to delete old labels themselves:     # Proceed with [Rebuild the Content Indexes From Scratch on Confluence Server|https://confluence.<USER>confkb/how-to-rebuild-the-content-indexes-from-scratch-on-confluence-server-110035351.html]   # Test if the macro is rendered correctly"""

summary: "Conversion thread writes error logs with improper user and URL"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    """"Format cannot be converted"""" error log which is generated due to CONFSERVER-38055 operation doesn't represent right user and URL. The old log is logged repeatedly even after the other user accessed the other URL.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Install vanilla Confluence   # Create user *admin* and *testuser1*   # Log in to the Confluence with *admin* user   # Upload any Powerpoint file to the page   # Go to the property setting of the above attachment and change the MEDIA_TYPE to any wrong one. e.g. _application/<USER>   # Try to preview the above file from the page. You will get an error for example like this.  {code:yaml}  2020-05-14 02:22:58,119 ERROR [conversion-thread-0-internal] [conversion.impl.runnable.ConversionRunnable] doWork Format cannot be converted   -- referer: http://confluence.url/pages/resumedraft.action?draftId=65608&draftShareId=b3d96768-6ca1-4b8e-ad62-c385a0521cc9& | url: /plugins/drag-and-drop/upload.action | traceId: e2d9a05d6820f303 | userName: admin | action: upload    h3. Actual Results    Even though this is an operation by *testuser1*, userName is logged as *admin* and the whole logged content is same as the one in Step 6.  {code:yaml}  2020-05-14 02:30:29,155 ERROR [conversion-thread-0-internal] [conversion.impl.runnable.ConversionRunnable] doWork Format cannot be converted   -- referer: http://confluence.url/pages/resumedraft.action?draftId=65608&draftShareId=b3d96768-6ca1-4b8e-ad62-c385a0521cc9& | url: /plugins/drag-and-drop/upload.action | traceId: e2d9a05d6820f303 | userName: admin | action: upload  {code}  Apparently the first log that encountered this """"Format cannot be converted"""" is repeated when the error is replicated in the same instance.    h3. Workaround  Restarting the Confluence will reset the memory of the old """"Format cannot be converted"""" log."""

summary: "If a page title includes a ]], an extra space is added to the entries within an XML backup that reference this title "
description: """h3. Issue Summary    If a page title includes a ]], an extra space is added to the entries within an XML backup that reference this title.    Under the following circumstances, this can cause the ensuing import of this XML backup file to fail:   # If the total length of the title is 255 characters.   # Or, if certain plugins are used which create entries in the *OS_PROPERTYENTRY* table that include the title and hit the *string_val* field's 255 character limit.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  h5. Example 1:   # Create a page with a title of 255 characters including a *]]*:       # Create an XML backup.   # If you locate the title value within the entities.xml, notice that it is now 256 characters long and includes an extra space after *]]*:      h5. Example 2:   # Install the <USER>Page Approval plugin.   # Create a page with a number of characters in the title that when included with the base URL wrapped in an <a> tag, is around 225 characters. Also this title must include a *]]*. For example:       # Add an attachment to the page.   # For the page ID in question, check the _string_val_ field of the *OS_PROPERTYENTRY* table for an entry that looks like this. Notice that the entry includes the base URL wrapped in an <a> and this entry is 255 chars long:       # Create an XML backup.   # If you locate this value in the entities.xml, you'll see that it's now 256 characters long (with an extra space after the *]]*) which is long enough to cause a subsequent import of this file to fail.    h3. Expected Results    The XML backup process should should not add an extra space when it encounters double *]]* characters.  h3. Actual Results    Certain entries in the backup XML file can exceed 255 characters.    If you import this XML backup, it will fail with the following message in the application logs:    h3. Workaround    There are a couple of possible workarounds:   # Edit the entities XML file to remove the extra space. To make this easier, you could search for strings that match the pattern ]]   (eg. ]]spacespace) and replace with ]]  (eg. ]]space).  If the file is particularly large, you could try using the [sed command|https://www.cyberciti.biz/faq/how-to-use-sed-to-find-and-replace-text-in-files-in-<USER>unix-shell/].   # Locate the pages that have a *]]* in the title and either:   ## Remove one of the brackets.   ## Or, insert a space between the two brackets.   *** And if using the Page Approval macro, add another attachment to the page to refresh the entry in the *OS_PROPERTYENTRY* table.   ## Then create a new XML backup."""

summary: "Editing Excel file through Office Excel macro to show last row/column does not work"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  Attempting to show the last row/column from the Office Excel macro is not appearing as it should on consecutive changes, and it will remain as originally set.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Attach an Excel file on a page  # Insert the Excel file with an Office Excel macro and set columns to 3 and rows to 3.  # Edit the Office Excel macro and insert the last row is 5 and the column is also 5.  # Click """"Save""""     h3. Expected Results  The Excel document should only show the number of tables applied from the last row/column (5/5).    h3. Actual Results  The changes are not applied as they should and the Excel file remains the same without any changes (3/3).   [^ExcelTest.mp4]     h3. Workaround  As may be seen in the video, the workaround was to edit the file with the Companion App through {{Edit Excel}} feature and {{Upload}} it then only the changes are made for the {{last row/column}} set from the Office Macro.    (i) The bug still persists after:  * Disabling modules from Confluence previews related to Companion App  * Uninstalling Companion App  * Enabling Edit in Office dark mode  * Clearing plugins cache and in _Incognito Mode_  * Entering safe mode"""

summary: "Additional $ added to variable when reusing variables in templates"

Per [Create a Template > Template Variables|https://confluence.<USER>doc/create-a-template-296093779.html#CreateaTemplate-Templatevariables]:  {quote}You can add the same variable more than once in the same template, which is useful if you need the same information in more than one place on the page.  {quote}  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a new template.   # Instead of typing $, use the template menu to create a new variable:  !image-2020-05-13-16-16-28-947.png!   # Now, try to select the same variable from that menu in order to add a 2nd copy to the page.    h3. Expected Results    The variable is reused on the page.  h3. Actual Results    An additional $ is appended to the variable, creating a brand-new variable.    !6.15.8.png|width=348,height=221!  h3. Workaround    From the documentation on Templates:  {quote}Hint: Type $ and the variable name, then press Enter to add a new variable or to select an existing variable from a list of suggestions. The suggestions dialog shows variables already defined in this template.  {quote}  Confirmed reusing a variable in this way works as expected."""

summary: "Inspect permissions shows incorrect anonymous permissions"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    When users have had username clashes, the Inspect Permission feature interpret's """"Unknown users'"""" permissions as anonymous permissions    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Have a Confluence with an external user directory, and two users, usera and userb.  # In Confluence, grant userb individual permission to a space, or global """"Can use"""" permission to the instance.  Do not grant anoymous access to the space or globally.   # In the external directory, delete userb  # Sync confluence with the external directory  # Observe that the space/global permissions are still shown, but they are now shown as belonging to """"Unknown user""""  # Rename usera to userb  # Observe that the space/global permissions are still shown as belonging to """"Unknown user""""  # Restart Confluence  # Use the Inspect permissions screen to check the permissions in the space or globally.  Particularly check <anonymous> permissions    An alternative way to produce this bug is to have multiple external directories that provide different users with the same username.    h3. Expected Results  The original userb permission should have no effect on inspect permissions    h3. Actual Results  The original userb permission will show as granting anonymous access.  This will show both as the permission anonymous users have, and will also be shown as all groups having that access to the space via anonymous access.    h3. Workaround    You can use the following query to get a list of spaces and users that have these permissions:      Then visit the space permissions screen for any spaces listed, and, assuming the Unknown users are genuinely obsolete remove the space permissions of the Unknown users. Under most circumstances Unknown user permissions will be obsolete and have no further value. Possible exceptions are if the Confluence instance's user directories have been temporarily rearranged or altered and so the other users will be restored later.    And restart Confluence.  Or disable and enable the """"Inspect Permissions - Gatekeeper"""" plugin.    This bug is fixed in 7.4.1 and 7.5.0    """
comments: "A fix for this issue is available to Server and Data Center customers in Confluence 7.5.0  [Upgrade|https://www.<USER>software/confluence/download?utm_source=confluence_bugfix&utm_campaign=confluence_bugfix&utm_medium=email] now or check out the [Release Notes|https://confluence.<USER>display/DOC/Confluence+7.5+Release+Notes?utm_source=confluence_bugfix&utm_campaign=confluence_bugfix&utm_medium=email] to see what other issues are resolved." summary: "Using the Spaces List macro in a Confluence Page may overload the database"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  The [Spaces List Macro|https://confluence.<USER>doc/spaces-list-macro-139375.html] creates a panel the page with a list of Spaces available in the Confluence instance.  The user adding this macro can choose from the following options on which Spaces will be listed:  {quote}   * *(blank) -* All spaces in your site, with tabs.   * *all* – All spaces in your Confluence site.   * *category* – Spaces grouped according to space categories.   * *favorite* – Spaces which you have added to My Spaces.   * *new* – spaces created within the last 7 days.  {quote}    The options *all* and *category* (which is the default when the option is left on _blank_) will fetch *all* the Spaces from the Confluence instance and show that as an output within the page.    For Enterprise-sized instances, having thousands of Spaces is a common situation.  Depending on how the cache is, Hibernate will need to retrieve this heavy data from the database, since it won't paginate the request.  If the page containing the macro is requested by multiple users at the same time, then it may create an event on which the database server is overloaded, cascading the issue to any other operation that may require information from the database.      h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Install a vanilla instance of Confluence.   # Create a Page in a sample Space and add a Spaces List Macro with the default options.   # Create 4,000 Spaces.    #* You may use the following script to create them.     # Go to *Cog icon* > *General Configuration* > *Cache Management* and flush all caches.    #* This will force Confluence to go the database on subsequent requests.   # Run 10 simultaneous requests to display the page with the Spaces List Macro.    #* You may use a script similar to the below to run the 10 requests.      h3. Expected Results  All requests are completed in a timely manner without overloading Confluence nor the database.  If the number of Spaces are too high, the resulting list will be paginated, similar to the presentation seen in the [Space Directory|https://confluence.<USER>doc/the-space-directory-777010843.html].    h3. Actual Results  A single request takes a long time to complete and the simultaneous requests may overload the database if the information can't be retrieved from the cache.    The results below were taken from a test instance when requesting the target page once from the browser depending on how many Spaces are in the Confluence instance.  ||Number of Spaces||Processing Time||HAR image||  |100|0.5s| !har-img-001.png|thumbnail! |  |500|1.13s| !har-img-002.png|thumbnail! |  |1,000|2.33s| !har-img-003.png|thumbnail! |  |2,000|4.71s| !har-img-004.png|thumbnail! |  |4,000|12.74s| !har-img-005.png|thumbnail! |    Monitoring the server resources shows an increase on the CPU usage for the database server.    If thread dumps are taken during the period of high load, we can see threads with stack traces similar to the below:      Depending on how many requests were sent to the target page, the administrator may need to restart Confluence to drop the ongoing requests.      h3. Workaround  Currently there's no workaround to prevent the behavior described above when the Spaces List Macro is used in a page.  Confluence administrators may choose to completely disable this macro to prevent issues affecting the whole instance:  # Go to *Cog icon* > *Manage Apps*.  # Search for _Dashboard Macros_ under _System_ Apps.  # Expand the _Dashboard Macros'_ modules.  # Disable the *spaces (spaces)* module.  """

summary: "High CPU Usage (100% spikes) not released when inserting a docx file with charts to a page"
description: """h3. Problem  When importing a docx file with charts attached to a Confluence server, it will cause the CPU usage to spike to 100%. In order to get the threads released, we need to shutdown/kill the Confluence Java process.    h3. Environment  I have been able to reproduce in both 7.4.0 and 6.15.7.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  * Insert the docx file attached to a Confluence page in a local v7.4.0 instance and using the insert menu (please have a look at the video mp4 attached).    h3. Expected Results  The CPU usage should go down once the insert is completed.     h3. Actual Results  The CPU usage is not released and Confluence needs to be restarted.    Sample Stack Trace from a runaway RUNNABLE thread that has hit this condition:       h3. Workaround  * Few workarounds are working in 7.4.0 such as saving the file as either a DOC or as a PDF file instead of DOCX. Then the import will not cause CPU spikes.  Instead of importing the document from the insert menu. You can use the """"..."""" menu from the page to attach the .docx file there and it will not cause CPU spikes.  * Remove the charts from the file prior to upload.     h3. Notes    I have attached the recording where I show the workarounds  - import the DOCX document from the """"..."""" menu on the page, browsing the Attachments and attaching the document there (not inserting it inside the page)  - how the PDF and DOC version insertion is working fine whereas it is causing high CPU usages for the DOCX file."""

summary: "Allow other ways to use confluence.cluster.interface on Windows"
description: """h3. Summary  +As a+ system admin on Windows  +I want to+ be able to set network interface parameter {{confluence.cluster.interface}} on a fixed way  +So that I can+ not use the name generated by Java and use one that is fixed on Windows instead    h3. Problem Definition  Customers report that the network interface name (for example, _eth0_) changes by itself on Windows, sometimes, on reboot.  That is not a consistent behavior and cannot be reproduced.  In this case, it changed form _eth6_ to _eth7_ and caused Synchrony to fail due to Hazelcast not finding it:      More information:  https://stackoverflow.com/questions/58632235/get-name-of-a-specific-network-interface-in-java  http://hg.openjdk.java.net/jdk8/jdk8/jdk/file/82b276590b85/src/windows/native/java/net/NetworkInterface.c#l172  {quote}  * Windows does not have any standard for device names so we are forced  * to use our own convention which is based on the normal Unix naming  * convention (""""lo"""" for the loopback, eth0, eth1, .. for ethernet devices,  * tr0, tr1, .. for token ring, and so on). This convention gives us  * consistency across multiple Windows editions and also consistency with  * Solaris/Linux device names. Note that we always enumerate in index  * order and this ensures consistent device number across invocations.  */  {quote}     h3. Workaround     Use the instruction on this kb, whenever that behavior is observed:  [Hazelcast CANNOT start on this node. No matching network interface found.|https://confluence.<USER>confkb/hazelcast-cannot-start-on-this-node-no-matching-network-interface-found-646447553.html]"""

Issue Summary    When events are created in a calendar on personal spaces, the reminder mails are not sent to the other users added to the event, except the one that created the event.  The same does not occur for events created on public spaces.    h3. Environment  * Any Confluence version after 6.13  * Team Calendars installed, any version since 6.0.37    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Confluence instance with Team Calendars installed and at least 2 users (for this example *admin* and *u1u1*);   # Create personal space for each user and add a calendar to each one;   # Log-in as *admin* and create an event for the near future on his personal space's calendar   ## Add both users (*admin* and *u1u1*) to the event and set a reminder.   !adminCal1.png|thumbnail!    # Wait for the time the reminder should be sent;   # Log-in as *u1u1* and create a different event also for the near future and in his own calendar, similar to the one from Step #3;   !u1u1Cal1.png|thumbnail!    # Wait for the time the reminder for this new event should be sent.    h3. Expected Results    Both users receive a reminder mail at the time of both steps #4 and #6    h3. Actual Results    * At the time of step #4 only user *admin* receives a reminder mail;  * At the time of step #6 only user *u1u1* receives a reminder mail;    Note: both users receive a notification mail when added to the event created by the other.      h3. Workaround    Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

Steps to Reproduce  # Visit a space  # Click create and don't type anything to the draft  # Apply restrictions using the padlock  # Click close to save the draft  # Visit Space tools > Permissions > Restrictions    h3. Expected Results    Drafts are not listed on that menu.    h3. Actual Results    Drafts are listed as if they were published pages:     !Screen Shot 2020-04-28 at 11.04.04 AM.png|thumbnail!     If the draft has no title, it is listed as *#webwork.htmlEncode($content.displayTitle)* instead of """"Untitled"""".     h3. Notes    This causes confusion as users may think there are corrupted pages on the system, whereas instead, those are just drafts that can have empty titles. Another problem is that this allows an easy """"exploit"""" of the following bug, since clicking on the list item will load the draft as a page with */pages/viewpage.action?pageId=<DRAFTID>*    * https://jira.<USER>browse/CONFSERVER-57697    This can then lead to other issues since the user can like, comment, and do other actions that aren't supposed to happen with a draft. That creates inconsistencies on the DB side that can cause issues later while trying to manipulate this object.    h3. Workaround    Either remove the restrictions on such empty drafts or delete them using the following API call:    """

summary: "The internal synchrony-proxy is unable to establish WebSockets"
description: """h3. Summary    Synchrony proxy fails to establish WebSocket connections, the handshake process times out.    h3. Environment  * Fresh install of Confluence 6.13.7, Server or Data Center  * No proxy or load balancer, although it can be reproduced with those in place as well    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Edit setenv.sh and remove the XHR fallback property or set it to false  # Start Confluence and try to edit a page while watching the Network tab in the dev tools pane.    h3. Expected Results    WebSocket is opened and used in the editor session.    h3. Actual Results    WS request returns a 101 code as expected, but the handshake never finishes:     !Screen Shot 2020-04-27 at 4.55.05 PM.png|thumbnail!     This causes a loop where the browser keeps trying to establish the connection if XHR is disabled:        If XHR is enabled, it switches to this method, which causes WebSockets to never be used in this scenario. Based on the data, it seems that the handshake cannot be completed in time so the request times out.     h3. Notes    This is a big problem where the network environment is complex and can't be changed. An easy alternative to those scenarios would be to just allow WS connections through the internal proxy to improve the editing experience while minimizing the changes. With this bug, that is not possible.    One interesting item that showed up during testing is that sometimes it works after several attempts of the browser. That is quite inconsistent and it was not possible to determine a pattern or cause, but the main point is that it never works on the first attempt.    What is interesting is that we see successful messages while checking catalina.out, which also will loop until the WS is actually established:      Notice they are separated by 5 seconds, which is the current WS timeout.    h3. Workaround    Configure a reverse proxy or load balancer to connect directly to Synchrony, bypassing the internal proxy."""
comments: "This looks like the bug we had in the Tomcat HttpNioProtocol connector when using SSL that prevented the connection from being upgraded to secure websockets - see https://bz.apache.org/bugzilla/show_bug.cgi?id=62791. This was fixed in Tomcat 9.0.13, and 9.0.19 shipped in Confluence 6.13.5. 6.13.7 shipped with Tomcat 9.0.22, and had other synchrony startup issues on Windows with JDK 8u222. None of these would account for exactly the symptoms as described in this issue."

summary: "extra format of Page Restriction Lock Icon "
description: """We are using v7.2.1 (as at 2020-04-26)    (apology if this has been addressed in later versions already)    When there is no View Restriction, the lock icon is a an unlocked solid grey padlock    When it is Edit restricted it is STILL the same unlocked solid grey padlock    When it is View and Edit restricted it is a locked solid red padlock         I propose that this is changed to    # When there is no View or Edit Restriction, the lock icon is a an +unlocked black outlined padlock+ i.e. white filled.   # When it is ONLY View Restricted, no Edit Restricted, it is a locked solid grey padlock, i.e. grey filled   # When it is ONLY Edit restricted, i.e. everyone can view and a nominated editors,  it is a is a locked +red outlined padlock+ i.e. white filled.   # When it is View AND Edit Restricted, it is a is a locked +solid red padlock+ i.e. red filled.    That way there is a differentiation between the types of restrictions that have been applied.    The outlined padlock, locked or unlocked, always indicates no View Restrictions.    The solid filled padlock indicates Edit Restrictions     """

In the same macro, you can do things like filter by child pages.     The mandatory nature of the label* requirement seems spurious. summary: "Related Labels: Exclude Label(s)"
description: """Hello, I would like an """"exclude label(s)"""" property be added to related labels.    Here's why:    First, I have a situation where templates are marked with a label themselves. This means that all pages instantiated from the template share that label. We'll call them """"Cars"""" and """"Planes"""".    I need to do this, because I have pages that run page properties reports on each type, respectively. So far, so good.    However, the template itself has a related-labels macro in it, so that readers can see which cars and planes are associated with each other. This doesn't work so well, because, since all Cars share a Cars label, they are all implicitly related. That's not so good.    I might also have been able to make the current system work if Related Labels had a """"count"""" option. For example, if I could have said """"2,"""" then the template property would have been removed implicitly.               """
comments: "Update for clarity. I am using related labels with no labels, meaning all labels related to those on the page. The difficulty occurs when the template stamps in a common label, which does not mean that the page is really related to other pages that have that label (or, I don't want it to be, anyway)"

Try highlighting plain text.  {quote}  The following error is shown in the browser developer tools:      {panel:title=Technical observations}  I have observed that a working link looks as follows:    whereas a link causing this issue looks like the following two examples:    The _<ac:plain-text-link-body>_ attribute is only added when the link name and actual link reference is not the same.  {panel}    h3.
