summary: "Historical versions of attachments are considered as dependants to be re-indexed when their page is updated"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  Historical versions of attachments are considered as dependants to be re-indexed if their page is updated. This will lead to an issue when new contents cannot be indexed timely and the content index is backing up. The system is also hammered by the huge amount of unnecessary readings from the cache (to check if the versions are indexable) and I/O operations (for deleting the Lucene documents of historical versions of attachments).    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Move a page that contains thousands of versions of attachments.   # Monitor the journal main_index. It will be updated slowly to keep up with the last journal entry ID in the database.    h3. Expected Results  Only the latest version of attachments on the updated page will be re-indexed.  h3. Actual Results  All Lucene documents of historical versions of attachments are deleted. No new documents for them are added.    h3. Workaround  Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""
comments: "A fix for this issue is available to Server and Data Center customers in Confluence 7.5.0  [Upgrade|https://www.<USER>software/confluence/download?utm_source=confluence_bugfix&utm_campaign=confluence_bugfix&utm_medium=email] now or check out the [Release Notes|https://confluence.<USER>display/DOC/Confluence+7.5+Release+Notes?utm_source=confluence_bugfix&utm_campaign=confluence_bugfix&utm_medium=email] to see what other issues are resolved." [SEP] "If you're running the Confluence 6.13 Enterprise release, a fix for this issue is now available in Confluence 6.13.12, which you can find in the [Download Archives| https://www.<USER>software/confluence/download-archives]." [SEP] "Will there be a fix for the Confluence 7.4.x Enterprise release?" [SEP] "Hi [~<USER>    This fix will land in Confluence 7.4.1 which is currently scheduled for 9th of June.    Regards,    <USER> [SEP] "If you're running the Confluence 7.4 Enterprise release, a fix for this issue is now available in Confluence 7.4.1, which you can find in

summary: "Publishing a page with a large table might be slow if Collaborative Editing is enabled on Confluence"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  Updating a page with a large table might be slow on Confluence if Collaborative Editing is enabled.  This causes frustrated users since sometimes it may take dozens of seconds to save a visually simple page.      h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Install a vanilla instance of Confluence.   #* This was validated on Confluence Server 6.13.10 and 7.3.3.  # Make sure Collaborative Editing is enabled.  # Create a sample Space.  # Create a sample Page and publish it.  # Edit the recently created page, make any simple change to the content and publish it.   #* You will notice the page is saved pretty quick.   #* Monitoring the browser requests from the developer tools shows a quick response on the {{PUT}} request to {{<Confluence Base URL>/rest/api/content/<pageID>?status=draft}}.   !har-img-001.png|thumbnail!   # Edit the page again, create a table with 30 columns and 100 rows and publish the new version.   #* You will notice the page takes more time to be saved.   #* Monitoring the browser requests from the developer tools shows an increase on the response time related to the {{PUT}} request to {{<Confluence Base URL>/rest/api/content/<pageID>?status=draft}}.   !har-img-002.png|thumbnail!   # Edit the page again, add 30 more rows to the table and publish the page (you may use the attached sample storage format).   #* You will notice the page takes more time to be saved.   #* Monitoring the browser requests from the developer tools shows an increase on the response time related to the {{PUT}} request to {{<Confluence Base URL>/rest/api/content/<pageID>?status=draft}}.   !har-img-003.png|thumbnail!   # Create a new sample Page and publish it.  # Edit this page, add plain text content worth of ~700KB (you may use the attached sample storage format) and publish the page.   #* You will notice the page is saved pretty quick, although there's an increase it isn't as high as it was with the table, although the size of the transferred content is about the same.   #* Monitoring the browser requests from the developer tools shows a quick response on the {{PUT}} request to {{<Confluence Base URL>/rest/api/content/<pageID>?status=draft}}.   !har-img-006png.png|thumbnail!   # Disable Collaborative Editing.  # Go to the same page with the large table.  # Edit the page, adding a few lines of plain text and publish the page.   #* The page is saved pretty fast, taking around the same time as the plain text page.   #* Monitoring the browser requests from the developer tools shows a quick response on the {{PUT}} request to {{<Confluence Base URL>/rest/api/content/<pageID>?status=current}}.   !har-img-007png.png|thumbnail!       h3. Expected Results  Saving a page with a _complex_ table is quickly processed by Confluence whether Collaborative Editing is enabled or not.      h3. Actual Results  Saving a page with a _complex_ table takes more time than what happens with plain text pages about the size.  Disabling Collaborative Editing takes less time, showing there's additional complexity on processing the request when Synchrony is in the play.    If we take thread dumps while the {{PUT}} request is being processed, threads with stack traces similar to the below may appear.  *Confluence*:      *Synchrony*:      h3. Workaround  _Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available._"""

summary: "Synchrony not working with NTLM authentication"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    After upgrading DB to MSSQL 2017 edition and migrating from jTDS driver to use JDBC driver (8.2.0) leads to Synchrony service not running/working.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   * After migrating from jTDS driver to use JDBC driver (8.2.0), opt to use NTLM authentication (mixed mode authentication) by configuring the datasource connection parameter in the server.xml i.e., by configuring the URL below:    {code:java}  url=""""jdbc:sqlserver://url:port;databaseName=dbname;integratedSecurity=true;authenticationScheme=NTLM;domain=xyz""""  {code}   * Noticed that the Confluence is working fine, i.e., browsing the pages, viewing attaching, admin related tasks <USER>  * Page edits, creating new page or save/publish changes isn't working.   * In general, Synchrony isn't working, though the UI indicates the service is up and running.    h3. Expected Results    Confluence and Synchrony to work  h3. Actual Results    Synchrony or Page edits/creating new page/save or publish changes doesn't work.    The below exception is thrown in the atlassian-synchrony.log file:    h3. Workaround   * Disable CE/Synchrony   * Adding/Creating a local SQL account in DB server same as AD account which starts establishing connection with database.   * [Using-kerberos-integrated-authentication|https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/sql/connect/jdbc/using-kerberos-integrated-authentication-to-connect-to-sql-server?view=sql-server-ver15]    Please note that we will add if there are any new workarounds are discovered."""

summary: "Update Personal space key when Updating the username "
description: """Updating the username in LDAP or in Confluence does not reflect the changes in the UI or in the database when the Personal Space was created before the username changes.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Create one user(<USER>.  # Create the Personal Space.  #* The SpaceKey will be """"~<USER>""".  # Update the username to """"gvianabr"""".  # Go to the Personal Space of this user.    If you check the """"spaces"""" table, the spacekey will remain """"~<USER>""" and the only option to update it is through an UPDATE statement.     Confluence should have one option to update the SpaceKey if you are the space's owner.    h4.Notes  Suggestion raised from https://jira.<USER>browse/CONFSERVER-34386 since that ticket was raised 6 years ago.   """
comments: "{panel:title=Internal Notes|borderStyle=none|titleBGColor=#fff2a0| bgColor=#fff8c9}  I have raised this new ticket because the old one did not have any UIS since that time it wasn't implemented. This one we can keep track of recent cases and increase the UIS.  {panel}"

summary: "Support Active Directory authentication for SQL Server when running on Linux"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    In Confluence 6.5 and newer the JDBC driver was changed from the JTDS driver to the Microsoft SQL Server driver.     Unfortunately, it looks like the Microsoft JDBC driver does not support domain-based auth when running on Linux. It works on Windows:  * [How to use Windows integrated authentication for Microsoft SQL Server in Confluence 6.5 or newer|https://confluence.<USER>confkb/how-to-use-windows-integrated-authentication-for-microsoft-sql-server-in-confluence-6-5-or-newer-961785880.html]    This has led to customers with working JDBC configurations not being able to connect the same way after upgrading to Confluence 6.5+ in Linux    h3. Workaround    Required, if there is no workaround please state:  # Switch to password-based auth (not fun)  # Go back to the old JTDS driver (not tested and not supported by Atlassian)"""

summary: "RuntimeException: Cannot read resource assets/icons/confluence-icon-video.svg"
description: """h3. Summary    Uploading multimedia file results in 'java.lang.RuntimeException: Cannot read resource assets/icons/confluence-icon-video.svg stacks in atlassian-confluence.log.   h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a new or edit existing page   # Upload mp4 file    h3. Expected Results    No stacks in logs  h3. Actual Results    File is uploaded, however the below exception is thrown in the atlassian-confluence.log file:     """

summary: "TDM merger takes long time to complete with multiple inline tasks"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    When Collaborative editing is disabled, TDM Merger takes long time to complete when user goes to edit Confluence page that is include many inline tasks.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Disable Collaborative editing   # User A goes to edit a page that include many inline tasks (Version 1)   # Confluence auto-saves a draft based on Version 1, and user does not save the page   # User B edits the same page and saves, promoting the page to Version 2   # User A views his drafts (or edits the page again)   # Confluence tries to merge the three versions (Version 1, Version 1 draft, and Version 2), and results in Confluence slow performance    h3. Expected Results    Merge complete without any performance issues  h3. Actual Results    Single task is taking up-to 2 minutes to complete:    Thread dump shows a lot of threads consumed by TDM Merger:    h3. Workaround    Enable collaborative editing"""

summary: "MSI and EXE versions of Companion can be installed and run side by side"
description: """If you install both the MSI and EXE versions of Companion, you will have 2 versions installed and both can run simultaneously. You will also see both appear in Windows' add/remove programs screen.  h3. Expected behaviour    A potential solution would be to prevent installation of one version when the other is detected to already be installed.  h3. Workaround    Manually uninstall one of them."""

summary: "Events macro for Team Calendars 'Send email to attendees' needs a configurable mailto separator option"
description: """Using the Events macro for Team Calendars (disabled by default, enable in the UPM under Team Calendars) there is a 'Send email to attendees' (mail icon) link in the events table that  opens up the local email client and pre-populates the TO field in a new email message with all the attendee email addresses.    By default, the email addresses are separated by commas. This will work with all email clients that conform to the RFC 6068 specifications.    However, Microsoft Outlook defaults it's email client to only recognize semicolons to separate email address. This creates an issue where the user needs to manually change the commas <,> into semicolons <;> in the TO field so that the aforementioned email can be sent.    While users can change their 'list separator character' in the regional settings for their Windows machine to use commas instead, for some groups this isn't possible.    There should be an option in the Events macro settings for the 'Send email to attendees' <USER>link to either use semicolons or commas when populating the TO field."""

summary: "Archived pages are not accessible when you import a space exported from Cloud"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Confluence Cloud is introducing the ability to archive pages. This is currently in beta.  If you import a space, exported from cloud, that contains archived pages, the pages are not visible, because there is no UI to display the archive.     h3. Steps to Reproduce   # In Confluence Cloud, create a space, some pages, then archive some of these pages (... > archive)   # Export the space to XML  # Import the space into Confluence Server or Confluence Data Center    h3. Expected Results    The pages are available (in the page tree, at the root of the space, in the trash,... somewhere they can be accessed and restored)    h3. Actual Results    The pages are present in the XML export, but are not accessible through the Confluence UI.     h3. Workaround    As a workaround, restore any pages you want to keep from the Archive before you export the space. """

summary: "Issue Macro for the ability to display issue on the page Confluence for customers JSD"
description: """Before we can use Jira Issue macro, the Confluence and Jira application must be connected via Application Links. People viewing the page will see the publicly accessible issues from the Jira site. If your Jira site has restricted viewing (that is, people need permission to view issues) then they will need to authenticate before seeing the restricted issues.     When JSD customers are authenticating themselves, due to the permission issue, they cannot see the issue macro with the error """"Data cannot be retrieved due to an unexpected error"""".     h3. Suggestions    It would be great if issue macro functionalities can be opened to JSD customers without granting Jira application access """

summary: "Inserting a JIRA issue macro in a Chart Macro causes value not to be shown on the chart"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Inserting a JIRA issue macro inside a chart on a Confluence page causes the value to not appear on the chart when the page is saved. However, if the page is previewed, the values from the JIRA issue macro is showing correctly on the chart.  h3. Steps to Reproduce    Environment: JIRA v8.1.3 and Confluence v6.13.4   # Insert a Chart Macro on a page   # Insert a table inside the Chart Macro with two columns and few rows populate it with random values and leave one column empty   # Create a JIRA issue macro and insert it in the empty column   # Save the page    h3. Expected Results    Values from the JIRA issue macro should be shown on the chart.  h3. Actual Results    Values do not appear on the chart and only appearing when previewed.   [^JiraIssueTest.mp4]  h3. Workaround    Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Missing Edit button for Confluence admin if the admin does not have the view and edit space permission."
description: """h3. Issue Summary  By default, Confluence admin should have the permission to edit and view all pages in Confluence regardless if they have the space permission or not. However, If Confluence admin does not have the view/edit space permission, the edit button is missing for the Confluence admin.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Create a space and page  # Configure the space to make only {{confluence-users}} group have the edit and view space permission   !screenshot-2.png|thumbnail!   # Create a new admin user account and add the account to {{confluence-administrator}} group only   !screenshot-1.png|thumbnail!    !screenshot-4.png|thumbnail!   # Login as the newly created admin account  # Access any page in the space created in step 1    h3. Expected Results  The admin account should see the edit button and edit the page    h3. Actual Results  The edit button is missing for the admin   !screenshot-3.png|thumbnail!     h3. Workaround  The workaround is to grant the edit permission for the Confluence admin group."""
comments: "Being a member of the confluence-administrator enables the user to have View privileges. The documentation covers it:  _Members of this group can’t edit pages by default. They need to grant themselves space permissions, or add themselves to the page restrictions in order to edit._    https://confluence.<USER>display/DOC/Global+Permissions+Overview    This is not a bug and this is working properly. Closing the ticket."

summary: "Zooming out on a page causes panel macros with embedded links or attachments t to render inconsistent behavior."
description: """h3. Issue Summary  Zooming out on a page containing panel macros with embedded links or images render with a scroll bar but others don't . This is only reproducible on Chrome.   h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Open a Chrome browser.   # Create a page.  # Add multiple panel macros.  # Insert a link or image in all the macros and update the page.   # Zoom out.     h3. Expected Results  All panels should exhibit the same behavior. Either all display with a scroll bar or not.   h3. Actual Results  Some panels render with scroll bar and others do not.    !Screen Shot 2020-04-09 at 3.39.49 PM.png|thumbnail!   h3. Workaround  Use FireFox or IE.     Tested on Link and Windows   Chrome 80.0.3987.149"""

summary: "After linking Confluence with Jira, myWorkClientChecker TimeoutExceptions are thrown during Confluence startup"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  After linking Confluence with Jira, myWorkClientChecker TimeoutExceptions are thrown during Confluence startup    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Set up application link with Jira.   # Authorize access for the application.   # Restart Confluence    h3. Expected Results  Confluence starts up without any errors    h3. Actual Results  Confluence starts up and throws timeout errors related to Workbox.    The below exception is thrown in the atlassian-confluence.log file:      h3. Workaround   Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""
comments: "Additionally, if -- like in our instance -- a few apps are installed, the 5 second timeout may cause a whole bunch of Queue Overflows for the Event Queue ...    (Confluence 7.3.3)    We suspect, that this had a number of other impacts on our instance. Some caches of custom plugins of ours were out of date, for example, because the cache warmup, which the statup should have triggered, did not take place.    So this issue _might_ produce a whole bunch of other issues down the line ...    Edit: Found related issue, for the symptoms described: https://jira.<USER>browse/CONFSERVER-58567 (which just asserts that confluence is 'stressed' in this case)"

tag|https://www.w3docs.com/learn-html/html-p-tag.html]: {quote}The

tag defines a paragraph of text. It is a block-level element and always starts on a new line. {quote} So the empty paragraph tags should already be replaced with {{


}} when page is created from the REST API. Examples of empty paragraph tags: * {{

}} * {{

}} * {{

(any number of whitespaces)

}} * {{

}} * {{

}} * {{

}} h3. Steps to Reproduce # Create a page via REST API using the example: # Open the page in Confluence; # Edit the page but don't make any change; # Click on *Update*. h3. Expected Results The page should be displayed the same in Step 2 and after Step 4. h3. Actual Results The page is displayed with no empty lines in Step 2, as seen in !3-Lines-v1.png|thumbnail! The storage format for page's v1 is:   After Step 4 the page is displayed with empty lines in between, as seen in !3-Lines-v2.png|thumbnail! The storage format for current version is: h3. summary: "Inspect permissions shows incorrect results for mixed-case groups or users"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Any users or groups with uppercase characters in the username/groupname are not shown as having permissions in the inspect permissions feature. Note that such usernames and group names can only be created using external directories.    The error happens on initially loading the data at start-up, so any groups or users added during testing without a restart are ok until the cache reinitialisation.  h3. Steps to Reproduce    When we add a group (Group1) from an external directory and give it permission to view a space, it works fine. It shows up in the inspect permissions table with an upper case G, and respects permissions updates.   # When we do something that triggers a cache reinit (eg, re-ordering user directories), Group1 now shows up in the table as group1 with a lower case g and no longer respects permission updates.    # Try to give group1 permission to view a space and it doesn't show up in the table.    h3. Expected Results    Permissions can be updated as per usual.  h3. Actual Results    !screenshot-1.png|thumbnail!    Permission update is not reflected  h3. Workaround    None"""
comments: "A fix for  this issue is available to Server and Data Center customers in Confluence 7.3.5    [Upgrade|https://www.<USER>software/confluence/download?utm_source=confluence_bugfix&utm_campaign=confluence_bugfix&utm_medium=email] now or check out the [Release Notes|https://confluence.<USER>doc/confluence-7-3-release-notes-983794557.html?utm_source=confluence_bugfix&utm_campaign=confluence_bugfix&utm_medium=email] to see what other issues are resolved." [SEP] "A fix for this issue is available to Server and Data Center customers in Confluence 7.4.0  [Upgrade|https://www.<USER>software/confluence/download?utm_source=confluence_bugfix&utm_campaign=confluence_bugfix&utm_medium=email] now or check out the [Release Notes|https://confluence.<USER>doc/confluence-7-4-release-notes-994312218.html?utm_source=confluence_bugfix&utm_campaign=confluence_bugfix&utm_medium=email] to see what other issues are resolved.  "

summary: "Page Title overlaps with Page Path on Chrome"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Page Title overlaps with page path when clicking on spaces post opening a page in Confluence.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Open a page in Confluence   # Click on """"spaces""""   # Page Title overlaps with page path as depicted in attached screenshot    h3. Expected Results    Page Title should not overlap with Page Path  h3. Actual Results    Page Title overlaps with page path when clicking on spaces post opening a page.  h3. Workaround    Currently there is no known workaround for this behaviour. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Revise ecosystem recommendations about CacheManager vs CacheFactory"
description: """The [doco that tells app developers how to do caching in Confluence Server|https://developer.<USER>server/confluence/how-do-i-cache-data-in-a-plugin/] explicitly says to use {{CacheManager}} instead of {{CacheFactory}}, because of how the latter is implemented.    The problem is that {{CacheManager}} is annotated as being for internal use only (e.g. because it has a {{shutdown()}} method that should not be invoked by apps), whereas {{CacheFactory}} was originally intended to be used by app developers and is therefore annotated with {{@PublicApi}}.    We should resolve this contradiction to avoid (further) confusion to the ecosystem.    N.B. it's not simply a case of switching the annotations between these two types, because:  * there's existing code that will be made to appear like it's using the wrong API, and  * atlassian-cache is a cross-product library, and it's entirely possible that in the other products, there's no problem with using the {{CacheFactory}}.      """

summary: "Viewing Jira issues filter does not show Epic link and name"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Similar to the following closed issue: CONFSERVER-43561: Epic Link column in Confluence's """"Insert JIRA Issue/Filter"""" is not displayed properly (fixed in Confluence Server 7.0.4)    Jira issue filter does not show the Epic link and name if the page was directly accessed prior to being authenticated. This is specific to each individual Jira issue filter and different filters are not affected across separate pages.  h3. Steps to Reproduce    Pre-requisites:   * Confluence and Jira have an Application Link established and working.   ** Versions tested:   *** Confluence 7.1.2, 7.2.2, 7.3.3   *** Jira 7.13.4, 8.3.4   * In Jira, there is at least one existing issue which is linked to an Epic in the same project.    Steps:   # Create a new page in Confluence and write a title   # Insert """"JIRA Issue/Filter""""   # Write a filter to grab all the issues from project FLP   # Open the """"Display Options"""" and edit the columns to be shown:   ** Key   ** Summary   ** Epic Link   ** Epic Name   # Click """"Insert"""" and save the Confluence Page...   # Restart Confluence   # Access the page using the direct link(Example: http(s)://[hostname]:[port]/display/TEST/Jira+issues+2)   # Authenticate to the page to view the filter   # The Jira issue filter does not show the Epic link and name. It also is not hyperlinked.     h3. Expected Results   # The epic link should be a link in Confluence   # The epic link should also show the name of the Epic, to conform to what is seen in Jira   # The epic link should also be color-coded, as in Jira.    h3. Actual Results   # The Epic Link is *not a link* in Confluence. The field name implies that it should be a link to the Epic.   # The Epic Link in Jira shows the Epic name, while in Confluence it shows the Key of the corresponding issue   # The Epic Link in Confluence is not color-coded as it is in Jira.    The following image shows the differences:    !image-2020-04-07-21-56-15-809.png|thumbnail!  h3. Workaround    Restarting Confluence and accessing the page after authentication appears to correct the behavior. """
comments: "This is a rather annoying bug which on the face of it should be easy to resolve. One good aspects of Jira and Confluence is how they work well together and when you have silly bugs like this with no work around which also creates additional work for yourself when reporting it's frustrating. A few days ago it was working where the Epic Link was displaying the epic name, ok wrong but acceptable. Today it only shows the epic id without epic name and the Epic Name is still blank. :(" [SEP] "We are facing the same problem epic link is only shown the issue key of the epic as text but not in color and not as link and epic name is not shown at all in the Jira issue filter macro in confluence after we upgraded Jira DC instance to LTS version 8.5.8. Confluence is still on the LTS Version 6.13.10 (so no s´changes made here in the last month).    Please fix this, as our Confluence user's complains about lost of functionality, because now they have to manually switch to Jira and search there for the epic with the given

summary: "Companion App 1.0.0 doesn't work with Self Signed SSL Certificate"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Companion App 1.0.0 doesn't work with Self Signed SSL Certificate    When Confluence is served behind a Self-Signed SSL Certificate, editing an attachment will not work and there isn't any error message logged in the {{companion.log}} either.  h3. Tested Environment    Confluence 7.3.1   Confluence 7.4.0(EAP)   Companion App 1.0.0   Windows 10   MacOS  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Install a fresh installation of Confluence   # Setup Confluence to be serving on HTTPS using a *self-signed certificate* following [this documentation|https://confluence.<USER>doc/running-confluence-over-ssl-or-https-161203.html]   # Configure the browsing computer to trust the self-signed SSL certificate following the *Workaround* in [this KB|https://confluence.<USER>confkb/edit-attachment-feature-does-not-work-when-using-self-signed-certificate-964959893.html]   # Confirm the browsing computer has Companion App v1.0.0 installed and it is running   # Attach an attachment in Confluence Page   # Edit the attachment using Companion App    h3. Expected Results    Companion App should work as expected, allowing the user to edit the attachment locally  h3. Actual Results   # The browser pop-up requesting to launch Companion App is shown (/)   !Firefox Pop-up.png|thumbnail! !Chrome Pop-up.png|thumbnail!   # Companion would ask for trusting the domain if it is the first time accessing to the Confluence (/)   # Browser's connection to Companion App timed out and display the message dialog for Companion App download (x)   !Timeout.png|thumbnail!   # There isn't any error message logged in the {{companion.log}} (x)      h3. Workaround   * Use CA-signed SSL Certificate or serve Confluence under HTTP in order to continue using the Companion App.   * Enable the [Edit in Office|https://confluence.<USER>doc/edit-in-office-using-the-office-connector-170493096.html#EditinOfficeusingtheOfficeConnector-EnableEditinOffice] option to continue connecting via HTTPS as this option does not make usage of the Companion App."""

This occurs in Firefox (68.7.0esr) only but *not* Chrome (8{color:#5f6368}0.0.3987.163){color}. However, Firefox is mandated by our IT.    Please can you review the status of this issue as it effectively means that the Atlassian Companion App is no longer usable." [SEP] "My team is affected in a similar way as mentionned above. summary: "As an administrator I would like to disable all 3rd Party Apps on startup of a Confluence Data Center"
description: """h3. Problem Definition    Disabling 3rd party apps on startup is a great functionality to troubleshoot issues caused by installed Apps (aka Plugins, Addons).    This functionality is available in the Server deployment by using the {{--disable-all-addons}} attribute as described in [this KB|https://confluence.<USER>doc/start-and-stop-confluence-838416264.html].    If we try to use the same functionality in a Data Center instance it won't work and we get the following message in {{atlassian-confluence.log}}:    h3. Suggested Solution    Since Apps' state is mapped in the database, Confluence could check if the cluster is being initiated (one node only running) and accept the option to disable 3rd Party Apps.  h3. Workaround    As workaround, you may disable each App from the database following the instructions in [this KB|https://confluence.<USER>confkb/how-to-manually-remove-malfunctioning-add-ons-113705118.html]."""

summary: "delete space permissions and site restrictions from deactivated users"
description: """h3. Problem    Deactivated users are still shown under the Space permissions. If users are to be removed, they need to be deleted under user management. But this totally removes the user from the system.  h3. Suggested Solution    _It will be clearer to the end-user, if the deactivated users are removed from the following locations:_   # _Under the Space permissions area as they are already deactivated._   # _Under site restrictions, since no new site permissions will be shared with this user, until they are activated again._    _Once the users are activated, the user names should be populated again under in these two locations._  h3. Why This Is Important    _This will help if deactivated users are reactivated in the future, there is no need to create the users again. Instead, they can just be activated and they will show up under the space permissions area._  h3. Workaround    _NA_"""

summary: "When disabling Widget Connector and pasting a youtube link into a Confluence page, the link will immediately disappear. "
description: """h3. Issue Summary    When disabling Widget Connector and pasting a youtube link into a Confluence page, the link will immediately disappear. It seems the widget connector has to be enabled     Earlier versions of Confluence such as v 5.10.9 Individual modules could be disabled in the Confluence Paste to prevent auto converting to the Widget Connector using the steps from [How to Turn Off Autoconvert|https://confluence.<USER>confkb/how-to-turn-off-autoconvert-269981212.html]    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # disable widget connector    # Insert youtube link into a confluence page     To disable auto converter, (which resolves this in older versions) in a newer version of Confluence the following steps can be used:     to force disable the auto covert widget connector from Confluence Paste, spin up a version of Confluence (5.10.9) that allows for disabling the modules for Confluence Paste and getting the output from this query:        Take this output and put into a newer instance such as 7.2.1 instance with the following query:      Restart confluence and cleared the plugins cache and this forced disabled that module.     h3. Expected Results  Link should stay on the page    h3. Actual Results  Link immediately disappears      h3. Workaround  In the later version of Confluence (tested on Confluence 7.4.0), even disabling the {{*com.atlassian.confluence.plugins.confluence-paste*}} from the {{BANDANA}} database table will still have the pasted link disappear immediately.  However, the *Undo* (eg: CTRL+Z) function will be able to bring the disappeared link into the editor"""
comments: "This may seem like a low priority issue, but it's very confusing for our 400+ users if they paste a link, and then it disappears. It makes people lose confidence in the Confluence platform.          " [SEP] "In our internal Confluence, we have no access to external internet sites. This widget does not work in restricted environments, for that reason we must be able to disable this widget auto-converting links to widgets, and keep these links as normal links."

comments: "Few Suggestions from customer's users to be added with regards to format:   * A user profile flag (or parameter)   Some sort of visible user profile flag or parameter should indicate account deactivation (in addition to a name modification).   This could (should) e.g. be displayed inside the respective user profile.    This measure would add more dependability and clarity.   (amongst other things, by reducing the risk of users just being renamed (or renaming themselves) to ""<USER>inactive)"").    · Integration example:   Name: ...   E-Mail: ...   ...   Account activity: ""deactivated by admin"" / ""active"" / ...   .   .   * Wording when deactivated   I would suggest calling the adjective ""deactivated"" - instead of ""inactive"".   The reason is that this explains the situation more correctly. Users would not be tempted to assume that the (temporary?) deactivation was issued and can be revoked by the users themselves."

summary: "Jira Issues Macro cannot display custom field values from Toolkit Plugin for Jira"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    JIM is unable to render the values of custom fields provided by [Toolkit Plugin for Jira|https://marketplace.<USER>apps/5142/toolkit-plugin-for-jira?hosting=server&tab=overview].  h3. Environment    * Confluence 6.13.5  * Jira 8.1.0  * Toolkit Plugin for Jira 0.36.1    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Install Toolkit Plugin for Jira  # Add the custom fields provided by it to a project, tested *Days since last comment* and *Last public comment date*  # Add those fields in the *Collums to display* section of JIM in Confluence    h3. Expected Results    Fields values are rendered as in Jira:     !jira.png|thumbnail!     h3. Actual Results    Fields can be added but they don't display any value:     !confluence-jiramacro.png|thumbnail!      h3. Notes    Those fields render as expected in the Filter Results gadget:     !confluence-filter-results.png|thumbnail!     Although the plugin that provides this functionality is unsupported, we can see that this seems to be a limitation of JIM while rendering those values. Checking the XML sent from Jira while searching for the project, we can confirm the fields and their values are included.    Another improvement would be the option of disabling the highlighting of issues in Confluence on the UI. That can be achieved by editing the plugin JAR file and finding the corresponding template you want to edit at the moment.    h3. Workaround    Use the Filter Results gadget instead of JIM if you would like to display those fields."""

summary: "Missing Restriction icon on the page for a user who does not have edit permission on space"
description: """h3. Problem summary    Missing Restriction Icon on the page for a user who does not have edit permission on space. The user will never know why the Edit option is missing for him and think its an issue with his instance.  h3. Scenario    Consider a scenario where space has restricted permission. The group added to space doesn't have Edit permission. Now when the users who are part of that user try to access the page, they will not see the edit option on the page. There is no way they would know that their edit access is restricted by admin at the space permission level.    Ideally, the pages should show page restriction icon for the page to avoid confusion.    For instance, for Space, the *confluence-users* group have doest have edit permission for space:    Hence the users of this group are not able to view the edit option on the page.    But the page doesn't show any restriction fo the user. Hence users don't know if the page is restricted for them to edit  h3. Suggestion    Please add a restriction icon to such pages for where the user permission is restricted at space level."""

summary: "Header titles accessed from a Table of Content Macro are not fully visible or cropped"
description: """h3. Problem    When accessing a header/anchor on a page from a Table of Contents Macro, the header title will be either cropped by the Confluence top bar menu or hidden by the Confluence white floating banner menu.  h3. Environment    We can reproduce the issue locally on Confluence Server latest version v7.3.4 and also tried on v7.2.0 and Confluence Data Center v6.15.7.  h3. Steps to Reproduce    Create a new page that contains:   - a Table of contents at the top of the page   - some headers h4: the number of sections has to be big enough so that the pages cannot be displayed in a single instance in the web browser. You have to scroll up or down to access    - a Table of contents at the bottom of the page   - publish/update the page   - Scenario1: access some of the headers section/anchors via the top ToC Macro. The header title will be cropped/not visible due to the blue/top menu banner.   - Scenario2: access some of the headers section/anchors from the bottom ToC Macro. The header title will be hidden by the floating/white banner.    To illustrate the 2 scenarios you will find attached, a video where I show how I reproduce the issue on my *Chrome Version 80.0.3987.149 (Official Build) (64-bit)* with a local Confluence v7.3.4 Server instance. You will also find attached an example of page content in storage format (to be imported via source editor plugin) to speed-up the testing.  h3. Expected Results    When accessing a section from a Table of Contents Macro, the title of the section that is accessed should always be displayed/visible at the top of the page. We should offset the blue/top menu banner and the floating/white banner in Confluence in all cases.  h3. Actual Results    Please find the recording attached.      h3. Workaround    No workaround available at this stage."""

summary: "The Plan your Upgrade page fails to load if Synchrony is misconfigured"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    The """"Plan your Upgrade"""" page will fail if Synchrony's node isn't properly configured. Even though the Collab. Editing my be OFF, the page will still fail to load.    (!) This error has been happening for instances that have been migrated from the standalone version to DC. We have reproduced the issue with that environment.    h3. Steps to Reproduce    # Create a Confluence instance 6.9.0  # Upgrade UPM and ATST  # Turn off collaborative editing  # Install Confluence DC license  # Restart Confluence and configure DC instance, don't configure anything on synchrony side  # Visit Plan your upgrade configuration page.  # Issue is visible """"Looks like we're having trouble retrieving some information. Please refresh the page""""  # Once Synchrony is configured correctly and Confluence is restarted the error is gone.      h3. Expected Results    The page will load when Synchrony is misconfigured but the Collab. Editing is turned off      h3. Actual Results    Although the Collab. Editing is turned off, the page checks Synchrony's configuration. As the Synchrony JVM wasn't setup after moving to DC, the below error will be logged:        h3. Workaround    There are two workarounds:    * Setup Synchrony's JVM(DC) when facing the issue.  * Upgrade to Confluence 6.12(or above) and let Confluence manage Synchrony's. """

summary: "Update 7.3 release notes "
description: """Uses requested to update the release note for version 7.3 to include the note on the recent change in the 7.3 documentation related to mail resources.    While reviewing following URLS:   * [Setting Up a Mail Session for the Confluence Distribution in version 7.3|https://confluence.<USER>doc/setting-up-a-mail-session-for-the-confluence-distribution-6328.html]   * [Setting Up a Mail Session for the Confluence Distribution in version 7.2|https://confluence.<USER>conf72/setting-up-a-mail-session-for-the-confluence-distribution-982323710.html]         While configuring mail session for the Confluence 7.3, we need to move to two extra jar to the different location as describe above KB.    Name of the jars are following:   {quote} # javax.activation-x.x.x.jar   # javax.activation-api-1.2.0.jar{quote}       However this change information is not added in release notes of 7.3, so for users it is obvious to move other two jars.    If release notes contain, such change information it would be helpful for users.     """
comments: "This information has now been added to the 7.0 upgrade notes, as the change was introduced in 7.0  https://confluence.<USER>display/DOC/Confluence+7.0+Upgrade+Notes#Confluence7.0UpgradeNotes-ChangetohowyouconfigureyourmailserverintheConfluencedistribution    The documentation for Confluence 7.0 to 7.2 has also been updated.  Sorry, the change had been made from 7.3 onwards but actually applies from 7.0. " [SEP] "This was already covered in the 7.0 upgrade notes, but as it is clearly causing problems, I've added a reminder to the 7.1 - 7.4 upgrade notes as well.     I've also added a known issues to the versions affected by https://jira.<USER>browse/CONFSERVER-59040. "

summary: "Provide an ability to upgrade third party libraries like Jquery seperately from Confluence "
description: """*Scenario*   There are many third-party libraries used in Confluence which might be affected by some security vulnerabilities. In many of these cases, they are resolved in the later version of these libraries.   Generally, we would have to wait for the next Confluence upgrade that will contain these updated libraries to mitigate these security risks.    *Suggestion*   Confluence should provide the ability to upgrade third party libraries like Jquery separately i.e without the need to upgrade the Confluence itself. This would help a lot in mitigating security risks and also prevent delays in waiting for the next Confluence version containing the updated libraries."""

summary: "Importing space from Cloud fails due to duplicated lower_username"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Importing a space backup of Cloud instance fails violating unique constraint """"unq_lwr_username""""  {code:none}  com.atlassian.confluence.importexport.ImportExportException: Unable to complete import because the data does not match the constraints in the Confluence schema. Cause: PSQLException: ERROR: duplicate key value violates unique constraint """"unq_lwr_username"""" Detail: Key (lower_username)=(<EMAIL>) already exists.  {code}    This is replicable when   * The format of username in the backup is email address. e.g. <EMAIL>  * The instance already has both of following two users which has the same email address  (<EMAIL>)  in  ** {{lower_user_name}} column and   ** {{lower_email_address}} column    This issue also occurs when the target server/datacenter instance has different users with the same email address.    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a space export in Cloud instance (supposing it has a space creator {{<EMAIL>}} )   # Create a vanilla Confluence Server instance    # Add following users in Server instance  ** which has username  {{<EMAIL>}} and email address {{<EMAIL>}}   **  which has username {{foo}} and email address  {{<EMAIL>}}    # Import the Cloud export in step 1 into the Server instance     h3. Expected Results  Space is imported successfully.   Also space creator are expressed as unknown user following [Import a space from Confluence Cloud|https://confluence.<USER>doc/import-a-space-from-confluence-cloud-967876449.html]     h3. Actual Results    Space import fails generating """"unq_lwr_username"""" violation error on the screen.  The below exception is thrown in the atlassian-confluence.log file:      h3. Workaround    Resolve the duplication in the Server instance beforehand.   In the above case, remove or rename the user which has username {{<EMAIL>}} """
comments: "I don't understand why has only minor severity. This affects the restore of a backup, which resulted in a total loss of data for our wiki. During the restore process the process *first* removes the existing data, before it tries to restore. When this fails (due to this bug) the data is lost.    In my opinion this is a very severe issue!    Fortunately we already learned not to rely alone on the internal Confluence backup, so we could restore externally most of our content." [SEP] "As Atlassian said for Cloud to Server/Datacenter migration: Always take the latest version this fails here because the latest version got this bug!    As for now: Our customer is not able to migrate from Cloud to Datacenter...."

"""

summary: "When removing inner nested user macro, some html tags are left behind"
description: """h3. Problem    Deleting user macro from a page, leaves behind some HTML tags    Deleting a nested inner macro causes the first macro to display $body because of the html tags left behind.  h3. Environment   * Confluence Server 7.2.1   * Chrome Version 80.0.3987.149 (Official Build) (64-bit)   * Server: Ubuntu 18.04.4 LTS    Steps to Reproduce   # Under the guide [Writing User Macros|https://confluence.<USER>doc/writing-user-macros-4485.html], go to the section Example user macros"""" and copy the """"Color and Size""""   # Create a user macro into your confluence as the example.   # Create a blank page   # Edit the new page and add the """"Color and Size"""" macro   # Publish the page   # Edit the page again and create a new """"Color and Size"""" macro inside the first macro (nested).   # Publish the page   # Edit the page again, delete the inner macro.   # Remove the blank spaces from the first macro content   # Publish the page   # You will see that the macro will display $body insted of just leave it empty.    h3. Expected Results    The page should display nothing.  h3. Actual Results    The page displays """"$body"""".  h3. Notes   * Every time when adding the user macro into the page, we can see that Confluence add the following before the actual macro into the page source code:    * When we delete this macro when editing a page, Confluence deletes the actual macro HTML tags, but keeps that first part    h3. Workaround  * Edit the page source code (using the source code editor plugin for example), and remove that part that is left behind when deleting the macro:  """

summary: "When coping indented notes from word, outlook, or any text editor the formatting is not being carried over into Confluence."
description: """When coping indented notes from word, outlook, or any text editor the formatting is not being carried over into Confluence.      h3. Issue Summary    When users are taking notes locally in a notepad, word, outlook for example When a bullet """"unindents"""" more than one level, Confluence misses that. Not only does this put that bullet at the wrong level, it also means all later bullets are shifted by one level. Add this up over a long series of meeting notes, and the end result looks very little like the original. I have attached a picture showing the issue. Note the indent level of the last line """"Back to First level"""". In Word, it really is back to the far left, however in Confluence it is actually at the Second level.    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a sample word doc, or text with indents   # Create table in any supported browser and paste the content into confluence     h3. Expected Results  Indents should be properly formatted    h3. Actual Results  Indents are shifted over one level      h3. Workaround    NO workaround available seems to happen consistently across all supported browsers.    """
comments: "Note that text is DELETED if you outdent. That seems higher priority than low, IMO."

To reproduce:    * Create around of 4 to 10 resolved comments.    h3. Suggested Solution    Make the scroll bar always visible.    h3. Workaround   There's no workaround for the scroll bar, only the designed behavior."""

description:
"""h3. Issue Summary Since Confluence 7.3.1, potentially from 7+ there has been a change to the HTML markup of the page tree expand/collapse action where a HREF=undefined instead of the context path or #. h3. Steps to Reproduce # Create a paraent page and some children # View the source code of the page # Search for the word undefined. h3. Expected Results This is an example from 6.15.7: {code:html}
A
* *href=""""undefined?src=contextnavpagetreemode""""* undefined is the problem h4. Notes This can cause alerts with monitoring tools such as SiteImprove. From what I can tell, first *href* reference should be blank but we appear to be duplicating the next href, but without the context path. h3. Workaround No known workaround. summary: "Full Space PDF Export causing OutOfMemoryError"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  Generating a PDF export of a large space can cause an OutOfMemoryError and causing the JVM to crash.    h3. Environment   * Confluence Server    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Navigate to a large space with lots of pages and attachments.   # Generate a full Space PDF export.    h3. Expected Results  The PDF export process completes without issues after some time.    h3. Actual Results  The process starts consuming JVM memory over time, causing initial slowness then ultimately crashing the JVM due to OutOfMemoryError.    h3. Notes  This behavior can be triggered by any user with the ability to generate space PDF exports and can cause outages in Production environments. Server downtime.    h3. Workaround  Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available.  A few things can be done to prevent the OOME:  # Increase JVM heap size to allow the PDF export process to complete.  # Break down a large Spaces into smaller Spaces.  # Disable PDF exports altogether: [How to disable PDF Export for Pages or Spaces|https://confluence.<USER>confkb/how-to-disable-pdf-export-for-pages-or-spaces-711361415.html]."""
comments: "So do you think better way is kill exporting process?"

summary: "Table Cell focus/cursor is not working if the column contains Date Macro in Firefox"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  Table Cell focus is not working if the column contains Date Macro  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a *Table* in a Confluence Page.   # Insert a *Date Macro* (using the default Date Picker) in one of the column.   # Click on the column to point the *focus/cursor* in the column above the Date Macro.    h3. Expected Results  Focus/cursor pointed in the right column.  h3. Actual Results  Focus/cursor still inside the column that contain the Date Macro.    h3. Workaround  Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior in *Firefox* Browser. User may use a different browsers (Chrome, Internet Explorer) to prevent the issue."""

summary: "As a Confluence admin I want entries from Space Import to be added to the Index Journal Entry instead of triggering a search index rebuild"
description: """h3. Problem Definition    [Restoring A Space|https://confluence.<USER>doc/restoring-a-space-152036.html] with """"Build Index"""" checked will trigger a full site reindex (in the case of a Data Center, on all nodes), which can cause heavy load to the database and filesystem.    h3. Suggested Solution  A space import should trigger an incremental index - newly added content inserted on _journalentries_ table and processed in the normal index queue flush tasks."""

summary: "Use standards for differentiating bullet list indentation levels"
description: """I am asking you to revisit CONFSERVER-12263.     It is standard that different levels of indentation of a bullet list (unordered list) have different symbols.    Confluence uses the same, solid bullet for all levels of list indentation. This makes no sense, and it removes an important visual cue.    Please have Confluence use standards by default, and in this case, that means allowing user agents to naturally vary the bullet style per different levels of list hierarchy."""
comments: "[~<EMAIL>] thanks for your persistence with this issue. I'll put this ticket in review to understand more about this one.    Thanks  Makisa | Senior Product Manager, Confluence Server and Data Center"

summary: "Image Effects do not render on .BMP or animated .GIF files"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Image effects do not render on .BMP or animated .GIF files.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Insert a .BMP or .GIF file on a new page. (Example attached.)   # Click on the image and click Properties.   # Click on an image effect.    h3. Expected Results    The image effect is applied to the image.  h3. Actual Results    There are no image effects applied.  h3. Workaround    Convert the image to a .png or .jpg."""

summary: "Prevent the deletion of space names when configuring sidebar"
description: """When users create a new space, the space name is a required field. Similarly, on the Space Details page, it's not possible to delete the space name.    However, under Space Tools > Configure sidebar, it's possible to delete the space name. This can lead to unexpected behavior (e.g. third-party apps like the Attachment Checker break) and can make it difficult to determine what a certain space is in the space directory.  h3. Steps to reproduce   * Create a space   * Navigate to Space Tools > Configure sidebar   * Edit space details   * Delete the space name    h3. Expected results    This should not be allowed  h3. Actual results    Space name is deleted and replaced with an empty string."""
comments: "I think the fix should be quite simple to enforce a check that the space name should not be empty string.    The change will make the behaviour consistent with other entry points of updating the space name.    Thank you."

summary: "Standardize attachment temp file creation directory"
description: """h3. Summary  To have a standard location for temp files to be created when an attachment is uploaded    h3. Environment  Confluence 7.3.2    h3. Behavior  When a file uploaded via browser upload, the temp file is created in {{<confluence home directory>/temp}}    When a file uploaded via drag and drop, the temp file is created in {{<confluence home directory>/attachment/mod(3 least significant digit in spaceId)/mod(next 3 least significant digit in spaceId)/spaceId/mod(3 least significant digit in pageId)/mod(next 3 least significant digit in pageId)/pageId/contentId/}}    Hence to have all temp files to be created in {{<confluence home directory>/temp}}. This is to standardize the process"""
comments: "This is important for enabling for example virus scanning of attachments when using confluence Data-center. "

summary: "Editing a date of a weekly recurring event, shifts the event a day before/after to the edited date of the recurring event"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  Editing one of the dates from recurring event resulting in:  * The event before/after the edited event shift to the edited event  * The recurring event does not change the name    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # A weekly recurring event is created.  # The weekly recurring event is created successfully and shown on the calendar as it should.  # Edit one of the event occurrences (either by editing the name or adding the """"Who"""").  # Click OK to save the changes made.   [^TeamCalendarsTesting.mp4]     h3. Expected Results  * Changes should be applied to all the occurrences of the recurring event.    h3. Actual Results  * Note that the event that is supposed to be a day before/after is shifted to the date of the edited event.  * It may be observed that the events before/after do not respect the changes applied.    h3. Workaround   Currently, there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Attempting to add Gadget Feed XML to a Jira site that has a forward proxy causes 407: Authentication header required"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    If you have Confluence and Jira on separate appservers and there is a forward proxy *requiring authentication* between the two managing application connection, attempting to add a Gadget Feed XML will cause a generic error and fail to connect. In HTTP logging, this will show HTTP code 407: Proxy authentication required when attempting to make this connection.    h3. Steps to Reproduce    # Connect Confluence and Jira via application link  # Turn on forward proxy (in an example case Squid was used but Apache can also be leveraged or another proxy)  # Attempt to add a gadget feed XML in <USER>   h3. Expected Results    - You are able to add a Gadget Feed XML or an individual Jira gadget in Confluence    h3. Actual Results    - You get an error instead on the Gadget Feed page in Confluence:     !image-2020-03-19-15-11-22-774.png|thumbnail!     h3. Workaround    Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior with an outbound proxy enabled. A workaround will be added here when available    You could disable the proxy authentication temporarily, add the Gadget Feed, and then re-enable the proxy. Once the Gadget Feed has been processed, things work normally."""

summary: "When inserting the live drop down macro to a table on the last few columns of wide tables get overlapped."

Workaround    This issue does not affect the latest version of firefox 74.0"""

It will be great to have the ability to use the current DateTime as the title."""

summary: "Image paste undo in IE11 requires two Cmd+Z/Ctrl+Z"
description: """Steps to reproduce:  * Create new page in Internet Explorer 11  * Paste an image into the editor  * Watch the upload dialog saving the image as attachment  * Press Cmd+Z (MacOS) / Ctrl+Z (Windows or Linux)    Expected result:  * Image is removed    Actually happens:  * Image is still visible, sometimes it gets resized (larger)  * There's a highlighted square in the top left corner of the image and another Cmd+Z/Ctrl+Z is needed to remove it    Notes:  * If saved, the image is not stored on the page  * This may be related to similar bug in other browsers but with different symptom: CONFSERVER-59623  * Collaborative editing does not seem to have any effect on this bug"""

summary: "%MCEPASTEBIN% shown after undo-ing image past in Chrome/Firefox/Edge/Safari"
description: """Steps to reproduce:  * Create new page  * Paste an image into the editor  * Watch the upload dialog saving the image as attachment  * Press Cmd+Z (MacOS) / Ctrl+Z (Windows or Linux)    Expected result:  * Image is removed    Actually happens:  * A block of text with %MCEPASTEBIN% appears in the document and another Cmd+Z/Ctrl+Z is needed to remove it    Notes:  * If saved, this block of text is not stored in the page  * Affects Chrome 79, Firefox 72, Edge 80 (Chromium), Edge 18. In Chrome, the block of text is very narrow and appears to be written vertically. Firefox shows it as regular text.  * Only reproducible when collaborative editing is enabled. Issue Summary  Opening file type that has no app associated with it in Companion App throws error repeatedly    When opening a file type that has no app from local computer associated with, the Companion App throws a meaningful error message but it was thrown 5 times where the user is forced to click the *OK* button repeatedly to dismiss the said error message.    h3. Tested Environment  Confluence 7.3.1  Confluence 7.3.3  Companion App 1.0.0    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Upload an attachment to Confluence 7.3.x where the file type has no app associated with from the local computer  (i) Example: A file that has no filename extension  # Edit the attachment    h3. Expected Results  Companion App should show an error once informing the user of the issue    h3. Actual Results  Although a meaningful error message is shown, but the error box needs to be manually dismissed *5 times*   [^File Without Extension.mp4]     Error Message shown:      h3. summary: "Saving PowerPoint and Excel file using Edit in Office does not provide a smooth experience"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  Saving PowerPoint and Excel file using Edit in Office does not provide a smooth experience    When [Edit in Office|https://confluence.<USER>confkb/enable-edit-in-office-as-a-dark-feature-in-confluence-978217013.html] is enabled in Confluence either via Dark Features(Pre-Confluence7.3) or Admin UI (Confluence 7.3 or above), the PowerPoint and Excel file can be edited locally and have the changes uploaded back to Confluence.  However, the saving experience is not great and can be confusing for user.    h3. Tested Environment  Confluence 7.3.1 / 7.3.3  Microsoft Office 2016    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Upload a PowerPoint or Excel file attachment onto a Confluence page  # Enable *Edit in Office* option according to [this documentation|https://confluence.<USER>confkb/enable-edit-in-office-as-a-dark-feature-in-confluence-978217013.html]  # Edit the PowerPoint and/or Excel file attached on the page    h3. Expected Results  When the user try to save (CTRL+S) their changes, it should be uploaded into Confluence seamlessly    h3. Actual Results  Error messages appear when attempt to save changes and the user has to first *discard changes* and attempt to save it again in order for the save and upload to be successful   [^Excel - NOT OK.mp4]    [^PowerPoint - NOT OK.mp4]     h3. Workaround  When the attachment is first downloaded and opened in the Excel/PowerPoint locally, first perform a save to trigger the error and choose to _Discard Changes_ then start editing as the subsequence savings will then work correctly.    h3. Note  Word document has no such issue and can be saved correctly on the first go.   [^Word - OK.mp4] """

summary: "java.io.tmpdir has no effect on changing the installation temp directory"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  java.io.tmpdir has no effect on changing the installation temp directory    In the [this documentation|https://confluence.<USER>doc/confluence-home-and-other-important-directories-590259707.html#ConfluenceHomeandotherimportantdirectories-Tempdirectory(installationdirectory)] about changing the installation temp directory. It is advised to use the Java parameter below:    In the {{catalina.sh}} and {{catalina.bat}} file, the comment below suggests the same as well:  {code:title=catalina.sh}  #   CATALINA_TMPDIR (Optional) Directory path location of temporary directory  #                   the JVM should use (java.io.tmpdir).  Defaults to  #                   $CATALINA_BASE/temp.    However, having this Java parameter set has no effect on changing the installation temp directory    h3. Tested Environment  Confluence 6.15.1  Confluence 7.3.3    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Stop a vanilla Confluence and [add the system property|https://confluence.<USER>doc/configuring-system-properties-168002854.html] below:    # Restart Confluence    h3. Expected Results  The Installation Temp directory will be changed according to the Java parameter included    h3. Actual Results  The Installation Temp directory is not changed despite the Java parameter can be seen to parsed successfully into Confluence:   !installation temp not respected.png|thumbnail!   Meanwhile, the original installation temp directory will still be used and a second set of {{java.io.tmpdir}} can be seen    h3. Workaround  Add this line into {{setenv.sh}} according to [the Jira KB|https://confluence.<USER>jirakb/how-to-change-the-temporary-directory-648545076.html]:  {code:title=setenv.sh}  CATALINA_TMPDIR='/path/to/new/installation/temp'  {code}    h3. Note  The newly changed installation temp directory from the workaround step may not be created by Confluence if it doesn't already exist due to CONFSERVER-59610"""

summary: "Confluence not able to re-create Installation Temp directory in Linux"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  In the case if the Installation Temp directory has not already existed, Confluence failed to re-create the directory during the startup. This might happen if a customer choose to [change the Installation Temp directory|https://confluence.<USER>doc/confluence-home-and-other-important-directories-590259707.html#ConfluenceHomeandotherimportantdirectories-Tempdirectory(installationdirectory)] to a path that is not already existed.    h3. Tested Environment  * [Confluence 7.3.3|https://www.<USER>software/confluence/downloads/binary/atlassian-confluence-7.3.3-x64.bin] (x)  * [Confluence 7.2.0|https://www.<USER>software/confluence/downloads/binary/atlassian-confluence-7.2.0-x64.bin] (x)  * [Confluence 7.0.1|https://www.<USER>software/confluence/downloads/binary/atlassian-confluence-7.0.1-x64.bin] (/)    (i) The issue seems to have started from Confluence 7.2    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Download [Confluence 7.3.3 Linux Installer|https://www.<USER>software/confluence/downloads/binary/atlassian-confluence-7.3.3-x64.bin] and install it  # Go through the setup wizard to have a bare minimum of Confluence up and running  # Stop Confluence  # Remove the {{*<Installation>/temp*}} directory  # Start Confluence    h3. Expected Results  Confluence will re-create the {{*<Installation>/temp*}} directory and startup as usual    h3. Actual Results  Confluence did not re-create the {{*<Installation>/temp*}}, causing the plugins that require to be loaded in this directory to fail on starting. For example Synchrony and the user-installed plugins in the database.    h3. Workaround  Manually create the Installation Temp directory before starting Confluence and ensure that the user running Confluence has full read/write permission to it.    h3. Side Note  The same issue is not observed in the Windows environment"""

summary: "Removing and re-adding a user directory with global permissions on an external group will lead to incorrect user's permissions on the ""Inspect Permissions"" page"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  When we remove and re-add a user directory global permissions are not updated in case you have """"Can Use"""" permission on a group from an External Directory    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Create a group """"test-group"""" in the external AD  # Add a user """"test-user"""" into this group  # Add and synchronise the directory  # Give """"test-group"""" global """"Can Use"""" permissions in Confluence  # Give """"test-group"""" any space level permission  # Check in """"Inspect Permissions"""" that the user have permissions to use this space  # Completely remove external directory from """"User Directories""""  # Then add it again  # Synchronise, but do not change the order  # Go to """"Inspect Permissions"""" and check the permissions of """"test-user""""    h3. Expected Results  # The user should have permissions to use the space, because """"test-group"""" still has global and space permissions    h3. Actual Results  # Inspect Permissions shows that user does not have """"Can Use"""" permissions    h3. Workaround  # Option 1: Changing external directories will triggers cache update, and """"Inspect Permissions"""" will pick up permissions correctly  # Option 2: Disabling/enabling the Gatekeeper app will force an update on the cache and you will see the proper permissions for """"test-user""""  """

summary: "Bulk instead of individual e-mails from Confluence"
description: """+As a+ system admin  +I want+ Confluence to send notifications in bulk  +So that+ E-Mail servers are not overloaded with lots of individual e-mails, one for each update for each recipient, whenever a page gets updated.    h3. Problem Definition  Customers may face e-mail storms from Confluence due to notifications being sent for pages and blogs watched.  As Confluence generates one individual e-mail for each user, this can escalate to several users being notified for several pages, causing a heavy load on e-mail servers.    h3. Suggested Solution  Bulk e-mail notifications so that instead of one message per user, it could send one message for the total watchers or grouping them for configurable number if the watcher list is large, for instance, one message for each 30 watchers. (BCC them?)    h3. Workaround  None at the moment, but you can consider:  * Reducing the events notified:  [How to globally disable e-mail notifications with page content|https://confluence.<USER>confkb/how-to-globally-disable-e-mail-notifications-with-page-content-718670714.html]  * Increasing the time for batching e-mails:  [Email Notifications|https://confluence.<USER>doc/email-notifications-145162.html?_ga=2.217851798.1001520385.1583151685-296208751.1582325698]"""

summary: "Deleting a group may take a long time due to Space permissions calculations"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    During a group deletion, Confluence retrieves all space permissions (for each space) from DB, and then filters them by that group.    Eventually, it means that Confluence loads permissions for all spaces from DB.    In the case of a Data Center instance, it takes even longer because all this information is loaded to the distributed cache.   In broad terms, that explains why it may take so much time to confirm the deletion of a group: Confluence is calculating space permissions prior to the deletion. Screenshot below.   The deletion itself is pretty fast. But depending on the number of spaces and groups an environment has, the space permissions calculations can take from several seconds to several minutes.    !screenshot-1.png|thumbnail!       *Environment*    Observed in these versions:   * Confluence 6.13.4, 6.13.6, but this is also observable in any 6.x versions.    h3. Steps to Reproduce    *Scenario 1 (single space/group)*   # install a vanilla instance   # create a sample space, named SpaceA   # create a sample group, named GroupA   # create space permission in SpaceA related to GroupA   # flush Cache to ensure data will be loaded from DB   # now go to *Confluence Administration > General Configuration > Groups*   # delete GroupA   ** it will show a confirmation screen, loading all the spaces permissions that are related to this group   ** this time will load pretty fast because there are just one space and one group   ** in a controlled test, this scenario took 600ms to complete   # cancel the deletion    *Scenario 2 (multiple spaces / single group)*   This is not a real scenario but we tested it to illustrate an increase in complexity.    However, it's still far from a customer real environment due to scalability (customers tend to have thousands of spaces/groups instead of hundreds)   # install a vanilla instance   # create 100 spaces, named Space1 \{2,3,4 <USER>   # create a sample group, named Group1   # create space permission in each Space(N) related to Group1   # flush Cache to ensure data will be loaded from DB   # now go to *Confluence Administration > General Configuration > Groups*   # delete Group1   ** it will show a confirmation screen, loading all the spaces permissions that are related to this group   ** this time it may take a little longer due to space permissions calculations showing the behavior   ** in a controlled test, this scenario took 2.1 secs to complete   # cancel the deletion    h3. Expected Results   * Scenario 2   ** The group deletion confirmation screen to load without any further delay.    h3. Actual Results   * Scenario 2   ** The group deletion confirmation screen took a longer time than Scenario 1 due to space permissions calculations.   (!) This only gets worse the more spaces/groups you add to the scenario (we tested 100 spaces related to 100 groups and it took around 4.5 seconds)   In a real situation with a prod environment from a customer, ~6600 groups related to ~790 spaces, it takes +2 minutes to complete the space permissions calculations.    h3. Workaround    Since the problem lies in the space permissions calculations, you can bypass it and go straight to the actual group deletion action, using the RPC-API SOAP call.    Example below.   For reference: [Confluence XML RPC and SOAP APIs|https://developer.<USER>server/confluence/confluence-xml-rpc-and-soap-apis/]  {code:bash}  #!/bin/bash    ADMIN_USRNAME=<adminuser>  ADMIN_USRPWD=<adminpwd>  CONFLUENCE_BASE_URL=""""http://<host>/confluence""""  GROUP_NAME=<group_name>  API_METHOD=removeGroup    echo """"Deleting group ${GROUP_NAME} ...""""  curl \      --user $ADMIN_USRNAME:$ADMIN_USRPWD \      -H """"Content-Type: application/<USER>""" \      -H """"Accept: application/<USER>""" \      -X POST \      -d '{ """"jsonrpc"""" : """"2.0"""", """"method"""" : """"'${API_METHOD}'"""", """"params"""" : [""""'${GROUP_NAME}'"""", """"""""], """"id"""": 7 }' \      ${CONFLUENCE_BASE_URL}/rpc/<USER>rpc/confluenceservice-v2?os_authType=basic 2>/dev/null | jq -r '.'  {code}"""

summary: """Watch This Blog"" button in Recent Blog list is misleading"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  When user is visiting a Blog Post in confluence. There are 3 different options to _Watch_ the *Blog* content in a Space:  # Watch blog post  # Watch for new blog posts in this space  # Watch all content in this space   !Watch Blog.png|thumbnail!     These options are self-explanatory and have been further explained in [this documentation|https://confluence.<USER>doc/watch-pages-spaces-and-blogs-139579.html]    However, when the user is visiting the _Recent Blog Posts_ page from the sidebar, the user is presented with """"Watch this Blog"""" button which can be misleading (refer to the _Misleading Button_ section below)    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Create multiple Blog Posts in a Space and publish it  # Click on the """"Blog"""" option on the left pane and user will be brought to a list of _Recent Blog Posts_  # Click on the """"Watch This Blog"""" option on the top right   !Watch This Blog.png|thumbnail!     h4. Misleading Button  A *Watch this Blog* button shown in this page can mislead user to believe this is a button to *Watch Every Blog* in the space, or at least the list of blogs that are shown in this page.    In fact, this button functions as the same with the """"Watch for new blog posts in this space"""" option that is described earlier. By clicking on this option, the user will only receive notification from any new blog posts but not from any pre-existing post update   !Watch Compare.png|thumbnail!     h3. Suggested Solution  Either:  * Change this to an option to """"Watch All Blog Posts"""" in the space; Or  * Rename the button to something less misleading such as """"Watch for new blog posts"""""""
comments: "Would be good if ""Watch this blog"" translated to tick ""Watch for new blog posts in this space"" as well as watch any new blogs that are created so that edited blogs send out notifications too without having to go in each time to watch each blog post, otherwise updates could be missed."

summary: "'Page properties report' macro does not works as expected if it's placed within the merger of both 'Panel or 'Expand' macros."
description: """h3. Issue Summary    """"Page properties report"""" works fine if its inside either a """"Panel"""" macro or an """"Expand"""" macro but """"Page properties report"""" does not works as expected if it's placed within the merger of both """"Panel"""" or """"Expand"""" macros. The expand macro in a panel macro creates a second html thead-tag.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a page with 'Page properties' macro   # Add a table inside the 'page properties' macro as mentioned in [Page Properties Macro|https://confluence.<USER>doc/page-properties-macro-184550024.html]   # Add a Label to the page and publish the page.   # Copy the page and create multiple copies of the page with different page title on the same space.   # Create a new page on the same space.   # Add a 'Panel' macro.   # Inisde the 'Panel' macro add a 'Expand' macro.   # Inide the 'Expand' macro add """"Page properties report' macro   # Add the same Label name which was created on step 3 under 'Label' and under 'In space'  mention current space.   # Save  and Publish the page.    h3. Expected Results    Screenshot attached : ExpectedResults   The first row of the Page Macro displays properly.  h3. Actual Results    Screenshot attached : ActualResults   The first row of the Page Macro displays abnormally taking the backgroud color as set for the panel macro.    Upon looking at the html, we see two thead tags as per below attached screenshot.    !Screen Shot 2020-03-10 at 8.16.12 PM.png|thumbnail!  h3. Workaround    Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Separate the Existing and Deleted Pages in the Space Tool > Permission > Restricted Pages Window "
description: """Confluence currently include the *deleted pages* in the list of Space Tool > Permission > *Restricted Pages*.     As this may create confusion to the Space Admin user while reviewing the restricted pages, it is best to separate the Existing and Deleted Pages under the *Restricted Pages*."""

summary: "Saving a page will throw mix content if macro table of content is present on it alongside with few links."
description: """h3. Issue Summary  Saving a page will throw mix content if a macro table of content is present on it alongside a few links and without Collaborative editing (set to off).    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Configure Confluence to run over HTTPS. The base URL needs to be over https as well.  # Disable collaborative editing on Confluence 7.3.1  # Create a new page  # Add an external link on a page. (below is example storage format)    # Add macro Table of Contents  # Open inspect tools and preserve logs  # Save the page    h3. Expected Results  All requests loaded inside will be over https, like this:   [^working_6.13.6.har]     h3. Actual Results  One will fail, and it will try to resolve over HTTP:  [^table_of_content.har]       h3. Note  The issue is not present in 6.13.6, 6.13.1, 6.13.4 and on 7.3.2  [^7.3.2_fixed.har]   The issue is not present with 7.3.1 and Collaborative editing enabled.  [^wokging_with_ce.har]     h3. Workaround  Upgrade to 7.3.2 where the issue is not observable."""

summary: "Problem with Table of Contents Macro on Different Browser Versions"
