The cursor is not pointing correctly to the headings, titles.  h3. Environment    .   Tested this out in Confluence version 6.15.9 using Chrome Version 80.0.3987.87.  h3. Steps to Reproduce    Create a page with Table of Contents macro. Attempt to view a page with Table Of Contents macro. Move the cursor over the headings/titles to see if this is occurring correctly.  h3. Expected Results         The cursor should move correctly over the heading and other content within the page.  h3. Actual Results         The cursor skips or the movement of the cursor is not normal.  h3. Workaround    _There is no workaround available at this point._  h3. Notes"""

summary: "The first line of text after anchor macro is not showing after the linking jump"

Issue Summary  Since the linking to anchor jumping does not take into consideration the page operation menu bar, the first 2 lines after the anchor macro will be hidden by the menu bar.    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a page that contains a minimum content of 2 paginations.    # Add an anchor macro in the middle of the page and add 1 line of text after the macro.    # Add a link to the anchor at the beginning of the page.    # Save the page and click on the anchor macro    h3. Expected Results  It should jump to the anchor position and show the line of text after the macro.    h3. Actual Results  The line of the text is hidden by the page operation menu.  !Screenshot 2020-03-06 at 10.54.08.png|thumbnail!    h3. Workaround  Add 2 new brake lines between the anchor macro and the text."""
comments: "This is a duplicate of CONFSERVER-57767"

summary: "GroupUpdatedEvent is not taken into account by Gatekeeper"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    When running a full or incremental remote directory synchronization, the changes pulled in from that remote directory are not reflected in Gatekeeper.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Configure an external user directory  # Synchronize the users and groups from there  # Go to Inspect Permissions  # Set some permissions for a group (i.e. *_groupA_*)  # Make some changes in the remote directory (such as update the group name to *_groupA1_*)  # Now go back to User Directories  # Disable that directory, click Synchronize (this will force a full sync when we enable it again)  # Enable it again and click Synchronize    h3. Expected Results  The changes in the remote directory should be reflected in Confluence as well as in Gatekeeper. The group name should be updated and carry the permissions set on the old name      h3. Actual Results  New group with new no permissions set.  (!) If we rename this group to the same one as before, those permissions will appear again    h3. Workaround  The system administrator can disable and then enable the plugin to force a refresh of the user cache"""

summary: "Gatekeeper will ignore status updates (enabled/disabled) on newly added users from an external directory"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    When adding a new user from an external user directory (connector), any status updates to this user (enabled/disabled) will not be picked up by Gatekeeper. Confluence will show this user as disabled. In Inspect Permissions, when you check the checkbox """"Don't show disabled user accounts"""" Gatekeeper still shows the disabled user.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Create a user in an LDAP/AD (Active Directory)  # Synchronize the Active Directory  # Make sure that this user is visible in Inspect Permissions  # Disable that user in LDAP/AD  # Synchronize the Active Directory  # In Confluence, this user will show as disabled  # Go to Inspect Permissions, check the checkbox """"Don't show disabled user accounts""""    h3. Expected Results  The disabled users won't appear in the list    h3. Actual Results  Users are still loaded in that list even though they are disabled    h3. Workaround  Disable the Gatekeeper plugin and enable it again for this to work and any subsequent status updates"""

summary: "Task report macro uses ""username"" fields to join tables (instead of lower_username)"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    When task report macro is sorted by user name (""""assignee""""), a new join is added to SQL query:    As both um.username and cu.user_name do not have indexes, the database has to scan tables (it leads to performance degradation).    Full SQL query statement:    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Prepare a Confluence instance with a lot of inline tasks and pages   # Create a page and add a task report macro sorted by """"assignee""""   # Enable SQL logging in Confluence   # Open a page with task report macro   # Retrieve the SQL query from logs and check its execution plan (by using DB tools)    h3. Expected Results    SQL query does not perform full scan on cwd_user and user_mapping tables  h3. Actual Results    SQL query performs full scan on either cwd_user or user_mapping tables (or both)  h3. Workaround    Do not sort tasks by """"assignee""""."""
comments: "A fix for this issue is available to Server and Data Center customers in Confluence 7.5.0  [Upgrade|https://www.<USER>software/confluence/download?utm_source=confluence_bugfix&utm_campaign=confluence_bugfix&utm_medium=email] now or check out the [Release Notes|https://confluence.<USER>display/DOC/Confluence+7.5+Release+Notes?utm_source=confluence_bugfix&utm_campaign=confluence_bugfix&utm_medium=email] to see what other issues are resolved." [SEP] "A fix for this issue is available to Server and Data Center customers in Confluence 7.4.3  [Upgrade|https://www.<USER>software/confluence/download?utm_source=confluence_bugfix&utm_campaign=confluence_bugfix&utm_medium=email] now or check out the [Release Notes|https://confluence.<USER>doc/confluence-7-4-release-notes-994312218.html?utm_source=confluence_bugfix&utm_campaign=confluence_bugfix&utm_medium=email] to see what other issues are resolved.        If you're running the Confluence 7.4 Enterprise release, a fix for this issue is now available in Confluence 7.4.

Actual Results    The date picker will be under the macro's panel/ dialog:    !image-2020-03-05-15-21-50-142.png|width=187,height=305!  h3. summary: "Gatekeeper will still load users and groups from a user directory that is disabled"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    When you disable an external user directory, Gatekeeper should not show the users and groups coming from that user directory.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Configure an external user directory  # Synchronize the users and groups from there  # Go to Inspect Permissions  # You should see the list of new users and groups there (In the case of a delegated user directory, once a user logs in)  # Now go back to User Directories  # Disable that directory    h3. Expected Results  Those users and groups shouldn't show in the Inspect Users and Groups list    h3. Actual Results  Those users and groups are still in that list and Gatekeeper displays the following message _The explanation for this permission could not be found_   !gatekeeper-error.png|thumbnail!     h3. Workaround  The only workaround is to do the following:  # Remove the user directory  # Enable and Disable the Gatekeeper plugin"""

summary: "ApplicationDirectoryRemovedEvent is not taken into account by Gatekeeper"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    When an existing user directory is removed, Gatekeeper is not aware of it and does not update its user cache.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Configure an external user directory  # Synchronize the users and groups from there  # Go to Inspect Permissions  # You should see the list of new users there  # Now go back to User Directories  # Disable and Remove that directory    h3. Expected Results  Those users shouldn't show in the Inspect Users list      h3. Actual Results  Those users are still in that list    h3. Notes  ApplicationDirectoryRemovedEvent is sent when the user directory is removed    h3. Workaround  The system administrator can disable and then enable the plugin to force a refresh of the user cache"""

summary: "Like and Stop Watching page options from email notification has an expiration"
description: """h3. Problem    When we click on the """"like"""" or """"stop watching page"""" buttons on the email notifications that were received more than 7 days ago, we are redirected to an HTTP 401 error page.  h3. Environment    Confluence Server  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # User A watches a page   # User B makes changes to the content on this page   # User A gets notified via email   # After 7 days, User A clicks on either the """"like"""" or """"stop watching page"""" buttons on the email and gets redirected to 401 unauthorized error    h3. Expected Results    The """"like"""" and """"stop watching page"""" buttons should not expire  h3. Actual Results    The """"like"""" and """"stop watching page"""" buttons expire after 7 days.  h3. Workaround    Click on """"View Page"""" from the email and like or stop watching page from the Confluence UI.  h3. Notes    n/a"""

Actual Results :    As above information is not available in our public documents.    So it is not clear for our users, who is authorised to 'Delete a page hierarchy'.    Having said that, I'd suggest, If we could include this information in [Delete and restore page|https://confluence.<USER>conf72/delete-or-restore-a-page-982322679.html] .      """

This is a security concern and should not be disclosed.  h3. Expected Results   * Error is displayed but the Tomcat version information is hidden.    h3. Workaround    Alter the behavior of the error reporting by adding the following entry to your server.xml file:    This will suppress the server information being displayed as part of the error report."""

summary: "Ability to update JDBC drivers for Microsoft SQL Server Authentication"
description: """Currently, it is not recommended to update the JDBC drivers for Microsoft SQL in Confluence as new driver versions are not yet tested and it is unknown what issues might occur when using a different driver version. Based on the [documentation|https://confluence.<USER>doc/database-jdbc-drivers-171742.html], it is recommended to use the bundled Type 4 JDBC driver since it is the driver version that has been tested with Confluence.     As an example faced by a user, updating the JDBC driver version causes Confluence to not start up with the new JDBC driver because Windows authentication to the SQL Server is not working. However, it is possible to start Confluence with the new version when the {{.dll}} was renamed to the old name as {{sqljdbc_auth.dll}}. In regards to this, it may be seen that Confluence only recognizes the old naming of the {{.dll}} file. Confluence should be able to recognize the {{.dll}} file that is stored in {{<confluence-install>/confluence/WEB-INF/lib}} without the change of the naming.    As a suggestion, Confluence should be able to support the newer version of JDBC drivers."""

Actual Results  The icons tend to be rendered as a solid square, and the shapes don't display at all:    Excel document:   !CleanShot 2020-03-03 at 15.30.45.png|thumbnail!     Excel macro version:   !CleanShot 2020-03-03 at 15.31.45.png|thumbnail!     h3. Workaround   Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "If one node crashes, user session information stored on that node will be gone, and user will need to authenticate again."
description: """If one node crashes on DC setup, user-session information stored on that node will be gone, and the user will need to authenticate again.    The solution for this is to use SSO or remember me cookie functionality, but they will also show invalid session:   !4AAF218.png|thumbnail!     The user will not need to enter credentials but he will need to reload the page.    It would be a good solution to introduce Redis or some other caching mechanism so that the user login information is not lost if one node crashes. Alternatively, as with the Crowd, we could store session information inside the DB, and the application can retrieve it if there is no information in the cache."""

Steps to Reproduce  # In Confluence Cloud, create a page and add some decisions.  **  !Cloud-dec.PNG|thumbnail!   # Export the space, or copy the storage format of the page, and move it to Confluence server.  **  [^Confluence-export.zip]   # The page will appear as below in Confluence server.  **  !Server-dec.PNG|thumbnail!     h3. Expected Results  # The decisions icon to be displayed correctly.    h3. Actual Results  # The local-id of the decision icon from Confluence Cloud is displayed in the screen.    h3. Workaround  Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Confirm dialog before exporting whole Space as PDF"
description: """h3. Problem Definition    Triggering a full Space Export to PDF can cause an OutOfMemoryError (OOME) and crash the Confluence JVM, causing an outage. The export can be triggered accidentally while attempting to run a Custom Space Export to PDF as tracked under CONFSERVER-35540.    h3. Suggested Solution    Requesting to include a Confirmation dialog/prompt under *Space Tools > Content Tools > Export > PDF > Normal Export > Export button*.    The Confirmation dialogue should include a Cancel and Ok button, but also some verbiage to inform the user that a PDF export is a memory-intensive operation, and exporting a large Space (with multiple pages and attachments) can cause slowness and, worst cases, a full outage.    This should be simple enough to implement, and might prevent a few accidental clicks.    h3. Workaround   # Fix the OOME as tracked under CONFSERVER-55021 and CONFSERVER-59643.  # Include a *Cancel button* during the Space PDF export process - CONFSERVER-45603 was closed as """"Won't Do""""."""

summary: "Hide the stack-trace messages exhibited by the Webserver in the UI"
description:
"""If a request to the Webserver is sent with an URL containing wrong characters or with invalid objects, a stack-trace may be returned as response and be exhibited in the User Interface. h3. Examples For a request sent to the Webserver with an URL containing wrong characters, the following stack-trace is shown in the UI: {code:html} HTTP Request GET /plugins/servlet/samlsso?redirectTo=-""""www.ernw.de HTTP/1.1 Host: confluence.mbusa.com User-Agent: Mozilla/5.0 (X11; Linux x86_64; rv:70.0) Gecko/20100101 Firefox/70.0 Accept: text/html,application/xhtml+xml,application/xml;q=0.9,*/*;q=0.8 Accept-Language: en-US,en;q=0.5 Accept-Encoding: gzip, deflate Connection: close Upgrade-Insecure-Requests: 1 HTTP Response HTTP/1.1 400 [...] [...]

HTTP Status 400 – Bad Request

Type Exception Report

Message Invalid character found in the request target. The valid characters are defined in RFC 7230 and RFC 3986

Description The server cannot or will not process the request due to something that is perceived to be a client error (e.g., malformed request syntax, invalid request message framing, or deceptive request routing).

Exception

java.lang.IllegalArgumentException: Invalid character found in the request target. The valid characters are defined in RFC 7230 and RFC 3986 org.apache.coyote.http11.Http11InputBuffer.parseRequestLine(Http11InputBuffer.java:467) org.apache.coyote.http11.Http11Processor.service(Http11Processor.java:294) org.apache.coyote.AbstractProcessorLight.process(AbstractProcessorLight.java:66) org.apache.coyote.AbstractProtocol$ConnectionHandler.process(AbstractProtocol.java:770) org.apache.tomcat.util.net.NioEndpoint$SocketProcessor.doRun(NioEndpoint.java:1415) org.apache.tomcat.util.net.SocketProcessorBase.run(SocketProcessorBase.java:49) java.util.concurrent.ThreadPoolExecutor.runWorker(ThreadPoolExecutor.java:1142) java.util.concurrent.ThreadPoolExecutor$Worker.run(ThreadPoolExecutor.java:617) org.apache.tomcat.util.threads.TaskThread$WrappingRunnable.run(TaskThread.java:61) java.lang.Thread.run(Thread.java:745)

Note The full stack trace of the root cause is available in the server logs.

h3. Request A way to keep these stack-traces from wrong requests hidden from the UI, so users don't need to see them at all. A friendly message could be exhibited instead in order to indicate something went wrong"""

summary: "With language set to German, Advanced Search defaults to custom type search if any content was searched prior to redirect"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Starting with Confluence 6.14, when the default language and indexing language is set to German, redirecting from quick Search to Advanced search via advanced search link, defaults to Custom Type instead of All content.  h3. Environment    Confluence 6.14.* with new Quick Search, default language/indexing language set to German  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Set default language and indexing language to German   # Add a comment on a page   # Search for the same comment in quick search   # Comment is not found as comments are excluded from default quick search as per statement in [Search|https://confluence.<USER>doc/search-139528.html] page.   # Click Advanced Search to be redirected to Advanced Search    h3. Expected Results   * Comment is found as Advanced search searched *All Content* types by default    h3. Actual Results   * Comment is not found as Advanced Search switches to the *Custom* content type search that includes only Space, User, Page, Blog post and Attachment   !quicksearch1.png|thumbnail!    h3. Workaround   * No workaround at this time as it seems that even reverting the default language/indexing language to English will no longer resolve the issue.      This issue is very similar to a recently closed bug, https://jira.<USER>browse/CONFSERVER-58072.  The difference is the language setting."""

summary: "Using Confluence With Google Chrome In Spanish Causes Errors In Logs"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Whilst using Google Chrome configured with Spanish (Latin America) as the primary language, there are situations where an error may be seen in the logs indicating the {{es-419}} is not a valid language tag. This issue is similar to that seen in [CONFSERVER-29922|https://jira.<USER>browse/CONFSERVER-29922] which was fixed, but there are some additional scenarios where this issue arises.    Environment Notes   * Google Chrome with the top language set to Spanish (Latin America)    h3. Steps to Reproduce  h4. Scenario 1   # Install the Confluence Source Editor Plugin   # Using Google Chrome, change the top language in Chrome to Spanish (Latin America)   # Make a new page in Confluence   # Edit the Page using the source editor, and submit a change    Submitting the change in the source editor will result in an error in the logs.    h4. Scenario 2  # Install the Confluence Source Editor Plugin  # Using Google Chrome, change the top language in Chrome to Spanish (Latin America)  # Edit a page, leaving the editor open  # In a seperate tab, navigate to Confluence and log out    The editor tab will continue to poll back to Confluence every 30 seconds resulting in an error in the logs.    h3. Expected Results    No 'es-419, is not a valid language tag' error in the logs    h3. Actual Results    Currently the error gets generated when we add this content.   The below exception is thrown in the catalina.out file:  {code:title=Scenario 1}  27-Feb-2020 23:40:33.723 SEVERE [http-nio-8090-exec-3] com.sun.jersey.spi.container.ContainerResponse.logException Mapped exception to response: 500 (Internal Server Error)   javax.ws.rs.WebApplicationException: java.text.ParseException: String, es-419, is not a valid language tag   at com.sun.jersey.server.impl.model.HttpHelper.clientError(HttpHelper.java:273)   at com.sun.jersey.server.impl.model.HttpHelper.getAcceptLanguage(HttpHelper.java:228)   at com.sun.jersey.server.impl.VariantSelector.selectVariant(VariantSelector.java:290)   at com.sun.jersey.spi.container.ContainerRequest.selectVariant(ContainerRequest.java:696)   at com.sun.jersey.spi.container.AdaptingContainerRequest.selectVariant(AdaptingContainerRequest.java:286)   at sun.reflect.NativeMethodAccessorImpl.invoke0(Native Method)   at sun.reflect.NativeMethodAccessorImpl.invoke(NativeMethodAccessorImpl.java:62)   at sun.reflect.DelegatingMethodAccessorImpl.invoke(DelegatingMethodAccessorImpl.java:43)   at java.lang.reflect.Method.invoke(Method.java:498)   at com.sun.jersey.server.impl.application.WebApplicationImpl$2.invoke(WebApplicationImpl.java:301)   at com.sun.proxy.$Proxy2631.selectVariant(Unknown Source)   at com.atlassian.plugins.rest.common.Status.variantFor(Status.java:304)   at com.atlassian.plugins.rest.common.error.jersey.NotFoundExceptionMapper.toResponse(NotFoundExceptionMapper.java:21)   at com.atlassian.plugins.rest.common.error.jersey.NotFoundExceptionMapper.toResponse(NotFoundExceptionMapper.java:16)   at com.sun.jersey.spi.container.ContainerResponse.mapException(ContainerResponse.java:480)   at com.sun.jersey.spi.container.ContainerResponse.mapWebApplicationException(ContainerResponse.java:444)   at com.sun.jersey.server.impl.application.WebApplicationImpl._handleRequest(WebApplicationImpl.java:1475)   at com.sun.jersey.server.impl.application.WebApplicationImpl.handleRequest(WebApplicationImpl.java:1419)   at com.sun.jersey.server.impl.application.WebApplicationImpl.handleRequest(WebApplicationImpl.java:1409)   at com.sun.jersey.spi.container.servlet.WebComponent.service(WebComponent.java:409)   at com.sun.jersey.spi.container.servlet.ServletContainer.service(ServletContainer.java:558)  ...  Caused by: java.text.ParseException: String, es-419, is not a valid language tag  ...    h3. Workaround    Currently there is no way to mitigate this issue directly within Confluence.    It is possible to work around the issue at the load balancer/reverse proxy level using a rewrite rule. The header provided by Chrome will look similar to   {code:title=accept-language String}  Accept-Language: es-419,es;q=0.9,en-GB;q=0.8,en-US;q=0.7,en;q=0.6    This removes the {{es-419}} tag leaving only the {{es}} tag, whilst maintaining other language settings.  (!) This may change the Spanish dialect on pages received by Spanish (Latin America) users."""
comments: "Apologies to anyone watching this ticket. Whilst updating the bug, I realised I had accidentally saved my progress a number of times, likely triggering emails to you all.    I've updated the ticket with a potential workaround, and an additional scenario in which this can occur."

summary: "Inspect Permissions does not match user permissions for a space"
description: """h3. Problem    The new feature of Inspect Permissions is not matching the current user's permissions for a space.  h3. Environment    Confluence Datacenter 7.3.x     .  h3. Steps to Reproduce        # Navigate to any space   # Press *Space tools > Permissions*   # **In the Groups section, click on *Edit Permissions* button   # Remove all the permissions from the confluence-users group   # Press *Save All*   # In the same screen, at the Individual Users section, click on *Edit Permissions*   # Type a user X that don't belong to the confluence-administrator group   # Give this user only the following permissions:   ## All > View   ## Blog > Add   ## Restrictions > Add/Delete   # Inspect the permissions for the user X (they wont match).    h3. Expected Results    The expected results are that the inspect permissions shows the following for user X:   # All > View   # Blog > Add   # Restrictions > Add/Delete    h3. Actual Results    The inspect permissions is showing that the user X have almost all permissions, as we can see in the attachment """"Screen Shot 2020-02-27 at 17.34.06.png"""".       h3. Notes"""
comments: "Hi [~<USER>, I tried reproducing the issue as you described and it worked for me.  If you click on the permissions it should tell you more information about why it thinks that user has those perms.     Note that when I tried to reproduce it actually the user is show as _not_ having restrictions permission, because they do not have permission to edit pages, which is actually required to edit restrictions on pages.    !detail-view.png|thumbnail! " [SEP] "Note that there _is_ a very significant bug with inspect permissions not working for nested groups in directories CONFSERVER-59513." [SEP] "Not a bug."

summary: "Disable changing encoding in Confluence administration"
description: """{panel:bgColor=#e7f4fa}    *NOTE:* This bug report is for *Confluence Server*. Using *Confluence Cloud*? Problem  The only encoding that Confluence support is UTF-8  However, admins may change this setting in General Configuration > Formatting and International Settings  Setting Confluence default encoding to UTF-16 from this page will renders 'chinese' characters for spaces and even the login screen. To verify this is the problem run in the database:    if it returns a different value of UTF-8 in the result, then it is affected.    h3.Steps to reproduce:  # Set up a new Confluence  # Go under the _Administrator Panel > General Configuration > Formatting and International Settings_﻿ and change _Encoding_ field from UTF-8 to UTF-16.    *Expected Result:*  Confluence doesn't allow the change    *Actual Result:*  Confluence becomes unusable, styles are broken and administration sections are locked.  Can't be reverted back unless a DB change is done.    h3.Workaround  # Shut down Confluence and perform a database backup  # Run:    # Start Confluence  """

summary: "Update ""Configuring Crowd to Work with SSL"" documentation"
description: """h3. Problem  [Configuring Crowd to Work with SSL|https://confluence.<USER>crowd/configuring-crowd-to-work-with-ssl-151520306.html] needs a bit of a sprucing up.     h4. Example connector out of date  The example connector (which most customers use by default) is missing many of the configuration that is included in the default 8095 connector included in Crowd. This can lead to issues that result in a support ticket.    h4. There is not section on adding a redirect  Confluence includes a section on adding a security constraint to automatically redirect traffic from http to https, this is missing in the Crowd documentation. While it's possible to figure out how to add this by reviewing other Atlassian product documentation (or Tomcat documentation), it's easy to miss all of the areas in which you need to add the security constraint (as Crowd has 4 webapps, vs 1 for Confluence).     h4. There is no step to update the base url  Updating the base url is an important step that is easy to forget, the documentation should include it.    h3. Recommendation  h4. Update the example connector to include all settings from the 8095 default connector:  {code:borderStyle=solid}  <Connector acceptCount=""""100""""                     connectionTimeout=""""20000""""                     disableUploadTimeout=""""true""""                     enableLookups=""""false""""                     maxHttpHeaderSize=""""8192""""                     maxThreads=""""150""""                     minSpareThreads=""""25""""                     useBodyEncodingForURI=""""true""""                     URIEncoding=""""UTF-8""""                     compression=""""on""""                     sendReasonPhrase=""""true""""                     compressableMimeType=""""text/html,text/xml,application/xml,text/plain,text/css,application/<USER>application/javascript,application/x-javascript""""                     port=""""8443""""                     protocol=""""org.apache.coyote.http11.Http11Protocol""""                     SSLEnabled=""""true""""                      scheme=""""https""""                      secure=""""true""""                     clientAuth=""""false""""                      sslProtocol=""""TLS""""                     sslEnabledProtocols=""""TLSv1,TLSv1.1,TLSv1.2""""                     keystoreFile=""""${user.home}/.keystore""""                      keystorePass=""""changeit""""                     keyAlias=""""tomcat""""                     keyPass=""""changeit"""" />      h4. Add a section that details how to update the base url:  # In your browser, go to !https://confluence.<USER>doc/files/590259974/590058056/1/1398739558493/Cog.png! > General  # Change the Base URL field from http to https  # Save"""

summary: "In Confluence, Notification shows wrong Jira link if multiple Jira links are used"
description: """h3. Problem  In Confluence, if there are multiple Jira links created, when notification is received in Confluence for one link, the link contained is always the primary Jira link.  h3. Environment  6.14.2    h3. Steps to Reproduce  Create multiple Jira links in Confluence, such as below:  Jira A  Jira B (primary App Link)  Jira C  Jira D    When watching an Issue in """"Jira A"""" and a comment or mentioning is done, that user gets the info in the Confluence """"Notification Box"""". But this notification does not point to the Issue from """"Jira A"""", instead it replaces it with a link to Jira B (which is the primary App Link).    h3. Expected Results    The link should be the link of the app-link through which the jira issue is being viewed.    h3. Actual Results    In the notifications (the bell icon at the top right)  The link is always of that of the Primary link. So even if a jira link belonging to a different app-link.    _(Add actual results for current action)_  h3. Workaround    The notification should contain the link of that of which ever app link the jira issue is connected to.    _(Add a workaround, if available)_  h3. Notes"""

summary: "Confluence Throws ""ContentUpdatedEvent"" Error while Connected to a Database via Datasource"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    While Confluence is connected to the database via DataSource, we will see the following error in the {{atlassian-confluence.log}} despite the page publish successfully:    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Install Confluence and [connect any supported database via datasource|https://confluence.<USER>doc/configuring-a-datasource-connection-937166084.html]   # Create a new page   # Publish the page   # Check the {{atlassian-confluence.log}}    h3. Expected Results    The page publishes successfully with no errors in the log.  h3. Actual Results    The page publishes successfully but with errors in the log.  h3. Notes    If 2 users are editing the same page this error is thrown when the first user publishes (or saves) the page and later when the second user tried to publish or save he will get a pop-up with the error """"Can't sync with the server. Refresh the page to try to re-establish the connection."""". Refreshing the page allows to proceed.  h3. Workaround    Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior if you want to use a datasource connection.   If you want to get rid of the errors in the logs, or the error pop-up in case of collaborative editing, you can connect to the database using the [default direct JDBC connection method|https://confluence.<USER>doc/database-configuration-159764.html#DatabaseConfiguration-Databaseconnectionmethods]."""
comments: "JNDI Datasources don't get much love from Atlassian. This is sad, because configuring the database connection in the appserver (Tomcat), rather than the application is just better in every way, operationally:   * Data is portable between prod and staging. I can rsync my Confluence home directory from prod to staging, and I don't need to hack confluence.cfg.xml to update the database details, because the staging Tomcat is configured to connect to the staging database.   * I don't have database credentials leaking into my application backups.   * JavaMelody (JRASERVER-67553) or JMX-based monitoring tools can introspect the Datasource to monitor database connections." [SEP] "+1 on @<USER>   JNDI based datasources separate application configuration from db connections & allow for a pooled & more detailed configuration using the application server (Tomcat) environment. We're using this for several years w/o any problem.  It's documented as an option. So either don't support JNDI connections anymore (Hint: remov

summary: "Clicking preview on attachment in page if using administrator account will result in preview not found (404)"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  Clicking on a preview of the attachment (let's say PPTX file) on a page, with an admin account, that does not have explicit edit/view permissions on a page, will result in 404, preview not find. Furthermore, the sandbox will not be triggered to generate the preview, if the preview was never generated.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Create a single node DC instance.  # Create an admin (admin2) account that does not have an explicit view or edit permissions on a space.  # Create an admin (admin1) that has explicit permissions on that space.  # Attach pptx file and save the page.  # Check with the user admin1 if the preview is visible, and it will be visible.  # Now log out and log in with admin2 (user does not have an explicit view and edit permissions on this space).  # Click on the preview of the pptx file    h3. Expected Results  Preview will be visible.    h3. Actual Results  Preview will not be visible, and in Networks tools tab we will see 404   !Screen Shot 2020-02-21 at 1.56.31 PM.png|thumbnail!     h3. Notes  Sandbox process for conversion will not be triggered.      h3. Workaround  Currently, there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""
comments: "It's important to mention that this fails for documents which are converted. If you upload a pdf it's visible, because it's not converted. PPTX should reliably reproduce this bug"

summary: "Inspect permissions and people who can view ignore parents of nested groups"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  The new Inspect permissions feature does not respect nested groups.  This also applies to the """"people who can view"""" feature.    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Use an external user directory server   # Have a user who is directly in group ChildGroup, which is a child group of ParentGroup   # Ensure the user is known to confluence, e.g. by logging in as them.   # Give ParentGroup permission to do something in Confluence. E.g. give ParentGroup access to view pages in a space   # Inspect permissions for the user    h3. Expected Results  The permissions for the user should show that the user gets permissions from ParentGroup    h3. Actual Results  The permissions for the user disregard permissions from ParentGroup    h3. Impact & Workaround  To check if you are impacted by this issue, please run the following query on your database:    If this query returns one or more results, then you are impacted and permissions assigned to the resulting parent/intermediate groups won’t be recognised by the People who can view or Inspect permissions features.  (!) *Please note that this doesn’t mean that these permissions aren’t enforced, it just means that the Inspect permissions and People who can view features will not reflect them.*  If you are impacted by this issue, we recommend you go to *Manage Apps* and temporarily disable the *Inspect Permissions - Gatekeeper* system app. This will disable the People who can view and Inspect Permissions features, and prevent users and admins relying on information that is incorrect."""

summary: "Disabling a system scheduled job continues to run post restart of Confluence"
description: """h2. Summary    Confluence uses [Scheduled Jobs|https://confluence.<USER>doc/scheduled-jobs-96567525.html] to automatize processes that are required to function properly. It's possible to enable/disable these jobs (managed jobs).    However, it was identified that Confluence will not respect these changes if we restart Confluence due to a code bug:      This code intends to schedule for the unmanaged jobs but it doesn't check if the current job is managed or unmanaged before scheduling. Basically, disabling a system scheduled job will continue to run post restart of Confluence.    h2. Steps to Reproduce    We will use the scheduled job {{cleanupTrigger}} as an example.   # Install Confluence 7.1.2   # Let the *cleanupTrigger* run once   # Then disable it and check the database:     # Shutdown Confluence   # Set the server time to *~1:50 am*   # add to {{log4j.properties}} the following properties to track the job execution upon application restart:     # Start Confluence - we should have the following in the logs indicating that the job is enqueued to run at the default scheduled time:      h2. Expected Results    A *managed* Scheduled Job will remain disabled.  h2. Actual Results    A *disabled managed* Scheduled Job will be rescheduled to run after a restart.  h2. Workaround    This bug was identified and tested with the {{cleanupTrigger}} job (Office Connector). Follow the workaround from the related bug for *this job*:   - CONFSERVER-58957 - Preview macros for PDF and PowerPoint stops working after sometime    For other jobs, any new workaround found in the near-future will be provided in this report."""
comments: "See also https://jira.<USER>browse/CONFSERVER-55455 (Scheduled jobs settings are reverted back after Confluence restart)"

summary: "Installation process of Confluence Server 7.3.1 stops due to java.xml.crypto file"
description: """When running the Windows x64 installer for Confluence Server 7.3.1 (atlassian-confluence-7.3.1-x64.exe), the initial file extraction process stopped after a few seconds and the error message """"Could not open file legal\._java.xml.crypto"""" was displayed.    !image-2020-02-18-11-35-17-996.png!    After ruling out a corrupted download and our virus scanner interfering, it turned out that we use ransomware protection that blocks *.crypto-files. We were then able to install Confluence by whitelisting this file name. Still, please consider renaming this file to something that doesn't use an extension that is commonly used by ransomware."""

summary: "oauth servlets do not support ""no-check"" header for POST/PUT/DELETE requests"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Servlets require CSRF tokens for POST requests.    But, at the same time, they do not support """"no-check"""" headers, so it is impossible to send POST requests from JS code.   h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Enable html macros in Confluence   # Add an html macro with JS script which sends POST request to {{<Base-URL>/plugins/servlet/oauth/authorize}} Note: """"no-check"""" header should be enabled   # Navigate to the page    h3. Expected Results    JS script should get an answer from the proxied resource  h3. Actual Results    JS script returns """"403 XSRF Token missing message""""  h3. Notes    (Optional - If Necessary)  h3. Workaround    Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""
comments: "/plugins/servlet/oauth/authorize is backed by com.atlassian.oauth.serviceprovider.internal.servlet.authorize.AuthorizeServlet, which explicitly implements its own XSRF checks.    In the short term: we should fix the AuthorizeServlet to use XsrfRequestValidator instead of XsrfTokenValidator so that it gets automatic support for the no-check header.    Longer term: We should port the XSRF opt-in support in Confluence's ServletModuleContainerServlet into the plugin framework's implementation, and upgrade atlassian-oauth to use that instead."

Workaround  FireFox Works as intended  Safari: Works as intended  """

summary: "Provide a feature that notifies users when a change in Confluence may affect the look of their content."
description: """Often times, various changes in Confluence in the form of HTML/CSS customizations, installation of third party plugins, may (unintentionally) produce unwanted changes in the way data/content is displayed in Confluence.     Often times, the end user does not know how/ or why the content that used to """"look"""" on way, suddenly look differently which causes frustrations, especially if the new look distorts the content significantly from how the author originally wants it to look like.     It would be great addition to Confluence if:  # Confluence would automatically detect that certain customisation changes _might_ affect the look and feel of a content and then notifies authors in this regards.   # This will help authors verify if there are any changes in the look of their content and as such help them either to edit the content to suite the new look or help them notify the admins of their respective Confluence instances that a recent customisation or plugin has caused their content to behave in a different way."""

summary: "Start Confluence Data Center in Safe Mode"
description: """Unlike Confluence Server, it's not possible to start Confluence Data Center in Safe Mode, as also indicated on the KB article below:   * [How to manually remove malfunctioning add-ons|https://confluence.<USER>confkb/how-to-manually-remove-malfunctioning-add-ons-113705118.html]    It would be helpful to have such feature on Confluence Data Center, since the only way of disabling plugins currently available is by accessing the database and running a SQL query, which requires a database backup as a good practice."""

summary: "Update the REST API to allow for more endpoints to match previous offerings."
description: """h3. Problem Definition    Since Confluence 5.5, previous Remote APIs, such as JSON-RPC and XML-RPC, have been deprecated and are no longer supported. However, many of the methods available through these API endpoints have not been ported over to the REST API, which is now being utilized.    This can cause issues for any users that were utilizing these older endpoints and are unable to make the switch to the REST API.    While it's technically possible for users to still use the older API endpoints,  it's not specifically stated if these endpoints will be made available in the REST API or if at all, and is also another concern.    h3. Suggested Solution    Create equivalent endpoints in the REST API to match what is available through the older Remote APIs.    h3. Workaround    Depending on the specific functions needed, the Confluence CLI can provide many of the same functionality, but it is a paid third-party add-on."""

summary: "Page Operations Menu display can be too long for low-resolution settings"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    If the resolution of a device is set very low, when viewing a page, the '...' (Page Operations Menu) can be too long, and it is not possible to scroll all the way to the end.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Open a Confluence page in your browser.  It should be a very short page, with only 1 line of text perhaps.   # Re-size the browser window to be quite short - less than 600pixels   # Click the '...' menu on a Confluence page    h3. Expected Results    The menu would be scrollable and all options would be visible  h3. Actual Results    The menu is too long to display on screen, but you cannot scroll down to the bottom options.  !image-2020-02-10-14-49-00-293.png|width=311,height=501!  h3. Workaround    In Confluence, go to the 'Stylesheet', and edit the 'Global Stylesheet' to contain the following:    You can play with the {{max-height}} setting to be bigger or smaller, depending on what resolution you need it to work on.  This will force the menu to be much shorter in height, but it will be scrollable."""
comments: "We have 24 items in the menu, so it doesn't fit for some users.    There're thousands of users at Autodesk with very different hardware, resolutions and zoom settings.    The workaround of limiting the size of the menu may make the situation better for one group of users but will inconvenience users with higher resolution.    Not to mention time spent investigating the problem internally and with Confluence tech support.    Please increase the priority of the issue. It's an embarrassing bug to have. Thanks." [SEP] "we're also hitting this bug sometimes on screens with 1080p resolution!    This makes it nearly impossible for many users to delete a page!    Possible Workaround: reduce Browser zoom to 90%.         This bug also affects 7.4.1 and 7.6.0!"

summary: "Disabling user account welcome notification email"
description: """h3. Problem    When a new user is created in Confluence, the user is sent a welcome email with the following content:        There is currently no way to disable this email being sent to the new user. There is a setting, which allows the Administrator to not receive this email, but not the user.  h3. Suggested Solution    _There should be a notification setting, that can be disabled so that users do not receive email for this particular event. There is currently no direct way to achieve this._   h3. Why This Is Important    _This is important as we do see customers interested in disabling this option, not just for administrators, but also for normal users._  h3. Workaround    _There is currently no workaround to achieve this._"""

summary: "Copying the first image that is posted as an answer to a Confluence Question into a new answer results in an error."
description: """h3. Problem    The first image that is posted in an Answer to a Confluence Question cannot be copied and pasted to a new response.    The second, third, and subsequent images that are posted as CQ Answers *_are_* able to be copied and pasted into new responses.  h3. Environment    Confluence 6.13.9, Confluence Questions 2.7.20  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a new Confluence Question   # Create a new answer to that question that includes an image   # Create a second new answer   # Select the image and the text of the first answer   # Paste the image and text of the first answer into the second (or subsequent) answer   # Receive """"Oops - an error has occurred"""" message and notice that the answer was not posted    h3. Expected Results    The second answer is attached and no error is thrown  h3. Actual Results    User receives """"Oops - an error has occurred""""   Application logs show OptimisticLockingFailureException:    h3. Workaround    Download the image and re-upload it instead of copying it from the first answer.  h3. Notes    I can reproduce this issue in my test environment on every attempt."""

summary: "Jira Status Report blueprint it's not rendering the pie charts' content"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  * The Jira Status Report template in Confluence doesn't render the *Overall status*, *Priority*, *Component* and *Issue Type* pie charts of it.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # In Confluence, click on *... >> Jira Report >> Status Report*  # Select the Jira project  # Create the page  # Publish the page    h3. Expected Results  * The pie charts render the results related to the selected Jira project    h3. Actual Results  * The pie charts won't render the results. The following warning message is shown instead:  {quote}  java.lang.IllegalArgumentException: parameter value cannot be null  {quote}  * Checking the chart's storage format:    * If the pie chart, for the same project, is added to the same page, the results are rendered correctly, and here's the storage format of the chart:    ** If we compare both storage formats, we'll notice that the default pie chart is missing the *ac:name=""""jql""""* parameter.    h3. Notes     * Confluence 7.1.2 is using the *Confluence Jira Plugin v11.0.12*    h3. Workaround  * Replace the *Confluence Jira Plugin v11.0.12* with an earlier version, *Confluence Jira Plugin v11.0.11*. To replace this plugin, you'll need to:  *# Navigate to your *installation directory/confluence/WEB-INF/atlassian-bundled-plugins*  *# Backup this folder  *# Delete the *confluence-jira-plugin-11.0.12.jar* file and replace it with the  [^confluence-jira-plugin-11.0.11.jar]  file  *# Restart your Confluence instance    *OR*    * Manually add the pie chart macro to the affected page"""

If these are also affected, would be nice to have a fixed Jira Plugin available/shipped with upcoming Confluence Enterprise Release (7.4).    Thanks." summary: "An illegal reflective access operation has occurred While Running Confluence on JRE higher than 1.8"
description: """h3. Problem    Noticing the following issue while running Confluence version 7.3.1 on JRE versions higher than 1.8.:    We under stand that this error message was introduced from Java 1.9. Even-though the latest versions of Confluence are compatible till Java 11 and some of the docker and standalone installer versions come packaged with this version.  h3. Environment    Noticed this in 7.3.1  h3. Steps to Reproduce    User standalone installer of Confluence 7.3.1 for either Windows or Linux. This error is noted in random or during upgrade.  h3. Expected Results    No error is expected.  h3. Actual Results    Observing the error message noted above.  h3. Workaround    None.  h3. Notes"""
comments: "Generally: Confluence will continue to work successfully in Java 11, despite these warnings in this code, and in Felix OSGi container code paths, and probably in several other places too. In the near future, we should aim to eliminate each of these warnings so that customers can run without any worries that Confluence might break.    Note that the specific code being called out above is making a final field in hibernate Environment modifiable, so we can inject a bridging implementation of a BytecodeProvider that works for both Hibernate 5 and 2 code paths. This is no longer required in hibernate 5.4.11 or above, but we would need to upgrade to that, and cut another release of hibernate.adapter to use its replacement APIs.  " [SEP] "Be good to have this fixed, the message is alarming when you see it on start up. Appreciate not a show-stopper for Confluence, but definitely a good thing to fix."

summary: "Option to bulk remove links to undefined page"
description: """h4. Issue summary  # In Confluence,we can create links to page that do not exist yet.  ** [Undefined Page Links|https://confluence.<USER>doc/undefined-page-links-139580.html]  # While we can view the list of Undefined Page links in Confluence in the Space admin page, to remove these links, we'll have to open the page and remove the links.    h4. Suggestion  # Have the ability to bulk remove these undefined page links from the Undefined Pages section."""

summary: "Confluence Search - Allow entry of a Parameter Value for a Macro"
description: """Currently, you can perform a search for the usage of a macro on a page.      It would be helpful if a search could be done for the parameter value used within that macro.    For example, perform a search for all pages using the excerpt-include macro that is pulling in an excerpt from the page 'Best Practices'.    macroName:excerpt-include:page=ABC:Best Practices"""

summary: "Provide a feature to bulk remove a particular macro from a Confluence instance. "
description: """There are instances where customers would like to remove every instance of a macro from all Confluence pages.     The current only procedure users can do, is to head over to each page and remove these macros one by one which is not ideal especially if there are a lot of spaces and pages to be covered.     h5. The ask:  Provide a feature that allows users to bulk remove macros from space level or global level. """

summary: "Drop matching licences policy asap, please!"
description: """Dear Atlassian,    I've been a fan of your tools for the last decade, and regard very positively the evolution in the products and growth of you offers.    However, the matching users licence policy is now completely outdate, and I strongly suggest you review it. Given the high number of plugins Confluence available, it happens quite often that we would like to start using a given plugin for on of our company's divisions only, limiting the licence (and price) of the plugin.    Due to your matching licences policy, this is not possible, which many times stops us from getting additional products complementing your tools.    IMHO, one of the main reasons for Atlassian's products success is the highly flexible and usable User Management approach, but oddly enough, the matching licences policy is a big obstacle to going deeper into the Atlassian ecosystem.    I therefore suggest you implement a more flexible licensing policy, where the system admin can 1) choose a sub-set of users from Confluence licence which have access to a given plugin, or 2) select all users if the licences match."""

summary: "Label List macro should not list labels tagged to pages with View and Edit restriction"
description: """h3. Issue Summary:    Hello Team, one of our customer and I were able to find a behavioural issue with Confluence and the Label List Macro.    A page when created with View and Edit Restrictions only to Admins and the Creator tagged to a label, the label(tagged to above restricted content) is listed in the 'Label List' macro when a different user creates a page with label list macro.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Log in as UserA creates a PageA with the LabelA and saves the page.   # The PageA has a Viewing and Editing Restrictions with only UserA with Edit and View access.   # UserB logs in to Confluence and lists down the pages in the same SPACE and doesn't see PageA.   # UserB creates a page and uses the Label-List macro to list down all the labels tagged to pages in that SPACE and the does see LabelA listed.   # However UserB clicks on the [#LabelA] ends up seeing-  {panel}  h4. Labelled content    There are no pages at the moment.  {panel}    h3.Expected Results:    The Label List macro's list should not list the labels tagged to restricted pages and content.      h3. Actual Results    The Label List Macro does list down the labels which are tagged to restricted Content(Page) thereby resulting in the message - _There are no pages at the moment_ when the disallowed users click on the label.  h3. Workaround    —    Required, if there is no workaround please state:   Currently, there is no known workaround for this behaviour. A workaround will be added here when available."""

summary: "Support PDF Inline Comment in File Previews"
description: """h3. Summary  The user seeing broken PDF comments after uploading the PDF file in a Confluence page.  (i) This is not Confluence's own """"Inline Comment"""" feature.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Create a PDF and [add comments|https://www.adobe.com/content/dam/Adobe/en/feature-details/acrobatpro/pdfs/adding-comments-to-a-pdf-document.pdf]  # Create a page  # Add the PDF file created in step 1  # Click on the PDF file to view it    h3. Actual Results  !pdf_comment.png|thumbnail!    h3. Expected Results  User should be able to see the PDF comments    h3. Notes  An extended improvement would be to have PDF's Inline comment incorporated together with Confluence's Inline Comment feature on the read and write of the PDF documents."""

summary: "Google Translate Extension's icon added to the bottom of the page after editing"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    If Google Translate Extension is installed in the browser, having text selected when saving/updating a page will cause the extension's icon to be added to the bottom of the page.    This icon cannot be removed later when editing again.  h3. Environment   * Any Confluence version after 6.13   * Chrome browser   * Google Translate Extension installed    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a page;   # Set the Translate Extension options to a different language than the one in Confluence and to display icon;   # Edit the page, add some text and click on *Update* without any text selected;   # Edit again, select some text and click on *Update*;   # Edit again and check for the presence of the icon;   # You need to open in *Source Editor* to remove the section containing the icon.    Check the attached video for details.  h3. Expected Results    The extension's icon, that is not displayed when the Editor is open, should not be added to the page's storage format when text is selected.  h3. Actual Results    The extension's icon is added to the page's storage format and visible when viewing the page but not when editing.  h3. Workaround    Set the Google Translate Extension option to """"Don't display icon or pop-up"""" to prevent this issue.    For pages where the icon was added, use the free [Source Editor|https://marketplace.<USER>apps/1210722/confluence-source-editor?hosting=server&tab=overview] app to remove the icon's section, as seen on the attached video."""

summary: "Provide In-House solution for Missing Attachments Script"
description: """h3. Problem Definition    Many users that encounter issues with missing file system based attachments end up having to utilize our python script here:    * [How to resolve missing attachments in Confluence|https://confluence.<USER>confkb/how-to-resolve-missing-attachments-in-confluence-201761.html]    This is not always an ideal process for users as it requires Python to be installed on the environment that may be locked down for security.     The process itself can also lead to issues, as there are several different factors (SQL queries, running the script, permissions, <USER> that can lead to issues.    h3. Suggested Solution    A more painless solution to this issue would be to implement the Missing Attachments script  (written in Java) within Confluence. This would help provide a clearer process for users to resolve this issue."""

summary: "Notification Email Grammatically Incorrect"
description: """h3. Problem    Notification email of multiple Edit on Pages send an email stating: """"There's 14 new edits on this page"""".    When multiple edits are made, multiple emails are sent, with the incorrect wording.  h3. Environment    Confluence  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Edit multiple pages.   # Check the notification emails.    h3. Expected Results    The notification email should be grammatically correct, if there are more then one change,    it should be """"There are"""" or simply remove the """"There's"""" from the sentence.  h3. Actual Results    The notification email writes: """"There's XX new edits on this page""""  h3. Workaround    _._    Notes"""

summary: "Looping over/deleting all old version thumbnails when attaching file"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    When adding a new attachment, Confluence is looping to retrieve all versions of the same attachment and to delete all thumbnails for the historical attachment. This might cause a performance issue on Azure when there is a large number of versions of the same attachment.   h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Deploy a Confluence instance on Azure   # Create a page and add the same attachment 200 times.   # Add the same attachment again.    h3. Expected Results    The attachment should be added instantly.  h3. Actual Results    The operation reach the session timeout and it failed to add the attachment  h3. Workaround    Reduce the number of versions on the same attachment."""

summary: "Text on PowerPoint's thumbnail appears to be garbled on CentOS7"

Since the issue above has more votes, we'll be moving the watchers and linked cases over to the issue above.    Cheers,  <USER> {panel}    h3. Environment:  * CentOS7 (3.10.0-1062.9.1.el7.x86_64)  * Confluence 6.13.9  * java-1.8.0-openjdk-1.8.0.232.b09-0.el7_7.x86_64    h3. summary: "Add option to strip images from email notifications "
description: """Currently, email notifications do not include any images or other attachments. Instead, an empty box is shown with a question <USER> This is confusing and visually unattractive. Besides, it gives end users the impression that Confluence is not working properly.    People have been complaining about this for years, both in the Confluence and JIRA forums. See e.g. [this Confluence thread |https://jira.<USER>browse/CONFSERVER-9443?focusedCommentId=426627&page=com.atlassian.jira.plugin.system.issuetabpanels%3Acomment-tabpanel#comment-426627] and [this JIRA thread|https://jira.<USER>browse/JRASERVER-41675]    So why not add an option to the mail server configuration settings under Administration > General Configuration > Mail Servers, to strip all images / attachments from email notifications? This would be an elegant solution for end users (who would rather see no images at all than broken images) and also means no complex solution has to be built for handling permissions to download / view images from restricted Confluence posts or pages.  """

summary: "Menu of a Page and Share options overlap"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  Menu of a page and Share options overlap    h3. Steps to Reproduce   1. Create a new page or go to existing page   2. Click on '...' to display the page menu   3. Click on Share button      h3. Expected Results   When you click on Share, Page Menu option should disappear and only Share option should be available.     h3. Actual Results  Both options would be available i.e Page Menu and Share.     h3. Workaround  No workaround available.    """

summary: "Texts in powerpoint thumbnail is not rendered correctly depending on Java version"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Texts in powerpoint thumbnail is not rendered correctly depending on Java version. Characters are overlaid each other.    As far as I confirmed,   * (/) [AdoptOpenJDK jdk8u202-b08|https://adoptopenjdk.net/releases.html] (which is a [bundled one in most version of Confluence|https://confluence.<USER>doc/bundled-tomcat-and-java-versions-1005786018.html]) can render correctly   * (/) [Oracle JRE jre-8u241-<USER>x64|https://www.oracle.com/technetwork/java/javase/downloads/jre8-downloads-2133155.html] can render correctly   * (x) [AdoptOpenJDK jdk8u242-b08|https://adoptopenjdk.net/releases.html] is affected   * (x) [AdoptOpenJDK jdk-11.0.6+10|https://adoptopenjdk.net/releases.html] is affected   * (x) [AdoptOpenJDK jdk-11.0.5+10|https://adoptopenjdk.net/releases.html] is affected (it's bundled with 7.4.0)    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Prepare Linux OS and install some fonts to enable server side rendering  {code:bash|title=I've tested with Ubuntu}  sudo apt-get update  sudo apt-get install -y fontconfig  apt-get install -y fonts-dejavu  {code}     # Install Confluence from tar.gz and configure it to use *[AdoptOpenJDK jdk8u242-b08|https://adoptopenjdk.net/releases.html]*   # Open the editor, and drag and drop [^sample.pptx] on your browser.    h3. Expected Results    [^sample.pptx] is rendered correctly.  h3. Actual Results    The text is squashed.    !Screen Shot 2020-02-05 at 11.50.49.png|thumbnail!  h3. Workaround    Use jdk8u202-b08 as [Supported Platforms|https://confluence.<USER>doc/supported-platforms-207488198.html] suggests.  {quote}Some AdoptOpenJDK versions have bugs that impact Confluence. We recommend version jdk8u202 (see CONFSERVER-58784).  {quote}  Or, if you are already using Java 11, upgrade to version 11.0.7."""
comments: "I'm having this issue on 2 different Windows servers with Confluence 7.3.2    However I've not found a JDK that works yet. I've tested the following   * AdoptOpenJDK standalone 11.0.6.10-hotspot   * AdoptOpenJDK bundled 11.0.5    Edit: I'd missed changing the jvm.dll used by Tomcat, so now have at least got it working on    * AdoptOpenJDK standalone 1.8.0 202   * Oracle JDK standalone 1.8.0 241" [SEP] "As AdoptOpenJDK standalone 1.8.0 202 is no longer available for download any more  and also is quite far behind in security fixes, have anyone a working solution with a more recent Java version?    We have encountered same problem on latest Confluence (7.3.5) but havent found a solution yet." [SEP] "After some testing with different JDK we downloaded the AdoptOpenJDK released earlier this week  11.0.7 . It seems like problem is solved in this new version of OpenJDK. " [SEP] "Have anyone tried latest 8u252-b09 ? It's released around same time as 11.0.7, so hopefully it should have same fix ?" [SEP] "8u252

summary: "Confluence on Windows was vulnerable to DLL hijacking - CVE-2019-20406"
description: """The usage of Tomcat in Confluence on the Microsoft Windows operating system before version 7.0.5, from version 7.1.0 before version 7.1.1 allows local system attackers who have permission to write a dll file in a directory in the global path environmental variable variable to inject code & escalate their privileges via a DLL hijacking vulnerability.    h3. Acknowledgment    We would like to thank Peleg Hadar of SafeBreach Labs for reporting this vulnerability."""
comments: "This is an independent assessment and you should evaluate its applicability to your own IT environment. CVSS v3 score: 6.7 => Medium severity    *Exploitability Metrics*  || Attack Vector | Local |  || Attack Complexity | High |  || Privileges Required | Low |  || User Interaction | Required |    *Scope Metric*  || Scope | Unchanged |    *Impact Metrics*  || Confidentiality | High |  || Integrity | High |  || Availability | High |    https://asecurityteam.bitbucket.io/cvss_v3/#CVSS:3.0/AV:L/AC:H/PR:L/UI:R/S:U/C:H/I:H/A:H" [SEP] "Why isn't the Long Term Support version 6.13.x being updated to address this vulnerability? I thought that was the whole promise of the Long Term Support concept?"

summary: "User Management: Change Default Filter from 10 to 100 Users"
description: """Summary: Ability to change the default user filter from 10 users  h3. Problem    Under the _User Management_ > _Users_ option, if you change the filter from 10 to 100, navigate away and come back to this page, the user list defaults to 10 again.  h3. Suggested Solution    If we change the filter to display a number of users from 10 to any other number (20, 50, or 100), Confluence should be able to stick to those settings next time when the admin comes back to the _Users_ page.  h3. Why This Is Important    For a Confluence site with a large number of users, it can be ineffective for admins when Confluence always goes back to the default number 10, and they need to make changes to the list every time they visit the page.  h3. Workaround    N/A"""

summary: "JIRA Macro couldn't render issues when the custom field contains multi-byte characters"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    JIRA Macro couldn't render issues when the filter contains multi-byte characters.  h3. Environment   * Confluence 6.15.9 and 7.2.1   * JIRA 8.1.0       h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Install JIRA 8.1.0   # Create a project   # Create two custom fields as follows   ## Create a custom field with Russian characters (срок исполнения)   ## Create another custom field with English characters(period of execution.)   !image-2020-02-04-16-17-54-311.png|thumbnail!   # Install Confluence 6.15.9 and integrate it with the JIRA   # Create a space and a page   # Use JIRA Macro with the following two types of custom fields   # Check if the macro works fine in the preview dialog    h3. Expected Results    Even if we use Russian characters in the custom field, the issue should be rendered on the page as it works fine with the other custom fields which contain only English characters.   !image-2020-02-04-16-27-58-182.png|thumbnail!    !image-2020-02-04-16-22-01-748.png|thumbnail!  h3. Actual Results    When we use Russin characters in the custom field, the issue wasn't rendered on the page correctly.   !image-2020-02-04-16-21-31-983.png|thumbnail!  h3. Workaround   # Stop Confluence   # Add the following line into the {{setenv.sh}}/{{setenv.bat}} file  {panel:title=setenv.sh}  CATALINA_OPTS=""""-Dfile.encoding=UTF-8 ${CATALINA_OPTS}""""  {panel}  {panel:title=setenv.bat}  set CATALINA_OPTS=-Dfile.encoding=UTF-8 %CATALINA_OPTS%  {panel}     # Start Confluence"""

summary: "XLSX files are not converted in the sandbox, and can get stuck and run CPU to 100%"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    In Confluence Data Center 6.10 and above, the document conversion process should be handled by a """"sandbox"""" - an external process pool that can crash or be timed out without impacting Confluence.  However, it appears that some spreadsheets that contain graphs can still be converted the old way, and can become stuck, eventually running cpu up to 100%.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Install Confluence Data Center 6.10 or above   # Attach an XLS or XLSX that contains a graph to a page.    h3. Expected Results    When viewing the file in preview mode, Confluence should render the preview using the sandbox.  h3. Actual Results    Confluence will skip the sandbox, and attempt to generate the thumbnail using an HTTP thread, which may become permanently stuck, requiring a re-start.  The thread should report as stuck the Tomcat logs:    {code:title=catalina.out}  23-Jan-2020 08:16:24.416 WARNING [Catalina-utility-2] org.apache.catalina.valves.StuckThreadDetectionValve.notifyStuckThreadDetected Thread [http-nio-0.0.0.0-8090-exec-62] (id=[1760]) has been active for [69,192] milliseconds (since [1/23/20 8:15 AM]) to serve the same request for [https://confluence.example.com/display/ABC123/Test+Page?src=contextnavpagetreemode] and may be stuck (configured threshold for this StuckThreadDetectionValve is [60] seconds). There is/are [2] thread(s) in total that are monitored by this Valve and may be stuck.   java.lang.Throwable    at sun.java2d.pisces.Renderer.curveBreakIntoLinesAndAdd(Renderer.java:259)    at sun.java2d.pisces.Renderer.curveTo(Renderer.java:395)    at sun.java2d.pisces.Stroker.emitCurveTo(Stroker.java:487)    at sun.java2d.pisces.Stroker.drawRoundCap(Stroker.java:277)    at sun.java2d.pisces.Stroker.finish(Stroker.java:441)    at sun.java2d.pisces.Stroker.moveTo(Stroker.java:348)    at sun.java2d.pisces.Dasher.goTo(Dasher.java:155)    at sun.java2d.pisces.Dasher.lineTo(Dasher.java:210)    at sun.java2d.pipe.RenderingEngine.feedConsumer(RenderingEngine.java:366)    at sun.java2d.pisces.PiscesRenderingEngine.pathTo(PiscesRenderingEngine.java:484)    at sun.java2d.pisces.PiscesRenderingEngine.strokeTo(PiscesRenderingEngine.java:363)    at sun.java2d.pisces.PiscesRenderingEngine.strokeTo(PiscesRenderingEngine.java:163)    at sun.java2d.pisces.PiscesRenderingEngine.getAATileGenerator(PiscesRenderingEngine.java:562)  ...  {code}    The above thread will never report as completed, even after hours.  Thread dumps will show the exact same stack trace as the above, indicating that Confluence is getting stuck during the conversion process.      h3. Workaround    If you already have stuck threads such as the above, the only resolution is to re-start Confluence to release those threads.     To prevent this from happening again, we recommend removing graphs from any XLS documents contained on pages. If you need to review pages to see which have graphs on them, it would be safest to Disable the XLS view file module to prevent any further performance issues while accessing these pages:     # !https://confluence.<USER>download/attachments/157679812/Cog.png?version=1&modificationDate=1361250253653&api=v2! -> General configuration -> Manage Apps   # Select _System_ from the drop down menu and search for _Office Connector_   # Select the Office Connector Plugin and expand. Click the *+* sign to reveal the underlying modules   # Find the *{{viewxls}}* and *{{viewxls-legacy}}* modules and click Disable    At that point, visit the page noted in the stuck thread error log, and check for any XLSX or XLS files that may contain graphs.  Remove the graph, delete the attachment from Confluence, and re-upload the version without graphs."""
comments: "A fix for this issue is available to Server and Data Center customers in Confluence 7.8.0  [Upgrade|https://www.<USER>software/confluence/download] now or check out the [Release Notes|https://confluence.<USER>display/DOC/Confluence+7.8+Release+Notes] to see what other issues are resolved."

Steps to Reproduce    Step 1:- Create a page with the title *""""bienvenue à bord""""* and save the page.   Step 2:- Insert a link in some other page and search a page *""""bienvenue à bord""""* and save the link.   Step 3:- Now edit the link which has created in the last step, here the search box shows the *""""bienvenue & agrave; bord""""*.  h3. summary: "Allow Admins to influence search results through a mechanism available in the UI "
description: """Currently, Confluence has a very specific algorithm that it considers when returning Confluence Search Results. Sometimes, these results are not what the admin would expect OR are not as relevant to the admin.     While we do have additional search syntax/refinements that can be leveraged to yield more relevant results to the user, it would be ideal if the admin themselves could assign value to certain assets such that they are surfaced more easily through a basic keyword search. """

summary: "Can't search on all parts of a group name when sharing page"
description: """h3. Summary  When a user wants to share a Confluence page to a group, the user needs to know the exact name of the group. Currently, there are no preview of all available groups and the search feature for the groups is not smart enough to detect the full group name if the user input is a substring of the name.     For example: In a Confluence instance with the following groups: hr group, finance group. If a user types """"group"""" in the Add People field of the search dialog, Confluence will not retrieve the above groups as options the user can choose from. The user must start his search with """"finance"""" in order for Confluence to retrieve finance group as one of the options.      h3. Steps to Reproduce  * Given a Confluence instance that has the following groups: finance group, hr group  ** Navigate to a page and then click """"Share""""  ** In the panel, under Add People, type group     !Screen Shot 2018-06-12 at 10.16.49 PM.png|thumbnail!     h3. Expected Results  Confluence will give a list of all groups that has the text """"group"""" in it.     h3. Actual Results  Confluence does not retrieve any group.     h5. Improvement Suggestions:  # Make group search """"smart"""" enough to be able to retrieve all groups that has a particular sub-string from the search parameter.  # Display all groups in the dropdown for easy access.    """
comments: "From [~346088d68a33] ~    https://getsupport.<USER>browse/CSP-244117    {quote}We have a group naming convention where group names start with 'gl_aa_confluence_' and then some name.  When sharing a page this is not so handy, as the last part of the group names are not shown (the group name is too long to fit the width of the pop-up window)  It would be helpful if, like with users, one could search on part of the group name. For a user one can search on firstname, lastname, username, <USER>  When searching a group one has to type the exact group name starting with the first letter.{quote}" [SEP] "Unfortunately this is not a bug, as it was designed to work like this. The issue is also already covered under the CONFSERVER-55901 suggestion so this is being closed as a duplicate."

description: """h3. Issue Summary    Prerequisites:   - collaborative editing - multiple people editing at the same time and using different text colours    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Two users collaboratively edit the same page by choosing two different text colours   ** User-1 user red colour   ** User-2 user green colour   # As one point when on of the user presses the _""""front arrow""""_ button, the complete text colour switches to default text colour (i.e., black)   ** Details can be seen in the video attached    h3. Expected Results    Editor should show both colour during collaborative editing.  h3. Actual Results    The behaviour of the editor is captured in this video.   [https://drive.google.com/file/d/1SGJCvXvO7dbPuNGcwI3OBWYFMJBbHW0n/view]  h3. Workaround    Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Search Not Working Correctly for Certain String Combinations in Confluence  6.15.9"
description: """h3. Problem:  In Confluence Server version 6.15.9 certain string combinations are not searchable.    h3. Environment  Confluence Server version 6.15.9    h3. Steps to Reproduce    1. In Confluence 6.15.9 create a page with a random title. Within the page, add a string """"BE - Belt Extendible""""    2. Save/Publish this page.    3. From the top right corner of Confluence Dashboard, search for Strings BE, BE*, BE - , BE - Belt Extendible.    h3. Expected Results  The page containing the String BE - Belt Extendible should be listed.    h3. Actual Results  The page is not listed for any of the combinations.    h3. Workaround  Remove the - and save or add another string with - removed    h3. Notes"""

We have always considered the app to be stable, however, we have also received feedback that some customers will only trust mature software, which is indicated by a 1.x version release number.     With the latest updates, we are also taking the opportunity to release Companion with a 1.0.0 version number to reassure teams that Companion is trusted for production use.    Makisa | Senior Product Manager, Confluence Server and Data Center  """

summary: "Upgrading to ATST plugin v 1.23.1 gives false alarm for jvm code cache"
description: """{panel:title=Issues fixed in ATST (1.23.3)}  # The fix for this bug has been released in the ATST 1.23.3 plugin.  # Updating the app from the *Manage Apps* page should fix this issue.   {panel}      h3. Issue Summary    Users that have updated to the latest ATST version available in the marketplace, that was 1.23.1, may receive a failed health check regarding JVM Code Cache memory.    The failed health check error shows up regardless of the Code Cache memory size specified in the JVM parameters.  h3. Steps to Reproduce    This problem has multiple causes, so isn't directly reproducible.  h3. Expected Results    The troubleshooting tools only show up an error in Code Cache memory size when there's an actual problem  h3. Actual Results  An error is displayed even though the code cache size is adequate    h3. Workaround   * The actual ReservedCodeCacheSize may still be too small. Increase it by following [Confluence crashes due to CodeCache is full. Compiler has been disabled|https://confluence.<USER>confkb/confluence-crashes-due-to-codecache-is-full-compiler-has-been-disabled-780864803.html] article.   * Restart Confluence  """
comments: "I can confirm this affects us even after increasing the code cache size to 384m as well as 512m" [SEP] "Increased to 384m, 768m, and currently 1g but error still shows. Please fix bug ASAP." [SEP] "A new version of ATST (1.23.3) has been released that fixes this problem by removing the health check. We will be re-evaluating the value of this health check as it has caused multiple problems." [SEP] "Hi Team,    we to currently getting  same error on confluence even after increasing from 256 to 384m         regards,    <USER> R" [SEP] "Upgrading to ATST v1.23.3 definitely fixed the issue for me. Will be waiting for a long term fix since the health check is just disabled."
