summary: "Navigating through inline comments using the arrows breaks on basic pages"
description: """h3. Problem  Page positioning issue while navigating through multiple inline comments.    h3. Environment  Tests performed with Confluence versions 6.13.4 and 6.13.10, specifically for Data Center version to reach a similar environment to the customer.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  * Create a page with random content (customer used Lipsum Generator) and add the _Info, Note, Tip and Warning macros_ that come bundled with Confluence. Issue can also be reproduced with the Message Box Macro that is bundled with the Content Formatting for Confluence plugin version 6.4.3 from Adaptavist.   * Add any of those macros to the page with the random content, and add inline comments to the plain text but also to the macros content, since those macros are meant to enhance the plain text.  * While navigating the multiple inline comments, it is noticeable the inline comments added to the text inside the macros, either the _Info, Note, Tip and Warning macros_ or the _Message Box Macro_ makes the positioning in the page be completely lost, since navigating through the inline comments seems to either ignore the inline comments inside the macros or makes the positioning of the page focus, that was supposed to be over the inline comment highlighted text, goes either to the beginning of the page, or to some random place in the middle or all the way to the bottom.    h3. Expected Results  Navigating through inline comments should allow the page focus to be over the highlighted text for the inline comment.    h3. Actual Results  Navigating through inline with macros _Info, Note, Tip and Warning macros_ or the _Message Box Macro from Adaptavist_ shows the same behaviour, which is the wrong focus for the page that contain inline comments inside those macros, making the navigation through the page erroneous.    h3. Workaround  No workaround available. Removing the macros allow the navigation through the inline comments to work properly, but it defeats the purpose of making it possible to focus the positioning of the page over the highlighted content.    h3. Notes    Customer test data and instructions:  - Confluence 6.13.10 (Data Center).  - Chrome version is currently 79.0.3945.88 (although the problem exists across other Chrome versions and at least on the Safari browser as well from one user on a Mac).  - The testing shows that there seems to be a bug with the Message Box macro from Adaptavist's app called Content Formatting for Confluence (currently running v6.4.3). The behavior is that although clicking the up/down arrows jumps the highlighting to the appropriate comment, it does not """"give focus"""" to that comment if the comment is within a Message Box macro. Instead it either scrolls all the way to the top of the page, or the bottom of the page (seemingly determined by whichever is closer to the current position on the page). The only deviation from this is when the next comment is """"in view"""" (visible) in the current browser window (adjusting the window size can be helpful to demonstrate this).  - The testing shows that there seems to be a bug with the Info, Note, Tip and Warning macros that come bundled with Confluence. The behavior is that although clicking the up/down arrows jumps the highlighting to the appropriate comment, it does not """"give focus"""" to that comment if the comment is within one of those four macros. Instead it simply stays at the current position. The only deviation from this is when the next comment is """"in view"""" (visible) in the current browser window (adjusting the window size can be helpful to demonstrate this).  - As the behaviors are very similar between the Adaptavist and Confluence macros, I suspect there is some underlying code (from the Confluence macros) that Adaptivist is using (or some of the same classes?)."""

summary: "JIRA Report Plug In - Images copied/pasted not saving locally on page"
description: """h3. Problem    Images copied over from Jira Report Plugin don't render for anonymous users. However, all other content is viewable.     (i) A login user can view all content.   h3. Suggested Solution     Enable anonymous users to view Jira macro images.   h3. Why This Is Important    _Admin requires a way for anonymous users to view images copied over from the Jira report._   h3. Workaround    _Manually attach Images to page._   h3. How To Replicate   # Create app link to Jira.   ** !JiraAppLink.png|thumbnail!   # Create a Jira gadget.   ** !JiraGadget.png|thumbnail!   # Create a page.   # Copy and paste the Jira report from Jira to Confluence page.   ** !confluence-userView.png|thumbnail!   # enable anonymous users.   # view page as an anonymous user.   ** !anonymous users.png|thumbnail!"""

summary: "Allow users to select which letter will be used in their Collaborative editing text cursor"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    As of today, users with the same first name will be assigned the same letter to their Collaborative text cursor.    If we take in consideration the behaviour we have under CONFSERVER-59400, the usability of our collaborative editing feature is considerably degraded  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a new page   # Edit it with multiple users (be sure to have at least two of them with the same first name)    h3. Expected Results    We are able to discern between them easily  h3. Actual Results    Users will be assigned the same initial (first name initial), making it hard to discern between them.    If we take in consideration the behavior under CONFSERVER-59400, it becomes impossible to discern who is editing where.    Example being:    !SameColorSameLetter.jpg!  h3. Workaround    Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Assign different colors to all users editing a page with collaborative editing"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Currently, it is possible for users to be assigned the same Text Cursor color. This causes confusion and makes collaboration harder.    This problem is increased when users have the same first name. In this situation, they can have the same color and letter to describe them within the editor.    Upon testing, it seems that we are able to cycle through ten different colors and the colors can be repeated.    +_The suggested resolution is to keep the editor from assigning the same color for more than one user and increase the color pool from which the system can select._+  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a new page in Confluence   # Edit it with multiple users (preferably more than 10 users)   # Make sure that Collaborative editing is turned on       h3. Expected Results    Every and each user has a different color to describe them  h3. Actual Results    Colors can be repeated, causing confusion:    !SameColorSameLetter.jpg!                      h3. Workaround    Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "User mentions broken in Templates after Space import"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    When importing a Space with Templates from Confluence A to Confluence B, any mentions in the Templates will get broken, even if the usernames in the source and target instances are the same.    It seems related to CONFSERVER-34994. Templates use the *user_key* to make the reference to the target user, instead of the *username*.      h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create the same user (e.g. 'test.user') in Confluence A and Confluence B;   # In Confluence A, create a Template with a mention 'test.user';   # Export the space containing the template;   # Import into Confluence B;   # In Confluence B, create a page using the template imported;   # Save the page and check the mention;    h3. Expected Results    We should see a proper mention to 'test.user';  h3. Actual Results    We see *Broken Link* or *Not an unresolved user* errors instead of the mention.    h3. Workaround    This KB contains a script to fix all the user_keys after the import:   - [https://confluence.<USER>confkb/user-mentions-appear-as-broken-link-after-xml-import-717062858.html]"""

summary: "Rendering of blank Alt property within image tag"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    If a value is set for the alt property of an image that's embedded within a page, Confluence will display the _alt_ property name and it's value within the rendered HTML code. However, if no value is set for the _alt_ property, then Confluence will not display anything for the _alt_ property, including it's title.    Ideally, the _alt_ property is outputted but with no value so that screen readers will not break when trying to process an embedded image.  h3. Steps to Reproduce    For a given image embedded within a Confluence page:   # Edit the page.   # Select the image and click on _Properties_.   # Click on *Title*.   # Leave the *Alt text* field blank.   # Save the page.    h3. Desired Results    If you right click on the page to view the rendered HTML and locate the image tag containing the image, there should be a reference to the _alt_ property within the image tag like this:    h3. Actual Results    If you right click on the page to view the rendered HTML and locate the image tag containing the image, you'll notice that there's no reference of the _alt_ property within the image tag:  """
comments: "We would like to use alt="""" for decorative images. Screen reader recognizes this syntax and skips over decorative images." [SEP] "If it helps, this applies to: WCAG 2.0 Success Criterial 1.1.1 (Level A) [https://www.w3.org/TR/2008/REC-WCAG20-20081211/#text-equiv-all]    For more info, please see the WCAG's Techniques doc: H67: Using null alt text and no title attribute on img elements for images that AT should ignore at [https://www.w3.org/TR/WCAG20-TECHS/H67.html]     Also: [https://www.w3.org/WAI/tutorials/images/decorative/]     " [SEP] "Texas A&M University - Corpus Christi: We would like to use alt="""" for decorative images. Screen reader recognizes this syntax and skips over decorative images."

summary: "Anchor link usage instructions are misleading"

Problem    After creating an Anchor on the page, the next step of *help* instructions says to _""""type this""""_ to create the link to the anchor and shows the syntax example as the hashtag sign and the word """"anchorname"""" like this: *#anchorname .    This can create confusion when the user follows these exact steps.  h3. Suggested Solution    Suggest improving this by changing text to something along the lines of:   * """"Type the hashtag sign *""""#""""* and the name you gave the anchor...""""    h3. Why This Is Important   * Reduce user frustration   * Make it easier to use the anchor link macro."""

This error only appears when there's not meeting notes in selected space.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create two spaces.   ** Space A   ** Space B   # On Space A, create a new page and add the """"Content Report Table"""" macro.   # On the settings of the macro, use:   ** Labels: meeting-notes   ** Space: Space B    h3. Actual Results    The pop-up window displays with a spinning busy indicator and never goes away. Clicking the Next button does nothing. Clicking the Close button will close the pop-up. summary: "Check for existing Synchrony process before upgrading Confluence"
description: """Upgrade can be completed even when Synchrony process was still running. Cannot edit pages after upgrade until stopping the existing Synchrony process."""
comments: "The stop process for some reason on occasion seems to leave the synchrony process still running.  A user can stop Confluence with the intent of doing an upgrade, and the upgrade will go along and skip a critical step to establish the synchrony connection.  It would be nice if the stop process checked if all was stopped as expected and alert if not.  The upgrade process could do the same where it checks to make sure Confluence/synchrony are not running.    Cheers..." [SEP] "This should be automatic.  As a result of this not happening our users lost content after an upgrade and their trust in the product is damaged.  This is unfortunate as we are trying to have a smooth adoption across R&D.    In my opinion, this reads like a bug rather than a suggestion.  This opinion is shared in my organization."

summary: "Global page blueprints and Space templates are not populated until ••• 'Create from template' is clicked"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    On new installations the Global blueprints are not populated which also causes an empty template page under Space Tools until a user clicks the ••• '*Create from template*' button.    Note you do not need to create a page from a blueprint/template, you can close the window. All that is required is you click three ••• next to create. Then the Global Blueprints are populated and Space Tools now shows templates.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Fresh Confluence installation (Or one where no one has clicked *Create from template*)   # Go to:  !https://confluence.<USER>doc/files/747833194/747833195/2/1517274813281/cog.png! *> General Configuration > Global Templates and Blueprints*   ## Notice there are only 3 Space blueprints and 3 System templates - missing the *Global page blueprints*   # Go to a *Space > Space Tools > Content Tools > Templates*   ## Notice there are zero templates listed   # Click the ••• *Create from template* button   # Close the window without creating a page   # Go to Global Templates and Blueprints or Space Tools templates and you will now see a fully populated list. *Global page blueprints* are now listed under Global Templates and Blueprint templates are listed under Space Tools templates.    h3. Expected Results    All available templates are loaded and available to edit upon install before clicking on *Create from template* or creating a page from a template.   Perhaps triggered during the onboard process for example.  h3. Actual Results    No Global page blueprints are listed or any templates under Space Tools.   The only default blueprints under Global templates are the following:   * Knowledge base   * Team Space   * Documentation    h3. Workaround    You must click on ••• *Create from template*. There is no need to actually create a page, you can either close the window or create a page and the blueprints/templates will now be populated."""

Do you think this is sufficient? Is there a particular support case related to this request?" summary: "Updating ATST Troubleshooting and Support Tools breaks MySQL health checks"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Updating Atlassian Troubleshooting and Support Tools to the latest version 1.23.1 breaks the MySQL character set and collation health check which fails incorrectly.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Confluence is connected to MySQL and you have verified your encoding and collation is correct utf8/utf8_bin   # Update ATST to the latest version 1.23.1   # Go to: *General Configuration > Troubleshooting and support tools*   # Notice the following errors:  {quote}Encoding: What does this check do?   Checks if the collation used by the tables, columns, and database defaults in your MySQL database is correct.   Result   Exception during health check invocation java.lang.RuntimeException: java.sql.SQLException: You can't operate on a closed ResultSet!!!  {quote}  {quote}Collation: What does this check do?   Checks if the character set used by the tables, columns, and database defaults in your MySQL database is correct.    Result   Exception during health check invocation java.lang.RuntimeException: java.sql.SQLException: You can't operate on a closed ResultSet!!!  {quote}    h3. Expected Results    The test should pass as the error is only thrown after updating ATST without any changes made to the DB.  h3. Actual Results    The collation and encoding MySQL health checks fail    The below exception is thrown in the xxxxxxx.log file:    h3. Workaround  h5. Option 1 - Disable the MySQL modules   # Go to:  !https://confluence.<USER>doc/files/747833194/747833195/2/1517274813281/cog.png! *> Manage Apps >* Expand *Atlassian Troubleshooting and Support Tools >* Expand *modules* > Disable the following *modules*:   ## *confluence.healthcheck.mysql.character.set.name*   ## *confluence.healthcheck.mysql.collation.name*    h5. Option 2 - Downgrade ATST to the previous version 1.22.0   # Download ATST 1.22.0 from [here|https://marketplace.<USER>download/apps/1217697/version/12200001]   # Shutdown Confluence   # Go to *<CONF-INSTALL>/conlfuence/WEB-INF/atlassian-bundled-plugins*   # Backup and delete the file *plugin-confluence-1.22.0.jar* and *replace* with the downloaded 1.22.0 version   # [Clear the plug-in cache|https://confluence.<USER>confkb/how-to-clear-confluence-plugins-cache-297664846.html]   # Start Confluence     * For some reason, this was not enough to downgrade the plugin, if it still fails, follow on:   * (!) *Please remember* to take a database backup before making any changes.     # Shutdown Confluence   # Follow [Manually remove plugin from DB|https://confluence.<USER>confkb/how-to-manually-remove-malfunctioning-add-ons-113705118.html]   ## Run the following query:     ## You should see the ASTS 1.23.1 listed, similar to below:  {code:sql}  PLUGINDATAID PLUGINKEY                                       FILENAME                                                LASTMODDATE           ------------ ----------------------------------------------- ------------------------------------------------------- -------------------   1081345      com.atlassian.troubleshooting.plugin-confluence plugin.1358229087590107522.plugin-confluence-1.23.1.jar 2020-01-22 12:41:34{code}   ## Run the following query using the PLUGINDATAID from above for the ATST plugin - in this example it's *_1081345_*:  {code:java}  delete from PLUGINDATA where PLUGINDATAID=XXXXXX;{code}   # [Clear the plug-in cache|https://confluence.<USER>confkb/how-to-clear-confluence-plugins-cache-297664846.html].   # Start Confluence   # You should now find the ATST version has been downgraded to 1.22.0. You can confirm from the UPM and also the health check will now pass."""
comments: "We have released a new version of ATST (1.23.2) for Confluence that contains a fix for this problem"

summary: "Blogposts created same day are not sorted correctly"
description: """h3. Note - This bug doesn't affect version 6.x. It is affecting version 7.x  h3. Summary    When several blogs are created on the same day, they are not getting sorted based on created DateTime. The last blog created appears at the end of the blog list.  h3. How to replicate   # Create a blog post called """"Blog 1"""".   # Add some content. Close the editor and save it.   # Create another blog post and publish it as """"Blog 2"""".   # Add some content to Blog 2. Close the editor and save it.   # Create another blog post and publish it as """"Blog 3"""".   # Add some content to Blog 3. Close the editor and save it.    h3. Expected results    On the Blog post page, the order of the blogs should be like below   * Blog 3   * Blog 2   * Blog 1    h3. Actual results    The blogposts are instead listed in this order (top to bottom):   * Blog 1   * Blog 2   * Blog 3    h3. Note    The blog created on the next day will appear at the top of the blog page list. For e.g., if Blog 4 is created on the next days, the order of the blogs in the blog list is   * Blog 4   * Blog 1   * Blog 2   * Blog 3"""

summary: "Adding the Confluence Pages gadget twice in a dashboard causes the second gadget to fail the authentication with Confluence"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    When adding the Confluence pages gadget to a Jira dashboard twice, will cause Jira to fail the authentication on the second gadget upon the auto-refresh if other gadgets are present on the dashboard.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Add the Confluence Pages gadget twice to a dashboard.   # Configure it to auto-refresh after 15 minutes.   # Add other gadgets to the dashboard.   # Wait for the gadgets to auto-refresh.    h3. Expected Results    The dashboard should update both Confluence Pages gadgets and display the linked pages.  h3. Actual Results    The first gadget does authenticate correctly and updates its contents, but the second gadget instance fails to authenticate.  h3. Workaround    Don't add other gadgets to the same dashboard."""

summary: "Creating a page from a template with macro remove the links on the macros"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    When a template with a macro that links to content is created and, subsequently , creating a page from it. The links are stripped from the macro.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a page template.   # Insert a macro that links to some resource, e.g., an excerpt macro linking to a page. This happens with other macros that link to content as well.   # Save the template and create a page from it or edit it again   # Instead of the linked content and random error is thrown.    h3. Expected Results    Macros show linked content  h3. Actual Results    Nothing is shown on logs. Macro does not have the link to the content. The links are replaced with *unnamed link*.  h3. Workaround    Currently there is no known workaround fro this behaviour. A workaround will be added here when available. """
comments: "Based on your own policy this is a Major severity bug:    As for our case, we have several hundred templates used daily for all our processes. Without links and mentions our team effectiveness is drastically reduced. This must be prioritised. There is no workaround for this. We have over 200 users affected by this." [SEP] "Can confirm our server is facing this issue after upgrading from 6.15.7 to 7.2.1." [SEP] "We can reproduce this issue after upgrading from 7.1.2 to 7.2.1 too" [SEP] "We can reproduce this issue after upgrading from 7.1.2 to 7.2.1 too" [SEP] "We can reproduce it on 7.2.1 on several customer instances.    What is the timeline to resolve this problem?" [SEP] "[https://getsupport.<USER>browse/CSP-267773]"

summary: "Blogpost macro should allow sort by publication date"
description: """We use the Confluence Blogpost macro a lot as that is the first thing people see on our intranet when they open Confluence. To make sure people get the newest content first we want to show them the blogposts sorted by publication date.    Instead we only have the option to sort by _title_ _creation date_ and _modified_ which is not the ordering we want to show.     It's strange that the concept of _publication date_ is very prominent on blogposts but we cannot use it in the Blogpost macro.     That is a major missing feature for us as the other 3 options fail to show us newly published blogposts that have not been modified and created recently."""

summary: "Space categories breaks the recently updated dashboard macro with it unable to render"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Having any space categories will break the *recently updated dashbaord* macro once you click on the *categories* tab. From that point if you refresh the page the macro will not be able to render at all.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Installed new installation 7.0.2   # Create a new space/s   # Add categories to the spaces: Space Tools > Overview   # Create a page   # Add *Recently updated dashboard* macro and publish   # Click on the *space categories* tab of the macro, notice the the following error:       # Refresh page - notice the macro will no longer render at all:      h3. Expected Results    The recently updated macro category tab displays as per normal  h3. Actual Results    Once the recently updated macro category tab has been viewed, the macro is then broken for that user from then on.    The below exception is thrown in the xxxxxxx.log file:      h3. Workaround   # Add a *spaces list* macro to any page - even a new test page. Configure scope to *category*   # Go to a page with an affected *recently updated dashboard* macro and refresh the page   # If it still fails, go the *spaces list* macro page and filter on the affected category (Best to click on every label to refresh them all)   ## Or possibly add another category to a space. This seemed to work on occasion without the need to filter all labels as above.   # Now refresh a page with the affected macro, it should now work.    This somehow triggers a refresh of the categories and the macro should work again."""
comments: "We have the same issue on 7.3.5 version" [SEP] "Same here 7.4.1" [SEP] "Noticed on 7.4.4 also"

summary: "Don't add headings from included files to TOC macro"
description: """h1. observed behavior    Using a third-party macro (specifically, {{Bitbucket file}} from the {{}}[Include Bitbucket add-on|[https://marketplace.<USER>apps/1212529/include-bitbucket-for-confluence?hosting=cloud&tab=support]], I included a Markdown file in a confluence page for reference.    The TOC macro obviously parsed the page AND the included file and now also contains the headings of Markdown file. They are inserted at the respective level (i.e. level 1 of the markdown is also level 1 of the page) and thus break the existing page layout.     Also, the links then don't work.    The only way to work around this is to exclude all headings from the markdown in the TOC macro. But as the Markdown file is obviously edited on Bitbucket, this filtering will not work for long...  h1. steps to reproduce   # create a page with the TOC macro and some headings (best shown if you have mutliple levels)   # include a markdown file with some headings using the {{Bitbucket file}} macro   # Check the generated TOC    h1. expected behavior    The markdown file is not parsed and not included in the TOC, keeping the page layout intact.  h1. suggestion    Either:   * provide a filter to exclude the content of specific macros from being parsed and used in the TOC macro    Or:   * parse the content of the macro and insert it a level below the current header level.    h1. background    I wasn't sure whether to post this as a bug or as a suggestion. Seeing that [creating the TOC from HTML pages (see bullet under """"Notes and known issues""""|[https://confluence.<USER>doc/table-of-contents-macro-182682099.html]] seems to describe a similar but wanted behavior, I guess that having this could also be considered expected behavior.    If you create your page from multiple other pages, this seems fine. But for documentation purposes including markdown files (README.md, mostly) but having them collapsed is something we do quite often. This provides all required info in one place, but having this content show up in the TOC is distracting and creates more confusion.    If required, I'm happy to provide more details and / or  screenshots of a sample page which illustrates the issue."""
comments: "Might be a duplicate of / related to issue CONFSERVER-59222"

Though, if the Space has many pages, individually selecting pages can be a pain.    h3. *Suggestion*    This process of selecting multiple pages can be improved in a couple of ways.  # If the selections of pages can be made once and then saved via a configuration file  #* This will allow users to upload configuration next time and export  # Confluence can remember last selected pages"""

summary: "Display a warning before sharing a page to a lot of people"
description: """Last day it happened that a user wanted to share a Confluence page to a group of some people. He accidentally selected the wrong group and so he shared to > 1,000 people. And all of them received an email notification, which was considered as spam by most of them and so we had a huge discussion about security and how to avoid sending email notification to such a huge number of people. I did not find a solution to limitate email notifications for Confluence.         I know from Outlook, if one tries to send an email to distribution list with more than 100 people, Outlook displays a warning """"are you sure to send to >100 people?"""".         So my feature request is: I'd like to have such a warning also displayed in Confluence when someone tries to share a page to more than 100 people."""

summary: "When mentioning users, the user search flexibility needs to be configurable to find bracketed names"
description: """h3. Problem Definition    When @ mentioning users on a Confluence page, users are not found if bracketed text is entered.    +For example+:   Fullname = 홍길동(Gildong Hong)    Searching for @Gil doesn't find anything, but searching for @(Gil works fine. The administrator does not expect users to add the bracket symbol, as some users are known by english names or nicknames in brackets. Administrators also don't expect users to use the workaround of prefixing bracketed names with [~    For example: [~Gil  h3. Suggested Solution    Relax the fuzzy search distances in the fuze.js plugin to find bracketed usernames, like JIRA currently allows.    Having the user mentions in Confluence work the same way as JIRA would provide a more consistent user search experience across popular Atlassian products.  h3. Workaround    To @ mention names like above, you can either:   a) Include the bracket character as part of the name. Using the example above, @(Gil    or   b) Prefix the bracketed name with [~ For example: [~Gil      """

summary: "Awareness of restricted content"
description: """Restriction feature in Confluence could be enhanced with ability to let users actually see restricted content exist and therefore request access if it seems relevant for them.    Today, restricted content is not visible at all for all users which are not authorized and they are only aware of its existence when they have page URL and they can't open the content. But, in some cases, it will be helpful to directly present links to such content in page tree (ideally with a pad lock in front of Title to directly inform content is indeed restricted for given user), so user can easily request access to content owners.    *Reasons :*   * Broader awareness of restricted content (Spaces or sections are not """"empty"""" or """"missing info"""", users simply don't have access to content due to restrictions)   * Avoidance of restricted content duplicates (Users are recreating similar content thinking it doesn't exist)   * Ease access request as users can directly see content which is indeed restricted for them.   * Ability to distinguish restricted content for limited users (not visible at all) and restricted content for larger audience (links in page tree).    *Limits:*   * It should remain possible to not show restricted content titles in Page tree.     *Look'n feel*    An extra option in current restriction drop down menu.    """"Limited viewing and editing restricted - Everyone can view Titles in Page tree, only some can fully view and edit"""" to be placed 3rd position between """"Editing restricted"""" and """"Viewing and editing restricted"""".     """

This is not the behaviour when we are using pageid instead of its name.**    *Suggested* *behaviour*   * Make Confluence able to accept search parameter when using this type of URL during login"""

Please close them and try upgrade again    This will make admins more aware of the scenario."""

summary: "Set blog post date to the date it was published, not started"
description: """When a new blog is created, by default the publish date goes to when the post/draft was first initiated, rather than the date it actually ends up being published. If the author neglects to change the publish date using the optional field while still in draft, they have no recourse to correct it, which becomes a problem for users who start drafts weeks or months ahead of when they are published. If CONFSERVER-11740 is not going to be implemented any time soon, perhaps this is an alternative solution that may be easier to do. """

summary: "Sandbox should try to gracefully die"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    When the sandbox conversion fails (OOM, timeout, <USER>, the JVM kills the sandbox abruptly instead of killing it gracefully. I suspect the Aspose conversion library uses something like deleteOnExit(), which means that for the temporary font files it creates, no delete method is ever called given how we kill the sandbox so suddenly.    This might have other negative side effects that we don't know about, so we should attempt to kill the sandbox gracefully before we SIGKILL it.  h3. Steps to Reproduce    By looking at the java.io.tmpdir while we kill the Sandbox, we can see the difference between killing gracefully and not killing gracefully:    h3. Expected Results    Files are always cleaned up, even when sandbox crashes.  h3. Actual Results    Not killing gracefully means that Aspose conversion library does not clean up tmp files properly. See above for how to reproduce.  h3. Workaround  The location these tmp files are saved comes from the java.io.tmpdir parameter. A possible workaround for this issue could be to set that to a directory that gets cleaned up regularly by a scheduled job, for example <confluence-home>/temp.  """
comments: "Dear Atlassian team,  we are affected by this and need to do a separate regular check + clean-up to avoid instability on our Confluence DC platform. We'd highly appreciate any kind of fix for this issue.  Best regards  matthias"

A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Embedded documents in a Word document do not get imported to Confluence"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Some Word documents are embedded with other documents such other word documents or powepoint documents.     Importing such Word file into Confluence to be converted to a Confluence page, will not upload these attachments. Instead, these attachments will be displayed as a an image.   h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a Word file that has another embedded Word document.    ## To do this open Word > *Insert > Object*    ## **Select the type of object that will be attached and embed it to the Word document.    # Head over to *Confluence* *>* any space.    # Click *... >>* Import Word Document    # Upload the above word document from *1* and complete the wizard.     h3. Expected Results    The word document will be converted to Confluence page, and the embedded document will be uploaded as an attachment to this page.   h3. Actual Results    The word document will be converted to Confluence page. However, the embedded document do not get uploaded an in its place an image of the embedded document will be uploaded.   h3. summary: "Crowd session timeouts with SSO are ignored by Confluence which also breaks the timeout for other integrated apps"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    When Confluence is integrated with Crowd using SSO, Conlfuence does not respect the session timeout configuration from Crowd and seems to update around every 2mins. If you are only logged into Jira and/or Crowd the timeout works as expected. If you are logged into Conlfuence along with any orther application then the timeout no longer works for any application.   Setting the inteneral Confluence timeout in the web.xml also has no effect. It appears to be related to the *Confluence Quick Reload plugin*.  h3. Steps to Reproduce (Affects multiple verions)   # Install from scratch Crowd 3.4.0 or 3.70 - likely to affect more versions   # Install Conlfuence 6.14.2, 7.1.1 or 7.01 - likely to affect more versions   # Install Jira 7/8+   # Create an application within Crowd for Jira and Confluence   # Add a test user and groups to an internal Crowd directory   # Add your internal Crowd directory to your Crowd applications (Jira/Confluence)   # Set the Crowd session timeout to *5 min*   # Configure the external Crowd user directories in both Confluence and Jira under *User Directories*   # Update *seraph.config.xml* and *crowd.properties* in each application under *<CONFLUENCE INSTALL>/confluence/WEB-INF/classes* & *<JIRA INSTALL>/atlassian-jira/WEB-INF/classes*   ## Leave session validation at 2mins   # Open a different browser and log in as the test user to Jira and minimise   # Wait 7mins (session timeout + session validation interval)   # Navigate or refresh Jira - notice you are logged out of Jira   # Still using the same browser log into Confluence and open Jira to, then minimise the browser   # Wait 7m as above and notice you can refresh or navigate in either Jira or Confluence.     * Updated Conlfuence web.xml set timeout to 10m <session-timeout>10</session-timeout> which still has no affect    h3. Expected Results    All applications respect the session timeout configured within Crowd and automatically logout within the timeout + session validation interval.  h3. Actual Results   * If logged into Confluence the session timeout no longer works for Jira or Crowd and most likely any other integrated SSO app.     * There is nothing immediately of note in the logs, debugging and/or SQL loggin on both Crowd and Confluence plus HAR files. Nothing out of the ordinary.   * It does seem to be related to the *Confleunce Quick Reload Plugin* and/or the *In-App notifications*.   * You will be logged +_eventually_+ but it has not relation to the session timeout configured either in Crowd or Confluence.    {panel:title=Session test timelines}  Jira   11:22 - logged in and minimized   11:28 - maximized and refreshed - session logged off    Confluence   11:29 - logged in and minimized   11:38 - maximized and refreshed - session still there   ---(testing web.xml setting)   11:40 - refreshed and minimized   11:54 - maximized and refreshed - session still there   12:21 - maximized and refreshed - session logged off  {panel}  The customer noticed the following requests which may play a role, many of these requests are made - but only around every 2mins is the session updated within Crowd, whether that is related or not.    h4. Notes    So in Confluence case, both, Crowd and internal timeout on web.xml file were ignored   Jira continues to work as expected so does Crowd as long as you are not logged into Confluence.   Disabling Confluence Quick Reload Plugin results in working Crowd timeout    Whilst this seems Confluence related switching from database vs memory for token storage had no affect either from Crowd.  h3. Workaround   # Disable """"*Confluence Quick Reload Plugin*"""".   # [Disable In-App notifications|https://confluence.<USER>doc/configuring-workbox-notifications-301663830.html]: """"Does not provide in-app notifications"""".   ## Alternatively, you can set the active polling interval greater than the session timeout.    {panel}  These can be disabled from the Universal Plugin Manager (UPM):   * Go to >  !https://confluence.<USER>doc/files/747833194/747833195/2/1517274813281/cog.png! *> Manage Apps >* Filter on *System Installed > Disable as required*   Or set your polling interval greater than the session timeout.{panel}"""
comments: "In our environment the following REST calls cause the problem:    /rest/mywork/latest/status/notification/count?_=  (Confluence In-app Notifications)    /rest/quickreload/latest/0?since=  (Confluence Quick Reload Plugin)    Workaround:    1. Disable In-App notifications: ""Does not provide in-app notifications"". Alternatively, you can set the active polling interval greater than the session timeout.    2. Disable ""Confluence Quick Reload Plugin""."

summary: "Contributors Summary: Additionally allow counting people who only have created (not updated) the page"
description: """We would like to use the Contributors Summary Macro to count how often each person has created pages in a subarea to determine the person in charge to create the next journal (which is the one that has created least pages).    The Contributors Summary Macro seems to be a good fit from first sight, however, we are missing an option for the parameter column to only count created (not updated) pages per user. It should be a minor change from Atlassian's side but would be a great relief for us."""

Instead a following error is returned:  {quote}There was a problem converting this attachment  {quote}    This issue seems to be a regression of the following closed bug report that was fixed in 6.14: CONFSERVER-20223    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Head over to a Confluence page.   # Add a PDF attachment to the page.   # In the Edit mode of the page, head over to the toolbar > Insert > Other Macros > PDF and Insert the macro to the page.    h3. Expected Results    The PDF macro will display the PDF in the page.    h3. Actual Results    An infinite spinning wheel will be displayed and the following error is returned:    The following stack trace appears in the logs:        Example:      or        h5. summary: "Bundle Central License Availability plugin with Confluence"
description: """Confluence currently doesn't have an intuitive way to work with Licensing and Licensed User seats. See this feature request for background on this topic:  https://jira.<USER>browse/CONFSERVER-54271    Atlassian has since released a plugin to help administrators find which users are counting towards a license called the *Central License Availability* plugin: [https://marketplace.<USER>apps/1220745/centralized-license-visibility]    And while the plugin doesn't ultimately solve the UI request (Crowd DC is required to take advantage of that feature), it does remove hurdles for admins who need to pull lists of active users counting towards a license with all types of implentations.     This [popular KB|https://confluence.<USER>confkb/how-to-get-a-list-of-active-users-counting-towards-the-confluence-license-298978076.html] can be used in some situations, however it has major caveats with respect to LDAP nested groups and duplicate users across directories. Both of these caveats can be safely ignored when leveraging the Central Visibility plugin. As a result, we would request that the plugin is bundled with Confluence files moving forward to aid in license management. """

summary: "Trash - Timestamp & date deleted"
description: """The trash does not indicate when a page was deleted. Add a column that shows what day something was deleted to make recovery easier.         The issue is if a page or series of pages are deleted by mistake and you do not know the title, it is hard to track down what was deleted. A timestamp or a date deleted field for each item in the trash would help resolve this because you could track back to the rough time range the pages were deleted and find all the others deleted at the same time."""
comments: "Thanks for raising this issue, <USER>     After comparing this issue against another known report [CONFSERVER-4792 - Display more details for pages in the trash: "who","when","why","what"|https://jira.<USER>browse/CONFSERVER-4792] we realized that both are addressing the same scenario.  As such, We will be closing this request now as a duplicate and will ask you to cast your votes in the original request so we can measure the impact and interest more accurately.    Kind Regards,  Monique  Atlassian Team.    "

summary: "Limiting the ""Restrictions Add/Delete"" permission to own pages"
description: """The space permission """"Restrictions Add/Delete"""" allows users to alter the page restrictions of any page they have access to.   Please implement an additional space permission that allows users to only alter the page restrictions of a page they created themselves. In essence, this would work like the """"Delete own"""" permission.    Currently, it is possible for users to alter the page restrictions of a page created by someone else, without the original author knowing.    This has already been discussed in 2012, with no solution: https://community.<USER>t5/Confluence-questions/I-need-to-restrict-my-restrictions-on-a-page/qaq-p/20744"""
comments: "Hi [~<USER>, Thanks for raising this ticket.    Delete Own permission has been implemented since Confluence 5.10. See:  * https://jira.<USER>browse/CONFSERVER-3542  * https://confluence.<USER>doc/space-permissions-overview-139521.html    Is this ticket requesting any other requirements other than the ""Delete own"" permission? " [SEP] "Hello Monique,    this ticket is not about the ""Delete own"" permission, I only used it as an example. I would like to achive the following: Right now users can be given permission to change the restrictions of _any_ page in a space, or _no_ pages in a space. I would like to be able to give users the permission to only change the restrictions of a page that _they have created_ (i. e. a page they ""own"")."

summary: "LDAP auto-synchronisation stops after a Space Import"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    LDAP directories defined in *Confluence Administration* » *User Directories* stops auto-syncing on the synchronisation interval.    h3. Environment    * Confluence connected to an LDAP directory with a sync interval of 2 minutes    h3. Steps to Reproduce / Diagnose:     # Install Confluence   # Connect Confluence to an LDAP directory with a sync interval of 2 minutes   # Enable the following DEBUG     # Watching {{atlassian-confluence.log}}, it will sync with LDAP every 2 minutes   # Drop the XML Space import zip into the Restore Directory   # Through Confluence Administration, perform the Space Import and immediately close the Administration page as soon as you press *Restore*    (this is just a sample reproducible scenario)    h3. Expected Results    # We see in the logs that Confluence does unschedule the LDAP job but once the Space import is complete, it should re-enable the LDAP scheduled job    h3. Actual Results    # We see this in the log when the LDAP job is unscheduled    # It never gets added back in by Confluence even and hence, the LDAP job no longer syncs on the configured sync interval    h3. Workaround    * Restart Confluence to reset the LDAP job    (i) If Confluence is running as Data Center, shutdown all nodes, then start up each node, one at a time"""
comments: "This has been impacting our Confluence environment for about a year. It doesn't appear to be limited to LDAP as we use a Crowd back-end and have the exact same issue."

summary: "Chrome New Tab Refresh Directs to HTTP"
description: """h3. Problem  After opening a link and refreshing a page by pressing return (enter) on the address bar in Chrome the page refreshes an HTTP address    h3. Environment  * Chrome   * Confluence configured for SSL ([redirecting all HTTP traffic to HTTPS |https://confluence.<USER>doc/running-confluence-over-ssl-or-https-161203.html#RunningConfluenceOverSSLorHTTPS-Step5.AddasecurityconstrainttoredirectallURLstoHTTPS])    h3. Steps to Reproduce  * Open Confluence in Chrome  * Open a Confluence link on a new page (Cmd + Click)  * Refresh the page by putting cursor in the address bar    (i) Recording *failure.mov* attached showing success and failure    h3. Expected Results  * Page refreshes with same HTTPS url successfully    h3. Actual Results  * The page attempts to load using HTTP and fails with """"Bad Request This combination of host and port requires TLS."""" (see *error.png*)    h3. Workaround  * Issue is not reproducible in other browsers  * Refresh using Cmd (ctrl) + R    h3. Notes  * Not all pages are affected, but no clear pattern"""

summary: "Correct 'Assign Space Permissions' documentation"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Confluence's '[Assign Space Permissions|https://confluence.<USER>doc/assign-space-permissions-139460.html]' documentation explains how to manage and recover space admin permissions as below:  {panel}  h2. Manage and recover space admin permissions    If you're a Confluence Administrator you can recover permissions to a space, for example if the only person with Space Admin permissions to a space leaves your organisation.    *To recover Space Admin permissions:*   # Go to    !https://confluence.<USER>doc/files/747833194/747833195/2/1517274813281/cog.png|width=16!  > *General Configuration* > *Space Permissions*.   # Locate the space in the Individual Spaces list and choose *Recover Permissions*.    You can then choose *Manage Permissions*, and add any other appropriate permissions to the space. Requests to recover permissions are recorded in the Confluence audit log.      {color:#ff0000}Users with <USER>permissions are able to manage permissions for all spaces, they do not need to first recover permissions.{color}  {panel}  The paragraph highlighted in red above states an inaccurate information.      In order to be able to *Manage Permissions* for all spaces without needing to first *Recover Permissions*, users need to be members of the *confluence-administrator* group. If an user only has *<USER> permissions, it is still necessary to first recover permissions of a space  h3. How to Improve    Change the paragraph from:  {panel}  Users with <USER>permissions are able to manage permissions for all spaces, they do not need to first recover permissions.  {panel}  To:  {panel}  Users members of the confluence-administrator group are able to manage permissions for all spaces, they do not need to first recover permissions.  {panel}"""

summary: "When a long URL is displayed in Jira Issues Macro, the table is too wide in Chrome/Safari."
description: """h3. Problem Definition    When a long URL is included in a Jira issue and displayed in the Jira Issues Macro, the wrapping behavior makes the table much wider than desired in Chrome/Safari. The table is so wide that users must scroll sideways within the table.    In Firefox, the wrapping behavior is as desired and no sideways scrolling is necessary.    It appears this long URL wrapping issue can be reproduced outside of Atlassian products by putting a long URL into this test site:    [https://www.w3schools.com/cssref/tryit.asp?filename=trycss_text_white-space]    The {{white-space: normal}} property wraps the long URL onto three lines in Firefox:   !GoodWrap_Firefox.png|thumbnail!    The {{white-space: normal}} property wraps the long URL onto just two lines in Chrome:   !BadWrap_Chrome.png|thumbnail!  h4. Behavior in Confluence    Good behavior in Confluence using Firefox:   !GoodWrap_Firefox_Confluence.png|thumbnail!    Bad behavior in Confluence using Chrome:   [^BadWrap_Chrome_Confluence.mp4]  h3. Suggested Solution    Make long URLs wrap in Chrome and Safari at a length that does not make the user need to scroll sideways.  h3. Workaround    View the page in Firefox.    We notice that tables in Confluence are forcing a wrap on the cells based on a percentage or pixel value, which seems to avoid this issue.   (?) Can we take advantage of this fact to get the Jira Issue Macro to behave similarly?   (?) As a workaround, can we make a custom macro or modify the CSS wrap to get the desired behavior?"""

summary: "Attachment History can be removed without Delete Attachment permission"
description: """Today I learned that a user who:  * Has space admin permission  * Does not have delete attachment permission (which they could totally grant themself)    When trying to delete attachmens:  * Cannot delete attachments  * *Can* delete versions of attachments. (including *the last* version of an attachment)    I don't believe gatekeeper discusses this, and I guess effective permissions probably should.    Our attachment documentation correctly documents the above, although it doesn't exactly call it out:    {quote}  Delete an attached file  You'll need the 'Delete Attachment' space permission to delete an attached file.     To delete all versions of an attached file:    Go to the page that contains the attachment.  Go to  > Attachments  Choose Delete next to the attachment you want to delete.  Choose Delete to confirm your action.  Deleted files can be restored from the trash. You'll need to be a space admin to do this.     Space Admins can also delete a specific versions of an attachment:    Go to  > Attachments  Click the expand arrow next to the attachment name to see the list of attachment versions  Choose Delete next to the version you want to delete.  Deleted file versions are not recoverable from the trash.  {quote}    Sidenote for [~<USER>:  """"*a* specific version{*}s*""""    I don't know if anybody cares about this."""
comments: "Thanks [~<USER> for your input. I also think this bug is not in the scope of Gatekeeper."

Information The same Space Export can be imported into Confluence 6.15.1 and the issue is not replicable so this is likely a new bug introduced from Confluence 7 CONFSERVER-18824 was raised to suggest the {{*maxClauseCount*}} to be configurable but the request has been closed. h3. Workaround Refine the filter to have a lesser page involved in the search. It can be done by adding more criteria so the search don't hit the 1024 limit."""
comments: "This bug is affecting me after upgrading to 7.2.1, worked before upgrading (not sure which version was used before). We use the wiki within our production facility for showing instructions to workers.    Because we list children pages (~300) with this macro and there is an error now it is affecting us heavily.    Please fix soon!" [SEP] "One of our customers has the exact same problem as shown above.    The CQL that leads to the error is:    *label = ""safetydatasheet"" and space = currentSpace() and parent = currentContent()*    Even if I go to advanced search and set the parent page there, the issue occurs instead of showing search results.    After editing the CQL to    *label = ""safetydatasheet"" and space = currentSpace() and ancestor = currentContent()*    the page property report (and the search) works as expected and without any problems." [SEP] "Thanks a lot for this very helpful comment!    Works here, too. So fortunately there is a workaround :)" [SEP] "I got issues with the ""parent"" parameter

summary: "Ability to customize color theme via UI in code block macro"
description: """h3. Summary  In the code block macro, it's only possible to add additional languages, but not to add a custom color theme via the UI.    h3. Suggestion  Provide ability to customize theme per color via UI"""
comments: "It would also be okay to define a color scheme via a config file or custom CSS.    For our coding guidelines I want to make ""good code"" blocks green and ""bad code"" blocks red... but there's only light and dark background, no red and green."

summary: "PDF exports of a Page return a Page Not Found after changing the path to the temp directory in confluence.cfg.xml"
description: """h3. Steps to replicate   # *Stop Confluence*   # Change the following property in <confluence-home>/*confleunce.cfg.xml*:    to the following path:       # *Start Confluence*   # Export *a page* to PDF.    h3. Expected Behaviour    Confluence should prompt the PDF export file download.  h3. Actual Behaviour    Confluence return a Page Not Found in the UI pointing tot he following URL:    h3. Notes   * *PDF export file generated at the new location* despite the Page Not Found.   * (i) The issue does not persist for *PDF export of a Space*.    h3. Workaround    No workaround."""

summary: "Preview/Excel macro uses US date format (MM/DD/YYYY) for Excel documents containing dates in European format (DD/MM/YYYY)"
description: """  h3. Issue Summary  Preview/Excel macro uses US date format (MM/DD/YYYY) for Excel documents containing dates in European format (DD/MM/YYYY)      h3. Environment  * Confluence 7.2        h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Create MS Excel document.   # Add date to one of the spreadsheet cells in European Format for example 30.12.2019 or 30/12/2019  # Create new page upload above document to the page.   # Add new Excel macro and by using previously uploaded Excel document   # Save the page  # Preview the page,    h3. Expected Results    Date from the spreadsheet cell is displayed as 30/12/2019        h3. Actual Results  Date from the spreadsheet cell is displayed as 12/30/2019    h3. Workaround   Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available    h3. Additional notes  Above bug occurs regardless of user language profile setting or global language setting in Confluence"""
comments: "This is probably related to, if not the same, as this known issue, open since 2012    https://jira.<USER>browse/CONFSERVER-27237"

summary: "Null is shown in date picker "
description: """h3. Desciption    When using a date picker in a Confluence page in other languages the month is shown incorrectly. It appears to have a null or blank after the month and year.  h3. Environment    Confluence 7.2.0  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Change the profile language to a non US/UK language   # Create a new Confluence page and add a date   # The picker is shown with the month and the incorrect translation or bug    h3. Expected Results    The month and year is shown correctly without any suffix.  h3. Actual Results    In Russian, the month and year is shown as """"Сентябрь 2019нет""""   In German, the month and year is shown as """"September 2019Null""""   In Finnish, the month and year is shown as """"Syyskuu 2019tyhjä""""    !date picker.gif|thumbnail!    h3. Workaround    Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""
comments: "Hi,    I think the suffix comes from the i18n/i18n_*.properties file of the specific confluence-language-pack-*-7.2.0.rc2-20191209053236.jar in confluence/WEB-INF/atlassian-bundled-plugins/. In this file there is a parameter ajs.datepicker.localisations.year-suffix. The value is e.g.   * RU: \u043d\u0435\u0442   * DE: NULL   * FI: tyhj\u00e4   * UK: null    If I change the value to *null* no suffix will be shown." [SEP] "Hi,    i have the same bug in Confluence 7.3.5 btw." [SEP] "Same bug in Confluence 7.4.0" [SEP] "I agree. In the german language pack its also in 7.4.0 the same bug." [SEP] "We are also affected with this bug in Confluence Server 7.5.1, but only in german language pack, in english all is fine." [SEP] "It's so disappointing, that this issue persist since language-pack 7.20.rc2 to confluence-language-pack-de_DE-7.4.0.rc1-20200417033920.jar in Confluence 7.5.2 (LTS)    We loved the translation platform which is shut-down to correct such small problems on ourselves and get a new language-pack on

summary: "Searching of an image in Insert Image and Files filter doesn't return any results if an alphanumeric word is used for search when indexing language is set to Custom Japanese"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Searching of an image in Insert Image and Files filter doesn't return any results if an alphanumeric word is used for search when the indexing language is set to Custom Japanese  h3. Steps to Reproduce  This is only applicable when indexing is set to Custom Japanese  1. Attached files to some pages  e.g ABC001.JPG,ABC002.JPG,ABC003.JPG  2. On a new page  go to insert files and images  3. Search on other pages  4. Search with ABC -> find 3 files.   5. Search with ABC0 -> No search results found.   6. Search with ABC002->No search results are found    h3. Expected Results  The search should return the image name if the alphanumeric text is used  h3. Actual Results  It returns no results      h3. Workaround   Currently, there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Selecting text on a table header row does not work"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Trying to select text from a single Header Row Cell does not work. We need to either select nothing or all header cells.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a page   # Insert a table with header row   # Try to select the text within that header row    h3. Expected Results    Only the desired text is highlighted  h3. Actual Results    No text is highlighted or every header cell is highlighted.         !HeaderTextHighlightServer.gif|width=501,height=189!  h3. Workaround    Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Upgrading with Windows installer fails due to logon user change for Windows service"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  Upgrading with Windows installer fails due to logon user change for Windows service    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Install Confluence 7.1.0 or earlier with Windows installer   # Observe that the logon user for Windows service is *Local System*   !local_system.png|thumbnail!    # Upgrade to Confluence 7.1.1 or later with Windows installer    h3. Expected Results  Upgrade should complete without any issues.    h3. Actual Results  Upgrade does not run and logon user for Windows service is now *Network Service*. Upgrade fails due to user change.    !network_service.png|thumbnail!     {{service.bat}} has hard coded in NetworkService account for user.    {code:title=service.bat}  """"%EXECUTABLE%"""" //IS//%SERVICE_NAME% --ServiceUser """"NT AUTHORITY\NetworkService"""" ^  {code}    The change to *Network Service* was made to fix the vulnerability documented below:  * [CONFSERVER-58808|https://jira.<USER>browse/CONFSERVER-58808]: DLL hijacking in Confluence Server Windows installations    h3. Solution    For fresh installation or upgrades, the *NT AUTHORITY\NetworkService* will be granted the full permission for following folders:  * Installation folder: C:\Program Files\Atlassian\Confluence  * Application data folder: C:\Program Files\Atlassian\Application Data\Confluence    Make sure the *NT AUTHORITY\NetworkService* account is configured on your Windows Server."""
comments: "Hi [~<EMAIL>] and all,    The change in service.bat is expected and described here in this security issue https://jira.<USER>browse/CONFSERVER-58808  We have changed the logon user to *NT AUTHORITY\NetworkService* in order to mitigate the security risk for Confluence instance running as Windows service.    Changing it back as *Local System* account is not expected here in term of security matter    I am closing this ticket now    Thanks,    Minh"

summary: "Internal Server Error (500) on REST API CQL content search for Subpage when the Parent Page does not have a Child Page"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  Internal Server Error (500) on REST API CQL content search for Subpage when the Parent Page does not have a Child Page    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Get a *Page ID* of a Parent Page that does not have any Child Page.  # Request for the Subpage of the Parent Page:      h3. summary: "Importing a space on Confluence Data Center triggers a full site reindex on all nodes in the cluster"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Rebuilding the search index in the UI in Confluence Data Center as explained [in this KB article|https://confluence.<USER>doc/content-index-administration-148844.html] will run the reindex *only* on the node in which the task was triggered.   However, [restoring a Space|https://confluence.<USER>doc/restoring-a-space-152036.html] with """"Build Index"""" checked will trigger a full site reindex on *all* nodes, which can cause heavy load to the database and filesystem.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Go to Confluence Administration > General Configuration > Backup and Restore.   # Under Upload a site or space export file, click Choose File and browse for your space export file.   # Check _Build Index_ box.   # Choose Upload and import.    h3. Expected Results   * The site reindex is expected to be executed only on the node that triggered the space import and not on all nodes simultaneously.   * *If* a full site reindex should run on all nodes, then it should be done in a serial and unparalleled way (one node at a time).    h3. Actual Results   * A full site reindex is triggered on all nodes concurrently.    h3. Workaround    Uncheck """"Build Index"""" and [Rebuild The Content Indexes|https://confluence.<USER>confkb/how-to-rebuild-the-content-indexes-from-scratch-on-confluence-data-center-833941594.html] manually after space import."""
comments: "A fix for this issue is available to Server and Data Center customers in Confluence 7.7.4  [Upgrade|https://www.<USER>software/confluence/download?utm_source=confluence_bugfix&utm_campaign=confluence_bugfix&utm_medium=email] now or check out the [Release Notes|https://confluence.<USER>doc/confluence-7-7-release-notes-1004960930.html] to see what other issues are resolved."

summary: "Team Calendar stylesheet needs to be better scoped"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Team Calendar stylesheets conflicts with stylesheets of some apps like [MultiExcerpt|https://marketplace.<USER>apps/169/multiexcerpt?hosting=server&tab=overview]      h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Confluence v7.0.2  # Install [MultiExcerpt|https://marketplace.<USER>apps/169/multiexcerpt?hosting=server&tab=overview] app  # Install Team Calendar (TC) v6.0.47  # Create a page with 8 *MultiExcerpts*  # Create a new page and add *MultiExcerpt Include* macro  # From the drop-down menu, not all of the 8 excerpts are visible   !cal_enabled.png|thumbnail!   # Disabling the Team Calendar resolved the issue and all 8 excerpts were visible   !cal_disabled.png|thumbnail!     h3. Expected Results    From the drop-down menu, all of the 8 excerpts should visible.   8 is just the threshold number, any excerpts greater than 8 will not be visible.       h3. Actual Results    The drop-down menu should populate the full list of excerpts.    h3. Notes    Analyses of the web-requests with both TC enabled and disabled shows that full data set is always returned from Confluence, though it is not rendered properly if TC is enabled.    The following stylesheet of TC needs to be better scoped, so it change's only it's own behaviour and doesn't conflict with other apps.  {code:css}  .aui-select2-drop.aui-dropdown2.aui-style-default {      max-height: 200px;      overflow-y: hidden;  }  {code}    h3. Workaround    Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

As soon as it's touched in any way, it breaks.  h3. Notes   * Searched atlassian-confluence.log file and found the following:   **        """

summary: "When Chrome requires ""SameSite=None; Secure"" for cross-site cookies, warning messages are displayed in the Developer Tools console"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Starting in February 2020, Chrome version 80 (and later Firefox and IE as well) will start enforcing different requirements on cross-site cookies. Reference [https://blog.chromium.org/2019/10/developers-get-ready-for-new.html] for full details on the change in Chrome's behavior.    Warnings about this can be seen in the Developer Tools console when navigating to pages that have content from an app-linked Jira instance.   h3. Steps to Reproduce   # (prior to the Chrome version 80 rollout) In Chrome version 77+ go to """"chrome://flags/""""   # Search for """"SameSite"""" and enable """"SameSite by default cookies"""" and """"Cookies without SameSite must be secure""""   # In a Confluence instance which has an application link to a Jira instance, navigate to a Confluence page that has integrated content from Jira (gadgets, issue filters, <USER>)     h3. Expected Results    Jira content will be displayed and no warning messages about the cross-site cookies will be displayed in the Developer Tools console.   h3. Actual Results    Jira content is displayed and the below message is displayed in the Developer Tools console:    h3. Workaround    Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""
comments: "Affecting Jira 8.5.4" [SEP] "Our app is affected as well. We are doing an integration of Confluence content in another system and this policy prevents the data to be loaded."

summary: "Email notifications shows entire page instead of just modified changes"
description: """When a page is edited and the """"Notify watchers"""" option is checked in Confluence, the application will send to the page watchers a notification containing a small block of information about what was changed on that page.    However, if the page is complex, split into multiple sections, the notifications from that page will contain the entire page content and not only the small notification block of what changed.    h4. Expected results    Confluence should send small notification blocks nevertheless the page size or complexity."""
comments: "This has the same root cause as CONFSERVER-59279 and so they will be tracked as one ticket."

summary: "Editing Meeting Notes template affects the way bulleted list is displayed during editing"

Expected Results    The name of the attendees should remain on the same line as the bullets of the list  h3. Actual Results    The name of the attendees move to a new line  h3. Workaround    Publishing the page and editing again show the bulleted list correctly"""

summary: "Clicking on ""Expand All"" in Page Tree macro while tree is expanded causes the links to disappear"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    The following is occurring when using the Page Tree Macro:  * Clicking on """"Collapse all"""" while the tree is Collapsed causes the links to disappears  * Clicking on """"Expand all"""" while the tree is Expanded in Page tree macro causes the links to disappear  * If there are no child pages, both links are hidden.    The *finishLoadingMessage()* is not called and the status is not cleared, causing the links to be hidden.    Please see attached video.    h3. Steps to Reproduce     # Insert Page Tree macro   # Edit its properties and enable """"Show Expand/Collapse Links"""">> save   # Click on Expand All while the tree is expanded, or Collapse all while the tree is collapsed    or     # Insert Page Tree macro  # Edit its properties and enable """"Show Expand/Collapse Links"""">> save  # Add a page, but no child pages  # Click on Expand All or Collapse all    h3. Expected Results    The links shouldn't disappear and should work as expected if clicked while the tree already expanded or collapsed, or whether there are child pages are not.    h3. Actual Results    Links disappear or become text, see attached video    h3. Workaround    There is no workaround at this moment"""
comments: "Affects 7.3.3 too" [SEP] "7.4.x LTS is also affected."

summary: "Personal space URL redirects to the user profile page"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    URLs with the format <base url>/display/~username are displaying the user profile page, instead of taking you to the personal space.       h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Visit <base url>/display/~username  for a user in your site that has a personal space    h3. Expected Results    The personal space displays.     h3. Actual Results    Displays the profile page for the user.      !image-2019-12-19-18-16-36-717.png|thumbnail!     h3. Workaround    To access a personal space, either append a page title to the end of the URL, e.g. <base url>/display/~username/home  or use the format <base url>/spaces/viewspace.action?key=~username"""

summary: "PDF Export of a space fails with TOC should be replaced error"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    PDF export is failing when you include all child pages of the home page in an export.     h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Export space to PDF > choose custom, and make sure the homepage and all child pages are selected.  Omit any pages at the root.     h3. Expected Results    Space is exported with no problems.     h3. Actual Results    Space export fails with the following error:    {panel}TOC should be replaced only with TOC of the same size. It is important because TOC size will affect TOC content (page offsets). This is a bit of chicken and egg problem, which makes us generate TOC twice.{panel}     !image-2019-12-19-17-47-58-721.png|thumbnail!     h3. Workaround  Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Confluence ""attachment updated"" flag doesn't show for referenced attachments"
description: """*Current behaviour*    Confluence will show a flag indicating that attachments on the page were updated. However, this only works for attachments that are """"owned"""" by (directly attached to) the page. If the page is referencing an attachment from another page, and that attachment is updated, the notification flag doesn't show.     *Expected behaviour*    The attachment update notification flag should when referenced attachments are updated. """

Issue Summary    The [Confluence Source Editor|https://marketplace.<USER>apps/1210722/confluence-source-editor] version 2.0.0 plugin when installed in Data Center is not available when an user is editing a template on *Space Tools > Content Tools > Create New Template*  h3. If you try to edit the template, there'll be no page editor available.   !screenshot-2.png|thumbnail!   # The same behavior is *not* observable if you create a new page: when you're first creating a page, the page editor does not appear, which is expected:   !screenshot-3.png|thumbnail!   # But, when you edit the page, you have the page editor available:   !screenshot-4.png|thumbnail!    h3. summary: "Comparing page versions or email diffs of pages with sections shows the entire page - not just changes"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Comparing page versions with sections compares the entire page instead of just changes    This also affects email notifications that show the difference of what has changed in a page between versions  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a page with multiple sections   # Add large amount of texts, a few pages worth   # Edit the page somewhere in the middle   # Compare the published version with the current one   # Notice the entire page is displayed and does not truncate large chunks of unchaged text as expected into •••    h3. Expected Results    Large portions of unchanged text are hidden or truncated into ellipses •••  h3. Actual Results    The entire page is shown for comparison making it harder to find individual changes.  h4. Notes    Taken from our KB [page history & comparison|https://confluence.<USER>doc/page-history-and-page-comparison-views-139379.html]:  {quote}When you view a page comparison, all large sections of unchanged text are hidden and reduced to an ellipsis (. . .).  {quote}  Which is true for pages without sections. If you use the same amount of text on a page with out sections, it will hide the large portions of text unchanged showing you only the modifications.  h3. Workaround    Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""
comments: "Dear Atlassian team    We would appreciate if you could increase the priority of this issue significantly.    Imagine you are searching for changes on confluence pages of just a few printed pages in size. As a result of this issue, it will take you significantly longer to find any changes. This affects productivity of all people using Confluence in our organisation and easily translates to hundreds of hours wasted, even thousands over years.    We are particularly hit, because we started to work with page layouts using sections in almost all our templates.... something that has spread out too far to step back from in our organisation.    Please review the priority, try to understand that the lack for a proper diff capability can become a showstopper and impact Confluence adoption dramatically in organisations like ours.     And please don't have us ask all our confluence users to vote for this issue. Atlassian's approach for identifying priorities is sometimes challenging the purpose of the support offering

summary: "Allow Image set to Original size that is placed in Responsive Table to fit in browser size"
description: """h3. Information    Setting the size of the attached image to """"Original"""" will allow the image to scale(resize) automatically to fit the screen browsing Confluence (eg: Browser) in the case if the browser size is smaller than the image.    Same goes to placing an image with the size set to """"Original"""" _inside a table_ with *Fixed Width*, the image will scale(resize) according to the width of the table that is set by the editor.  h3. Problem Definition    However, placing an image with the size set to """"Original"""" _inside a table_ with *Responsive* width will cause the _responsiveness_ of the table to scale up its border to the actual Original image size, ignoring the *browser size* and showing a horizontal scroll bar in the table, which can break the experience of displaying a large sized image in a Responsive table.    (i) The behavior described above can be seen in the videos below:   * [^Original Image Size.mp4]   * [^Image Scale vs Table Scale.mp4]   ** Image that is used in the test: [^atlassian logo2.png]    h3. Suggested Solution    Allowing both the Table and Image to be _responsive_ to the *browser size* so everything can be shown in the screen without scrolling"""

summary: "Uploading attachment (via file browser on Attachment macro) on a page with Team Calendar macro results in misleading error message"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  Misleading error message ((!) _Error(s) occurred_) would be shown on the Confluence UI when trying to upload an attachment (via file browser) with the Attachment macro, on a page with Team Calendar macro embedded to it.     h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Add a Team Calendar macro *and* Attachment macro to a Confluence page.  # Attempt to upload a file via the Attachment macro using the file browser option.  # Notice a misleading error thrown in the Confluence UI.  (i) Should you reload the page, notice that the attachment is indeed being uploaded successfully to the page.   (i) The same issue is not reproducible via drag and drop option.    h3. Expected Results  The attachment is uploaded successfully without any error message.    h3. Actual Results  The attachment gets uploaded successfully (only listed on the page after the page is reloaded) with a misleading error message thrown in the UI.   !Screen Shot 2019-12-13 at 2.34.00 PM.png|thumbnail!     h3. Workaround  Upload the attachment via the Attachment macro using the drag and drop option."""
