summary: "'Create' dialog in Confluence not showing template options but spinning load graphic when attempting to create page from Jira reports"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Select _Create Page_ from a Jira report results in _Create_ dialog in Confluence not rendering and just showing spinning loading graphic, when Confluence setup for anonymous access but no spaces have anonymous access.  h3. Steps to Reproduce    From confluence   - enable _Anonymous Access_ from Global Permissions   - under Space Permissions ensure no spaces have _anonymous access_ to viewing spaces and adding pages   - Ensure user is not currently authenticated on Confluence server   - Log in to Jira and attempt to create a page from a report that has the option (eg sprint report)    h3. Expected Results   - The _Create_ dialog is populated with templates to create a page from    h3. Actual Results   - The dialog doesn’t complete loading showing just a spinning _loading_ graphic    h3. Workaround   - disable general anonymous access   - enabling anonymous access to at least one space   - pre-authenticate to confluence prior to attempting to create a page         *Notes*    HAR file shows the returned _promotedSpaces_ JSON text has an empty array for the _spaces_ list    As opposed to a working system which would show like    This is what appears to stop the dialog from being populated with templates"""

summary: "Questions for Confluence Multiple Emails"
description: """When a user attaches images to a question submitted in """"Questions for Confluence"""", separate emails go out for every image and file separately. So the users receive a lot of unnecessary emails. These should be grouped together in a single email. Really any changes that a user makes within a couple of minutes should be grouped together in a single email message like upvoting and accepting answers.    !image-2019-12-11-16-33-21-819.png!    !image-2019-12-11-16-34-07-695.png!     """

summary: "Removing Outlook or Google sync from a Team Calendar should remove events in TC"
description: """As a user, the usage and result of synchronization can be confusing, and is not consistent with most other calendar apps.    The current integration works beautifully, but relies on humans to never err when selecting a calendar to sync with.     A simple mistake may popolate TC with hundreds of unwanted events.    Worse, there is no API nor any other bulk update method for TC.    When important company calendars are impacted, especially those which already contained many events, they cannot simply be deleted and recreated. The only recourse may be manually deleting each event.    For all other calendar clients users are familiar with, removing the sync setup removes the events, however, this is NOT true with Team Calendars.    TC should drop events copied from external calendars when the synchronization is removed.     """

summary: "Panel Macro with text creates blank pages in PDF Export"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  A certain amount of text within *Panel macro* will create blank pages upon PDF export before and after the macro    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a Test Confluence page   # Insert a Panel macro   # Add 29 or 30 new lines of text to macro   (i) Number of lines is not a rule, there should be enough content to fit into a single page upon export to PDF, it can be paragraphs, lines, or just wall of text  You can use  [^30 lines in panel.txt] storage format as a test   # Export page to PDF    h3. Expected Results   * Upon export, you should get 1 or 2 pages depending on the amount of content on the page    h3. Actual Results   * A blank page will appear before and/or after the panel macro upon export, different amount of lines/text creates a different effect   ** 28 lines result in correct export   ** 29 lines adds a blank page before panel macro   ** 30 lines adds a blank page before and after panel macro   ** 31 lines result in correct export    h3. Workaround  Adding the following to *PDF Stylesheet* results in correct page break within the *Panel macro*  """

summary: " Confluence Server and Data Center - Atlassian Companion Man-in-the-Middle - CVE-2019-15006"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    There was a man-in-the-middle (MITM) vulnerability present in the Confluence Previews plugin in Confluence Server and Confluence Data Center. This plugin was used to facilitate communication with the Atlassian Companion application. The Confluence Previews plugin in Confluence Server and Confluence Data Center communicated with the Companion application via the atlassian-domain-for-localhost-connections-only.com domain name, the DNS A record of which points at 127.0.0.1. Additionally, a signed certificate for the domain was publicly distributed with the Companion application. An attacker in the position to control DNS resolution of their victim could carry out a man-in-the-middle (MITM) attack between Confluence Server (or Confluence Data Center) and the atlassian-domain-for-localhost-connections-only.com domain intended to be used with the Companion application. This certificate has been revoked, however, usage of the atlassian-domain-for-localhost-connections-only.com domain name was still present in Confluence Server and Confluence Data Center. An attacker could perform the described attack by denying their victim access to certificate revocation information, and carry out a man-in-the-middle (MITM) attack to observe files being edited using the Companion application and/or modify them, and access some limited user information.    h3. Affected Versions  Versions of Confluence Server and Confluence Data Center affected by this vulnerability:  * from 6.11.0 before 6.13.10 (fixed in 6.13.10)  * from 6.14.0 before 6.15.10 (fixed in 6.15.10)  * from 7.0.1 before 7.0.5 (fixed in 7.0.5)  * from 7.1.0 before 7.1.2 (fixed in 7.1.2)    EAP and beta versions: 7.2.0-rc2, 7.2.0-rc1, 7.2.0-beta1, 7.1.0-rc3, 7.1.0-rc2, 7.1.0-rc1, 7.1.0-beta1, 7.0.1-rc1, 7.0.1-beta2, 7.0.1-beta1, 6.15.0-rc1, 6.15.0-beta2, 6.15.0-beta1, 6.14.0-rc1, 6.14.0-beta1, 6.13.0-rc1, 6.13.0-beta1, 6.12.0-rc1, 6.12.0-beta1, 6.11.0-rc1, 6.11.0-beta1    h3. Fixed Versions  h4. Confluence Server  Version 7.2.0 available at https://www.<USER>software/confluence/download  Version 7.1.2 available at https://www.<USER>software/confluence/download-archives  Version 7.0.5 available at https://www.<USER>software/confluence/download-archives  Version 6.15.10 available at https://www.<USER>software/confluence/download-archives  Version 6.13.10 available at https://www.<USER>software/confluence/download-archives    h4. Confluence Data Center  Version 7.2.0 available at https://www.<USER>software/confluence/download/data-center  Version 7.1.2 available at https://www.<USER>software/confluence/download-archives  Version 7.0.5 available at https://www.<USER>software/confluence/download-archives  Version 6.15.10 available at https://www.<USER>software/confluence/download-archives  Version 6.13.10 available at https://www.<USER>software/confluence/download-archives    h3. Notes  For additional details, see the full advisory: https://confluence.<USER>doc/confluence-security-advisory-2019-12-18-982324349.html"""
comments: "This is an independent assessment and you should evaluate its applicability to your own IT environment. CVSS v3 score: 5.8 => Medium severity    *Exploitability Metrics*  || Attack Vector | Network |  || Attack Complexity | High |  || Privileges Required | None |  || User Interaction | Required |    *Scope Metric*  || Scope | Changed |    *Impact Metrics*  || Confidentiality | Low |  || Integrity | Low |  || Availability | Low |    https://asecurityteam.bitbucket.io/cvss_v3/#CVSS:3.0/AV:N/AC:H/PR:N/UI:R/S:C/C:L/I:L/A:L" [SEP] "A fix for this issue is available to Server and Data Center customers in Confluence 7.0.5  [Upgrade|https://www.<USER>software/confluence/download?utm_source=confluence_bugfix&utm_campaign=confluence_bugfix&utm_medium=email] now or check out the [Release Notes|https://confluence.<USER>doc/confluence-7-0-release-notes-973479448.html?utm_source=confluence_bugfix&utm_campaign=confluence_bugfix&utm_medium=email] to see what other issues are resolved." summary: "Upgrade to Postgres 10 on AWS RDS Confluence can't find tables "
description: """h3. Issue Summary    This ticket is used to track a bug on RDS - after Postgres 10 upgrade, Confluence may fail to start.    [https://dba.stackexchange.com/questions/237359/aws-rds-postgresql-master-user-lost-all-privileges-after-upgrading-from-v9-6]       When ugprading to Postgres 10 on AWS RDS, Confluence can't find start, will errors 'bandana table does not exist'.    It's because after upgrade, public schema lost access privileges.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Connect to postgres sever (psql)   # connect to confluence database by \c confluence   # list out schemas \dn+    h3. Expected Results   # rdsadmin user has access privileges      h3. Actual Results   # access privileges are lost.      h3. Workaround    Grant schema access to confluence user.  """

summary: "Excerpt include macros that have tables insert a blank line space above macro"
description:
"""h3. Issue Summary Excerpt include macros that have tables insert a blank line space above macro h3. Steps to Reproduce # Create an _excerpt_ macro 1 line break below a heading with tables. (Shift+Enter *not* Enter) # Add an excerpt include macro 1 line break below a heading. # Notice the extra whitespace above the excerpt include macro h3. Expected Results * Macro looks and behaves the same as the _excerpt_ macro h3. Actual Results * A blank line


is inserted above the macro. This means it's impossible to replicate the look of the excerpt macro when trying inlcude below a header on a line space and not a new paragraph. You should be able to replicate the look of the _excerpt_ macro when using the _excerpt include_ macro. h3. Workaround * You can manually remove the spacing using the Confluence Source Editor"""

summary: "Add named user roles to space and page permissions"
description: """Jira supports named user roles for projects, which helps simplify the process of assigning permissions to different users. With so many different permissions on Confluence spaces, it gets annoying having to copy the relevant permissions to each user or group based on how that user should use the space. Then, when the names are all sorted alphabetically, it's hard to tell who plays what role within the space: who are the """"admins"""", who are the """"editors"""", who are the """"viewers"""", <USER>    I would like to ask that a new named role feature be added to Confluence so that I can manually create roles for a given space, such as """"Viewer"""" or """"Editor"""", assign permissions to that role, then assign roles to users. It would also follow that the same roles for a space be available for restricted pages withing the space, effectively acting like a custom group available only within that space. This feature could exist alongside the existing permissions structure, in case people prefer the current approach.    A workaround is to create a global group within Confluence (or the upstream directory server(s)), e.g. """"Custom Space XYZ Editors"""" and assign permissions to that group, but that requires admin input and pollutes the global group namespace. Instead, named roles within a space's permissions would not have either of these problems."""

summary: "Show as link button doesn't work on Microsoft Edge"
description: """h2. Issue Summary  One can attach a document to a Confluence page and select between displaying the attachment as a thumbnail or as a link. By default, a thumbnail is displayed and one would have to click on it then select *Show as link* to display a link instead of a thumbnail.    When attempting to do that on Microsoft Edge, the operation enters an endless loop and attempts to display the file instead of converting the thumbnail to a link. Therefore, one is unable to convert attachments from thumbnails to links using Edge.    h2. Steps to Reproduce  # Create a page.  # Attach any Office file to it.  # Save the page.  # At this point, the attachment should be displayed as a thumbnail.  !Screen Shot 2019-12-09 at 12.58.39.png|thumbnail!  # Edit the page.  # Click the thumbnail and click on *Show as link*.  !Screen Shot 2019-12-09 at 12.23.21.png|thumbnail!    h4. Expected Results  Thumbnail is converted to link as can be seen below and one is able to save the page changes:  !Screen Shot 2019-12-09 at 12.23.41.png|thumbnail!    h4. Actual Results  Edge actually opens the document contents and display them:  !image.png|thumbnail!    Once you close the preview you can save changes, but nothing really change as Edge is unable to convert the attachment to a link.    h2. Workaround  Not exactly a workaround, but use any other browser instead of Edge."""
comments: "We are using confluence 6.13.9 DC and we are affected by this bug when using edge (latest stable version)."

summary: "'Stop Watching' does not work for Pages from Spaces the user no longer has permissions"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    If a user no longer has permissions to a Space, he cannot stop watching specific pages from that space, as the link in the *Watches* pages does not work.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Using an Admin account, create new space and a Page called """"Sample"""" within the space;  ** Ensure the User account has View permissions on the new Space;   # Logout and login with the User account;  # Open the """"Sample"""" page from new space and click on *Watch* > *Watch page*;  # Logout and login with the Admin account;  # Open the new space *Permissions* page and remove permissions for the User;  # Logout and login with the User account;  # Click on the profile picture . *Watches*;  # The """"Sample"""" page is listed in the *Page Watches* section  # Click on *Stop Watching*  ** Link becomes *Start Watching*  # Refresh the page  ** Link reverts back to *Stop Watching*    h3. Expected Results    The page should be removed from the list when clicking *Stop Watching*. Optionally the Page watch should be removed when removing the User's space permission (this is what happens to the Space watch).    h3. Actual Results    The page watch is not removed when removing the User's space permissions nor when clicking *Stop Watching*.    h3. Workaround    A Space admin can remove the user's page watch via the [*Manage Watchers*|https://confluence.<USER>doc/manage-watchers-218271870.html] dialog."""

summary: "Add customizable ""privacy"" and ""imprint"" Links in Footer"
description: """The European GDPR forces every platform provider of internet applications to provide proper privacy and imprint information individually on that platform.    We encountered that there is no easy way in Confluence to provide that Information.   As you as software provider need to keep us as customer your might assist us keep Confluence running by providing at least a simple ability to set custom """"privacy"""" and """"imprint"""" links on every Confluence site (I think in that footer where also """"about Confluence"""" and """"Report a Problem"""" and so on is linked is a good place). We could create websites to present this information.    A better approach would be to have the gdpr/privacy/imprint information to be customizable in Confluence admin interface and simply klickable. But afaik this might be a bigger change."""

Issue 1  # Create several Blogs in SpaceA  # Load PageA again  (!) Issue 2  # Edit PageOne in order to edit the Blog Posts macro  #* Set the {{Content Type to Display}} option to *entire*  (!) Issue 3    h3. Expected Results  h4. Issue 1 & 2 & 3  Table of Contents macro should not show any headings related to the Blog Posts macro    h3. Actual Results  h4. Issue 1  The """"Blog stream"""" within the Blog Posts macro is shown in ToC macro section:   !Issue 1.png|thumbnail!     h4. Issue 2  The """"Blog Posts"""" within the Blog Posts macro is shown in ToC macro section:   !Issue 2.png|thumbnail!     h4. summary: "Documentation regarding external process pool need to be updated "
description: """h3. Problem Definition    Some documentation regarding external process pool needs to be updated for clarification.   * [External Process Pool for Confluence Data Center|https://confluence.<USER>doc/external-process-pool-for-confluence-data-center-958779098.html]   This page doesn't refers to *page* *export* to PDF, which is also in scope of sandbox processing.   * [Startup check: available memory|https://confluence.<USER>confkb/startup-check-available-memory-951405871.html]   This page introduces JVM parameter {{conversion.sandbox.disable}}, which won't work actually.    h3. Suggested Solution    The former page should be described as below.  {quote}The external process pool currently handles the following actions:   * Document conversion (thumbnail generation for file previews)   * *Exporting a page to PDF*   * Exporting a space to PDF{quote}  The latter should be as below.  {quote}For Confluence 6.12 and later, set *pdf.export.sandbox.disable* and *document.conversion.sandbox.disable* system property to true and both document conversions and PDF export will be handled by Confluence.  {quote}  Note. [Recognized System Properties|https://confluence.<USER>doc/recognized-system-properties-190430.html] introduces the above parameters and they work.    h3. Workaround    This is just a documentation fix. So no workaround needed."""
comments: "Documentation has now been updated. "

summary: "Allow linking of copied pages so that they stay in sync"
description: """I would like to have the same document exist in multiple spaces, and to have changes made in any location propagate to all locations. Thanks!"""

summary: "Show space names on the 'All updates' page"
description: """At the moment, the 'All updates' Dashboard page provides a useful overview of recent changes to the wiki.    However, any large wiki tends to end up with:    1) Identical page names in multiple spaces and    2) Page names that are ambiguous unless you know which space they are in.    For example - a few that I can see right now: 'System tests', 'Incidents', 'Meetings'. This makes it hard to read the 'All updates' page and get a good understanding of what's changing (unless you happen to know which team the author works in). To improve this, I suggest *adding the space name alongside the title of the updated page*.         And hey, there's plenty of horizontal space!    !image-2019-12-05-14-30-56-800.png!         I believe this is the default page that users see when opening a Confluence wiki, so it has sense to make it good! However, the same issue can be seen on other, less-important pages - such as 'Popular', 'Recently worked on' and 'Recently visited'."""

summary: "Pages get incorrect background when browser window is narrow"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Many pages in Confluence have a display issue when the contents don't fit in the width of the browser window (a horizontal scroll bar is added) since the white background does not extended to the page full width.    h3. Steps to Reproduce     # Open the *Profile* page (via the profile picture);   # Reduce the width of the browser windows until an horizontal scroll is displayed;   # Open the *Space Directory* page;   # Open the *Manage Apps* Admin page.    h3. Expected Results    All pages are displayed correctly, with the white background covering all the contents in the right areas.    h3. Actual Results    The page contents to the right don't have a white background, thus looking to be out of place.     !Profile.png|thumbnail!      !SpaceDirectory.png|thumbnail!     The *Manage Apps* page is correctly displayed as the white background extends to the full width of the contents.     !ManageApps.png|thumbnail!     h3. Notes    The issue has been observed in versions 6.13.x, 6.15.x and 7.1 of Confluence using Firefox and Chrome browsers.    It seems to affect most pages that have non-resizable content (tables, images, title) that is wider than the available width of the browser window, such as (in addition to the ones mentioned above):  * *Page History*  * The different pages in *Space tools*  * Any space page with wider content, in particularly tables    The pages in the *Administration* console, such as *Manage Apps* mentioned above, are correctly displayed, even the one containing wide tables.    h3. Workaround    Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""
comments: "Good morning Bruno,    Thank you for adding my name on it as reporter.     Is there something i could do to help you solve this bug? Or just stay tuned and..?          Thank you,    Have a great day" [SEP] "Hi [~<USER>,    Thank you for opening this ticket to bring attention to this issue. There is a specification for the minimum window size for Confluence to work, and it is known that parts of Confluence won't display correctly if your browser window size is less than 1024x768. This information is available in the [Supported Platforms|https://confluence.<USER>doc/supported-platforms-207488198.html] page in the Browsers > Good to know section.    I will close this ticket by now but feel free to reopen it if there is extra information to be taken into account for a future change.    Cheers"

summary: "Line wrapping doesn't work on Preformatted Text inside a Info Macro"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  If the text style inside the Info Macro is _Preformatted_, then the line wrapping shows correctly in the Edit mode but doesn’t work in the View/Published mode    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Create a page with an Info Macro  # Put a long piece of text inside the macro  # Format the Text as Preformatted  # Publish the Page    h3. Expected Results  The text should be wrapped within the macro in the View mode    h3. Actual Results  Text overrides the macro limit      h3. Workaround  Use any format other than Preformatted to render text correctly    """

summary: "Find and replace is broken in editor mode in Firefox"
description: """h3. Steps   # Open editor using Firefox   # Use Cmd+F or Ctrl+F shortcut to open find and replace   # Search for a word that is repeated more than once in the editor    *What happened...*    Only one occurrence is highlighted. Pressing next does not move on to the next occurrence.    *What should have happened...*   It should find the all the occurrences of the search keyword as you click next."""

summary: "Restrictions don't work as expected when containing disabled users/groups"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  * If there are disabled users/groups listed in a page's restrictions, the restrictions won't be applied as expected    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Add restrictions to a Confluence page  # Disable one of the users/groups added to the page's restrictions  # With an enabled user, try to access the page to view/edit it    h3. Expected Results  * The enabled user will be able to view/edit the restricted page, regardless of the other users/groups restrictions    h3. Actual Results  * The restrictions are inconsistent and not being applied correctly due to the disabled user/group    h3. Notes   * The query below should return some details on the users/groups that are mentioned in the restrictions of the affected page, allowing users to identify if there are disabled users/groups in the restrictions of it:      h3. Workaround  * Remove the disabled user/group of the page's restrictions"""

summary: "Need default text colour ""#172b4d"" on the color option in the page editor"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    The text color option is missing the default text color, so the text color cannot be changed back to default once it has been changed.    The default text color has been changed from black to dark blue(#172b4d) on the recent version, but the default color does not exist on the color option. So, if the text color has been changed, there is no way to go back to the default color.   !color-option.png|thumbnail!   h3. Environment    All the environment.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Install Confluence 6.14.3 or higher   # Check the default text color is dark blue(#172b4d)   # Confirm the color option does not have the same color.    h3. Expected Results    The text color option should have the default color. So, the text can be changed back to the default color.  h3. Actual Results    The default text color does not exist on the color option.  h2. *Workaround*    The text color can be changed back to default if the same color selected again. For example, if the text color has been changed to *Black*. To revert back to the default color, the same *Black* color can be selected again."""
comments: "You can revert to the default color of font by using *More > Clear formatting* option." [SEP] "How about just making the default color black? Changing all text to blue was a terrible choice."

summary: "Ability to show/hide custom field IDs to field names"
description: """As a Jira power user, I need to ability to show/hide custom field IDs when choosing fields to add to columns, as well as in the column headings, so that I can differentiate between fields of the same name.    We are a large enterprise with several thousand fields, many of which have the same name (even though they're different field types). We need the ability to differentiate easily by custom field ID (and by field type if possible) when trying to select fields to include as columns in the Issue List view. Additionally we would like the ability to display the field IDs next or under the field names in the header row, e.g.: Field (ID). Each user should have the show/hide option for this."""

summary: "Attachments can't be inserted when creating blog post or page, using the Multimedia macro"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    When creating a blog post or page, before saving it, you may want to attach and insert a multimedia file (not an image file). After uploading the file, and trying to insert the multimedia macro, the uploaded file won't show up on the file list, and can't be included on the page, until the blog is posted.    Subsequent editing (after the blog post or page is first submitted) allows the multimedia macro to find the uploaded file with no other changes.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Make sure Collaborative Editing is *enabled*   # Create a new Blog Post or Page   # Upload a multimedia file (such as a .mp4 video file)   # Insert the Multimedia Macro    h3. Expected Results    The newly uploaded file would be available on the Multimedia Macro file list, and can be inserted on the page  h3. Actual Results    The file list on the Multimedia Macro will be empty  h3. Notes    This error only occurs with Collaborative Editing enabled.    It also affect the [View File macros|https://confluence.<USER>doc/view-file-macro-170494360.html]; if you replace the type of file and the macro in the steps to reproduce above, you get the same result.  h3. Workaround    There are two possible workarounds to be able to insert multimedia files successfully, on blog posts:   * Save the Blog Post or Page first. After editing the blog post or page, the uploaded multimedia files will show up on the macro's file list.   * [Disable Collaborative Editing|https://confluence.<USER>doc/administering-collaborative-editing-858772086.html#AdministeringCollaborativeEditing-Changetheeditingmode], as this issue does not occur when it's turned off"""
comments: "[~<USER>, can you confirm if this error happens only on blog posts or does it also happen on other pages?" [SEP] "This same error affects the [View File macros|https://confluence.<USER>doc/view-file-macro-170494360.html] (using similar reproduction steps) and happens also when creating new pages, not just when creating new blog posts." [SEP] "We are also affected when publishing our companies news."

Is ""it's a bug"" an excuse to NOT fix this within a timeframe? We are a paying customer, and this is a problem."

summary: "Updating a page via REST API causes 501 error after upgrading to Confluence 7+ due to contentproperties table modification"
description: """{panel:title=Atlassian Update - Feb 2020}  Hi All,    We have prepared a fix for this issue and we have made this fix available in the Confluence EAP release 7.4.0-m05 and in Confluence 7.3.3.     We went through all cases reported for this issue (at least all we were able to reproduce), and fixed it by introducing a new recovery mechanism for unreconciled page updates. Recovery should happen transparently for all callers, so the API should behave as it used to before 7.0 (but now without danger of losing unpublished changes). Please give it a try and provide feedback.    On a side note please keep in mind, that in rare cases ExternalChangesException can still happen in the case where content update fails for some reason, so please don't forget to handle it appropriately. The fix also includes changes to solve the problem with visibility of ExternalChangesException class in the plugins and changes to the confusing exception message.    Lastly, if you need more fine access over the content reconciliation process, please consider using PageUpdateService. Its prepare() method can be used to ensure that content and draft are synchronized and are ready for updates. Methods of this service can be called within as well as outside of the active database transaction.    Thank you for your patience and we look forward to receiving your feedback on this fix.    Regards,    The Confluence Team  {panel}  h3. Issue Summary    Updating a page via REST API causes 501 error after upgrading to Confluence 7+ the first time it's called.   The propertyname = 'sync-<USER>source' or equivalent from the table *contentproperties* is replaced with the value *restored* in all rows.   Viewing the page or making the call again will succeed as it sets the '*propertyname*' back to '*synchrony*' or '*synchrony-ack*' as expected.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Install Confluence 6.15.9 and make sure the Demonstration Space was created (You can create other pages for testing)   # Run the following query:       #    ## Make sure that none of the properties is set to 'restored'. They should be either 'synchrony-ack' or 'synchrony' as described on [How do drafts work|https://confluence.<USER>confkb/how-do-drafts-work-on-confluence-938043306.html].   # Upgrade Confluence to 7.1.0   # Make sure not to view any of the pages   # Execute the same query again. You should be able to see that every property was set to 'restored':       # Pick one of the rows with 'restored' value and it's contentId and execute following REST       # Notice the following response:      h3. Expected Results    The page is updated accordingly.  h3. Actual Results    A 501 error is received and the page is not updated.  h3. Workaround    Making the call again or viewing the page will allow it to work as it sets the _stringval_ in *contentproperties* back to synchrony or synchrony-ack.   * 7.0.2 - Disabling/enabling collaborative editing clears the 'sync-<USER>source' values and allows the calls to work. This method *does not* work for 7.1.0.    h4. Notes    Known to cause issues with some plugins like K15 *Scroll Versions* when trying to publish pages, as the same error is thrown when using the JAVA API methods {{saveContentEntity()}} and {{saveNewVersion()}} from the {{com.atlassian.confluence.pages.DefaultPageManager}} class.   Calling again the methods *does not* *always* work in setting the _stringval_ in *contentproperties* back from """"restored"""" as opposed to the REST API so that workaround for plugins is not reliable."""
comments: "It can be triggered not only by executing the mentioned REST request, but also by calling pageManager.saveContentEntitiy or pageManager.saveNewVersion. That means that any Add-On that updates the pages is affected by this bug. " [SEP] "A bigger problem we see is that the ""restored"" status could prevent some of our upgrade tasks from doing their job properly. And there is no way to trigger them again. We would be very happy if this bug could be fixed so that our customers do not lose their data and can continue to use all the features of Scroll Versions." [SEP] "This is heavily impacting us when generating documentation and publishing content over the REST API. After the upgrade to 7.0.2, any jobs that publish content using the REST API basically <USER> I don't understand how this could be Low / Minor?" [SEP] "I totally agree to Mr. Fritschi's comment. Since updating to 7.0.2 we encounter heavy impact on our process to publish content via the ReST API. Since we've got many pages to publish regularly the w

summary: "Provide the ability to bulk remove old versions of attachments. "
description: """Currently Confluence do not have the feature to bulk remove old versions of attachments. For some customers, the build up of attachments over the years can be very troublesome.     Having the feature to bulk remove old versions of attachments can be very useful towards reducing amount of space being used by these attachments.            """

summary: "After moving a page with the Content by Label macro to a new space, no content for macro is rendered. "
description: """h3. Issue Summary    After moving a page with the Content by Label macro to a new space, Macro displays, *There is no content with the specified labels*   !NoContent.png|width=158,height=58!  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Add labels to pages in a space.    # Create a new page with the Content by Label macro.    # In the *Label* filters, add the labels from step 1.   # Add the *In space* filter and select *Current space.*    # Move the pages to a new space.    !ConfigCBLmacro.png|width=58,height=97!    h3. Expected Results    Page with Content by Labels to render content in macro field.  h3. Actual Results    No data is rendered in the fields   (i) Note: it is reproducible by adding similar labels from step 1 to pages in the new space. Then only move the page with the Content by Label macro.     h3. Workaround   # Edit page with the macro.   # click *Edit* macro.   # Remove *Current space* from *In space.* Or   # Update the *Current space* for the name of the previous space.    # click S*ave.* """

summary: "Confluence + Crowd Syncs is not performant when a large number of groups is synced"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  Syncs between Confluence & Crowd (with delegated LDAP directory) can cause longer than expected sync times due to a large number of groups. This is due to difference in the DAO models between Crowd and Confluence and the volume of groups as required by the sync.     h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Integrate Confluence and Crowd  # Set up a Confluence application in Crowd  # Create a directory and add a delegated connection to AD with ~ 40k groups. Associate this directory with the Confluence application in Crowd  # Sync the directory from Confluence    h3. Expected Results  # Sync times will remain relatively low and will not take longer over time.    h3. Actual Results  # Sync times gradually increase over time as groups are added to the data set.    h3. Workaround  Confluence doesn't come with indexes on the lower_group_name, but adding one can certainly improve processing speed:    For Postgres:  """
comments: "Thanks for creating this, <USER> Please consider increasing the priority of this request. It's a real headache for Enterprise clients." [SEP] "Bravo to *<USER> for diligently working through this problem and finding a viable workaround. When we implemented the new index in our large Confluence instance, Crowd sync times fell to 20-25% of the pre-index value. Simply put, the workaround has a significant impact."

summary: "Add a group in the manage watchers list"
description: """As a space administrator I want to add a group to the manage watchers list to have less administration for watchers.         A standard use case is for a team to want to be notified on changes to a page.    Adding or removing members form the group makes more sense than adding and removing individuals from the watch list for all pages owned by the team."""

summary: "Copy taste bullet list from Confluence to Word will not display correctly."
description: """If we copy the bullet list from Confluence to Word, all the numbers (or dots) from the list will be gone.    It would be good to have seamless copy-paste functionality between Confluence and Word."""

description: """The """"Insert numbering column"""" feature is very useful – and I personally use it daily.    Confluence Server could also use an """"Insert checkbox column"""" feature.    Working very much like """"Insert numbering column"""", the """"Insert checkbox column"""" button would simply add a column containing a single checkbox to each row in the table.      This would allow a long list of rows to instantly be transformed into a task list!"""

summary: "Ability to support Date Time custom fields as a range or single dates"
description: """To show Change Request calendars in Confluence, based on scheduled changes in Jira, we'd like to have support for custom fields of the type Date Time instead of just Dates. The timeline should also show the times instead of just the date. """

summary: "Sort users/groups alphabetically in Space Permissions page"
description: """As of now, Confluence does not have a feature to sort users/groups in Space Permission and Global Permission page alphabetically. It would be easier to check the users/groups name if it is possible to sort the names alphabetically.   !image102.jpg|thumbnail! """

This workaround below from [~<USER> works on Confluence 7.3.1 and Firefox 72.  * Add the following into the *Custom HTML* at the *At end of the HEAD*   ** """

description: """h3. Issue Summary    User password update is not logged in the audit log.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Navigate to Confluence administrator > *Users* from the sidebar.   # Click on a <user name> and select *Set Password*.   # Change password and *save*.   # Navigate sidebar and click on *Audit log*    h3. summary: "Usage Statistics improvement"
description: """The Confluence [Usage Statistics Macro|https://ecosystem.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/USGTRK/overview] can only display the events 'view', 'create', and 'update', but it has no option to display the actual count of existing pages or blogs.    Although mentioned in the documentation, the event 'remove' also doesn't return consistent results.    The [Site Statistics|https://confluence.<USER>doc/viewing-site-statistics-123043962.html] and [Space Activity|https://confluence.<USER>doc/view-space-activity-2818381.html] pages show only 'Viewing' and 'Editing' events.    This is a request to enhance the Confluence Statistics functionality by adding the count of existing pages/blogs in the *Usage Statistics Macro* and in the *Global Activity* and *Space Activity* pages - and fixing the documentation regarding the functionalities available."""

summary: "Search on Q&A does not include topics"
description: """Searching for answers on questions using keywords in the search bar. The results shown in the drop-down do not include the topics. The latter must be searched under topics. It would be convenient if the search bar offered """"text"""" and """"topic"""" matches.    !image-2019-11-27-11-45-04-559.png!"""

summary: "Automatic user synchronization stops if manual synchronization is performed"
description: """Automatic user synchronization (when a synchronization interval is set) stops working when manual synchronization is run.    *Steps to Reproduce*  # Create a User Directory in Confluence (e.g. with a JIRA server or AD)  # Define a Synchronisation Interval (e.g. 5 minutes)  # Check it is running fine.  # Click *Synchronize* on the directory listing.  # Wait for the Synchronisation Interval again. It won't run.    *Workaround*    To have it working again, it is necessary to Edit the User Directory and set the Synchronisation Interval again.    Issue happening with version 7.0.1."""

summary: "When Confluence base URL is set to HTTPS, the Roadmap Planner Macro content breaks when exported as PDF or Office file"
description: """h3. Description    When Confluence base URL is set to HTTPS, the Roadmap Planner Macro content breaks when exported as PDF or Office file    h3. Steps to reproduce  # Create a page and insert the Roadmap Planner Macro. Generate content for it.  # Have SSL configured for Confluence at Connector level  # Set the base URL to https://confluence-url.fqdn:port-if-needed  # Go to the page that created in step 1  # Export the page as PDF or Word    h3. Expected result  Page content fully export with correct content from Roadmap Planner Macro.    h3. Actual result  Page export is broken, not showing image content from the macro.    h3. Workaround  Set the URL to http://localhost:8090/your-context    Additional Info: Customer originally affected by this bug uses Scroll Office Plugin from K15t GmbH to customize the office templates as per their company standards, since default Confluence export content from pages may not have customized formats. They used this plugin during their tests. Is it possible to test any results with this plugin as well? Thank you."""

summary: "Page Properties Report macro ""Sort by"" for date values does not work when date is added via a user macro"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    The Page Properties Report macro does not sort correctly when using the """"Sort by"""" display option on a column that contains dates that are added via a user macro, as opposed to using date picket.  h3. Environment    In order for the test to be accurate, you need an existing Confluence instance with pages already created in different dates  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a user macro named """"CreateDate"""" with the following code:  {code:none}   ## @noparams   #set( $createD = $content.getCreationDate())<time Datetime=""""$action.dateFormatter.formatGivenString(""""yyyy-MM-dd"""", $createD)""""/>{code}     # On the older page, add a Page Properties macro with the following table:  |*Description*|<enter some text>|  |*Created*|<insert the macro """"CreateDate"""">|     # On the newer page add a Page Properties macro as in the previous step;   # Add the label """"testdate"""" to the 2 pages;   # Create a new page and add the Page Properties Report macro configured with the """"testdate"""" label and the *Sort by* display option set to """"Created"""".    h3. Expected Results    The table should be sorted by the """"Created"""" column.  h3. Actual Results    The table is sorted by last modified date, as default.  h3. Workaround    Currently there is no other workaround for this behavior except for entering date via the Date Picker macro. summary: "Unable to return search results for .xlsx files"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Searching for text in *.xlsx* files is not returning the *.xlsx* file as a result.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create an .xlsx document in Excel. Include unique text within the body. Attach to a page in Confluence.   # Create a second Excel document, with another set of unique text. Save as .xls file instead. Attach this to a page in Confluence as well.   # Ensure Confluence is reindexed.   # Attempt to search for the 1st document with the unique text that was in the body. Searching for the 2nd document will return that document. Note that searching text in the title will still work. This issue is with text in the body.    h3. Expected Results    Searching for text within an .xlsx file should return that attachment as a result, along with a preview of the text.  h3. Actual Results    According to [Search|https://confluence.<USER>doc/search-139528.html]:  {quote}When you enter a search term, Confluence looks for content in all spaces (including personal spaces), pages, mail, personal profiles, and space descriptions. It also looks at the content of some attached file types (Word, Text, PowerPoint, Excel, PDF, and HTML).  {quote}  However, searching for text in .xlsx files is not returning any results. When the document comes up in search results, it also does not preview any text from the document, only any comments in Confluence associated with the attachment.    When searching text in .*xls* file, it previews the first few lines of the spreadsheet.    (i) Confirmed that search of text in .doc, .docx, and .xls _does_ return results.  h3. Workaround    Save the spreadsheet as an *.xls* file."""
comments: "Thank you for creating this issue.         Please note that using XLS is not an option in many cases, as not every XLSX file can be converted to XLS losslessly.  I suggest categorizing this issue major according to the Server bug fix policy, therefore." [SEP] " I agree. Searching and retrieving all content is an essential feature, not just an 'add-on'.   Therefore this bug should be upgraded to high priority." [SEP] "Confluence version 6.15.9 is also affected by this bug." [SEP] "We have the same problem in Confluence 7.3.2" [SEP] "We also have this problem in Confluence 7.3.2" [SEP] "We have this problem in Confluence 7.1.2" [SEP] "Same problem here on Confluence 7.4.0." [SEP] "Hi,    using xls is not a solution due to security issues with this file format. Right now our firewall doesn't accept xls files anymore. So you suggest that our users convert each file to xls & then add it to a Confluence page? We are using a heavily automated process generating Confluence pages from Jira <USER>issues & automatic

summary: "Ability to Toggle Version Information on Confluence Pages On/Off"
description: """h2. Suggestion Summary  Confluence should provide administrators with a way to toggle displaying version related information on/off.    h2. Suggestion Details  If unauthenticated, one can access Confluence's landing page and retrieve version related information from three places:    * Login page footer.  * Response Head AJS Tags.  * Response Body What's New Link.    Someone without good intentions could use this information to search for possible security vulnerabilities over the internet and hack Confluence. Therefore, if in a public facing instance, hiding these pieces of information can become a requirement for administrators.    h2. Workaround  An administrator can modify Confluence core files to prevent this information from being displayed. The knowledge base below teaches how to do that:  * [How to Hide Version Information on Confluence|https://confluence.<USER>confkb/how-to-hide-version-information-on-confluence-980460833.html]"""
comments: "I agree with 'Someone without good intentions could use this information to search for possible security vulnerabilities over the internet and hack Confluence'         It was our immediate thought after we saw the version of Confluence plastered all over the website and any notifications. It would be good to just be able to toggle a switch or setting to stop displaying the version you are using from any notification or splash screen instead of editing all the different xml files to accomplish this." [SEP] "We recently performed a Hacking simulation on our Corporate Site and received similar feedback on the risks from sharing Confluence version number in plain view.     Obviously, we rather not customise our installation, to allow smooth and quick upgrades. Introducing this ""Toggle switch"" would allow this flexibility to System Admins to resolve this risk and in our case satisfy our IT Security team.         Please take this into consideration and pick up this suggestion to next versions scope.     Rega

summary: "Add page metadata to tmp files created as part of file upload process"
description: """Currently, if a file, such as an image file, is uploaded to a Confluence page, the apache commons-fileupload process spins off tmp files in the <confluence-home>/temp directory. As part of this process, the file names for these files will include a randomly generated alphanumeric unique key. Unfortunately, this has no tie back to the actual page that the file was uploaded to.    As such, if one of these uploaded files is flagged by anti-virus software as having a virus, then it's very difficult to trace where in the Confluence content this file exists.    This request is to record the page ID or content ID of the page receiving the file in the tmp file's file name so that an administrator can then inspect the page to see if the infected file is present."""

summary: "Confluence.cfg.xml template does not work when automating DC set up"

JIRA for example does not store the information like server id, license information and cluster details in the dbconfig.xml. Whereas confluence has all those information in the confluence.cfg.xml.     For that reason, we where able to automate the installation of JIRA server and DC easily. We were also able to automate the installation of confluence server with a special trick to not copy the [confluence.cfg.xml|https://bitbucket.org/atlassian/dc-deployments-automation/src/master/roles/confluence_config/templates/confluence.cfg.xml.j2] file on every build of our code. Because a copy of the template without license and server id will assume a fresh install.    When doing the server installation,   {code:java}   <?xml version=""""1.0"""" encoding=""""UTF-8""""?><confluence-configuration>  <setupStep>setupstart</setupStep>    <setupType>custom</setupType>    <buildNumber>0</buildNumber>  <properties>      <property name=""""confluence.database.choice"""">postgresql</property>      <property name=""""confluence.database.connection.type"""">database-type-standard</property>      <property name=""""hibernate.dialect"""">com.atlassian.confluence.impl.hibernate.dialect.PostgreSQLDialect</property>      <property name=""""webwork.multipart.saveDir"""">${localHome}/temp</property>      <property name=""""attachments.dir"""">${confluenceHome}/attachments</property>    <property name=""""hibernate.connection.driver_class"""">{{ atl_db_driver }}</property>      <property name=""""hibernate.connection.url"""">{{ atl_jdbc_url }}</property>      <property name=""""hibernate.connection.username"""">{{ atl_jdbc_user }}</property>      <property name=""""hibernate.connection.password"""">{{ atl_jdbc_password }}</property>      <property name=""""hibernate.c3p0.min_size"""">{{ atl_db_poolminsize }}</property>      <property summary: "As a Confluence Space administrator I would like to be able to create public pages within my space"
description: """When managing a large space/ organization it would be a good feature to be able to share viewing rights of pages with people outside of the permission scheme of the page tree / space.     The way it is currently done within our organization is to set up two top-level areas. One public and one private within the same space. This approach means a lot of copy-pasting between the two areas to keep them in sync, which creates a lot of manual work for space administrators."""
comments: "Hi [~<USER>    Thanks for raising this issue. The description here is quite similar to the following suggestions that we already have:   * CONFSERVER-37884 Ability to grant higher permissions for pages than their parent permission (restrict <-> grant)   * CONFSERVER-7089 create the ability to add page permissions for users and groups    Please check if the suggestions above are what we're looking for. Feel free to ""vote"" and watch for those issues instead, so that we'll be able to accurately track the interest of this feature.    Thanks!   Monique - Atlassian Support"

summary: "Native app for Confluence Server"
description: """In line with the native application for Jira Cloud on macOS ([https://www.<USER>blog/jira-software/jira-cloud-app-for-mac]), a native Mac app for Confluence would be great to see."""

summary: "Disabling SAML override in Confluence Data Center doesn't work"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Disabling SAML override in Confluence DC, to ensure no users can log in to Confluence via SAML/SSO only, still allows users to use default login URL and access the instance with local credentials.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Configure Confluence DC with SAML/SSO (steps not covered here)   # Disable SAML override by setting  {code:<USER>  {    """"allow-saml-redirect-override"""": false  }{code}  to   _false_ via REST Call to {{[https://localhost:PORT/contextPath/rest/authconfig/1.0/saml]}} URL     * For reference: [Enable Default Login Page To Bypass Saml In Confluence Data Center|https://confluence.<USER>confkb/enable-default-login-page-to-bypass-saml-in-confluence-data-center-957972845.html]    h3. Expected Results    Once {{allow-saml-redirect-override}} is set to _false_, it's expected the default login page redirects the request do the Idp and allow *no* further local users to log in to Confluence.  h3. Actual Results    Users *still* able to access Confluence using local credentials via {{[https://localhost:PORT/contextPath/dologin.action]}}   or   {{[https://localhost:PORT/contextPath/dologin.action?auth_fallback]}}  h3. Workaround   # The default login URL can be blocked at the Tomcat level, using the instructions in this KB:   [How to block access to a specific URL at Tomcat|https://confluence.<USER>kb/how-to-block-access-to-a-specific-url-at-tomcat-966668691.html]   # The URL can also be blocked or redirected at the proxy.    (!) *Note:* To enable the [auth_fallback URL|https://confluence.<USER>confkb/enable-auth_fallback-to-bypass-saml-in-confluence-data-center-958442925.html] during maintenance windows, the URL blocking workarounds will need to be reverted."""

Our organizations information security policy do not allow, basic authentication by entering username password on login screen to access our confluence site. Since, we have multi factor authentication in place via SAML login, a single possibility of bypassing SAML is a security vulnerability for us. Please provide workaround to atleast block this url via reverse proxy settings.         Thanks,    sachin" [SEP] "Hi Team,    When we can expect the resolution for the bug reported , any mitigation you can suggest or steps we can disable this url  url/dologin.action     We have another Client facing Instance on version 6.15.9, it seems that to is affected by the same bug    This is very critical for us .    Regards    Avin     " [SEP] "This was brought up by our security team today and needs to be fixed ASAP. Please give us a workaround until this is fixed. I do not agree this is a Sev 3 Minor issue. A security hole like this shoul

summary: "Moving the pointer past the page limit when selecting content in a section causes the cursor to jump to top of the section"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    When editing a page that contains sections, if during text selection in the section the mouse pointer is moved to outside the section limits and the left-button is released there, the cursor jumps to the top of section.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a page and add at least 2 sections;   # In the second section add several lines of text;   # Use the mouse to select text and with left-button pressed, move the cursor/pointer to outside the section width, either left or right;   # With the pointer outside the section, release left-button.    h3. Expected Results    Highlighted/selected text is kept and cursor stays at the end of the selected text (right or left, depending on the direction used when moving the mouse pointer).  h3. Actual Results    No text is selected/highlighted and the cursor jumps to the top of the section.  This can result in accidental content when entering text, as the new text will be added on top of section and not replacing highlighted text as expected and this might go unnoticed.    Please check attached screen recording [^SectionHighlight-recording.mp4]  h3. Workaround    As the issue has been only observed using Chrome, use a different browser to avoid the problem.  h3. Notes    The issue has only be observed using Chrome and it does not occur when using IE, Edge or Firefox."""

summary: "Macro Title missing when copy pasting the same macro in 'Unpublished Changes'"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  Macro Title is missing when copy pasting the same macro in a page and closing the page without updating it (Unpublished changes).     h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Create a new page and add 'Column' macro  # Save the page  # Edit the page  # Copy & paste the same 'Column' macro in the page  # Close the page without updating it  # Edit the page again 'Unpublished changes'    [^Recording #30.mp4]    h3. Expected Results   !image-2019-11-15-11-26-47-724.png|thumbnail!     h3. Actual Results   !image-2019-11-15-11-26-32-178.png|thumbnail!     h3. Workaround  Update the page to see the Macro title.    Note: *Affected macros* - Excerpt, Column, Code Block, Section"""

summary: "Multimedia Macro should have option for viewers to view as full screen"
description: """The multimedia macro has some controls to supposedly set the size of the file .. all that seems to do is control the size of the black background box, but not the size of playing window :(    The playing window seems to be a fixed size with what seems to be a non-functioning """"Full Screen"""" size button.    So what I see is a video occupying ~25% of my screen - I have to zoom my complete browser window to get something that is view-able    It seems that you just need to enable the View Full Screen button    Also seems a similar issue reported here has been closed to lack of interest :(    In my case I am managing a Confluence instance with 5000 Users and we are about to launch various video files so please count my single vote as 5000 votes!!  I hope that is interest enough???    https://jira.<USER>browse/CONFSERVER-34159     """

summary: "Page Properties Macros have no wiki markup example in documentation"
description: """h3. Summary    Some of the Confluence Server macros do not have a wiki markup example, so it's not clear if it's possible to insert using wiki markup.    There _is_ however, an example of parameters which mentions inserting into the page using wiki markup   * [+Page Properties Macro+|https://confluence.<USER>doc/page-properties-macro-184550024.html]   * [+Page Properties Report Macro+|https://confluence.<USER>doc/page-properties-report-macro-186089616.html]    h3. Suggested Edit    Include wiki markup example, or state clearly if it's not possible."""

summary: "Make it more obvious where to find parameters in View File macros"
description: """h3. Summary    For the View File Macros, such as:   * [+Office Excel Macro+|https://confluence.<USER>doc/office-excel-macro-375849173.html]     * [+Office PowerPoint Macro+|https://confluence.<USER>doc/office-powerpoint-macro-375849178.html]     * [+Office Word Macro+|https://confluence.<USER>doc/office-word-macro-375849176.html]     * [PDF Macro|https://confluence.<USER>doc/pdf-macro-375849180.html]    The parameters are listed on a separate page: [View File Macro|https://confluence.<USER>doc/view-file-macro-170494360.html]    It does state on the page that for more details, read the View File Macro documentation, but it's not clear that is also where the parameters for these macros are listed.  h3. Suggested Edit    Add some text to clarify this, e.g. For a list of parameters, please see [View File Macro|https://confluence.<USER>doc/view-file-macro-170494360.html]"""

summary: "Some macros have no documented parameters or wiki markup example"
description: """h3. Summary    For some macros, there is no documented wiki markup or parameters section like there is for other Confluence macros ([https://confluence.<USER>doc/macros-139387.html])   * [Jira Chart Macro|https://confluence.<USER>doc/jira-chart-macro-427623467.html] - there is no documented wiki markup or parameters section like there is for other Confluence macros   * +[Content by Label Macro|https://confluence.<USER>doc/content-by-label-macro-145566.html]+ - Same as above, has Macro Display Options, no wiki markup, and it's not clear if those parameters could be used in any possible wiki markup    h3. Suggested Edit    State clearly whether it's possible to add the chart macro to a page with wiki markup and if there are any parameters that can be used."""

summary: "No Parameters heading on the documentation for some macros"
description: """h3. Summary    On a few of the documentation pages for Confluence macros, there is either no *Parameters* section, or there is one, but it's called *Macro Display Options* instead.   * [+HTML Macro+|https://confluence.<USER>doc/html-macro-38273085.html] - There's a note that says there are no Parameters, but it's not under a **Parameters heading, like some macros have even when there aren't any.   * [Favorite Pages Macro|https://confluence.<USER>doc/favorite-pages-macro-148581.html] - Same as above     * [Page Index Macro|https://confluence.<USER>doc/page-index-macro-182682092.html] - Same as above    h3.  Suggested Edit    Rename *Macro Display Options* to *Parameters* to match other documentation pages"""

summary: "Activity Stream documentation points to Gadgets documentation"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    On the Confluence Server Macros documentation ([https://confluence.<USER>doc/macros-139387.html]), *Activity Stream Macro* no longer has its own documentation and links straight to the Gadgets documentation ([https://confluence.<USER>doc/gadgets-204047542.html])    In addition, as of +Confluence 7.0+, support has been ended for this macro according to the Activity Stream Gadget documentation ([https://confluence.<USER>doc/activity-stream-gadget-218275789.html])  h3. Suggested Edit    Either remove the macro from the listing of gadgets for the current documentation, or point it directly to the Activity Stream Gadget documentation instead."""

summary: "Some spaces don't hit on search if group name is duplicated in internal and external directory with difference letter case"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    If multiple directories have same group name with difference letter case (e.g. _group1_ against _GROUP1_ ), spaces which has permission against the group don't hit on the search.    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create _user1_ and _group1_ on an internal directory   # Create _user1_ and _GROUP1_ on a LDAP server   # Grant """"Use Confluence"""" permission to _group1_    # Create _group1space_ space and grant a permission to _group1_    # Add LDAP server on step 2 as an external directory and bring it to highest priority   # Login as _user1_ with LDAP password   # Search _group1*_    h3. Expected Results  _group1space_ is shown in the list   !Screen Shot 2019-11-14 at 10.58.27.png|thumbnail!     h3. Actual Results  The space is not in the list   !Screen Shot 2019-11-14 at 10.59.43.png|thumbnail!    Also, you see """"Case incorrect. Correct case is: GROUP1 """"   !Screen Shot 2019-11-14 at 11.00.13.png|thumbnail!     Although the _user1_ can look the page of _group1space_ if they entered the URL directly.    h3. Workaround  Avoid duplicated group name removing _group1_ from internal directory and configure the permission again."""

summary: "Confluence Redundancy"
description: """As confluence becomes critical to organization, its important to have this available all the time. However, for any reason there might be a unplanned downtime and business gets affected. Natively, Confluence does not have mirroring concept. The request is to build a native capability in Confluence to mirror entire site or spaces in read-only format (ideally data should be available to the mirror site in real time). This is not a DR site but a redundant site always available to users irrespective of main confluence availability.     """

summary: "Daily update email with link to the day before"
description: """The usefulness of Confluence's daily update email would increase dramatically if it did not include the link to compare to the previous version, but the version of the previous day per changed page.  Currently you have to check each time, if the previous version may be from the same day. Then you have to go to the versions, select the last version of the previous day to compare them with the current one.  Only then can you really get all the changes.  Currently, it may be that the linked update is just the addition of a comma, but the page has changed drastically compared to the previous day."""

summary: "Ability to use cookies in Companion APP communication to Confluence in order to authenticate."
description: """In companies where authentication is based on the cookies, if the cookie does not exist the login page will be shown. It would be beneficial to include cookies in this communication in order to prevent problems when downloading attachments.    Below, is the example of negotiation flow:  1. The last command instructs the client to download the document file from a URL.  2. The URL contains the credentials required to authenticate to Confluence via a provided JWT query string.  3. The Companion app then tries to download the document from the given URL.  4. Since it does not inherit browser cookies and they are not passed to it in any way the document that is downloaded contains our login page.    """
comments: "Hello - Please see [7.2 Upgrade notes|https://confluence.<USER>doc/confluence-7-2-upgrade-notes-979421823.html] for how we have addressed issues with using Companion in SSO environments with a new token-auth path for Companion requests. As we have taken this approach for authentication, we will not be exploring the use of cookies. In order to clear about our plans, we're closing this request now. Thanks again for your comments and support of this suggestion.    You may also be interested in an update that I recently posted on Community regarding Companion: [File editing in Confluence Server: Atlassian Companion updates are almost here!|https://community.<USER>t5/Confluence-discussions/File-editing-in-Confluence-Server-Atlassian-Companion-updates/td-p/1285578]. Companion 1.0.0 is available with Confluence Server and Data Center customers in Confluence 7.3.1. [Upgrade|https://www.<USER>software/confluence/download?utm_source=confluence_bugfix&utm_campaign=confluence_bugfix&utm_medium=email] now or check out th

summary: "SOAP API removeUser needs to be able to delete users with content as can be done through the UI"
description: """The REST API does not offer a way to delete users, however the SOAP API does = """"removeUser"""". Currently, if a user has content associated to them, removeUser will fail. This functionality to delete a user if they have content is supported through the UI.    The feature request is to fix the SOAP API removeUser so that it will allow users with content associated to them be deleted via API call.    The problem exists in the implementation of SOAP API removeUser.     UsersSoapService#removeUser invoke UserContentManager#hasAuthoredContent. Below is the code for DefaultUserContentManager.     Because of this the delete user operation fails due to authored content existing.    This is further complicated by the fact that when creating a fresh new user, by default a draft entry (title {{null}}) will be created in Demo Space in {{Content}} table.     Verify here:      _ff8080816dec764a016dec79d2180002_ is the newly created user that hasn't edited any content. This is a performance optimization, when dummy draft is created for new users just to warm up browser cache with editor resources but results in creating a condition where you can never delete a user via API because they will always have content associated with them."""

summary: "Enhance Confluence WebDAV Plugin Logging Capabilities"
description: """h2. Suggestion Details  When not authorised users try to access resources in Confluence that interacts with WebDav (Edit in Office, for example), Confluence will throw errors in the logs. Some of them can be suppressed by adjusting Log4J logging levels but some are non-parsed exceptions that are captured at code level but untreated by Log4J. Those can't be suppressed. If discovered that the errors are caused by an expected scenario, one wouldn't be able to fully suppress them from logs.    h2. Cause    If the *Edit In Office* session is inactive, the session will timeout after 10 minutes and will results in similar error messages.    h2. Log Examples  {code:title=Error Log 1}  2019-10-19 15:38:37,297 ERROR [http-nio-8090-exec-86 url:/plugins/servle...8b7c-73a6daf1thb6/] [extra.webdav.servlet.ConfluenceWebdavServlet] service Unexpected error   -- url: /plugins/servlet/confluence/editinword/395745421/attachments/ocauth/83348372-8b7c-73a6-thb6-73a6daf1thb6/ | traceId: 3gb5858faabb1a68 | userName: anonymous  org.apache.jackrabbit.webdav.DavException   at com.atlassian.confluence.extra.webdav.ConfluenceDavSessionProviderImpl.getConfluenceDavSession(ConfluenceDavSessionProviderImpl.java:123)   at com.atlassian.confluence.extra.webdav.ConfluenceDavSessionProviderImpl.attachSession(ConfluenceDavSessionProviderImpl.java:192)   at com.atlassian.confluence.extra.webdav.servlet.ConfluenceWebdavServlet.service(ConfluenceWebdavServlet.java:103)   at javax.servlet.http.HttpServlet.service(HttpServlet.java:741)  .... Existing Partial Workaround  One can suppress the first error message by setting the class below as FATAL on Logging and Properties, given it is the one generating such logging:  {code}  com.atlassian.confluence.extra.webdav  {code}    h2. summary: "Create from Template macro does not use specified macro parameters when space key is set to '0'"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Using the [Create from Template|https://confluence.<USER>doc/create-from-template-macro-317196995.html] macro, if the space key entered in the parameters is '0', then the specified template is not used. Instead, you will see the generic Create Page screen.    As a result of this, the specified title will also not be used.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a space, set the space key to 0   # Create a new page, and add the Create from Template macro. Choose any desired template.   # Set the Space Key in parameters to the space you just created, 0.   # Save page.   # Try to create a page from the macro.    h3. Expected Results    Desired template should be used, along with any other parameters that were entered.  h3. Actual Results    Sent to Create Page dialogue.  h3. Workaround    No current workaround. The space key will need to be renamed following the [Copy Space|https://confluence.<USER>confkb/how-to-copy-or-rename-a-space-in-confluence-169578.html] procedure.  h3. Notes    This only occurs when the space key is just the number '0'. It does not occur with the following circumstances:   # Any other single digit   # Any combination of numbers, including ones that start or end with 0"""

summary: "Numbered list displayed incorrectly on Firefox"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Numbered list displayed incorrectly on Firefox  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a page   # Add numbered list to the page ( [refer to the storage format|https://jira.<USER>secure/attachment/380256/Numbered%20List.txt] )    h3. Expected Results    Numbering sequence is correct (1,2,3,4)   !image-2019-11-11-16-29-27-819.png|thumbnail!  h3. Actual Results    Numbering sequence is incorrect (1,3,3,6)   !image-2019-11-11-16-29-44-100.png|thumbnail!    This is because the numbered list follows the sub numbered list from the previous numbered list.  {quote}1. Line 1   a. Line 1.1   b. Line 1.2   3. Line 1.3  {quote}    h3. Workaround    This issue is caused by invalid HTML. Manually edit page to reenter numbered list, ensuring there are no <ol> or <ul> elements directly under other <ol> or <ul> elements. If there are, they should be wrapped in an <li> element as per the HTML specification."""
comments: "After some investigation we concluded that this is not a bug on Confluence's side. This only occurs when invalid HTML is pasted into the Confluence editor. As per the [HTML5 specification|[https://html.spec.whatwg.org/#the-ol-element]], an ordered or unordered list (<ol>/<ul>) may only have a list of 0 or more list item (<li>) elements. As such, it is up to each browser to attempt to interpret the invalid markup. In this case Chrome exhibits the desired behaviour and Firefox does not.    The proper fix would be to correct the source of the HTML to no longer produce invalid HTML." [SEP] "How to fix the HTML?  I have no way to access the raw html on the Edit page."

summary: "Page selection for custom exports broken when there's a lot of pages"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  # Page selection for custom exports broken when the total number of pages in the Space is high.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Create 11,000 pages, and split them under two parent pages, like so,  **   # Go to *Space Tools >> Content Tools >> Export >> Export PDF*  # Try a custom export, and selecting just Parent 1 and the pages under it, and just those pages would be exported.  # Try a custom export, and select Parent 2, and the pages under it, and it will export only around 2000 or so of the pages.  # If you were to add another sub page tree under Parent 2, and try exporting just the 500 pages with the parent page, instead of 501 pages, it will start exporting over 600 or so pages.  ** This number changes depending on the position of the sub page and the number of sub pages.  **   # (/) An interesting thing to note is that if we were to move this 500 page and it's parent to the top of the Page tree, and selecting it, the number of pages being exported is correct.  ** So it seems the page selection is broken near the bottom of the list of pages.    h3. Expected Results  # The number of pages being exported should match the number of pages that is selected(ticked) in the custom export.    h3. Actual Results  # The number of pages being exported is different, sometimes more, and sometimes less than the number of pages that is selected.    h3. Workaround  # Currently, if we were to move the pages to the top of the Page tree, the export seems to work as expected."""

summary: "Ability to define default Columns to display in Jira Issue Macro Table"
description: """h3. Problem Definition  When inserting Jira Issue Macro with multiple issues (JIM as a table), the *Columns to display* is always fixed in the list and sequence below:  # Key  # Summary  # Issue Type  # Created  # Updated  # Due Date  # Assignee  # Reporter  # Priority  # Status  # Resolution    They can also be seen in the Storage Format below, specifically in the *columns* row:       !Columns to display.png|thumbnail!     Although user can edit the list of *Columns to display* on each of the Jira Issue Macro, but it is not possible to have a customized set to be *Default* so that every time a Jira Isse Macro is inserted, the customized default Columns to display is always used.  Having to edit each and every Jira Issue Macro everytime a user inserts one can sum up to be a huge overhead    h3. Suggested Solution  Allowing user or admin to change the default set of *Columns to display* for Jira Issue Macro in Confluence    h3. Possible Workaround  As a workround, user can copy & paste the edited Jira Issue Macro then edit JQL part to display a different list of issues. This can be done via:  * Copy the Jira Issue Macro in the Page Editor directly  * Copy the Storage Format of the edit Jira Issue Macro by making use of the [source editor|https://marketplace.<USER>apps/1210722/confluence-source-editor?hosting=server&tab=overview] and paste the edited storage format back to the page    """

summary: "Improve installation process by preventing any other request to brake installation."
description: """If we start installation process and after entering the license information and during the DB setup, we try to open Confluence instance, we will get an error 500 messages and DB will get corrupted.    The only solution, in that case, would be to destroy the data in db, delete confluence.cfg.xml and restart the instance.    This can happen due to multiple reasons, like monitoring tool, or load balancer checks.    Therefore it would be good to have a safety mechanism, that would redirect the Confluence request, for example, based on session, to a static page until installation is finished."""
