summary: "Confluence Source Editor button not showing up"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    The [Confluence Source Editor|https://marketplace.<USER>apps/1210722/confluence-source-editor] button may not show up upon loading the page editor.    h3. Environment    * Confluence Source Editor plugin version 2.0.0.  * Confluence Data Center version 6.13.8 - affecting Pages.  * Confluence Server version 6.13.7 - affecting User and Global Templates, does not affect Pages.    h3. Steps to Reproduce    For Data Center:   # Install the Confluence Source Editor version 2.0.0 in a Data Center 6.13.8 environment.   # Navigate to a page and click on Edit.    For Server:   # Install the Confluence Source Editor version 2.0.0 in a Server 6.13.7 environment.   # Navigate to a User Template or Global Template and click on Edit.    h3. Expected Results    The page editor will show the Source Editor < > icon in the top right indicating the source editor has loaded    !image-2019-10-30-10-43-15-558.png!  h3. Actual Results    The < > icon may be missing upon loading the page editor. Subsequent attempts to load the page editor may succeed in loading the source editor or may not.     h3. Workaround    Downgrade to the 1.1.3 version of the Confluence Source Editor plugin.    h3. Notes  Following the steps in [Confluence Source Editor Button Is Not Visible During Page Edit|https://confluence.<USER>confkb/confluence-source-editor-button-is-not-visible-during-page-edit-733940215.html] did not resolve this, but are an appropriate step to try when troubleshooting this issue."""
comments: "I believe this was created as a result of us opening a PS ticket. If it helps at all, I was using the Chrome browser v78.0.3904.70. I found I was able to consistently reproduce the issue when editing a page by using F5 (to cause it to display, though it might take a couple tries) and CTRL-F5 to cause it to not display again." [SEP] "This bug in Source Editor v 2 also affects Server Confluence vs. 6.13.7.  Downgraded the plugin to 1.13.1 and the editor now displays without issue on all pages. "

summary: "Ability to search content by deleted username"
description: """Currently, Confluence does not allow user to search for pages/spaces created by deleted/disabled user using the username of the deleted/disabled user. User is only able to search pages/spaces created by delete/disabled user with the page titles. This will cause difficulties for the admin to keep track of the pages/spaces created by deleted/disabled user.  For Example:  *<USER> created a few pages in Confluence and the Confluence admin has deleted/disabled *<USER>s* user account.  *<USER> is able to search the page created by *<USER> using the page title. However, when *<USER> tries to search the pages/spaces created by *<USER> with entering *<USER>s* username in the *contributor* field in the search filter. The search result will not return pages created by *<USER>."""

summary: "Support dark mode in Confluence"
description: """Confluence should support dark mode using the {{prefers-color-scheme}} CSS media selector [https://drafts.csswg.org/mediaqueries-5/#prefers-color-scheme|https://drafts.csswg.org/mediaqueries-5/#prefers-color-scheme]. More information including browser adoption can be found at [https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/@media/prefers-color-scheme|https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/@media/prefers-color-scheme]."""
comments: "Totally dig this idea, especially with the new and improved (as of 2020) white-background top navigation bar.  Dark Mode, please!  This is listed as CONFSERVER - is there a Cloud version?  That's what we would need this to run on.  If there's an admin that can add a link to a Cloud version, I would go vote for that as well." [SEP] "I think it should be both a cloud and server option.  Having a dark mode is a defacto standard in modern web design in my opinion." [SEP] "There is a Confluence Cloud ticket here: [CONFCLOUD-69537|https://jira.<USER>browse/CONFCLOUD-69537] as requested [~<USER>" [SEP] "Thanks, @freecode!  Votes added to every Dark Mode issue I could find starting with that one. :)" [SEP] "Please put a dark theme feature for confluence." [SEP] "I totally want this feature for my nightly adventures. Please prioritize this as it would be a great boost to dev productivity"

summary: "Improve 'Restrict a Calendar' documentation to explain that calendar owners cannot be restricted"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Team Calendars' '[Restrict a Calendar|https://confluence.<USER>teamcal/restrict-a-calendar-660737259.html]' documentation explains how to restrict viewing and/or editing.    However, the documentation does not make clear that the Restrictions were designed in a way that the calendar creator has the privilege to keep control over the calendar even if the restrictions are modified, which can cause confusion for the customers ([CSP-261644|https://getsupport.<USER>browse/CSP-261644]).    This mean they can always edit/view a calendar they created, change its name, change the related space, <USER>  h3. How to Improve    Although this is a design choice to avoid other users taking full control of calendars they have not created, the inclusion of a paragraph explaining that calendar creators cannot be restricted helps to clarify this."""

summary: "Add Migration Wizard to move data between different database types"
description: """h3. Problem Definition  Migrating Confluence between supported Database platforms (MySQL, Postgres, SQL Server, Oracle) requires using the XML backup process (a time and resource intensive process).    h3. Suggested Solution  Add functionality similar to the Bitbucket Server Database Migration Wizard (see [Connecting Bitbucket Server to an external database|https://confluence.<USER>bitbucketserver/connecting-bitbucket-server-to-an-external-database-776640378.html])"""

summary: "Clicking the Anchor or Table of Content macro links jumps to the content but doesn't include the content header"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    When using *Anchors* or *Table of Content macro* the jump to the content doesn't include the content header.    Reproduced with Confluence 6.13.4 and Confluence 6.13.8.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a page with a lot of text separated by a few headings.   ** Or use sample page:  [^Anchor and ToC macro page]    # Click any of the links in the Table of Contents macro or the Anchor link (in the sample page the anchor link is in the """"For more information please *see more details here.*"""" sentence)    h3. Expected Results    When clicking the link we would expect to see the content header and the content itself:   !Screen Shot 2019-10-29 at 10.58.58.png|thumbnail!  h3. Actual Results    When clicking the link, the anchor actually skips the header and jumps to the content immediately below the header:   !Screen Shot 2019-10-29 at 10.59.38.png|thumbnail!  h3. Workaround  Use Firefox or IE as issue only reproducible on Chrome for now."""
comments: "This also affects version 7.0.3 when using Chrome, as most of our customers do.     " [SEP] "This does only affect chrome - Firefox and IE works fine for us.         Only reproducible if you click on ToC link without scrolling." [SEP] "This is a lot worse than just the header missing:   We have a numbered FAQ (with ToC) used by non technical end-users. Eg:   # How do I do X?   ## Solution is ABC   # How do I do Y?   ## Do ABC, or if that doesn't work:   ## Do DEF    If we send the URL to item #2, the top of their page reads:   ""Do DEF""    We get a lot of confusion from end-users because of this.         Re ""Only reproducible if you click on ToC link without scrolling."" - confirmed that scrolling works around this bug, but it is also reproducible if you use the URL itself." [SEP] "Reporting for all of our users.   * Chrome Version 81.0.4044.129 (Official Build) (64-bit)   * Confluence 6.13.6" [SEP] "Closing this issue as a duplicate of https://jira.<USER>browse/CONFSERVER-57767    "

It would be nice if they would be able to see the content of the events in the calendar, instead of a blank one.     h3.Steps to replicate  # [Allow Unlicensed Access to users from Jira <USER>https://confluence.<USER>confcloud/give-access-to-unlicensed-users-from-jira-service-desk-827106210.html]  # Create a knowledgebase page with a Team Calendar on it  # Visit the Knowledgebase article    h3.Expected Behavior  The user is able to see the public events on the calendar    h3.Actual behavior  The calendar is completely empty"""

summary: "Chart Macro doesn't render in JPG"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Selecting *jpg* for image format in """"Chart Macro"""" properties results an error, the macro cannot be rendered and shows the following error instead:    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create or edit a page   # Insert Chart Macro   # Edit properties > select JPG under """"Image format""""   # Shows error """"Error rendering macro 'chart' - javax.imageio.IIOException: Invalid argument to native writeImage""""    h3. Expected Results    Chart Macro should render properly when selecting JPG under image format  h3. Actual Results    Chart Macro is not rendered and instead shows an error when *JPG* is selected for image format under properties      h3.  *Additional Notes*    In some later recent versions of Confluence like (7.6.1), a 0 byte JPG image is rendered and we will see a broken image like follows.   !chart-1.png|thumbnail!     Analysing HAR file shows that PNG is rendered properly, but JPG results in a 0 byte image.  {code:title=PNG}          """"request"""": {            """"method"""": """"GET"""",            """"url"""": """"http://localhost:8090/download/temp/chart5847754948494120556.png"""",            """"httpVersion"""": """"HTTP/1.1"""",            .        """"response"""": {            """"status"""": 200,            .          """"content"""": {              """"size"""": 7464,              """"mimeType"""": """"application/x-download"""",              """"compression"""": -1,              """"text"""": """"iVBORw...""""              """"encoding"""": """"base64""""        h3. Workaround    Select PNG in image format instead of jpg under Chart Macro properties"""

summary: "Jira Roadmap gadget shows MSG_gadget.roadmap.issues.resolved"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    The Jira Roadmap gadget in Confluence shows labels that aren't being properly transformed into the """"friendly"""" versions. For example, instead of showing """"1 of 2 issues resolved"""", the gadget shows """"MSG_gadget.roadmap.issues.resolved"""".    h3.Environment  * Confluence 6.15.8  * Jira Core and Jira Software 8.4.1  * Both applications running under HTTP     h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Create an application link between Confluence and Jira (with Jira Software installed)  # Create a software project in Jira and generate data with multiple issue versions   # Resolve an issue with and without a resolution. The following JQL should return results:  \\       # Add the Jira Roadmap gadget to Confluence:  \\    # Add the roadmap gadget to Confluence    h3. Expected Results  The gadget should show all the proper labels. For example: [^roadmap-on-jira.png]    h3. Actual Results  The gadget shows untransformed labels. For example: [^roadmap-on-confluence.png]    h3. Workaround  Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Confluence displays wrong GMT on User Profile when under Daylight Savings"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  Confluence can display the wrong GMT information if the User Time Zone is under Daylight Savings.    h3. Issue Impact  There's no impact aside from wrong information being displayed to users on their Profile. Pages and blogs are saved with the right time information.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Login to Confluence as any user.  # Click on the Profile Picture in the right upper corner.  # Click on Profile.  # Click on the Settings Tab.  # Observe that Eastern Time is displayed as GMT-0500 even though from March to November it is supposed to be GMT-0400 due to DST - This is one example, other timezones may be affected as well:  !Screen Shot 2019-10-25 at 15.45.01.png|thumbnail!    h3. Expected Results  Confluence should be considering the DST offset to display the right GMT on that screen when the user timezone is affected by DST.    h3. Why this happens  Confluence is leveraging the [Java Time Zone getRawOffeset|https://docs.oracle.com/javase/7/docs/api/java/util/TimeZone.html#getRawOffset()] method. It does return GMT-0500 as EST Timezone. It then parse it to display the information to users on screen:      Confluence should have been leveraging the _observesDaylightTime_ and _getDSTSavings_ methods as well in order to consider DST offsets when building the display information to users. Possible solution below:      h3. Workaround  None"""

summary: "Request Access should prioritize space admins to be notified before system admins"
description: """In CONFSERVER-45045, the fix for the Request Access feature states that:  {quote}Basically two things were updated:    Before the update we always sent one request email. Now we can send from 1 to 5 request emails to different people.   We have changed the rules how email recipients are found. Now we choose them from page contributors and space admins, sort this list and send the emails to the top 5 people.  {quote}  However, and this comes from a customer's use case:  {quote}the overall concern in this feature is that in some cases people who have contributed to the page, can still see the page, and have 'Restrict' or 'Admin' space permission (sorted by last edit date), might actually be the empty set when the notified admins list is set up.    And then, because to make life simpler admin wise, we have generally granted confluence-admins (i.e. the system admins) admin permission on all the spaces and then there are the space admins that are really the content owners/managers of the space. So for space administrators who can see the page (sorted alphabetically) that set of people is combined and the 5 closest to A in the alphabet are chosen.    We would really like to prefer that people who have space admin NOT via the group of system administrators be sorted in this list above people who are system administrators.  {quote}  This looks like a very solid use case and, thus, this suggestion is opened to actually focus on space admins to be the priority of the notified users when someone asks for access on a restricted page."""
comments: "Just to add a bit more.  The content owning space admins truly know whether a requestor should be granted access to the restricted content in question and the system admins (people with that additional privilege) who also happen to have space admin permissions to help solve problems will not have the knowledge to make that decision.  When a user request then gets routed to 5 system type admins and not the content owning space admins, it creates problems in users being able to get access when they need it for business reasons    I would expect this issue is common with an Enterprise level deployment."

summary: "Allow whitespaces to be included as part of a wildcard search"
description: """h3. Summary    Currently, if you have a page titled *Test Page*, the following search terms will not return the page as a result.   * Test*Pag*   * Tes*Pag*    The page will only be returned if the search term explicitly includes the whitespace. For example:   * Tes* Pag*   * Te* Pa*    h3. Suggestion    It'd be great if whitespace can be included as part of the wildcard search."""

summary: "Inserting HTML entities in a code or no format macro converts the characters"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  HTML Unicode characters inserted into a code macro or no format macro are being converted to the ASCII value.  This this does not occur when not placed in a one of those macros but it would seem those macros were made to be able to hold formatting and text without converting any of it.    {panel:borderStyle=solid|borderColor=#3C78B5|titleBGColor=#3C78B5|bgColor=#E7F4FA}  It was initially thought it was HTML hex entities only, but it appears to be any HTML entity, Hex, Decimal or """"named"""" variant according to customer reports. Further screenshots attached.    {panel}      h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a test page   # Paste the following '&#x2f ;' (without the space otherwise it converts here to)   # Add a code or no format macro and paste '&#x2f ;' (without the space otherwise it converts here to)   # Notice it's immediately converted to a forward slash    h3. Expected Results  It retains all formatting and characters.    h3. Actual Results  It converts the HTML hex value to it's ASCII symbol    Notes:  List of hex values: http://www.howtocreate.co.uk/sidehtmlentity.html    h3. Workaround  Does not occur in panel or markup macro using Markdown syntax."""
comments: "I suggest adding to the description that this behavior is, by far, not limited to HTML Hex entities. HTML decimal entities and ""named"" HTML entities are affected, as well. In my opinion, this should increase the bug's severity rating and priority.    See also here: [https://imgur.com/a/P4SUTDp]    Of course, the workaround can be used by those who are aware of the bug. However, a normal user is not aware of this, and in many cases doesn't even notice their pasted text being changed, rendering the resulting code block unusable for others.    (I was referred to this issue by Atlassian support after reporting this bug via CSP-273803.)"

Expected Results  WebDav Client is functional   webdav configuration is accessible    h3. Actual Results  WebDav client shows empty folder    !image-2019-10-25-15-16-59-313.png|thumbnail!   Webdav configuration isn't accessible, showing error below      !image-2019-10-25-15-16-03-650.png|thumbnail!       The below exception is thrown in the atlassian-confluence.log file:      h3. Workaround    """

summary: "Inconsistency in Jira Issue Link Count on Confluence Page"
description: """h3. Summary  Inconsistency in Jira Issue Link Count on Confluence Page.  When a *mentioned in* link is removed from Jira, the *Jira link* counter in Confluence page will be removed. Simply re-adding the Jira Issue again into the Confluence page does not trigger the *mentioned in* link to be re-added again.    h3. Replicated Environment  Confluence 6.15.2  Confluence 6.15.8  Jira 7.11.1  Jira 8.3.3    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Establish Application Links between Confluence and Jira  # Create a new page and insert an Jira Issue Macro with a single issue  # Publish the Page   (i) Upon publishing the page, Confluence sends a POST request to Jira to add the _mentioned in_ link in Jira Issue:    # Verify the *Jira link* on top of the page has 1 count (which is the issue that has just been inserted)  # Verify the linked issue in Jira has *mentioned in* of the page in Confluence  # Manually remove the *mentioned in* link from Jira  # Refresh Confluence page to confirm the *Jira link* no longer shows  # Edit the page, add in _another Jira issue Macro_ with the same issue and Publish the page  (i) At this point the page will have 2 different Jira Issue Macros and both are pointing to the same Jira Issue    h3. Expected Results  Since the Issue has been re-added into the page, Confluence should send the remote link request again so the *Jira link* counter can pickup the issue link again    h3. Actual Results  Confluence does not send the POST request to add the _mentioned in_ link to the Jira Issue thus the *Jira link* still has no count    h3. Workaround  # Remove all Jira Issue Macro(s) that is related to the specific Jira Issue  # Save/Publish the page  # Edit the page again and re-add the Jira Issue Macro    h3. Information  The *Jira link* counter on top of Confluence page get the counts from Jira by sending the request below upon page load:    Thus, if the *mentioned in* link doesn't exist in Jira's Issues, the *Jira link* count will not display the correct count number"""

summary: "initial setup- Parse regex password policy + offer password on next page"
description: """When setting up crowd/confluence there is an option for password policy which includes a regex edit box. We managed to brick our installation by including slashes e.g. the begin and end of the regex. You should parse the regex on submission to avoid needing up. In addition on the next page when creating first admin user I suggest offer a random password based on the regex submitted from previous screen, just in case you have accidentallly submitted a stupid rule in your regex passpword policy."""

summary: "Adding attachments to a page via REST API does not append to existing e-mail thread"
description: """I'm using Confluence 6.15.2 but this does not seem to have been fixed as of 7.0.2: [https://confluence.<USER>doc/confluence-7-0-release-notes-973479448.html].    Adding new attachments to a page creates a new e-mail thread instead of using the e-mail thread generated with create content (POST /rest/api/content). When adding new attachments is done manually, it correctly concatenates to one thread.    This functionality seems to be on Confluence's side: [https://confluence.<USER>conf615/email-notifications-967338126.html].    """"*Email threading*: Confluence will attempt to group all the email notifications about changes to a specific page together.""""    !image-2019-10-24-17-13-08-798.png|width=598,height=47!"""

summary: "Add ability to disable 'Notify Watcher' when adding/updating attachments to a page through REST API"

summary: "Create a macro for Recently Visited Pages"
description: """Current functionality for recently viewed pages exists on the dashboard and a user's profile. However, it would be really convenient to have a macro in which Recently Viewed Page can be embedded onto a page.     I'd want to embed this macro directly on a landing page (i.e., with everything else I have) so that end users can see what they recently visited without needing to navigate off of the page. I'd also like to embed it on the home pages of general spaces so that the macro shows them which pages they recently viewed on that space.         Some useful configurations might be:    *Include Space*: If nothing, all pages the user recently viewed would display. Otherwise, if a specific space is referenced, it would show the user only the pages they've viewed on that space.    *Exclude Space*: Does not include viewed pages from specified spaces. summary: "REST needs the ability to GET content in true XHTML format"
description: """from: [Confluence Storage Format|https://confluence.<USER>conf66/confluence-storage-format-943961944.html]  {quote}We refer to the Confluence storage format as 'XHTML-based'. To be correct, we should call it XML, because the Confluence storage format does not comply with the XHTML definition. In particular, Confluence includes custom elements for macros and more. We're using the term 'XHTML-based' to indicate that there is a large proportion of HTML in the storage format.  {quote}  When issuing a GET to pull the content of the storage format the response is not really in XTHML as it contains the unexpanded macro confluence tags.  h4. Request    It is requested that functionality be extended to allow a GET call that pulls down content in true XHTML format."""

summary: "User List macro performance concerns in Confluence 6.13"
description: """h2. Issue Summary     [User List macro|https://confluence.<USER>conf613/user-list-macro-964960934.html] performance regression since Confluence 6.6  h2. Environment    Confluence 6.13  h2. Steps to Reproduce   # Have the User List macro enabled in Confluence 6.13   # Run performance testing to compare against Confluence 6.6 with it also enabled    The results will show that 6.6 outperforms 6.13 with the User List macro enabled.   h2. Expected Results    Performance results will be similar or better in 6.13 than in 6.6  h2. Actual Results    Confluence 6.6 outperforms 6.13 until the User List macro is disabled, after which 6.13 is as good or better.   h2. Notes    This issue has been observed to cause high GC activity and the Total Transaction /sec during performance testing to trend downward with Average Response time to trend upwards. This is stable when the plugin is disabled.   h4. Errors in the logs    The following will be found in the application log:    h4. User Login Listener    Confluence 6.6's implementation of the User List macro has the """"User Login Listener"""" disabled by default:    !UserLister_6.6.png|width=575,height=341!    In Confluence 6.13, the """"User Login Listener"""" stopped showing in the module list because of refactoring done to use spring-scanner. Unfortunately, because of this, there is no way to disable the submodule but to disable the plugin itself.   h2. Workaround    Disable the User List macro in version 6.13 """

Expected Results    Confluence should accept a user name and email address with  Single quote (')   h3. Actual Results    Get an error Usernames cannot contain the characters '+', ',', '\', '<', '>', ', or """"."""

summary: "Remove any DOCTYPE declaration if passed through 'body.storage.value' in an API call"
description: """h3. Issue summary  If you create a page using the REST call {{/rest/api/content}} and include a DOCTYPE declaration in the storage format, it will throw the following error.      Removing the DOCTYPE declaration allows the page to be created successfully.    h3. Suggestion  Add a method to detect the DOCTYPE declaration if this is passed in the {{body.storage.value}} of a REST API call, and remove it so the request is successful."""
comments: "From the linked JiraSD issue CSP-261096:  {quote}  Or more precise Section 3.1.1 ""Strictly Conforming Documents""  > There must be a DOCTYPE declaration in the document prior to the root element.  > The public identifier included in the DOCTYPE declaration must reference one of the three DTDs found in DTDs using the respective Formal Public Identifier.  > The system identifier may be changed to reflect local system conventions.  Source: [https://www.w3.org/TR/xhtml1/#strict]     (...)    A simple fix on your end would be to just discard that single line when it is passed through the api, it does not require any parsing and at least from my point of view it would simplify using normal xhtml parsers and sanitizers to generate confluence pages.    Also from https://confluence.<USER>doc/confluence-storage-format-790796544.html I derived the intuition that the confluence storage format is xhtml with extended shema (like the standard defines at: [https://www.w3.org/TR/xhtml-modularization/schema_developing.htm

summary: "Approving page access request revokes access privileges of all others if page had been moved from restricted page tree"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  Suppose a user send a request access to a certain page. Approving the request revokes access privileges of all others except the requester. This happens if the page is affected by CONFSERVER-32712 .     Two underlying problems  * [Requesting access to a restricted page|https://confluence.<USER>doc/page-restrictions-139414.html#PageRestrictions-Getaccesstoarestrictedpage] is enabled even though it only has inherited restriction.   * Approving the request revokes other's privilege to view the page     h3. Environment  Confirmed in Confluence 7.0.2 but would be replicated in the newer version as well.    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Add SMTP server for Confluence   # Login as {{admin}}, an administrator user   # Add {{user1}} and {{user2}}    # Remove them from {{confluence-users}}   # Grant """"Use Confluence"""" permission individually to them    # Create pages with such tree structure  {code:none}    RootPage      ├── pageA      └── pageB     # Move page C to below page A  {code:none}    RootPage      ├── pageA          └── pageC      └── pageB  {code}  # Login to {{user2}} and visit pageC    h3. Expected Results  {{user2}} can see pageC.  Otherwise {{user2}} cannot send request access even the page is affected by CONFSERVER-32712 .     h3. Actual Results    * {{user2}} cannot see pageC and the access request screen is enabled. Request can be sent as    !Screen Shot 2019-10-18 at 16.40.20.png|thumbnail!   * Approving request will make PageC be only visible to {{user2}}     !Screen Shot 2019-10-18 at 16.40.50.png|thumbnail!     h3. Notes  The potential problem of this issue is CONFSERVER-32712, so resolving the bug will also resolve this.    h3. Workaround  Don't send access request to any page that is affected by CONFSERVER-32712"""

summary: "REST API Endpoint for Content Indexing Queue pending items"
description: """For monitoring purposes, it would be helpful to have an endpoint that would return the output of the page http://<confluence>/admin/viewindexqueue.action, focusing the output to the amount of pending indexing items in the queue.    That'd help customers have visibility of their index queue in a proactive way, having them take measures when that queue is increasing and not decreasing a given period of time."""
comments: "We have a very large instance of Confluence Data Center.  Recently, we ran into an issue with a scheduled job that flooded our content indexing queue causing it to back-up.  This in turn caused our performance to degrade and end-users were not able to find information in Confluence Search because the content had not been indexed.     Issues reported by the end-users prompted us to investigate and then we discovered the content indexing was backed up.    This caused to do a full re-build of our index.    If we would have received an earlier warning we could have proactively addressed the issue and prevented rebuilding the entire index."

Expected Results    Save Succesful  h3. Actual Results    When clicking Save and Test, gets the following error message:  {panel}  Oops - an error has occurred   System Error   Cause   Stack Trace:[hide]    Referer URL   http://<base-URL>/plugins/servlet/embedded-crowd/configure/ldap/?directoryId=294914&xsrfTokenName=atl_token&xsrfTokenValue=01a6c92ddc7fa94142217ecb29a43faf0602002c  {panel}  !save-userdir-error.png|thumbnail! summary: "Unable to insert emoticons in Confluence editor if Hip Chat is disabled"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    When editing a page and it is tried to insert an Emoticon using the Emoticon menu, no Emoticon is inserted.  h3. Environment   * Confluence 6.15.9     * Ubuntu 16.04   * Chrome, Firefox and Safari    h3. Steps to Reproduce    When editing a page, select the *Insert more content* option and enter the *Emoticon* menu.   # Go to any page and access the editor   # Open *Insert more content* at the top bar and enter the *Emoticon* menu   ** If the HipChat plugin is fully disabled, this is the menu exhibited:   ** !select emoticon window.png|width=173,height=242!   # Try inserting any Emoticon    h3. Expected Results    The Emoticons are inserted in the page. It was suppose to work without the need of enabling any plugin, specially when integration with HipChat is being discontinued.  h3. Actual Results    The page remains unchanged and no emoticon is inserted.  h3. Workaround    Enable the HipChat plugin again:   # Go to *General configuration* > *Manage Apps*   # Click the dropdown menu in the center and select *All Apps*   # Locate and enable *Confluence HipChat Emoticons Plugin*   # Go to any page and access the editor   # Open *Insert more content* at the top bar and enter the *Emoticon* menu   ** This the menu exhibited now:   ** !insert emoticons with hipchat plugin enabled - nonadmin user.PNG|width=303,height=285!    The dialogue box will be telling the users """"...Confluence is not integrated with Hipchat. Please contact your administrator for integration."""", which can be inconvenient."""
comments: "If you don't want to enable the HipChat plugin, another workaround is to use keyboard shortcuts to add emoticons. They're documented here: [https://confluence.<USER>conf61/symbols-emoticons-and-special-characters-877187546.html#Symbols,EmoticonsandSpecialCharacters-Insertemoticons]"

summary: "Jira issue macros taking more than 1 minute are not displayed"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    On pages having multiple Jira issue macro (usually tables) the response time from Jira can be longer than 60 seconds. This is more than the front-end JavaScript configured in Confluence.    h3. Environment    * Confluence v6.12.0  * Jira v8.3.3      h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Create a page with multiple Jira issue Macros  #* Especially filter which display as tables  #* Data from Jira should be large, so it reaches Confluence after 1 minute  # Refresh the page in Confluence    h3. Expected Results    Confluence will display the output of all Jira issue macros.      h3. Actual Results    Confluence doesn't display the output for Jira issues which are received by Confluence after 1 minute and shows the following error.      Some Jira issues macros will still be displayed, whose data has been received under 1 minute.    h3. Notes    Confluence front-end pulls the data from backend via the following JavaScript (JS)  {code:title=fetching-job.js}  this.TICK_RETRIES=[0,1*g,1*g,2*g,3*g,5*g,8*g,13*g,13*g,13*g]              """"url"""": """"<BaseURL>/rest/jiraanywhere/1.0/jira/clientIds/""""  {code}    This can be seen in HAR file and have two responses:  * 200 --> Means all data has been received  * 202 --> Still receiving    This means that if the last web-request had 202, we will run into this issue.    h3. Workaround    Required, if there is no workaround please state:   Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Servlets do not support ""no-check"" header for POST/PUT/DELETE requests"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Servlets require CSRF tokens for POST/PUT/DELETE requests.    But, at the same time, they do not support """"no-check"""" headers, so it is impossible to send POST requests from JS code.   h3. Environment    (Optional - If Applicable)   *   *  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Enable html macros in Confluence   # Add an html macro with JS script which sends POST request to <confluence-url>/plugins/servlet/applinks/proxy. Note: """"no-check"""" header should be enabled   # Navigate to the page    h3. Expected Results    JS script should get an answer from the proxied resource  h3. Actual Results    JS script returns """"403 XSRF Token missing message""""  h3. Notes    (Optional - If Necessary)  h3. Workaround    Required, if there is no workaround please state:   Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Questions-page is not rebuilt after rebuilding index from scratch. "
description: """h3. Issue Summary    After executing the steps in """"How to Rebuild the Content Indexes From Scratch on Confluence Server"""", the questions list is empty for popular tab. [How to Rebuild the Content Indexes From Scratch on Confluence Server|https://confluence.<USER>confkb/how-to-rebuild-the-content-indexes-from-scratch-on-confluence-server-110035351.html]  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Shut down Confluence   # Perform a safety backup of the following two directories to a different location:   ** <confluence-home>/index/   ** <confluence-home>/journal/   # Start Confluence   # Navigate to <USER>> Content Indexing and click """"Rebuild""""   # Disregard step 7 in KB.    h3. Expected Results    Popular questions list populate with new data.  h3. Actual Results    Popular questions list is empty.  h3. Workaround    Restore <confluence-home>/index/questions-edge directory from backup."""
comments: "[https://getsupport.<USER>browse/CSP-261503]" [SEP] "Questions content is based on having a populated edge index. Performing a reindex from scratch removes this directory which is then rebuilt as users create questions, provide answers, and upvote/downvote content. Unfortunately, the edge index is not something that is built when a re-index is performed. This is not a bug, but is the indexing working as expected.    Step 7 of the directions in the KB, which brings back the edge index from before the reindex from scratch, is the only way to immediately populate these pages. We recommend always copying it back into the new index when performing a Reindex from Scratch." [SEP] "If it can't be rebuilt, it's not index, why put it into index folder? Sounds like a bug to me.    DC KB doesn't mention it at all:  https://confluence.<USER>confkb/how-to-rebuild-the-content-indexes-from-scratch-on-confluence-data-center-833941594.html  https://confluence.<USER>doc/administer-your-data-center-search-index-879956107.html

summary: "Implement ""/<ID>/descendant/page"" Rest API endpoint on Confluence server"
description: """h3. Problem Definition    At the moment, there is no method available to get all children pages from a parent page, including all levels of the tree. Only direct children are returned from the existing methods.    h3. Suggested Solution    Implement the same endpoints that are available on Confluence Cloud:    * [Get content descendants|https://developer.<USER>cloud/confluence/rest/#api-content-id-descendant-get]  * [Get content descendants by type|https://developer.<USER>cloud/confluence/rest/#api-content-id-descendant-type-get]    Both requests are available on server instances already. However, the first one only returns attachments and comments. The second one returns the following error if accessed:  * /rest/api/content/<ID>/descendant/page      h3. Why this is important    Making those methods available would greatly reduce the effort to build the full page tree using Rest API.     h3. Workaround     At the moment, the only option is to recursively navigate through the tree, one level at a time.  """

summary: "After collaborative editing is turning off, synchrony proxy should stop attempts to talk to synchrony "
description: """h3. Issue Summary    After turning off Collaborative editing, synchrony proxy will still try to connect to synchrony until handshake timeout is reached thus a number of http threads can be created during this. In the catalina.out, there are a number of warning that synchrony proxy failed to stop http exec threads.   h3. Environment   * Confluence Server 7.0.1   * Synchrony-proxy running in front of Synchrony    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Login as admin   # Go to general configuration -> collaborative editing    # Turn off collaborative editing   # Check catalina.out (could be done by creating and downloading support zip)    h3. Expected Results    Synchrony proxy stop trying to talk to synchrony. No http threads will be holding to connect to synchrony until timeout.     h3. Actual Results    Synchrony proxy is still trying to connect to synchrony until timeout.     The below exception is thrown in the catalina.out file:    h3. Notes    Confluence might become unresponsive while the internal synchrony-proxy is still trying to reach Synchrony. You can confirm if that is the case by taking thread dumps, all HTTP EXEC threads will be in the following state:      h3. Workaround    h4. Option 1  Set system properties -Datlassian.synchrony.proxy.handshake.timeout.sec=1 to set a short timeout for synchrony proxy handshake.    h4. Option 2  Point your Load Balancer or Reverse Proxy directly to Synchrony and avoid using the internal proxy. More details [here|https://confluence.<USER>doc/possible-confluence-and-synchrony-configurations-958779064.html#PossibleConfluenceandSynchronyConfigurations-DirecttoSynchronywithareverseproxy]."""
comments: "Confluence Data Center 6.13.4 is also affected." [SEP] "Priority should be high, because it crashed our system. All Tomcat Threads were consumed by Synchrony.proxy.    We restarted confluence multiple times, but it didnt help." [SEP] "This affected 7.4.1 Data Center, after disabling Collaborative editing, all nodes went unresponsive.  restarts did not fix problem.  Work around Option 1 fixed the problem."

Environment   * Confluence v7.0.1   * Chrome v77.0.3865.90   * MacOS v10.14.6   * CA v0.6.2    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Open a Word Document in CA   # Edit the document   # Close the window of Word   # A prompt will be shown _Do you want to save the changes made to the document?_   # Select *Save*    h3. Expected Results    CA should detect *Save* and option of *Upload* in Confluence should be accessible.    h3. Actual Results    CA doesn't detect the *Save* action and option of *Upload* in Confluence is greyed out.    h3. *Notes*    In Word, if a document is directly saved, then we do see the option to Upload. It's just when Save is done after the prompt, CA doesn't display the option.    h3. Workaround    Recover files manually from the [file system|https://confluence.<USER>confkb/how-to-access-confluence-files-edited-with-the-atlassian-companion-app-955178822.html]."""

summary: "Additional line breaks added between tables while pasting text on Fixed width table"
description: """h3. Summary  Additional line breaks added between tables while pasting text on fixed width table    h3. Environment  Confluence 6.6.11  Confluence 6.15.2  Confluence 7.0.1    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Turn Collaborative Editing *OFF* in Confluence  # Create a new page  # Insert a table and set it to *Fixed width*  # (optional) enlarge the cell's width  (i) The bug can still be replicated without this step but enlarging it can help easily identify the extra line break  # Insert another table below in the same page  (i) This can be either Fixed width or Responsive  # At this point, the storage format should look something like below:    # Copy any text and paste it into the upper table with Fixed width set    h3. Expected Results  No extra line break will be added in between tables    h3. Actual Results  Extra line breaks will be added *randomly* on every paste action   [^line break between table.mp4]   (i) Note that not every paste triggers the issue    This can be further verified by spotting the extra lines in the Storage Format:      h3. Workaround  * Enable Collaborative Editing; or  * Manually remove the extra line breaks    h3. Information  The main criteria for this bug to be replicated are:  * Collaborative Editing must be turned *OFF*  * There is more than 1 tables in the page and the table at the higher position needs to be set to *Fixed width*    The bug can still be replicable regardless of the conditions below:  * The number of Rows and Columns is irrelevant  * Whether the table has Header row/column is irrelevant  * Whether the text is copied from Confluence page itself or somewhere else without any format (eg: Notepad) is irrelevant  """

summary: "Feature improvement to File conversion service"
description: """In reviewing [File conversion service causes out of memory errors in Confluence 5.7 or above|https://confluence.<USER>confkb/file-conversion-service-causes-out-of-memory-errors-in-confluence-5-7-or-above-703398523.html] the suggestions that were provided are not the most ideal situations in handling what essentially is a situation where there could be an unbounded memory utilization that will take out the entire application.  h2. Documented Suggestions and There Issues    The suggestion to increase the heap increases assume that we know what the max amount of memory is going to be needed to complete the operation, which we don't. Therefor, it becomes a game of chicken. Also requires the reservation of large amounts of memory that probably wont actually be used.    Disabling the file conversion service is not really a good choice, as it makes working in the UI awkward as panels show but with no content, thus making the implementation look broken.    The last suggestion for Data Center to reroute the file conversion traffic essentially is saying to take a node and dedicate it to just this operation, but it still doesn't solve the issue of the node/instance going out when a large file is hit. It also introduces configuration complexity and yet another thing that can go wrong to have to handle that makes the overall product look unstable.  h2. Proposed Solution    As this has to do with a situation of a potentially unbounded memory need event, much like you have already accomplished with the Synchrony service, this service should be pushed off to an independent process that is controlled by the Confluence parent process. Thus one could monitor the process for health, re-configure it 'on the fly', restart it, and have it do the potentially hazardous work without putting the Confluence Core process at risk. There are a number of 3rd party plugins that have worked out how to move 'high risk' work off to child processes so that they don't put the 'prime' instance at risk also. This should also help with the perceived overall stability of the app as having to restart it randomly doesn't help."""

summary: "Widget Connector should support youtube URLs from youtube-nocookie.com domain"
description: """Widget Connector should support youtube URLs from *youtube-nocookie.com* domain    According to European Data Protection Laws Youtube Videos have to be embedded using the www.youtube-nocookie.com URL.   Widget Connector does not work when using a youtube-nocookie domain.    +*Current Behaviour*+   Widget Connector currently accepts youtube links like the following format:   [https://www.youtube.com/watch?t=15&v=LhHKkodOPFo]    +*Suggested feature*+    Should also be compatible with youtube links from youtube-nocookie domain like the following:   [https://www.youtube-nocookie.com/embed/LhHKkodOPFo]"""

summary: "Publishing or discarding draft of the page will reset position of scroll in sidebar depending on the position of the scroll of discarded/saved page."
description: """h3. Issue Summary  Publishing or discarding a draft of the page will reset the position of the scroll in the sidebar depending on the position of the scroll of the discarded/saved page at the time of discarding/publishing.    h3. Environment  Confluence 6.15.9    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Create a loot of pages in the sidebar (so that scroll appears).  # Create one large page (so that scroll appears).  # Move scroll on the sidebar to the top.  # Edit the large page.  # Scroll to the bottom of the page.  # Publish or discard changes on that page.  # Observe the position of the scroll in the sidebar area.    h3. Expected Results  The sidebar scroll will be on the top of the page or where we left it.    h3. Actual Results  The scroll will be at the bottom, mimicking the scroll position of the edited page (last known position)    h3. Notes  Large content users will be impacted by this as they constantly need to find the correct location and exact page if they want to edit the neighbor page.    h3. Workaround  A potential workaround until the fix is implemented might be to reset position of the scroll to position of selected page in sidebar:    """

summary: "Separate Plugin Administration permission from System Administrator permissions"
description: """h3. Problem Definition    Currently, in order to administer Confluence plugins, users need to have <USER>Permissions.    If a Confluence administrator needs to allow some users to administer only the Confluence plugins, giving those users <USER>permissions will give them total control over the Confluence instance, which is inconvenient.  h3. Suggested Solution    Separate the permission to administer plugins from <USER>permissions.    This way, users can be authorized to administer plugins without having permission to the remaining General Configurations (*Configuration*, *Users & Security*, *Look and Feel* and *Administration*).  h3. Workaround    Currently there is none."""

summary: "Excel tables don't maintain text alignment format"

Tested with Confluence 6.13.6 and 7.0.2 versions.    * Excel file structure and alignment:  !correct_formatting_excel_office.PNG|thumbnail!    * View file macro on Confluence (correct):  !correct_formatting_excel_view_file.PNG|thumbnail!    * Excel file macro on Confluence page (incorrect):  !incorrect_formatting_excel_macro_page.PNG|thumbnail!    h4. Workaround    Use the view file macro instead of the excel macro, or open the file directly on office excel program."""

summary: "Attachments label doesn't auto-populate label when uploading files via Attachment Macro"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Attachments label doesn't auto-populate label when uploading files via Attachment Macro  h3. Environment    * Confluence v7.0.2  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Login to Confluence and create a page   # Add the Attachment Macro to the page with the following configurations   ** Attachment labels : {{any}} for this example we have used {{filepreview}}   # Click *Save* on the Attachment Macro dialog and then click on *Update* on the bottom left of the page   # Click on *browse for files* and upload a file   # Drag and drop a file to the Attachment Macro    h3. Expected Results    Files are added to the page as an attachment and the label specified in the attachment label macro is added to the file  h3. Actual Results    Files are added to the page as an attachment and the label specified in the attachment label macro isn't added to the file  h3. Workaround    After uploading the file, click on the attachment icon and add the label to the file. File can be previewed on the *Attachment Macro*. Upload a file by clicking on *browse for files*, the file is attached with the specified attachment label    For drag and drop, the label has to be manually added    !Attachment Macro bug.gif|thumbnail!"""

summary: "Source Editor is losing changes when page is saved"
description: """(i) This is cloned from an archived ticket SOURCE-109  h3. Issue Summary    Adding in HTML/CSS code to the source editor on a page does not always provide the desired result. With tables for example it is possible to alter the table appearance using the source editor and it appears in the editing window. As soon as the changes are saved and the page is displayed these customisations are wiped. Going back into the edit view and then the source editor reveals that all the added HTML/CSS has been removed.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a page, save it and then go back to edit to get access to the Source Editor code button.   # Add some customisations to a table or section of text, such as colour change using HTML. Preview the page to see the changes in effect.   # Save page    h3. Expected Results    The change is reflected properly.  h3. Actual Results    Once the page is displayed, all previewed changes are lost. And if you go back in to edit the page and open up the source editor, all added in changes wiped.  h3. Note    This is understandable if it breaks the underlying code of Confluence, but it is happening when adding in simple features such as a colour change or specifying the width of a table.   This has proved problematic when displaying large amounts of documentation information for reference, most of which needs to be in a consistent custom format.  h3. Workaround    Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Team Calendars could show a note with the timezone of the user"
description: """h3. Problem Definition    Team Calendars does a great job of simplifying how timezones are handled by displaying the events according to what is defined in the user profile. Here are the details:    * [Understanding Time Zones and the Display of Event Times in Team Calendar|https://confluence.<USER>confkb/understanding-time-zones-and-the-display-of-event-times-in-team-calendar-806457993.html]    However, this is not clear until the end-user checks the document above. While looking at the TC contents, we cannot see the timezone indicated anywhere, which can cause some confusion.    h3. Suggested Solution    A simple tooltip or note could be created to indicate which timezone is set in the user profile and explaining that this is being used to display the events. Example:    {quote}  You are viewing events in GMT-X timezone which is currently set in your profile. Click here to change it.  {quote}    Adding a link to the user profile would also be very helpful in case the user needs to update it.    h3. Why this is important    The proposed feature would avoid confusion about timezones in TC. Also, creating a link that is displayed in the macro or the events so the user can quickly change the setting would improve the quality of life while using the product."""

summary: "Preview macros for PDF and PowerPoint stops working after sometime"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Previewing PDF or Office files via Office macros like PDF macro stops working after sometime  h3. Environment    7.0.1 and above  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Add an Office (or PDF) attachment to a page   # Use Powerpoint (ppt) macro to preview the attachment   # The attachment will work   # Wait overnight   # Open the page with another browser or open with private browsing mode (not to use browser cache)    h3. Expected Results    Browser displays image of the office file   !Screen Shot 2019-10-04 at 14.37.20.png|thumbnail!  h3. Actual Results    The preview image is broken.   !Screen Shot 2019-10-04 at 14.37.00.png|thumbnail!  h3. Notes    The preview image is stored under {{<confluence-home>/viewfile/temp}} directory, and apparently {{scheduledjob.desc.cleanupTrigger}} job is clearing up the temp directory. This is a scheduled job that runs at 2 AM automatically.  h3. Workaround    Disabling the [Scheduled Job|https://confluence.<USER>doc/scheduled-jobs-96567525.html] scheduledjob.desc.cleanupTrigger may *not* stop the job from running. This is due to a new bug that disabling a system scheduled job (e.g. cleanupTrigger) continues to run post restart of Confluence:   - CONFSERVER-59501 - Disabling a system scheduled job continues to run post restart of Confluence    In order to properly disable it, we need to *disable* the *Office Connector* module related to the {{cleanupTrigger}} job.   # Navigate to Manage Apps in Confluence (requires Confluence admin permission)   # Filter it by *System* apps and search for *_Office Connector Plugin_*. Click on it to expand.   # Click on *Modules* to expand the list and look for {{File Cache Cleanup (cleanupTrigger)}}.   # Leave the mouse cursor on it and the *Disable* button will show up. Click on it.   (!) For broken files, it's necessary to reupload in order to recreate them.    Or, utilize the thumbnail feature of [Display Files and Images|https://confluence.<USER>doc/display-files-and-images-245827130.html] instead of Office macro."""
comments: "We feel this is more important than a Minor severity. Many of our customers utilize the Office Powerpoint macro to display Powerpoints on Confluence pages as offline presentations. This bug should be escalated." [SEP] "This bug affects version 7.2.0 too." [SEP] "I agree with <USER>above. We are hosting training material for our customers on Confluence. This problem has appeared since version 7.0.0 and has not yet been resolved." [SEP] "This bug affects version 7.3.1 too." [SEP] "Great to see there is a fix for this now - will it be ported back to the 7.4 Enterprise release?" [SEP] "I see the not-74 label. Is there any reason why this fix shouldn't be in Confluence 7.4 Enterprise Release give

Steps to Reproduce   # In a test space create [undefined page links|https://confluence.<USER>doc/undefined-page-links-139580.html] in at least 2 different pages;   # Open the *Script Console* under the Administration console;   # Copy the contents of the attached [^Script.txt] into the console;   # Edit the space key in line 24 to match your test space;   # Click on *Run* - verify that the output lists the different pages created and the undefined links created in step #1;   # Open the pages listed on the output of the previous steps;   # Go back to the *Script Console* and uncomment the code {{dlm.removeLink(link2)}} in line 37;   # Click on *Run*.    h3. Running the script after step #Y just returns {{null}} and the below exception is thrown in the {{atlassian_confluence.log}}:    h3. Notes    The classes and methods in question are documented in:   * [Class ContentEntityObject|https://docs.<USER>ConfluenceServer/javadoc/6.15.9/com/atlassian/confluence/core/ContentEntityObject.html]   * [Class DefaultLinkManager|https://docs.<USER>ConfluenceServer/javadoc/6.15.9/com/atlassian/confluence/links/DefaultLinkManager.html]    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Configure two directories with a user which exists in both directories   # Change the order of directories    h3. Expected Results    User cache must contain the user from the directory with the highest priority  h3. Actual Results    User is not updated in user cache  h3. summary: "Pagination in the Page Properties Report macro causes Handy Status macro to stop working"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    When the *Page Properties Report* lists results in more than 1 page, the *Handy Status* macro no longer allows to change status  h3. Environment   * Confluence 6.15.9   * [Handy Macros for Confluence|https://marketplace.<USER>apps/1214971/handy-macros-for-confluence?hosting=server&tab=overview] 3.0.9    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Open *Space tools* > *Apps* and then create a Status set with 2 different statuses under *Handy Macros*   # Create 2 pages, each with the Page Properties macro, containing a table such as:  |Description |<enter some text>|  |Status |<insert a Handy Status macro>|   # Add the same label, for example """"pageprop"""", to the 2 pages;   # Create a new page and add the *Page Properties Report* macro, specifying the label created in the previous steps;   # Viewing the latest page, click on the Status values for each item and change to the other one;   # Edit the page, change the option *Number of items to display* to 1 and click *Update*;   # Viewing the page, click on the Status value for the item displayed;   # Click on *Next* and click again on the Status value for the other item displayed.    h3. Expected Results    It should be possible to change the status in the steps #7 and #8, as it was in the step #5  h3. Actual Results    Clicking on the status in the steps #7 and #8 does nothing    The below exception is thrown in the {{atlassian_confluence.log}} file but only for the first time the issue occurs:    Reproducing the issue again does not cause any further exceptions, until Confluence is restarted.  h3. Notes    This issue has only been reported with the *Handy Status* macro although it's possible that other interactive macros are affected.  h3. Workaround    Increase the option *Number of items to display* in the *Page Properties Report* macro  to a high enough value that prevents pagination, in order for the macro to work correctly."""
comments: "The reason of the issue is that ConversionContext.getOutputType() is ""preview"" when a macro renders.  "

summary: "Upgrade tasks to Confluence 7.0.1 will fail on MSSQL if dbo schema is not used"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    During a Confluence upgrade to Confluence 7.0.1, when using SQL Server, if the schema used to store the Confluence tables is not *dbo*, the upgrade fails.    Current documentation does not mention the {{dbo}} schema as a requirement for using Confluence with SQL Server: [Database Setup for SQL Server|https://confluence.<USER>doc/database-setup-for-sql-server-9258.html].  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Install a 6.x version of Confluence, using SQL Server, and store the Confluence tables on any schema other than dbo on the database   # Upgrade to 7.0.1    h3. Expected Results    Confluence will get upgraded successfully  h3. Actual Results    The below exception is thrown in the atlassian-confluence.log file:    h3. Notes    Although it's not mentioned on the error message, this error happens because of some earlier upgrade tasks which did not get executed:    These statements reference those tables by """"dbo.tablename"""" directly, and so they will silently fail if the schema used is anything other than {{dbo}}.    This is caused by a particular new function from Confluence 7 which is called during the upgrade tasks, with the source code below:    As seen on the source code, the statement referenced on the logs is formed by """" DROP TABLE dbo."""" + tableName + """", with the {{dbo}} schema hardcoded.  h3. Workaround    There are two possible workarounds to the problem.    The first one is to move the Confluence tables to the {{dbo}} schema, before the upgrade, as that allows the upgrade to complete successfully.    Second workaround is to manually remove {{EVENTS}} and {{SNAPSHOTS}} tables before the upgrade:  {code:none}  drop table EVENTS;  drop table SNAPSHOTS;  {code}  This will allow the tables to be created automatically during the server startup and all subsequent upgrade tasks for indexes creation will be applied successfully."""
comments: "As it was me to find this bug, may I ask if it whill be better to move all tables in Confluence and may be in Jira databases to dbo schema to avoid problems in future?    We have now Jira tables in js schema and Confluence in cs schema." [SEP] "[~<USER>, thanks for finding this problem.    This is a bug from Confluence side, the product should support the default schema of the DB user, not just assume {{dbo}} schema. It's going to be fixed in the nearest Confluence version. I think you can continue to use custom schemas for Jira and Confluence, we are not planning to remove support for them (at least from Confluence perspective)." [SEP] "h4. [<USER>Pyshnohraiev |https://jira.<USER>secure/ViewProfile.jspa?name=<USER>Thanks foк your comment.    As we have allready moved to dbo, we'll wait for next upgrade. And then test it with old schema. " [SEP] "A fix for this issue is available to Server and Data Center customers in Confluence 7.0.4  [Upgrade|https://www.<USER>software/confluence/download?utm_source=con

summary: "Disable Space Watching"
description: """For privacy/confidentiality concerns, it's not enough to be able to delete space watchers, we need to disable watching. We have a large enterprise and can't disable watching across the enterprise so we need to be able to completely disable watching on a space basis. Preferably configurable under Permissions or a simple check-box on Manage Watchers.    Stylesheet allows the removal of the Watch button for a space, but does not disable the shortcut (like removing buttons from the header does.) If there is something else we can remove using Stylesheet that would also be acceptable."""

summary: "Let administrators skip the welcome screen"
description: """Currently after having installed a fresh Confluence instance, administrators get a welcome screen to create a space.    There is no possibility to skip this screen so the admin is enforced to create a space what he just might not want to do at this time. Especially as he can't modify the space key or description at this dialogue.    The situation gets even more annoying, as this screen also shows up while spaces already exist (e.g. for new users with admin privileges). So in most cases it's unnecessary to create another additional space. But the user has to and to remove it afterwards with several clicks.    So please just add a link resp. button to cancel this screen. As well as all other welcome screens and tutorials should be able to be cancelled at any time. (This is already in discussion in other suggestions)."""

summary: "Provide an option where users can watch pages only."
description: """When a user watches a space, what happens here is that the user becomes automatically a watcher of all the content of the space including pages and blog posts. It would be a great addition to have an option that allows user to *watch* *only pages*. This way, they will not get notified about blog posts. """

summary: "Icon for space dropdown for Confluence question is ugly"
description: """* Create a new question   * select any space and look at the dropdown"""

summary: "As a Confluence user I'd like to have options to create reports with information from the Jira projects"
description: """h3. Problem Definition    There are very limited options when trying to create a report in Confluence based on Jira project's information.       h3. Suggested Solution    It would be great to have a more dynamic macro in Confluence to integrate with Jira to create reports on releases for example.  In the example below, customer is trying to use Page Properties Macro and Page Properties Report Macro to try to achieve this goal:    Currently we see a very static information like:   !report_macro.png|thumbnail!    But it would be great if we had the option to group releases under specific projects, and have an option to count releases and other data for example.   !Report.png|thumbnail!      h3. Why this is important    At this point the options available are paid third-party plugins and having this new feature would be adding a great value to the Confluence - Jira integration.    h3. Workaround    Use third-party plugins to achieve this."""

summary: "Page properties ID breaks the pagination"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Page Property Report Macro resulting in additional pagination added whereas all the results are already available on the first page itself. If you try to click the second page it results in browser throwing 500 error.    h3. Steps to Reproduce    # Create multiple pages and include the same label names on each page (Say 10 pages).  # Create page property macro on each page and add property ID for only 5 of them. Created Label name should be mapped in the macro.   !Screen Shot 2019-10-01 at 6.36.09 PM.png|thumbnail!   # Create a new page and then add a Page Properties Report macro. Specify the label name, Property ID   !Screen Shot 2019-10-01 at 6.36.45 PM.png|thumbnail!   # Set Page Properties Macro to display 5 items/pages per page    h3. Expected Results    The report should display all the 5 pages on the first page itself and shouldn't present the second page.    h3. Actual Results    Page property macro page display first page with all the results but there is additional pagination being added which leads to confusion and clicking the page 2 button would result in 500 error.     [^Page Property.har]     h3. Workaround    Currently, there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "If a user tries to log in to Confluence before the record is synchronized with LDAP, then the user is not properly indexed and might not be searchable"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  When an user is created in the LDAP and he/she tries to log in to Confluence before a [directory synchronization|https://confluence.<USER>conf613/synchronizing-data-from-external-directories-964961194.html] occurs, then the user might not be properly indexed.    As a result, the user will not be available to many services relying on the [Lucene index|https://confluence.<USER>conf613/content-index-administration-964961390.html], such as [searching|https://confluence.<USER>conf613/search-964961041.html], [space permissions|https://confluence.<USER>conf613/space-permissions-overview-964960242.html], [page restrictions|https://confluence.<USER>conf613/page-restrictions-964960419.html] and _others_.    Some of the symptoms that could be noticed by Confluence users are:   * Affected users don't appear in the search results.   * Affected users can't be mentioned in pages.   * Affected users can't be searched in the macro configuration panel.   * Affected users can't be assigned to Space Permissions nor to Page Restrictions.   * Affected users can authenticate to Confluence and navigate through Spaces and Pages, as well as create content.    h3. Environment  This bug only occurs if you are using a [connector to an LDAP|https://confluence.<USER>conf613/connecting-to-an-ldap-directory-964961184.html], such as a MS AD.  The issue was reproduced using a test instance of MS AD, but it might happen if you are using any other LDAP supported in Confluence.    The bug is described with a direct connector to AD, but it can be reproducible when there's an external Crowd between Confluence and the LDAP.    This bug was validated on Confluence *6.13.8* and on *7.0.2*.  This affects both the *Server* and *Data Center* deployments.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  The steps to reproduce are separated in different scenarios that could trigger the bug.    h4. Scenario 1 -- new user  In this scenario the user never existed in AD, and then was properly created in the AD and the user tried to log in before a directory synchronization occurs.    Below are the steps to reproduce the bug.  # Install a Confluence vanilla instance.  # Configure a connector to AD.   #* Make sure the _Update group memberships when logging in_ option is set to _Every time the user logs in_.  # Confirm the initial synchronization worked successfully.  # In AD, create a new user that is *not* member of the _confluence-users_ group.   #* The user *must not* be part of any group that gives the _can-use_ global permission.  # Log in as the target user *before* a directory synchronization occurs.   #* The authentication is successful, but the user cannot navigate on Confluence and will receive a _Not Permitted_ page, since the user is not part of the _confluence-users_ group.   #* At this point, the user is added to the Lucene index not as a licensed user.     !lucene.index.img001.png|thumbnail!   # In AD, add the target user to the _confluence-users_ group.  # Log in to Confluence with the target user again *before* a synchronization occurs.   #* This time the user is able to authenticate and navigate on Confluence.     h5. Expected Results  Confluence triggers an update on the Lucene index, marking the associated record (Lucene document) as a licensed user, since now the user is part of the _confluence-users_ group.  The user is _searchable_ and appears in the [People Directory|https://confluence.<USER>conf613/search-the-people-directory-964961057.html].    h5. Actual Results  The user profile is properly updated in the user directory, but the information in the Lucene index is not updated.  That means the user is not _searchable_ and cannot be found in the People Directory.    Running the directory synchronization will not update the user in the Lucene index, unless something changes on the AD side.    h4. Scenario 1a -- new user  In this scenario the user is properly created in AD and logs in _after_ a directory synchronization occurs.    Below are the steps to reproduce the bug.  # Install a Confluence vanilla instance.  # Configure a connector to AD.   #* None of the AD groups should confer can-use access.   #* Make sure the _Update group memberships when logging in_ option is set to _Every time the user logs in_.  #* Make sure the directory in Confluence is configured to auto-add users to """"confluence-users""""  # Create a new user in AD  # Confirm the initial synchronization worked successfully  # User should appear on the """"Users"""" screen but should NOT be searchable in the index (cannot be at-mentioned, <USER>). This is expected.  # Log in as the new user  # The authentication is successful and the user has access.   # At-mention the user, or search for them in the People Directory     h5. Expected Results  After logging in, the user is re-indexed as a licensed user since they belong to the _confluence-users_ group, and as such and can be mentioned and found in the People Directory.     h5. Actual Results  The user is not re-indexed as a licensed user, and so cannot be found in any feature that uses the index. Running the directory synchronization will not update the user in the Lucene index, unless something changes on the AD side.    h4. Scenario 2 -- disabled user  In this scenario, the user already existed in AD and was previously (properly) synch'ed with Confluence.  The user is then disabled in AD and then enabled again -- a similar problem occurs if the user is deleted in AD and then re-added..  If the user tries to login before the next directory synchronization, then the bug occurs.      Below are the steps to reproduce the bug.  # Install a Confluence vanilla instance.  # Configure a connector to AD.   #* Make sure the _Update group memberships when logging in_ option is set to _Every time the user logs in_.  # Create an user in AD as part of the _confluence-users_ group.  # Force a directory synchronization in Confluence.  # Log in to Confluence as that user.   #* The user should be able to authenticate and navigate through Confluence.  # In AD, disable the user.  # Force a directory synchronization in Confluence.   #* At this point, the Lucene index should be updated with this information and the user will not be _searchable_ nor appear in the People Directory.     !user.directory.img001.png|thumbnail!  !lucene.index.img003.png|thumbnail!   # Enable the user in AD, but do not trigger a directory synchronization in Confluence.  # Try to log in as the target user in Confluence.   #* The log in is successful and the user can navigate in Confluence.    h4. Expected Results  Confluence triggers an update on the Lucene index, marking the user record as active, since now the user is enabled in the directory.  The user is _searchable_ and appears in the [People Directory|https://confluence.<USER>conf613/search-the-people-directory-964961057.html].    h4. Actual Results  The user profile is properly updated in the user directory, but the information in the Lucene index is not updated and the user still shows as inactive.  That means the user is not _searchable_ and cannot be found in the People Directory.    Running the directory synchronization will not update the user in the Lucene index, unless something changes on the AD side.       h3. Notes  * Subsequent directory synchronizations (manual or automatic) will not trigger an update of the user's profile unless something changes on the user's attributes.   ** In other words, the affected user will not be re-indexed if nothing changes on the AD side.   ** This is true whether the directory is configured with full or incremental synchronization.  * In a Data Center instance there may be an additional symptom on which all the use cases work fine in some nodes, but it doesn't work on other nodes -- for example, an user can be mentioned in all nodes, except one.   ** This could happen if a re-index was manually triggered in specific nodes, which would update the Lucene index with proper information.      h3. Workaround  Try to modify the user details, forcing indexing of that specific user:  # Go to *Cog icon* > *User management* and search for the affected user.  # Access the user profile and click on *Edit Details*.  # Add any sample text to the _About Me_ attribute and click on *Submit*.  # After a couple of minutes, check if the user can be searched.      If the above doesn't work, try to force user indexing for all users.  # Access {{<Confluence Base URL>/admin/force-upgrade.action}}  # In _Upgrade task to run_ select _userIndexingUpgradeTask_.  # Click on _Force Upgrade_.    This will trigger an user indexing task -- if using DC, it will be triggered on all nodes of the cluster.    If this doesn't resolve the issue, then it could be a different bug causing it and a full reindex may help."""

summary: "Ability to manage page watches by space"
description: """h3. Problem Definition  When managing watchers of a space, space admins only have the ability to remove   watchers entirely from the space if they are watching the entire space as opposed to individual pages.     h3. Suggested Solution  Would like the ability to bulk remove watchers of certain pages entirely from a space in manage watchers as opposed to having to go to each page individually to remove a watcher of that page. """

summary: "Certain alignments on AUI Inline Dialog elements do not flip if space constrained and runs off page"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  When using AUI Inline Dialogs according to https://docs.<USER>aui/8.5.1/docs/inline-dialog.html#alignment  {quote}If there is not enough room to display an inline dialog with the desired alignment the inline dialog will flip alignment.{quote}    Which is true for the example on that page when using *right middle* alignment. The dialog flips as expected.  But when using *bottom left* alignment for example the dialog does not flip if the space is constrained and the window runs off the screen.      h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Add a HTML macro to the right hand side of the page (I used a full width table)    # Use exact example with right middle* alignment from [space contrained alignments|https://docs.<USER>aui/8.5.1/docs/inline-dialog.html#alignment] and place on the right hand side of a page, table may make it easier.    # Click / (hover if configured) and notice how the dialogue flips as intended and is shown on the left   # Change the alignment to *bottom left*   # Notice now the dialogue runs off the screen if there is enough text    h3. Expected Results  * It flips alignment to *bottom right*    h3. Actual Results  * It runs off the page as it does not detect the space constraint    h3. Notes    This was first noticed using the third-party add Requirement <USER> I was then able to replicate the same situation using our AUI controls manually.  * Also affects *top* alignments as well  * I've included a storage format of the page so you can confirm I was using the correct AUI properties.    h3. Workaround  Currently there is no workaround, due to the dynamic nature of the HTML simple CSS/HTML customimsation is not possible.  If a workaround is discovered it will be added here."""
comments: "Hi [~<EMAIL>].  Thanks for reporting this.   I passed the bug onto the AUI team.  I'll close the Confluence ticket now but feel free to watch the AUI bug report: https://ecosystem.atlassian.net/browse/AUI-5081"
