summary: "If the Marketplace is unreachable, UPM causes Confluence 7.0.1 to hang for 30sec per UI click"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    If the Marketplace is unreachable, UPM 4.0.6 causes Confluence 7.0.1 to hang for 30sec per UI click.  h3. Environment   * UPM 4.0.6 in Confluence 7.0.1     * marketplace.<USER>non-responsive. This needs to be a timeout, not a straight connection refusal by a firewall.    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Add entry in /<USER>hosts to simulate marketplace being unreachable:       # Click anything in Confluence and get ready to wait, and wait....    h3. Expected Results    The Confluence UI should remain responsive even if Marketplace is unreachable.  h3. Actual Results    The result of each click in the UI (eg. clicking on Spaces, or trying to load the Confluence admin screen) takes approximately 30 seconds, giving the impression that Confluence performance is very poor.  h3. Notes    If the IP that marketplace.<USER>resolves to refuses or accepts the connection immediately, there is no problem - it's only when the resolved IP fails to connect and eventually times out.    Confluence thread dump on a hanging thread:      h4. Stuck Thread  {{catalina.out}} may report stuck threads that are related to the Marketplace connection:      h3. Workaround    In Confluence, disable connectivity to Marketplace:    # Navigate to the cog icon > *General Configuration* > *Manage Apps*  # Click on *Settings* at the bottom of the page  # Uncheck *Connect to Atlassian Marketplace* and click *Apply*      h3. Workaround via REST API    If Confluence is completely inaccessible we can use the following REST API to disable connectivity to Marketplace.        This ticket is also tracked under [UPM-6053|https://ecosystem.atlassian.net/browse/UPM-6053]"""
comments: "Requires a fix for UPM-6053 in UPM" [SEP] "Fixing this properly depends on a new feature in atlassian-cache. I'll ensure this is safe to land in Confluence 7.0.x." [SEP] "-A fix for this issue is available to Server and Data Center customers in Confluence 7.0.3-   -[Upgrade|https://www.<USER>software/confluence/download] now or check out the [Release Notes|https://confluence.<USER>doc/confluence-7-0-release-notes-973479448.html] to see what other issues are resolved.-" [SEP] "Wow, this is the reason what I got the white screen and 504 error.    ELB in AWS gives 504 when the site is not reachable for 30 seconds, so I thought 7.0.3 version of confluence is broken. This version throws so many stuck thread error and gives white screen about 30 seconds, and finally gives the error.    However, if I did not use the ELB then it would work as the description says. I just want to let you know that the situation is worse with the environments in AWS." [SEP] "Please note: this issue is not fixed in 7.0.3 like Zac says.

summary: "PDF export needs an option to allow not creating new page for child page"
description: """PDF export creates a new page for each child page on 6.13.0 and onwards.  Previously, all the child pages were included in the parent page.  I have tested on below 2 versions to see the difference:  - [^created_by_6.13.0.pdf] : Each child page created a new page.  - [^created_by_6.0.1.pdf] : No new page for the child page.    The customer wants to have a new option to allow old behavior, so all the child pages can be included to parent without any page break."""
comments: "Thank you <USER>for creating this issue.          This would be awesome to have this feature back as we have created all our documentation and manuals with this being a key function. Now when exporting to PDF the page size of the manuals has increased significantly.          Looking forward to seeing some progress on this one.          Thanks team!"

summary: "Triggering ""Recommended Updates Email"" in scheduled jobs does not work."
description: """h3. Issue Summary  Triggering """"Recommended Updates Email"""" in scheduled jobs does not work.    h3. Environment  Confluence 6.15.4    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Write a page.   # Create a loot of comments so that the page can be picked up by Recommended Email updates functionality.   # Trigger Recommended Updates email scheduled job in Scheduled jobs of Confluence Administration    h3. Expected Results  The Recommended Updates email will be sent.    h3. Actual Results  No email is sent.    h3. Workaround   Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Day/month/year order is shown incorrectly in date picker "

summary: "Ability to restrict page export permissions from specific users or groups"
description: """h3. Problem Definition    In Confluence Server, the users are able to [disable PDF export|https://confluence.<USER>confkb/how-to-disable-pdf-export-for-pages-or-spaces-711361415.html] but not other file format like Word and XML. summary: "Block simultaneous edits for Atlassian Companion"

"""

summary: "Responsive table will not be responsive if the column was resized"
description:
"""h3. Issue Summary When a responsive table is resized once while editing the page, the column will only be responsive while there's no empty space at the end of the line. As soon as a space is added to the line, the line breaks. In other words, if there's a huge line with no spaces, the table will be responsive. h3. Environment * Confluence 6.15.X h3. Steps to Reproduce # Create one table with two columns. # Set the table as responsive. # Add text to the tabe. You will see that it is responsive according to the number of text being inserted(more text the column will go to the right). # Move the line that separates the two columns. # Add the text but when you are next to the line that separates the columns, add an empty space. (i) There's a MP4 video reproducing the error. h3. Expected Results * The table will continue to grow to the right since it is responsive. * The empty space char shouldn't be treated as line break. * Table should show """"Fixed Width"""" instead of """"Responsive""""(After resizing the columns manually). h3. Actual Results The empty space will add a line break, like the table is no longer responsive, but if you do not add the empty space, the line will continue to be responsive. h3. dwadawdawdawdawd dawd aw daw aw daw

summary: "Plans to end support for IE11"
description: """{panel:title=Atlassian status as of July 2020|borderStyle=solid|borderColor=#B2D4FF | titleBGColor=#B2D4FF | bgColor=#EAF1FC}  Microsoft has not released any major updates for IE11 since 2015, and has [recently discouraged the use of IE11 as a default browser|https://techcommunity.microsoft.com/t5/Windows-IT-Pro-Blog/The-perils-of-using-Internet-Explorer-as-your-default-browser/ba-p/331732].    To allow us to continue to take advantage of modern web standards to deliver improved functionality and the best possible user experience across all of our products, we have decided to end support for IE11. *Confluence 7.4 (LTS) is the last IE11-supported version.*    End of support means we will not fix bugs that are specific to IE11, and will begin to introduce features that aren't compatible with this browser.    Find out more about this change from our [announcement|https://community.<USER>t5/Feedback-Forum-articles/Retiring-IE11-support-for-Atlassian-cloud-server-and-data-center/ba-p/1185312].    If you have questions or concerns about this change, please comment on this issue.    {panel}"""

summary: "Page PDF export size significantly increases if page consist of .jpeg/jpg images"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Page PDF export size significantly increases if page consist of .jpeg/jpg images  h3. Environment    Confluence 7.0.1, 6.15.9  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Prepare  .jpeg and .png files. Note the size of both files.   # Create two Confluence pages. Add .jpeg image to the page A and .png attachment to page B   # Export both pages to PDF   # Check the size of generated PDF export files    h3. Expected Results    Size of PDF export is similar to the size of the images (or smaller due to the compression)  h3. Actual Results   * PDF export from page A with .jpeg attachment (85KB) is 422kb   * PDF export from page B with .png attachment (355KB) is 180kb    h3. Notes   * For jpeg attachment (3.2mb) page pdf export increases to 17mb   * Adding multiple jpeg attachments multiplies the increase in PDF export size    h3. Workaround    We found a 2 steps workaround which consists of:   * Using the *Export to Word* feature to get the Confluence page saved as a *doc* file first   * Then, open the *doc* file in Microsoft Word, go to *File > Print* and select the *Microsoft Print to PDF* in the printer dropdown list to save the .doc file to a .pdf file."""

summary: "ConversionContext.getContentTree returns one page when --data-version is included"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    When using {{ConversionContext.getContentTree}} API to get all pages in a tree it returns 1 or current page for Confluence server 6.12.1 but returns all for 6.11.0 but only with {{--data-version}} argument on ASDK debug.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Download sample app: [^conf-transformer-test.zip]   # Run """"atlas-clean && atlas-run -v 6.12.0 --data-version 6.12.0"""" in conf-transformer-test folder   # In Demonstration space:   # Go to Space tools -> Content tools -> Export   # Select PDF   # Select type (I've tried both Normal and Custom)   # Notice """"Found 1 pages"""" in log and debugger for each page in PDF   # Create DEV space with 4 pages (3 siblings and 1 child page)   # Follow the same steps as for DS and get the same result    h3. Expected Results    ConversionContext.getContentTree returns a object containing all pages to be included in export  h3. Actual Results    ConversionContext.getContentTree returns an object containing only current page  h3. Notes   * API returns expected number of pages when command is {{atlas-clean && atlas-debug -version 6.11.0}} or {{6.12.0}}   * From server ecosystem team: """"--data-version should match --version unless one might want to test for an upgrade path from an older database version. In some cases not setting --data-version and setting --version  can have undesired results because it may try and set --data-version to latest and that could be a cloud version though it's unlikely""""    h3. Impact   * From vendor:  {quote}My add-ons provide special behavior when multiple pages are exported in one PDF. Unfortunately this is no longer possible in Confluence 6.13 because of ConversionContext.getContentTree now only returns the current page, not the list of all pages included in the PDF. There doesn't appear to be any other way to get this information.    This change means that my users will lose a useful feature.  {quote}   * Vendor has *_1.5k_* customer installs potentially affected by this   * This has not been tested on prod instance    h3. Workaround    Use {{atlas-clean && atlas-debug -version 6.11.0}} or {{6.12.0}} when debugging"""

summary: "To Display all Space Administrators on Space Tools can bring Confluence down"
description: """h2. Issue Summary  Confluence provides one with the ability of checking who are the administrators of a given Space by going to Space Tools -> Overview and checking the names right next to the line that says *Administrators*. If this given Space has too many Administrators, the list may be collapsed and an ellipsis button may appear at the end of the line for one to expand and see the full list. Confluence can hit 100% of CPU usage {color:red}and may go down{color} depending on the size of the full list.    h2. Steps to Reproduce  This can be easily reproduced by adding a group with multiple users as Space Admin. It is unknown how many users would be necessary to trigger this behaviour, however, it has been observed that an instance with 14.000 users within a group ran into this problem when this group was added as Space Admin.  # Ensure that you have a group with a few thousand users at least on it.  # As an administrator, click on the Cog Icon in your right upper corner and then click on General Configuration.  # Look for Space Permissions.  # Select a space and click on Manage Permissions.  # A list of groups and users will appear. Click on Edit Permissions nearby the group list.  # Look for the group with multiple users and check the Admin checkbox under the Space column.  # Save the changes.  # Now, go to that Space, click on Space Tools and then click on Overview.  # Click on the Ellipsis button right next to the Administrators line.  # At this point, CPU usage will increase and the application may get stuck forever running the following endpoint:  {code}  /spaces/viewspacesummary.action?key=SPAC&showAllAdmins=true  {code}    h2. Expected Results  Instead of crashing, the application should either display a list of Administrator or a message saying that the list is too long to be rendered.    h2. Actual Results  CPU goes wild, the list is never rendered and the application most than likely needs to be restarted to get functional once again.    h2. Notes  Ideally, the application should avoid rendering huge lists or there should have been a pagination mechanism to avoid this problem from happening.    h2. Workaround  Avoid using groups as Space Administrators unless these groups have a small list of users within them.    h2. Extra - Technical Notes - AKA What Happens in the Backend  First, Confluence will grab a list of everyone that has Administrator Permissions for that space - This include user IDs and group IDs:  {code:sql}  select permission0_.SPACEID as SPACEID2_52_0_, permission0_.PERMID as PERMID1_52_0_, permission0_.PERMID   as PERMID1_52_1_, permission0_.SPACEID as SPACEID2_52_1_, permission0_.PERMTYPE as PERMTYPE3_52_1_, permission0_.PERMGROUPNAME   as PERMGROU4_52_1_, permission0_.PERMUSERNAME as PERMUSER5_52_1_, permission0_.PERMALLUSERSSUBJECT as PERMALLU6_52_1_, permission0_.CREATOR   as CREATOR7_52_1_, permission0_.CREATIONDATE as CREATION8_52_1_, permission0_.LASTMODIFIER as LASTMODI9_52_1_, permission0_.LASTMODDATE   as LASTMOD10_52_1_ from SPACEPERMISSIONS permission0_ where permission0_.SPACEID='SPACE_ID_HERE';  {code}    Once that's done, the user IDs and group IDs will be used to create a list with actual names so one knows who the administrators really are:  {code:sql}  select internalus1_.user_name as col_0_0_ from cwd_membership hibernatem0_ cross join cwd_user internalus1_ cross join cwd_group internalgr2_   where hibernatem0_.child_user_id=internalus1_.id and hibernatem0_.parent_id=internalgr2_.id and (hibernatem0_.child_group_id is null)   and internalgr2_.lower_group_name='GROUPS_AND_USERS_FROM_STEP_1' and internalgr2_.directory_id='DIRECTORY_ID_FROM_STEP_1' order by internalus1_.lower_user_name;  {code}    With the results of the second query, the list is rendered. However, if the second query returns multiple results, such as thousands of users, Confluence will attempt to render a list with all of these names, which crashes the application."""

summary: "Non-ASCII title on page tree in space export is garbled when hosted on Windows"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  Non-ASCII title on page tree in space export is garbled when hosted on Windows    h3. Environment  * Hosted on Windows  ** I've replicated on Windows 10 but this symptom is observed on Windows Server 2016 as well.   ** This is not replicable when Confluence is hosted on Linux.      h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Install Confluence on Windows based OS   # Create a page with title """"日本語ページ""""   # Go to {{spaces/flyingpdf/flyingpdf.action?key=<space-id>}} and choose """"Custom Export""""    h3. Expected Results  Page tree displays """"日本語ページ"""" without being garbled    h3. Actual Results  Title is garbled and unreadable.   !Screen Shot 2019-09-20 at 15.08.07.png|thumbnail!     h3. Note  Title is not garbled on XML export or HTML export. So it is considered as a misbehaviours of {{flyingpdf.action}}  * {{spaces/exportspacexml.action?key=<space-id>}}   * {{spaces/exportspacehtml.action?key=<space-id>}}    h3. summary: "PDF export causes out of memory due to long indented numbered list in the page"
description: """h3. Summary    PDF export can cause Out Of Memory if the page contains a long indented/nested numbered list.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Create a page  # Edit the storage format with Source Editor and add the contents of this file:  #*  [^longindentedlist]   (!) This is a long indented numbered list, eg:    # Export the page to PDF    h3. Expected Results    The export completes.    h3. Actual Results  This can be seen in the atlassian-confluence.log file on a server instance:      This can be seen in catalina.out:      Notice that the renderer is in the process of parsing the numbered list based on the stack above (*ensureChildren* and *createChildren* methods).    (!) On a Data Center instance, the sandbox crashes while creating the export even if you increase its heap size significantly.     h3. Notes    This *problem does not affect versions below 6.13*, which indicates it is related to the bug fix below:    * https://jira.<USER>browse/CONFSERVER-54773    In 6.12 and earlier versions, the renderer does not identify the indented list and builds it as a normal numbered list, example:  # The following list in the Confluence page:    # Becomes this in the exported PDF:      The export tool is now able to identify the indented list and render it. However, the side effect is that it may OOM if the list is too long.    h3. Workaround    Remove the indented numbered list from the page or turn it into a normal numbered list (not indented) before exporting."""

summary: "Team Calendars time picker uses incorrect time format"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    When using any version of Team Calendars in Confluence version 7.0.x the time picker in displays times in 12h (AM/PM) by default and does not follow the settings in Team Calendar. In other versions of Confluence it works as expected.  h3. Environment    Confluence 7.0.x    Tested with:   Team Calendars 6.0.42   Team Calendars 6.0.37   Team Calendars 6.0.32       h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Install Team Calendars in Confluence 7.0.x   # When creating a new event the time format is in 12h (AM/PM)    h3. Expected Results    It is in 24h format, or follows the configuration settings  h3. Actual Results    It is always set to 12h (AM/PM)  h3. Workaround    No workaround is known at the moment"""
comments: "This isn't really a new bug in either Confluence 7.0.1 or TC. It's likely that users might notice it when Confluence is upgraded from a version older than 6.10.0 to a version newer than 6.10.0. The reason for this is the edit event dialog uses the user's locale to format the time, but the calendar view uses the TC configured format to display the time. If the user's locale is set to US English they see ""1:00 PM"" and if the user's locale is set to UK English they see ""13:00"". In versions older than Confluence 6.10.0 there was a bug where Confluence would not serve up the page in the right locale for the same language. In other words, a US English user could get UK English and vice versa. In most cases, this resulted in US English users getting served UK English locale. This is why prior to the upgrade, if you set up a 24 hour format, it would have worked by chance (because UK English locale uses 24 hour) and after the upgrade, Confluence correctly serves US English and reverts to 12 hours and it appears t

summary: "Confluence 7.0.1 Docker build does not on a multi node environment"
description: """h3. Issue summary  # While the Confluence Docker image does not seem to be officially supported as of yet (see CONFSERVER-55369), we do do have notes on our official page on how to set it up for Data Center.  ** https://hub.docker.com/r/atlassian/confluence-server  # The latest build however seems to be hitting into the same issue below, where the nodes advertise their private networks to each other and thus refuse to let them join the cluster.  ** BSERV-10837    h3.Workaround  At present the best workaround is to setup a docker-overlay network  https://docs.docker.com/network/overlay/    h3. Suggested Solution  Hazelcast has a solution to this by way of the configuration property  {{hazelcast.local.publicAddress}} but it is not exposed. Exposing this property should solve this problem."""

The user would like to have the feature of automatically create a JIRA issue macro when they create pages and include some keyword (summary, Description, issue type, and project key.) in the page content.    h3. summary: "The first login remove the data in remembermetoken table after the upgrade"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  Regardless of what TTL has been set in {{seraph-config.xml}} for remember me token. The data in the Confluence *remembermetoken* table will be removed after the upgrade when users try to log in at the very first time.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Install Confluence 6.6.12  # Create some users and set the *remembermetoken created date* (Big data) with the query below:  {code:language=sql}  update remembermetoken  set created = '1560230578'  where id = '360453'  {code}  # Upgrade the Confluence to 6.15.7  # Check the *remembermetoken* table, data was surviving the upgrade process  # Login test users, data removed from the *remembermetoken* table accordingly    Test results:  h4. Upgrade from Confluence 6.6.12 (default TTL is 1 year) to 6.15.3 (default TTL is 14 days)  ||Users||Surviving the upgrade||Token is not removed from the first login||  |User 1|(/)|(x)|  |User 2|(/)|(x)|  |User 3|(/)|(x)|  |admin|(/)|(/)|    h3. Expected Results  The token should not be removed from the database unless they are expired after the upgrade    h3. Actual Results  The token in database been removed and users will have to login again    h3. Note  It seems only the token that created on the day of the upgrade was not removed.    h3. Workaround  Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""
comments: "We have over 4000 users that need to manually re enter their details every 2 weeks now, where previously the remember me function held users details for as long as they used the feature. A lot of users do not remember their details, and do not apply for a new password once the feature expires after two weeks. That means less people have access to our intranet - the stats have dropped by 30% since the issue happened. This is a issue as we need all staff to be up to date with all of the policy changes, have quick access to the latest forms,  news <USER>     It's also making any planned upgrades more difficult."

summary: "Whitespace on user e-mail addresses should be sanitized on input"

The issues resulting from the current input method can be seen, for example, by following these steps:  # Create bogus user by setting their password , and with a valid email, but add whitespace at the beginning or end of the email in the email field.   # Use """"forgotuserpassword.action"""" using the above email without whitespace"""

Suggested Solution      Add the ability to choose if the user wants to display the word *issue* in the link.    h3. Why this is important       * Some customers use Confluence to build reports and might want to use only the number of issues returned as a link, not the word *issue*    h3. Workaround   N/A"""

summary: "Hibernate Sessions can't be fully flushed in upgrade tasks"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  Bug raised in Devhelp: https://ecosystem.atlassian.net/browse/DEVHELP-3241  {quote}  Hi,    I have explained more here: https://community.developer.<USER>t/transactions-in-activeobjects-upgrade-tasks/29700/2    Basically, the Data Center verification requires that we ensure we don't have """"big"""" Hibernate sessions in upgrade tasks, when we go over thousands of objects. They tell us to flush the session and commit the transaction regularly. But Atlassian doesn't provide any way to do that, unless I'm mistaken.     To reproduce the test, use the code in the 1st answer of the CDAC post.    Our product is not impacted yet because we've never had to perform a large upgrade task, but we need to be ready for the next time we need to update our data.  {quote}    h3. Expected Results  Provide a way to create isolated/REQUIRES_NEW transactions    h3. Actual Results  With the current proposed solution (which itself uses the non-recommended API ComponentManager.getInstance()): """"IllegalStateException: Transaction already active""""    h3. Workaround    Try to get a reference to the existing hibernate session (created by the parent) and then calling clear() and/or flush() on it, but we never tried this and I don’t know if we’d have run into the same problems with the unavailable OSGi packages that you noted."""
comments: "We, at Requirement <USER> have run into this issue, and we would profit from a solution. Thank you."

summary: "Page title with dot (.) at front breaks anchor link"
description: """h3. Steps to reproduce  # Create a page with title that contains a dot sign at the beginning of the title.  ** *Example: .Page  # Add the Anchor macro and give it a name.  ** *Example: Anchor1*  # While pasting the link with the anchor syntax directly in the browser works.  ** http://localhost:8090/display/S3/.Page#.Page-Anchor1  # If we were to use the link above, it loads the top of the page, and not the anchor.    h3. Workaround  # Add another word at the front of the title so that it does not start with a dot."""

summary: "Table editor collaboration request"
description: """Dear Confluence Cloud Team,    On behalf of the TechUp team I am reaching out to you asking for a collaboration request with your team around one of our Confluence apps Smart Tables currently on the Marketplace.  We believe that by building flawless and user-friendly experiences, we provide our customers the means to increase their teams' efficiency and engagement towards the Atlassian tools.    *Why Smart tables?*  Confluence users are eager to leverage native Confluence page editor's capabilities in order to perform their day to day tasks. Thus, building out tables in a few clicks by enriching the native confluence table using powerful operations and functions in the page editor experience is the main use case we addressed thanks to Smart tables.   As opposed to the other app's design and data scheme, we decided to lay the emphasis on a """"Confluence page editor"""" experience avoiding the use of macros, or importing an excel sheet in Confluence bringing therefore to the end-user the closest experience to the native confluence comfort level they are used to and more importantly an outstanding User experience by reducing the number of possible clicks to achieve their goal.   Smart tables has today more that 700 active instances on server in less of two years from the product launch and our customers shared the same feedbacks: """"ease of use"""" , """"user friendliness""""....    [check here|[https://marketplace-cdn.<USER>files/images/aaa9eafa-3d02-4b8d-af27-96c8c580664c.png]]         *New confluence editing experience:*  As a Marketplace vendor and fans of Atlassian toolset we totally support the company's growth shift to cloud-based services and products and are working towards this common objective.    The new editor of confluence pages, currently available in the Atlassian cloud relies on a React implementation of ProseMirror (the @atlaskit/editor). This new implementation does not allow us to interact with the editor instance in order for example to modify the content of cell when is formula is set.  That is a probably is a React / Component pattern which forces the state of the Editor component to be managed by the internal renderer. That is why, we would hope that you guys expose an Access point / bridge allowing extensions to be made on the new editor.      We are available to go through the technical details together maybe through a conference or any other format that is convenient for you.   We are looking forward to working together in order to support our customers with the new editor experience in the Atlassian cloud.    Cheers,"""

summary: "Unable to Indent Task List"
description: """h3. Summary  Unable to Indent Task List    h3. Environment  Confluence 6.15.8  Confluence 6.15.9  Confluence 7.0.1    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Create a page and insert a [Task|https://confluence.<USER>doc/add-assign-and-view-tasks-590260030.html] in the page so a checkbox will show  # Click on the Indent button from the editor so that the position of the checkbox can move to the right   !Indent Button.png|thumbnail!     h3. Expected Results  The checkbox from Task List Macro indented to the right     h3. Actual Results  Nothing happened    h3. Workaround  * Copy & Paste the _indented task_ from any existing page content  * Manually editing the Storage Format with plugin like [Source Editor|https://marketplace.<USER>apps/1210722/confluence-source-editor?hosting=server&tab=overview] and insert the hidden task list in front similar to the example given in the section below      h3. Information  This indentation can be performed in Confluence 6.13.0:   !Indent Task.png|thumbnail!       When the task list is indented, investigating the Storage Format we can notice that it is done by placing hidden task list in front of the displaying task list where the Storage Format will look something like this:    (i) Tasks with the _negative value_ in the {{task-id}} will be hidden    """

summary: "Generating a report of all macros/plugins used by a space but currently missing from the system"
description: """h3. Problem Definition    As an admin, you may run into the following scenario:   * You have a very populated Confluence instance   * You are migrating a space from your existing Confluence instance to a new one   * You want to make sure you have installed on the new instance all the *macros/plugins* the migrated space needs to work properly   * You are able to consult the *Macro Usage* page, where the used macros are listed. The pages where they are being used are also listed.   * You would like to generate a report of all the macros/plugins *used* by a space that are currently *missing* from the system    When a page uses *macros/plugins* that are disabled or not installed on the instance, the only indication of that is the *Unknown Macro* message displayed on that page.    On very populated spaces or sites, it may take a long time before noticing the missing any *macros/plugins* on the pages.  h3. Suggested Solution    The *Macro Usage* page can include a list of pages with missing *macros/plugins*. Identifying their spaces would be a plus.  h3. Workaround    Searching page by page for missing *macros/plugins*."""

summary: "As a Confluence administrator I would like to configure a timeout specific to long running user macros"
description: """h3. Problem Definition  An [User Macro|https://confluence.<USER>conf613/writing-user-macros-964961202.html] is a great way to extend Confluence functionalities, since the administrator can code virtually anything.    Depending on what the macro is coded for, its operation may take a long time to complete and, while it is running, it is prone to impact on Confluence performance.  Currently, there's no mechanism to kill the inner operation performed by the user macro if it is running for longer than a threshold.    h3. Suggested Solution  Introduce a configurable timeout to user macros and an option to asynchronously run them.  If a macro is taking a long time to complete, kill the macro rendering thread after the specified threshold.    Although the macro should be extensively tested for performance and security reasons, it would be great if Confluence could safeguard itself from expensive operations.     h3. Workaround   _No workaround so far."""

summary: "Registering custom SMTP transport not working"
description: """Hi,    we are trying out if our library is still working in Confluence 7.0, but it seems it isn't working any longer. It still worked fine in Confluence 6.x    We are putting our library in the libs-folder in Confluence, and inside, we register our custom SMTP transport, which so far was always picked up. But now, it doesn't get called any longer.    Can you help us out why this might happen? It looks like our library isn't loaded any longer, but we can't find any hints in the logs why this might be the case. Do you know where this might be protocoled?    If you need further information, I'm happy to provide it of course.    Thanks  <USER>""
comments: "We upgraded javamail from 1.4.5 to 1.6.2 in 7.0, but I can't see any reason for this to break. Have you tried debugging through `javax.mail.Session#loadProviders` to see which `javamail.providers` file it eventually loads?" [SEP] "Thank you for your help. I'll look into this and will report my findings." [SEP] "So, I got finally around to debug this Problem and also found why it didn't work :)    javamail in Version 1.6.2 is no longer using javamail.providers as its main source of checking what Transport/Store protocols to use, but uses a ServiceLoader instead. This loads a file in META-INF/services/javax.mail.Provider, in which the class names of classes are listed, which extend javax.mail.Provider. These Providers basically just wrap the values once provided in javamail.providers into a class.    Once I defined this and created the corresponding classes, our custom SMTP Transport was again used." [SEP] "An Addendum, it seems that it only works if the class loader picks up our jar where we keep our provide

summary: "Revoking the Add Page Space permission from all groups and users in a specific Space still allows confluence-administrators to create a new blank page in that Space"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  Members of the {{confluence-administrators}} group are treated as a _special user_ in Confluence as described in [Global Permissions Overview|https://confluence.<USER>doc/global-permissions-overview-138709.html#GlobalPermissionsOverview-Comparingtheconfluence-administratorsgroupwiththeadministratorpermissions].  This documentation states that members of that group would still consider the [Space Permissions|https://confluence.<USER>doc/space-permissions-overview-139521.html] and [Page Restrictions|https://confluence.<USER>doc/page-restrictions-139414.html] when editing a page. However, there's no reference whatsoever to creating a new page, which uses the same permission on the Space level.  The problem is that even when the _Add pages_ space permission is revoked from all groups and users within a Space, members of the {{confluence-administrators}} can still create a page in that Space, while editing an existing page isn't possible.  It would be expected that consistency occurs among the same space permission, preventing members of the {{confluence-administrators}} from creating pages in the above situation.    h3. Environment  * This was validated on Confluence Server 6.13.4 and 6.15.4.  * It is expected this bug affects any version prior to, and including, 6.15.X.  * This also affects Confluence Data Center deployments.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Install a vanilla instance of Confluence.  # Create a sample Space.  # Logged with an user part of the {{confluence-administrators}} group, edit the permissions of this Space.   #* Make sure only the {{confluence-users}} group has permissions assigned, removing any other group and users.   #* Make sure only the _View_ and the _Space Admin_ permissions are assigned to the {{confluence-users}} group.  # Go back to the Home Page of the target space and note that the _Edit_ button in that page is gone.  # Click on the _Create blank page_ button.    h3. Expected Results  Confluence doesn't allow a page to be created in the current Space, since the _Add Page_ Space permission was revoked from all groups and users in that Space.    h3. Actual Results  Confluence still allows users of the {{confluence-administrators}} group to create a page.    h3. Notes  If trying to create a page using a template other than the blank page, for example the _How-to article_ template, the admin is able to navigate through the template form, but creating the page fails with a _Not Permitted_ error page as the image below.   !create-from-template-img001.png|thumbnail!     h3. Workaround  _Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available._"""

summary: "Confluence upgrade process should not chown/chmod home directory."
description: """h3. Problem Definition  Currently running the upgrade using the installer, calls for a chown and chmod on all the home directory. In case of big home directory folder, this can take a lot of time and increases the downtime of Confluence.    h3. Suggested Solution  Would be great if you could change the code in the upgrade to instead ask the user yes/no - if we want to run chmod and chown. And/or also this option in the varfile     h3. Workaround  You can opt for a manual upgrade process where you will need to download the installation archive file (.zip/.tar.gz) and follow the steps as described in [Upgrading Confluence Manually|https://confluence.<USER>doc/upgrading-confluence-manually-255363437.html] to attempt an upgrade without having to run any of the ownership change commands """"Assuming that they are correct""""."""
comments: "Thanks for raising this as a suggested feature!     Our home directory is 300gb (and growing). These two processes take about ~2 hours each with that amount of data. Increasing our maintenance window by 4 hours. Which is not feasible.    Another workaround is to manually kill the chown & chmod processes. The installation will finish successfully anyway. But that workaround is manual. In a process that we want fully automated in our AWS environment.    Cheers,  Emil" [SEP] "For us the upgrade took almost 5-6 hours to complete because of the above mentioned issue.     I would like to vote for suggested solution. " [SEP] "Our attachment folder is currently 3,3 TB in size. Our update with the installer takes quite a lot of time. Please suggest this raised issue."

summary: "Cannot add empty task list when Collaborative Editing is disabled"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Cannot add an empty task list (so it shows just the tick box) to Confluence editor when Collaborative Editing is disabled.    (on) Works when Collaborative Editing is enabled (/)    h3. Environment    * Affects Confluence 6.14+ - v4 Editor(x)  * Does not affect Confluence 6.13.x - v3 Editor (/)    h3. Steps to Reproduce     # Install Confluence 6.14.0   # *Disable Collaborative Editing*   # Add a task list but with no text (so it shows just a tick box)    h3. Expected Results    # Published page should show an empty tick box    h3. Actual Results    # Published page does not show anything where the tick box was showing on the Edit page    When an empty task list is added *with Collaborative editing disabled*, this is displayed in the Source Editor from the Edit Page:        - no task list when it is expected above (x)    When an empty task list is added *with Collaborative editing enabled*, this is displayed in the Source Editor from the Edit Page:        - empty task list as expected above (/)    This confirms the empty task list is already lost +during the Edit+ when Collaborative Editing is disabled.    h3. Workaround    * Adding a space will show an empty tick box when Collaborative Editing is disabled;  * Alternatively, enable Collaborative Editing which is not affected by this bug  """

summary: "Official support for deploying Confluence Server on Kubernetes"
description: """In the interest of expanding the officially supported hosting options, it would be advantageous to have a tested and supported method/process for deploying Confluence on Kubernetes, as it's an option that's suited for hosting containerized applications and is therefore a good fit for running Confluence."""
comments: "Having got Confluence Data Center (cluster of 2 nodes) running on Docker Swarm, I thought it would be a good exercise to use Kubernetes as the management framework. I have tried running Confluence (Data Center - so I can run a cluster) on Kubernetes and the following is currently my blocker.    Whilst it is possible to mount a DFS share (!) into a container started by Kubernetes, for shared documents (using the fstab~cifs plugin), I am having great difficulty with providing persistent local storage to the containers.     Within my Docker installation, I had two mount points: local home directory and installation directory. I attempted to reproduce this approach for kubernetes, however it doesn't seem to work - and I think its due the order things get instantiated.    In lay-persons terms, Docker Swarm appears to do the following: create the container, mount volumes into the container, start the image - and so any external storage is visible when the image pull happens and the container then executes. Kuber

If possible, it would also be helpful to be able to customize it according to which space is being used.    In my company we have three scenarios where that feature would be useful:   # Using confluence to display only pages, it would be useful to implement it with our site without disturbing the design. We could simply link directly to specific pages without being limitation and when the user click on the top right icon, he would be sent back to our site, basically having a better user experience (in that scenario). As it is now, i need to create a link on a page to achieve the same result.   # Im creating a space for our documentation in English and another in Portuguese. If i set my icon to send the user to the English space as the standard, when a user is reading the Portuguese version and clicks on our icon, he will be redirected to our English page, if that was contextual, it would be very practical.   # Considering the previous scenario, if i was able to work with external links, i would be able to resolve my problem simply trought the site side, not trough confluence.    Thanks in advance."""

summary: "When editing a page in Confluence hovering the close button displays ""Keep the draft or discard the changes."""
description: """h3. Issue Summary   The hover text on the close button on the edit page screen says """"Keep the draft or discard the changes."""" This message is confusing as the discard changes feature has been moved over to the ... button and the close button always keeps changes as unpublished drafts.     h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Create a page.  # Select edit button to edit the page.  # Hover over the Close button.    h3. Expected Results  A message in the hover text that reflects the changes made with the close and ... buttons.     h3. Actual Results  A misleading message that appears to be a remnant from the previous functionality of the close button.    !incorrect hover text on Close button.png|thumbnail!     h3. Workaround  There is no work around currently available. """
comments: "not reproducable in 7.1.0 - seems to be fixed there"

summary: "Turning on profiling has no effect on generated logging"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Enabling profiling has no effect on logging in Confluence 7.0.1.  h3. Environment    Confluence 7.0.1  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Enable profiling via the UI:  *Confluence Administration >> General Configuration >> Logging and profiling >> Enable profiling*   # Check atlassian-confluence.log    h3. Expected Results    Additional profiling logging should be added to atlassian-confluence.log for each active thread. For example:    h3. Actual Results    No expected profiling output is generated.       h3. Workaround    * Navigate to *Confluence Administration* >> *General Configuration* >> *Logging and profiling*  ** Click *Enable Profiling*; *and*  ** Manually add *{{com.atlassian.util.profiling.Timers}}* as *DEBUG* logging  ** Manually add *{{com.atlassian.util.profiling.UtilTimerStack}}* as *DEBUG* logging        """
comments: "I have updated the docs to call out this issue, and provide steps for the workaround. " [SEP] "We are also seeing this issue on 7.2.2, the workaround worked though. " [SEP] "Issue persists in version 7.4"

summary: "After upgrading Confluence, in product notification incorrectly indicates this version vulnerable to CVE"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    After upgrade Confluence from 6.15.4 to 7.0.1, some sites are prompted with a CVE warning. Upon review, the CVE details clearly indicate that 7.0.1 and higher are not affected and in turn this notification does not apply.    This causes unwanted review and time spent to review a CVE that is not applicable to our upgraded product.  It can also cause panic and frustration to administrators that just upgraded to incorrectly see this message.    h3. Environment    * Confluence server, upgrade from 6.15.4 to 7.0.1  * Have Confluence 6.15.x installed  * Have a valid Commercial license type activated in product      h3. Steps to Reproduce    # Wait a few days to allow Confluence to check in the SEN with the older, vulnerable version of Confluence.   # Upgrade to Confluence 7.0.1   # Upon the first login, after the upgrade you're prompted with:   !Screen Shot 2019-09-11 at 9.12.16 AM.png|thumbnail!     h3. Expected Results    Upon the upgrade of a product to a version outside of a CVE notice we should not be prompted that we're still vulnerable to said CVE.    h3. Actual Results  We're prompted that we're still vulnerable to a CVE that is not relevant to our upgraded version.    h3. Notes  In order for this to happen, it appears the admin would have to upgrade in between the time period that Atlassian has collected the version number associated with that SEN and the time Atlassian has sent this whisper notification to appear in product to that SEN.     h3. Workaround  Ignore the CVE notice as it does not pertain to our current version."""

summary: "Confluence Android Mobile App allows user to create page in space that does not have Add Page permission"
description: """h3. Summary  Confluence Android Mobile App allows user to create page in space that does not have Add Page permission    h3. Environment  Confluence 6.15.8  [Confluence Server Android App|https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=com.atlassian.confluence.server] v0.2.21  Tested on Android 7.1.1    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Create a new user and a new space grand only the accesses below:  #* Global Permission: Can Use  #* Space Permission(for the new space): All - View  # Login the newly created user from the web browser to make sure this user can only view the new space but not edit nor create any content  # Login Confluence using Android Mobile App and click on the """"+"""" button on the bottom right to create a new page  # Click on the _3 dots_ on the top right corner and select *Location*  # On the Top Center menu, navigate to *Spaces* to show all spaces in Confluence  # Select the newly created space  # Give the page a title and Click *Publish* to create the page    h3. Expected Results  User should not be able to create page in the space that is not granted with sufficient permission    h3. Actual Results  A page is created in the space      h3. Workaround  Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""
comments: "I've had a look at this and was able to replicate the issue.    The mobile APIs called in order for this scenario are:    {code:title=Actually creates the page. Permissions checks are present here but as we haven't switched space yet it is successful.}  POST /conf/testing/rest/api/content          {code:title=Updates the page with the new location (permissions checks do not occur here)}  PUT https://mobile.bb-svr-dev.internal.<USER>conf/testing/rest/api/content/19496981?status=draft  {code}    It's arguable that permissions checks should be done in the page update API but we can opt to handle it the same way as web by not listing spaces where the user does not have `Add Page` permissions.    I have created some related tickets for us that will address this issue.  "

summary: "Confluence doesn't respect directory order while creating a group when connected to Crowd or Jira Directory"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Confluence creates a group in Crowd or Jira, even it their directory order is lower.  h3. Environment    * Confluence 7.0.1   * Crowd 3.5.0   * Jira 8.3.3    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Create a User Server in Jira  # Connect Confluence as a [User Server|https://confluence.<USER>doc/connecting-to-crowd-or-jira-for-user-management-229838465.html] to Jira  # Give this directory *Read/Write* permissions  # From the Directory Order in Confluence, make sure that Jira is at the *second* priority  #* Internal Directory should be highest  # Create a group in Confluence    h3. Expected Results    Group should only be created in Confluence's Internal Directory, as all operations should be executed on the highest ordered directory      h3. Actual Results    Group is created in Jira's directory    h3. Notes    The same steps can be repeated for Crowd as well. Creating a group in Confluence will result in a group in Crowd, even though Crowd directory has a lower order    h3. Notes    This behaviour is not according to our guidelines about [Multiple Directories|https://confluence.<USER>doc/managing-multiple-directories-229838494.html] ,  {quote}  Changes to users and groups will be made only in the first directory where the application has permission to make changes.  {quote}    This also causes other unexpected issues if Jira or Crowd is connected to other LDAP servers because of daisy chained group propagation. """

summary: "Recently updated dashboard display problem"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  There are some display problems with the recently updated dashboard.  * <time> element should represent datetimes only, however we can see timestamp + space + opening bracket  * The opening bracket has different colour and size than the ending bracket    h3. Expected Results  * Only datetimes in <time> element  * Align opening and ending brackets    h3. Actual Results  !Screen Shot 2019-09-11 at 1.25.08 pm.png|width=500!     h3. Workaround  Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""
comments: "The {{time}} element can contain any text but its {{datetime}} attribute must contain the date and time in ISO 8601 format. Leaving it empty violates the HTML specification.    Having the exact machine-readable date and time in the {{time}} element’s {{datetime}} attribute facilitates client-side customization of the date format, for users who prefer to see absolute dates and times rather than relative expressions such as “2 hours ago”."

summary: "Add toggle flag when copying a page to include/exclude attachments"
description: """As an end user, I would like to be able to make copies of pages and only their content while not including some or all attachments on the page. I would like for there to be a modal/wizard during the copy operation (or maybe an advanced copy operation?) that allows me to designate what attachments to grab (images but not .ZIPs, for example)."""

summary: "As a Confluence user I would like to include disabled/inactive users in a task report macro"
description: """h3. Problem Definition  When users are [disabled|https://confluence.<USER>conf613/delete-or-disable-users-964961157.html] in the Confluence user directory we are unable to search them because they are not active in the Lucene index.  Because of this, I am unable to create a [task report macro|https://confluence.<USER>doc/add-assign-and-view-tasks-590260030.html] targeting disabled users, since they won't appear in the _Assigned to_ search box when configuring the macro.    h3. Suggested Solution  Make it possible to search for disabled users when configuring the task report macro.     h3. Workaround   The workaround below is valid if you need to have a page with a task report macro targeting a disabled user.    # Install a Source Editor App available in the Marketplace.   #* Suggested Apps are [Confluence Source Editor|https://marketplace.<USER>apps/1210722/confluence-source-editor?hosting=server&tab=overview] (unsupported) or [Source Editor for Confluence|https://marketplace.<USER>apps/1215664/source-editor-for-confluence?hosting=server&tab=overview] (supported by _[Mohami|https://mohamicorp.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/DOC/pages/1966116/Source+Editor+for+Confluence]_).  # In the Confluence database, get the {{user_key}} of the target user -- this key will be used later.    # In a Confluence page, add a task report macro and configure it targeting any sample, active user.  # Edit the same page and now use one of the Source Editor Apps to configure the source of the page.  # Search for the macro definition, which might be similar to the below.    # Where you see {{<ri:user ri:userkey=""""<sample user key>""""/>}}, change the key with the one you retrieved from the database and apply the changes to the page.   #* If you want to add more users, simply add more {{<ri:user ri:userkey=""""<sample user key>""""/>}} tags inside the {{<ac:parameter ac:name=""""assignees"""">}}.      If you want a list of pages with tasks assigned to a target user, without the need to have a task report macro, you may rely on a REST API call as below.  You may run this API as a Confluence user, instead of an admin, but you may not have access to all pages on which the target user had tasks assigned.  """

summary: "Unable to change content alignment of multiple cells in one operation in IE11"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  It is unable to change content alignment of multiple cells in one operation in IE11. Only single cell changes its content alignment even if multiple cells is selected.     h3. Environment  IE11     h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Open an editor in Confluence   # Add 2x2 table   # Highlight all cells    # Click """"Align Center"""" button      !Screen Shot 2019-09-10 at 16.55.16.png|thumbnail!     h3. Expected Results  Contents in all cells are aligned to Center    h3. summary: "Allow admin to select the email notification language when creating a new user account."
description: """h3. Summary  Currently, when a new user account is created in Confluence, Confleucne will send the email notification with the language configured in the Language page. This may cause some confusion and inconvenient for user from different country.    h3. Suggestion  Allow the admin to configure what language of the new account email notification will be send to the user when creating the user."""

summary: "Suggestion for collaboration with Smart Tables on the new editor experience"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Issue raised in DEVHELP:    {quote}  Dear Confluence Product Team,    On behalf of the TechUp team I am reaching out to you asking for a collaboration request with your team around one of our Confluence apps Smart Tables currently on the Marketplace.   We believe that by building flawless and user-friendly experiences, we provide our customers the means to increase their teams' efficiency and engagement towards the Atlassian tools.  Why Smart tables?   Confluence users are eager to leverage native Confluence page editor's capabilities in order to perform their day to day tasks. Thus, building out tables in a few clicks by enriching the native confluence table using powerful operations and functions in the page editor experience is the main use case we addressed thanks to Smart tables. As opposed to the other app's design and data scheme, we decided to lay the emphasis on a """"Confluence page editor"""" experience avoiding the use of macros, or importing an excel sheet in Confluence bringing therefore to the end-user the closest experience to the native confluence comfort level they are used to and more importantly an outstanding USer experience by reducing the number of possible clicks to achieve their goal. Smart tables has today more that 700 active instances in less of two years from the product launch and our customers shared the same feedbacks: """"ease of use"""" , """"user friendliness""""....  New confluence editing experience:   With the new confluence editing experince, we are have working hard in order to make our product compatible with new table editor api.  However for technical reasons, its appears that the new editor is locked by Atlassian and it is impossible for us based on the current api to make smart tables compatible unless we go through macros.  We still believe in the added value of our product outside the scope of macros and for that reason we reach out to you guys to find out if there a way of collaboration together.  Our vision for the current version of smart tables was to extend the current editor through the """"formulas button"""" (please check attached)    Our suggestion for you guys is that we can work together on a technical solution that we will make the new confluence editor extendable, we are convinced that we will bring new horizons of possiblies and not just only for calculation.  {quote}    h3. Workaround  No workaround"""
comments: "Hi [~<USER>,    thanks for raising this concern.  Can you please be more specific as to what the editor API does not let you do?   Was there any recent change that affected your plugin?" [SEP] "Hi @<USER>   Since the new page editor experience will be soon available on the Confluence server, we are eager to make sure that our apps will still be delivering a value added to our customer.   In other terms, we need to support the new editor experience that is currently deployed in Atlassian cloud and coming soon to the server.   Going Forward , we suggest to team Up with you guys on this topic and work together on a discovery plan.         Let me be more technical, currently our app [Smart tables|[https://marketplace.<USER>apps/1218080/smart-tables-for-confluence?hosting=server&tab=overview]]  works on the top of the current confluence page editor which is TinyMCE.  We developed our app based on the exposed functions of TinyMCE and also some triggers in order to implement ""Excel"" like formula experience i

summary: "Option to default to full results for Quick Navigation searches"
description: """When you search in Confluence and Quick Navigation is enabled, pressing enter takes the user to the top Quick Navigation result if one exists. There is an option to open the full search results instead, but this requires a user to scroll down and select that singular option.    What seems like it would make more sense from the user perspective of someone searching for content is to default to showing the full search results. If a user sees a Quick Navigation result that is applicable, they can scroll to it and select it instead of hitting enter to see the full list of results.    So this request would be to add a configuration related to Search, or Quick Navigation, that would make showing the full search results the default behavior when clicking enter.     There is the option of disabling Quick Navigation, which then always opens the full search results, but then you lose the nice Quick Navigation suggestions when they are applicable. """

summary: "Bulk change ownership during XML Space Import"
description: """As a +system admin+    I want to +have a way to change pages owners (author, last modifier) during a Space import operation+   So that +If I receive an exported space in XML format, I can easily do a """"from-to"""" operation to assign pages to new owners+  h4. Suggestion Summary   - Customer company A is bought by company B.   - Both have Confluence, Directories are not integrated   - Sysadmins on company B create users for employees from company A, but they do not always match the ones they had in A (such as, A is johnsmith, B is jsmith)   - Customer would rather have pages assigned correctly, than listing _unknown user johnsmith_    h4. Proposed Solution    Import operation could allow admins to input a from-to CSV file indicating old user names and new ones, to prevent that issue.  h4. Possible Workarounds   - Have the space imported in a non-production instance and run the procedures from:   [How To - Transfer Content Ownership in between Users|https://confluence.<USER>confkb/how-to-transfer-content-ownership-in-between-users-958450595.html]   - Try find-and-replace operations on the XML file such as        Again, we must be mindful about the space name. I don't think you gonna have spaces with usernames on it, but, just in case, be mindful about that. If that happens, after you have SED'ed your way, open the entities.xml file and look for the tag below:    Once you found it, just replace the name accordingly to respect the previous space name and save the file. Then follow the steps previously outlined here and we should be fine.  """

summary: "remove required label from page property report macro"
description: """Since cql was introduced, a lot of macros rely on cql-conditions. The page property report macro does as well. A powerful tool was introduced with cql to express conditions. Though I can use a lot of diverse data to describe filtering I am still forced to use a label - even if I have other structural data like metadata by Communardo for example.  It would be sufficient just to rely on metadatasets, but I am forced to make sure that metadatasets and labels are adressing the same pages. I have two structural criterias describing the same content.    If the label wouldn't be necessary to make page property report output a list then it would decrease a lot of effort to synchronize and double our information architecture.    Please make parameter label not required or better allow free use of cql to to describe content whose page properties have to be shown."""

summary: "Default title for tip, info, note and warning macros"
description: """Global settings to define default values for tip, info, note and warning macro titles settings would greatly increase readability and consistency across such alerts.    Ideally they could be overwritten with individual space configurations."""

summary: "Page changes not reflected on mobile app until page is manually refreshed"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  * When a page is edited in Confluence, and if it was already opened in the Confluence mobile app, the content is not refreshed until we manually refresh the page using the pull down.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Create a page in Confluence using the desktop browsers.  # Open the page in the Confluence mobile app.  # Edit the page in the desktop browser.  # View the page in the Confluence mobile app, and it will still show the older content.  ## If we navigate back, and open the page again, it still shows the older content.  ## If we close the app, and open it again, it still shows the older content.  # Until we manually refresh the page, the older content is shown.    h3. Expected Results  # Best case, the page is updated automatically on page edit.  # Or a notification should be shown saying that the page was edited, and the content *should be refreshed* when we navigate away and return to that page, or when the app is reopened.    h3. Actual Results  # Until the page is manually refreshed using the pull down, the older content is shown.    h3. Workaround  # Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior."""
comments: "Hi,  Thanks for raising this issue, this would be a great  feature to add, but its not on  our current roadmap and the application is working as designed.   Closing this issue." [SEP] "Hi,     from my point of view the application should not show old content after reopening the page or even the app. This is not what the user expects.    Best regards, Kathrin Steinhauser"

description:
"""h3. Issue Summary Copying large tables can lead to the loss of emoticons and replaces them with
tags. It seems as though there is some limit I'm unable to determine. h3. Steps to Reproduce # Use the storage format included in the comments # Attempt to copy/pase any more than ~15 rows # Notice all emoticons have disappeared h3. Expected Results * Table is copied with all data h3. Actual Results * Table is missing emoticons h3. Notes * No errors found in the logs * Tried testing with TinyMCE online and it did not seem to have a problem with that much data * Unable to find a definite method to trigger this scenario. In my attemps to create my own tables with more data the copying seemed to work, bvut with this storage format it is reliably reproduced. * Tried removing all merged cells and any extra macros. This is how it appears after pasting into another page with the missing data: !MissingEmoticons.png|thumbnail! h3. Workaround Using the Confluene Source Editor you can copy the

summary: "Page version should be updated when page attachment gets new version"
description: """h3. Problem Definition    When a page attachment gets updated (new version of the attachment) there is no change of the page version. This leads to users not being aware of the change in the attachment, even they check the page's history.    h3. Suggested Solution    The page version should increase when a page attachment gets a new version. This could be a configurable option to allow the administrator to choose the behaviour.  """

summary: "Bold category headers in Confluence administration"
description: """It's currently difficult to navigate quickly to a topic in the left panel because there's no easy way to scan to a category header in General Configuration. We end up having to read through all the topics till we get to the desired one.    Right now, a category header is in all caps. But in the same font style. I'm suggesting making the category header bold so that it become easier to scan and get to a topic header and then the actual topic.     So we'd have configuration, Atlassian market place, users and security, look and feel and any third party added application headers in bold.    I'd also suggest being able to collapse this list so we expand and view only the section that we have the topic in.          """

summary: "Failed to extract index from Word doc file smaller than 16MB."
description: """h3. Issue Summary  h3. The following error is seen when attaching a 12MB Word file:  {code:java}  2019-08-14 17:02:52,191 WARN [attachment-text-extraction-worker-2] [bonnie.search.extractor.BaseAttachmentContentExtractor] addFields Error indexing attachment (Attachment: test v1.0.docx v.1 (31099938) user1)2019-08-14 17:02:52,191 WARN [attachment-text-extraction-worker-2] [bonnie.search.extractor.BaseAttachmentContentExtractor] addFields Error indexing attachment (Attachment: test v1.0.docx v.1 (31099938) user1)com.atlassian.bonnie.search.extractor.ExtractorException: java.lang.Exception: Error reading content of Word XML document: Document too big for text extraction, bailing out at com.atlassian.confluence.extra.officeconnector.index.word.WordXMLTextExtractor.extractText(WordXMLTextExtractor.java:53) {code}  h3. Environment    The issue can be reproduced with   * Confluence 6.15.2, 6.15.9   * MS SQL Server   * Postgres9.6    h3. Steps to Reproduce    The sample doc files(attached) can be used to reproduce issues.   - sample_file2.docx: filesize:14181362 Byte, and no error.   - sample_file3.docx: filesize: 14259624 Byte, and error.     # Create a page and attach sample_file3.docx   # Check confluence log, and will see the exception, *Document too big for text extraction*    h3. Expected Results  h3. No error for less than 16MB word doc file.  h3. Actual Results    The below exception is thrown in the atlassian-confluence.log file after enable debug:   * com.atlassian.confluence.internal.index   * com.atlassian.confluence.search.lucene   * com.atlassian.bonnie.search.extractor      h3. Workaround    No workaround  {panel:title=Atlassian status as of July 2020|borderStyle=solid|borderColor=#b2d4ff|titleBGColor=#b2d4ff|bgColor=#eaf1fc}  The size check is based on the size of the file when it's uncompressed. As  .docx files are compressed, text extraction may fail even though the file size on disk appears to be under the 16mb limit.    You can increase the limit using the {{officeconnector.textextract.word.docxmaxsize}} [system property|https://confluence.<USER>display/DOC/Configuring+System+Properties].    We plan to improve the error message for this check in a future release.  {panel}"""
comments: "The KB article should be updated. It is misleading (i.e. not all 16Mb of word doc would be indexed always)." [SEP] "Any update on this?" [SEP] "Any update on this? It has been six months since opened initially." [SEP] "Sorry to hear this has been causing difficulties. Just wanted to drop a comment here to explain that this check is working as expected.  It uses the size of the file when uncompressed. Because .docx files are compressed, text extraction can fail, even when the file size appears to be under the 16mb limit.     We can definitely improve on the error message in the logs, to make it more descriptive. " [SEP] "Instead of showing a message in the log file, is there any way to create a warning pop-up message (e.g. indexing would not work this file) when the file is attached to a page?    That would be more useful for a user."

summary: "New Line and Space in .ics Fails to Import into Team Calendars"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    When a field runs long, *ics* syntax dictates that the field will continue on a new line after beginning with a space (see lines 13, 46, 55 for examples in *sample-failure.ics*). Team Calendars fails to read this notation.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Navigate to Calendars   # Select Ellipsis (...) next to Add Calendar   # Import Calendar   # Attach *sample-failure.ics*    h3. Expected Results    Team Calendars will read in the ics file.  h3. Actual Results    """"*The uploaded data does not seem to be iCalendar content*"""" error presented and the upload fails.  h3. Notes   * sample-failure.ics tested to successfully import into Apple Calendars and Outlook   * Also reproduces via *Subscribe by URL*    h3. Workaround   * Remove space and new line manually to make field all on one line (see attached *sample-success.ics*)"""
comments: "Hi, how do we implement the workaround? as I am seeing this issue with a couple of calendars (.ics) files." [SEP] "For us (original reporter) we had to reduce the length of the relevant schedule names in the .ics calendar's source (O365, Google Calendar, PagerDuty, <USER>.          It is not immediately obvious which line in an ics is causing issues- as best memory serves, we had to find the specific error to figure out which line in the ICS was failing by reviewing the application error logs $confluence-home/logs/atlassian-confluence.log" [SEP] "thanks for the update <USER> It appears I can open the calendar in Excel. I will check there, as well as check with our admin to check the logs." [SEP] "[~<USER> you will be able to open any .ics file in a text editor (sublime, notepad, <USER>) and you will see a line like such where a new line is created:    Edit that line to look like this:    If you have further trouble, reach out to our support team at [https://support.<USER>]!" [SEP] "thanks Cole, yes; That'

summary: "Whitespace added after a table within a bullet list or numbered list is removed when the page is published or previewed"
description:
"""h3. Issue Summary Whitespace added after a table within a bullet list or numbered list is removed when the page is published or previewed h3. Environment Observed in Confluence 6.6.16 and reproduced with Confluence 6.15.8 h3. Steps to Reproduce # Create a numbered or bulleted list # After a few points, insert a table # Add another point after the table # Attempt to add white space after the table by pressing enter (or shift-enter, a common Atlassian community suggestion) or enter and a few spaces. The editor shows white space added between the table and the next item in the list. # 'Preview' or 'Publish the page. The white space is not displayed. h3. Expected Results What is displayed in the editor should remain in the preview/published version. 1. Item 1 2. Item 2 with table ||Heading 1||Heading 2|| |Col A1|Col A2| \\ \\ 3. Item 3 h3. Actual Results The list shows no white space after the table. 1. Item 1 2. Item 2 with table ||Heading 1||Heading 2|| |Col A1|Col A2| 3. Item 3 h3. Notes * Similar to [CONFSERVER-58404 : White space is removed between code block macros|https://jira.browse/CONFSERVER-58404] * Setting frontend.editor.v4.disable in darkfeatures.action doesn't help h3. Workaround Four workarounds have been tested, although they are admittedly clunky. +Workaround #1+: 1. After adding/editing the table, press ENTER. This creates a new bullet point below. 2. Press backspace (this deletes the bullet point) 3. Press the space bar twice. 4. Click 'publish' or 'preview'. The single line of white space is preserved. +Workaround #2+: 1. Insert a 'section' macro underneath the table. This appears in the editor but shows as white space in preview (or published version). +Workaround #3+: 1. Use the source editor to add html after the table: & nbsp;
2. Publish the page +Workaround #4+: 1. Click """"+"""" (insert more content) and select 'Markup' 2. Insert markup """"\\ \\""""and press 'preview' or 'publish'"""

summary: "UPM doesn't count user installed add-ons properly"
description: """h3.Steps      # Install Confluence 7-beta1  # Navigate to Manage Apps  # There are no user installed add-ons       *What happened...*   The following warning is logged,       *What should have happened...*    The log mentions *205 user-installed add-ons*, although there is none.      *Notes*    The issue looks similar to https://ecosystem.atlassian.net/browse/UPM-5640 - though, it has been marked as *Fixed*"""

summary: "Add database validation query to Confluence /status URL"
description: """h5. Problem Definition     When running a health check url for Jira it returns a JSON that provides a database validation query.     *Example of health check URL: *[https://app-URL/jira/rest/api/2/serverInfo?doHealthCheck=true|https://app-url/jira/rest/api/2/serverInfo?doHealthCheck=true]    *Example of JSON:*             When running the equivalent for Confluence: *http://<base_url>/status*    It returns:        h5. Suggested Solution   This does not provide a db validation query. Requesting that this be inserted as a feature when running http://<base_url>/status."""
comments: "One reason that this would be a better check is that some orgs have regular patching and reboots of hosts, and due to this, the Confluence database could be running at the time that the Confluence App started, but have been rebooted and not come up cleanly after that point. In such a case the healthcheck would show as ""RUNNING"" while the app would be in a broken state.    A db validation query would solve that, and someone must agree that it's a useful addition, because it was implemented in Jira already."

summary: "Update Documentation to include what Jira user permissions are needed for users to be able to create Software Project space in Confluence. "
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Currently the following document mentions that users in Confluence can create spaces using existing templates.  * [Create a Space From a Template|https://confluence.<USER>doc/create-a-space-from-a-template-829076185.html]    One of these templates is Software Project space template. The documentation above does not specify any limitation on who can create this space in Confluence. However, it seems that only users with *Create Issue* permission in JIRA are the only users that can create such spaces. This is because, when a user clicks on the blueprint, they are required to select the JIRA project from a drop down, this drop down is empty if the user do not have a Create Issue permission in JIRA.         h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Setup both Confluence and JIRA with Application Links.   # In JIRA, ensure that a user belongs to the Default Permission Scheme  ** This scheme would have Create Issue permission set   # In Confluence, Login as the above user and, head over to *Spaces* > *Create Space* > *Software Project* space  # This will prompt the user to select the Project in the component field. The user will be able to view all the projects that has the Default Permission Scheme set to it.   # Now, in JIRA, head over to the Default Permission Scheme and remove the Create Issue permission.   # If we go to Confluence, now, the projects that belong to the Default Permission Scheme no longer displays when creating a *Software Project* space      h3. What's required  The above document should contain information on what are the permissions that the user needs for them to be able to create the Software Project Space      """

summary: "Exporting pages to PDF with a backslash in the page name causes a HTTP Status 400 error"
description: """h3. Issue Summary   * Backslash ( \ ) characters in page names cause the PDF page exports to fail with 'HTTP Status 400 - Bad Request', due to the PDF file naming conventions   * Problem only affects Data Center installations   * The PDF is actually created in <confluence_home>/temp/ but is not presented to the user ('HTTP Status 400' occurs)    h3. Environment    Confluence 6.12.x Data Center and onwards    Server versions are unaffected.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Install & login to Confluence Data Center (Any version from 6.12 onwards)   # Create a page with a backslash character in the page name   # Click the ellipsis (...) and choose 'Export to PDF'    h3. Expected Results    The page should be converted to PDF and the PDF download presented to the user.  h3. Actual Results    The PDF is generated in <confluence_home>/temp/ but the browser will display HTTP Status 400.         {code:java}  HTTP Status 400 – Bad Request  Type          Status Report  Message       Invalid URI  Description   The server cannot or will not process the request due to something that is perceived to be a client error (e.g., malformed request syntax, invalid request message framing, or deceptive request routing).Apache Tomcat/9.0.22  {code}     h3. Notes   * In Confluence Server, the filename format is    However, if special characters exist in the page name (such as %, \, /, $, &, %, :, ?) the PDF filename format changes to:       * In Confluence Data Center, this also appears to be true, but the expected naming for special page name characters changes from Confluence Data Center 6.12 onwards due to a change in Confluence 6.12 DC where PDF exports now get handled by the external process pool (sandbox).  [6.12 Release Notes|https://confluence.<USER>doc/confluence-6-12-release-notes-957143056.html] have further details.    h3. Workaround    There are currently 2 ways to workaround this problem:    +Workaround #1+:   # Change the backslash () in the page name to another character (eg. / )    +_or_+    +Workaround #2:+   # Disable the PDF export sandbox with the following CATALINA_OPTS in setenv.sh on each node in the cluster:     # Perform a rolling restart of each node for the change to take effect.      (!) Note: This has the potential to cause extra heap usage within the JVM, as it brings PDF export operations back into the main heap space rather than running it externally.      """
