summary: "Adjust Confluence docker official image - server.xml shouldn't be overwritten"
description: """When using official docker image for Confluence edited server.xml file is reverted back to default after  container restart.  """
comments: "This is intentional - the built in server.xml should be configured by using the documented environment variables.    If you need to overwrite server.xml with your own custom version, you must overwrite the _template_ file, located at {{/opt/atlassian/<USER>server.xml.j2}} inside the container. More details on the various ways to do this are documented under the ""Advanced Configuration"" section of [https://hub.docker.com/r/atlassian/confluence-server]    For example, you might build your own Docker image based on ours and embed your custom server.xml:      Alternatively, you could mount your custom server.xml over the top of our built-in template at run-time:      (Note this {{docker run}} example doesn't specify any other params you would normally need, it's just an example of the {{-v}} / {{--volume}} parameter)    Overwriting the server.xml.j2 template is the official, supported way to supply a custom server.xml instead of configuring Tomcat via the documented environment variables. I hope that helps!"

summary: "As a Confluence administrator I would like to configure Space level redirect rules to forward users to a different instance"
description: """h3. Problem Definition  When migrating Confluence Spaces from one instance to another, users may still access pages in the legacy instance without knowing the content was migrated and a more recent version is available.  Users may be directed to the legacy instance because of bookmarks or links available in external systems (not Confluence).  It would be interesting if we could adjust a rewrite rule directly in Confluence configuration to send users to the new instance when they are trying to read content from migrated Spaces.    h3. Suggested Solution  Provide the ability to configure directly in Confluence rewrite rules based on Spaces (Space key, for example) to send users to a new Confluence instance.     h3. Workaround   Review the options highlighted in [How to redirect Confluence users to a new URL|https://confluence.<USER>confkb/how-to-redirect-confluence-users-to-a-new-url-976758203.html]"""

summary: "Enable tif/psd thumbnails by default when memory issues are resolved"
description: """In out 7.0 release, we upgraded the thumbnail/preview generation library and had to accept higher memory usage as a result for some TIF and PSD thumbnail/preview generation.    We chose to disable thumbnail/preview generation for these two file formats for now, but customers can enable them by using the relevant system properties if they wish.    When the underlying memory usage issues are fixed, we should enable them by default again."""

summary: "Space directory is empty post upgrade to Confluence 7.0.1 Beta 2"
description: """h3.Steps      # Install Confluence 5.10.0   # Confirm there are spaces in the Space Directory page   # Upgrade to Confluence 7.0.1 Beta 2     *What happened...*     After upgrade and logging into Confluence, the Space Directory is empty     *What should have happened...*    The Space Directory should show the Spaces that was present before the Upgrade     *Workaround*    Perform a manual re-index post upgrade.    Although I do recommend customers to re-index post upgrade to be 100% sure indexes are up to date, this was never a mandatory Upgrade step that it sounds like we must now perform.    *Additional Info*    |5.10 -> 6.15.9|shows Space Directory immediately post-upgrade without needing to re-index (/)|  |5.10 -> 6.12|shows Space Directory immediately post-upgrade without needing to re-index (/)|  | | |  |5.10.0 -> 7.0.1 RC1|does not show Space Directory immediately post-upgrade (x)|  |6.0.1 -> 7.0.1 RC1|does not show Space Directory immediately post-upgrade (x)|  |6.2.3 -> 7.0.1 RC1|does not show Space Directory immediately post-upgrade (x)|  |6.3.4 -> 7.0.1 RC1|does not show Space Directory immediately post-upgrade (x)|  |6.4.3 -> 7.0.1 RC1|shows Space Directory immediately post-upgrade without needing to re-index (/)|  |6.5.2 -> 7.0.1 RC1|shows Space Directory immediately post-upgrade without needing to re-index (/)|  |6.6.13 -> 7.0.1 RC1|shows Space Directory immediately post-upgrade without needing to re-index (/)|  |6.7.2 -> 7.0.1 RC1|shows Space Directory immediately post-upgrade without needing to re-index (/)|  |6.9.3 -> 7.0.1 RC1|shows Space Directory immediately post-upgrade without needing to re-index (/)|  |6.12 -> 7.0.1 Beta 2|shows Space Directory immediately post-upgrade without needing to re-index (/)|    *Reporter*: <USER> *E-mail*: [<USER><EMAIL>]"""

summary: "Error using MultiQueueTaskManager taskManager API"
description: """We are testing our Balsamiq Wireframes app on the latest Confluence Server EAP.    Our app implements a notification/mention features that send email using this Confluence API.    [https://developer.<USER>server/confluence/sending-emails-in-a-plugin/]    The feature is working properly on Confluence 6.X.    Using the 7.01-beta EAP the API doesn't work anymore.    Using the beta2, we have a runtime error when invoking the following command:  taskManager.addTask(MAIL, mailQueueItem);  url: /bas/notify | traceId: 0415ca86a165c103 | userName: anonymous  2019-08-29 14:34:17,580 ERROR [https-jsse-nio-8444-exec-5] [confluence.plugins.mockups.BasServlet] service error : java.lang.IllegalArgumentException: argument type mismatch  Using the beta1 we had this error when we tried to compile the app bundle.     !https://files.slack.com/files-pri/T03FHM6DN-FMF8JJXAR/image.png|width=807,height=495!"""
comments: "[~<USER> thanks for reaching out !    The error you are experiencing is due to our platform components upgrade    It is tied to this particular dependency    The main difference is that {{com.atlassian.mail.queue.MailQueueItem}} no longer extends {{com.atlassian.core.task.Task}} which leads to compile errors around our task managers used in email notifications.    You essentially need to wrap the {{MailQueueItem}} in a lambda before passing it down to the task manager    We are now reviewing and updating our developer docs    Thanks again for your feedback !! much appreciated     "

summary: "Add email address as parameter for REST API"
description: """h3. Problem Definition  Currently only username is a parameter for group updates and user information search.    h3. Suggested Solution  For getting user information or group updates, add email address as parameter."""
comments: "In our particular use case, there are times in automation where we want to get the username of the user given the user's email address using the res, <USER>rpc or other APIs. and then use that username with other APIs.    There seems to be no way to do this in Confluence server as the existing APIs either public and private only seem to take username.    By contrast Jira has APIs that can use either email address or username to look up a user.               "

summary: "Cannot cut/copy and paste table rows/columns between tabs/editor sessions"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    """"Paste table row"""" button shows as inactive when copying rows from a page opened in another browser tab  h3. Environment   * Confluence 6.15.8    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Open one page with a table in one browser tab   # Open another page with a different table in the second tab   # Cut the table row in the first tab   # Paste the table row into the table in the second tab.   # The """"Paste table row"""" button on the second tab shows as inactive (light grey). but clicking it still pastes the cells.       h3. Expected Results  The """"Paste table row"""" button should show as active (dark grey) after copying rows from a page opened in another browser's tab    h3. Actual Results  The """"Paste table row"""" button shows as inactive (light grey) after copying rows from a page opened in another browser's tab    h3. Workaround  Clicking on """"Paste table row"""" button still paste the copied rows even when it shows as inactive (light grey),  """
comments: "While it can definitely be confusing, this is in fact intentional behaviour. So I am converting this from a bug to a suggestion. The cut/copy table column and row feature does not actually use the system clipboard. You can see this by copying something into the clipboard and then cutting a table row/column. When you use the system paste command (e.g. Ctrl+V), the table row/column is not pasted, instead the original contents of the clipboard are. Since browser tabs do not share memory and we're not using the system clipboard, this behaviour is to be expected with our current implementation.    Regards,  <USER>  Engineering Manager, Confluence Server  Atlassian"

summary: "Not possible to change the CSS styling of the page title that's exported into a PDF"
description: """h3. Problem Definition   * Currently, the only way that you can change the styling of the PDF page title is by altering the .wikicontent h1 style element in a custom PDF stylesheet. While helpful, this isn't a good solution if you want the page title and h1 elements to have different stylings.    h3. Suggested Solution   * Allow the ability to style the PDF page title by itself. The following classes were tried, but none impacted the styling of the page title:     - assistive   - with-breadcrumbs   - aui-page-panel   - content-navigation view   - section-primary first   - normal ajs-menu-item    h3. Workaround    If you modify the .wiki-content class in the style sheet, that will render change to the page title.  However, the changes don't always match expectations and the changes will apply to the content nested within H1 heading tags."""
comments: "Same issue here. I tried to customize the h1 ""title"" header using #title-text, #title-heading and .pagetitle (class and id found exploring the inline version or HTML exported version of the page) without success." [SEP] "Doing some tests with an empty CSS style-sheet, the result shows a difference between the page title and Heading 1. Can you just document how to differentiate? " [SEP] "Hi,    Could you share the {{confluencedefaultpdf.css}} or whatever is used as default CSS configuration for PDF *page* export? Based on that we shall be able to find what class / id shall be used." [SEP] "Here is a workaround:    ...  /* Title */  *h1:first-child* {      text-align: left;      font-size: 34pt;      font-variant: small-caps;      font-weight: bold;      padding: 200px 0px 100px 0px;  }  ..." [SEP] "{{And here is another one, maybe ""cleaner"":}}   ...    /* Title */    {{h1.title-header {}}   {{    text-align: left;}}   {{    font-size: 34pt;}}   {{    font-variant: small-caps;}}   {{

summary: "Creating a blogpost with a duplicate title fails even if the 'Posting Date' for the identical-titled blogs are different"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Confluence allows you to create a blogpost with duplicate titles if the *Posting Date* of the blogs are different.    However, if you create a new blogpost with today's date as the *Posting Date* and using an existing blogpost's title, it will fail due to this error.    !CONFSERVER-58789 Error.png|thumbnail!    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create one blogpost titled '*ABC*' and set the *Posting Date* to a date in the past (e.g. Aug 8, 2019).   # Create a second blogpost titled '*ABC*' as well, and don't alter the *Posting Date*. Let it remain as today's date (e.g. Aug 29, 2019).   # Publish the blog.    h3. Expected Results    The blogpost should be created.  h3. Actual Results    The blogpost fails to be created with this error appearing in the editor:  {quote}A page with the title 'ABC' already exists in this space. Enter a different title for your page.  {quote}  h3. Workaround    After you receive the error message, click on the *Posting Date* at the bottom of the page and *don't make any changes*. Just close the pop-up by clicking into the editor's body and publish the blog. It will be successfully created."""

summary: "Collaborative Editing cannot be enabled on Confluence 6.15.8 installed on Microsoft Windows"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Collaborative Editing cannot be enabled on Confluence 6.13.7/6.15.8 when Confluence Server is running on Microsoft Windows    h3. Environment    Reproducible on:    * Microsoft Windows 7  * Microsoft Windows Server 2019    h3. Steps to Reproduce    # Install Confluence 6.15.8 on Microsoft Windows (install as Service or run as a Batch)  # Check Confluence Administration » General Configuration » Collaborative Editing  #* It's disabled out of the box (x)  #* Try to enable Collaborative Editing and it will fail (x)    h3. Expected Results    Collaborative Editing should work    h3. Actual Results    Collaborative Editing fails to enable    The below exception is thrown in the atlassian-confluence.log file:              h3. Root Cause    An update to the bundled AdoptOpenJDK8u222-b10 in Confluence 6.15.8 has resulted in a library dependency issue resulting in the Synchrony process failing to start.    h2. *Resolution*    An urgent release has been made to address this issue.    * Please upgrade to Confluence *6.13.8* if you are on the 6.13 Enterprise Release.  * Please upgrade to Confluence *6.15.9* if you are on the 6.15 release.    h2. *Workaround*    Downgrade the bundled Java AdoptOpenJDK from 1.8.0_222-10 to previously bundled JVM (i.e. *1.8.0_202-b08*) resolves the issue.    (on) Substitute {{C:\Program Files\Atlassian\Confluence}} below if you have Confluence installed in a non-default location.    # Download AdoptOpenJDK 8u202-b08 from https://github.com/AdoptOpenJDK/openjdk8-binaries/releases/download/jdk8u202-b08/OpenJDK8U-jre_x64_windows_hotspot_8u202b08.zip  # Shutdown Confluence  # Rename *{{C:\Program Files\Atlassian\Confluence\jre}}* to *{{C:\Program Files\Atlassian\Confluence\jre8u222-b10}}*  # Extract *OpenJDK8U-jre_x64_windows_hotspot_8u202b08.zip* to {{C:\Program Files\Atlassian\Confluence}} - it will extract with a sub-directory called {{﻿jdk8u202-b08-jre}}  #* Rename the new {{C:\Program Files\Atlassian\Confluence}}\﻿jdk8u202-b08-jre to *{{C:\Program Files\Atlassian\Confluence\jre}}*  # Start Confluence and Collaborative Editing should now start"""
comments: "Within the [OSHI|https://github.com/oshi/oshi] library, the constructor of [WindowsOSVersionInfoEx instantiates WmiQueryHandler|https://github.com/oshi/oshi/blob/master/oshi-core/src/main/java/oshi/software/os/windows/WindowsOSVersionInfoEx.java#L70] which [imports com.sun.jna.platform.win32.WinNT|https://github.com/oshi/oshi/blob/master/oshi-core/src/main/java/oshi/util/platform/windows/WmiQueryHandler.java#L36] resulting in a NoClassDefFoundError.    Unknown as to why the WinNT class is undefined, interesting that a downgrade of the JVM resolves the issue." [SEP] "A fix for this issue is available to Server and Data Center customers in Confluence 6.13.8  [Upgrade|https://www.<USER>software/confluence/download?utm_source=confluence_bugfix&utm_campaign=confluence_bugfix&utm_medium=email] now or check out the [Release Notes|https://confluence.<USER>doc/confluence-6-13-release-notes-959288785.html?utm_source=confluence_bugfix&utm_campaign=confluence_bugfix&utm_medium=email] to see what other issues are resolv

summary: "Confluence documentation for Installation/Initial Setup not clear about Reverse Proxy initial usage"
description: """h3. +Problem+    In [Installing Confluence on Linux|https://confluence.<USER>doc/installing-confluence-on-<USER>143556824.html] and [Installing Confluence on Windows|https://confluence.<USER>doc/installing-confluence-on-windows-255362047.html] KB pages, under *Step 9. Start using Confluence*, is the only place where we can see the mention about the usage of *reverse proxy* during the initial installation for Confluence.  {quote}That's it! Your Confluence site is accessible from a URL like this: http://<computer_name_or_IP_address>:<port>If you plan to run Confluence behind a reverse proxy, check out [Proxy and SSL considerations|https://confluence.<USER>doc/administering-collaborative-editing-858772086.html#AdministeringCollaborativeEditing-proxy] before you go any further.     Here's a few things that will help you get your team up and running:   * [Set the server base URL|https://confluence.<USER>doc/configuring-the-server-base-url-148592.html] – this is the URL people will use to access Confluence.    * [Set up a mail server|https://confluence.<USER>doc/configuring-a-server-for-outgoing-mail-151078.html] – this allows Confluence to send people notification about content.     * [Add and invite users|https://confluence.<USER>doc/add-and-invite-users-138313.html] – get your team on board!   * [Start and stop Confluence|https://confluence.<USER>doc/start-and-stop-confluence-838416264.html] – find out how to start and stop Confluence.{quote}  h3. +Suggested Fix+    Add a recommendation at the beginning of the first steps of each page(both [Linux|https://confluence.<USER>doc/installing-confluence-on-<USER>143556824.html] and [Windows|https://confluence.<USER>doc/installing-confluence-on-windows-255362047.html] KBs) about the best practice of not using reverse proxy at the beginning of Confluence installation, due to possible timeouts it can happen at mainly the Database Initial Setup page. If the customer requires to have set the reverse proxy since the beginning of Confluence Setup, recommend that he should change the {{timeout}} settings for the {{ProxyPass}} for a value bigger than 2 minutes.  h3. Workaround    N/A"""
comments: "[https://getsupport.<USER>browse/PS-45985]"

As we already have confluence user and files are written by it, please change the entrypoint.py to use something like this:  """

Certain files (such as server.xml) are locked to the root user, both in our Docker image and when using our Linux installer, because certain product features or third party addons might otherwise provide a mechanism for an end user to overwrite server.xml through the application, and use it as a vector to achieve remote code execution.     Our images have been updated to simply log a warning when started as a non-root user, and to skip the bootstrapping of critical files which cannot be overwritten. " [SEP] "Given the above notes, I'm going to close this one out as working as intended."

Steps to Reproduce   # Create user1 and user2   # Log in as user1    # Create calendar with name """"user1calendar""""   # Create an event for sub-calendar """"Event""""   # Login as user2   # Add """"user1calendar""""   # Create an event for sub-calendar """"Travel""""   # Login as user1 again.   # Set calendar restriction of """"user1calendar"""" to restrict viewing privilege only to user2    h3. Expected Results  user1 is able to see events on all sub-calendars i.e. """"Event"""" and """"Travel"""" because they are creator of calendar.   Otherwise, user1 shouldn't be able to see any sub-calendars because the calendar is restricted to user2.    h3. Actual Results  user1 can see """"Event"""" but cannot see """"Travel"""".  And user1 keeps seeing Warning on their dashboard and top of calendar.  {quote}  Warning(s) occurred.  Unable to load events of user1calendar. You are not permitted to view its events. For access you may want to contact your administrator.  {quote}   !Screen Shot 2019-08-27 at 11.59.15.png|thumbnail!    !Screen Shot 2019-08-27 at 12.26.50.png|thumbnail!     h3. Note  * Due to CONFSERVER-51216, it's not easy to know who created the calendar. Just respecting calendar restriction even to creator as described in the second option of """"Expected Results"""" would be straightforward.    * In the example above the user1 sees """"Unable to load *user1calendar* """". summary: "support for storing URLs longer than 255 characters"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Currently the """"EXTRNLNKS"""" and """"TRACKBACKLINKS"""" tables use a VARCHAR 255 data type for URL and and LOWERURL columns.     It is requested that Confluence would support longer URLs in these tables and any others that store URLs.   h3. Impact    This can break imports if they contain URLs greater than 255 characters.   h3. RFC Standard     [RFC 2616|http://www.faqs.org/rfcs/rfc2616.html] (Hypertext Transfer Protocol HTTP/1.1) section 3.2.1 says  {quote}The HTTP protocol does not place any a priori limit on the length of a URI. Servers MUST be able to handle the URI of any resource they serve, and SHOULD be able to handle URIs of unbounded length if they provide GET-based forms that could generate such URIs. A server SHOULD return 414 (Request-URI Too Long) status if a URI is longer than the server can handle (see section 10.4.15).  {quote}  That RFC has been obsoleted by [RFC7230|http://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7230#section-3.1.1] which is a refresh of the HTTP/1.1 specification. It contains similar language, but also goes on to suggest this:  {quote}Various ad hoc limitations on request-line length are found in practice. It is RECOMMENDED that all HTTP senders and recipients support, at a minimum, request-line lengths of 8000 octets.  {quote}  h3. Search Engine Support for Long URLs    The [sitemaps protocol|http://www.sitemaps.org/protocol.html], which allows a site to inform search engines about available pages, has a limit of 2048 characters in a URL.     There's also some research from 2010 into the [maximum URL length that search engines will crawl and index|https://web.archive.org/web/20111110004006/http://www.seomofo.com/experiments/title-and-h1-of-this-post-but-for-the-sake-of-keyword-prominence-stuffing-im-going-to-mention-it-again-using-various-synonyms-stemmed-variations-and-of-coursea-big-fat-prominent-font-size-heres-the-stumper-that-stumped-me-what-is-the-max-number-of-chars-in-a-url-that-google-is-willing-to-crawl-and-index-for-whatever-reason-i-thought-i-had-read-somewhere-that-googles-limit-on-urls-was-255-characters-but-that-turned-out-to-be-wrong-so-maybe-i-just-made-that-number-up-the-best-answer-i-could-find-was-this-quote-from-googles-webmaster-trends-analyst-<USER>mueller-we-can-certainly-crawl-and-index-urls-over-1000-characters-long-but-that-doesnt-mean-that-its-a-good-practice-the-setup-for-this-experiment-is-going-to-be-pretty-simple-im-going-to-edit-the-permalink-of-this-post-to-be-really-really-long-then-im-going-to-see-if-google-indexes-it-i-might-even-see-if-yahoo-and-bing-index-iteven-though-no-one-really-cares-what-those-assholes-are-doing-url-character-limits-unrelated-to-google-the-question-now-is-how-many-characters-should-i-make-the-url-of-this-post-there-are-a-couple-of-sources-ill-reference-to-help-me-make-this-decision-the-first-is-this-quote-from-the-microsoft-support-pages-microsoft-internet-explorer-has-a-maximum-uniform-resource-locator-url-length-of-2083-characters-internet-explorer-also-has-a-maximum-path-length-of-2048-characters-this-limit-applies-to-both-post-request-and-get-request-urls-the-second-source-ill-cite-is-the-http-11-protocol-which-says-the-http-protocol-does-not-place-any-a-priori-limit-on-the-length-of-a-uri-servers-must-be-able-to-handle-the-uri-of-any-resource-they-serve-and-should-be-able-to-handle-uris-of-unbounded-length-if-they-provide-get-based-forms-that-could-generate-such-uris-a-server-should-return-414-request-uri-too-long-status-if-a-uri-is-longer.html]. They found the limit was 2047 chars, which appears allied to the sitemap protocol spec. However, they also found the Google [SERP|http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Search_engine_results_page] tool wouldn't cope with URLs longer than 1855 chars.  h3. Browser Acceptance    Below are character limits for various browsers:    h3. Recommendation    Practically speaking, supporting the storage of URLs with length < 2048 characters should be sufficient (VARCHAR 2048).  h3. KB Article    Knowledgebase article tracking the associated import error: [Space or Site imports fail when URLs in import file are longer than 255 characters|https://confluence.<USER>confkb/space-or-site-imports-fail-when-urls-in-import-file-are-longer-than-255-characters]"""

summary: "User Loses all Local Group Memberships If LDAP Sync is Unable to find the User, but the User appears again in subsequent syncs"
description: """h3. Summary    When users are filtered out/synchronized out from Confluence and re-synchronized back into Confluence, they will lose their Local Group Membership.   This is a regression from CONFSERVER-28621  h3. Environment  * Confluence 6.15x  * Cannot replicate this issue on 6.14.0. Expired users are shown as """"Disabled""""   * Unable to replicate on Confluence 7.0.1-beta1    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Add a connection to LDAP in {{<USER>>> User Directories}} with the *Read Only, with Local Groups* option   # Sync the directory and make sure that LDAP users are returned   # Add 1 LDAP user to a local group (membership)   # Change the User Object Filter in the directory's configuration in {{<USER>>> User Directories}} to a dummy filter, such as the following:       # Sync the directory again (Notice that the LDAP users are missing)   # Revert the User Object Filter to the previous working filter   # Sync the directory again (notice that the LDAP users are back, but their local group memberships are gone)    h3. Notes    This issue could happen if expired users get filtered out from Confluence due to the """"Filter out expired users"""" settings, and re-synchronized back into Confluence after their expiry has been refreshed  h3. Workaround   # Restore the instance's database backup to a new database (i.e. not production) prior to the point where memberships were lost.   # Follow the instructions in step 1 of [Migrating Local Group Memberships Between Directories|https://confluence.<USER>display/CONFKB/Migrate+Local+Group+Memberships+Between+Directories] to generate a CSV file of users and their memberships.   # Run through the rest of the instructions in that KB article to populate the production instance's group memberships."""
comments: "A fix for this issue is available to Server and Data Center customers in Confluence 7.0.1  [Upgrade|https://www.<USER>software/confluence/download?utm_source=confluence_bugfix&utm_campaign=confluence_bugfix&utm_medium=email] now or check out the [Release Notes|https://confluence.<USER>doc/confluence-7-0-release-notes-973479448.html?utm_source=confluence_bugfix&utm_campaign=confluence_bugfix&utm_medium=email] to see what other issues are resolved.  "

summary: "As a Confluence administrator I would like that every URL associated to an operation performed within a Space had the Spacekey"
description: """h3. Problem Definition  Some of the Confluence operations use the spacekey within the URL as the example below:   * Create page action: {{<Confluence Base URL>/pages/createpage.action?spaceKey=<SpaceKEY>&fromPageId=<pageID>&src=quick-create}}   * Display page: {{<Confluence Base URL>/display/<SpaceKEY>/<PageTitle>}}    The information in the URL can be used to create some analytics information about Confluence usage, such busies Spaces and Pages.    The problem is that on some operations, the URL is not consistent and it doesn't display the {{Spacekey}}.  Below are some examples of these operations:   * Edit page: {{<Confluence Base URL>/pages/editpage.action?pageId=<pageID>}}   * View page information: {{<Confluence Base URL>/pages/viewinfo.action?pageId=<pageID>}}   * View page action: {{<Confluence Base URL>/pages/viewpage.action?pageId=<pageID>}}    Refer to [Confluence URL list|https://confluence.<USER>confkb/confluence-url-list-317949394.html] for other examples of this.    h3. Suggested Solution  Whenever an operation occurs within a Space, add the {{Spacekey}} in the URL, either as part of the path or as a query parameter.     h3. Workaround   _None so far_"""
comments: "Any updates on this?"

summary: "Configuring the Tomcat connector with a protocol different from NIO might cause a false warning about maximum HTTP thread size"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  Configuring Confluence's Tomcat connector with a protocol different from {{Http11NioProtocol}} may cause the startup check to alert about the [{{maxThreads}} attribute not being configured|https://confluence.<USER>confkb/startup-check-http-maxthreads-configuration-939930122.html].    Confluence may provide this warning in two different ways:   # In the UI with an error similar to the below.     !confluence-startup-check-img001.png|thumbnail!    # In {{atlassian-confluence.log}} with entries similar to the below.      h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Install a Confluence vanilla instance.  # Configure the default Tomcat connector to use the NIO2 protocol.    # Restart Confluence.    h3. Expected Results  Confluence starts properly and the startup health check identifies the Tomcat connector is properly configured with the expected {{maxThreads}} attribute.    h3. Actual Results  Confluence starts and will log entries similar to the below in {{atlassian-confluence.log}}:      Also, the following screen may appear in the UI while Confluence is being started.    h3. Notes  After confirming the Tomcat connector is properly configured in {{server.xml}}, consider this warning as cosmetic and disregard it.  This is caused because the startup health check code is looking for the {{HTTP}} string as the value to the protocol attribute.    While using the {{Http11NioProtocol}}, then the value will indeed be {{HTTP}}.  However, when using something different such as the NIO2, the value will not be HTTP and the startup health check will create the warning.   !confluence-startup-check-img002.png|thumbnail!     h3. Workaround  _Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available._"""
comments: "this happens to me in 7.4.0 ER release as well" [SEP] "getting this with {{org.apache.coyote.ajp.AjpNio2Protocol}} too on 7.4.1" [SEP] "[~<USER> we have finished off the work for Http11Nio2Protocol. summary: "Asynchronously render attachments when loading a Confluence page"
description: """h3. Problem Definition  Files can be rendered in a Confluence page using different macros, such as the [{{view-file}}|https://confluence.<USER>doc/view-file-macro-170494360.html] or an internal link as described in [Display Files and Images|https://confluence.<USER>doc/display-files-and-images-245827130.html].    Rendering files within Confluence may be an expensive task specially depending on how many files a page needs to render. Confluence needs to run some tasks on the background before displaying the file in the UI.    Since these tasks are performed on the same thread that is loading the page, tasks associated to attachments may negatively contribute to the total time a page takes to load.    h3. Suggested Solution  If we could perform all the backend processes from these macros asynchronously to the page loading, then it could decrease the time it takes to load the page.  Note that this would not decrease the total time to load the entire page, but would at least load the content and release the browser to the user while more heavy loading is occurring on the backend.  This is already in place for the Jira Issues macro and something similar could be implemented here.     h3. Workaround   As a workaround, the [Future Macro|https://bobswift.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/CACHE/pages/42631201/Future+Macro] could be used, where available, to asynchronously load heavy content.    As an example, loading a sample page with 50 {{view-file}} macros took _997 ms_ on a vanilla instance:        The same page took _412 ms_ to load when all the files are loaded from a single _Future macro_, giving the user a feeling the page is loading faster while another background job loads the files.  """

summary: "Local File Disclosure via Word Export in Confluence Server - CVE-2019-3394"
description: """Confluence Server and Data Center had a local file disclosure vulnerability in the page export function. A remote attacker who has Add Page space permission would be able to read arbitrary files in the *<install-directory>/confluence/WEB-INF/* directory *and it's subdirectories*, which may contain configuration files used for integrating with other services, which could potentially leak credentials or other sensitive information such as LDAP credentials. The LDAP credential will be potentially leaked only if the Confluence server LDAP credential is specified in atlassian-user.xml file, which is deprecated way of configure LDAP integration.    *Affected versions:*   * All versions of Confluence Server from 6.1.0 before 6.6.16 (the fixed version for 6.6.x), from 6.7.0 before 6.13.7 (the fixed version for 6.13.x), and from 6.14.0 before 6.15.8 (the fixed version for 6.15.x) are affected by this vulnerability.    *Fix:*   * Confluence Server and Data Center versions 6.15.8 is available for download from [https://www.<USER>software/confluence/download].   * Confluence Server and Data Center versions 6.6.16 and 6.13.7 are available for download from [https://www.<USER>software/confluence/download-archives] .    For additional details, see the [full advisory|https://confluence.<USER>x/uAsvOg].  h3. Workaround    Please see the [full advisory|https://confluence.<USER>x/uAsvOg] for mitigation information."""

summary: "Loading a page with links to multiple attachments from the same source page makes Confluence to check for permissions on every link instead of just once"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  On a Confluence page, users may choose to add links to attachments that are from other pages within the same or different Space.  Attachments can be added as a _preview_ or a _link_. More on this functionality can be found in [Display Files and Images|https://confluence.<USER>doc/display-files-and-images-245827130.html].      Since these are internal links, Confluence needs to ensure the current user has the necessary permissions to view the target attachment by checking the current permissions and restrictions of the page where the attachment resides.  It is important to note that restrictions are not directly applied to attachments, but to their pages.    If a page contains multiple links to different attachments from the same source page, Confluence will validate permissions to every attachment, instead of doing this task just once.    Since permissions validation could be an expensive task within Confluence, performing the same validation multiple times instead of just once might become a problem.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Install a vanilla instance of Confluence.  # Create a sample Space, let's call it _Target Space_.  # Create a sample _source page_ as part of _Target Space_.  # Create 100 attachment files to the _source page_ -- the following shell script might help on that.    # Create a _target page_ under _Target Space_.  # Edit the _target page_ and add all the attachments from the _source page_ as attachments links.   #* In the storage format, the macro would look similar to the below.    # Enable [page profiling|https://confluence.<USER>doc/troubleshooting-slow-performance-using-page-request-profiling-200987.html].  # As a regular user (not part of {{confluence-administrators}} group), access the view mode of the _target page_.    h3. Expected Results  In order to load the attachments' links, Confluence would validate if the current user has access to the _source page_ just once and load all 100 links based on that initial validation.    h3. Actual Results  Confluence checks for permissions for each and every link, even though they are from the same _source page_.  Checking {{atlassian-confluence.log}} with page profiling enabled shows an entry similar to the below,       In this case, the more links we add to attachments from the _source page_, the more permissions validations we see in the profiling:      If attachments are added as file preview, then the page profiling would slightly change as the example below:      h3. Notes  The same occurs if we add a link to the same attachment multiple times. Confluence disregards it is the same file and performs permissions check on every link.  Since permissions and restrictions are on Space and page level, we shouldn't perform the validation on every attachment, just on source pages.    h3. Workaround  _Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available_"""

summary: "Pages with emoticons created in older versions are not displayed, nor can be edited"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Pages with emoticons created in older versions of Confluence (at least 3 or older) simply display error {{com.atlassian.confluence.content.render.xhtml.XhtmlException: Unmarshaller encountered element with missing shortcut}} and no content, in version 6.15.6 or later.    If Collaborative Editing is *On*, editing the page will display a pop-up:  {panel}  This page is taking longer to load than usual. Give it a few moments, then try refreshing. Still having issues? Contact your Confluence admin.  {panel}     h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Setup an instance with Confluence 6.15.6 and install the [*Confluence Source Editor*|https://marketplace.<USER>apps/1210722/confluence-source-editor?hosting=server&tab=overview] app;   # Create a page and add two (/) emoticons to it;   # Edit the page with the *Source Editor*;   # Replace the second {{<ac:emoticon ac:name=""""tick""""/>}} with:    (!) Insert the instance's *Base URL* in the text above.     # Press Apply;    h3. Expected Results    The page is displayed, showing one (/) emoticon and text (unknown emoticon).  h3. Actual Results    The editor shows the error {{com.atlassian.confluence.content.render.xhtml.XhtmlException: Unmarshaller encountered element with missing shortcut}}    Opening the *Source Editor* again will not display previous storage format.  h3. Notes    This happens when the emoticons are stored in the page in a format similar to:    We could only verify that this format was used up to version 3. In case of a customer having such pages created in a old version, will see the problem after an upgrade to 6.15.6 or later.    Running the same steps in version 6.15.4 does not reproduce the error.  h3. Workaround    The page's storage format can be changed using the *Confluence Source Editor* app. Search all occurrences of {{class=""""emoticon""""}} and replace it with one of the following values (depending on the actual emoticon to be displayed):  |:)|class=""""emoticon emoticon-smile""""|  |:(|class=""""emoticon emoticon-sad""""|  |:P|class=""""emoticon emoticon-cheeky""""|  |:D|class=""""emoticon emoticon-laugh""""|  |;)|class=""""emoticon emoticon-wink""""|  |(y)|class=""""emoticon emoticon-thumbs-up""""|  |(n)|class=""""emoticon emoticon-thumbs-down""""|  |(i)|class=""""emoticon emoticon-information""""|  |(/)|class=""""emoticon emoticon-tick""""|  |(x)|class=""""emoticon emoticon-cross""""|  |(!)|class=""""emoticon emoticon-warning""""|  |(+)|class=""""emoticon emoticon-plus""""|  |(-)|class=""""emoticon emoticon-minus""""|  |(?)|class=""""emoticon emoticon-question""""|  |(on)|class=""""emoticon emoticon-light-on""""|  |(off)|class=""""emoticon emoticon-light-off""""|  |(*)|class=""""emoticon emoticon-yellow-star""""|  |(*r)|class=""""emoticon emoticon-red-star""""|  |(*g)|class=""""emoticon emoticon-green-star""""|  |(*b)|class=""""emoticon emoticon-blue-star""""|    This will allow the page to be displayed and edited.       h3. Alternate Workaround    Disable the Confluence Hipchat Emoticons Plugin"""

summary: "User mention search invocation does not work as expected"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  # Some users are unable to be mentioned in Confluence depending on how quickly or slowly we type out their usernames.  # Sometime the search is triggered just after one typed character and then never issued again    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Unable to reproduce consistently on all environments.    h3. Expected Results  # Users appear in mentions no matter how slowly or fast their usernames are typed.    h3. Actual Results  # Users only show up if we type their username really slowly, one by one, or if we type it out very fast.  # Users are not listed if we type their username in a normal typing speed.    h3. Workaround  # Type the username very fast, or slow.    h3. Notes  # We're likely to hit into the bug when we have a lot of users (>200), with the same prefix to their name (usersam, usersan, usersav..).  # It appears when the user is not found via the first or second CQL search, the autocomplete just stops searching for the user.  ## User types one letter  ## Autocomplete invokes search too early; 100 results returned; some matching usernames are not included  ## User types another letter  ## Autocomplete invokes search; 100 results returned; some matching usernames are still not included  ## User types more letters  ## Autocomplete never triggers the search again; user is unable to complete the name, since it's not included in previous search results    """

summary: "Plans to end support for WebDAV plugin"
description: """{panel:title=Atlassian status as of September 2019|borderStyle=solid|borderColor=#B2D4FF | titleBGColor=#B2D4FF | bgColor=#EAF1FC}  We ended support for the WebDAV plugin in Confluence 7.0, and plan to remove it completley in a future Confluence release.    The WebDAV plugin is used for:    * Edit in Office (via the Office Connector)  * Connecting to Confluence using a WebDAV client.     Import from Word and Export to Word are not affected, as they don't rely on WebDAV.  The Atlassian Companion app replaced the Edit in Office feature in Confluence 6.11.     End of support means Atlassian will not fix bugs related to this plugin in Confluence 7.0 or later versions.    {panel}    Feel free to comment on this issue if this change will have a significant impact on your Confluence site."""
comments: "This is disappointing. WebDAV may not be well known, but it is critical for those that have evangelized its use within their organization.    I would go back to [this blog post |https://www.<USER>blog/archives/webdav_in_confluence] where ""tremendously excited"" was used to announce WebDAV support in Confluence 2.3. The user stories haven't really changed since then, and no other standardized solutions exist.    If Atlassian insists on dropping support for the WebDAV plugin it would be nice if they open source it or create an agreement with one of the partners to provide paid support.    Other References:  [Ask HN: Is WebDav still relevant|https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=17152768]  [Why my client really loves WebDAV and network locations|https://techcommunity.microsoft.com/t5/SharePoint/Why-my-client-really-loves-WebDAV-and-network-locations/m-p/694194]"

summary: "Linking image renders image as HTTP instead of HTTPS"

Expected Results  Attached image renders as expected.    h3. Actual Results  Image cannot be rendered due to mix content (HTTP). The image still renders upon page save and on page load.    h5. Google Chrome  !chrome.png|thumbnail!     h5. Firefox  !firefox.png|thumbnail!     (!) Note that the request url is HTTP instead of HTTPS.    h3. Workaround  If using a Reverse Proxy in front of Confluence, you may right a redirect rule as suggested in [CONFSERVER-58681|https://jira.<USER>browse/CONFSERVER-58681].    Note that applying this workaround will allow content to be presented in the UI, but an HTTP call will still be made.    """

summary: "Navigating through inline comments using the arrows breaks if navigation hits an inline comment on a text warn/info macro"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  Given a Confluence page with a lot of content and has inline comments, navigating between the inline comments will glitch, should the following conditions are met:  # The page contains a warn/info macro with inline comments.   # The above macro is located at the area of page that requires the user to scroll down to view it. (The issue does not happen if the macro is located at the top of the page)  When navigating through all the inline comments using the top and bottom arrows on the left side of the inline comment section, the navigation glitches when you hit the inline comment that is located in the warn/info macro.    In normal cases, when a user navigates through the inline comments the page will auto scroll to the text referred to by a specific inline comment. However, in the case of this bug, Confluence will not scroll to the text referred to by the inline comment in the warn/info macro. Instead Confluence, will auto scroll to the top of the page.       h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Create a page in Confluence with a lot of content. The content needs to be enough, so that you need to scroll down when viewing the page.   # At the bottom of the page, add an warning macro   # Add a text inside that macro and add some inline comments to the text.  # Add inline comments in the various areas of the page.   # Save the page.   # Click on any of the text with inline comment  # Navigate through the comments using the arrows on the top right side of the inline comment.  # Navigate until you reach the info/warn macro's inline comment.    h3. Expected Results  # The browser will auto scroll to where the info/warn macro is.  # The inline comment will be displayed at the same level where the info/warn macro is.    h3. Actual Results  # The browser will move to the top of the page.   # The inline comment will still be displayed at the same level where the info/warn macro is. This means that the user can scroll down until they find the inline comment box and continue navigating from there.       h3. Workaround  No known workaround at the moment.   """
comments: "Content spanning multiple pages are where the inline comment capability is actually most useful for facilitating reviews.    Hence this defect is affecting many teams within our company." [SEP] "For out case it looks like it happens when the comment was added into ""{scroll titel"". The next comment is shown correctly, but the window didn't jump to it so you need to scroll manually which is a pain and not usable in long documents (current LLD have 150 pages). This is independent from used browser, happens with Firefox and Chrome.      <USER> [SEP] "A fix for this issue is available to Server and Data Center customers in Confluence 7.0.3  [Upgrade|https://www.<USER>software/confluence/download] now or check out the [Release Notes|https://confluence.<USER>doc/confluence-7-0-release-notes-973479448.html] to see what other issues are resolved.      "

summary: "Links to Spaces themselves do not throw the error page when a user does not have permission"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  # When creating links in pages, we can create a *link to a Space*, or link to the Space's Home page.  # Both of these should direct the user to the Space's Home page.  # When a user without permission clicks on a link a Space, the page with the links just refreshes to the top.  # When a user without permission clicks on a *link to the Space's Home page*, the Page Not Found error is shown.      h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Create two Spaces, Space 1 and Space 2.  # In one of the page in Space 1, create a link to Space 2.  ** *Link to Space:*   # Log in as a user with permission to Space 1, but not Space 2.  # Click on the link in Space 1, and the page will just refresh to the top.  ** If the user has the permission to Space 2, the Home page of Space 2 is loaded.  # Compare that with what happens when we click on the link to Space's 2 Home page, and we hit into the Page Not Found error.  ** *Link to Space Home:*     h3. Expected Results  # Either remove the Space link option, and just offer the Space's Home link option.  # Or, redirect the user to the Page Not Found error, so that they know they do not have permission.    h3. Actual Results  # Page just refreshes with no error shown.    h3. Workaround   # Use the Space's Home page when linking to other Spaces.  """

summary: "Allow session timeout to be configured from Confluence Administration"
description: """Currently, the only way to change the session timeout on Confuence server is to [edit a file|[https://confluence.<USER>confkb/how-to-adjust-the-session-timeout-for-confluence-126910597.html]].  This does not work well for the containerized version of Confluence, since changes to the file system do not survive a restart.  The only option to change the session timeout for a containerized Confluence installation is to write a custom container that changes that one setting in the file every time the container starts. summary: "Page Tree Macro Generates Invalid HTML "
description: """h3. Issue Summary    When using a Page Tree Macro on a page and inspecting the source code, invalid HTML is used, according to the html-spec [html-spec|https://www.w3.org/TR/html53/document-metadata.html#pragma-directives]  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Insert a Page Tree Macro on a page   # Inspect Source Code - search for data-macro-name=""""pagetree""""    h3. Expected Results    The HTML is valid.  h3. Actual Results    The HTML is invalid and does not meet the specifications outlined here: [html-spec|https://www.w3.org/TR/html53/document-metadata.html#pragma-directives]    A div-tag is not allowed as a child of ul-tag.    There is no functional issue at the moment, but it is possible it can cause issues in the future with new browser versions.  h3. Workaround       Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Mentioned users (@ mention) in a blog post with a link to a page, get notification when that page is renamed"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Users that are mentioned, via @ mention, in a blog post that contains a link to a Confluence Page, will receive notification (email and workbox), when that page is renamed (changing Page title) although the blog post itself was not changed.  h3. Environment    Confluence instance with 3 user accounts and a Space already created.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Using _User A_, create a blog post; add a link to Space home page but with a different text and @ mention _User B_;   # Login as _User B_ and open workbox → there are notifications about  """"1 new mention"""" in the blog post;   # Login as _User C_ and watch the blog post;   # Login as _User A_ and rename the Space home page;   # Login as _User B_ and open workbox → there will be new notifications about """"1 new mention"""" in the blog post;   # Login as _User C_ and open workbox → there are no notifications about changes in the blog post.    h3. Expected Results    There should be no notifications for _User B_, like there are none for _User C_.  h3. Actual Results    _User B_ gets notifications about """"1 new mention"""" when there was no such change in the blog post. When users have email notification enabled, they will also receive an email. These notifications are misleading and incorrect.  h3. Notes    This same wrong behaviour is not observed when doing the same steps on a Page, instead of a blog post. Notification is not sent to @ mentioned users when a linked page is renamed.  h3. Workaround    Currently there is no known workaround for this behaviour. A workaround will be added here when available"""
comments: "Several of our customers are also affected by this. They use Better Content Archiving app, and when the app archives (moves) a page to another space, that is enough to trigger a blog post notification to @ mentioned users on a blog post that has a link to the moved page. So it's not just title change, but any event performed on the page can trigger the notification on a linked blog post." [SEP] "This appears to be a larger issue than only renaming a linked page. Tested on Confluence Server 6.13.0 / 6.13.7    What happens:  A blog post contains a link to a Confluence page and contains @mentioned users. The linked Confluence page is moved, renamed, or modified. This triggers a false blog post notification that sends an email notification out to the @mentioned users.    Due to how much Blogs and linked pages are utilized, this bug has caused confusion and lots of false notifications. " [SEP] "I noticed this is classified as: Severity 3 - Minor  According to the bug fix policy: _The application or specific featu

summary: "Filter the results of the Content Report Table macro by multiple labels"
description: """h3. Problem Definition      * The *Content Report Table macro* doesn't allows users to filter it down by multiple labels. It seems as the macro uses *OR* logic when multiple labels are specified, instead of the *AND* logic. For example, if you filter by *labelA* and *labelB*, it will show in the results pages that have either one of the labels, but not both.    h3. Suggested Solution      * Provide an option to filter by multiple labels in the same page.    h3. Why this is important       * This is important to filter down pages that have multiple labels in it, not only one specific label.    h3. Workaround   N/A"""

summary: "Provide a confirmation dialog when a user initiates the Copy option in a new tab."
description: """Currently, users are able to copy a page via the Copy option in the ... > Copy option in any Confluence page. Normally, when a user clicks on that option, they are presented with an option to choose which Space and what parent that the new copied page will belong to.     However, in the case if the user clicks the *Copy* option by right clicking on the mouse and selecting, open in new tab, a new tab will be opened in the browser with the new copied page already in Edit mode. This automatically create a draft record in the database. While this should be fine for normal cases, this current behavior is believed to be the cause of unintended creation of drafts when users use scanners such as the FireFox Link Analyser Plugin.    When the above browser plugin is used, and user scans links in a particular Confluence page, the plugin will go through all the links in the web page, including the *Copy* option. Because of the way the *Copy* option currently behaviors, the plugin will cause Confluence to create a draft record in the database. This is an issue, for users who uses these scanners on their large instances, as their database becomes large with these records.  """

summary: "Exporting a page with non-English title to PDF results in a broken filename"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    When exporting a page with non-English title to PDF, then resulting HTTP response will contain incorrectly formatted {{Content-Disposition}} header.    h3. Environment  This bug is applicable to both Server and Data Centre instances.  This bug is observable in any browser.    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a page with non-English title   # Open development tool for the browser and select network monitoring    # From the page menu select """"Export to PDF""""    h3. Expected Results  The resulting filename offered for download starts from the page title and contains non-English characters.    h3. Actual Results  The filename of the PDF file will be broken:  * Microsoft Edge, IE, and Firefox are exporting the file with underscores  * Safari is exporting with question marks  * Chrome offers download with the correct filename based on the last URL segment instead of {{Content-Disposition}} header     Despite different behaviour between browsers, the {{Content-Disposition}} header is incorrectly encoded in all of them.     h3. Notes    Exporting to Word produces correct HTTP response with proper {{Content-Disposition}} encoding.    Additional information:  * https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTTP/Headers/Content-Disposition  * https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc5987    h3. Workaround    Currently there is no known workaround for this behaviour. A workaround will be added here when available."""

summary: "Concurrently purging all items from the trash may throw an error with HibernateOptimisticLockingFailureException"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  The Space administrator can purge all pages that are in the Space Trash as described in [Empty the trash or permanently delete a page|https://confluence.<USER>doc/delete-or-restore-a-page-139429.html#DeleteorRestoreaPage-purgeEmptythetrashorpermanentlydeleteapage].  Depending on the amount of content to be purged, this operation may take some time. This depends on how many pages, history versions, attachments, images and other items that are associated to deleted content.    If the _purge all_ functionality is triggered again for the same set of content (by the same user or a different one), then a system error with a stack trace is sent to the space administrator.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Install a vanilla instance of Confluence.   #* This was validated in Confluence Server 6.15.4.  # Create two users as part of the {{confluence-administrators}} group -- say it {{admin}} and {{admin1}}.   #* It is expected you run the following steps as a Confluence administrator.  # Create a sample blank Space.   #* Space Name: Purge Content; Space Key: PC  # Under the _Purge Content Home_ page, create a new blank page.   #* Page name: Purge Content - Root Page  # Create 1985 pages as child of _Purge Content - Root Page_.   #* Since we are dealing with a vanilla instance, then we are reproducing the issue with an exaggerated number of pages; in a production environment this could happen with hundreds of pages considering history versions, attachments and so on.   #* The following shell script may help you to create the child pages.    # Delete _Purge Content - Root Page_ and all of its child pages.   ## Go to _Purge Content - Root Page_ > *...* > *Delete*   ## Choose _Also delete child pages_ and then click on *Next*.   ## Click on *Delete* on the confirmation page.  # Simultaneously access {{<Confluence Base URL>/pages/viewtrash.action?key=PC}} using both administrators users created a few steps before.   #* You may do this using different browsers.  # As {{admin}}, click on the _Purge All_ link.   #* Don't click on the *OK* button yet -- _wait for it_.  # As {{admin1}}, click on the _Purge All_ link.  # As {{admin1}}, click on the *OK* button to confirm the purge operation.   #* The delete operation starts, but there's no meaningful information about it.  # Quickly switch to the {{admin}} browser window and click on the *OK* button to confirm the purge operation.      h3. Expected Results  On the second time the operation is confirmed (in this case triggered by {{admin}}, Confluence would notify the user the same operation is already occurring, gracefully dealing with concurrent (same) tasks.    To {{admin1}}, it would be expected to have a meaningful feedback in the UI to advise the operation was ongoing (and maybe lock the UI). Maybe something similar when a large set of pages are being deleted as in the image below.   !delete-pages-img001.png|thumbnail!     h3. Actual Results  Confluence sends {{admin}} to a *System Error* page with a stack trace similar to the below.   !purge-system-error-img001.png|thumbnail!       h3. Notes  The purge operation will not fail, since it would be running in the background. However, the second user might feel it failed because the System Error.    Although the operation and the stack trace error is similar, this issue is different from bug [CONFSERVER-57823|https://jira.<USER>browse/CONFSERVER-57823] -- _""""Purge All"""" space trash fails with """"org.hibernate.StaleStateException"""" due to Confluence's handling of CustomContentEntity_.    h3. Workaround  _Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available_"""
comments: "This issue also affects Confluence 6.13.7" [SEP] "This issue also affects Confluence 7.2.1" [SEP] "Affects 7.3.1 Data Center edition." [SEP] "This issue also affects Confluence 6.13 Data Center"

summary: "Add warning about permissions to install to the download prompt for Companion App"
description: """h3. Summary    The download prompt that is displayed the first time selects 'Edit with' in file preview, should have a warning to users that they need permissions to download and install on the local machine.  h3. Current behaviour    The [Download and install the Atlassian Companion app|https://confluence.<USER>conf613/administering-the-atlassian-companion-app-959289208.html#AdministeringtheAtlassianCompanionApp-DownloadandinstalltheAtlassianCompanionapp] mentions the option to distribute the app, in case users aren't able to install applications themselves. But the prompt that is displayed does not give the same warning, so most users will attempt to download and install the app  h3. Desired behaviour    The download prompt could containing a warning message, like:   _Warning, you'll need to have permissions to download and install the application locally. If that is not the case, or you are not sure, please contact your administrator._"""
comments: "This would be extremely helpful for us as we have a whole process users need to go through to install apps on their systems and the  only reasonable place to link for this would be in this pop-up" [SEP] "I wrote a workaround for this problem to use, until we get an option to edit the warning, drop it into the user HTML end of Header and define where the link should point to (make sure this works on your Browser/OS combination! Empty Cache after insertion) :          Edit: forgot to mention: the Interval is needed, because we still have customers using IE11. The Observer does not work correctly, therefore I had to use the 90s style.. :)     " [SEP] "Hi everyone, thanks for your patience. I recently posted an update on Community regarding Companion: [File editing in Confluence Server: Atlassian Companion updates are almost here!|https://community.<USER>t5/Confluence-discussions/File-editing-in-Confluence-Server-Atlassian-Companion-updates/td-p/1285578]. First updates for Companion will be available with 7.

summary: "Quote in comment is not working"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    [https://www.<USER>blog/archives/give-fast-feedback-instant-quotes#QuicklyaddquotestocommentswithInstaQuote-Itworkswithcomments,too]    `quote-in-comment` has been broken. It seems `quote-in-comment` is affected by `inline-comment`. Highlighting a piece of text in comments section only fires `com.atlassian.confluence.plugins.confluence-inline-comments:create-inline-comment` event rather than `com.atlassian.confluence.plugins.confluence-inline-comments:quote-in-comment`.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Make a comment on a page   # Highlight a comment    h3. Expected Results    The comment icon should appear and the user should be able to make a comment to an existing comment  h3. Actual Results    No icons appear.  h3. Workaround    Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""
comments: "I _think_ this is on purpose.  I didn't work on it so I could be wrong.  I think there was briefly a time when selecting some text, and clicking the comment button, would add a new page-level comment that starts with a quote of the selected text.  Inline comment overrides that button.  There may be _some_ circumstances where the quote-in-comment is still meant to work." [SEP] "I think quote-in-comment is meant to happen when you attempt to select some text that can't have an inline comment applied to it (e.g. in a macro). The selected text is quoted for a page-level comment, or at least I remember it was. " [SEP] "Looks like this is a really old blog and inline comments has replaced this functionality."

summary: "Rebuilding Content Indexes From Scratch on Confluence Data Center does not Reindex some of the Apps (Plugins) Data"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Following the [content index rebuild from scratch in Confluence Data Center|https://confluence.<USER>pages/viewpage.action?pageId=833941594], Confluence will then rebuild its index from the database *automatically* on startup and all of contents in your site should be re-indexed within this process.    (i) The auto-reindex process is only applicable in Confluence Data Center instances as this is a part of [Index Recovery|https://confluence.<USER>doc/administer-your-data-center-search-index-879956107.html#AdministeryourDataCentersearchindex-Indexrecovery] action, in which index rebuild from scratch enforces.    However, it's been noticed that some of the apps (plugins) data does not get reindexed successfully after performing content index rebuild from scratch. This then resulted in missing or unsearchable apps (plugins) data in Confluence.    The issue occurs due to the asynchronous nature of the two particular processes within Confluence startup:   - Plugins load, which includes 2 phases.   -- early plugins startup: all plugins that *do not* require DB connections are loaded.   -- late plugins startup: plugins that *require* DB connections are loaded.   - Auto re-indexing process.    As these processes are asynchronous, the auto re-indexing process will then be initiated shortly after the late plugins startup commenced. Since the late startup phase plugins are still loading/ongoing, it is then possible that the auto-reindexing process then could not get all of the content extractors to be re-indexed and will then only re-index some of the apps (plugins) data (which requires DB connections to be loaded).   This resulting in missing or unsearchable apps (plugins) data in Confluence Data Center instance post performing index rebuild from scratch.    Please refer to the sample log snippet below:    h3. Environment    This issue affects Confluence Data Center instances.  h3. Steps to Reproduce    (i) In our test environment, we're able to replicate the issue for Confluence Questions app.  h4. Test 1 - Confluence DC auto-reindex   # Set up a Confluence Data Center instance and install Confluence Questions add-on in this instance.   # Create a question _""""testquestion""""_ with a topic _""""testtopic""""_.   # Stop Confluence.   # Now, please then [rebuild Confluence contents indexes from scratch|https://confluence.<USER>pages/viewpage.action?pageId=833941594].   # After auto-index finishes and starts up, you should find that {{<ConfluenceBaseURL>/questions/topics}} shows no topics.    h4. Test 2 - Manual reindex   # Now, go to <USER>>> Content Indexing page ({{<ConfluenceBaseURL>/admin/search-indexes.action}})   # Click """"Rebuild Index""""    # After manual-index finishes, you should find that {{<ConfluenceBaseURL>/questions/topics}} now shows topics as expected.    h3. Expected Results    Rebuilding content indexes from scratch should rebuild *all of Confluence's contents* on startup.  h3. Actual Results    Rebuilding content indexes from scratch does not rebuild all of Confluence's contents, resulting in some of the apps' data (which required DB connections to be loaded, e.g. Confluence Questions app) to be missing or not searchable.  h3. Workaround    This issue can be workaround by triggering the content indexing manually from the *Content Indexing* page ({{<ConfluenceBaseURL>/admin/search-indexes.action}}) after Confluence have been started up."""

summary: "Incremental syncs of LDAP users from JIRA to Confluence can cause the Confluence Audit Log to be filled with frivolous ""User details updated"" entries"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  In specific conditions, the Confluence Audit Log can be filled with """"User details updated"""" entries, despite no user details actually changing.    JIRA needs to be connected to LDAP via a Delegated Authentication directory with """"Update User attributes on Login"""" enabled. Confluence then pulls the users from JIRA via an incremental sync (with JIRA acting as a user server).    After a user logs in to JIRA, and Confluence subsequently performs an incremental sync of the JIRA user directory, Confluence will create an Audit Log entry for each of those users who have authenticated into JIRA. In a larger environment, can quickly fill up the Audit Log UI such that it is no longer useful for an administrator.    h3. Environment  Tested in:  * Confluence 6.13.4  * JIRA 8.3.0  * LDAP server: Apache Directory Server 1.5    h3. Steps to Reproduce    *Prep work:*  # In JIRA, create a Delegated Authentication directory to LDAP with the """"Update User attributes on Login"""" option checked (which itself requires """"Copy User on Login"""" to be checked as well)  # Also configure the directory auto-add users to a JIRA permission group, such as {{jira-core-users}}, so that they can properly log into JIRA  # In JIRA, go to User Server and add an application, so that Confluence can connect to JIRA for user management  # In Confluence, create a JIRA Server user directory and connect to JIRA. Under """"Advanced"""", make sure Incremental Sync is enabled (this is the default behavior)  # Log into JIRA with an LDAP user  # In Confluence, sync the JIRA directory to pull in the user    *Reproducing the problem:*  # In JIRA: log out with the LDAP user, and log back in  # In Confluence: using an admin user, trigger an incremental sync of the JIRA user directory  # Go to <USER>> Audit Log and observe the results    h3. Expected Results  Nothing is logged in the Confluence Audit Log relating to the user    h3. Actual Results  Every JIRA authentication followed by an incremental sync to Confluence results in a """"User details updated"""" Audit Log entry for said user. When the admin clicks """"Show more"""", the entry is blank as no user details are actually updated.    h3. Workaround  Only way to avoid this is to either disable incremental syncing in Confluence (since this problem does not occur with full syncs), or disable """"Update User attributes on Login"""" in the LDAP directory JIRA-side. However, changing these options will have functional impacts in JIRA and Confluence that an admin should be aware of:  * Disabling incremental sync can cause Confluence to do more a lot work than needed when pulling user management content from JIRA  * Disabling """"Update User attributes on Login"""" means that when users authenticate, JIRA will no longer update the users' records in its database with whatever is upstream in LDAP"""

summary: "As an admin, I want license to be loaded from database when adding new node"
description: """h3. Problem Definition  Confluence license currently is stored in confluence.cfg.xml file. This can cause issues when provisioning a new node after a license upgrade has been done. Requires a new image is taken everytime the license is updated.    h3. Suggested Solution  Load license from database for new nodes.     h3. Workaround  Plan on updating license via the UI or update via [How to change the Confluence License in confluence.cfg.xml|https://confluence.<USER>confkb/how-to-change-the-confluence-license-in-confluence-cfg-xml-720405151.html]"""

summary: "Significant number of attachments added to the page affects preview rendering time for single attachment"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Significant number of attachments on the page affects preview generation time for single attachment  h3. Environment    Confluence 6.15  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Copy sample .png or .jpg attachment 1000 times and change name of each attachment file (for example 1.png, 2.png, 3.png <USER>)   # Create new Confluence page   # Upload first 10 attachments to the page  # Make sure the attachments are on the body of the page   # Save the page, open browser developer tools   # Click on first uploaded attachment   # Save generated HAR file from the browser tools    Repeat above steps after uploading next 50, 100 and 1000 attachments  h3. Expected Results    Time to generate attachment preview is the same regardless of attachments uploaded to the page  h3. Actual Results    Time required to generate attachment preview significantly increases with number of attachments uploaded to the page.    h3. Workaround    Divide page into multiple sections and move each section to the  separate child page    h3. Notes    One of the POST request uses the following endpoint during preview.      With profiling logs turned on, it takes a few milliseconds with 1 attachment on the page. Increasing the number of attachments to 90, increasing the time for this significantly.       Profiling logs show that number of calls to {{PermissionManager.hasPermission()}} keeps on increasing with the increase of attachments. Thread dumps also shows thread is busy getting permissions.    Though, the issue still happens if the page is unrestricted.     If a HAR file is captured, the response of the POST request contains permissions of all attachments on the body of the page."""

summary: "Confluence 7.0 EAP feedback wrt. effects of CDN integration on content exports"
description: """This has been discussed with <USER>from Atlassian here: [https://community.developer.<USER>t/confluence-7-0-eap-released/28060/33]  {quote}The internal Confluence PDF exporter injects a webResourceIntegration bean which has an API to query the state of the CDN system (WebResourceIntegration#getCDNStrategy) and remove that from the rendered DOM.   Avoiding fetching from the CDN on PDF exports is a precaution done for several reasons:    Some site admins might be reluctant to open up communications from the Confluence instance to the CDN (without this, only one way traffic is needed).   The way the internal PDF exporter works with web resources, there may be scenarios where such a request could poison the CDN cache with resources that we don't want to make their way to a user who views that page, for example.   Adding to the whitelist is an option we're considering but it will not solve the above difficulties.    I'd be happy to discuss the technical details of this but it would be better to do it in a ticket.   ...  {quote}  All of our exporters render content in the PDF output mode - regardless of the actual filetype (Word, PDF, ...) like this:    Since the built-in PDF exporter also needs to handle the CDN links specifically I was wondering if it wouldn't make sense to simply disable CDN integration when rendering content in this or related output modes. Another option could be a property in the conversion context or a thread local.    What do you think?    Thanks!"""
comments: "Heya [~<EMAIL>],    The current surefire solution that I can recommend (and the one that we went with internally) is to fetch the CDN URL using the injectable:    and querying the CDN strategy using:    This can return null (for no CDN) or a PrefixCDNStrategy which you can use to retrieve the CDN URL prefix.         The web resource manager (or WRM) is a complex and stateful system which, at the moment, doesn't allow us to easily flick CDN support on and off via a ThreadLocal or similar.    Adding the CDN URL to the whitelist is a sensible thing to do and is definitely on our backlog.         Can you confirm that the above solves your problem, even if not in the most ""elegant"" way?" [SEP] "Hi [~<USER>, sorry for the late response.    Concerning your question: We should probably be able to solve it that way although it will most probably require some rather dirty reflection hacks. Since we still have to support older versions of Confluence (currently 6.0), we can't compile against Confluence 7 and use the
