summary: "Exporting a page to PDF via sandbox produces documents with broken encoding"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Exporting a page to PDF on Data Centre (i.e. via sandbox process) will produce a document with unreadable text because of broken encoding.  h3. Environment    Data Centre  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Ensure that appropriate fonts are [installed|https://confluence.<USER>doc/create-a-pdf-in-another-language-175473.html] before exporting to PDF   # Create a page with non-English text   # From the page menu select """"Export to PDF""""    h3. Expected Results    PDF file is produced and all non-English text is readable.  h3. Actual Results    PDF is produced, but all non-English text is broken. UTF-8 is misinterpreted as Windows-1251 (if on Windows platform) or with other incorrect encoding.    Example of a broken encoding:  {quote}Ð—Ð°Ð3Ð3⁄4Ð»Ð3⁄4Ð2Ð3⁄4Ðo   Ð¢ÐμÐoÑ�Ñ‚ Ð¿Ð3⁄4-Ñ€ÑƒÑ�Ñ�ÐoÐ ̧.   English text.   ç3⁄4Žå›1⁄2é©¬é‡Œå...°å·ž  {quote}  The name of the file contains underscores instead of proper title.  h3. Workaround #1    Use {{conversion.sandbox.java.options}} system property to propagate encoding setting to the sandbox process.    First, we will have to discover Java propertied for the sandbox process:   # Go to """"Confluence administration"""" -> """"Logging and Profiling"""" and enable this package in the logging settings: {{com.atlassian.confluence.impl.util.sandbox}} , use INFO level. Press """"Save"""" button at the bottom of the page.   # Ensure that no sandbox processes are running. If they do, then kill them.   # Export a page to PDF.   # Search for the message in the Confluence logs:   {{[impl.util.sandbox.SandboxProcess] start Sandbox 0: Starting sandbox process: ...}}   # Copy and save all the command line parameters that start with {{-D}} .   You may remove the {{com.atlassian.confluence.impl.util.sandbox}} logging now.    Now we overwrite sandbox parameters with what we just found plus encoding:   # [Specify|https://confluence.<USER>doc/configuring-system-properties-168002854.html] {{conversion.sandbox.java.options}} system property for Confluence:  {code:bash}  -Dconversion.sandbox.java.options=<all Java -D options copied from the log, separated by comma>,-Dsun.jnu.encoding=UTF-8,-Dfile.encoding=UTF-8     # Restart Confluence    (i) *Caveat:* If you change Confluence networking properties (proxy host, proxy port, <USER>, then you will have to change the {{conversion.sandbox.java.options}}, as well. The system will not pass Confluence networking properties to sandbox process if {{conversion.sandbox.java.options}} is specified.  h3. Workaround #2    (!) Warning: this workaround will reduce stability of the node and disable isolation for PDF exports. This is not recommended for production environment.    Disable sandbox process for PDF export altogether:   # Specify Confluence system property: {{-Dpdf.export.sandbox.disable=true}}   # Restart Confluence"""

summary: "Subscribing to Team Calendars in Confluence with events from restricted or inexistent Jira projects might fail"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  Confluence users can subscribe to Team Calendars and get an updated list of events from Confluence in their preferred calendar _app_ as described in [Subscribe to and from Team Calendars|https://confluence.<USER>teamcal/subscribe-to-and-from-team-calendars-273285883.html].  If users subscribe to a Calendar that has events from a restricted or an inexistent Jira project (see [Add Jira Events|https://confluence.<USER>teamcal/add-jira-events-241566244.html] for further information), then Confluence will throw an error instead of gracefully bypassing events associated to that project.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Install a vanilla instance of Jira Software.   #* This was validated with Jira Software 7.9.2 but should not be tied to a specific Jira version.  # As the Jira administrator, create a sample project.   ## Go to *Projects* > *Create project* > *Create sample data*.   ## Select *Project management* and click on *Next*.   ## Use the following information to create the project: _Name_: {{Test Project}} ; _Key_: {{TSTPRJ}}; _Project Lead_: {{jira administrator}}  # Restrict the _Browse Projects_ permissions just to Jira administrators, removing the permission to any logged user.  # Create a regular Jira user, which is added just to the {{jira-software-users}} group.   #* _username_: {{user001}}  # Install a vanilla instance of Confluence.   #* This was validated with Confluence 6.13.3 but should not be tied to a specific Confluence version.  # [Install Team Calendars|https://confluence.<USER>teamcal/install-team-calendars-241566248.html].   #* This was validated with Team Calendars 6.0.37.  # Create a regular Confluence user, which is added just to the {{confluence-users}} group.   #* _username_: {{user001}}  # Create an application link between Confluence and Jira.  # Create a blank space: _Space Name_: {{Test-Team-Calendars}}; _Space Key_: {{TTC}}  # Go to {{<Confluence Base URL>/display/TTC/calendars}} and add a new calendar.   #* _Name_: {{Test Calendar}}  # Within _Test Calendar_, click on *...* > *Add event*.   #* Select _Event Type_: {{Jira Issue Dates}}; _Name_: {{Test Project Calendar}}; _Display_: {{JQL (advanced)}}; _JQL_: {{project = """"Test Project""""}}; _Show_: {{Issue Created Date}}   #* At this point the events are added and the administrator has visibility to the events in the calendar.   # As {{user001}} access {{<Confluence Base URL>/display/TTC/calendars}} and click on _Subscribe_.   #* Choose _Calendar_: {{Test Calendar}}; _Calendar app_: {{iCal}}  # Copy the _Team Calendars URL_ and paste it in the browser.    h3. Expected Results  A pop-up is given by the browser so the user can download an _.ics_ file.  Confluence gracefully handles the fact the Jira project is restricted and doesn't include events from this sub-calendar, but still shows events from other sub-calendars.    h3. Actual Results  Confluence answers with HTTP 500 status and with a {{java.lang.NullPointerException}} to the user.  An error similar to the below is logged in {{atlassian-confluence.log}}:      The user is unable to subscribe to the Calendar.    h3. Notes  The same problem occurs if the target project is unrestricted in Jira, but had its name/key changed after the calendar was created in Confluence, invalidating the JQL query.    h3. Workaround  There's no straightforward workaround to the _subscribe use case_ and still keep the original data in Confluence.  The problematic JQL can be determined by the error in Confluence log.  One of the following options can be used with their possible consequences:   * If the Jira project was renamed, then search for the Calendars pointing to it and update the JQL filter.   * If the Jira project was removed, then search for the event pointing to it and remove it.   * If the Jira project is restricted to certain users, removing it from a _public_ calendar may be an option to certain use cases.    If applicable, exporting the calendar to an _.ics_ file as explained in [this KB|https://confluence.<USER>teamcal/export-team-calendars-content-to-other-calendars-240913738.html] still works.  """

summary: "Importing a space from Confluence Cloud that has upper-cased groups causes a permissions error message"
description: """h3. Summary      * When importing a space from Cloud to Server, if there are any upper-cased groups, they'll show up with the following message in the Server's space permission      h3. Environment   N/A    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # In Confluence Cloud, create an upper-cased group. For example, Confluence-Test  # Add this group in the permissions of a space  ## Go to *Space tools >> Permissions*  ## Click on the *Edit* button  ## Searcj for the group created and save the changes  # Go to the *Content Tools >> Export* tab  ## Click on the option *XML* and on the *Next* button  ## Click on the *Export* button  ## Download the file  # In Confluence Server, go to *General Configuration >> Backup & Restore*  ## Under the *Upload a site or space export file* section, click to choose a file  ## Click on the *Upload and import* button  # Once the upload completes, access the imported space  # Click on *Space tools >> Permissions*    h3. Expected Results      * The groups will have been imported and, in case there was any with an upper-cased name, convert it to lower-case    h3. Actual Results     * Next to the group name, the following message will show up:      h3. Notes   N/A     h3. Workaround     * Create the same Cloud groups in Confluence Server, in lower-case, and replace them under the space permissions"""

summary: "Confluence Excerpt That Populates Jira Description"
description: """We generally have a process of creating Epics in Jira and linking them to a Confluence page that contains the detailed requirements (i.e. prior to create the Jira tickets for the work). It would be nice to have the ability to have an Excerpt type macro in confluence where you can link the excerpt to a Jira ticket and have that information displayed in the ticket. This would prevent the typical cut-and-paste problems and keeping the data in-sync. While, in principle, I think of this as being linked into the Jira Description field, it may make sense to have it as a separate field. I do realize this poses a problem, because if you have more than one Excerpt linked to a single Jira ticket, what would be the best way to display that information. I would envision it as a page tool, where the most recently updated excerpt is show on the ticket with navigation buttons to swipe through the excerpted segments. Overall, the idea is really to have a Jira ticket that has tight integration to a single Confluence page.  """

summary: "Using ""|\["" in Wiki Markup previews the Wiki Markup macro in a corrupted manner"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  # Using *|\[* in Wiki Markup previews the Wiki Markup macro in a corrupted manner instead of rendering the macro correctly.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Create a new page, and add a *Markup* from *Insert More content*.  # Type the following, *|\[*.  # Instead of rendering the *Wiki Markup macro* properly, it just previews the source of the macro.  ** ||<ac:structured-macro ac:name=""""unmigrated-wiki-markup"""" ac:schema-version=""""1"""" ac:macro-id=""""fe0fa06e-af61-4e4a-992f-49590991c434""""><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[||[]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro>||    h3. Expected Results  # The *Wiki Markup macro* is added properly, and the value is previewed inside it.    h3. Actual Results  # The *Wiki Markup macro*'s source is previewed instead.    h3. Workaround  # Insert the *Wiki Markup macro* using |\ followed by anything else.  # Or insert the *Wiki Markup macro* by add something else before, *|\[*."""

summary: "Create from Template macro, set to a user created space template, does not work after Space import"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    After importing a Space that contains a user created template and a page with the *Create from Template* macro set to it, when pressing the macro button the Create window is displayed blank and no page is created.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a new Space;   # Create a *User Created Template* via *Space tools* > *Content Tools* > *Templates* > *Create New Template*;   # Create a new page in the space and insert the *Create from Template* macro, choosing the template created in step 2;   # Test the macro by clicking on the """"Create From Template"""" button - A new page is created from the template;   # Export the space to XML via *Space tools* > *Content Tools* > *Export*;   # Delete the Space via *Space tools* > *Overview* > *Delete Space*;   # Import the Space via *Admin* > *Backup & Restore*;   # Open the Space's page created in step 3 and click on the """"Create From Template"""" button.    h3. Expected Results    A new page is created from the template, like in step 4 above.  h3. Actual Results    The *Create* window is displayed blank. Clicking on *Next* does nothing and the only option is to click on *Close*. See image below.   !CreateFromTemplate - 01 - AfterImport.png|thumbnail!  h3. Workaround    Edit the page with the *Create from Template* macro, select the macro and click on *Edit*, and re-select the user template in the *Edit ‘Create from template’ Macro* window."""
comments: "This bug is realy annoying and disrupts our content distribution to many servers. "

summary: "Add SAML support to Team Calendars for subscribing to calendars"
description: """Currently, in order to subscribe to an iCal style calendar, such as a Team Calendars calendar on a different Confluence instance, you are required to use a username and password when you subscribe to the calendar. It would be beneficial to be able to leverage SAML for this authentication rather than forcing the use of a username and password. There may be instances where the specific SAML implementation doesn't make it possible to use a static username and password for the authentication."""

summary: "Provide a feature to generated fixed preview URL links for all attachment versions"
description: """h5. Current State    Currently when an attachment has multiple versions, all the versions *except* the latest version have *fixed* preview URL links.     These links can be obtained by:  # Clicking on an attachment until it is opened in Preview Mode.  # When an attachment is in this mode, you can copy the link from the browser's URL field.     As mentioned, these links are fixed for each version of an attachment except the latest version. For most of the versions, this allows users to have a constant link to refer to a particular version. This link can be shared to other users which allows those users to directly view the attachment in Preview mode. This is not the case, however, for the latest attachment version. This version will always be referred to by a temporary URL link. Which means, whenever there is a newer version, that version will take over this URL link. When this happens, the now second to latest version, will only now be assigned a fixed URL.    h5. What is required   The feature that is required here is to have a feature that generates fixed URL links for all versions of attachments so that this link can be used, shared to other users. When these users click on these links, they will be automatically shown the attachment in Preview mode.     """

summary: "Add Support for Aurora Serverless Databases on AWS"
description: """{panel:bgColor=#e7f4fa}    *NOTE:* This suggestion is for *Confluence Server* including *Confluence Data Center* and only specifically Aurora Serverless. Are you looking for Aurora non-serverless? [See the corresponding suggestion|https://jira.<USER>browse/CONFSERVER-36248].    {panel}    Amazon has developed a MySQL compatible serverless database server, [Aurora|http://aws.amazon.com/rds/aurora/], that is available for use as part of AWS. As there are many instances of Confluence installed in AWS, Aurora serverless support should be added into Confluence. According to Amazon information, Aurora is designed to be compatible with MySQL and postgresql, so that existing applications and tools can run without requiring modification."""

summary: "Unsynced from Directory tab takes too long to load"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    While accessing Unsynced from Directory under User Management, it takes around 16+ minutes to load the results.  h3. Environment    Confluence 6.13.6   MySQL 5.6.37  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Go to   !https://confluence.<USER>support/files/790796813/947856965/1/1522298459209/cog-new.png|width=16!    ** > *General Configuration* > *Users*   # Click on Unsynced from Directory   # The page would take over 16+ minutes to load the first 10 sets of users on Page 1   # Upon clicking page 2 in order to load another set of 10 users it would again take around 16+ minutes    h3. Expected Results    Upon clicking Unsynced from Directory tab the results should show up sooner.  h3. Actual Results    Upon clicking Unsynced from Directory tab it's taking around 16 minutes to fetch the results  h3. Notes    SQL logging was enabled through Logging & Profiling and clicking Unsynced from Directory tab would result in firing the below SQL Query which will be captured in {{atlassian-confluence.log}}  {code:sql}  select confluence0_.user_key as user_key1_58_, confluence0_.username as username2_58_, confluence0_.lower_username as lower_us3_58_ from user_mapping confluence0_ where confluence0_.user_key<>confluence0_.username and (confluence0_.user_key<>confluence0_.lower_username and not (exists (select 1 from cwd_user internalus1_ where confluence0_.username=internalus1_.user_name)) or confluence0_.lower_username is null) order by confluence0_.username asc  {code}  Upon further research come across an article for [MySQL |https://dev.mysql.com/doc/refman/5.7/en/order-by-optimization.html#order-by-filesort]which basically tells us that the filesort is kind of a last resort for the expensive """"order by"""" query.  h3. Workaround    Hide the Unsynced from Directory tab from the User Management Screen by adding the following script under  !https://confluence.<USER>support/files/790796813/947856965/1/1522298459209/cog-new.png|width=16!    *> General Configuration > Custome HTML and then insert the script within *At the End of the Body* section.  {code:java}  <script>   AJS.toInit(function(){      AJS.$('#unsynced-tab-link').parent().hide();   });  </script>  {code}"""
comments: "Hi,   Faced the very same behaviour yesterday, despite the fact that we're using Confluence 6.15.4 on PostgreSQL 9.6.11. The request was similar to your's:    It was running at least 50minutes (probably more, had to kill it). Plan of the query didn't look quite optimal and used seq scan:    Issue was resolved by creating two additional indices as following:    Plan after:         After that page took less than a second to load.               " [SEP] "For mysql, I'm doing:              That will make the page load very fast. Not sure if there's any side effect when doing the repo syncs.    I hope that next upgrades won't break it." [SEP] "I've seen this issue on 6.15.9 and 7.3.1.  "

summary: "As a user, I want to upload new versions of a file with a different filename and still maintain version history"
description: """h3. Problem Definition  Filename for drafts include a version number that we want to maintain, but want to have Confluence also maintain a version history when uploading.    h3. Suggested Solution  When uploading a file with different filename, allow option to select existing file that it matches with to maintain version history.     h3. Workaround.  # Insert the file into your page by following [Display Files and Images|https://confluence.<USER>conf68/display-files-and-images-947170479.html]  # Preview the file by clicking on the file thumbnail or link on the page.  # When previewing the file, in the top right there is an """"Upload New Version"""" !https://confluence.<USER>conf68/files/947170491/947170495/1/1521520378048/Upload-New-File-Version.png!  # After the new version is uploaded, a pop-up will appear providing the option to add additional comments on what changes were made."""

summary: "Companion App occasionally does not start due to duplicated autostart entries"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Duplicated Companion App autostart entries on the Windows startup programs list may occasionally stop the Companion App from starting properly after system start-up. Companion App still works and can be manually started afterwards.    What causes the duplicated startup entries to appear is still unknown, and disabling the automatic start from the Companion App settings does not remove the startup entry, although the Windows Registry still lists a single entry for the Companion App.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Simply start up the system with the Companion App automatic start configured in the application itself    h3. Expected Results    Companion App would start normally.  h3. Actual Results    Due to the nature of this issue, it will only appear when the startup entry for the Companion App was duplicated somehow, with root causes that are still unknown. If the current machine is affected by this issue, it will yield the following results:   * Starting the system will yield an error related to starting the Companion App   * Companion App will not start automatically, but can be started manually   * Checking the startup programs menu, there are two entries for the Companion App, but disabling the automatic start from the App itself will not remove the second entry    h3. Workaround    Since the second autostart entry was added to Windows by mistake, it's possible to identify it and disable it manually, to prevent the issue from happening:   # Disable the Companion App's """"Launch on Startup"""" option   # Open the system's autostart list, and Disable (right click > """"Disable"""") the remaining autostart entry for the Companion App   # Enable """"Launch on Startup"""" again, which will add the second (working) autostart entry         !companion_deactivated.png!"""
comments: "Closing as this cannot be reproduced or determine the root cause."

description: """For Jira, there is a config.sh script to set up things like https and custom keystores.  This does not exist on Confluence and would be immensely helpful.  Either that, or build into the initial configuration process to configure for https.  Having to manually alter multiple .xml files is leading to confusion and failure."""

summary: "Page Index macro does not sort diaeresis and umlaut alphabetically"
description: """h4. Issue summary  # When we create a page with diaeresis, or umlaut, like ä for example, although they are sorted alphabetically in the Page Tree, they appear at the bottom in the Page Index macro.  # If that's the first character of the Page title, it does not appear in the Page Index macro at all.    h4. Steps to replicate  # Create pages called, Abc, Acc, and Aäc, and äbc in a Space.  # Add the *Page Index* macro in any page in the Space.  # The Aäc page will be under the Abc and Acc page.  # The äbc will not be in the list of pages at all.    h4. Current behavior  # Diaeresis, or umlaut not sorted properly, or not at all,  in the Page Index macro.    h4. Current behavior  # Diaeresis, or umlaut should be listed and should be listed alphabetically in the Index macro, just like the Page Tree.    h4. Workaround  # None """
comments: "This bug was reported in July, but has not been corrected so far. This is not acceptable.     Could you take it into consideration ?    Best regards"

summary: "Team Calendars should take space edit permissions into account"
description: """Currently anyone that can view a space can perform actions on the calendar view of that space including:   * Adding a new calendar   * Importing an existing calendar    It seems problematic that if a user can only view content in a space, that they are able to modify the calendar view. On the other hand, there is no specific """"add/edit calendar"""" permission in a space like there is for pages, blogs, comments and attachments. So it is unclear how this will work. One suggestion might be that only space administrators could add or import calendars but that is changing how team calendars works. Another might be that it should follow the """"add/edit pages"""" permission.    In addition to that, users can currently create calendars and associate those calendars with spaces that they may only have view permissions on. Again, a suggestion might be to only allow this for space administrators as there is no """"add/edit calendar"""" permission in a space. Or perhaps it should check against the """"add/edit pages"""" permission instead."""
comments: "Users created too much calendars in one space, please improve this function ASAP:("

summary: "PDF and Office Documents fails during preview"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Since then file attachment (except image files like png, jpg) upload causes an issue where the attachment is always embedded within the View File Macro which results in an error while saving a page. This is seen after upgrading Confluence from 6.6.8 to 6.13.6    h3. Steps to Reproduce    # Create a new Page  # Initiate attachment upload of a PDF or an Office document from the UI option at the top or Drag n Drop  # We cans see that the attachment is already embedded within the view file macro   !Screen Shot 2019-07-21 at 3.14.46 PM.png|thumbnail!   # Upon saving the page, we see an error as below:    !Screen Shot 2019-07-21 at 3.12.01 PM.png|thumbnail!   h3. Expected Results    Attachment to be uploaded without view file macros interference or render the attachment upon page load without any errors    h3. Actual Results    The below exception is thrown in the atlassian-confluence.log file:    h3. Cause  You are running Data Center and storing attachments in DB, then PDF and MS Office documents conversion will fail (hence the broken previews). It is because all conversions for DC are performed in a sandbox.  h3. Workaround   * Migrate the attachment storage from DB to disk: [Attachment Storage Configuration|https://confluence.<USER>conf613/attachment-storage-configuration-964961342.html]. The DB storage is deprecated for a long time and strongly discouraged.   * In addition, File View macros are [deprecated for 6.13|https://confluence.<USER>doc/end-of-support-announcements-for-confluence-210239673.html#EndofSupportAnnouncementsforConfluence-features201511]. They will be reintroduced in 7.0, but most likely with a different implementation.   * Disable sandbox feature. This will avoid sandbox document conversions while generating previews. However, This solution is not recommended because it will reduce the stability and security of the data center."""
comments: "_""In addition, File View macros are [deprecated for 6.13|https://confluence.<USER>doc/end-of-support-announcements-for-confluence-210239673.html#EndofSupportAnnouncementsforConfluence-features201511]. They will be reintroduced in 7.0, but most likely with a different implementation.""_    Well, are they?  On Jan 2019, Atlassian updated their documentation to say that the decision to deprecate View File macro had been reversed.    [https://confluence.<USER>doc/end-of-support-announcements-for-confluence-210239673.html#EndofSupportAnnouncementsforConfluence-features201511]    https://jira.<USER>browse/CONFSERVER-39829"

summary: "Allow Images in Templates (Or notify of restriction)"
description: """h1. The scenario    I recently was making a template for our quarterly meetings.    I was copy pasting from a page that already existed that we made as we went and intended to turn into a template.    We had a screenshot of our initial portfolio plan for the quarter in it and I wanted to keep that photo in the template to serve as an example when creating the next meeting note page like this one.    So I copy pasted my old page, removed content that wasn't the base, added instructional text in spots, and hit save.  It looked like it saved just fine at first glance, however when I went to go look at it the next day the template was no where to be found. Not in the space templates, not in global templates, not in drafts, it was nowhere.    So I went to recreate it and this time after hitting saved looked for it in the template list to see it was missing. So I hit back so I could not lose my work this time and in a new tab made a simple test template. It saved correctly so I slowly added pieces of my template hitting save along the way till I found that as soon as the picture was added any changes made with adding the picture (presumably any other attachment as well, this is untested) are note saved when you hit save, they just disappear.  h1. How to recreate   * Create a template in a space or global confluence   * Copy paste in a screenshot of something (or other image, I only tested copy paste)   * Hit Save   * Find that the template is missing from template list.    Or edit an existing template, add a picture, hit save, then view the template and note the missing picture.  h1. The suggestion    It would be nice to be able to have images in templates, especially for example purposes or other possibilities like a company logo or something.    However, I could see how maybe templates are simply not meant to have attachments in them, in which case, at least a in confluence warning should pop up informing the user that their template can't be saved with an attachment.    Maybe even give the option to remove attachments and save with one button on templates.    Thank you,    -JJ     """

summary: "Allow administrators to recover deleted spaces"
description: """h4. Problem statement    There are instances where a space is accidentally removed from Confluence without the administrator knowing that this action is irreversible.    The only way to restore the space again would be to use an external site/space XML backup, or a DB dump, which is a cumbersome process just for a single space to be restored to Confluence again.  h4. Suggestion    Similar to deleted pages, allow deleted Confluence spaces to have its own """"trash"""" in the administration console where a Confluence administrator can restore/recover."""

Expected Results    * The translation of the """"Share this page with others (sou k)"""" sentence is shown    h3. Actual Results   * The tooltip is showing in English instead  * Example the issue in French   !Screen Shot 2019-07-19 at 09.34.40.png|thumbnail!     h3. Notes   When having the {{i18ntranslate=on}}, we can see that it has the following keys:  * share.button.tooltip2.page  * share.button.tooltip2    Which have been correctly set in the French JAR file:  {{confluence-language-pack-fr_FR-6.15.0.rc1-20190311231818}}   !screenshot-2.png|thumbnail!      !screenshot-1.png|thumbnail!     h3. Workaround   N/A"""

summary: "Impossible to add JIRA pie chart macro when the primary applink of JIRA is not reachable or the Oauth token expired"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  When the primary applink of JIRA is not responsive, it is impossible to add a JIRA pie chart macro on the page. The macro popup window will disappear instantly with no error message on the UR, but with the following JS error in the console:       h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Create 2 JIRA application links on Confluence.   # Stop the JIRA instance of the primary applink  # Open a page and try to add a pie chart macro    h3. Expected Results  The macro popup window should show with the list of available JIRA server.    h3. Actual Results  The popup window closes immediately without any error message as well as access the JIRA pie chart via the JIRA issue macro.     h3. Notes  Apparently, if the user has not yet created the JIRA Oauth Token on the primary JIRA instance, we will encounter the same issue.    h3. Workaround  * Remove the unresponsive JIRA instance from the primary applink.  * In case the JIRA instance is reachable, ask the end-user to create JIRA issue macro with the primary JIRA instance to create the Oauth Token before adding the pie chart macro."""

summary: "Moving a page to a new space might fail with no warning to the user because of an issue with an incoming link from a blogpost"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Moving a page to a new space fails with no warnings to the user in the UI.   The URL might even show the {{moved=true}} statement, but the page remains in the same old space, under the same parent page.    Checking the {{atlassian-confluence.log}}, the following entries show the move page action failed and was rolled back on the Hibernate level.    Moving the page to a new parent within the same space works fine.  h3. Environment    The issue occurred on an environment with the following characteristics:   * Confluence Server 6.6.13   * Oracle Database   * The page to be moved has an (internal) incoming link from a Blog post    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a _Sample Page_ within a _Source Space_.   # Create a Blog post within another Space.   # In the Blog post, add an outgoing link to the _Sample Page_.   # Move the _Sample Page_ from the _Source Space_ to the _Target Space_.    h3. Expected Results    The page is properly moved to the _Target Space_.  h3. Actual Results    The page is not moved and no error nor warnings are shown to the user.    The below exception is thrown in the {{atlassian-confluence.log}} file:    h3. Notes    So far, the issue could not be reproduced on another environment so we are unsure on what the triggers are.   They will be included to the bug report when identified.    The issue occurs on the refactoring task to update the contents of pages with outgoing links to the moved page.   The important marker in the exception stack trace is the following, indicating the issue is related with an outgoing link to the moved page in a Blog Post:    {panel:title=CREATION DATE}  *There is a problem when the blogpost is created. Creating blogpost (using the REST API or the editor), two entries are created on the database, one with CREATIONDATE and other with the CREATIONDATE empty:*    !Screen Shot 2020-07-13 at 18.34.03.png|width=409,height=137,thumbnail!    *If the BLOGPOST is edited after, all the entries will have the CREATIONDATE set as expected. So, the problem only happens if a never-edited blogpost is linked to a page and the page is moved also this can affected the pages update in some cases.*  {panel}  h3. Workaround    The workaround could be to temporarily remove the outgoing link to the page moved in the Blog Post, move the page and then re-add the link.   You can check the [Page Information|https://confluence.<USER>doc/view-page-information-139408.html] to get the incoming links.   # Run this query on the database:        # Add a valid CREATIONDATE for all the entries without    """
comments: "Another strange thing about the unmovable page. The blog post that references the page also has itself in the list of outgoing links (in the Page Information)."

summary: "Include script to capture thread dumps in the installer bundle"
description: """h2. Recommendation    We should include scripts used to capture thread dumps in the product installer we ship. As an admin, having these scripts available at the ready during a performance incident will provide ease of mind and ease of capture, particularly should another staff member be monitoring the instance at any given time.    When the product is unresponsive, the option to capture thread dumps inside of a support zip may not be available through the web interface.  h2. How to Capture Thread Dumps - Linux   * [Generating A Thread Dump|https://confluence.<USER>doc/generating-a-thread-dump-188095.html]    h4. Manual commands  h6. Option 1 - jstack  {panel}   # First, identify the Process ID for Confluence (referred here as the $CONF_PID) using the command: {{ps aux | grep -i java}}   # Using the $CONF_PID while logged in as the user that runs Confluence, run the following script in the terminal:    (i) *NOTE*: This will produce 12 text files in the directory from where this was run. If the 'conf_threads' files contain no data, then you will need to provide the *fully qualified path* to the *jstack* binary in the script. For example: If your *jstack* binary exists under /usr/java/bin/jstack , then the script would need to be modified as follows:       # Please, make sure to zip and attach all .txt files generated after running the commands (conf_cpu_usage and conf_threads) to the ticket.{panel}  h6. Option 2 - kill -3  {panel}   # First, identify the Process ID for Confluence (referred here as the $CONF_PID) using the command: {{ps aux | grep -i java}}   # Using the $CONF_PID while logged in as the user that runs Confluence, run the following script in the terminal:       # The thread dumps will be placed in your catalina.out file   # Zip the catalina.out and conf_cpu_usage files that are generated and upload to your ticket{panel}  h4. Bash Script    h2. How to Capture Thread Dumps - Windows   # [Download PsExec|https://technet.microsoft.com/en-us/sysinternals/bb897553.aspx] from Sysinternals   # Unzip the *PSTools* directory to a suitable place on your server.   # Open the unzipped *PSTools* folder, hold shift and right-click inside it, then select *Open command window here* (if you don't have the 'Open command window here' option you can run a command prompt as Administrator and navigate to the location of the PSTools folder using the command prompt)   # Identify the PID of the java (tomcat) process using Task Manager > Processes   # At the command prompt type {{psexec -s jstack XXXX > thread_dump_YY.log}} (where XXXX is your PID and YY is the number of the thread dumps taken to append to the name of the log file) then hit enter.   ## If the jstack command is not recognized, you may need to enter the full path such as: {{psexec -s """"C:\Program Files\Java\jdk1.8.0_201\bin\jstack.exe"""" XXXX > thread_dump_YY.log}}   # You'll need repeat the above 5 or 6 times, about 20 or 30 seconds between each execution, changing the YY each time, for example:       # You should now have 6 files named thread_dump_YY.log in the PSTools folder.    (i) *Note:* If you still have issues with jstack, add the full path of your Java 'bin' directory to the server's system path variable. The Java 'bin' directory is usually in {{Program Files\Java\bin\jdk<version>\bin}}. If you add this path to the system variable then you will need to restart the command prompt again for the change to be picked up and the {{jstack}} command to work."""

summary: """Recently Worked on"" page loads slowly"
description: """h4. Issue Summary    Slow page loading times can result when opening the """"_Recently Worked on_"""" page.  h4. Environment   * 6.15.3   * MySQL 5.7    h4. Steps to Reproduce    This problem occurs while opening the """"_Recently Worked on_"""" page.  h4. Expected Results    Page loads within a reasonable amount of time  h4. Actual Results    The page loading is slow taking up to 14 seconds  h4. Symptoms    Aside from the slowness observed in the UI, this problem has the following symptoms:   * If a HAR file is captured while loading the page, the following URL call will take quite a bit of time to complete:       * If SQL debugging is enabled, the following method call will take the longest to complete while processing the above URL:       * If thread dumps are taken while loading the page, most threads will reference calls to _com.atlassian.confluence.core.DefaultContentEntityManager.getVersionsLastEditedByUser_.   * While the actual SQL queries execute quickly, most of the processing is taken up within the Hibernate layer.    h4. Analysis    The following Java method was found to be inefficient as it is supposed to retrieve all pages (including drafts) that a specified user edited in the past, but only the latest versions of these documents:    However, it retrieves *all* versions of these pages and ends up discarding the majority of the returned records which is most likely causing the delay in rendering the results.  h4. Workaround    A possible workaround would be to disable the _recently-viewed-plugin_ altogether to prevent these queries from being executed. If this is acceptable, then it's the only way to reduce the load time until proper fix is implemented.  Please also see the work around listed in CONFSERVER-57457 as this appears to be related."""
comments: "This is still an issue in version 7.4.3."

summary: "Have a single place for the time format setting for Events in the Team Calendars app"
description: """h3. Summary    Confluence should provide a single place where users or administrators can set the time format used for Events in the Team Calendar app.  h3. Current behaviour    As mentioned in the [Setting 24 hour format in Team Calendar|https://confluence.<USER>confkb/setting-24-hour-format-in-team-calendar-960163482.html] KB article, currently users have two places where they can configure the time format in Team Calendar:   # Under *Admin* > *General Configuration* > *Team Calendars* and change _Display time format_ - this affects the Events display time in Team Calendar   # Under *User Settings* and changing the *Language* - this affects the time format when creating a new Event       {panel:bgColor=#ffffe0}  (!) In addition to this, changing the format under *Admin* > *General Configuration* > *Formatting and International Settings* has no effect in Team Calendar. This setting should apply to all date and time representations in Confluence, including Team Calendar and users might consider a bug the fact it has no impact.  {panel}  h3. Desired behaviour    Have only one place where the time format setting is set and that it affects all time representations in Confluence, including in Team Calendar.   The *Formatting and International Settings* is the most logical place for this.  h3. Short-term workaround    A descriptive text could be added under *Admin* > *General Configuration* > *Team Calendars* to indicate that the _Display time format_ only changes how the times are displayed for existing events in the Calendar and not how times are displayed when creating a new Event."""

summary: "Dates in table column prevent cell selection in Firefox"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Inserting a date into the first cell of the first column in a table prevents the selection of other cells in the same column, this issue happens only in *Firefox,* other browsers are not affected like Chrome, IE, Safari.  h3. Environment   * Confluence 6.15.6   * Firefox 68.0 (64-bit) on Windows    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a table   # Add a date using // in the first cell of the first column   # now all the other cells in the first column are not selectable    h3. Expected Results    User should be able to select all cells in the first column no matter what data is inserted in the header.  h3. Actual Results    User can't select any cells in the first column by mouse clicking  h3. Notes   * Viewing the source-code of the page, shows contenteditable parameter for <time> tag which is the date value in the first cell is set to """"false""""   * setting the value of the parameter to """"true"""" makes the cells selectable in Firefox    h3. Workaround    The only workaround at this moment is to navigate to the desired cell using the arrows keys."""

E.g. we have group """"Northern Members"""" that have a certain set of permissions. We want to include an invitation link in all mailouts so Members can sign themselves up. Currently administrators would have to manually add users to groups as they sign up.    Kind Regards,  <USER>""

summary: "Inserting a very long code block can break the page scrolling on a Confluence page, shows as blank"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  When a long code block is present on a given page, it can cause the right side bar scrolling to break resulting in a blank page in which the content is not visible.     h3. Environment  * Confluence  * Code Block Macro    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Install Confluence   # Add a page and add the attached the storage format via source editor to the page and save.   # Scroll down the page without clickinf the side bar scroller icon    h3. Expected Results  You will be able to scroll down the entire page    h3. Actual Results  Half of the page gets blank after scrolling through the code and you cannot see towards the bottom of the page    h3. Notes  This issue appears related to the length of data include in the code block.     h3. Workaround    Shorten the amount of data included in the code block."""

summary: "Find <string> and replace with <same string><text> in Firefox hangs which resultantly blanks out page once published"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    In Firefox, using the Confluence Editor Find and Replace of say """"30"""" with """"30th"""" causes the Editor to hang.    Eventually, Firefox allows you to Stop the long loading page and then writes:    30thththththththth...........    to the page.    h3. Environment    Specific to Firefox.    h3. Steps to Reproduce     # Use Firefox (reproducible on Firefox v67 or v68).  # Create a page that contains the text """"30"""".  # Use the Confluence built-in find/replace feature to find """"30"""" and replace it with """"30th"""".  # Click the """"Replace all"""" button.  # The browser/page will hang for a while.  # Firefox will display a yellow banner """"A web page is slowing down your browser. What would you like to do?"""" with options """"Stop It"""" or """"Wait"""".  # Click """"Stop It""""  # The page now has a line that looks like """"30thththththththththththth.....""""  # Continue to edit the page (delete the extra th's and add a new line)  # Click the """"Update"""" button to save your changes  # You'll now be greeted with your saved page but it will be blank.  # You can restore your previous version, but the edits you made will have been lost.    h3. Expected Results    Find and replace should find the matching text """"30"""" and replace all occurrences with """"30th"""".    h3. Actual Results    # The page now has a line that looks like """"30thththththththththththth.....""""  # Continue to edit the page (delete the extra th's and add a new line)  # Click the """"Update"""" button to save your changes  # You'll now be greeted with your saved page but it will be blank.  # You can restore your previous version, but the edits you made will have been lost.    (i) Other search combinations does work in Firefox, this appears to be specific with Confluence Find <number>, replace <same number><text> in Firefox    e.g.    Confluence Find """"30"""" with """"30th"""" does not work in Firefox (x)  Confluence Find """"test"""" with """"testing"""" does not work in Firefox (x)  Confluence Find """"30"""" with """"40th"""" does work in Firefox (/)  Confluence Find """"world"""" with """"galaxy"""" does work in Firefox (/)    h3. Workaround    The Confluence Find and Replace of a <string> with the <same string><text> works as expected in Chrome, Safari and IE11"""
comments: "I could reproduce this behavior for the following versions of Firefox:   * Firefox 68.0.1 (64bit/Arch Linux)   * Firefox 67.0.4 (64bit/Arch Linux)   * Firefox 66.0.2 (64bit/Arch Linux)   * Firefox 64.0.2 (64bit/Windows 7)   * Firefox 63.0.3 (64bit/Windows 7)   * Firefox 63.0 (64bit/Windows 7)    The last version that does not show the described behavior was Firefox 62.0.3. Consequently the earliest version with which the bug can be reproduced is Firefox 63.0." [SEP] "The bug also affects Confluence 6.6.15." [SEP] "The bug persists on the latest Enterprise release (v6.13.10) of Confluence."

summary: "Bulk rename/remap external users"
description: """h2. Use case  Currently, it is possible to [rename external users|https://confluence.<USER>doc/change-a-username-427623582.html] as long as the users are from the same external user directory.    However, sometimes it is necessary for an organization to swap to an entirely different user directory (e.g. OpenLDAP to Active Directory), containing a different set of usernames (e.g. """"jsmith"""" to """"<USER>smith""""). This often occurs during mergers and acquisitions, for example. In this case, if the usernames are changed, Confluence has no way to link up the contents associated with old usernames to the new usernames.    This feature request is to request a tool to allow administrators to re-map content from one set of old usernames to new usernames, in bulk.    h2. Additional notes  * This is a similar use case to CONFSERVER-56448, but specific to *external users* (i.e. LDAP accounts) and specifically renaming in bulk  * This is related to, but not identical, to the feature request to merge two accounts into one: CONFSERVER-11990"""

summary: "Calendar fails to import if it contains URLs with encoded special characters"
description: """h3. Summary    Importing a calendar that contains an URL field with encoded special characters fails with a NullPointerException.    h3. Steps to Reproduce    # Export a calendar on a different application (.ics format)  # The source calendar must contain links starting with special characters on a new line in event descriptions  # Try to import the calendar    h3. Expected Results    The calendar is imported.    h3. Actual Results    The import fails and we see the message """"The uploaded data does not seem to be iCalendar content"""" on the UI:     !Screen Shot 2019-07-16 at 2.29.18 PM.png|thumbnail!     The follow exception is thrown in the {{atlassian-confluence.log}} file after enabling DEBUG mode for the class *com.atlassian.confluence.extra.calendar3*:       h3. Notes  As mentioned in the log, the offending line is 174, If we inspect the  [^sample-cal.ics]  attached, we can see that it contains the following URL:        The *%3C* combination is the ASCII encoding for the """"<"""" tag. This means that the original calendar had the following link in its description:        So far, we identified only one situation where links like this are encoded by the export tool and cause the problem:    * Link starting with a """"<"""" tag at the start of a new line    Although this is an unexpected situation and the encoding is performed by the source calendar tool during the export, Team Calendars should handle it more gracefully. Instead of halting the import, it should continue it but log an error about the invalid URL field.    h3. Workaround    To identify the offending event, proceed as follows:    # Visit Logging and Profiling  # Add the following class:    # Set the log level to DEBUG  # Click Save at the bottom of the page  # Try to import the calendar again  # Check the *atlassian-confluence* log  # The error message will point the offending line as follows:    # Once that is identified, edit the ics file and remove the encoded tags so the link looks like this:    # Save the file and try to import it again."""

summary: "Pasting content with images to a template does not save the changes"
description: """h3. Summary  # When editing or creating a template in Confluence, if we were to paste any content from another page with image, the content will appear on screen, but if we were to save the template, the changes will be lost.    h3. Steps to replicate  # Edit an existing or create a new global template.  # Find a page in Confluence that has images and edit and copy the content with the image.  # Paste the content onto the template, and the content will appear.  # Save the template, and check on it again, and the content that we paste will be missing, and if we edited an existing template, the older content will still be there.    h3. Expected behavior  # Content that was pasted onto the template should be there after the template is saved.    h3. Current behavior  # Content that is pasted is missing as soon as the template is saved.    h3. Notes  # Issue seems to be with the image, as seen in the log entry below, and also as just pasting text does not cause the issue.  # Reproducible on all Chrome. IE and Firefox.    h3. Error in the logs.  * {code:Java}  2019-07-16 22:24:34,833 ERROR [http-nio-8090-exec-11] [confluence.pages.actions.HeartbeatAction] startActivity No content found or no permission to view content with contentId: 9928705   -- referer: http://localhost:8090/pages/templates2/editpagetemplate.action?entityId=9928705 | url: /<USER>startheartbeatactivity.action | traceId: 8756d1f7f6211a13 | userName: root | action: startheartbeatactivity  2019-07-16 22:24:49,021 WARN [http-nio-8090-exec-5] [xhtml.editor.embed.EditorEmbeddedImageResourceUnmarshaller] unmarshal Error unmarshaling editor embedded image   -- referer: http://localhost:8090/pages/templates2/editpagetemplate.action?entityId=9928705 | url: /pages/templates2/doeditpagetemplate.action | traceId: 9a6f88a530cf09a9 | userName: root | action: doeditpagetemplate  java.lang.IllegalArgumentException: Content must not be null   at org.springframework.util.Assert.notNull(Assert.java:134)   at com.atlassian.confluence.pages.attachments.CachingAttachmentDao.getLatestAttachment(CachingAttachmentDao.java:72)   at com.atlassian.confluence.pages.DefaultAttachmentManager.getAttachment(DefaultAttachmentManager.java:145)   at com.atlassian.confluence.pages.DefaultAttachmentManager.getAttachment(DefaultAttachmentManager.java:111)   at com.atlassian.confluence.pages.DelegatorAttachmentManager.getAttachment(DelegatorAttachmentManager.java:114)   at com.atlassian.confluence.pages.CachingAttachmentManager.getAttachment(CachingAttachmentManager.java:185)   at sun.reflect.GeneratedMethodAccessor1099.invoke(Unknown Source)  {code}    h3. Workaround  # After pasting the content, remove the image, and add it manually into the template.  """
comments: "Closing as duplicate of CONFSERVER-40753"

summary: "Macro name retains the language setting of the last editor"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  If one page is modified with multiple users with different languages, the Macro name retains the language setting of the last editor.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  * Site default language is English  * User A's language is set to the site default  * User B's language is set to German for example  * B inserts a macro, B see it displays in German  * User A modified the page, the macro name that user A saw will be German.    h3. Expected Results  Users should see the macro name accordingly to the language settings    h3. Actual Results  The Macro name retains language setting of the last editor    h3. Workaround  Users have to save the page and edit again to see the correct Macro name translation."""
comments: "Hi Lei,    Thank you for raise this issue! Hope this can be fixed soon :)    Thanks again!"

summary: "Companion App editing fails on user session timeout"
description: """h3. Problem description    When editing a file with the Companion App a window is opened in the preview with the option to upload the changed file and save the changes made to the attachment.    If the user’s session times out, (this is after 60 minutes by default in Confluence) and if the user then attempts to upload the changes the action will fail with no error message given as there is no longer a session for the app to connect to and upload the file.  h3. Expected behaviour    This process to fail gracefully, either by announcing that the session has expired or by offering to login again and renew the session so the user can continue to edit the file.  h3. Current workarounds    It is now currently only possible to extend the user session length in the web.xml as shown here:   * [How to adjust the session timeout for Confluence|https://confluence.<USER>confkb/how-to-adjust-the-session-timeout-for-confluence-126910597.html]    However this situation will still arise if the editing process exceeds the new session length."""
comments: "As of 7.3.1, Companion 1.0.0 has its own file dialog which has a Confluence-independent timeout of two weeks."

summary: "SAL / LoginUriProvider.getLoginUri() returns invalid value if absolute login URL is used"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    From App Vendor:  {quote}  1. Set Confluence context path to 'confluence'    2. In seraph-config.xml use absolute URL as login.url  - eg http://example.com/SSOLogin?target=${originalurl} like in the example here https://docs.<USER>atlassian-seraph/2.6.1-m1/configuration.html    3. In a Confluence plugin, use LoginUriProvider.getLoginUri(targetUrl) to redirect the user to the login screen    -> the login URL will incorrectly have the Confluence's context path prepended to it, ie it will be '/confluencehttp://example.com/SSOLogin?target=...'    To reproduce with Scroll Viewport (bug found while investigating a support request from customer):  1. Create a Viewport to a space requiring login  2. Try to access that Viewport while not logged in  -> you'll be redirect to the incorrect login URL that Viewport gets from LoginUriProvider    ---    The bug is Atlassian Shared Access Layer (https://developer.<USER>server/framework/atlassian-sdk/shared-access-layer/)    The problem seems to be that this line does not check whether the login.url is absolute:  https://bitbucket.org/atlassian/atlassian-sal/src/275619451fef2331a18689e37c8c6b0e79d7bcbd/sal-core/src/main/java/com/atlassian/sal/core/auth/SeraphLoginUriProvider.java#lines-49    In Confluence source, there is also eg com.atlassian.seraph.util.RedirectUtils.getLoginUrl() that does check if the login URL is absolute or not. This is probably why the login redirect works in Confluence with a config like this, but not with Viewport.    this issue exists in the sal-core library as shipped with these Confluence versions:    Confluence 6.0.6 -> sal-core 3.0.8  Confluence 6.15.4 -> sal-core 3.2.0-abbce37    I have not checked other Confluence / SAL versions.  {quote}    h3. Exptected  Correct absolute login URL - eg 'http://example.com/SSOLogin?target=...'    h3. Actual  Incorrect login URL - eg ' /confluencehttp://example.com/SSOLogin?target=...'    h3. Workaround    Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""
comments: "We are running 6.13.10(ER) and have seen this issue pop up as well. This is also a behavior that doesn't occur all the time. It's very intermittent.    <USER>

summary: "eliminate colons in autonaming of image attachments"
description: """I have discovered a bit of an annoyance when pasting an image into a page where the image doesn't have a specific name of its own    e.g. you might use a snipping tool or select an image from some other document and copy it, then go to the Confluence page in edit mode and paste the image in.    Confluence treats that as an attachment but as it has no specific name so logically Confluence automatically assigns a name - that name is based on date and time stamp e.g. it would assign a name to the attachment like    image2019-7-13 8:37:43.png    the problem is the colons in the time stamp portion.      if you later want to download all attachments, the zip file is created ok and will download    But when you open the zip file, WINZIP pops up a message that it is an invalid filename.  The message suggest that you should still be able to unzip the file but I have failed to be able to do so and the file appears empty.         So it would be extremely helpful if Confluence used something other than colons in the auto-assigned filename ... even just stripping out the colons in the time stamp would help as I currently have to work through dozens of attachments on hundreds of pages and manually remove the colons in the file via the Properties option for each attachment.    It would be even more helpful if the auto file name process included reference to the User who created it, rather than just """"image""""         Given Confluence has its own interpretation of the meaning of a colon in a page/file name, it would seem logical not to allow its use when autonaming image attachments being pasted into a page.     """
comments: "anybody else suffering this hassle?"

summary: "Using children display macro on a parent page breaks the table format from child pages"
description: """Using children display macro on a parent page breaks the table format    h3. Issue Summary  If children pages have tables with columns of a certain width, they do not display with the same width in the parent page.    h3. Environment  * Confluence 6.12.0, 6.15.2  * All browsers    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # With _Responsive_ format in the editor:   #* Create a child page with tables and drag the columns to adjust their width.  #* On the parent page, use the _Children Display_ macro and select Excerpt Display as “rich content”. Publish the page.    # With _Fixed Width_ format in the editor:   #* Create a child page with tables and drag the columns to adjust their width.  #* On the parent page, use the _Children Display_ macro and select Excerpt Display as “rich content”. Publish the page.    h3. Expected Results  The _Children Display_ macro should display tables with the same width as the child pages so that the width of all the tables on the parent page look the same.    h3. Actual Results  # With _Responsive_ format in the editor:   #* The widths of the columns are not the same as the child page.    # With _Fixed Width_ format in the editor:   #* This completely breaks the table and displays each character on a new line.    h3. Workaround  Currently, there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available  """

summary: "Sorting a column with dates on the Page Property Report macro does not work correctly in some language settings"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    The *Page Property Report* macro does not sort correctly columns that have dates, inserted in the listed pages using the *Date* macro, when Confluence is set to German or French languages.  h3. Environment   * Have the browser set to German or French language   * Alternatively change the User settings in Confluence for either German or French language    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create 3 pages, each with the *Page Properties* macro, containing a table such as:  |Description|<enter some text>|  |Date|<insert a date with the *Date* macro|   Enter different values for each page, namely different days and months for the dates.   # Add the same label, for example """"pageprop"""", to the 3 pages;   # Create a new page and add the *Page Property Report* macro, specifying the label created in the previous steps;   # Viewing the latest page, click on the Date column and check the order.    h3. Expected Results    The list in the page is correctly sorted per date, either ascending or descending depending on the previous sorting order.  h3. Actual Results    The list order is inconsistent.  h4. Notes    This has been reproduced with German and French language but other languages may be affected as well.  h3. Workaround    There is no known workaround other than selecting English as the language settings for the user."""

summary: "Removed mentions from page do not get highlighted in the mail notification."
description: """h3. Issue Summary    In a page that has content, when a mention is removed from the content, this mention does not get -strikedthrough- and highlighted in red in the mail notifications for that page.    Normally, when a content gets removed from a page, that piece of content that is removed will be highlighted in red and strikethrough.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Head over to a page with content. This content must contain mentions.   # Remove some of the content including the mentions.   # When saving the page, ensure that *notify* *watches* option is set.    # Click Publish.    h3. Expected Results    Watchers will receive the mail notification, with all removed content (including mentions) strikedthrough and highlighted in red.   h3. Actual Results    Watchers will receive the mail notification, with all texts that were removed strikedthrough and highlighted in red. The exception here is mentions.     Notice the difference between the email notification below and the page history comparison. In page history comparison, removed mentions are strikethrough and highlighted. While in Email notification, it does not.     !Screen Shot 2019-07-12 at 4.49.24 PM.png|thumbnail!    !Screen Shot 2019-07-12 at 4.52.18 PM.png|thumbnail!  h3. summary: "Logs flood excessively with ""Queue Overflow happened"" WARNs when Confluence has a performance problem"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    We are seeing that in many instances of Confluence performance issues, the logs are being spammed with the following types of messages:    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Generate a flood of asynchronous calls to Confluence to simulate HTTP thread contention in the application    h3. Expected Results    The logs will not be flooded with these messages and other potentially important information related to the root of the problem may be found in the log files    h3. Actual Results    This message can be logged thousands of times in quick succession, leaving the admin with a useless log file that doesn't aid in RCA  h3. Workaround    The current workaround is to extend the default logging so that more logs are kept in archive, in order to give administrators a greater window to capture the relevant logs. This can be done my modifying the following two parameters in <confluence_install>/confluence/WEB-INF/classes/log4j.properties, which influences size of log files and number of logs kept in archive, respectively:    Note however that increasing the logs kept will impact the disk footprint and size of Support Zips generated.    *Extra Workaround:*  You can also choose to get rid of the noisy logging by going to Logging and Profiling and setting below class as FATAL:  """
comments: "This problem was fixed in CONFSERVER-55028. Fixed at versions 7.4.4, 7.6.2 and 7.7.2"

summary: "Moving content between spaces and updating content properties results in 500"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    When moving a page to another space and app updates the content property, an error 500 is thrown:    {code}  java.lang.IllegalArgumentException: Can't add an owner from another space  {code}    h3. Environment    * Confluence 6.13.x  h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Create a JSON Content Property in a page  # Move the page to  different space  # Update the JSON Content Property  # Error thrown: """"java.lang.IllegalArgumentException: Can't add an owner from another space"""" error is thrown    h3. Expected Results  Update of the JSON content property in a moved page should not fail    h3. Actual Results  Error thrown    The below exception is thrown (logs from customer sent to app vendor):    h3. Notes  * From community :https://community.<USER>t5/Answers-Developer-Questions/Confluence-REST-API-java-lang-IllegalArgumentException-Can-t-add/qaq-p/550967  * Related to https://jira.<USER>browse/CRA-1259?_ga=2.140707825.254316899.1561970127-2025536084.1502958597  * Vendor's customers gets the following error when *Comala Workflows* tries to update an existing property associated with a page. The error message from [CRA-1259] seems to be the same one but it is thrown in a different line    h3. Workaround    From vendor:  {quote}  Just to clarify and after being able to provide a workaround in our implementation by detecting the differences in space ownership to reset the property, I think that that update should be automatically done by Confluence. Simple listening to a page moved event to update it and not make the creator of the property responsible for doing so.  {quote}"""

summary: "Find and Replace feature not working properly on Microsoft Edge"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    When editing a Confluence page on Microsoft Edge, the """"Find"""" feature (and by extension, the """"Replace"""" feature) do not work correctly - the text on the Find field is not searched for or highlighted.  h3. Environment    Windows OS, using the Microsoft Edge browser (tested on Windows 10, Microsoft Edge 44.17763.1.0). Issue is reproducible on a fresh Confluence installation, with no browser add-ons on Edge; not reproducible using other browsers.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Edit any Confluence page   # Click the magnifying glass at the top of the editor header bar   # Insert some text on the Find field, and click """"Find""""    h3. Expected Results    The page will be searched for the selected word, and the Confluence editor will highlight it.  h3. Actual Results    Nothing happens when the """"Find"""" button is clicked - consequently, the """"Replace"""" function can't be used (and """"Replace all"""" does not work either).    Opening the console from the Edge browser tools, we see the following message:    This error is returned every time the """"Find"""" button is clicked.    !confluence editor find.png!  h3. Workaround    Use different browser (Chrome/Internet Explorer) or the browser's """"Find on page..."""" (Ctrl+F) option."""

This change <USER>the ability of copying a Confluence variable (template) to the same or another template as it consider the variable as an image.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Install Confluence 6.14.0 or later   # Go to Global Templates and Blueprints page   # Click on Add global page template and name it TemplateTest   # Create a variable (using the $ shortcut) as $test   # Copy and paste the variable on the same page   # Save the template   # Click on the Create from Template button (. . .)   # Choose the TemplateTest and notice that it's broken    To confirm the problem, install an earlier version of Confluence, for example 6.13.x and follow the same steps, you will notice that the problem is not replicable  h3. Expected Results    The variables are working  h3. Actual Results    The manually created variable works and the copied variable shows up as an image  h3. Inspecting the HTML    If we inspect the HTML via the browser console, we have the following results:    Variables in 6.14.0 while in editor mode (editing the template)   - Working variable:     - Copied variable:     -- Notice the new attribute {{data-image-src}} and missing attribute: {{data-variable-name}}.     Variables in 6.14.0 while in editor mode (using the template to create a page)   - Working variable:     - Copied variable:      Same variables on a Confluence 6.13.0 while in editor mode (editing the template)   - Working variable:     - Copied variable:     -- Notice that there are no changes that would break it.    Variables in 6.13.0 while in editor mode (using the template to create a page)   - Working variable:     - Copied variable:      h3. Workaround    Manually add the variable instead of copying it"""

summary: "Enable use of x-forwarded-for to log browser IP addresses"
description: """{panel:bgColor=#e7f4fa}    *NOTE:* This suggestion is for *Confluence Server*.    {panel}    h3. Problem    Confluence access logging is not able to log client IPs when a proxy is place. Load balancers and reverse proxy devices commonly use the """"x-forwarded-for"""" [HTTP header|https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTTP/Headers] to enable logging of the actual visitor IP address rather than the proxy IP address.    h3. Suggested Solution    Enable configuration of Confluence to log the IP address from the """"x-forwarded-for"""" header to the access logs.      h3. Workaround    Customers have reported that modifying the log4j configuration of Confluence is not a viable workaround. Fortunately, this can be achieved using the access logs that are enabled by default in the Tomcat container that ships with Confluence.    # Edit the file {{CONFLUENCE_INSTALL_DIR/conf/server.xml}}  # Insert this line before the line with """"AccessLogValve"""":   #* (!) Escape the """"."""" characters in PROXY_IP_ADDRESS with a backlash. Example: {{internalProxies=""""1\.1\.1\.8""""}}  # Configure AccessLogValve to accept the substituted IP from RemoteIpValve by adding the requestAttributesEnabled attribute to the AccessLogValve:     For full details see the Tomcat [RemoteIpValve documentation|https://tomcat.apache.org/tomcat-7.0-doc/api/org/apache/catalina/valves/RemoteIpValve.html]."""

summary: "Deleting user from Unsynced from Directory causes Team Calendar to send out emails for all events the user was in"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  # *Unsynced from Directory* functionality was added in Confluence 6.13 due to GDPR requirements, where we can remove all details of a user who is no longer in Confluence and anonymize their names.  # This also removes them from the *Who* section of events in team calendar, which in turn spams all users who are watching these calendars.  # The longer a user has been using team calendar\more events, the more emails get sent out when they are removed via the Unsync.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Sync a user from an external directory to Confluence, and add them as participants in some events.  # Remove them from the external directory and sync it to Confluence.  # The user will now be in the *Unsynced from Directory* tab in the user management page.  # Delete the user from here.  # Any user who is watching the calendar that the event is in will receive an email for each event that the user was in.     h3. Expected Results  # No email should be sent for the removal of the user from these events.    h3. Actual Results  # Each event that the user was in would trigger an email to watchers of the calendar.    h3. Workaround  # Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "As a Confluence administrator I would like to remove personal drafts associated to disabled accounts/users"
description: """h3. Problem Definition  [Disabling an user|https://confluence.<USER>doc/delete-or-disable-users-138318.html] means that the administrator is revoking user's access to Confluence and the user won't be able to curate content anymore:  {quote}  When an user account is disabled:      * The user won't be able to log in.      * The user won't be included in your license count.      * People won't be able to see the user in the People directory, mention them, or select their name/username as a search filter.      * Their full name will still appear on any spaces or content they created.      * They will be listed in User Management admin screens.      * Their account can be re-enabled (this will restore the connection to their content).      * Any content they created will be maintained.    {quote}    When Collaborative Editing is disabled, users create personal drafts that accessible just to them.  If an user is disabled, their personal drafts are kept in the database (and attachments in the file system) when no one else can access these contents.  Depending on the size of the Confluence instance, the content footprint may be too large and periodically removing it may be a good thing.    h3. Suggested Solution  Create a scheduled task to delete personal drafts associated to users that were disabled.  Since disabling an account is something that can be reverted, this scheduled task could check those accounts that are disabled for some time (i.e. one month).     h3. Workaround   As a workaround, the Confluence administrator can discard drafts from disabled users using a REST API method.    The SQL query below identify the ID of personal drafts associated to users that were disabled:      With this list in hand, the following REST API method can be used to discard each personal draft:  """

summary: "Remember cursor position in Edit mode"
description: """When user choose to edit the Page, cursor focus is always positioned at the beginning of the Page.   h5. Problem statement  Problem occurs when users are editing very long Pages, they are forced to scroll down and find the specific chapter they are aiming to edit.  h5. Solution  The solution for this would be to _remember_ the last position of the cursor when the Page was last saved, and on next edit to open the Page in edit mode with the cursor focus in the same spot as the last time, thus enabling user to smoothly continue where he/she left off."""

summary: "Restore of Confluence XML exported out of Confluence is failing to be imported into new instance"
description: """h2. *Environment*    XML Site Exports created out of Confluence 6.14.0+ may fail to be imported into new Confluence instance.    h3. *Affected versions*    Only these versions are affected by this bug:    * All Confluence 6.14.x  * Confluence 6.15.0 -> 6.15.7    h2. *Steps to reproduce*    # Perform a Site XML Backup from any Confluence 6.14.0+  # Setup a new Confluence instance and import the above XML backup and the import fails    h2. *Expected behaviour*    The XML site export created """"successfully"""" from Confluence 6.14.0 should be able to import into a new Confluence instance.    Alternatively, the export should be marked as Failed if it fails to produce a data integrity XML backup.    h2. *Actual behaviour*    The Site XML is failing to import into a new Confluence instance with this reported error:          where:        is referring to attempting to read the {{confluence.server.id}} from BANDANA.    Diving deeper, this is due to the entities.xml in the XML export missing this block:        Deeper comparison of the entities.xml with the Source Database, the exported entities.xml is actually missing a lot more BANDANA rows and other TABLE rows.    h2. *Validation*    * Reviewed [Restoring Confluence XML site backups fails: Import failed. Check your server logs ...|https://confluence.<USER>confkb/restoring-confluence-xml-site-backups-fails-import-failed-check-your-server-logs-921471138.html] which has not helped.  * Proceed with this check:    # Obtain the total number of a few tables row count (e.g. BANDANA, BODYCONTENT, EVENTS, <USER>..) from the Source Database    # Extract out the XML backup and run this GREP / FINDSTR to obtain how many BANDANA, BODYCONTENT, EVENTS rows are present in the entities.xml  {code:title=On Windows}  findstr """"class=\""""ConfluenceBandanaRecord\"""""""" entities.xml | find /i """"class"""" /c    findstr """"class=\""""BodyContent\"""""""" entities.xml | find /i """"class"""" /c    findstr """"class=\""""Events\"""""""" entities.xml | find /i """"class"""" /c    # Also confirm that entities.xml is indeed missing the confluence.server.id row  {code:title=On Windows}  findstr """"confluence.server.id"""" entities.xml      The result of the Database row count of BANDANA, BODYCONTENT and EVENTS should match the number of BANDANA occurrences in entities.xml. If the numbers are vastly different - then you have encountered this issue.    h2. *Workaround*    There is no reliable work around available for this bug.    h2. *Resolution*    A bug has been identified in the XML export logic and we must upgrade to a fixed version of Confluence. For a complete XML backup:    # Take a database level backup of your current Confluence Database  # Upgrade to Confluence 6.15.9+ or Confluence 7.0+ in a [Staging Environment|https://confluence.<USER>doc/create-a-staging-environment-for-upgrading-confluence-866094180.html]  # Perform the XML export on the upgraded version which will now produce an XML export zip file that can be imported into the destination instance (which should be the same version as the Exported instance)    (!) It is currently advised to take database level backups or use database level backups when possible"""

summary: "additional display columns in Attachments macro"

If the Attachments macro had the ability to show the Labels column, then they could use Labels as the document reference number.  They could sort by the Label column and even use normal Ctrl+F in their browser search to find what they are looking for.    Other configurable columns would be the name of the last User who uploaded the last document version, and of course last version date."""

Be great to see the original document metadata fields shown (ie Office, Cateogry, Subject, Title, Author, Create, <USER>    Thanks,         <USER> [SEP] "Whilst my original suggestion is about a generic field that the Space Users can create ""on the fly"" with whatever text they want to add, it is a great additional suggestion <USER>.. would be great if importing a document automatically read the document metadata, af
