summary: "Add support for Apple SSO login methods to native iOS Confluence Server app"
description: """We support SSO login as long as the login that is being done inside the in-app web-view is successful. We can see that the for login to work the Apple-specific SSO {{AppleConnect}} extension is needed, which works with iOS Safari browser. The login fails because the in-app web-view is built from Apple's {{WKWebview}} which may not support AppleConnect extension.    The mobile app can solve this by using {{SFSafariViewController}} (even though it's deprecated) within the app, which will allow our Connect Extension to be invoked accordingly.     Apple documentation recommends using the in-app webview class {{ASWebAuthenticationSession}} for web based login authentication, but {{ASWebAuthenticationSession}} does not allow the extension.     This should be resolved with the new plugin functionality in iOS 13, but until then we would need to adopt {{SFSafariViewController}}."""

summary: "Deleting users from Confluence does not delete their personal drafts"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  Since Confluence version 6.13, administrators have the option to delete users that created content.  Deleting an user will not delete the contents they created. Rather, it will anonymize these contents.    If Collaborative Editing is disabled, then users can have personal drafts.  The way personal drafts work means that no other user (even the confluence administrator) can perform operations (edit, publish or discard) on these drafts, only the user who created them.    When an user is deleted by the Confluence administrator, personal drafts and associated content (such as attachments) are not deleted.  Since these personal drafts cannot be accessed by any other user (and the delete operation cannot be undone), this means unnecessary records will still be in the database, as well as in the file system (attachments).    _Confluence administrator trying to discard a personal draft created by another user gives an error._   !drafts-img003.png|thumbnail!     This is not a security issue because the drafts cannot be associated with the deleted user anymore.    h3. Environment  This issue applies only to versions on which deleting an user that created content is possible.  This is from Confluence 6.13 onwards.  This applies only to personal drafts, created when Collaborative Editing (CE) is disabled.  Drafts created while CE is enabled are shared, so there's no meaning on deleting them.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Install a vanilla Confluence instance.  # Create an user (username: {{regular_user001}}) that is part of the {{confluence-users}} group.  # Access Confluence as {{regular_user001}} and create a sample Space.  # In that Space, create a page, but don't publish it.  # Wait for the draft to be autosaved by Confluence and click on the _Close_ button.   !drafts-img004.png|thumbnail!    # Accessing the _Drafts_ page under user's profile shows the drafts created by this user.   !drafts-img001.png|thumbnail!   # Access Confluence as the administrator and go the link {{<Confluence Base URL>/admin/users/viewuser.action?username=regular_user001}} to view the user profile.  # Click on the _Delete_ link and confirm the operation on the next screen.    h3. Expected Results  User is deleted, the content published is anonymized and personal drafts are removed, keeping footprint at the minimum necessary without lost of content.    h3. Actual Results  Personal drafts are not removed.  Searching for these drafts in the database confirms this.    h3. Workaround  As a workaround, the Confluence administrator can discard drafts from deleted users using a REST API method.  When an user is deleted from Confluence, a change is made in the {{user_mapping}} table, making the value from {{username}} attribute equals to the {{user_key}}.  Therefore, running the query below identifies users that were deleted.      With that information, Confluence administrator can identify the draft ID of users that were deleted by running the following SQL query:      Then, with each {{DRAFT_ID}} the administrator can run the following REST API method:    A 204 HTTP status response confirms the request was processed."""
comments: "Although I think this is very much needed, there is also a need to clean up drafts for users that are not being deleted. We are a very large enterprise with a lot of employees and contractors that come and go. I don't want their content to disappear, but if they have drafts, they can never be accessed again. These users are always disabled once they leave the company, and it is once they are disabled that their drafts will become inaccessible. There is no one that is going to be able to determine if the draft has value, so what is the purpose of keeping it?  I appreciate the query to identify, but please consider the 2nd part of this - allowing administrators to clean up after users no longer with the company but have content that we want to keep.      "

summary: "Allow Confluence Standalone Server started with AWS Cloud Formation Template to upgrade by updating the Cloud Formation Stack"
description: """h3. Problem Definition  Allow Confluence Standalone Server started with AWS Cloud Formation Template to upgrade by updating the Cloud Formation Stack    Upgradin the Confluence Standalone Server started using the [ConfluenceServer.template.yaml|https://bitbucket.org/atlassian/atlassian-aws-deployment/src/master/templates/ConfluenceServer.template.yaml] needs to be manually carried out following the standard [upgrade process|https://confluence.<USER>doc/upgrading-confluence-4578.html] instead of just updating the Cloud Formation Stack similar to the Confluence Data Center upgrade stated in the documentation below:  * [Upgrading Confluence Data Center in AWS|https://confluence.<USER>doc/running-confluence-data-center-in-aws-879956085.html#RunningConfluenceDataCenterinAWS-upgradeUpgrading]    The main reason why is that Confluence Standalone Server doesn't persist the home directory when an EC2 node is brought down. Hence changing the *Confluence Version* in the stack and terminating the existing EC2 will lost the Confluence Home Directory    h3. Suggestion  Allowing the Home Directory to be persisted even when the EC2 is terminated so the newly started EC2 after updating the stack can have a newer version while keeping the Home Directory data to proceed with the upgrade, similar to the Data Center.    This helps to cut down most of the manual work from upgrading the Confluence"""

summary: "Use DNS for cluster peers - K8s support"
description: """Hi,    I am currently trying to run confluence data center nodes on Kubernetes.   Since the underlaying network layer does not support multicast, I am stuck with tcp_ip cluster.type.   As pods get assigned new IP-addresses on rebuild and I need to rebuild the pods in order to change the configuration, there seems to be a circular dependency I cannot get over.    As I see many people struggling with this exact part on K8s, I'd like to suggest the property of cluster.peers to be DNS names, a bit like CONFSERVER-53527, but without the synchrony-part.    The config-property would be extended like this:    The naive behavior-changes of the cluster-node discovery would consist of a simpe DNS-query while parsing the config, then recheck the DNS every time a node fails.    This change would directly enable Confluence to be run on Kubernetes.     """

summary: "Pages not visible to groups in PageTree when permission is set via ContentPermissionManager class after an update"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  After upgrading our instance to version 6.15.1 (from 6.8.1) some of our users reported missing page hierarchies in the tree of a single space. All restrictions are looking fine, and the affected users can access the missing pages using direct links. However, they cannot see them on the tree on the navigation panel even when opening the page from a direct link.    Scope: we've set up a page tree and need to set the restrictions on page level individually. Because there's a lot of pages and branches the restriction handling must be achieved by API calls. Performance is an issue, so processing these restrictions needs to be fast.    Approach: We are using the following method: [https://docs.<USER>ConfluenceServer/javadoc/6.15.6/com/atlassian/confluence/core/ContentPermissionManager.html#setContentPermissions-java.util.Map-com.atlassian.confluence.core.ContentEntityObject-]    Consider the following snippet:    The pages show all restrictions in place as provided by the map, however, some users within some groups have issues seeing the pages on the Page Tree.   As far as we can tell the issue is not related to a single space.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Install addon that handles the space structure and restrictions in v6.8.1  # Update Confluence to v6.15.1    h3. Expected Results  The pages should be both visible to people in groups within the map provided to the method, as well as viewable and/or editable.    h3. Actual Results  In some circumstances, users cannot see pages on the Page Tree. They are able to access the pages missing in the Page Tree by using a direct link, however.    h3. Notes  * This is a follow up on a service desk issue with key CSP-248197. See information there for a more wide view on the issue and what has been tried to resolve the issue already.  * In order to isolate the issue, the following were done   ** We installed Confluence Server 6.8.1 and installed our addon that handles the space structure and restrictions. The space was set up, restrictions put in place as described below, and users within the groups affected could see everything on their Page Tree.   ** Now I proceeded to uninstall the addon to remove any customization, so the testing environment turned back to a standard installation. Users could still see everything in the Page Tree.  ** Then I proceeded to upgrade the Confluence instance from 6.8.1 to    6.15.1, and when the users checked in on the Page Tree, they found the pages missing. This leads to the conclusion that somewhere along the line of the upgrade process the permissions got messed up. And it looks like they can't be brought back to work by using the same API calls.    h3. Workaround  Currently, there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""
comments: "Update:   * We've proceeded to create a fresh install of 6.15.1, installed the addon and let the addon set up the space and permissions   * The issue still remains on the fresh install   * We also tested the behavior on an instance that was upgraded from 6.8.1, this time in a newly created space   * The issue remains in the new space   * Setting the permissions manually via the UI on an affected page makes the page visible in the tree until the addon starts its permission handling job again, after execution the pages are hidden from the tree again    The addon uses Java API version 6.15.1.    Our next step is to try different approaches when setting the permissions via the addon. I will report my findings once I got the results." [SEP] "Update:         I've went ahead and tested two alternative ways of setting permissions using the ContentPermissionManager interface.       h2. Alternative 1    Basically the same as the snippet in the opening post, but instead of using one single map, we're using two calls

summary: "Administrator Contact Form should use a standard mail server"

The reason of this is because there is no clear warnings nor errors were logged/provided when the contact form is not being sent successfully. The user cannot tell if the email is being sent, it'd be great if we can improve this particular section - to at least tracking the mail sent or log information in the mail logs.    Reference: https://confluence.<USER>doc/configuring-the-administrator-contact-page-216957115.html#ConfiguringtheAdministratorContactPage-DisablingtheAdministratorContactForm"""
comments: "The main problem here is that Confluence sends the e-mail as the user that filled out the form instead of using the globally configured outgoing mail address. This might not be allowed due to spoofing protections <USER> For instance user might have the e-mail [<EMAIL>|<USER><EMAIL>] and Confluence sends other e-mail notifications as [<EMAIL>.|<USER><EMAIL>.] The latter is ok but mails using b.com as the sender will be rejected by the SMTP (e.g. Amazon SES). Confluence logs will show the error but no error is shown to the user so the user will expect the administrators to have received a reply."

summary: "Store temporary files for Atlassian Companion App outside of the Roaming profile on Windows"
description: """h2. *Problem Description*    Editing files via the Atlassian Companion app in Microsoft Windows stores files in two locations:    1. Actual files downloaded and sent to the respective application:    * C:\Users\admin\.atlassian-companion    2. Temporary Cache files (including a CACHE copy of the downloaded file) is stored in:    * C:\Users\admin\AppData\Roaming\Atlassian Companion\Cache    Every time a file is edited, a copy is again put into the above Temporary Cache location inside the *Roaming* folder:        The *Cache folder* in the Roaming profile can therefore grow excessively large and hit size quotas on the Roaming profile folder.    h2. *Suggestion*    Provide a way to cap the upper limit on the Cache folder.    * [Chromium command-line params|https://github.com/electron/electron/blob/master/docs/api/chrome-command-line-switches.md] do support a maximum cache size:    {quote}  *{{--disk-cache-size=size}}*    Forces the maximum disk space to be used by the disk cache, in bytes.  {quote}  * Alternatively, this Cache folder should be written to {{C:\Users\admin\.atlassian-companion}}, outside of the Roaming folder    h2. *Work Around*    Atlassian Companion App uses the Electron framework which  generates/utilises these two folders:    * Cache  * GPUCache    Edited: It is safe to manually delete the folders - the entire AppData/Roaming/Atlassian Companion/Cache directory should be deleted rather than selectively removing files. If the folder is locked whilst Atlassian Companion App is running please exit the app, delete the folder and restart. This will reduce the disk space used in the Windows Roaming folder."""
comments: "A fix for this issue is available in Companion 1.1.0. View the [Release Notes|https://confluence.<USER>doc/atlassian-companion-app-release-notes-958455712.html] to see what other issues are resolved.    If Companion is installed via the MSI, then it will need to be manually updated to the new version.    "

summary: "Requests to aggregate Jira metadata in Confluence can exhaust HTTP threads and cause outages"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    When linked with Jira, if placed under high load and an unresponsive Jira, all Confluence HTTP threads can be occupied by Jira metadata aggregation tasks. This will leave no threads to process users' requests and eventually lead to outage.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create an app link between Confluence and Jira   # Block connections from Confluence to Jira e.g. use a firewall to block all traffic to Jira   # In Confluence, open more than 10 new pages every 5 seconds (This throughput will make the queue to process Jira metadata aggregation to grow gradually)    h3. Expected Results    Confluence should handle Jira's slowness gracefully.  h3. Actual Results    Confluence users observe intermittent failures then complete outage.    Captured thread dumps will show all HTTP threads busy fetching metadata from Jira:    h3. Root cause    Confluence Jira Metadata plugin uses a single ThreadPoolExecutor (maximum of 10 threads in a pool backed by an unlimited LinkedBlockingQueue) to aggregate metadata from Jira for every page load in Confluence. There are 2 types of aggregation tasks: Cache loading and Metadata fetching. The problem lies in the fact that a Cache loading task creates Fetching tasks then submits them into the very executor it is running in then blocks and waits until either its child tasks are finished or is timed out (currently set to 5 seconds). If all threads in the executor are occupied by Cache loading tasks then all of them will be blocked for 5 seconds for no good reason while new loading tasks will be put into a waiting queue. If we keep throwing more than 10 loading tasks (by opening 10 new pages in the browsers) every 5 seconds, this situation will get worse and worse and ultimately leads to an outage.  h3. Workaround    Disabling the """"Confluence Jira Metadata"""" plugin should bring Confluence back.    The only functionality that will be lost is the """"Jira Links"""" list at the top of the Confluence page if the page contains links to Jira. The actual links within the content of the page will still work."""
comments: "A fix for this issue is available to Server and Data Center customers in Confluence 6.15.8  [Upgrade|https://www.<USER>software/confluence/download?utm_source=confluence_bugfix&utm_campaign=confluence_bugfix&utm_medium=email] now or check out the [Release Notes|https://confluence.<USER>doc/confluence-6-15-release-notes-965554120.html?utm_source=confluence_bugfix&utm_campaign=confluence_bugfix&utm_medium=email] to see what other issues are resolved." We requested for it since a long time to our TAM and still waiting the fix before to migrate to 6.13.    Thanks.  " [SEP] "Hi [~<USER>,    This fix has just been released to 6.15.8. We normally let newly released changes that are deemed ER-worthy to soak for couple of weeks before backporting them to ER.         Hope this helps.         Cheers,    Nhan" [SEP] "If you're running the Confluence 6.13 Enterprise release, a fix for this issue is now available

Based on the [supported platforms|https://confluence.<USER>conf613/supported-platforms-964961686.html], there are no mentions of which flavor Confluence should support, therefore we must test with all provided or specify which one is supported.  h4. Problem Definition  Jira has an admin page to view global user sessions as detailed in [Monitor a user's activity|https://confluence.<USER>adminjiraserver072/monitor-a-user-s-activity-828787672.html].  It would be interesting to have something similar on Confluence too.  As listed in [this KB|https://confluence.<USER>adminjiraserver072/monitor-a-user-s-activity-828787672.html], benefits are:  {quote}   * Know who to contact before planned downtime.   * Regularly monitor the average number of active users to evaluating your licensing quota.   * View user count if the system is under duress.   * And more!  {quote}    h3. Suggested Solution  Implement on Confluence an admin page similar to the _Current User Sessions in JIRA_ ({{/secure/admin/CurrentUsersList.jspa}}).     h3. Workaround   _There's no workaround so far_"""

summary: "The health check should verify if the instance is running a vulnerable version based on our public security advisories"
description: """This request is to add a feature to the Health Check to check if the version of Confluence is affected by a security breach based on public security advisories.    For example, on March and April 2019, we had 2 security advisories which impacted all versions of Confluence. As soon as they were made public, the health check should add a warning/banner to the admin section of Confluence (any Confluence admin will see it), showing a link to the advisory and warning that they should upgrade Confluence.    This will implement the notifications sent to Main technical Contacts and users subscribed under Tech Alerts"""

summary: "Allow PDF exports to be larger than 2GB"
description: """h3. Problem Definition    Confluence fails when attempting a PDF space export which is larger than 2GB. If the PDF gets larger than 2GB, the exception below is thrown in the atlassian-confluence.log file:    There is an error in the GUI, but is not clear why the error is occurring:   !2Gb PDF Space Export Limitation.png|thumbnail!    This is caused by a 2GB limit for PDF exports, due to the libraries utilized.   * [Flying Saucer|https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Flying_Saucer_(library)] v9.1.6 library is used to render XHTML in memory for a space/page export.   * Flying Saucer uses [iText|https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/IText] v2.1.7 library to export the rendered contents into a PDF file.   * The iText library prior version 5.3 has the [limitation of 2GB|https://itextpdf.com/en/resources/faq/technical-support/itext-5-legacy/what-size-limit-pdf-file] for generated files.   * This limitation exists due to {{java.nio.channels.FileChannel}} [limit of 2GB|https://bugs.openjdk.java.net/browse/JDK-6347833] for memory mapped files.    h3. Suggested Solution    One or more of the following options:   * Improve the error message, which indicates the 2GB limit and update Confluence documentation to reflect the 2GB Limit.   * Update Confluence to use a version of FlyingSaucer/iText that doesn't have the 2GB limit.   * Update Confluence to export/compose the PDF to the file system and not directly to memory.    h3. Workarounds    Split the larger spaces into smaller spaces.    Additionally, we have tested a third party app that works with larger exports. Consider trying [Content Exporter (Word, PDF, HTML)|https://marketplace.<USER>apps/4909/content-exporter-word-pdf-html] if you require a single PDF that will be larger than 2GB."""

summary: "Deleting an attachment causes comments referencing that attachment to show ""unknown-attachment"""
description: """h3. Issue Summary  Deleting an attachment causes comments referencing that attachment to show """"unknown-attachment"""". It should instead show a more user-friendly message.    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Add a comment to a page and attach a file in the comment. This will add the attachment to the page and the comment will reference the file.   # While viewing the page go to """"..."""" > Attachments, and remove the attachment    h3. Expected Results  Attachment shows a user-friendly message that lets the viewer know the file referenced is no longer attached to the page, e.g. """"The attachment has been removed from this page""""    h3. Actual Results  Attachment shows a """"*? Unknown Attachment*"""" block in grey    h3. Workaround  None"""

summary: "Reduce the number of POST requests when retrieving attachments via the Attachments macro"
description: """Through Confluence version 6.13, requests for attachments by the Attachments macro were facilitated through a server side GET request.    However, since version 6.14, this functionality with the Attachments macro changed such that attachments are now retrieved via a series of client side POST requests that follow this format:   * [http://localhost:8090/rest/files/1.0/files/content/1409025/byAttachmentIds]    For a given page that includes a large number of attachments, this large number of asynchronous POST requests can appear as a DDOS attack by web application firewalls.    Would it be possible to revert back to using the GET or coalesce these POST requests into one request for all attachments, rather than issuing them one per each attachment?    Otherwise, the only workarounds for this issue would be to either:   * Reduce the number of attachments on a page.   * Modify the firewall rules to allow a higher number of POST requests."""

summary: "Confluence leaks group names when anonymous access is enabled"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    As a side effect of allowing anonymous access to Confluence, the REST API leaks information such as group names. If your organisation uses this information for verification or authentication in other areas then this information could be used to compromise or attack. There may also be information contained in group names that would violate the GDPR regulations by making it freely available.    h3. Environment    Any Confluence instance that is has anonymous access allowed at some level. There are some online examples below:    https://wiki.akraino.org/rest/prototype/1/search/user-or-group.<USER>max-results=50&query=a  https://confluence.<USER>rest/prototype/1/search/user-or-group.<USER>max-results=50&query=a    h3. Suggested behaviour    The ability to prevent this information from leaking when anonymous access is enabled. There are some external infrastructure changes that could be made to prevent this access, but it is not always a given that this could be implemented in the instance concerned. This should be part of Confluence's default settings."""

summary: "Download All Attachments of a page does not provide UI feedback about current status"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  In a Confluence Page, going to *...* > *Attachments* gives the user a *Download All* link that will _Download all the latest versions of attachments on this page as single zip file_.  This creates a temporary _.zip_ file in the {{<Confluence Home/temp}} folder makes this available to the user as a _downloadable_ file.    Depending on the size and on the number of attachments in the page, generating the _.zip_ file on the _backend_ can take seconds or minutes. While the file is generated, there's no feedback on the UI to the user about the current status until the file is ready to download.  If the user doesn't know that Confluence is properly processing this request, this lack of feedback may lead the user to think nothing is happening and click on the same button again.  Depending on user's expectations, this can repeat a number of times what can contribute the file system to fill in, since every time the user clicks on the button it will start a new thread in Confluence and create a new file in the temporary folder when the thread completes.      h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Install a vanilla instance of Confluence  # Configure the [maximum attachment size|https://confluence.<USER>doc/configuring-attachment-size-150040.html] to be bigger than 1GB.  # Create a sample page in any space  # Go to *...* > *Attachments*  # Attach a large file -- for example with 800MB  # Attach another file, but a smaller one this time -- for example, with less than 1MB  # Still in the _Attachments_ page, click on _Download All_    h3. Expected Results  Confluence provides a fast and meaningful feedback to the user about the progress on preparing the files to be downloaded and preventing the user from clicking on the _Download All_ button again while the request is being processed.    h3. Actual Results  Confluence does not provide any feedback to the user and, when the file is ready, browser's pop-up with the download file option will appear.       h3. Notes  Besides the UI issue, depending on user's actions this can create problems to the Confluence server, filling in the file system on which the {{<confluence home>}} folder is mounted.  If the users don't understand the file is being prepared, they may click on the _Download All_ button several times, which creates separate threads to serve the same request.  Each thread will create a new file in the {{<confluence home>/temp}} folder.    A suggested UI response could be similar to the status page when a Space Export is triggered.    In addition to this, users may get a 504 HTTP status if a reverse proxy is configured to do so.    h3. Workaround  In the *Custom HTML* admin page, add the following script _At end of the BODY_ Section:    This will disable the download link after the first time it is clicked."""

summary: "Horizontal alignment of expand/collapse-icons and their associated texts is offset"
description: """h4. Problem description   When viewing a page that contains the """"Expand"""" macro, the """"v""""-icon of the expanded view seems to be placed too low compared to the text right next to it. When the macro is collapsed, the """">""""-icon seems to be aligned properly (although maybe not pixel-perfect).    When looking at the sidebar, the """"v""""-icon of expanded parent pages, seems to be aligned properly whereas the """">""""-icon of collapsed parent pages seems to be placed too high compared to the text.    On the """"Reorder Pages""""-screen it's the other way around again, """"v""""-icons are placed too low, """">""""-icons seem to be ok.  h4. Environment  Tested with Internet Explorer 11, Firefox and Chrome with the zoom level set to 100%.  h4. Expected behavior  The icons should align properly, i. e. the tips of the icons should always be positioned at half-height of their associated texts, regardless of whether they are expanded or collapsed.  h4. Actual behavior  Using IE11, it's possible to highlight not only the text but also the icons by pressing Ctrl+A. This shows that the icons are never centered but rather stick to either the top or the bottom border of their associated texts.    Please see the attached screenshots."""

summary: "The 'New expanded search' result is not prioritizing the *Page title* as it used to in previous 'Quick Search'."
description: """h4. Problem Statement  The 'New expanded search' result is not prioritizing the *Page title* as it used to in previous 'Quick Search'.    h4. How 'Old Quick Search' gives results:  This search gives a result based on the following:  * title + boosting by recency + space preference    h4. How 'New Expanded Search' gives results:  This search gives a result based on the following:  * title (less sophisticated) + content body (number of matches of the word in the content) + boosting by recency    h4. Improvement  * Page title should still be the priority instead of less sophisticated, followed by the content body matches and recency."""

Expected Results    Returning to editor  h3. Actual Results    Going back to the page before the editor, e.g. blank page if you started in a new tab. summary: "Confluence asynchronous logging "
description: """Request that Confluence be architected to use asynchronous logging for enhanced performance.     Currently Confluence 6.x uses Log4j-1 which has AsyncAppender, however we are not programmatically implementing this feature.     [Log4j-2|https://logging.apache.org/log4j/2.x/] supports asynchronous logging (see the """"Improved Performance section), requesting that Log4j-2 or above be considered to be used in future versions of Confluence and Confluence implement this functionality. """

summary: "Companion App should provide a configuration option to prevent clearing files on startup"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Currently, on startup the Companion App will remove all attachments from its temporary directory. This is to prevent temporary files building up and taking up too much space.     However, some users would prefer to have more control over when these files are deleted. We could provide a configuration option in the Companion menu such as """"Clear temporary files on startup"""" that defaults to selected. If the user unticks this option then we would no longer perform the clear out of the temporary directory.   h3.      This suggestion is important in cases when the user did not upload changes, but it has saved them. And because of that, his changes will be gone, and we will not be able to recover them."""
comments: "This is a very common scenario for us. Because restarting your computer will (of course) restart the companion application and empty all files in its folder...    A better solution would be to ask the user which file(s) to be deleted at companion startup or delete the files after a time period instead (a month or so).    ======    Scenario: I started doing a report by editing a excel-file, using the edit in excel-feature in confluence. The file is opened in excel and I have been working on it for a couple of hours, and saving it continously as you don´t want anything to be lost. Due to some other issues I had to do other stuff, including restarting my computer. Next day I wanted to continue in updating my report.    My first thought was just to open excel, browse for the last used files, and continue my work from there. (and then upload the file manually since I know that the browser connection for the companion app is gone, once you close the browser.)    Everything looking good in excel, it remembered I u

Issue Summary    When editing the .properties file of a plugin to customize text as described in [How to edit bundled or system plugins|https://confluence.<USER>confkb/how-to-edit-bundled-or-system-plugins-302811979.html], the customized text does not display.  h3. Environment    Confluence 6.10 and above  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Stop Confluence   # Edit the text in the .properties file within a plugin's .jar file as described in [How to edit files in Confluence JAR files|https://confluence.<USER>confkb/how-to-edit-files-in-confluence-jar-files-103711179.html]   # [Clear the Plugin Cache|https://confluence.<USER>confkb/how-to-clear-confluence-plugins-cache-297664846.html]   # Clear Browser's cache   # Restart Confluence    h3. Expected Results    Edit the appropriate text for key entries in the {{.properties}} file, see the change in the web UI.  h3. Actual Results    The default text of the key is still shown, the customized text is not.  h3. Notes    This works in Confluence 6.9 and below.  h3. Workaround    This issue can be circumvented by creating a new key in the .properties file and then changing references elsewhere in the plugin to use this key instead of the default key.    # Stop Confluence   # Make a backup copy of the original .jar file   # Expand the contents of the .jar file   # Locate the text value you wish to change in the .properties file, notate the name of this key   # Create a new key with a similar name, adding a """".custom"""" to the key's name and customize the text   # Search the contents of the files expanded from the .jar for the original key's name   # Replace each reference found to the new key name in each of these files, save   # Recompress the .jar file together with these edited files   # Copy the customized .jar file back to its original location, overwriting the original   # [Clear the Plugin Cache|https://confluence.<USER>confkb/how-to-clear-confluence-plugins-cache-297664846.html]   # Clear Browser's cache    # Restart Confluence"""
comments: "Hi @all    in Confluence 6.15.6 that Workaround doesn't work! Could you provide a solution, please?    Thank you,    Vittoria"

summary: "Confluence Data Center CloudFormation deployment postgres collation incorrect"
description: """h3. Summary   Currently this repo appears to be deploying Confluence with the incorrect collation set to C instead of UTF-8    This issue seems to affect all files calling {{atl_createRemoteDb}}:    atlassian-aws-deployment//ami/scripts/init.d/atl-init-confluence.sh  atlassian-aws-deployment//ami/scripts/init.d/atl-init-jira.sh:  atlassian-aws-deployment//ami/scripts/init.d/atl-init-crowd.sh:  Example:     This appears to be setting Confluence Collation to C instead of UTF-8. According to our Postgres Docs for Confluence [here|https://confluence.<USER>doc/database-setup-for-postgresql-173244522.html], C is known to cause issues with Confluence and is not a supported config.    h3. Environment   * Confluence Server    h3. Steps to Reproduce    Deploy to AWS with our Cloud Formation Templates templates  h3. Expected Results   Confluence will be deployed correctly    h3. Actual Results   The user will receive WARN on start up that the database isn't configured correctly:        h3. Workaround  N/A - User will have to recreate DB with correct settings or have a DBA fix the collation issue on the existing db  """
comments: "This has been fixed in the latest version of the AWS Confluence Quickstart. The new version is using Ansible roles for instance and application configuration. For Confluence, we have specifically fixed this as part of the transition from custom AMIs configured via shell scripts.    The CloudFormation templates are available from official S3 bucket and via the guides as well ([https://aws.amazon.com/quickstart/architecture/confluence/]). To verify whether the template is using Ansible roles for configuration, you can just list all template parameters (either in YAML or AWS Console) and look for {{DeploymentAutomationRepository}} parameter. If it is present, then there shouldn't be any issue with collation.    This is the collation value that is used for Confluence in the Ansible role:    [https://bitbucket.org/atlassian/dc-deployments-automation/src/7c9add4439cd76695ac9c9392db20f5d3584f5c4/aws_confluence_dc_node.yml#lines-12]"

summary: "Increase managed Synchrony JVM maximum memory to at least 2GB"
description: """h3. Problem Definition  By default, in the managed Synchrony deployment (both for Server and Data Center), the default Synchrony heap is 1GB.  This is not enough for most of the deployments and it may often cause OOM problems on the Synchrony JVM.    On Confluence Data Center prior to the managed Synchrony, the startup script had the heap configured to 2GB by default.  Migrating to managed from the standalone cluster may create a problem if the default configuration is used.    h3. Suggested Solution  Ship Confluence (Server and Data Center) with a default Synchrony JVM heap size to at least 2GB by using the following JVM attribute:      Also, please consider if a higher value (such as 4GB) should be used as the default heap size on Data Center.    h3. Workaround   Manually configure the {{-Dsynchrony.memory.max=2g}} in each Confluence node.  See [How to Increase Synchrony Heap Allocation|https://confluence.<USER>confkb/how-to-increase-synchrony-heap-allocation-936516650.html] and [Setting properties and options on startup|https://confluence.<USER>adminjiraserver/setting-properties-and-options-on-startup-938847831.html] for further information."""

summary: "aui-spinner element is offset from Global Quick Search elements"
description: """There is an element that Confluence uses called {{aui-<USER>} that governs a loading animated icon when typing in Global Quick search. summary: "Move Team Calendars expensive tasks to the Sandbox Framework"
description: """h3. Problem Definition  Some Team Calendars operations are resource expensive and can interfere on other regular Confluence tasks.  This can lead to users experiencing overall slow downs on Confluence, which could be caused by punctual tasks.    h3. Suggested Solution  With the introduction of the [sandbox framework|https://community.<USER>t5/Confluence-discussions/Sandbox-Framework-for-Confluence-Server/td-p/1076306] we have now a way to move expensive processes to a separated JVM so that they don't interfere directly on Confluence performance.  For example, if a calendar export requires too much of the heap, it won't need to conflict with other regular Confluence operations for resources.    This suggestion is opened so that we can start to design Team Calendars on a way it can take advantage of the sandbox framework in any expensive task, making Confluence and TC more reliable and stable.     h3. Workaround   _None so far_"""

summary: "Confluence Datacenter instances throwing tons of Hazelcast exceptions : ConcurrentModificationException"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    When an app vendor did some load tests on their end, they encounter lots of {{ConcurrentModificationException}} exceptions  h3. Environment  Total Spaces 5019  Site Spaces 5019  Personal Spaces 0  Content (All Versions) 4068499  Content (Current Versions) 3966151  Local Users 10524  Local Groups 1008    Java VM Memory Statistics  Heap Memory   Used Memory (61 %)  Free Memory (39 %)  39 % Free  Maximum Heap Size 12288 MB  Total Allocated Memory 12288 MB  Used Memory 7517 MB  Free Allocated Memory 4770 MB  Total Free Memory 4770 MB    h3. Expected Results  No or less hazelcast exceptions    h3. Actual Results  Lots of hazelcast exxceptions    The below exception is thrown in the log file:   [^atlassian-confluence.log.zip]     h3. Notes  The root cause is these two methods:  {{com.atlassian.confluence.extra.userlister.DefaultUserListManager#registerLoggedInUser}}  {{com.atlassian.confluence.extra.userlister.DefaultUserListManager#unregisterLoggedInUser}}    When the system is under the stress test, these two methods are called frequently and concurrently. Both of them call {{com.atlassian.confluence.extra.userlister.DefaultUserListManager#cacheLoggedInUser}}, which retrieves the HashMap of user's sessions from the cache and modifies it. Once there are more than one threads that attempt to modify the HashMap, while Hazelcast is iterating over it (in order to serialise the HashMap), then we have the {{java.util.ConcurrentModificationException.}}    h3. Workaround    Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "The ""Jira Links"" section should be more proactive in creating RemoteLinks based on Jira issue macros in the page."
description: """h3. Problem Definition  When a Jira issue macro is inserted into a page, Confluence will attempt to contact that Jira instance and link the page to the issue. If for some reason the link attempt fails, then Confluence will not link the issue to the page, and the number of issue links will be invalid.    h3.Example  A customer may use something like ScriptRunner to create Jira issues in a Post Function, and then link them to a Confluence page. In cases where Jira hasn't finished reindexing an issue, Jira will return a 404 not found error for that issue, and the remoteLink will not be created.    h3. Suggested Solution  Confluence should check if the number of Jira links on the page matches the number returned by Jira's {{remote-link-aggregation}} endpoint (which is how Confluence determines the number of links on the page). If there is a mismatch, Confluence should attempt to recreate the links (although it would have to be done carefully to avoid inadvertently overwhelming Jira).     h3. Workaround  In cases involving automation, it's possible to manually create the missing remote links, either by a REST or Java API.    For normal cases where Confluence could not reach Jira at the time of saving the page, try removing the Jira issue macro, saving the page, and then re-adding the macro again."""

summary: "Add bulk option to stop watching pages"

You must go to viewnotifications.action then individually click each """"Stop Watching"""" link.    There should be an option to do this in bulk. Adding check boxes and clicking a button or doing something from your profile to say stop watching all pages in space X are viable options.     At the very least, when hitting viewnotifcations.acton adding a hidden query parameter like """"limit=X"""" would be more helpful so you can see more entries per page."""

summary: "When saving a page, Confluence removes text coloring on links to Confluence pages."
description: """{panel:title=Atlassian Update - 29 July 2019|borderStyle=solid|borderColor=#3C78B5|titleBGColor=#3C78B5|titleColor=#FFFFFF|bgColor=#E7F4FA}  After some analysis we've found that this issue has been duplicated by the issue at [CONFSERVER-57949 - Changing the color of links in Confluence pages does not reflect on the links once the page is published| https://jira.<USER>browse/CONFSERVER-57949]. This is a current and verified bug in Confluence, please watch that issue for further updates and more detailed workarounds.  {panel}    h3. Issue Summary  When saving a page, Confluence removes text coloring on links to Confluence pages.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Open the editor  # Insert a link to another page  # Highlight the link  # In the text color picker in the editor toolbar, change the color of the link  # Click *Update* to save the page    h3. Re-opening the editor also shows the text as default link color.    h3. Notes  * Can reproduce in Confluence 6.15.4, but not in 6.8.0  * Does not affect links to external URLs    h3. Workaround  Currently, there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""
comments: " Dear Customers,        After comparing this bug request against the known bug report  CONFSERVER-57949 - Changing the color of links in Confluence pages does not reflect on the links once the page is published we realized that both are addressing the same scenario. Since we consider CONFSERVER-57949 the main point of entry for this scenario, we will be closing this request now and will ask you to cast your votes in the original request so we can measure the impact and interest more accurately.    Best regards.       Monique | Atlassian <USER>

summary: "Attachments that includes apostrophe will be shown as Unknown Attachment after migrating from MySQL to Oracle using XML backup"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  When performing migrating from one Confluence instance to another using XML backup and where the source Confluence is running MySQL and the destination Confluence is running Oracle database, attachments that includes apostrophe will not render in the destination Confluence and instead an """"Unknown Attachment"""" will be displayed in pages where the attachment should be displayed.     The issue seems to only happen on pages, as these attachments can be seen, previewed and download through the *Attachments List* page.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Setup a Confluence instance that is using MySQL  # Include Attachments with titles that include apostrophe for example {{Capture d'écran.png}}  # Add these attachments into Confluence pages, via drag and drop or through *Insert files and Images*  # Perform a site XML backup.   # Setup a Confluence instance that is using Oracle  # Restore site backup from the first Confluence backup.   # Head over to the pages where the above attachments is suppose to be displayed      h3. Expected Results  The attachments will be shown I these pages.     h3. Actual Results  The areas of the page were the attachment is added is now displaying {{Unknown Attachment}} error.     h3. Notes  As the issue seems to only occur in pages but not in the Attachment List, it seems that it is caused by « ' » in image name were wrongly replaced with HTML escape . The escape sequence for apostrophes with Oracle is a double apostrophe.     h3. Workaround  Manually replace single apostrophes in the BodyContent table with double apostrophe    """

summary: "Jira's custom date field doesn't appear when creating TC event if the custom field context is restricted to a specific issue type"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Confluence allows you to insert Jira issue dates (using default or custom fields) into the Team Calendar by creating an event.    However, if your custom field is configured so that the issue types associated with the default configuration of the custom field is _not_ to all issue types available, then the custom field won't appear in the TC """"Create event"""" dialog.    (!) This only happens if the JQL/filter you use selects more than one project. If you select the projects individually, it works just fine.  h3. Environment   * Confluence 6.6.13, 6.14.0   * Jira 7.6.7, 7.7.2, 8.2.1   * Team Calendar 6.0.27    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create an applink between Jira and Confluence.   # In Jira, create two projects.   # Create a custom date field, then associate it with all screens in the two projects.   # Configure the custom field so that the applicable issue types are just set to one of them (i.e. """"Task"""").   ** !Custom Field Config - Doesn't work.png|thumbnail!   # Save the config and create a sample issue (type """"Task"""") in Jira.   # In Confluence, create a simple Jira Issue Macro on a page to enable OAuth for your user.   # Create a calendar and try to create an event of type """"Jira Issue Dates"""".   # Select a single project and you can see the custom field """"Release Date"""" appear.   ** !Single Project A - Works.png|thumbnail!   ** !Single Project B - Works.png|thumbnail!   # Now insert a JQL so that it includes two projects.      h3. Expected Results    The custom field """"Release Date"""" should appear as well.  h3. Actual Results    The custom field """"Release Date"""" doesn't appear when the JQL includes two projects. If you go back to just one project, it works fine.    !Two Projects A and B - Doesn't work.png|thumbnail!  h3. Note    Confluence makes a REST call to obtain the date fields to display in the dialog. For example:   * {{[http://localhost:8093/conf614/rest/calendar-services/1.0/calendar/jira/c8294f2e-923d-34cf-a218-858f95e7e0d0/date-fields/jql.<USER>query=project+in+(%22Project+One%22%2C+%22Project+Two%22])}}    The API results don't include the custom field if two projects are in the JQL.    If I only choose a single project _or_ perform the workaround below (with two projects), the custom field is returned.    h3. Workaround   # Choose only one project in the TC event you're creating.   # Change the applicable issue types for the custom field's """"Default Configuration Scheme"""" so that it's set to """"All issue types"""" and not specific ones.   !Custom Field Config - Works.png|thumbnail!"""
comments: "It also occurs if the context is restricted to projects. (Team Calendar: 6.0.49; Jira: 8.5.3; Confluence: 6.13.10)" [SEP] "A ticket has been raised in Jira Server for this issue. Please see https://jira.<USER>browse/JRASERVER-71485."

summary: "Imporve ""Jira Issue Macro"" with 'mentioned in' link"
description: """Currently, the """"Jira Issue Macro"""" has to mode :   * Single Issue   * Filter Issue    Only the 'Single Issue' generates a 'mentioned in' link in the concerned Issue.    In 'Filter Issue' mode, no link is generated. It is comprehensible since the returned issues can change depending on the date or the remote user.    Since 'Single Issue' is considered like the filter """"key = UDDTC-411"""", a possible improvement could be to allow the """"Mentioned in"""" link event for simple Issue Filter, with only key or issueKey as criteria with operators """"="""" and """"in"""".    Also a filter issueKey in (FOO-100, FOO-101) will generate the ''Mentioned in"""" link in both Issue.         Vincent """

summary: "Possibility to change space icons' shape"
description: """h3. Problem Definition      * Confluence 6.5 showed the space icons, under the *My Spaces* section in the left sidebar, as circular shapes. In Confluence 6.15, these same icons are now showing up in square shapes. This change affected customer who had the icons designed to fit circular shapes.    h3. Suggested Solution    * Provide an option that allows users to select in which shape they would like to display the space icons.    h3. Why this is important       * In Confluence instances that have many spaces, changing their icons to match the now square shapes can be quite demanding since this isn't something possible to perform in bulk.    h3. Workaround     * N/A"""
comments: "Having the ability to switch back to the circular space icons of the Confluence 6.5 era would be great. We use Confluence spaces at our R&D company to capture the knowledge and files for each of our projects, one space per project, and to represent these projects across the company, particularly to senior management. We had designed some nice logos for each project, which fit into the circular layout. Unfortunately, the new square layout crops them slightly and doesn't really visually match what we had originally designed. Obviously, everything functionally still works great, but having a really nice presentation was great for us too. In addition, it also maintains the same visual look across our related Jira and Bitbucket project instances. It would be really nice if you could provide an option to return to the circular layout."

summary: "Changing the PostgreSQL database collation break the Include Macro if the Page Title to include contain Russian Alphabet"
description: """h3. Step to Replicate  # Ensure that you have a running Confluence 6.15.2 instance with PostgreSQL database using 'C' as the collation.  # Create a page with title *Центр Консалтинга*  # Create another page with title *Серверная инфраструктур*  # Include the furst page *Центр Консалтинга* into the second page *Серверная инфраструктур* using the *Include Page* macro.  # Generate the XML site backup for this Confluence instance.  # Setup Confluence 6.15.2 instance with PostgreSQL database using 'UTF-8' or any other as the collation.  # Import the XML site backup into the new instance.  # Check out the second page *Серверная инфраструктур*.    h3. Environment  Confluence 6.15.2  PostgreSQL Database    h3. Expected Results  The *Include Page* macro in the second page *Серверная инфраструктур* works correctly.    h3. Actual Results  *Unable to render {include}* appear in the second page *Серверная инфраструктур*    h3. Notes  Customer who perform the steps in *Method 1* from [How to fix the collation of a Postgres Confluence database|https://confluence.<USER>confkb/how-to-fix-the-collation-of-a-postgres-confluence-database-966676071.html] to fix the database collation will be affected by this bug.    h3. Workaround  Updating the page title for the page to include resolve the issue. For example, update the title *Центр Консалтинга* to *Центр* and back to *Центр Консалтинга*.  *OR*  To simply add an arbitrary update to the affected page to create a new version of the page."""

Steps to Reproduce  move a page tree (parent and its children) from a regular space to a personal space (that isn't my own).      h3. Expected Results  The error message should state that there are specific page title conflicts similar to...   !error2.png|thumbnail!       h3. Actual Results  vague $error message. `$error` messages show when moving the parent page and there are title conflicts, but the error message shown does not say that directly   !error1.png|thumbnail!     h3. Notes    There are two ways of getting the list of pages that already exist with the same title on the destination space:    # Use the Copy Page Hierarchy instead of Move page,  # Or, get a [HAR file|https://confluence.<USER>kb/generating-har-files-and-analyzing-web-requests-720420612.html] while hitting the Move button and inspect the *movepage.action* request, the Response Content will contain the list of duplicate page titles"""
comments: "Originally reported via https://community.<USER>t5/Confluence-questions/quot-error-quot-codes-show-in-the-Move-Page-dialog-when-trying/qaq-p/1092949#U1093180    I'm getting some unusual behaviour when trying to move a page tree (parent and its children) from a regular space to a personal space (that isn't my own).    I'm getting these `$error` messages showing when I try to move the parent page, and I can't work out why this would be happening.    I know I have move permissions between the source space and the destination space because I moved items to it moments before encountering this error.    I have logged out and back in again, but the problem persists.     My Confluence version is Server 6.15.4   !error1.png|thumbnail!     -------    I've worked it out through trial and `$error`. :P    It seems that the Move Page modal handles page title conflicts really terribly from a UX perspective.    I tried to copy the pages from the source space to the personal space and got this much better error message.    !

summary: "Incorrect number of Known Issues in Log Scanner Results Email"
description: """ h3. Issue Summary    When using the Periodic Scan function in Troubleshooting and Support Tools > Log Scanner, the results e-mail sent at the designated time has an incorrect number.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Go to Troubleshooting and Support Tools  # Click on the Log Scanner tab  # Run a normal scan and take note of the amount of results.  # Choose Periodic Scan and specify the start time, frequency and recipients to send the scan results to.   # Check email, and then check the total.  This is inconsistent with the actual amount of issues listed in the email, as well as the regular scanner.      h3. Expected Results  The number is accurate and reflects the amount of issues displayed.    h3. Actual Results  The number is inaccurate in the e-mail.  In two tests, a scan with 4 results said 5, and a scan with 11 results (pictured) says """"Found 13 known problems in the log file..."""".     !Screen Shot 2019-05-29 at 10.44.19.png|thumbnail!    h3. Workaround     Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""
comments: "Tracked as https://ecosystem.atlassian.net/browse/ATST-952"

summary: "Confluence Import Word functionality does not fire Page Created Event"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    When importing a word document from the *...* menu and the word document does not contain any images {{PageCreateEvent}} is received by apps. However, if the word document contains images only {{AttachmentEvents}} and {{PageUpdateEvent}} are received by apps instead of the {{PageCreateEvent}}    h3. Environment    App developer tested on Confluence 6.15.2 but their internal bug report results from this issue 2 years old  h3. Steps to Reproduce    # Create two pages, one with image attachments only, one without  # Import the pages to Word  # Notice that {{PageCreateEvent}} is fired by doc without images and {{AttachmentEvents}} and {{PageUpdateEvent}} from doc with images      h3. Expected Results  Expect to get a PageCreateEvent when a page is created from a word import    h3. Actual Results  Get a PageUpdateEvent when the imported word document contains images.  h3. Notes    h3. Workaround    Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Sorting of the pages should honor default language set in Confluence Administration."
description: """If the default language in Confluence is set to for eg. Swedish, then sorting of the pages needs to be as per Swedish alphabet, and not as per English."""

summary: "Companion App certificate has expired"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    The certificate for the Companion App desktop applications has expired as of April 23rd 2019.   h3. Environment    N/A  h3. Steps to Reproduce    N/A  h3. Actual Results  h3. Notes    (Optional - If Necessary)  h3. Workaround    No workaround. """
comments: "Expected date for this fix?" [SEP] "This is critical - we have business users depending on this - please advise" [SEP] "A fix for this issue is available in Companion 0.6.1. View the [Release Notes|https://confluence.<USER>display/DOC/Atlassian+Companion+app+release+notes] to see what other issues are resolved.    If Companion is installed via the MSI, then it will need to be manually updated to the [latest version|https://update-nucleus.<USER>Atlassian-Companion/291cb34fe2296e5fb82b83a04704c9b4/latest/win32/ia32/Atlassian%20Companion.msi?_ga=2.261166863.323564345.1563926916-1578275846.1563926916]."

summary: "Possible modification needed for ""Watching a Space"" article"
description: """According to [Watch a Calendar|https://confluence.<USER>teamcal/watch-a-calendar-247070749.html], watching a Confluence space includes watching all calendars in that space.     However, watching a space that contains calendars *doesn't* trigger notifications when calendar events are added, edited, or deleted, as the article mentions. There's an existing bug for this issue: https://jira.<USER>browse/CONFSERVER-57698.    """
comments: "I am watching a space, but it does not send notification when a new version of a file is uploaded. I am depending on this notification to track completion on documents across many teams. It's important. I don't know of any other way to get this information easily. The workaround is to open the file and look at it to see if it's been modified. Very inefficient and tedious. Please fix.  "

summary: "Release Team Calendars with minified versions of JS and CSS files"
description: """h3. Problem Definition  Minification definition from [Wikipedia|https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Minification_(programming)]:  {quote}  Minification (also minimisation or minimization), is the process of removing all unnecessary characters from the source codes of interpreted programming languages or markup languages without changing their functionality. These unnecessary characters usually include white space characters, new line characters, comments, and sometimes block delimiters, which are used to add readability to the code but are not required for it to execute. Minification reduces the size of the source code, making its transmission over a network (e.g. the Internet) more efficient.  {quote}    Team Calendars (TC) does not minify its JavaScript (JS) files.  Checking a Batch JS file transferred when displaying a sample page, uncompressed Team Calendars JS content has a size of almost 2MB.  Using public available JS minification services, the size of the file was cut in half.    Since this occurs on page view, which encompasses the majority of Confluence usage, transferring optimised JS files can lead to faster page loading times, specially on large instances.    h3. Suggested Solution  Ship Team Calendars {{.obr}} with minified version of JS and CSS files.    h3. Workaround   _No workaround available so far._"""

summary: "New Expanded Search result order should consider 'Page title' with highest priority"
description: """h4. Problem Statement  The 'New expanded search' result is not prioritizing the *Page title* as it used to in previous 'Quick Search'.    h4. How 'Old Quick Search' gives results:  This search gives a result based on the following:  * title + boosting by recency + space preference    h4. How 'New Expanded Search' gives results:  This search gives a result based on the following:  * title (less sophisticated) + content body (number of matches of the word in the content) + boosting by recency    h4. Improvement  * Page title should still be the priority instead of less sophisticated, followed by the content body matches and recency."""
comments: "We recently upgraded to Confluence 6.15.2. Confluence QuickSearch was changed to re-prioritize search results.    Previously QuickSearch ranked page titles and headings highly.    Now your search text could be identical to a page title (or a heading) and the result does not appear on the first page.    Some examples attached.    [https://help.aroflo.com/download/attachments/64785001/LayoutsConfSearchResult.png?api=v2]    [https://help.aroflo.com/download/attachments/64785001/GPSConfSearchResult.png?api=v2]     "

summary: "Creating or deleting page comments in pages with several watchers might be slow"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  Depending on the number of watchers in a page (space watchers as well), creating or deleting page comments can take several seconds.  This happens because Confluence is processing notifications in the same thread as the one that triggered the action in the UI.  This might create an awful UI experience to the Confluence user, giving the impression the operation is not being executed.  The situation may worsen depending on user directory complexity, such as when nested groups are enabled and heavily used to configure space permissions and page restrictions.    The same occurs when adding or removing inline comments.    h3. Environment  This issue was validated on a vanilla Confluence Server on version *6.13.3*.      h3. Steps to Reproduce  h5. Creating a Page Comment  # Install a Confluence vanilla instance.  # Create 2,000 regular users in the internal directory that are part of _confluence-users_ group.   #* The following shell script may help automating that task.    # As the Confluence administrator, create a sample Blank Space -- let's call it _Test Space_.  # In _Test Space_ create a sample Blank Page -- let's call it _Page with 2000 watchers_.  # Add the 2,000 regular users as watchers of _Page with 2000 watchers_.   #* The following shell script may help automating this task.    # Log in to Confluence UI as a regular user and access _Page with 2000 watchers_.  # Write a Page Comment and click on _Save_.    h5. Expected Results  The comment is created almost instantly and the UI is released to the user to continue its work on Confluence.    h5. Actual Results  After clicking on _Save_, the operation takes a long time, giving to the user a spinning wheel in the comment editor.  !create_comment_img001.png|thumbnail!  Eventually, the operation completes and the user perceives that by seeing the comment in the Page.  !create_comment_img002.png|thumbnail!    Checking the HAR file generated while reproducing this use case shows a {{POST}} request to {{<Confluence Base URL>/rest/tinymce/1/content/{{pageID}}/comment?actions=true}} takes too long.    If Page Profiling is enabled, then a similar entry to the following is logged in {{atlassian-confluence.log}}, but we don't have information on what is causing the long time to complete it:      If thread dumps are taken while the action is being processed, then a stack trace similar to the one below can be found:      h5. Deleting a Page Comment  # Continue from the last step in the _Creating a Page Comment_ Section.  # Logged as a regular user, click on the _Delete_ button related to the recently added comment.  # Click on the _OK_ button in the pop-up with the confirmation question.    h5. Expected Results  The comment is deleted almost instantly and the user gets an obvious confirmation in the UI with the comment removed from the page.    h5. Actual Results  Clicking on the _Delete_ button triggers the delete action, but nothing changes in the UI so it gives the wrong impression to the user.  Refreshing the page shows the comment is still there what may trick the user on clicking the _Delete_ button again.    If a HAR file is generated while reproducing the problem, then a {{DELETE}} request to {{<Confluence Base URL>/rest/api/content/<comment ID>}} takes a long time to complete.  In the _backend_ side the delete operation is on-going, but it takes a long time to complete.    If Page Profiling is enabled, then a similar entry to the following is logged in {{atlassian-confluence.log}}, but we don't have information on what is causing the long time to complete it:      If thread dumps are taken while the operation is still running in the Confluence server, then a stack trace similar to the one below is found:      h3. Notes  The 2,000 watchers might be an exaggerated scenario, but it is used to show that something is wrong and is causing a bad UI experience to the Confluence user.  This issue is caused because Confluence is processing notifications within the same thread as the one executing UI operations.  Adding and deleting a comment to a page should be decoupled from the notifications tasks, meaning that any task related to notifications should be processed by a background thread.    Below is the result of a comparison on how long it takes to complete each action depending on the number of watchers in a page.  ||{{#}} of watchers||Create comment||Delete comment||  |0|254ms|1,309ms|  |100|1,230ms|4,301ms|  |500|6,866ms|18,552ms|  |1,000|14,216ms|36,892ms|  |2,000|52,487ms|78,998ms|    h3. Workaround  [Tuning the cache|https://confluence.<USER>doc/cache-performance-tuning-169119133.html] may help decreasing the time taken on each related operation, but won't remove the problem."""

summary: "Incremental user directory syncs from an AD connector removes users from Confluence groups"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    When an AD connector in Confluence incrementally syncs user directories it can remove users from their groups in Confluence. A full sync restores these users.   h3. Environment    Tested in Confluence 6.x that has embedded Crowd 2.10, but all Confluence versions are affected  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create an AD connector and set it to do incremental syncs with Active Directory   # During an incremental sync, the embedded Crowd within Confluence will check for all of recently changed groups.    h3. Expected Results    Any user added to a group in AD that is set to sync with Confluence would be added to the corresponding group in Confluence.  h3. Actual Results    Any user in the group that was not modified is removed from Confluence and only the modified users are added.     The below is an example INFO message of this from the atlassian-confluence.log file:    h3. Notes    During the incremental sync, for changed AD groups it will fetch their memberships. Unfortunately the embedded Crowd within Confluence will only ask for members who also were recently changed and then during the incremental sync, make them the only users existing in the local Confluence group. The embedded Crowd in Confluence will ignore all other users who were not a part in this synchronization as they were not changed, resulting in them being removed during the sync.   h3. Workaround    Set the AD connector in Confluence to do FULL syncs only by un-checking the """"Enable Incremental Synchronization"""" option in the Advanced section of the AD connector's settings. """

summary: "Copying and then pasting image from already existing image on the page will result in broken image in edit mode."
description: """h3. Issue Summary  Copying and then pasting an image from the already existing image on the page will result in a broken image in edit mode when using https.    h3. Environment  Confluence 6.15.4 running over HTTPS connection    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Upload an image to an empty page   # Copy the image   # Paste the same image to the same page    h3. Expected Results  The image will be displayed correctly.    h3. Actual Results  Image is not displayed at all, and in the DOM of the page, we can see that new image uses incorrect URL (src of the image) to the image as it uses HTTP instead of HTTPS.   !Screen Shot 2019-05-23 at 12.40.13 PM.png|thumbnail!     h3. Notes  The issue is not observable if the Confluence instance uses HTTP instead of HTTPS connection.  The issue is also visible with reverse proxy.    h3. Workaround  If instance uses a reverse proxy, make a rule to forward HTTP connection to https. That way the image will be resolved.  eg for Apache:  """
comments: "This is very annoying bug and it didn't happen when we were using Confluence 6.9    After updating to 7.1.0 this issue started to happen.    We are using Nginx proxy and tried workaround(similar with above instruction but in Nginx configuration) but issue still exists.    Please fix this bug." [SEP] "Hello [~<USER>,    If we've configured a permanent HTTP to HTTPS redirect on the NGINX side, we should not be hitting into the issue as the URL of the image should already be in HTTPS.  * [Redirect HTTP to HTTPS in Nginx|https://serversforhackers.com/c/redirect-http-to-https-nginx]    If the issue persist even after the redirect above, could you reach out to our support team using the link below, and we'll get back to you on there.  * [Contact Support|https://support.<USER>contact/]    Cheers,  Sattesh" [SEP] "Hi Sattesh,    I've already raise a ticket to support team and reviewed by senior team as well.    Ended up tracking this bug report and wait until it gets fixed."

summary: "Adding an mp4 video to an answer in Questions for Confluence returns Errors found in contents of the answer"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    User is unable to add an .mp4 video to an answer in Confluence Questions, when dragging and dropping the file into an answer, the Multimedia macro is created, however when trying to save the answer, the message """"Errors found in contents of the answer."""" is shown.  h3. Environment   * Confluence 6.15.2   * Questions for Confluence 2.6.15    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a new question and save it.   # Add an answer and drag and drop any .mp4 file into the answer.   # Notice that a Multimedia macro appears.   # Try to save the answer.    h3. Expected Results    The answer would be saved with the multimedia macro.  h3. Actual Results    (!) The answer isn't saved and the message """"Errors found in contents of the answer."""" shows up below the answer text box.   !Screen Shot 2019-05-06 at 17.58.45.png|thumbnail!  h3. Workaround   # On the menu bar for the answer's text box, click on the *Insert link* button.   !Screen Shot 2019-05-06 at 18.10.32.png|thumbnail!   # The *Insert link* window will be displayed, select the option *Files* on the left menu and upload the .mp4 file from there.   !Screen Shot 2019-05-06 at 18.10.41.png|thumbnail!   # Insert the file and save the answer. The video will be displayed in the answer as a link:   !Screen Shot 2019-05-22 at 18.15.50.png|thumbnail!"""

summary: "Confluence Search Filter Preset"
description: """h6. PROBLEM STATEMENT  Confluence allow users to set the search filter while searching the content however, users may need to repeat the filter everytime they search and new users who is not aware of the search filter will get a huge search result without filtering the search.    h6. SUGGESTION  It would be great if Confluence Administrator could add a custom search filter as a Search Preset to limit the search result when users search for a general keyword in Confluence Search.    h6. NOTES  Confluence user should be able to modify the following:  # Enable or disable the default search filter preset.  # a query text definition (to realize more complex presets – analog the advanced search)  #* Users should be able to disable the preset in their profile (enabled should be the default)  #* Users may be able to define their own preset (as an alternative to the admin default setting) in their profile."""

summary: "Jira Issues Calendar Gadget not working in Confluence"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    After updating from Jira 7.13.1 to 8.0.2 the """"Jira Issues Calendar"""" gadget stopped working on Confluence side.    Updated the Calendar plugin and the iCal plugin to 5.0.0 and 1.5.0 but the issue still remains.    The gadget does work in Jira but not in Confluence.   In Jira, the *__MODULE_ID__* seems to get replaced by a number.   This does not happen when the gadget is added to a Confluence page.  h3. Expected Results    The gadget should work as expected.  h3. Actual Results    In the JS console:    h3. Workaround    Note that this was only tested locally by the developer checking this error (no guarantees)    As a workaround I'd recommend to remove old gadgets jars and replace them with 4.3.7 version (I've tested it locally and it worked, but no guarantees here as it was quick and brutal test).   * stop confluence   * go to atlassian-confluence-6.15.7/confluence/WEB-INF/atlassian-bundled-plugins   * remove gadgets related jars   atlassian-gadgets-api-4.3.2.1.jar   atlassian-gadgets-directory-plugin-4.3.2.1.jar   atlassian-gadgets-embedded-plugin-4.3.2.1.jar   atlassian-gadgets-oauth-service-provider-plugin-4.3.2.1.jar   atlassian-gadgets-opensocial-plugin-4.3.2.1.jar   atlassian-gadgets-publisher-plugin-4.3.2.1.jar   atlassian-gadgets-shared-4.3.2.1.jar   atlassian-gadgets-spi-4.3.2.1.jar   * copy updated jars   atlassian-gadgets-api-4.3.7.jar   atlassian-gadgets-directory-plugin-4.3.7.jar   atlassian-gadgets-embedded-plugin-4.3.7.jar   atlassian-gadgets-oauth-service-provider-plugin-4.3.7.jar   atlassian-gadgets-opensocial-plugin-4.3.7.jar   atlassian-gadgets-publisher-plugin-4.3.7.jar   atlassian-gadgets-shared-4.3.7.jar   atlassian-gadgets-spi-4.3.7.jar   * start confluence, see if all the plugins are running and if calendar gadget is working correctly    Steps to reproduce:   * Start Jira 8.3.1 (or later) and install Jira Issues Calendar latest version: [https://marketplace.<USER>apps/293/jira-calendar-plugin?hosting=server&tab=support]   * Restart Jira server   * Create a project, add tickets and input """"DUE DATE"""" for Jira ticket   * Copy the Jira Issues Calendar gadget spec: XML file    * Start confluence 6.15.9 (latest 6.15.x)   * Go to confluence admin -> external gadget: add url Jira Issues Calendar gadget spec above   * Create application link Confluence < - > Jira   * Create a page: In editor to insert gadget (search for Jira Issue Calendar), and Input Project Name into the filter   * Saving the gadget config, then insert the gadget to the page   * Go to view page and see result (open browser console)   * Upgrade confluence to 7.0.1 to see the page again."""

summary: "Add custom filter in the quick search"
description: """h3. Summary    It would be great if there are additional custom filters for Confluence searches to let customers filter content or filters from apps in (i.e. filter by 'language' and 'version' ):     !Snip20190520_7.png|thumbnail!     h3. Background    From an app developer/vendor:  {quote}  We would like to add custom filters for Confluence searches to let customers filter content by for example version (Scroll Versions) or language (Scroll Translations).    We have been able to create filters that users can use with the 'advanced search' view (or when directly using cql). However we have not found a (supported) way to add extra filters to the new 'quick search' available starting from Confluence 6.15.    Similarly, with Scroll Versions it is possible to have multiple versions of the content of a Confluence space, and we would want to provide a possibility to limit the search to given version.    In the advanced search, apps can add custom integrations like 'language' or 'version' (see the attached screenshot), using https://developer.<USER>server/confluence/adding-a-field-to-cql/     !advancedSearchIntegration.png|thumbnail!     But these filters are not available in the quick search - and more """"advanced"""" filters probably should not be there to keep the UI easy to use. But 'language' and 'version' are important for users of the corresponding apps.    Additionally, in the new quick search experience, all the content types are visible by default. But we would actually prefer an option to choose that some of custom content types that our apps create are by default filtered out from the quick search results (while still being searchable when users want to) - this is because some of these custom content items can be confusing for a non-power user.  {quote}    h3. Impact    * _com.k15t.scroll.scroll-versions_ has *1,385* total app installations in the market"""
