summary: "As an administrator I would like to configure HTTP2 support in Confluence's Tomcat level"
description: """h3. Problem Definition  [HTTP/2|https://developers.google.com/web/fundamentals/performance/http2/] is designed to address several limitations found in HTTP/1.1.  Although Tomcat 9.X (and 8.5.X) supports HTTP/2, it can only be enabled with [Apache Portable Runtime (APR)|http://tomcat.apache.org/native-doc/], which is not natively compiled with bundled Tomcat in Confluence.    h3. Suggested Solution  Bundle Confluence with the necessary artifacts to enable HTTP/2 out-of-the-box and provide the necessary documentation to configure this.  Also, Confluence should be fully tested to work seamlessly with HTTP/2, as well as make use of some functionalities such as _push promise_ to improve application's performance.    h3. Why this is important  Full benefits of HTTP/2 can only be achieved if it is enabled all the way through the network, although you can get most of it by terminating HTTP/2 on a web server.  Also, it would be important to have HTTP/2 working when there's no web server in front of Confluence.    h3. Workaround   The supported workaround is to terminate SSL in a web server, such as NGINX and Apache, and configure it to accept HTTP2 connections."""

summary: "Hover image cursor has changed from a pointer (hand) to a move (4 way arrow) in TinyMCE 4"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    The cursor for the image hover in TinyMCE 4 has changed from a pointer 'hand' to a move '4 way arrow' which can be quite confusing when using linked images for navigation as the user is not aware it's actually a clickable link.  h3. Environment   * Confluence 6.14.0 and above   * TinyMCE 4 in use    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Add an image to a page   # Attach link to image & publish   # Hover your mouse over the image   # Notice the cursor is the 'move' cursor    h3. Expected Results   * Any link always displays the standard 'pointer' cursor or hand, indicating it's a link      h3. Actual Results   * All images now show the 'move' cursor, even when a link is included    h3. Notes   * Whilst this update to Tiny MCE 4 affects all images, it's really the links where this is most problematic   * The CSS that is causing this is:      * Previously it referenced, cursor: pointer;    h3. Workaround    Disable TinyMCE 4 editor and rollback to v3:   # Vist http://<conf-URL>/admin/darkfeatures.action   # Add *frontend.editor.v4.disable* to the list at the bottom    OR    Add this to the admin Stylesheet  {code:css}  .wiki-content img.confluence-embedded-image, .wiki-content img.editor-inline-macro, .wiki-content table.wysiwyg-macro {   cursor: pointer !important;  }  {code}"""

summary: "Exporting a Space or a Page to PDF fails on Confluence Data Center on Windows if the page title has a ':' character"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  On Confluence Data Center installed in a Windows server, when sandbox process is enabled to process PDF exports, exporting a page to PDF will fail if the title of the page contains a colon ({{:}}) character.  PDF export of a Space will fail as well if one of the pages has a {{:}} in its title.    h3. Environment  * Confluence Data Center installed on Windows.  * Sandbox process enabled to process PDF export.  * This was validated on Confluence DC *6.13.4* and *6.15.4*.      h3. Steps to Reproduce  h4. Page Export to PDF  # Install a vanilla instance of Confluence Data Center in a Windows server.  # Create a page with title """"Regular: page"""".  # On the _page view mode_, click on *...* > *Export to PDF*.    h5. Expected Results  PDF export of the page is successfully created and the user receives the browser pop-up to save the PDF file.    h5. Actual Results  A system error occurs an error page with a stack trace in the UI.   !page_export_img001.png|thumbnail!   Error excerpt with stack trace:      h4. Space Export to PDF  # Install a vanilla instance of Confluence Data Center in a Windows server.  # Create a sample Space.  # Create a page within this Space with title """"Regular: page"""".  # On the _page view mode_ click on *Space Tools* > *Content Tools* > *Export*.  # Select PDF export and click on _Next_.  # Select _Normal Export_ and click on _Export_.    h5. Expected Results  PDF export of the Space is successfully created and the user can click on a link in the UI to download the PDF file.    h5. Actual Results  Space export fails with message {{Failed to create a location and file for the PDF export}}.   !space_export_img001.png|thumbnail!   An error is logged in {{atlassian-confluence.log}} similar to the one below.      h3. Notes  This does not affect Confluence Server in Windows nor Confluence Data Center on Linux.  This is a regression of bug [CONFSERVER-24389|https://jira.<USER>browse/CONFSERVER-24389] -- _Export to PDF on Windows returns a stack trace if the name of the page has a ':' symbol_.    h3. Workaround  There are two options to circumvent this problem:  # Advise users to remove the ':' character from pages' title.  # Disable PDF exports to be handled by the sandbox process by adding the following JVM argument and restart Confluence:     #* Refer to [Configuring System Properties|https://confluence.<USER>doc/configuring-system-properties-168002854.html] on how to add this as a parameter in your instance."""
comments: "Seeing this issue in Confluence Data Center v6.15.10 also"

summary: "Display Only Changed Content in Confluence Emails"
description: """*Goal:* Have Confluence emails show only changed content    *Background:* Our customers receive multiple emails for updates to Confluence pages.    These emails often include the entire page content along with the changes. This becomes a pain point when getting an update on a task list and having to scroll down through the entire email to see the changes since the entire task list is included. """

summary: "Archived spaces are not processed by the scheduled Repair the Ancestors Table job"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    The *Repair the Ancestor* scheduled job do not process archived spaces. It does archive current spaces only. There is no confirmation whether this behavior is an expected/intended behavior. If it is an intended behavior, it would be best that the the following document is updated to include this piece of information:  * [Rebuilding the Ancestor Table|https://confluence.<USER>doc/rebuilding-the-ancestor-table-153948.html]    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Ensure that your Confluence instance have multiple spaces. For this example we have 4 spaces.  # In one of those spaces, head over to *Space Tools* > *Overview* > *Edit Space Details*  # Change the status to archived.  # Click Save  # Head over to *General Configuration* > *Scheduled Jobs* > *Repair the Ancestors Table* > *Run*    h3. Expected Results  The job will run and it will process all spaces and the following will be seen in the logs.    h3. Actual Results  The job will run and it will process only current spaces and the following will be seen in the logs.      h3. Workaround  If you wish to repair the ancestor table for archive spaces, first you will need to change its status to *current* , run the Repair ancestor job. Once done you can change the space's status to archive again. """
comments: "I've updated the documentation to make this limitation clearer in the meantime. " [SEP] "[~<USER>,     Thanks for updating the document.     Cheers."

Steps to Reproduce   # Create a Confluence page  # Add an *Expand* macro in it  # Inside the *Expand* macro, add a *<USER> diagram  # Publish the page  # When the page finishes to load, click to expand the content in the published Confluence page    h3. Expected Results      * The <USER>diagram will show up, as per the screenshot below:     !Screen Shot 2019-05-14 at 10.20.20.png|thumbnail!     h3. Actual Results     * The <USER>diagram doesn't load and is not shown inside the *Expand* macro, as per the screenshot below:     !Screen Shot 2019-05-14 at 10.20.41.png|thumbnail!     h3. description: """h3. Issue Summary    From an app developer:    For a use case of 1000 children of page, when using {{CQLSearchService}} and the REST API with {{cql}}    The results always return 500 results even when an option for {{startAt}} and {{limit}}. Also, this code:    returns 0 instead of 500 and    returns a false for {{PageResponse#hasMore}}    However, if the {{ancestor}} is used in CQL field instead of the {{parent field}} in the CQL query:    the count will return 1000 and the pagination request will return the expected records. The problem with the """"ancestor"""" field is that it will get all the descendants on not just the children pages which is not what the app developer needs since it returns all descendants.  h3. Environment    Version 5.10.9 and 6.6.13  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create more than 500 or 1000 children pages   # Run search REST API with CQL or with Java API    h3. Expected Results    Get the right amount of page or have the hasMore return true  h3. Actual Results    Getting lesser than 1000 pages and hasMore returns false  h3. Notes   * Vendor has tried this with REST API, UI, and Java API. summary: "Confluence stemming should be more lenient"
description: """Confluence currently uses the [K-stem algorithm|http://lexicalresearch.com/kstem-doc.txt] which can be quite restrictive in what it considers to be words that have the same stem. Some examples of words that one might expect to match but don't match are:   * Manage and Management   * Manage and Managers   * Manage and Managerial   * Memory and Memorial   * Memory and Memorize"""

summary: "Confluence 6.14+ should allow partial matches in search"
description: """In Confluence before 6.14.0 you were able to type partial words like """"proj"""" into the search box in the header and match pages with titles like """"Project X documentation""""    As of Confluence 6.14.0, when you do the same thing, it will only match that document if the full word is typed. i.e. you have to type """"project""""    This behaves the same way that advanced search used to behave before Confluence 6.14.0 which means it does not do partial word matches but does do stemming to try and match different forms of the same word (so """"projects"""" would also match """"Project X documentation""""). While this change in behaviour is not a bug, it can break workflows where documents of certain projects may have prefixes that used to match in the suggestions but no longer do.    In resolving this, Atlassian should be mindful of potentially impacting customers for whom matching documents based on a prefix would cause too many unwanted results.  h2. Workaround    Users can add an an asterisk ( * ) after the search term to expand the search. In the example above, searching for """"proj*"""" will match """"Project"""". However, this will match it in all fields of the document and not just the title as it did in older versions."""
comments: "This is very much needed as we have 100's of spaces and some with common and lengthy names. For example, we may have    <USER>- Greentree    <USER>- Advanced    Accounts Receivable - Greentree    Accounts Receivable - Advanced    Unless the user knows the name of the space in full then finding them among the 100's in not 1000's of pages that we also have with the words ""Accounts"", ""Payable"", ""Greentree"" and ""Advanced"" they have very little hope finding the space they want without first using the space filter. While not a big deal these are steps that never used to be needed to get to where you want. As we know what was and now what is, it is consensus across the business that this was a step backward for the product." [SEP] "Has this been resolved? Or has there been a functioning workaround? We are still experiencing it until now!!" [SEP] "I don't think, that it is only finding partial matches, that do not work, our confluence server shows completly strange behaviour. If I search for ""tem"", I get 7

Summary  The classes listed on [Troubleshooting failed XML site backups|https://confluence.<USER>doc/troubleshooting-failed-xml-site-backups-149212.html] to enable XML Backup/Restore debug logging are incorrect. summary: "Consistent display of confluence-administrator authority within Page Restriction dialog box"
description: """Currently, if a user restricts view and edit permissions to a page, the following behavior results:  # If you are logged in as a user in the confluence-administrator group, Confluence does not display and _Edit_ link on the page.  # However, if the confluence-administrator user clicks the page's padlock icon, the resulting permissions dialog displays the confluence-administrator user with View and Edit permissions.  # While the confluence-administrator can view the page, they need to take an extra step to edit the page by explicitly clicking the padlock icon on the _Restricted Pages_ tab within the Space Tools interface to remove the permissions.    So, in summary, this request is change the wording of the permissions displayed in the Permissions dialog for users who are in the confluence-administrators group to reflect the actual state of their permissions.  That is, until they remove the permissions from the _Restricted Pages_ tab, Confluence should show that confluence-administrator only has View permissions to the restricted page."""

summary: "Allow START_CONFLUENCE_JAVA_OPTS to be used as environment variables in setenv.sh"
description: """In setenv.sh from Confluence server 6.15, please change the following line:         To read:         This will allow me to set START_CONFLUENCE_JAVA_OPTS as an environment variable and the values will """"override"""" any values set previously in CATALINA_OPTS.  This is especially true for things like Xms and Xmx.         It is my strong prefernce to set those things as environment variables in my systemd startup service files and NOT have to touch setenv.sh on every upgrade.         Thanks,         -BL     """

summary: "Import Word Document does not import embedded tiff images from .docx files"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  When importing a word document with tiff images, the images are not present in the resulting Confluence page.     h3. Environment  * Confluence 6.13.4    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Create a word document with some text  # Add a few tiff images to the word document  # Save the word document as .docx  # Import the word document to Confluence:   !import-menu1.png|thumbnail!     h3. Expected Results  Embedded tiff images display in Confluence in the same manner they appear in the document.    h3. Actual Results  Embedded tiff images are not imported during the .docx word doc import process, and thus do not display on the newly created Confluence page.    h3. Workaround  # Open the existing .docx word document in Microsoft Word. (i)  # Perform a File > Save-as   # Select the file type """".doc (word 97-2004 document)""""  # Import the .doc file and the tiff images will be present    (i) On OSX, using Microsoft Word for Mac 2011 (14.7.7) for the conversion was successful, but using Word 16.24 was not successful. On Windows, the same conversion from docx to doc was successful."""

summary: "Confluence might show blank content or an old version of a page when an user tries to edit a page with Collaborative Editing enabled"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  When Collaborative Editing is enabled in Confluence and users try to edit a page, they might hit a bug on which a blank page is shown in the editor.  Sometimes, it is not a blank page, but an old version of it.    h3. Environment  This bug was reproduced on *6.6*, *6.13* and *6.15.4*.  Although not tested, this should affected any version from 6.0 onward.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  Below are described two ways to hit this bug, but with different characteristics to user, although the bug trigger is the same.  Because of simplicity, the first option might be more common than the other, which is when the user sees a blank page when accessing the editor.    h4. Option 1  # Install a vanilla instance of Confluence.  # Publish a new page with no content.   #* If you check records related to this page in the {{CONTENT}} table you have 2 rows.   #* The row related to the draft has {{creationdate}} and {{lastmoddate}} equal to {{null}}.   !drafts_img008.png|thumbnail!   # Disable Collaborative Editing.  # Edit the same page, add new content to it and save it.   #* If you check records related to this page in the {{CONTENT}} table, you have 3 rows.  # Enable Collaborative Editing.   #* The draft record that was created a few steps ago won't be [removed as a stale draft|https://docs.<USER>atlassian-confluence/6.2.0/com/atlassian/confluence/pages/PageManager.html#removeStaleSharedDrafts--] because its timestamps are null.  # Access the same page as before and click on _Edit_.    h5. Expected Results  Confluence shows in the editor the contents of the last published page.    h5. Actual Results  Confluence shows in the editor a blank page.    h4. Option 2  # Install a vanilla instance of Confluence.  # Disable Collaborative Editing.  # Create a page with a sample content.   !drafts_img003.png|thumbnail!    #* If you check records related to this page in the {{CONTENT}} table, you have just one row.   !drafts_img001.png|thumbnail!   # Enable Collaborative Editing.  # Access the page created a few steps before and click on _Edit_.  # *Without changing anything* on the contents, click on _Close_ to exit the editor.   #* If you check records related to this page in the {{CONTENT}} table, now you have 2 rows.   #* The new row added to the table is related to the draft record and has {{creationdate}} and {{lastmoddate}} equal to {{null}}.   !drafts_img002.png|thumbnail!   # Disable Collaborative Editing.  # Edit the same page again, add new content to it and save it.   !drafts_img004.png|thumbnail!    #* If you check records related to this page in the {{CONTENT}} table, you have 3 rows.   !drafts_img005.png|thumbnail!   # Enable Collaborative Editing.   #* The draft record that was created a few steps ago won't be [removed as a stale draft|https://docs.<USER>atlassian-confluence/6.2.0/com/atlassian/confluence/pages/PageManager.html#removeStaleSharedDrafts--] because its timestamps are null.  # Access the same page as before and click on _Edit_.    h5. Expected Results  Confluence shows in the editor the contents of the last published page.   !drafts_img006.png|thumbnail!     h5. Actual Results  Confluence shows in the editor an old version of the page, which is related to the draft record created when Collaborative Editing was first enabled.     h3. Notes  This bug is related to [CONFSERVER-57458|https://jira.<USER>browse/CONFSERVER-57458] in a way that it shows the same characteristics to the end user.  A bug new record was created because the trigger of is completely different.    h3. Workaround  _Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available_"""
comments: "We had this bug in 6.6.3 and updated to 6.13.8 and we have seen an increase in users reporting this issue and becoming hard for us to manage." summary: "Introduce a warning message in Children Display macro configuration when the Parent Page have a lot of Child Page."
description: """Due to the bug reported in CONFSRVDEV-7847 and CONFSERVER-43191, it will be useful for Confluence user to avoid adding the Children Display macro for pages that contains a lot of child pages (~3000) if we can introduce a warning message when the user specify the Parent page that contains a lot of child page inside the Children Display macro configuration.    Example message:  {quote}  The Parent Page contains xxxx of child pages. Adding this macro will delay the macro load time.  {quote}"""

summary: "Confluence DataCenter user provisioning with Okta"
description: """Our current Confluence instance is using MS Active Directory to manager user and group. Therefore, AD users and groups are synchronised from AD servers via LDAP.    We're planning to migrate Confluence instance to Data Center version. As part of migration we'll try to replace AD synchronisation by using Okta. I searched Confluence documentation and did find Confluence cloud works with Okta for user provisioning, while no document has mentioned Confluence server/datacentre version. Could you please confirm if it's feasible. If yes, may I have a implementation plan? Thanks before hands.         Regards    Wally """

summary: "LiveSearch Macro label filter not present in Search Page if transited from ""See More Results"" "
description: """h3. Summary  When restricting labels in a live-search macro, the auto-complete search results follow the label. However, when the user presses *See More Results* button, the search results include all content with no label filter.    h3. Steps to reproduce  Add LiveSearch Macro to a page  Under label to restrict in search option, restrict the results to only Pages  Save the page and perform live search  Choose *See More Results*    h3. Expected Result  Confluence Search page is showing only results of specified labels.    h3. Actual Results  Confluence Search page is showing results with any labels.    h3. Note  The label filter is properly active if the user presses """"Enter"""" or clicks the magnifying glass button. This occurs only on *See More Results* button.      h3. Workaround  Manually set the label from the sidebar, or press """"Enter"""" or clicks the magnifying glass button.    """

summary: "First-class import support for HTML and Markdown"
description: """This is carried over from a [community question|[https://community.<USER>t5/Confluence-questions/Import-HTML-files-into-confluence/qaq-p/804375]] regarding HTML import.    Imported HTML pages can't be edited using the Confluence WYSIWYG editor. Any further mods require direct editing of the markup (HTML).     As of Confluence version 6.15.2, the only document format with first-class* import support is Microsoft Word.      Given their prevalence, isn't it about time HTML and Markdown import were given first-class* support in the core product?    Thanks.    *Subsequent editing via WYSIWYG editor"""
comments: "I've linked this ticket from another community post regarding Markdown support here:  [https://community.<USER>t5/Confluence-questions/Import-markdown-into-Confluence/qaq-p/211797]    For our case, we have a lot of documentation across our Bitbucket repos in Markdown files (plus documentation elsewhere) that I'd like to be able to import via the via API into Confluence to consolidate some of our documentation in one place.  In researching this, I found out that was a non-starter because of numerous issues getting Markdown formatted correctly through that process.  Given that Bitbucket itself can render those Markdown documents ok, it seems like a prime opportunity for the Bitbucket and Confluence teams to come together to provide First Class Markdown support in Confluence." [SEP] "This is very much needed functionality. Please prioritise!" [SEP] "This should definitely be supported. Please provide a solution ASAP."

com.mchange.v2.c3p0.impl.NewProxyDatabaseMetaData.getTables(NewProxyDatabaseMetaData.java:2962)   at org.hibernate.tool.schema.extract.internal.InformationExtractorJdbcDatabaseMetaDataImpl.locateTableInNamespace(InformationExtractorJdbcDatabaseMetaDataImpl.java:448)   ... 25 more  {code}    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a database in Microsoft SQL Server with the *Confluence* name  # Install Confluence 6.13  # Connect Confluence to the Microsoft SQL Server database using the connection string url as *confluence* (instead of Confluence with an uppercase letter C).  # Upgrade Confluence 6.13 to 6.15.x    h3. Expected Results      * Confluence updates successfully    h3. Actual Results     * Upgrade fails with:  {code}  Caused by: java.sql.SQLException: The database name component of the object qualifier must be the name of the current database.  {code}    h3. Workaround     # Get the name of your database by running the following query:  {code:sql}  SELECT name, database_id, create_date FROM sys.databases;    {code}  # Compare it with the name of the database located in your _<confluence_home>/confluence.cfg.xml_ file  # Update the database name so it matches the exact name of the database from the SQL statement.  # Restart Confluence"""

summary: "Missing unique constraints and index in certain fresh Confluence installation"
description: """h3. Summary    When installing Confluence version from scratch, some constraints and indices are not being added.    This affects the following Confluence versions:   - 5.6.x   - 5.7.x   - 5.8.x    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Configure your DB Server as per the following documentation (depending on your DB Server):   -- [Database Setup for PostgreSQL|https://confluence.<USER>display/DOC/Database+Setup+for+PostgreSQL]   -- [Database Setup for Oracle|https://confluence.<USER>doc/database-setup-for-oracle-173821.html]   -- [Database Setup For MySQL|https://confluence.<USER>doc/database-setup-for-mysql-128747.html]   -- [Database Setup for SQL Server|https://confluence.<USER>doc/database-setup-for-sql-server-9258.html]   # Install a fresh Confluence instance as per [Confluence Installation Guide|https://confluence.<USER>display/DOC/Confluence+Installation+Guide]    h3. Expected Results    The following constraints/indexes should exist on the tables:  ||CONSTRAINT/INDEX NAME||TYPE||TABLE NAME||  |unq_lwr_username (or generated name on certain SQL vendors)|Unique Constraint|USER_MAPPING|  |bandana_unique_key|Unique Constraint|BANDANA|  |cp_unique_user|Unique Constraint|CONTENT_PERM|  |cp_unique_group|Unique Constraint|CONTENT_PERM|  |cps_unique_type|Unique Constraint|CONTENT_PERM_SET|  |cwd_group_name_dir_id|Unique Constraint|CWD_GROUP|  |cwd_unique_grp_attr|Unique Constraint|CWD_GROUP_ATTRIBUTE|  |cwd_user_name_dir_id|Unique Constraint|CWD_USER|  |cwd_unique_usr_attr|Unique Constraint|CWD_USER_ATTRIBUTE|  |cwd_unique_user_membership|Unique Constraint|CWD_MEMBERSHIP|  |cwd_unique_group_membership|Unique Constraint|CWD_MEMBERSHIP|  |ospe_entityid_idx|Index|OS_PROPERTYENTRY|    (i) The unique constraints specifically for Oracle/SQL Server is a bit different with the other Databases as the {{CONTENT_PERM}} only has _CP_UNIQUE_USER_GROUPS_ unique constraint and the {{CWD_MEMBERSHIP}} only has _CWD_UNIQUE_MEMBERSHIP_ unique constraint.  h3. Actual Results    The above mentioned unique constraints and index are not present in the database.  h3. Workaround    (!) Make sure to back up the database before applying any SQL commands that modify the database, and ensure Confluence is not running while applying the change   # First, let's check if there are any duplicate records in these Confluence tables:                           # Should there are no duplicate values being detected by the above queries, you may then run the below SQL statements to add the missing constraints and index.    (!) However, if there are any duplicate records in your Confluence DB, please then raise a new ticket in our Support Portal [http://getsupport.<USER>http://getsupport.<USER>] for further assistance.    h5. Notes:  There's a KB about this issue that addresses missing constraints and how to delete the duplicated records in the database, mostly around user management related tables:   * [User related issues in Confluence with the error message 'query did not return a unique result'|https://confluence.<USER>confkb/user-related-issues-in-confluence-with-the-error-message-query-did-not-return-a-unique-result-596181603.html?]"""
comments: "A fix for this issue is available to Server and Data Center customers in Confluence 7.1.0  [Upgrade|https://www.<USER>software/confluence/download?utm_source=confluence_bugfix&utm_campaign=confluence_bugfix&utm_medium=email] now or check out the [Release Notes|https://confluence.<USER>doc/confluence-7-1-release-notes-976776964.html?utm_source=confluence_bugfix&utm_campaign=confluence_bugfix&utm_medium=email] to see what other issues are resolved."

summary: "Synchrony fails to reestablish connection after a fail over when configured with a clustered database"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    When running Confluence in a clustered database and performing a failover, Synchrony will fail to reestablish connection to the database upon database engine rejoining.     All Synchrony transaction will fail after fail over is completed.     h3. Environment    Verified on Aurora and Postgres RDS running external Synchrony.     h3. Steps to Reproduce  1. Run Confluence with external Synchrony on a RDS Postgres  2. Open up collaborative editor with two sessions  3. Fail over RDS Postgres  4. Attempt to make changes in collaborative editor and observe Synchrony connection cannot be established    h3. Expected Results  Synchrony automatically establishes connection after fail over.     h3. Actual Results  Synchrony *does not* automatically establish connection after fail over.     h3. Workaround    Restarting the Synchrony node will force the connection to be reestablished. """
comments: "After the failover was finished, we were able to see 3 connections still using the reader Aurora node and this exception was thrown in the browser console when trying to edit collaboratively.  " [SEP] "Connection tuning seems to mitigate this problem. Setting reasonable values for the following properties (or env vars) will help synchrony reconnect to a database after a failover event            ||Property||Environment Variable||  |synchrony.database.idle.connection.test.period|SYNCHRONY_DATABASE_IDLE_CONNECTION_TEST_PERIOD|  |synchrony.database.max.idle.time.excess.connections|SYNCHRONY_DATABASE_MAX_IDLE_TIME_EXCESS_CONNECTIONS|  |synchrony.database.max.idle.time|SYNCHRONY_DATABASE_MAX_IDLE_TIME|  |synchrony.database.test.connection.on.checkin|SYNCHRONY_DATABASE_TEST_CONNECTION_ON_CHECKIN|              We need to test collaborative editing by setting configurations to different values and failing over and document the recommended settings          "

summary: "Custom field of type Label from Jira cannot be searched"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    While using Jira macro to create a new issue in Jira, the custom field of type *Label* is not searchable currently.    h3. Environment    * Confluence v6.15.1  * Jira v8.0.2    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Create an Application link between Confluence and Jira  # Create a custom field in Jira with type *Label*.  # When creating an issue in Jira, the custom field returned search results (/)   !jiraLabel.png|thumbnail!   #* *code* is the custom field in the above snapshot.  # When creating an issue from Confluence, the custom field doesn't return any search results (x)   !connieLabel.png|thumbnail!       h3. Expected Results    When searching from Confluence, the field should search for existing labels and auto-populate like Jira does.    h3. Actual Results    Confluence doesn't search and doesn't auto-populate.    h3. Notes    When the default label field is being typed, we see the following request recevied by Jira from Confluence.      Though, when the custom field is typed, there is no request received by Jira.      h3. Workaround     Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available."""

summary: "Embedded CID font is shown as blank in PDF preview"
description: """h3. Problem Definition    CID fonts which are embedded in PDF file are shown as blank in PDF preview.    You can see this problem with the following steps to reproduce.    # Attach  [^font-test.pdf] to a Confluence page  # [Preview|https://confluence.<USER>doc/display-files-and-images-245827130.html] the attached PDF file    This PDF file consists of the following contents and the each line is written with the corresponding font.    {panel}  * English characters with “Arial” Font → Test  * Japanese characters with “IPA Gothic” Font → テスト  * English characters with “Hiragino Kaku Gothic” Font (CID) → Test  * Japanese characters with “Hiragino Kaku Gothic” Font (CID) → テスト  {panel}    As each font is embedded in the PDF file itself, you can see that the second line with """"IPA Gothic"""" font in the PDF is previewed as expected even if the corresponding font file is *NOT* installed in Confluence server.    On the other hand, the third line and the fourth line with “Hiragino Kaku Gothic” Font (CID) are not shown correctly.    h3. Suggested Solution    It would be better if CID font is rendered correctly as well in PDF preview.    h4. Note:  Have tried installing the CID fonts in Windows and getting the following error as it is incompatible:   !screenshot-1.png|thumbnail!   CID fonts are unable to be installed on other platforms. Therefore, the only possible way is to allow Confluence to render the CID fonts in the PDF preview correctly. """

summary: "Support for electronic signatures"
description: """h3. Summary  Some processes (especially those used in companies that are agency regulated) require username/password to be entered when performing certain tasks, such as transitioning an issue through workflow. Some systems support this already (Seapine Test Track Pro comes to mind), and it would be nice if Confluence  did as well. Perhaps the solution could be adding a username/password type of field to workflow transitions.    h3. Expected Results  FDA Part 11 compliance    h3. Actual Results  Confluence is not compliant.    h3. Notes  For the server environment there are various add-ons:   # *Comalatech* using [Comala Workflows|https://marketplace.<USER>plugins/com.comalatech.workflow/server/overview?_ga=2.159191992.305044981.1512995098-1827667113.1507731271] - [support contact|https://comalatech.zendesk.com/hc/en-us].   # *AppFusion* also provides through the Atlassian marketplace the [CFR Part 11 E-Signatures (FDA Compliance) for Atlassian JIRA|https://www.appfusions.com/display/PRT11J/Home] that adds an e-signature JIRA field and audit reporting to your workflow.   # *InLabs* also provides [Electronic Signature plugin|https://marketplace.<USER>plugins/com.intenso.jira.plugins.cfr.electronic-signature/server/overview] to meet CFR Part 11."""

summary: "Deleted user cannot create a personal space after being recreated"
description: """h3. Summary    If a user is recreated with the same user name after being deleted in the past, it cannot create a personal space due to a space key conflict.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Create a user  # Log in as that user an create a personal space  # Delete that user account, this is the message we see in the UI   !delete-user-warning-615.png|thumbnail!   # Recreate the user with the same username as before  # Log in with the user and try to create a personal space    h3. Expected Results    Personal space is created as expected.    h3. Actual Results    The space creation fails as the space key already exists:     !failure-spacekeyexists.png|thumbnail!      h3. Notes    This situation is happening on Confluence versions 6.13 and above and it is a result of some changes related to GDPR. As we can see in the quotes below, the personal space of a user is no longer deleted automatically when the user itself is deleted:    {quote}  Deleting a user account does not remove any pages, comments, or other Confluence content. We also don't delete their personal space, as this may contain content owned by your organization.    This means that there will be some additional work to do after their account has been deleted to remove their personal space, and locate any references to the person in free text, or stored by third party apps or Synchrony in the database.  {quote}    References:    * [Confluence 6.13 Release Notes|https://confluence.<USER>doc/confluence-6-13-release-notes-959288785.html]  * [Personal spaces are not deleted|https://confluence.<USER>doc/delete-or-disable-users-138318.html#DeleteorDisableUsers-Personalspacesarenotdeleted]    This is a big problem for organizations that need to manage large user bases and that constantly need to delete and re-add users. Also, the failure did not happen in versions earlier than 6.13 as the space was automatically deleted during the user removal process:     !delete-user-warning-612.png|thumbnail!     The main problem is that the personal space key remains the same (*~<USERNAME>*). This is why the key conflict is returned when trying to create a new space.    In newer versions, any content authored by the user has its ownership changed to its userkey when it is deleted. If the same concept is applied to the personal space key, this problem would be avoided.    h3. Workaround    # Manually delete the personal space before deleting the user, or  # Disable the user instead of deleting it if that is a possibility"""

summary: "Default value of Remember Me (Seraph) Cookie is not specified in the docs "
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Currently, we have the following documentation covering the specifics of Confluence Cookies: [https://confluence.<USER>doc/confluence-cookies-160794071.html]    While we include an example of how to configure a custom TTL (time to live) for a *{{seraph.confluence}}* cookie, we do not specify the default TTL if a value is not explicitly declared.    This varies between versions of Confluence. _*For versions up to Confluence 6.6, the default TTL is 1 year*_. _*Confluence 6.7+ contains a default TTL value of 2 weeks.*_ We should declare this in the docs as the example can mislead users to believe that the default is 30 days as denoted in the example code block.  h3. Environment    Confluence   Seraph  h3. Workaround    Required, if there is no workaround please state:   Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""
comments: "Updated the Latest and enterprise release docs to reflect the correct default. Also changed the example to be 3 days instead of 30. "

summary: "Installer creates multiple users in OS each time it is executed"
description: """h4. Behavior    When installing Confluence using the installer on Linux OS, it creates a new user in the system to run Confluence with. Each time the installer is executed on the same OS, a new user is created. Upgrading Confluence with the installer also produced the same behavior.    Based on tests, Confluence created multiple Confluence users with the same name on the same OS. Depending on OS settings, we may see Confluence creating users and appending a number in front to differentiate them. EG: confluence, confluence1, confluence2, <USER>..  h4. Environment tested   * Confluence 6.15.3   * Ubuntu 18.04.1    h4. Recommended changes to installer process    Confluence installer should ask if you want to create a new user or use the existing Confluence user in Linux OS when installing/upgrading Confluence."""

summary: "Page Permissions dialog doesn't take into account space permissions with its messaging"
description: """h3. Problem Definition  When viewing the restrictions on an unrestricted page, you see the following dialogue at the top:    {quote}No restrictions. Everyone can view and edit this page.{quote}     !image-2019-05-09-15-38-44-627.png|thumbnail!     This would lead someone to believe that indeed, everyone on the instance has access to the page. However, it doesn't take into account any space permissions. Certain groups may have been restricted from the space, as well as individual users.    The subtext does clarify this, but making this more obvious in the page dialogue at the top.    The subtext:   {quote}Need to limit who can see this?    Right now everyone can view and edit this page if they've got the right space permissions. Use the dropdown above to restrict this page to only some people.""""{quote}    h3. Suggested Solution  Clarify the dialogue at the top, for example:    {quote}Everyone granted permissions to this space can view and edit the page.{quote}    *Note:* Even if I restrict a specific user or group in space permissions from editing the page (but allowing view permissions), the dialogue does not change, and still says everyone can view and edit the page, so we may need a full rewrite.    h3. Why this is important  When Restrictions are easy understand right away, there are fewer doubts on what needs to be done in order to grant/restrict access to a page. Ambiguous dialogue can cause confusion."""
comments: "{panel:title=Atlassian update: Check who can view a page released in Confluence Server and Data Center 7.3|borderStyle=solid|borderColor=#6554c0|titleBGColor=#6554c0|bgColor=#eae6ff|titleColor=#ffffff}  Hi there    Thanks for your interest in this issue. In [Confluence Server and Data Center 7.3|https://confluence.<USER>doc/confluence-7-3-release-notes-983794557.html], we released the ability for all users on a page to see a simple list of all the people who:   * are allowed to view the space, via space permissions, and   * are not prevented from viewing the page by page restrictions.    Although, we have not yet resolved the issue of messaging, this may help with ensuring confidence by confirming which team members can see the page. Head to [Check who can view a page|https://confluence.<USER>doc/check-who-can-view-a-page-992678937.html] to learn more.    Thanks   Makisa | Senior Product Manager, Confluence Server and Data Center  {panel}"

summary: "Custom content not available in rest/api/content/search or CQLSearchService.searchContent()"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  Contents with custom type is not returned in {{rest/api/content/search or CQLSearchService.searchContent()}}.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Create a custom content, for example content of type {{com.atlassian.confluence.plugins.confluence-questions:question}}  # Using the REST API Browser call {{/rest/api/content/search}} and search for the content given the type, in this example {{cql=type%20in%20(%22com.atlassian.confluence.plugins.confluence-questions%3Aquestion%22)}}    h3. Expected Results  Content will be returned.    h3. Actual Results  Content cannot be found / not returned.    h3. Notes  * This is a bug as also custom content types are considered content and the behaviour is inconsistent (and not documented) between different end points. Also the only method on CQLSearchService that actually finds the custom content 'search' is marked @Internal and its usage is discouraged, so there should be a working encouraged alternative for finding the custom content items    h3. Workaround  * Using {{/rest/api/search}} or CQLSearchService.search() returns the custom content"""
comments: "Hi [~<USER>,    You are right in assuming that the endpoint is supposed to return custom content types. I am unable to reproduce this issue on Confluence 6.15.3. This is the URI I am hitting which looks like the same one you are using:    {{/rest/api/search?cql=type%20in%20(%22com.atlassian.confluence.plugins.confluence-questions%3Aquestion%22}}    Questions are returned as expected by the end point. Can you test against a newer version of Confluence to see if it is fixed for you? There was some search related work done during Confluence 6.12 and Confluence 6.14 that may have resolved this issue.         Regards,  <USER>  Senior <USER>Lead, Confluence Server  Atlassian"

summary: "Editing pages in Confluence fails in Docker running behind reverse proxy with context path"
description: """h3. Summary  Connections to Synchrony fail when Confluence is running via official docker image, behind a reverse proxy, using the internal Synchrony proxy. This is due to an empty string value for confluence.context.path    h3. Environment  * Reverse proxy (with no Synchrony endpoint)  * Internal synchrony proxy enabled  * atlassian/confluence-server docker image    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Save the following docker-compose.yml file:  {code:borderStyle=solid}  version: '3.6'  services:    proxy:      image: outrigger/https-proxy:1.0      ports:        - """"443:443""""      environment:        UPSTREAM_DOMAIN: confluence        UPSTREAM_PORT: 8090        PROXY_DOMAIN: localhost        RATE_LIMIT: 0    confluence:      ports:        - """"8090:8090""""        - """"8091:8091""""      image: aacdf7d27208      volumes:        - confluence:/var/atlassian/application-data/confluence      environment:        CATALINA_CONTEXT_PATH: /conf        CATALINA_CONNECTOR_PROXYNAME: localhost        CATALINA_CONNECTOR_PROXYPORT: 443        CATALINA_CONNECTOR_SCHEME: https        CATALINA_CONNECTOR_SECURE: """"true""""  volumes:    confluence:    # Once past the setup wizard attempt to create a page (this is the last step of the setup wizard)    h3. Expected Results  You can add text to the page and see that changes are being save by Synchrony    h3. Actual Results  h4. When editing the page  Pages cannot be saved.     The following error appears in a pop-up:  """"Something went wrong after loading the editor. Copy your unsaved changes and refresh the page to keep editing.""""    The bottom right status states """"Can't save changes"""".   h4. In System Information  The confluence.context.path entry is empty, when it should show the context path as set in Tomcat    h3. Notes  Confluence sets confluence.context.path via a JVM argument. See <confluence-install>/bin/setenv.sh:  {code:borderStyle=solid}  CATALINA_OPTS=""""-Dconfluence.context.path=${CONFLUENCE_CONTEXT_PATH} ${CATALINA_OPTS}""""      In a normal server install of Confluence, if you find the CONFLUENCE_CONTEXT_PATH entry in System Information it will be the text entered as configured in <confluence-install>/conf/server.xml (in this case it would be /conf).      !server.png|thumbnail!     In the docker image, we set the <context path=""""""""> to use a variable (from the Confluence Dockerfile):  {code:borderStyle=solid}  && sed -i -e 's/Context path=""""""""/Context path=""""${catalinaContextPath}""""/' ${CONFLUENCE_INSTALL_DIR}/conf/server.xml      When we look at System Information for the docker image, instead of plain text it will shown the variable name:     !docker.png|thumbnail!     The confluence-context-path-extractor.jar parses the context path from <confluence-install>/conf/server.xml as shown:  {code:borderStyle=solid}  public class ContextPathExtractor {      public static void main(String[] args) {          final String catalinaHome = args[0];          File serverXmlFile = new File(catalinaHome + File.separator + """"conf"""" + File.separator + """"server.xml"""");          String confluenceContextPath = getAttributeFromXmlFile(serverXmlFile, """"path"""", """"//Context[@path]"""").orElse("""""""");          System.out.print(confluenceContextPath);      }  }    ** docker run example:  {code:borderStyle=solid}  docker run --name confluence -d \  -p 8090:8090 \  -p 8091:8091  \  -e CATALINA_CONTEXT_PATH=/conf \  -e CATALINA_CONNECTOR_PROXYNAME=localhost \  -e CATALINA_CONNECTOR_PROXYPORT=443 \  -e CATALINA_CONNECTOR_SCHEME=https \  -e CATALINA_CONNECTOR_SECURE=true \  -e CATALINA_CONTEXT_PATH=/conf \  -e catalinaContextPath=/conf \  -v confluence:/var/atlassian/application-data/confluence \  atlassian/confluence-server:6.15.2-ubuntu-18.04-adoptopenjdk8  {code}"""
comments: "This should now be fixed as we're generating server.xml from a template, and ContextPathExtractor can grab the context path value from the file, where it couldn't before. I'll close this out."

summary: "Font configuration workaround in Confluence docker image atlassian/confluence-server:6.15.2-alpine still results in fontconfiguration failure"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  After installing docker image [atlassian/confluence-server:6.15.2-alpine|https://bitbucket.org/atlassian-docker/docker-atlassian-confluence-server/src/6.15.2-alpine/Dockerfile], Confluence displays an error message indicating a failure with font configuration.    h3. Environment    Confluence 6.15.2 created from docker image  h3. Steps to Reproduce   * Install docker image [atlassian/confluence-server:6.15.2-alpine|https://bitbucket.org/atlassian-docker/docker-atlassian-confluence-server/src/6.15.2-alpine/Dockerfile]    h3. Expected Results    Since the docker image contain a work around for this issue, we'd expect not to see the fontconfiguration error:    h3. Actual Results    Once installed, Confluence displays a popup indicating a fontconfiguration failure error and displays the same issue within the instance health checks.  h3. Workaround    Use the Ubuntu distribution of {{atlassian/confluence-server}}, e.g. [atlassian/confluence-server:6.15.2|https://bitbucket.org/atlassian-docker/docker-atlassian-confluence-server/src/6.15.2/Dockerfile]"""

description: """Maybe this exists, but an easy way in the Admin page to see if you're running the current version (given the vulnerability issues), or need to upgrade the server."""

summary: "Application Navigator button doesn't work if the user is not authenticated"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    When clicking the App Switcher in Confluence (Application Navigator), the links don't come up and we receive an error message instead.     !appswitcher-fail.png|thumbnail!     h3. Environment   - Confluence 6.13+    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Install Confluence 6.13.0 or later;   # Go to *Administration > Application Navigator*;   # Add a new link;   ** If you have an Application Link configured (e.g. Jira), it will automatically add a new line.   ** If there's no Application Link, anything works, like Name: """"Google"""" - URL: """"https://google.com"""";   # Reload the page and click the App switcher (top right corner) button to confirm it's working;   # Log out and click the App switcher again.    h3. Expected Results    The links appear just fine.  h3. Actual Results    The popup only shows """"Something went wrong, please try again.""""    Nothing appears in the application logs, but browser network console reveals a 401 response if we click the """"Try again"""" link:    h3. Workaround    Enabling *Anonymous access* in Global Permissions is a possible workaround, since this is caused by an unauthorized request. As long as the spaces don't allow Anonymous access, no content will be visible."""
comments: "We are experiencing this issue, that the application navigator doesn't work if you aren't logged in. Sine not all users have access to all services (Jira/Confluence/Bitbucket) this is a limitations since they cant navigate from the default if they dont have access, meaning then will have to type the full URL manually." [SEP] "We also have the same error, with Confluence 7.2.1. As we have enabled external user management, while using Crowd, some customers and also colleagues can't find the link to Crowd, which is in the application navigation ..." [SEP] "We are using Confluence 7.1.0, if the user is not logged in to Confluence, this error occurs.    Wasn't like this when we were using 6.9.1." [SEP] "The new enterprise release Confluence 7.4.0 has the same problem." [SEP] "Same on 7.5.0"

summary: "Update all plugin feature update plugin that was free, but has changed to paid."
description: """h3. Issue Summary  The {{Update All}} plugin feature will update all the plugin even the plugin is changed from free to the paid version. According to documentation, a plugin that changed from free to paid is not eligible for the update.  {quote}  Only apps that are eligible will be updated. Apps may not be eligible because:        the app was free, but has changed to paid      your current app license doesn't allow upgrades to that particular version.   {quote}  (i) [Updating apps|https://confluence.<USER>upm/updating-apps-273875710.html]      h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Download an old version of the plugin that is free (e.g SU for Confluence).  # Go to Manage apps >> Action required >> Click Update All  # The plugin is being updated and request for a license.    h3. Expected Results  The plugin will not be updated due to it was changed from free to paid    h3. Actual Results  The plugin is updated.    """

summary: "Include versions supported for Team Calendar in their release notes"
description: """h3. Problem Definition  Currently, the Team Calendar release notes do not include the supported Confluence versions range. This was a problem for a customer with TC version 6.0.16 which states that """"Team Calendars 6.0.16 contains performance improvements that require Confluence 5.8.2 or higher.” But this particular version of 6.0.16 only supports Confluence until 6.10.2, but the customer was working with Confluence version 6.15.2.    h3. Suggested Solution  It would be nice to indicate the range of Confluence versions supported in the TC release notes for customers who refer to release notes for upgrading apps.      h3. Workaround  This range is indicated in the Marketplace link under the [Versions|https://marketplace.<USER>apps/72307/team-calendars-for-confluence/version-history] tab.  """

summary: "Option to hide page restriction dialogue box from users who do not have the Restrictions space permission"
description: """h3. Problem Definition  Page restrictions are currently viewable to users with only view rights as well as users who do not have the rights to edit page permissions within a space. This poses a security risk as it can reveal sensitive information regarding who has access to certain content.    h3. Suggested Solution  Provide options to hide the access list on a restricted page from users who do not have the [Restrictions space permission|https://confluence.<USER>doc/space-permissions-overview-139521.html]."""

summary: "Inconsistent results in the People Directory search tool"
description: """When searching for partial user's usernames, first names, last names or email addresses, the People Directory randomly returns or misses results.    Example userbase:  * Ashley Young  * Donald Donner  * Ronald Ronner  * Will Santos  * <USER>Perez  * Abcde Abcdf  * Abcdj Abcdk    Search results:  # """"<USER>""" = does not return _Ashley Young_.  # """"<USER>"""" = does return _Ashley Young_.  # """"Don"""" = does return _Donald Donner_.  # """"Don*"""" = does return _Donald Donner_.  # """"Donn"""" = does not return _Donald Donner_.  # """"Ron"""" = does not return _Ronald Ronner_.  # """"Ron*"""" = does return _Ronald Ronner_.  # """"Will"""" = does not return anything.  # """"Will*"""" = returns _William Perez_, but not _Will Santos_.  # """"Will Santos"""" = returns _Will Santos_.  # """"Abcd"""" = does not return either _Abcde Abcdf_ or _Abcdj Abcdk_.  # """"Abcd*"""" =  does return _Abcde Abcdf_ and _Abcdj Abcdk_.    The inconsistency is mainly when we search for partial user names (i.e. """"Don"""" vs. """"Ron"""")."""

summary: "Disable space admin password prompt"
description: """Currently, we can only disable the password prompt for both Space and Confluence administration.     {quote}  Confluence protects access to its administrative functions by requiring a secure administration session to use the Confluence administration console or administer a space.   {quote}    [Configuring Secure Administrator Sessions|https://confluence.<USER>doc/configuring-secure-administrator-sessions-218269595.html]    If will be nice if we could disable the password prompt when the admin accessing Space.  """

summary: "Special characters on page names can cause incorrect links to undefined pages due to existing database issues"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  When adding a link to a Confluence page with  special characters in its name (such as accented characters), after saving the page, the link changes to an undefined page with the same name.    When querying the database for the linked page, two pages with the same 'title', but different 'lowertitle' show up. One of them is the actual page, the other is the incorrectly created draft from the undefined page link.     This only seems to happen when the existing page already has an incorrect conversion of an accented character on the 'content' table, from upper to lowercase (the new draft has a correct 'lowertitle' conversion). This could be due to an instance migration, database update/migration, or similar operations - ultimately, this is the actual cause of the issue, but the problem may not become apparent until a link to the problematic page is created.    This can be checked with the following query:      h3. Environment  Issue is reproducible on PostgreSQL 9.6.12    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Find a page that has an incorrect lowercase title on the database, due to a special character on its title (using the above query, for example)  # On any page, create a link to the problematic page  # Save the page    h3. Expected Results  The link keeps pointing at the chosen page, after saving the page.    h3. Actual Results  The link changes to an undefined page link instead. Checking the database with the above example query, there are now two different pages with the same title, but different lowercase title.    h3. Workaround  Since the problem is caused by an existing incorrect conversion of a page title to lowercase, on the database, this can be fixed by deleting the undefined page from the database, and then manually fixing the page's 'lowertitle' field as well:  """
comments: "[~<USER>, this is the same issue as https://jira.<USER>browse/CONFSERVER-3723    Closing this in favour of that ticket."

summary: "Separate concepts of Creator and Author (and owner)"
description: """If I look at a Confluence article, it says 'Created by So-and-so at whatever-time.' If I look at the article's Page Description, it lists 'So-and-so' as the author of the page.    One use case where this is problematic is migrating data from other systems. If Mr. So-and-so is tasked with getting pages from the old system in to Confluence, *they do not become the author of the work they are reproducing.* As such, they require the ability to assign authorship. They can remain the page's creator because they created the page, but they did not author the work in that page.   # The creator of a migrated page is not necessarily the author (you do not become the author of something when you reproduce it -- ask a copyright lawyer)   # The owner of a page is not necessarily the author (the author could've left the company, but they're still the author)    So all of these concepts need to be clearly separated. Making creator/author the same by default will maintain the current behaviour. Adding the ability to assign an author will cover use cases where someone is tasked with migrating existing data into Confluence. Such a function should have associated permissions controls so that it is available to trusted members of a team.    There are various workarounds such as directly changing the database or using a macro to contradict what Confluence is telling you, but Confluence's job is document management. Having to do unsupported things to the db or having to jury rig a solution to track the most basic meta-data associated with a document makes me want to say words that would be counter-productive. So I'll leave it there.    Thank you for your time and consideration,"""
comments: "No-brainer.    In the Cloud version hacking in the database is no option.    I do not want to be an owner for life, just because I imported the pages, nor for the fact that I created pages for others to assist and continue.     To be notified of changes of 'your' page is annoying.    And if pages are imported form somewhere else (like after a company splitting up) you don't want to remain with pages owned by an 'alien'.     " [SEP] "{quote}I do not want to be an owner for life, just because I imported the pages, nor for the fact that I created pages for others to assist and continue.   {quote}  Amen. As an admin who creates tons of pages for others to help get them started, I can attest it is super annoying. We have people who end up copying pages, breaking links, <USER> because they want to be the creator. It's a mess. " [SEP] "As an admin I also have the problem where I create a space of just a page for someone who then takes it over, restricts it and yet I continue to get requests and queries relating

summary: "Built-in Interactive Search instead of Pagination"
description: """*Background*  Atlassian Applications such as Jira and Confluence implements _pagination_ as a user interface design feature. The purpose behind pagination is also to improve on Performance whereby loading of items are limited.    *Suggestion*  Most modern graphical user interfaces are using _Built-in Interactive Search_ which can be a better way instead of _pagination_. A search function would be able to return accurate results quicker instead of having the person to click on pages based on """"guesses"""" on where the items are.     If _pagination_ is still the way to go, then at least implement a global setting which defines the maximum number of entries one sees per page.    P.S: This might be a good source of reading, https://www.nngroup.com/articles/item-list-view-all/  """

Proxies also have no effect on this behavior.    h3. Workaround   Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "As a user I want to request access to a restricted Space"
description: """h3. Problem Definition  When users access a page within a restricted Space, they will receive a _Page Not Found_ response although the page exists.  Also, there's no way to request access to the target Space directly from the application similar to what we have for [restricted pages|https://confluence.<USER>doc/page-restrictions-139414.html].    Let's say we have the following scenario in Confluence:  # Have a regular Confluence installation.  # Create a regular user; let's call it _User001_.  # Create a Space (let's call it _SP1_) and make sure _User001_ doesn't have [_View_ permission|https://confluence.<USER>doc/space-permissions-overview-139521.html] to it.  # Create an unrestricted page in _SP1_ -- let's call it _Page001_.    If someone shares the _Page001_'s link with _User001_, the following page is presented in the UI:   !page_img001.png|thumbnail!     In this case, _User001_ has no information on who to contact to get access to the page.  Also, the information can be misleading, since the focus is on the large _Page Not Found_ message instead of _The page exists, but you don't have view permission for that space_.    h3. Suggested Solution  When the page exists, but the current user doesn't have access to the Space, Confluence should return a more meaningful message to the user instead of focusing on _Page Not Found_.  Also, there *must* be a mechanism on which the user can request access to the Space, similar to what already exists to restricted pages, which sends an e-mail (or in-app message) to the Space administrator(s).    h3. Why this is important  Confluence would be more friendly to the user if it had this functionality to request access to _restricted_ Spaces, specially when the user receives a direct link to a page.  The fact the user can't do that from the application breaks the flow and makes it more difficult to use.     h3. Workaround   _None so far_"""
comments: "As the administrator for a large confluence site, we delegate permission requests to the space admins.  we don't know the users or the spaces well enough to make judgement calls on who gets the access and who is rejected.    We want a list of space admins to be presented when access is denied so that the user knows where to request access.    if the request goes to the confluence application admin, it will only create more work and delays."

summary: "Having Collaborative Editing disabled and HTTPS will cause macros' titles to not show up"
description: """h3. Summary      When editing a page that has a macro, its title won't show up if you have HTTPS enabled and Collaborative Editing disabled. Confluence will make incorrect requests to the HTTP URL instead of HTTPS URL, causing this behavior.    h3. Environment   * HTTPS enabled  * Collaborative Editing disabled    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Enable Confluence to be running on HTTPS (Reverse proxy or at Tomcat)  # Disable Collaborative Editing  # Go to a Confluence page that has a *Panel* macro, for example  # Edit this page    h3. Expected Results      * The macros will show up its titles normally    h3. Actual Results     * The macro's won't show up its titles unless you edit them.    h3. Diagnosis    # Confirm that *Confluence Administration* » *General Configuration* » *Collaborative Editing* is currently disabled  #* This is identified in the Support Zip / application.xml:    # Capture a [HAR file|https://confluence.<USER>kb/generating-har-files-and-analysing-web-requests-720420612.html] when refreshing the page whilst in Edit mode  # Most of the web requests will be against HTTPS base URL  #* e.g. https://www.mybaseurl.com/confluence/......  # However, the URL to generate the Macro Titles (plugins/servlet/confluence/placeholder/macro-heading) are incorrectly made against the HTTP base URL instead of the HTTP base URL  #* e.g. http://www.mybaseurl.com/confluence/plugins/servlet/confluence/placeholder/macro-heading?definition=e2NvbHVtbjp3aWR0aD0zMyV9&locale=en_US&version=2    h3. Workaround   * Enable Collaborative Editing"""
comments: "We do not have the ability to enable collaborative editing.  That is a function we purposely have not setup nor plan to.   Any other workaround suggestions in the meantime?   This is super inefficient when editing!" [SEP] "I reported that behaviour several weeks ago, but was referred to CONFSERVER-57934. Customer support suspected a relationship between the missing macro title and the mixed content, because I have have identified that Confluence tries to load the title, which is actually an image, using 'http' instead of 'https' (which is refused for security reasons, depending on browser & settings).    In the comment section of CONFSERVER-57934 I have posted a potential workaround.     " [SEP] "Thank you <USER> This workaround is saving us a ton of headaches at the moment. Hopefully this will be fixed for good in an upcoming release." [SEP] "I tried that script at all 3 options for Custom HTML, but Page Properties Macro and Panel Macro still had a blank title during edit mode. 6.15.2 and Chrome.

summary: "Synchrony websocket error: No HttpDecoder and no HttpServerCodec in the pipeline"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Confluence becomes unresponsive after all http threads get stuck waiting on a response to establish a websockets connection with Synchrony. Synchrony process shows close to max CPU usage and the top output indicates that many of its threads are long running and consuming a lot of CPU.  h3. Environment   * Confluence Server 6.15.2   * Synchrony-proxy running in front of Synchrony    h3. Notes    Synchrony logs show the following error:    Synchrony-proxy logs also indicate that something has gone wrong during the handshake:    catalina.out:    Thread dumps will show most of the HTTP threads waiting for the Synchrony Proxy to get a Synchrony session, as follows:    h3. Workaround  h4. Option 1    Until the bug is fixed, a temporary workaround is to switch Synchrony into """"Limited"""" mode.    Follow the steps below to perform the workaround (if Confluence is unresponsive, perform a restart before proceeding).   * The [Administering Collaborative Editing|https://confluence.<USER>doc/administering-collaborative-editing-858772086.html#AdministeringCollaborativeEditing-Changetheeditingmode] article describes how to change Synchrony's operating modes     # Go to  !https://confluence.<USER>doc/files/747833194/747833195/2/1517274813281/cog.png|width=16!  > *General Configuration* > *Collaborative editing*.    # Choose *Change mode*.   # Select """"Limited"""" and choose *Change*.     (i) Synchrony can also be changed to """"Off"""" mode, but any shared drafts will become unavailable while in """"Off"""" mode.    * See [How Do Drafts Work on Confluence|https://confluence.<USER>confkb/how-do-drafts-work-on-confluence-938043306.html] for more information    h4. Option 2    If you want to keep CE enabled, point your Load Balancer or Reverse Proxy directly to Synchrony and avoid using the internal proxy. More details [here|https://confluence.<USER>doc/possible-confluence-and-synchrony-configurations-958779064.html#PossibleConfluenceandSynchronyConfigurations-DirecttoSynchronywithareverseproxy]."""
comments: "This has caused us partial outages the last several days. I don't want to have to put collab editing to ""off"" and lose all those drafts, and ""limited"" mode has several limitations (can't complete tasks, can't rename or move pages)." [SEP] "Confluence 6.12.2, same problem" [SEP] "same problem in confluence 6.11.2" [SEP] "We have the issue on 6.15.1" [SEP] "same problem in confluence 6.7.1" [SEP] "We've had daily crashes because of this bug. The load goes high rendering our instance unusable. The only way to bring it back is to restart synchrony. Limited isn't really an option, it breaks the purpose of the collaborative editing. I hope you can fix this bug asap.         I made a simple bash script that runs every minute, gets the PID of synchrony from ""confluence/<USER>temp/synchrony.pid"", checks the CPU load of the PID using ""ps"", and if above a treshold variable, sends a ""kill -HUP <pid>"" to restart synchrony, and sends me an email if it restarted it. Works well so far." [SEP] "We are seeing the s

summary: "The ndash character used for an attachment name it is not properly encoded when exporting and import spaces."
description: """h3. Issue reported:    The ndash character used for an attachment name it is not properly encoded when exporting and import spaces.  h3. Reproducible steps:    1. Take a pdf attachment and rename it to “test – 1.pdf”, or any other file name, the name has to contain “–”.   2. Upload “test – 1.pdf” file on Confluence page as attachment   3. Add this “test – 1.pdf” on page using PDF macro.   4. Export the space   5. Import the space  h3. Expected Results:    The pdf is properly displayed  h3. Current Results:    An error is thrown:  {quote}Error rendering macro ‘viewpdf’   The viewfile macro is unable to locate the attachment “test - 1.pdf” on this page  {quote}  !Screen Shot 2019-05-01 at 13.39.10.png|thumbnail!    If we view the attachment after an import the url will be:   [http://url/pages/worddav/preview.action?fileName=test+-+1.pdf&pageId=1234667]    Whereas the *Storage macro* is:    h3. Workaround    Update the *Storage format* and replace the ndash character with a dash    or    Edit the page and select the attachment again on the pdf macro."""
comments: "This also happens in Confluence 6.12.4" [SEP] "I've seen this in 6.6 too"

summary: "Pressing Enter while editing macro configuration remove the macro from the editor."
description: """h3. Summary    When adding some of the Confluence default macro in the page editor and then presses the Return key (Enter) while editing the macro configuration, the *Macro Configuration* dialog window is dismissed, and the macro is *deleted* from the editor and replaced with *a new line.* This behaviour affect most of the Confluence default Macro and *does not* affect the *View File macro (PPT, PDF).*  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Go to a page and click *Edit*   # Insert a macro for example, *Code Macro.*   # Click on the macro to *edit* the configuration and type something into the *Title* field   # Press *RETURN* (or *ENTER*)    h3. Expected Results    An macro is added to the page.  h3. Actual Results    Macro is deleted and a new line is added to the page instead.  h3. Notes    Reproducible in *Firefox* 66.0.3 (64-bit) not in Chrome and Safari (11.0.2).  h3. Workarounds   * Use the *Insert* button instead of the RETURN(or ENTER) key.  OR   * Use a browser different than Firefox."""

summary: "Accessing the Drafts page within a user profile throws a NullPointerException on getResumableDraftsBySpace"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  When a Confluence user access the [_Drafts_ page|https://confluence.<USER>doc/drafts-149040.html] within their profile, they receive a *System Error* in the UI instead of a table with all the unpublished drafts.    h3. Environment  * This issue was validated on Confluence Server 6.6.12 with PostgreSQL.  * It was validated with both Collaborative Editing enabled and disabled.  * It was also reported with MySQL database -- so it is not DB engine specific.  * This issue was validated on Confluence Server 6.13.3 with PostgreSQL and CE enabled.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Install a Confluence vanilla instance.  # As a Confluence user, go to any Space and click on the _Create blank page_ button.  # Give the page a title, add any content to the page and click on the _Close_ button.   #* When prompted to _close the editor_, click on the _Keep draft_ button.  # In the Confluence database, get the {{CONTENTID}} of the newly created draft and run the following SQL query.     #* This will make both the {{CREATIONDATE}} and the {{LASTMODDATE}} equal to {{NULL}}.  # As a Confluence administrator, go to *Cog icon* > *General configuration* > *Cache Management*.  # Search for *Content Objects* cache and click on the *Flush* button related to it.  # As the Confluence user who created the draft, go to *Profile* > *Drafts*.   #* Or access {{<Confluence Base URL>/users/viewmydrafts.action}}    A very similar stack trace can be reproduced if one of the spaces associated with the drafts has an empty title. To reproduce this:      h3. Expected Results  The page with unpublished drafts touched by the current user is shown.   !drafts_img001.png|thumbnail!     h3. Actual Results  A _System Error_ is shown in the UI with a _stack trace_ similar to the one below.   !system_error_img001.png|thumbnail!   For issues related to the dates, the NPE will be thrown by the class *java.util.Comparator.lambda$comparing$77a9974f$1(Comparator.java:469)*    {quote}  h5. Cause   org.apache.velocity.exception.MethodInvocationException: Invocation of method 'getResumableDraftsBySpace' in class com.atlassian.confluence.user.actions.ViewMyDraftsAction threw exception java.lang.NullPointerException at /users/viewmydrafts.vm[line 123, column 30]      at org.apache.velocity.runtime.parser.node.ASTIdentifier.execute(ASTIdentifier.java:223)    caused by: java.lang.NullPointerException      at java.util.Comparator.lambda$comparing$77a9974f$1(Comparator.java:469)     h5. Stack Trace:[hide]    {quote}    For issues related to the space name, the stack trace is almost identical to what is seen above, but the NPE comes from *at java.lang.String$CaseInsensitiveComparator.compare(String.java:1192)*    h3. Notes  There's still no reference on how an user created draft doesn't have both the {{CREATIONDATE}} and {{LASTMODDATE}}. This is also true for space names being null.  A bug report should be added here when it is found.    (!) Since both workarounds require direct modification in the database, please make sure you have a recent backup to perform a rollback if necessary.  Make sure you only run the steps below if you are certain you are affected by this bug.    h3. Workaround 1 - Fixing null dates  (!) Execute this if the NPE comes from *java.util.Comparator.lambda$comparing$77a9974f$1(Comparator.java:469)*    The known workaround is to manually modify the {{CREATIONDATE}} of the affected drafts directly in the database.    The following SQL query will show you the drafts associated with a specific user, but with {{CREATIONDATE}} and {{LASTMODDATE}} equal to {{NULL}}.  (i) Make sure you change {{CHANGE_THE_USERNAME}} to an actual _username_.  {code:title=Tested for PostgreSQL}  select c.*  from CONTENT c  join USER_MAPPING um on c.CREATOR=um.USER_KEY  where (c.CONTENT_STATUS='draft'  or c.CONTENTTYPE='DRAFT')  and (c.CREATIONDATE is NULL and c.LASTMODDATE is NULL)  and um.USERNAME='CHANGE_THE_USERNAME'  ;      h3. Workaround 2 - Fixing null space names    (!) Execute this if the NPE comes from *at java.lang.String$CaseInsensitiveComparator.compare(String.java:1192)*    # Identify if there are any spaces related to the user drafts with NULL names:    # If any space is returned, take note of the ID and run the following query:      After modifying the database for any of the workarounds, make sure to flush the affected cache or restart Confluence:  # As a Confluence administrator, go to *Cog icon* > *General configuration* > *Cache Management*.  # Search for *Content Objects* cache and click on the *Flush* button related to it."""
comments: "FYI, we had this problem with Oracle Database. Replace ""LIMIT 1"" by ""FETCH NEXT 1 ROWS ONLY"" when using Oracle Database."

summary: "Add support for usernames that have whitespace to Activity Stream Gadget"
description: """As a user creating an Activity Stream Gadget, I would like to be able to use the Activity Stream Gadget to filter activity by a user that has a space in their name. Currently, I cannot do this because the system treats whitespace as a delimiter in Username filter criteria. Commas are converted in the backend to spaces. Thus:    - <USER>- this is one user  - <USER>tcostales - thease are two users, """"<USER>""" and """"tcostales""""  - b costales - these are two users, """"b"""" and """"costales""""    h3. Steps to Reproduce  - Create a user in a Crowd internal directory that has spaces in their username  - Connect the internal directory to Confluence  - Sign in as the user and create content in a space  - Create an Activity Stream Gadget on a new page  - Set Filter criteria for Username > <the user you have with spaces in their name>  - Save changes to the filter, then save and publish the page    h3. Desired Behavior  - You will see activity by the user which has whitespace in their username    h3. Actual Behavior  - You do not see the activity    h3. Workaround  - None at this time, whitespace is not supported"""

summary: "View File macro loading indefinitely for anonymous user."
description: """h3. Issue Summary    View File macro (tested with PDF) is loading indefinitely for anonymous user.   !viewfile.png|thumbnail!  h3. Environment    Confluence 6.15.1  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Allow the *can use* permission for anonymous user in Confluence *General Configuration* > *Global Permission*.   # Create a space and allow the anonymous access in the *Space Permission*.   # Create a page in the Space and insert a view file PDF macro.   # Access the page anonymously.    h3. summary: "Improvement of image / picture handling"
description: """Hi guys,         a colleague asked me to create the following improvement request:    """"When uploading images for being displayed with the image viewer, 3 different sizes can be selected. Using the medium and large size - the image is displayed in correct size - but loaded in original size (it is not being re-rendered) which takes huge traffic and load times although it is not an extensive image on display. Displaying the small image - a re-rendered image is shown as expected for the others too.    I would suggest to change this, please re-render all 3 sizes for display on the page at the upload time. Then delete the original image (store in a temp folder due rendering process only) which saves webspace too."""""""
