description: """h3. Summary    It would be great if an app user is provided to vendors (what we do for Cloud) that has the necessary scope to administer, read, write, <USER> without relying on the permissions of a separate user that calls APIs on the vendor's behalf.    h3. Background    From vendor:  {quote}  The [ContentPropertyService|https://docs.<USER>atlassian-confluence/5.9.3/com/atlassian/confluence/api/service/content/ContentPropertyService.html ]  methods to modify properties check current user's edit/view permissions over the content. That does not allow apps to set property values when running scheduled jobs or when the user triggering an event does not have view/edit permissions over the content.    It would be very useful to have a Java API to modify content properties ignoring the authenticated user's permissions over the content. If there is an alternative, we would like to know too.    The user from the event triggering the ContentPropertyService call has not enough permissions over the content to edit the values, and in the case of scheduled jobs, there is no user at all. So in our case, instead of allowing a user with not enough permissions to perform that action, the ideal solution would be to allow the app to have its own user (similar to the cloud I believe). We have had a similar issue with CQL searches and view permissions. The solution to ask the client to define a user that will act on behalf of our app to bypass this could work but it is not very suitable.  {quote}    Request from [DEVHELP-2424|https://ecosystem.atlassian.net/browse/DEVHELP-2424]"""

summary: "Supporting DUO authentication with remember me option"
description: """When accessing a Confluence 6.12.2  instance through the Confluence Server mobile app on an iOS device that's integrated with DUO, DUO presents a """"Remember me..."""" checkbox. But, if you try to check it, DUO returns a message indicating that cookies need to be enabled to use select """"Remember me.."""" option.    The request is to support DUO authentication with remember me option."""

summary: "Display Space admin names on permission error page"
description: """When a user tries to access a confluence space they dont have access to, they get a permission error. If they can see a list of people who are space admins (not confluence platform admins), it'll tremendously save time for the user and the confluence platform admins. They'll know who to reach out to right away to get access."""

summary: "Disable CalDAV in Team Calendars"
description: """h3. Problem Definition      * There are scenarios when a user wants to disable the CalDAV option in Team Calendars, to avoid users to subscribe to other calendars.    h3. Suggested Solution      * Implement a way to disable the CalDAV through the UI    h3. Why this is important       * This feature helps our user to manage their instances better, depending on their needs and demands."""
comments: "CalDAV can have side-effects - if someone subscribes to a calendar and has edit permissions, they can accidentally majorly mess it up.    It should be disableable."

summary: "Jira Issue Macro can cause grey Div in front of content preventing use of the page"
description:
"""h3. Summary If a page contains a Jira issue macro that does not have an appropriate
' and '

' tag, it will cause a

tag to appear as a translucent grey field that will block access to the page below. h3. Environment Confluence 6.10.2 h3. Steps to Reproduce # post a storage format to a page similar to this: h3. Expected Results The page loads normally. h3. Actual Results Page appears with a translucent grey field that blocks the page. h3. Notes (Optional - If Necessary) h3. Workaround - Using the developer tools will allow you to remove the offending div for a single use. You can use this to edit the offending page. - Edit the page and ensure that the jira issue macros have no content surrounding them to the left or right. You may need to delete and re-add them"""

summary: "Improve ""Setting Up a Mail Session for the Confluence Distribution"" document"
description: """https://confluence.<USER>doc/setting-up-a-mail-session-for-the-confluence-distribution-6328.html    There are many other parameters that can be added to the Resource block when configuring a mail session in Confluence. (i.e. """"mail.smtp.ssl.enable"""")    This suggestion is to add a reference to the JavaMail documentation, which outlines the given parameters: https://javaee.github.io/javamail/docs/api/com/sun/mail/smtp/package-summary.html    The document could also mention that adding {{mail.debug=""""true""""}} in the Resource block in _server.xml_ will add debug messages to the Tomcat logs which can be helpful when troubleshooting Mail Server issues."""

summary: "Disabling companion app plugin submodules causes corruption of   pdfview macro preview"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Disabling companion app plugin submodules causes corruption of   pdfview macro preview  h3. Environment    Confluence 6.15.1, postgres 9.5, all supported browsers  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create new page   # Add pdf attachment and insert it into PDFview macro   # Go to Confluence administration -> manage apps. Search for Confluence plugin """"Confluence Previews""""   Disable plugin submodules (according to official documentation [https://confluence.<USER>doc/administering-the-atlassian-companion-app-958456281.html ):|https://confluence.<USER>doc/administering-the-atlassian-companion-app-958456281.html):]   ## Edit With plugin for the Media Viewer (companion-plugin)   ## Embedded 'Edit With' button (embedded-edit)   # Go to previously created page    h3. Expected Results    Pdfview macro is rendered  h3. Actual Results    Pdfmacro preview is corrupted:    !Screenshot_20190423_135858.png|thumbnail!  h3. Workaround    Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Excel worksheet that has cells that link to another worksheet, do not get updated when using Companion App. "
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Given two excel worksheets, where one of them has one or more cells linked to cells from the other worksheet. The links between the two cells do not get updated when accessed.    This is because when a worksheet is linked to another, the path to that other excel worksheet is recorded and when this file is uploaded to Confluence and accessed via the Companion App, this path remains and does not get updated. This causes errors, where the worksheet will be looking for linked worksheet via the path defined in the cell. The following error can be seen:  {quote}Some links in your workbook can not be updated at this time{color:#172b4d} {color}  {quote}  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create two Excel worksheets.   # Link one worksheet to another.    ## The following tutorial explains how to do this: [https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=_YgCJb2FF-4]   # Upload both Excel Sheets to Confluence.    # Open the linked Excel worksheet via the Companion App, this will open the worksheet.   # Select the the cell(s) that where the link to the other worksheet was defined.    # Notice that the path to the other worksheet (in the user's machine)remains the same.    ## This presents an issue when multiple users who are using different machines are editing the same excel sheet, as Excel will be looking for the linked sheet in the defined path    h3. Expected Results    The path needs to be updated, or there should be a mechanism to find out where the linked worksheet is and update the path accordingly.   h3. Actual Results    The path to the other file does not change          Also, please note that if the linking between excel sheets happen while using the Companion App, the following type of paths might be seen as well    The difference between both types, is that the earlier one the linkage between the excel sheets happen before the files were uploaded to Confluence. While the second one happens when the process of linking one document to another happens while using the Companion App  h3. Workaround    there is no known workaround at the moment."""

summary: "When adding a date picker to a table cell in Firefox, the cells above and below are not selectable.  "
description: """h3. Issue Summary    When adding a Date Picker to a table cell on a pate, it is not possible to click the table cells above or below the the cell in Firefox.  h3. Environment  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a page in Confluence using Firefox   # Add a table with at least 3 rows   # Click a cell in the middle row and click “//“ to add the date picker   # Try clicking the table cell above or below the date picker cell.    h3. Expected Results    The cursor appears in the selected cell.  h3. Actual Results    The cells above and below the date picker are not selectable.    h3. Note  * Issue is more prominent on empty cells.  * The issue may behave differently when the column contains both dates and texts.  * The issue is that the editor seems to put the cursor on the last date picker on that column  ** Other observation is that the cursor is placed on the first line below the title  * The issue is less prominent when the last cell of that column is not a date.    h3. Workaround    The issue is not present in Chrome, so as a workaround use Chrome instead of Firefox to access Confluence.    It also appears to work in Firefox if you click towards the left side of the cell roughly where the cursor would appear in that cell."""
comments: "Same here on Version 6.15.2" [SEP] "Same here also on 6.15.2" [SEP] "I installed a User Agent Switcher add-on in Firefox and if I tell Confluence that my Firefox is Chrome the issue does not occur, so it seems to be something Confluence is doing instead of a bug in Firefox." [SEP] "+500 User affected" [SEP] "still reproducable in 7.1.0"

summary: "Subsequent user mention autocompletes do not work after removing the previous name"
description: """h3. Summary  After deleting an in-progress user mention, the autocomplete feature breaks for any subsequent mentions on the same line    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Edit a page, and type part of a user's name on an @mention   # Delete characters (backspace) until only the '@' remains  # Start typing another user's name on the mention      h3. Expected Results  The autocomplete feature should change accordingly and suggest mentions based on the new name that is being typed    h3. Actual Results    The autocomplete list of usernames does not update    h3. Workaround  Delete the '@' symbol as well and start a new mention, now the autocomplete feature will work again            """

summary: "Confluence cluster can become unresponsive when hazelcast must return information related to many labels"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  Confluence can become unresponsive, waiting on hazelcast to return information relating to labels. This issue is only seen when using the <USER>Better Archiving plugin on a Confluence cluster with ~70000 users.       h3. Environment    Confluence 6.6.9 Data Center with 4 nodes  <USER>Better Archiving plugin v 8.1.0  h3. Steps to Reproduce    The steps to reproduce are difficult to articulate, as the issue occurs while Confluence is under normal usage, albeit with a large number of users. [~<USER> and [~<EMAIL>] have both looked at this previously, and they determined that when the issue is occurring, Hazelcast appears to be overwhelmed. It is believed that the plugin is overwhelming hazelcast with additional requests to the labels cache. Confluence already does a labels fetch on page load (as indicated by the threads similarly waiting on the hazelcast cache for labels information after the plugin had been disabled), but the midori plugin is at least doubling the number of requests to the cache for the same information by checking labels for every web item that might be displayed (the list provided to getDisplayableItems).    It does seem like threads are being processed (moving from WAITING to RUNNABLE), but it's happening quite slowly, which could indicate Hazelcast is backed up or that some nodes holding label cache data are responsive while others aren't.     h3. Expected Results  Confluence should remain stable while the <USER>Better Archiving plugin is enabled, even when Confluence is under high load    h3. Actual Results  Here are some sample threads that show the behavior we see when the issue is occurring:    {code:borderStyle=solid}""""http-nio-8090-exec-216 url:/display/EXAM/Page+Name comments: "My name is <USER> I am the lead developer of the Better Content Archiving app.    First, let me explain why we need to check the labels that many times.    As you may know, the [Content Status Indicator|https://www.midori-global.com/products/better-content-archiving-for-confluence/data-center/documentation/#page-status-indicator] is displayed in the top of all pages that are in a global space which is not in the blacklist. That means, that when the page is opened, Confluence calls our condition that decides whether to display that page fragment. The condition gets the space in which the current page is located, then gets its labels and checks if ""noarchive"" is among those (which would mean that space is blacklisted). This call sequence can also be seen in your stack trace.    So the label checks cannot be ""saved"" and cannot be made less frequently.    One thing we could do is introducing some sort of caching mechanism, but this is counter-intuitive. If Confluence caches the labels and the cache is distri

summary: "Tomcat 9.0.14 and later breaks Emoticons"
description: """eh3. Issue Summary    If you upgrade tomcat to version 9.0.14 - 9.0.19, emoticons are broken. Both emoticons on pages, and the emoticon browser do not load.  h3. Issues observed:   # Insert Emoticon Menu   !screenshot-1.png|thumbnail!   # Emoticon on pages:   !screenshot-4.png|thumbnail!   # Global Permission page:   !screenshot-2.png|thumbnail!   # User icon link:   !screenshot-3.png|thumbnail!    h3. Environment   * Confluence 6.13.4, tomcat 9.0.14   * Confluence 6.15.3 tomcat 9.0.17    java -cp catalina.jar org.apache.catalina.util.ServerInfo    In 6.15.2 the output looks like:    Server version: Apache Tomcat/9.0.12   Server built: Sep 4 2018 22:13:41 UTC   Server number: 9.0.12.0*  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Upgrade tomcat to 9.0.14 following directions [here|https://confluence.<USER>confkb/how-to-upgrade-the-tomcat-container-for-confluence-336757062.html]   # Step 2   Edit a page and load the emoticon browser and observe that the images don't load  h3. Expected Results    Emoticon images should load  h3. Actual Results    emoticon images dont load    h3. Notes    If you inspect a HAR file, you will see that in tomcat 9.0.13 and earlier, the content-type for the image is image/svg+xml;charset=UTF-8   After upgrading to tomcat 9.0.14 the content-type is text/html;charset=UTF-8    I checked the [release notes|https://tomcat.apache.org/tomcat-9.0-doc/changelog.html] for Tomcat 9.0.14, and it looks like the issue is caused by this change: """"The default Servlet should not override a previously set content-type""""    (Optional - If Necessary)  h3. Workaround  {panel:title=Workaround Warning|borderStyle=solid|borderColor=#ff7f7f|titleBGColor=#ff7f7f|bgColor=#e5e5e5}  The provided workaround of downgrading Tomcat to 9.0.12 restores SVG functionality, but leaves the instance exposed to CONFSERVER-58106: Update Confluence Server to use Tomcat 9.0.16 to address CVE-2019-0199 (fixed in Confluence Server 7.0.1, 6.15.3, 6.14.4, 6.13.5, 6.6.14, 6.15.4) which is fixed in Tomcat 9.0.16. For this reason, anyone who has undertaken the downgrade of Tomcat to restore functionality should plan an upgrade to Confluence 6.15.4 (or later) as a matter of urgency.    Customers who have implemented header rewrite rules at the reverse proxy layer, and are continuing to run Tomcat 9.0.16 are protected from the above CVE.    If you are unsure of your Tomcat version, you can use the below command to verify which version of Tomcat you're running. The command should be run from the {{<Confluence-Install>/lib}} directory    {panel}    We have since released Confluence 6.15.4 which contains a fix for this issue. We recommend upgrading immediately if you're impacted by the issue.     We no longer recommend the below workaround to downgrade Tomcat to 9.0.12, but it is available if required:   # Download the Tomcat 9.0.12 lib directory  [^Tomcat-9-0-12-lib.zip] , attached to this issue (alternatively you can download and extract the [apache-tomcat-9.0.12.zip or apache-tomcat-9.0.12.tar.gz|[https://archive.apache.org/dist/tomcat/tomcat-9/v9.0.12/bin/]] from the Apache archives).   # Stop Confluence   # *Back up* your installation directory.   # Remove the contents of your existing {{<install-directory>/lib}} directory.   # Copy the contents of the downloaded lib directory to your existing {{<install-directory>/lib}} directory.   # Restart Confluence   # Go to *General Configuration* > *System Information* and confirm Confluence is now using Apache Tomcat 9.0.12.   # Edit a page and confirm that you can successfully insert an emoticon.    Your browser may have cached the broken assets, so perform a hard refresh to confirm the fix has worked. If you suspect your users may also have cached broken assets, make a small change to your site colour scheme (for example change a colour and then change it back)."""

summary: "Database Integrity Checker for Confluence"

The Database Integrity Checker should be implemented to ensure that all Confluence data is in a consistent state. Couple of examples can be checked and validated:  # Ancestor table corruption.  # Decorator table.  # Plugin manager state map.  # Permissions.  # System administrators group.  # Unmigrated macros."""

summary: "Confusing error message when trying to insert inline comment in macro content"

Issue Summary    If you create and try to save an inline comment after highlighting the text in a Summary field contained within a Jira Issues macro, Confluence returns the message """"Could not save the comment.""""  h3. Environment   * Occurred in Confluence version 6.15.1, verified in version 6.13.0 with Jira 7.13.0    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create an app link integration between Confluence and Jira.   # Create a page in Confluence.   # Add a Jira Issues macro into the page. When configuring the display of the macro, ensure that the Summary field is included.   # Save the page.   # View the page in Confluence.   # Highlight text located within the Summary column and click on inline comment icon to add a comment.   # Enter comment and save.    h3. Expected Results    You are prevented from using inline comments on the output of a macro and get an error message stating that.  h3. Actual Results    Confluence returns the message """"Could not save the comment.""""    HAR file shows that the following REST call returns an HTTP 500 error:    Application logs show the following error:    h3. Workaround    None. Not being able to highlight text and add an inline comment is by design."""
comments: "Hi [~nirmala.shivakumar] and [~<EMAIL>],    Note that Confluence does not allow adding inline comments on content rendered by macros by design. I'm changing this ticket to be about fixing the confusing error message you get when you try to do so rather than about implementing support for inline comments on macro output. Thanks for understanding, and sorry for the inconvenience.    <USER>  Senior <USER>Lead, Confluence Server  Atlassian"

summary: "HTML macro shrinks and doesn't allow text entry"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  While editing a page with an empty HTML macro in it, the blank area will shrink upon page load and not allowing text entry.    See sample screencast:  [^Recording #9.mp4]     h3. Environment  * Reproducible on Chrome and Safari  * While the HTML macro does shrink in Firefox, but the text area is still accepting text from the cursor  * Test on Confluence 6.14    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Create an empty page  # Insert empty HTML macro  # Save the page  # Edit the page again  # Click on the blank area inside the HTML macro to place the cursor in it  # Start typing    h3. Every time I do global administrative tasks in Confluence, I need to spend time to find where the desired function is located in the long list. summary: "Unknown macro body is discarded with Collaborative Editing on"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    When you save a page with an unknown macro that previously had body content, the content will be lost.   h3. Environment   * Collaborative Editing On    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a new page with a parent macro A   # Add some content to macro A   # Save the page   # Disable macro A   # Edit the page (can still see macro A and its content)   # Save the page again   # Enable macro A again    h3. Expected Results    Once macro A is re-enabled, we can see its original content again.   h3. Actual Results    All of the content of macro A is lost.   h3. Notes  h3. Workaround    No workaround. """

summary: "Having big number of unread notifications for a user can cause OOM"
description: """If a big number of Confluence pages have been generated in a short period of time, it can also create a bunch of unread notifications sitting in the DB. When the system tries to fetch those notifications for a user, it doesn't apply any pagination and can fail with OOM. This has been reproduced during load testing scenario (with 100K notifications in AO_9412A1_AONOTIFICATION table).    The problematic method is AONotificationDao#findAllUnread in myworkday plugin. Solution to the problem is to apply pagination to workday plugin in Confluence.    h3. Workaround  Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Inconsistency SDK API 6.6.0: getDownloadPath() vs. getUriReference() in ProfilePictureInfo  and SpaceLogo"
description: """For com.atlassian.confluence.user.actions.ProfilePictureInfo [getDownloadPath|https://docs.<USER>atlassian-confluence/6.6.0/com/atlassian/confluence/user/actions/ProfilePictureInfo.html#getDownloadPath--]() is deprecated and {{[getUriReference|https://docs.<USER>atlassian-confluence/6.6.0/com/atlassian/confluence/user/actions/ProfilePictureInfo.html#getUriReference--]()}} should be used.    {{[getUriReference|https://docs.<USER>atlassian-confluence/6.6.0/com/atlassian/confluence/user/actions/ProfilePictureInfo.html#getUriReference--]()}}  includes the context path, ie. '/confluence'.    In com.atlassian.confluence.spaces.SpaceLogo there is no deprecation of [getDownloadPath|https://docs.<USER>atlassian-confluence/6.6.0/com/atlassian/confluence/user/actions/ProfilePictureInfo.html#getDownloadPath--]() which does not include the context path (there is no {{[getUriReference|https://docs.<USER>atlassian-confluence/6.6.0/com/atlassian/confluence/user/actions/ProfilePictureInfo.html#getUriReference--]()}} in this class).    For Javascript templating I would love to have a consistent way to get image paths, either with or without context path (as I would prefix with AJS.params.contextPath in the latter case)."""

summary: "Send in-app notifications for security vulnerabilities"
description: """h3. Problem Definition  When security vulnerabilities occur, Atlassian sends emails to the primary technical contacts associated with a license. Emails can be lost in email filters or spam boxes, which can leave administrators unaware of a vulnerability.     h3. Suggested Solution  Provide an in-app notification to Confluence administrators to make them aware of any vulnerabilities (in addition to sending emails).     h3. Why this is important   It is important that administrators be informed of vulnerabilities as soon as possible. By adding this feature, we will provide an additional channel through which an administrator can learn about vulnerabilities.     """

summary: "'Ask a question' form from the Questions List macro does not preserve the original formatting"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  When asking a question through the 'Ask a question' button from the Questions List macro, the resulting question is not formatted properly and loses the original line breaks    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Add the Questions List macro to a Confluence page, with the 'Ask a question' button enabled   # Select the 'Ask a question' button to open the questions form   # Input some text in different lines, separated by single line breaks   # Publish the question     h3. Expected Results  The question will be published as written, with the original formatting    h3. Actual Results  All the text from different lines is combined onto a single line, losing all line breaks    h3. Workaround  Asking a question through the 'Ask question' form on the Confluence Questions home page instead preserves the formatting as written"""

summary: "Unable to manage apps appropriately after migrating from Atlassian Cloud"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Unable to install/uninstall apps appropriately after migrating from Atlassian Cloud.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # [Migrate from Confluence Cloud to Server|https://confluence.<USER>doc/migrate-from-confluence-cloud-to-server-298979321.html]  # Try to install app (e.g. Team Calendars)  # Try to uninstall the app    h3. Expected Results    The app can be installed/uninstalled without any problem.    h3. Actual Results    When you try to install Team Calendars, you will see the following error. (However, the app is actually installed.)     !screenshot-1.png|thumbnail!     You will see the following error in {{atlassian-confluence.log}}.        When you try to uninstall Team Calendars, the uninstallation seems to succeeded. However, if you restart Confluence instance, the app is still listed in _Manage apps_ page.    h3. Notes    This problem is caused by the data which is imported from Cloud.      {code}  <list>    <string>{"userKey":"","accountId":"anonymous","date":1553834285704,"i18nKey":"upm.auditLog.install.plugin.success","entryType":"PLUGIN_INSTALL","params":["Jira Ops integration","jira-ops-confluence-integration","1.0.4-AC"]}    </string>    ...  </list>  {code}    The strings are HTML escaped but they contained the following JSON data.    {code}  {""""userKey"""":"""""""",""""accountId"""":""""anonymous"""",""""date"""":1553834285704,""""i18nKey"""":""""upm.auditLog.install.plugin.success"""",""""entryType"""":""""PLUGIN_INSTALL"""",""""params"""":[""""Jira Ops integration"""",""""jira-ops-confluence-integration"""",""""1.0.4-AC""""]}  {code}    A key """"accountId"""" is only used in Confluence Cloud and it shouldn't exist in Confluence Server.    h3. Workaround    Please remove the key """"accountId"""" from the problematic record with SQL below. (This SQL is tested on PostgreSQL.)    {code:sql}  update bandana set bandanavalue = replace(bandanavalue, ',"accountId":"anonymous"', '') where bandanakey like 'com.atlassian.upm.log.PluginSettingsAuditLogService:log:upm_audit_log_v3';  {code}    If you've installed some apps on Confluence Cloud, you also need to execute the following SQL.    {code:sql}  update bandana set bandanavalue = replace(bandanavalue, ',"accountId":"Unknown user"', '') where bandanakey like 'com.atlassian.upm.log.PluginSettingsAuditLogService:log:upm_audit_log_v3';  {code}    (i) Please make sure to take a backup before modifying the DB record."""
comments: "Thank you for the write-up. Our team encountered the exact same issue, and updating the database to remove the unecessary accountId fixed it. " [SEP] "I have the exact same issue in Jira too.  Wich's the ""bandana table"" equal in Jira DB ?" [SEP] "Hi Alejandro,    Probably too late to help you out, but it might help future Googlers, here are the queries required for Jira:  {code:sql}  SELECT * FROM propertytext where id = (SELECT id  FROM propertyentry WHERE property_key = 'com.atlassian.upm.log.PluginSettingsAuditLogService:log:upm_audit_log_v3');  {code}   In this result you can see the whole audit log, including the accountId entries that are incorrect on cloud:  {code:java}    id   | propertyvalue                                                                                                                            -------+----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------   10337 | #java.util.List

summary: "SpaceManager.getAllSpaces(SpacesQuery) causes ORA-01795 error"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  SpaceManager.getAllSpaces(SpacesQuery) causes ORA-01795 error if the {{spaceKeys}} contain more than 1000 entries.    h3. Environment  * Oracle 11g    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Calling the following where 'spaceKeys' contain > 1000 entries will cause the issue on an instance running Oracle 11g for example.       h3. Expected Results  The spaceQuery splits the SQL so as to not overflow the maximum allowed entries - I get a list of spaces as result without an error.    h3. Actual Results  Causes {{ORA-01795}} database error since Oracle doesn't support more than 1000 entries in a SQL query.    h3. Notes  * Error in question as defined by Oracle Docs: https://docs.oracle.com/cd/B10501_01/server.920/a96525/e1500.htm#1001274  * Similar confluence tickets with the same errors found:  ** https://jira.<USER>browse/CONFSERVER-43504  ** https://jira.<USER>browse/CONFSERVER-44260  ** https://jira.<USER>browse/CONFSERVER-22985 (fixed)  ** https://jira.<USER>browse/CONFSERVER-42654    h3. Workaround  Programmatically split the query in partitions to avoid going over the enforced maximum entries by Oracle."""

summary: "Team Calendars won't load high volume of Jira Issues"
description: """h3. Summary      * In Team Calendars, if you try to create a JQL filter showing over 700 Jira Issues, they won't load in the calendar even though the response shows as *{""""success"""": true}*    h3. Environment   * Jira Project with over 700 issues    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # In Team Calendars, select *Add Event*  ## Select *Jira Issue Dates* in the *Event Type* field  ## Add a name to it  ## In the *Display* field, select the *JQL* option and add a filter that would return over 700 entries. In my local test instance, the filter was:    ## Check the *Issue Created Date* check box  ## Click on the *OK* button    h3. Expected Results      * The calendar will load the issues as events in the calendar    h3. Actual Results     * No issues are shown and, upon checking the console, we can see that the _events.<USER> request will take a while to load and its response will be only *{""""success"""": true}*    h3. Notes     * This issue is very intermittent since I was able some times to display the required information.    h3. Workaround   N/A"""
comments: "In Confluence 7.4.1 this issue is very consistent for our users."

summary: "Granular control over permissions Space admins can grant"
description: """h3. Problem Definition    Space admins are giving other individual users admin privileges within spaces they control.    Permissions in this environment are managed using an automated tool (AGS) and it is not desired to grant permissions to individual users on the root of the space without approval or potentially at all.  h3. Suggested Solution    The desired outcome is to allow the Global admin to restrict a Space admin's ability to grant the """"admin"""" privilege of a space to individual users while still allowing the space admin to grant other permissions to individual users.    Stated differently, the desired ability is for the Global admin to remove the """"admin"""" privilege as an option for Space admin's to select. The Global admin would be the only one who can grant admin privilege at the space-level   h3. Why this is important    Global admins should have the ability to determine who can fill a space admin role, as individual space admins potentially give this right to users that should not have it.   The unauthorized admins then have rights not controlled via automation.  h3. Workaround    As a workaround, the Confluence administrator can hide the UI elements that are used to assign/remove the Space Admin permissions in the Space Tools page.   Note that this will not remove the functionality, but just hide the element so that users can't see these and, therefore, change its value.   The elements will be hidden from all users, also applying to Confluence administrators.   # Go to *Cog icon* > *General configuration* > *Stylesheet* to open the [_Global Stylesheet_|https://confluence.<USER>doc/styling-confluence-with-css-166528400.html] admin page.   # Click on the _Edit_ button, add the code below and then _Save_ it.   ** You may use any combination of the following options to hide one or multiple elements.      (!) If anyone needs to set the Space Admin permissions, then the <USER>must to remove this code from Confluence stylesheet."""
comments: "Hello Team,     On our Confluence environment, we are seeing several space admins giving individual permissions to other users as admins on the space.    We need a way on Confluence to block space admins to grant admins permissions to other users?    As we manage permissions using an automated tool we do not want to grant permissions to individual users on the root of the space.    We appreciate the effort here on this new feature. Thanks!" [SEP] "[~<USER> please see this possible workaround which may be of use to you:  h3. Workaround  As a workaround, the Confluence administrator can hide the UI elements that are used to assign/remove the Space Admin permissions in the Space Tools page.  Note that this will not remove the functionality, but just hide the element so that users can't see these and, therefore, change its value.  The elements will be hidden from all users, also applying to Confluence administrators.    # Go to *Cog icon* > *General configuration* > *Stylesheet* to open the [_Global Stylesheet_

summary: "Domain Name field with a warning won't change to Exact Match in Confluence's whitelist"
description: """h3. Summary   * When you whitelist an URL with a trail, it automatically marks the *Type* field as an *Exact Match*. If you change it to *Domain Name*, a warning sign is shown and you are not able to change it back to *Exact Match* since Confluence keeps pulling automatically the *Domain Name* option with a warning sign.    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Access your *Confluence Administration >> Whitelist*   # In the *Expression* field, add an URL with a trailing slash, for example, [https://www.google.com/] . The *Type* field will automatically show *Exact Match*.   # Change the *Type* field to *Domain Name*. A warning sign will show up.   # Change the *Type* field back to *Exact Match*.    h3. Expected Results   * The *Type* field is changed to *Exact Match*.    h3. Actual Results   * Confluence keeps pulling automatically the *Domain Name* option with a warning sign instead of changing it to the *Exact Match* option.    h3. Notes   * If you try to add the URL with the warning sign, it will freeze and won't let you change it until you refresh the page.   * The warning sign doesn't explain what is the issue with the URL added.    h3. Workaround   * Remove the trailing slash from the URL and save it as a domain name."""

summary: "Keep Excel hidden columns/rows hidden in Confluence"

Why this is important       * For some customers it is important and relevant to keep some columns/rows of their Excel files hidden and have it reflected in Confluence using the Atlassian Companion app.    h3. Workaround   N/A"""

summary: "Performance Issues on Confluence caused by Label related queries"
description: """h2. Problem Summary  Performance bottlenecks can be triggered by Label related queries on large Confluence instances.    h2. Problem Details  Confluence provides users with a handful of Label related macros. These macros can be used to find the most popular labels in a space, find recently used macros, to get a comprehensive report of pages that have specific labels and so goes on.     The main action behind such functionalities consists of the application going to the database to retrieve a list of labels based on the input provided by the macros. This is exactly where the problem happens, given the nature of the queries. Symptoms observed have been:  * Overall application slowness.  * Increased response time on the database size due to what seems to be a huge amount of data returned by the Label queries.  * Stuck threads waiting to interact with the database - This happens because the Label related threads make the database busy.  * Need to restart the application to make it usable once again.    h4. Thread Dumps  Thread Dumps generated during situations like the above mentioned one show the application stuck for long time executing an Hibernate Statement in the database. The statement comes from the _findMostPopular_ method:      h4. Database Logs  As far as the database logging goes, there are queries that correlates with the above thread dump (they are triggered by _findMostPopular_ method) and that takes a long time to be executed - Usually something in between 5 to 10 minutes:      Another example would be:      Also, the following query represents yet another example:      h2. Suggested Root Cause  Depending on the size of the Content Table, above queries can be quite expensive and take a long time to run. To know the size of one's instance, run the following query:      Tests show that instances with 15M entries in the Content table can take up to 90s to have the above queries returning results, regardless of indexes. Therefore, the problem is related to the scalability of the Label logic in Confluence.    h2. Which action can trigger such queries?  * Through a call to _/labels/autocompletelabel.action_ - This is a call to retrieve a list of labels to auto complete an user input when one is adding labels to a page. This call will only jeopardise the application health if there's no Contend ID in the URL - For example, when adding labels to an unpublished page where Content ID is still not referenced in the URL.  * When using the _Popular Labels_ Macro without a Space Key defined on it:   !Screen Shot 2019-05-15 at 17.53.32.png|thumbnail!     h2. Workaround  There are a few things we can do to mitigate the impact of this problem:  # To block calls to __/labels/autocompletelabel.action__ in a Proxy/Tomcat level. Downside is that auto-completion of labels won't work.  # To enforce users to define a Space Key when adding the _Popular Labels_ Macro.    *Extra:*  In order to know which pages have the _Popular Labels_ Macro without a Space Key set to it, one can run the query below - It has been tested in PostgreSQL and there may be a need to tweak it a little bit for other database flavours:      With the above list in hands, one can go to each page and edit the Macro to have a Space Key on it to workaround the performance issue."""

summary: "Use AdoptOpenJDK Slim on Alpine Linux Docker images in order to decrease the image size"
description: """Currently, the Alpine Linux Docker images are about the same size of the Ubuntu images, sometimes slightly bigger. As the value of Alpine is in great part its lower footprint, having bigger Alpine images somewhat defeats their purpose.     The Dockerfile used by Atlassian for Alpine and Ubuntu respectively use:  Alpine: FROM adoptopenjdk/openjdk8:alpine  Ubuntu: FROM adoptopenjdk/openjdk8:slim    The 'slim' version of adoptopenjdk is important. There is a 'slim' version for Alpine but Atlassian does not seem to use it:  adoptopenjdk/openjdk8:alpine-slim    I think this is valuable improvement to the Alpine images for Confluence (saves 150M)."""
comments: "We are preparing to deploy Confluence in Kubernetes and I need to choose which image to use.    I normally prefer the smaller image, which is usually Alpine.  However, the Alpine image is currently bigger than the Ubuntu image as described in this issue.    But I don't want to deploy the Ubuntu image now to later choose to switch to Alpine (since supporting Alpine is slightly different than Ubuntu).  So, I will use Alpine right way, but I hope that the new Docker image using Alpine can use adoptopenjdk/openjdk8:alpine-slim so that it will reduce the footprint in my infrastructures.    Thank you     " [SEP] "I have been working with openjdk:8-alpine to develop light-weight Atlassian containers with thus far, that may be an effective alternative, though I have not as yet implemented Jira 8.1.0" [SEP] "We started shipping our images based on {{adoptopenjdk/openjdk8:slim}} a few months back so I'll close this as resolved." summary: "Searching for user Willsomething by using Will will return all users up to 10 in permission list."
description: """h3. Issue Summary  Searching for user Willsomething by using Will will return all users up to 10 in the permission list.    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create users Test1, Test2, Willsomething1, Willsomething2 as username   # Search for user """"Will"""" in Global permissions and observe the suggestions.    h3. Expected Results  # Only and both Willsomething1, Willsomething2 will be visible in the list    h3. Actual Results  All users (up to functionality limit of 10) will be visible. In this case:   Test1, Test2, Willsomething1, Willsomething2    h3. Notes  The issue is not replicable with for example names """"Gojko"""" or """"Gojjko"""" so it appears to be related to 4 letters tokenization of the word """"Will""""      h3. Workaround  Currently, there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "As a Confluence administrator I would like to know if any App would be affected when disabling another App or a module of another App"
description: """h3. Problem Definition  When disabling a Confluence App (aka plugin or add-on), Confluence will automatically disable any other App that has a strong dependency on the former (or a module of it) with no warning messag in the UI.  For example, when disabling _Confluence Jira Plugin_, _confluence-software-blueprints_ will be disabled because the latter has a strong dependency on the {{jqlHelper}} module of the former.    h3. Suggested Solution  Before disabling an App, Confluence should check if any other has a dependency on it.  If there's any, then Confluence should advise that disabling _App A_ will cause _App B_ to be disabled.    h3. Why this is important  There's no clear definition on Apps' dependency on the Confluence UI when administering Apps.  That means Confluence admin might take an action without knowing the full consequences of it.     h3. Workaround   _no workaround so far_"""

summary: "Confluence Apps and modules automatically disabled does not create an entry in the audit log"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  In Confluence [Audit log|https://confluence.<USER>doc/audit-log-829076528.html] documentation it says that enabling or disabling Apps actions are recorded in the Audit log.  When an App is automatically disabled, because of a dependency for example, this is not recorded in the audit log.    h3. Environment  This was validated on Confluence Server *6.6.12* and *6.13.2*.  The test below uses  _Confluence Mobile App Plugin_ just as an example. This will affect other Apps as well.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Install a Confluence vanilla instance.  # Make sure audit log is enabled (it is by default).  # Disable _Confluence Mobile App Plugin_.  # As a result, _Workbox - Host Plugin_ is also disabled.  # Go to the _Audit log_ admin UI.    h3. Expected Results  The following entries are logged:   # Add-on disabled: Confluence Mobile Web Plugin.   # Add-on disabled: Workbox - Host Plugin.    h3. Actual Results  Only the action related to the App manually disabled is recorded -- in the example, _Confluence Mobile Web Plugin_.  There's no entry saying the _Workbox - Host Plugin_ was disabled as a result of this action.    h3. Additional Notes  In addition to the example above, an event is not recorded in the audit logs when an App is automatically disabled because of any internal problem within Confluence (or the App itself).    h3. Workaround  _Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available_"""

summary: "When clicking on Log in button provided by Questions for Confluence, there is an Invalid Redirect error"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    When viewing Questions for Confluence as an anonymous or logged out user, you are presented with an option to Log In to Confluence after clicking """"Ask a Question"""".  Clicking on this brings you to a login page at {{/login.action?os_destination=%252Fquestions}}.  Once you attempt to login, you receive a HTTP Status 400 error with the following details:    {quote}HTTP Status 400 – Bad Request    Type Status Report    Message Invalid URI: noSlash    Description The server cannot or will not process the request due to something that is perceived to be a client error (e.g., malformed request syntax, invalid request message framing, or deceptive request routing).{quote}    This is likely because of the """"25"""" in the URL.  If you browse to {{/login.action?os_destination=%2Fquestions}} you can login without issue.    h3. Environment    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Log out of Confluence  # Click on Questions in the top navigation bar  # Click on Ask a Question  # Click on Log In  # Attempt to Log in    h3. Expected Results    You are logged in and able to write your Question.    h3. Actual Results    You are presented with the HTTP Status 400 error.    h3. Workaround    The current workaround is to log in using the standard Confluence log in button, or remove the 25 from the URL before logging in, like:    {{confluenceurl/login.action?os_destination=%2Fquestions}}"""

summary: "Support for Fixed height of rows in Tables"
description: """When creating Tables with a few rows, it would be nice to be able to set the 'Fixed height' for the row just like the 'Fixed width' for the column.    h4. Current Setting  If you enter a long content in the box, the row height is automatically adjusted to the texts entered.   !screenshot-1.png|thumbnail!     h4. Expected setting  If the box has a long content, we can use the scroll instead of adjusting the row height according to the content."""

summary: "Enabling partial modules is not effective if plugin is enabled later in Universal Plugin Manager"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Suppose the following operation in Universal Plugin Manager--   # Enable partial modules in any plugin   # Enable any other Plugin    Then the first modules are reverted to disabled state in BANDANA table even though GUI displays wrongly as it is still enabled.   h3. Environment    Data center with two nodes  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Login to Confluence   # Go to Universal Plugin Manager   # Enable HTML module in Confluence HTML Macros   # Enable Confluence Mail Archiving Plugin    h3. Expected Results    The modules below are enabled in GUI of Universal Plugin Manager   * HTML module in Confluence HTML Macros   * Confluence Mail Archiving Plugin    Besides, the query result    includes following values.  {code:none}  <entry>                                                                                                                                               <string>com.atlassian.confluence.plugins.confluence-mail-archiving</string>                                                             <boolean>true</boolean>                                                                                                                       </entry>                                                                                                                                           h3. Actual Results    The modules below are enabled in GUI of Universal Plugin Manager   * HTML module in Confluence HTML Macros   * Confluence Mail Archiving Plugin    However the above query result includes *only* below.  {code:none}  <entry>                                                                                                                                               <string>com.atlassian.confluence.plugins.confluence-mail-archiving</string>                                                             <boolean>true</boolean>                                                                                                                       </entry>                                                                                                                                         {code}  i.e. The entry for {{confluence.macros.html:html}} was lost.  h3. Workaround    Enable the modules manually from Universal Plugin Manager after restarting Confluence."""

summary: "Attachments macro could have the ""Preview / View / Properties / Delete"" options above the ""Version History"""
description: """h3. Problem Definition    When using Attachments macro in Confluence for attachments with a lot of versions, we to have to scroll all the way down for the options to Preview / View / Properties / Delete, see screenshots below:     !Screen Shot 2019-04-05 at 11.32.05.png|thumbnail!    !Screen Shot 2019-04-05 at 11.32.15.png|thumbnail!     h3. Suggested Solution    It would make it a lot easier if those buttons were displayed above the Version History list.    h3. Why this is important     When attachments have a lot of previous versions it is not practical to have to scroll all the way down to be able to Preview, View or Delete the attachment.  The above screenshots I show 50 versions of a file but in many other cases there can be a lot more."""

summary: "Improve installer to detect fonts against rpm instead of yum."
description: """Suggestion is that the script in the Confluence install program use rpm instead of yum to detect the fontconfig lib in the RedHat branch of the fontconfig detection script as 'rpm -q fontconfig' could be used instead of 'yum list installed fontconfig' which is used today, and when using rpm the CIS umask enhancements does not hinder the query.    The problem is that when CIS is in play the umask for root is set to 077 instead of as usual 022, and this leads to when packages are installed with yum the yum specific files (and most likely some others too) ends up having access permissions set so unprivileged users cannot use yum to detect if a package is installed. If rpm is used to detect the package there is no problem.    """
comments: "For anyone else encountering this problem, the easy workaround is to install from the tar package instead."

It is unintuitive for the end user that one searchbox suggestions does not match the other searchbox from the sidebar-search.    Please update livesearch to include option to show full search result, just like the search bar. """

summary: "Cloud Migration Assistant app shows up in the list of apps even after being uninstalled"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Cloud Migration Assistant app shows up in the list of user installed apps even after being uninstalled from the UI  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Go to  !https://confluence.<USER>support/files/790796813/947856965/1/1522298459209/cog-new.png|width=16!  ** > Manage Apps   # Choose *User Installed* from the drop-down  # Uninstall Cloud Migration Assistant app, a message would pop-up stating the app has been uninstalled     !Screen Shot 2019-04-05 at 12.16.42 PM.png|thumbnail!     h3. Expected Results    Cloud Migration Assistant app is uninstalled completely  h3. Actual Results    After each restart Cloud Migration Assistant app shows up in the list again with the below     !Screen Shot 2019-04-05 at 12.35.27 PM.png|thumbnail!     h3. Notes    *  It would also throw the below error in the logs:  {code}  2019-03-26 17:04:43,015 ERROR [Caesium-1-3] [migration.agent.queue.QueueBroker] error An error occurred when getting the next batch for consumer type: CONFLUENCE_IMPORT. Message: org.hibernate.tool.schema.spi.SchemaManagementException: Schema-validation: missing column [scheduleDate] in table [MIG_PLAN]  com.atlassian.util.concurrent.LazyReference$InitializationException: org.hibernate.tool.schema.spi.SchemaManagementException: Schema-validation: missing column [scheduleDate] in table [MIG_PLAN]          at com.atlassian.util.concurrent.LazyReference.getInterruptibly(LazyReference.java:149)          at com.atlassian.util.concurrent.LazyReference.get(LazyReference.java:112)  {code}    * The only way to get around this log spam is to drop the tables associated with Migration Assistant and clear caches as per the [documentation|https://confluence.<USER>confkb/how-to-clear-confluence-plugins-cache-297664846.html]:  {code}  drop table MIG_ANALYTICS_EVENT;  drop table MIG_ATTACHMENT;  drop table MIG_SEQUENCES;  drop table MIG_STATS;  drop table MIG_STEP;  drop table MIG_TASK;  drop table MIG_WORK_ITEM;  drop table MIG_PLAN;  drop table MIG_CLOUD_SITE;  drop table MIG_DB_CHANGELOG;  drop table MIG_DB_CHANGELOG_LOCK;  {code}    h3. Workaround    Currently, there is no known workaround wholistically as far as the UI is concerned but if we check the status of the app from the database you will notice that the app is actually uninstalled    *DB Query*  {code:sql}  select PLUGINDATAID, PLUGINKEY, FILENAME, LASTMODDATE from PLUGINDATA;  {code}"""

summary:
"
get added after the Excerpt Macros once the page is saved upon upgrade"
description:
"""h3. Summary When placing macros back to back the editor in a page in Confluence 5.10.x, the preview and the saved mode looks fine. However, upgrading it to Confluence 6.x version, the page looks fine in preview mode but adds in extra space after the *Excerpt Macro* upon saving the page. h3. Steps to Reproduce # Create a page in Confluence 5.10.x # Insert an *Excerpt macro* one after another (adding some notes in it) # Save the page # The page looks fine in both edit and preview mode. # Upgrade the Confluence 5.10.x to Confluence 6.x # Edit the page with the *Excerpt macro* (looks fine in edit mode) # Save the page (extra line added after the *Excerpt macro*) # When you go back to edit mode again, you will see the extra line now. h3. Expected Results There should not be extra lines after the *Excerpt macro* !screenshot-1.png|thumbnail! !screenshot-2.png|thumbnail! h3. Actual Results There is an extra line added after *Excerpt macro* (
) !screenshot-3.png|thumbnail! !screenshot-4.png|thumbnail! h3. Workaround # Remove the extra space manually from the Edit mode and save the Page again. # Remove the
after the *Excerpt Macro* in the 'Source Editor'"""

summary: "Table selection not working in IE11 and Edge using mouse or keyboard "
description: """h4. Issue summary  # When using IE 11, multiple rows and columns of a table cannot be selected using the mouse or keyboard.  # Issue does not persist on Firefox and Chrome  # Issue does not persist on Confluence 6.11 and Confluence 6.6.12    h4. Steps to replicate in IE 11  # Create a table in a page, with a few rows and columns.  # Try selecting a few of the rows and columns using the mouse, and applying a background color change.  ** The change is only applied to the last cell  # Try selecting a few of the rows and columns using the keyboard (Shift + Arrow key), and applying a color change  ** The change is only applied to the first cell  # The color change is just an example, and all actions done by the selection has the same issue.  # Issue persist with collaborative editing on and off.    h4. Current behavior  # Unable to select multiple cells of a table in IE11 and Edge    h4. Expected behavior   # Be able to select multiple cells of table in IE11 and Edge as we could in Firefox and Chrome    h4. Workaround  # No known workaround."""
comments: "Is Version 6.14.2 affected, also?" [SEP] "Yes, it is." [SEP] "6.15.2 is affected, too." [SEP] "6.15.3 and 6.15.4 are also affected." [SEP] "I have the same issue in Confluence 7.4.0 with Edge.   Using the Keyboard I can select multiple cells (except for the 1st row)   Using the mouse cell selection does not work at all.    It works fine in Firefox.    Before our update it worked with confluence 6.13.4 and Edge too.         Edit: I just updatet to the Chromium based Edge browser, and there Table selection works."

summary: "Customer cannot pull Jira (8.0) issue dates events to Team Calendars"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  When the user has Confluence that integrates with JIRA 8, the user will not be able to pull _Jira Issue Dates_ events into team calendars. Additionally other bugs have similar findings:   [CONFSERVER-58025|https://jira.<USER>browse/CONFSERVER-58025]  [CONFSERVER-57960|https://jira.<USER>browse/CONFSERVER-57960]  [JRASERVER-68903|https://jira.<USER>browse/JRASERVER-68903] - This is likely the root cause of the problem    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Install JIRA 8   # Create Application link with JIRA 8   ## Select (shared users) to have OAUTH (impersonation)   # Install Team Calendar   # Add a JIRA issue date event    h3. Expected Results  The user is able to create the JIRA event, select issue due date and the events would then appear on the calendar on the appropriate date.    h3. Actual Results  The user is able to create the JIRA (8.0.0) Issue Event, but Jira issues with due dates never show up on the calendar. In Jira 7 this works as expected.    h3. Workaround   Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Adding a page comment may take a long time to complete"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  On Confluence, when creating a new page comment it will notify page watchers about the added comment.  If the page has too many watchers (thousands), then adding the new comment may take a long time to complete.  This also occurs if you have few watchers (dozens) and nested groups is enabled and in use.    h3. Environment  This was validated on Confluence Server 6.6.8, but might impact Data Center deployments and more recent versions.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Install a Confluence vanilla instance.  # Add 5,000 users to this instance.  # Create a sample page and add thousands of users as watchers to this page.   #* For example, test it with 2k and 4k watchers.  # Add a comment to the page.    h3. Expected Results  Even with a large number of watchers, adding a comment should instantly save the comment and release the UI to the users so they can continue to navigate on Confluence.    h3. Actual Results  It may take several seconds to save the comment and release the UI to the user and, depending on the configuration, the operation will timeout, although it will still continue on the backend giving the perception to the user that it failed.  During the tests, it took ~30s to save the comment with 2k watchers and ~150s with 4k.     h3. Notes  If we take a thread dump while the operation is still running on the UI, the below stack trace may be captured:      The {{saveComment}} method publishes an event about the new comment added to the page.  This is when the {{workbox}} plugin code comes into play with the {{onCommentCreatedEvent}} method, which will then run the entire process to check if the watcher has the necessary permissions before getting the notification.  This permission check is costly and can be exasperated if nested groups are in use, creating the issue even with dozen of watchers.    As a good user experience practice, Confluence should perform all the validations related to the notification in a separated thread, quickly saving the comment and releasing the UI to the user.    h3. Workaround  _none so far_"""

summary: "Pasting a table while using Edge from Windows 10 will only paste the text"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Unable to copy and paste the tables on Windows 10 when using Edge browser  h3. Environment   * Microsoft Windows 10   * Microsoft Edge   * Confluence 6.14.1    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a table on blank page   # Select all cells in a table   # Right-click on the table and choose *Copy*   # On a separate tab, open a new page   # Right-click and choose *Paste*    h3. Expected Results   * Table is pasted with all its structure preserved    h3. Actual Results   * Only table contents are pasted, the table is not included    h3. Notes    Not observed on 6.9.1  Same observed if using the keyboard shortcut  Including content outside of a table does not work  h3. Workaround    The issue is not observed on Mac OS  The issue is not observed on Chrome regardless of OS  Using the Table control menu in confluence to copy and paste rows/columns while the table is selected:   # Select whole table   # In the top bar click *Copy table row* or Ctrl+Shift+C   # !copy.png|thumbnail!   # In new space and page, insert a small one cell table to bring up the Table Management menu   # Then paste your table using *Paste table row* or Ctrl+Shift+V   # !paste.png|thumbnail!   # You might need to delete that one small cell you created   # Should work the same with copying and pasting columns"""

summary: "Possible to configure the length of the page title that could be displayed in the insert search box"
description: """h3. Summary  When trying to use the search for link function the long page titles go off the screen and do not wrap around. This makes choosing a link impossible and requires the customer to either guess or manually load the page they need as a separate page then copy the link and apply. Hover the page title also does not give the full title of the page.    h3. Actual Result  The page title has been truncated, and it is not possible to identify which page should be inserted in the case below  !manual.jpg|width=500!     h3. Expected Result  * Able to configure the length of the title should be displayed  * Hover the page title should be able to show the full title of the page    h3. Workaround  Navigate to the page then copy the link and apply"""

summary: "Confluence Search Feature Update - Unnecessary to type out entire word of a title of a specific content (page/attachment)"
description: """Hello!    I love Confluence, but in a recent update, while searching for a specific page title, we have to type out a whole word for the system to search for it properly whereas before, we could merely begin typing the first few letters of a page name and it would populate that in the search.     For example: I now have to type in """"Washington"""" before I can find our page called """"Washington County, Oregon"""".     Before the update, I could merely type in """"wash"""" and that page would come up as a searchable page.          I understand this may free any memory/speed up, but it really helps us out to not have to type out the full word. Some pages we have on here have some complicated names and we would love to not have to type out a long winded word/phrase.    h4. Workaround  Adding a wildcard * at the end of the search term, For example *wash** helps to obtain the search result for incomplete search term.    h4. Notes  It will be more helpful if we can introduce a message in the search result to inform users that truncation with * is required. Example message:  {quote}  Nothing found? Try truncation with *, e.g. wash*  {quote}"""

summary: "Jira macros does not honour cursor position in table cells"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  When a user inserts a JIRA issue into a table cell via *either*:  * The macro browser menu item   * Shortcut key (CTRL+Shift+J)     The Jira macro will get placed after the last JIRA issue that user inserted on the page and not in cursor position.    h3. Environment  * Confluence 6.14  * Replicable on multiple browsers    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Insert a 2x2 table into a page  # Place cursor at row 1 column 1  # Use the shortcut key (CTRL+Shift+J) to insert a *single* Jira issue  # Place cursor at row 1 column 2  # Use the shortcut key (CTRL+Shift+J) to insert another *single* Jira issue    h3. Screencast   [^Recording #8.mp4]     h3. Expected Results  The second Jira macro will place at row 1 column 2    h3. Actual Results  The second Jira macro will place at row 1 column 1    h3. Workaround  * Use the shortcut key by typing in """"{"""" to bring up the macro browser, type in """"Jira"""" to bring up the same Jira macro pop up screen to insert the Jira issue.  * Inserts a random character at the cursor position and then inserts the Jira macro (with any method)"""
comments: "Customer reporting this issue in Confluence Server 6.14.3 (both Edge and Chrome) and I've seen it in Chrome." [SEP] "I have a customer excerpering this issue in 6.15.2. Also in chrome and edge." [SEP] "I am experiencing this issue in version 6.15.7 when using the menu or shortcut keys (noted above). summary: "Confluence - Path traversal vulnerability - CVE-2019-3398"
description: """Confluence Server and Data Center had a path traversal vulnerability in the downloadallattachments resource. A remote attacker who has permission to add attachments to pages and / or blogs or to create a new space or a personal space or who has 'Admin' permissions for a space can exploit this path traversal vulnerability to write files to arbitrary locations which can lead to remote code execution on systems that run a vulnerable version of Confluence Server or Data Center.     *Affected versions:*  * All versions of Confluence Server from 2.0.0 before 6.6.13 (the fixed version for 6.6.x), from 6.7.0 before 6.12.4 (the fixed version for 6.12.x), from 6.13.0 before 6.13.4 (the fixed version for 6.13.x), from 6.14.0 before 6.14.3 (the fixed version for 6.14.x), and from 6.15.0 before 6.15.2  are affected by this vulnerability.     *Fix:*   * Confluence Server and Data Center versions 6.15.2 is available for download from [https://www.<USER>software/confluence/download].   * Confluence Server and Data Center versions 6.14.3 is available for download from [https://www.<USER>software/confluence/download-archives].   * Confluence Server and Data Center versions 6.13.4 is available for download from [https://www.<USER>software/confluence/download-archives].   * Confluence Server and Data Center versions 6.12.4 is available for download from [https://www.<USER>software/confluence/download-archives].   * Confluence Server and Data Center versions 6.6.13 is available for download from [https://www.<USER>software/confluence/download-archives].      For additional details, see the [full advisory|https://confluence.<USER>x/d5e8OQ]."""

summary: "Copied Page (including child) which has attachments includes absolute reference to the page itself"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    If you copy a page including child page/pages to any (the same or another) space, the destination page will contain absolute references to its page    h3. Steps to Reproduce    # Create a space  # Create a new parent page and a child page  # Add an image each to both the pages  # While on the parent page, click """"Copy"""", copy to another space, Include the child page as well  # Check the Storage format of the Parent page from the destination location/new space where the page was copied to    Reference : [Confluence-Storage-Format|https://confluence.<USER>doc/confluence-storage-format-790796544.html]    h3. Expected Results    Storage Format of the page from Source:         Storage Format of the page from Destination:         h3. Actual Results    Storage Format of the page from Source:         Storage Format of the page from Destination (New Space):         h3. Workaround    One way to get around this is to edit and re-publish each affected page in the new space before archiving it, as the storage format will be re-marshaled on the update and the unnecessary absolute references will be collapsed    """

summary: "Confluence should persist the view state in Team Calendars on calendar specific views"
description: """h3. Problem Definition    Event type selections on Team Calendars does not persist on calendar specific views after refreshing the page, or navigating away and returning.  h5. Repro Steps   # Go to a calendar page [https://base-url/display/SPACEKEY/calendars] - This will give you a conglomerate of all the calendars you have added to the space calendars.   # Click on one of the calendars within that space- You will directed to the calendar-specific view with a url like this   [https://base-url/display/SPACEKEY/calendar/xd7fd1cx-x7fr-3609-bexcb-d9b445fbdfz3?calendarName=Test%20Cal]   # From this calendar-specific view, uncheck an event type to remove those from the display   # Refresh the page, the selection does not persist (This is the problem), requiring you to unselect the Event Types again.    Screenshot:    !image-2019-03-27-17-19-35-178.png!  h3. Workaround    Instead of using the Calendar-specific view, utilize the My Calendars page, or the Space Calendars page. These views persist the view state after a refresh of the page.    For example, go to [https://base-url/calendar/mycalendar.action] or [https://base-url/display/SPACEKEY/calendars] and add    """"Your Calendar"""" or any calendar, and you will be able to maintain the selected and unselected options across a page refresh."""

summary: "As a Confluence administrator I would like to automatically create a Personal Space on user's first login"
description: """h3. Problem Definition  Personal spaces can only be created when the user follows the steps in [Create your personal space|https://confluence.<USER>doc/create-your-personal-space-777010869.html].  Currently, there's no option to automatically create a Personal Space for every user in Confluence that has the _Personal Space_ permissions.    h3. Suggested Solution  If the user has the _Personal Space_ [global permission|https://confluence.<USER>doc/global-permissions-overview-138709.html], create a personal space on the first login.  It could even be an opt-out option on the on-boarding plugin.    h3. Workaround   The Confluence administrator can create the personal space of a user by running the [{{addPersonalSpace}} RPC API method|https://developer.<USER>server/confluence/remote-confluence-methods/#management].  Below is an example of how to run that method using {{curl}}.    (!) Note that this RPC API method bypasses _Personal Space_ [global permission|https://confluence.<USER>doc/global-permissions-overview-138709.html], so it will create the space even if the target user doesn't have it.    With this, the admin can create an automation to add a Personal Space for new users after validating this user has the _Personal Space_ [global permission|https://confluence.<USER>doc/global-permissions-overview-138709.html]."""

summary: "As a Confluence administrator I would like that a default Space is not selected when a user clicks on the Create from template button"
description: """h3. Problem Definition  When the Confluence user doesn't have a Personal Space, the _Create from template_ button will automatically determine a target Space, which will be set as a default option in the _Select Space_ drop-down list. This Space might be randomly chosen from the user perspective.  This may create confusion, since some users might not have the understanding where the page is being placed and save pages in Spaces they have the create content permissions, but don't usually curate content.  This only occurs when the user is *not* accessing a page within a Space. That means it happens on dashboard pages, view profile, and other similar pages.    h3. Suggested Solution  *When accessing non Space pages*:  When a user clicks on the _Create from template_ button it will open the _Create_ window so the user can choose the target space and the template of the new page.  In that window the _Select space_ drop-down list should not have a default Space selected, requiring the user to choose a space by themselves.    *When accessing Space pages*:  The current logic should be used, automatically assigning the default to the current space.    h3. Why this is important  With more users joining Confluence, specially those that are not IT savvy, this suggestion can prevent confusion and have content created in the proper Space.    h3. Workaround   _none so far_"""
comments: "We have noticed a couple of times in our instance that Confluence has chosen to create the space in the personal space of the first user in the list of users (alphabetically).     This is very undesirable behavior."

summary: "As a Confluence user I would like to choose a target Space when I click the Create blank page button"
description: """h3. Problem Definition  When the Confluence user doesn't have a Personal Space, the _Create blank page_ button will automatically determine the target Space when a user is browsing dashboard pages. This target Space might be randomly chosen from the user perspective.  This may create confusion, since some users might not have the understanding where the page is being placed and save the pages in Spaces they have the create content permissions, but don't usually curate content.    h3. Suggested Solution  When a user is not in a specific Space and clicks on the Create blank page button, Confluence should ask which space instead of automatically assigning a default one when the user doesn't have a Personal Space.  How the buttons works could be a configuration option available to the Confluence administrator between the two options below:   # _Create blank page button_ automatically chooses a Space based on the current logic.   # When clicked, _Create blank page button_ asks the user which Space they want to create the blank page.    h3. Why this is important  With more users joining Confluence, specially those that are not IT savvy, this suggestion can prevent confusion and have content created in the proper Space.    h3. Workaround   As a workaround, Confluence administrator may choose to disable the _Create blank page_ button, reverting the functionality to something similar prior to [Confluence 5.9|https://confluence.<USER>doc/confluence-5-9-release-notes-785333580.html#Confluence5.9ReleaseNotes-One-clickcreateforblankpages] when the _Quick Create_ functionality was released.  To achieve this, Confluence admin needs to [add the following JVM property|https://confluence.<USER>doc/configuring-system-properties-168002854.html] to the startup script of your Confluence instance and restart the application.  """

summary: "Allow Confluence to be able to export images from URL of sites that do not allow images to be downloaded via non browser request"
description: """h4. Issue summary   # Certain sites do not allow images to be downloaded, or viewed unless the request is from a browser.   # If we were to use these images in a Confluence to be displayed via URL, the image will load on the page in Confluence, but on PDF exports, it will not be displayed.   # The following warning will appear in the log as well.   **        # This is because the PDF export request for the image on the server side, and the site does not allow the image to be download that way.   # Also, since the server will take some time to process this request, if there's a lot of these type of images on a page, the request might become long running and hang.    h4. Sample image URLs   # [https://dk.farnell.com/productimages/standard/en_GB/1865279-40.jpg]   # [https://dk.farnell.com/productimages/standard/en_GB/b&k-precision-bk2831e-40.jpg]    h4. Sample of a URL without any issues found online   # [https://images-na.ssl-images-amazon.com/images/I/91eYwmmel5L._SL1500_.jpg]    h4. Test   # We can know if the image would be affected or not by running the _wget_ command on the server that Confluence is on, against those URLs.   ** !wget-images.PNG|thumbnail!    h4. Workaround  # Get the image from the browser, and upload it to Confluence.    h4. Note  # Do note that a lot of these sites prevent this to protect their sites from being [ripped|https://whatis.techtarget.com/definition/ripping-digital-extraction].  # It would also be a good idea to show in the UI if we hit into these type of images during a PDF export, so that the user is aware of the issue."""

summary: "Option to disable Launch On Startup during installation of Atlassian Companion app"
description: """h3. Problem Definition    During installation, there is no option to disable Launch on Startup for Atlassian Companion App. for both .exe and .msi packages. Only once it is launched, we can disable the option.    h3. Suggested Solution  * Add an option during installation, where this can be disabled.  * For MSI packages, we can implement something similar to Roaming profiles that can be configured deploying through MSI package, we can try to add an option for this configuration as well.    h3. Why this is important  The customer has complained that they don't want the Companion app to automatically launch for all users. As many users don't need or use this app, it creates unnecessary confusion for the users and also cause drain on resources.    h3. Workaround   (Optional)  After first launch of the app, the user can disable the Automatic Launch on Startup.  """
