summary: "Allow users to edit pages in Confluence without the Add page permission"
description: """h4. Issue summary  # Right now, if a user would like to share a page in a Space to be edited by another user, they will need to grant that user the Add Page permission.  # This might not be the requirement of the user, as they would not want the user they're adding to add pages to a Space, but to only edit existing pages.  # This ticket is a duplicate of the CONFSERVER-26522 suggestion ticket that was closed on 2012.    h4. Current Behavior  # User will need to have the *Add Page* permission in order to edit existing pages.    h4. Suggested Behavior  # Create a separate permission called *Edit Page*, that would allow users with the permission to edit pages in a Space without the permission to add or create more pages."""

summary: "Sortable custom content properties"
description: """h3. Summary  We have currently implemented some reports using CQL queries. However, we have realized that we are not able to sort by content properties.    Having the ability to do this would really help us to properly implement our features.    h3. Note  This [documentation|https://developer.<USER>server/confluence/content-properties-in-the-rest-api/#cql-search-extension] was followed, but sorting also does not work not only for strings and texts but also with dates."""

summary: "Word Doc w/ Embeded Excel Chart Causes Memory Issues"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    When adding the Word Doc macro to display a Word document which contains a copy of an Excel chart with a logarithmic scale, Confluence will encounter slow performance issues and encounter a Java Heap issue after saving the page with the Word document.    *UPDATE:* This bug can also occur when viewing directly an Excel file with logarithmic scale attached to a page or any office file that contains an Excel file with logarithmic scale.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a Word Document and embed an Excel chart with a logarithmic scale.   # Upload file as an attachment to any Confluence Space (this alone will not cause the issue)   # Use the View Doc (Word ver) macro to display (not link) the Word Document on any page   # Save Page    h3. Expected Results    Once saved, the Confluence page should show the contents of the Word Document on the page.  h3. Actual Results    A Java Heap error will display on the page, causing slow performance on the instance. The following is shown in the logs:    h3. Notes   * Increasing the available memory the max allowed by the environment still causes the issue.   * The problem is not encountered if a normal chart is used in the word document.   * The problem word docs fairly small in size. Example of a doc that causes the issue:   ** [^Word-Doc-Problematic.docx]    h3. Workaround    Currently, there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""
comments: "This bug has crashed our confluence instance 5 times now... " [SEP] "This is confirmed in 7.3.2 as well. As this is crashing the instance, this should be fixed soon. " [SEP] "We found, for excel file, seems ""Logarithmic scale"" itself alone won't crash confluence.    But when an  excel file contains chart with following 2 settings *at the same time*, it will crash confluence.   * Vertical axis with ""Logarithmic scale"" option is checked;   * Chart's type is ""Stacked Column"";         We tested, and found even an excel with following simple data will crash confluence immediately every time, when a chart is created with above settings on these data.  ||x||y||  |a|18|  |b|26|  |c|200|  |d|1500|" [SEP] "A ""Stacked Bar"" chart with logarithmic scale will also cause confluence to crash. " [SEP] "As this could be used as a denial of service attack it should have increased priority.    One user can bring down system with one action..."

summary: "Expand Notifications Capability for Confluence Questions"
description: """Currently, only watchers of a specific topic will be notified when a question is asked regarding that topic. Some admins may be keen to receive all notifications, even if they are not necessarily watchers for a given topic.     Some organizations could benefit from having a larger, configurable notification scheme to help drive adoption of Confluence Questions. Some examples would be:  * reminder notifications to admins when questions haven't been answered (configurable threshold from time of creation)   * questions with answers and no accepted answer (configurable threshold) """

summary: "Expand Reporting Capability in Confluence Questions"
description: """Currently Confluence Questions doesn't have any reporting capabilities. Some admins would be keen to see metrics like the following:    * Time to first response  * Answer Rate  * Average time to answer  * Most popular tags  * Highest contributing users  """

summary: "Include Comments into quick search by default"
description: """h5. Problem Description  Current quick search is excluding comments by default unless specifically selected from the content type. This is per design as outlined in  [Search|https://confluence.<USER>doc/search-139528.html] page    h5. Proposed Behavior  A better behaviour would be if  * Comments are included by default into the quick search content type and can be unticked if the search is customised  * Quick search defaults for all users can be modified from admin panel depending on the business case"""

summary: "Advanced Search defaults to custom type search if any content was searched prior to redirect"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Starting with Confluence 6.14, redirecting from quick Search to Advanced search via advanced search link, defaults to Custom Type instead of All content as it was prior to 6.14  h3. Environment   * Confluence 6.14.* with new Quick Search    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Add a comment on a page   # Search for the same comment in quick search   # Comment is not found as comments are excluded from default quick search as per statement in [Search|https://confluence.<USER>doc/search-139528.html] page.   # Click Advanced Search to be redirected to Advanced Search    h3. Expected Results   * Comment is found as Advanced search searched *All Content* types by default    h3. Actual Results   * Comment is not found as Advanced Search switches to the *Custom* content type search that includes only Space, User, Page, Blog post and Attachment   !quicksearch1.png|thumbnail!    h3. Workaround   * Including Comments into Quick Search type, will include them into Advanced Search custom type search field   * Clicking Advanced Search link without typing anything into the search field will default to All Content in Advanced Search    h3. Resolution    Upgrade to Confluence 6.15.1 or above"""
comments: "This bug was fixed in 6.15.1" [SEP] "Seems to be a Bug in 7.1.2 again. I just reproduced it on my testsystem     " [SEP] "Please see https://jira.<USER>browse/CONFSERVER-59544 for German language"

summary: "Provide notification for newly logged in Internal Directory users to change their passwords"
description: """When Administrators set passwords for Internal Directory users, currently, there is no notification for the user, once logged in, to change their password. It is a good practice for users to change their passwords, especially passwords that are manually created for them by Confluence admins. Being notified of this, would encourage more users to change their passwords. """

summary: "Reduce number and cost of permission queries to render create buttons"
description: """h3. Problem    There are 2 types of create buttons in Confluence: Quick create (to create a page) and Blueprint create (will bring up a Create dialog with blueprint options). In order to decide if these buttons should be visible or not, for every page load, Confluence has to execute several DB queries to scan all _SPACES_ and SPACEPERMISSIONS tables to find any spaces in which current user has permission to create page/blogpost. You can find a sample query below:    These permission queries don't scale well, especially in MySQL. Below you can find average query time for a dataset of about 6,000 spaces and 300,000 records in SPACEPERMISSIONS table:  ||DBMS||Avg query time (ms)||  |Postgres|40|  |{color:#ff0000}MySQL{color}|{color:#ff0000}300{color}|  |SQL Server|20|  |Oracle|50|    Not only expensive, the number of these queries is also unnecessary high. See below for count for each page type and type of Create buttons:  || ||Dashboard||Page/Blogpost View||  |*Quick Create*|2|0|  |*Blueprint Create*|2|2|    Under very high load, these queries can put enormous pressure on DB and may even cause outages.  h3. Root cause    The code to render Create buttons doesn't scale well because it involves too many queries which can be costly in big instances. This ticket aims to alleviate some problems in CONFSERVER-57535 while the proper fix is being considered.       h3. Workaround    Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available    """

summary: "Changes for Cache Performance Tuning page"
description: """h2. Issue Summary    Our [Cache Performance Tuning|https://confluence.<USER>doc/cache-performance-tuning-169119133.html] page, currently states the following:  {quote}It is essential for the administrator of a large production installation of Confluence to size the caches to suit its environment.  {quote}  This can lead Administrators into tuning cache performance for cache performance's sake, without measuring what effect this has on customers/page loads. Thus we have no idea how effective the changes are, and much more importantly, if they are needed.  h2. Request    We've made the following addition to the page, just so we can better inform our customers.  {quote}(i) While this is an important task, having a comparison point is essential to help identify how effective the changes are, and much more importantly, if they are needed. (e.g. Comparing page load time, login time, <USER>).  {quote}  We'd like to have the changes made for all current and future versions of the page."""

summary: "No more option to apply fixed width setting to tables on exported PDF"
description: """h3. Summary    There is no more option to apply fixed width setting to tables on exported PDF.  It was actually supported in Confluence 6.2.1 ~ Confluence 6.13.0.    h3. Steps to Reproduce    # Create a wide table with *fixed-width* setting on a page  # Export the page as PDF    h3. Expected Results    # If *fixed-width* setting is applied to the table, the table should be shown with fixed width on the exported PDF  #- If the table is potentially wider than the PDF width, *it can't be helped that the table is cut-off on the exported PDF*  # If *responsive* setting is applied to the table, the table should be shown responsively on the exported PDF  #- Even if the table is potentially wider than the PDF width, the table should be shown without cut-off on the exported PDF    h3. Actual Results    The table is always shown responsively regardless of responsive / fixed-width setting.     h3. History of table column width problems  # Before Confluence 5.10.x  #- Due to CONFSERVER-34390, users have *no option at all* to export long tables without cut-off  # Confluence 5.10.x ~ Confluence 6.2.1  #- Thanks to [Confluence 5.10.x release|https://confluence.<USER>doc/confluence-5-10-release-notes-826894762.html#Confluence5.10ReleaseNotes-Takecontrolofyourtables], Confluence started to have an option to set responsive width to tables  (i) Actually, these versions of Confluence had worked as described in *Expected Results* section in this bug report.  # Confluence 6.2.1 ~ Confluence 6.13.0  #- Due to CONFSERVER-54275, all the table columns are shown with the same width regardless of responsive / fixed-width setting  # After Confluence 6.13.0  #- Tables are always shown responsively regardless of responsive / fixed-width setting ← Now!    h3.Workaround    There is no workaround so far."""

summary: "Add indexing support for Polish"
description: """In Confluence 5.10, and 6.2, new languages were added to Confluence. However, indexing support for those languages were not added and so instances generating content in those languages would not be able to index and search them properly. In 6.14.0 we added indexing support for a number of these languages but Polish was left out because of licensing issues which have since been resolved.      We will be using the Stempel analyzer from Lucene."""

summary: "Setting c3p0.min_size to a non-zeroed value causes Confluence to trigger an overhead of Database Connections"
description: """h2. Issue Summary    Confluence uses [C3P0|https://www.mchange.com/projects/c3p0/] as its database pooling layer, which provides tons of options for administrators who wish to tune the way their applications connect with their database through a JDBC Driver.    Among these options, there's one called {{hibernate.c3p0.min_size}}, which sets the minimum size that the database pool must have to operate throughout its lifecycle. Due to the way that Confluence handles its database pools, if this property is set to a non-zeroed value, the application may end up doubling the amount of database connections that the database will need to handle, which can cause performance issues and jeopardise resource planning.  h2. Issue Details    Confluence uses [Hibernate|http://hibernate.org/] as its ORM framework. Recently, Confluence has been bumped to Hibernate version 5, which is good, since new capabilities are added, but is also bad, given that the issue mentioned above happens.    The problem happens because during startup Hibernate is going to open parallel Database Connection Pool(s) to perform metadata queries and fulfil its own needs. This/these parallel Database Connection Pool(s) will use C3P0 as well to be configured. That being said, let's imagine the following setup:    This implies that Confluence will raise a Database Connection Pool that will have 30 connections opened with the database since startup and that can grow up to 60 connections depending on need. In a nutshell, this means that, at anytime, we'll have at least 30 connections established with the database.    That being said, an administrator can then plan database capacity accordingly, given that the administrator knows that the maximum amount of connections that the database will need to handle is 60 connections. Right? *Wrong!* :)    From Hibernate 5 onwards (which is our current version), Hibernate will also open database pool(s) as mentioned above. This means that during startup, we can double the amount of connections being sent to the database. As two pools are opened, we can send _max_size_ times two to the database, meaning that 120 connections can be established. If an administrator planned to have his database being able to hold up to 60 connections maximum, a bottleneck is established as the pool can grow bigger than what the database can handle.    Aside from that, Confluence will never close the parallel pool(s) opened by Hibernate, which means that if _hibernate.c3p0.min_size_ has been set to a non-zeroed value, once Hibernate is done using its pool, 30 connections are going to remain established with the database but are never going to be used, given that it is used only during startup.  h2. How to Validate Impact    Here's a step by step guide to check the bug behaviour on PostgreSQL:   # Stop Confluence.   # Edit <Confluence-Home-Directory>/confluence.cfg.xml and set the following properties as below:     # This is supposed to mean that the maximum number of connections that your pool can establish with the database is 60 connections.   # Start Confluence.   # Go to your database and run the following query once Confluence is up and running - Don't forget to replace your database name and database username in the query:     # This query should not return values bigger than 60, as it brings the amount of JDBC connections established by C3P0 with the database. Once you run it, you are going to see more than 60 due to the bug mentioned herein. On a testing environment (clean instance), the count reached to 81.    h2. Workaround    The workaround is to go ahead and set _hibernate.c3p0.min_size_ as 0. This way, once Hibernate is done using its database pool, unused connections will be removed from the pool based on timeout until it has zeroed out. In a matter of a few minutes, pooled but unused connections should no longer exist and the database won't have connections without usage tied to it.          """
comments: "This issue can be further negatively affected by a duplicate connection pool, which will respect the minimal connections settings: CONFSERVER-55542" [SEP] "This issue was fixed as a part of https://jira.<USER>browse/CONFSERVER-55542    Customers are advised to please upgrade to Confluence 7.4 ER."

summary: "REST API: Add ETag support"
description: """Add ETag support for those REST API calls that do not expand macros.    Use case:   * We have pages that are reset to an updated state on a daily basis.   * In the page history, we do not want to see the idempotent updates.   * However, we want to overwrite edits made by others (persons or scripts); i.e. the script cannot skip the update if the source data has been changed.  We want to send an alert to the page maintainer if this happens.   * Retrieving the data from Confluence to do an """"is this unchanged"""" check before updating risks a race condition. Also, we want to avoid triggering a flurry of alerts whenever a Confluence update returns more or different information."""

Go to Browse > Pages and click on """"Tree"""" and then use drag and drop to reorder the pages like this:  * Parent Page  ** Child Page 3  ** Child Page 2  ** Child Page 1    3. Remove Parent Page without removing child pages    4. Go to Browse > Pages. The ordering will be lost, and instead pages will be sorted alphabetically like this:  * Child Page 1  * Child Page 2  * Child Page 3    h4. Scenario 2    1. Create a page hierarchy like this:  * Parent Page  ** Child Page  *** Grandchild Page 1  *** Grandchild Page 2  *** Grandchild Page 3    2. summary: "Performing a second Space import from Cloud, after not choosing email matching, fails."
description: """h3. Issue Summary    _Background_    _Confluence 6.15.1 shipped with a new functionality which allows to enable additional matching logic upon importing Spaces from Cloud. In future, Cloud will stop to export usernames and replace them with email addresses (or unique number identifiers) in Space export. These emails can be matched with users' emails set in Confluence Server. Learn more in this KB article: [https://confluence.<USER>display/DOC/Import+a+space+from+Confluence+Cloud]_    The email-matching process is enabled on the admin page Backup & Restore by checking the """"Attribute content created in Confluence Cloud to matching users in this site (Cloud spaces only)"""" checkbox. If a User performs such Cloud import and forgets to set this checkbox, when they reimport again, having it set this time, such import would fail.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Perform a Cloud Space export. Check if the entities.xml file contains email addresses stored in ConfluenceUserImpl->name fields.   # Perform a Space import, do not check the """"Attribute content ...""""   # Observe all the content has been attributed to """"Unknown Users""""   # Delete the imported Space   # Perform a Space import again, this time check the """"Attribute content ..."""" checkbox.    h3. Expected Results    After second import, all of the content that had corresponding users in Server, should be properly attributed to them (e.g. properly display page creators, commenters, <USER>)  h3. Actual Results    After second import, all of the content in imported Space remains attributed to Unknown Users.  h3. Workaround    To recover from such failed import, you must remove the unnecessary entries from the USER_MAPPING table.    To do that, only delete these which match an email address and are NOT usernames in the *cwd_user* table:    # Make sure the problematic Space is deleted   # Execute the following query:     |{{delete from user_mapping where username like }}{{'%@%'}} {{and username not in (select user_name from cwd_user);}}|"""

Actual Results    As far as I confirmed,    * In 6.14.1  The link text was """"Re: Page A"""" in the editor but it turns to """"unnamed link"""" after saved.   !Screen Shot 2019-03-08 at 12.49.10.png|thumbnail!   {code:none}  2019-03-08 03:48:32,632 ERROR [http-nio-8090-exec-2] [plugins.rest.resources.BreadcrumbResource] generateBreadcrumbs Unsupported resource type: comment: Re: Page A v.1 (1245194)   -- referer: http://<base-url>/pages/createpage.action?spaceKey=LIN&fromPageId=1245191 | url: /rest/prototype/1/breadcrumb | traceId: c1d90c6d02c7f567 | userName: admin      h3. Workaround    Create a link to comment from Web Link following the instruction in [Links|https://confluence.<USER>doc/links-776656293.html]"""

summary: "Add ""Mentioned In"" Section to Jira Issue Macro"
description: """h3. Problem Definition    Currently, when an issue is added to multiple Confluence pages using the *Jira Issue Macro*, a *Mentioned In* section is created on the Jira Issue page that showcases all other pages that reference the issue. However, there is no way to display this same information in Confluence.    h3. Suggested Solution    There should be additional Section or Field Name that can be added to the Jira Issue Macro that pulls other Confluence Pages that are also linking to the same Issue.    h3. Why this is important    This feature would help teams better track Jira issues and their corresponding Confluence pages, give users one place to go to see the current status and easily navigate to the documentation associated with each issue."""

summary: "Migrated users that already existed on LDAP directory are unable to login"
description: """h3. Expected behavior  Users are able to login with LDAP password after being migrated from an Internal Directory to a Delegated LDAP, regardless of already being present in the target directory or not.    h3. Actual behavior  Users are either presented with a 500 error on the browser when trying to login or are unable to get past the login screen at all.    In the former scenario, when expanding details of the error, the below stack trace is seen on the browser and nothing is thrown at the logs.      In the latter case, the below is shown on the {{atlassian-security.log}}      h3. Steps to reproduce  # Create a user on the internal directory with the same name as an LDAP user;  # Create a directory of type *Internal Directory with LDAP Authentication* with the """"Copy user on login"""" option *unchecked*;  # Migrate users from the default *Internal Directory* to the Delegated one;  (i) Users can login successfully with LDAP password.  # Edit the Delegated directory to """"Copy user on login"""";  (!) Problem occurs.    h3. Environment  * Able to reproduce with AD, but *not* with OpenLDAP;    h3. Workaround  (!) _This workaround involves manual manipulation of the JIRA's database, please perform any change on a test environment first before applying into production and make sure to have an up-to-date backup._  # Stop JIRA;  # Get the directory's ID;    # Remove the value for {{external_id}} of the migrated users directly on the {{cwd_user}} table in JIRA's database;  #* For MySQL and PostgreSQL    #* For SQL Server:    # Restart JIRA;"""
comments: "Hey,    I did encounter the same issue.         Confluence version: 6.9.1    DB version:Mysql 5.6.40    User Directory: 2 User Directories. Internal with LDAP Authentication-OpenLDAP at the top one while Confluence Internal Directory second.         What I have done:   # Update the original User Directory, Internal with LDAP Authentication-OpenLDAP to Internal with LDAP Authentication-Microsoft Active Directory. Change the AD server in the new User Directory to another one.(the usernames are the same between the old AD and the new one)   # Login the Confluence, some users can login successfully, but some failed.   # Search the external_id in DB. I find some user's external_id is gibberish.   # Follow the 'Workaround', and it works well.          "

summary: "Description of predefined role CONNECT, RESOURCE should be replaced in Oracle database setup guide"
description: """h3. Problem Definition    [Database Setup for Oracle|https://confluence.<USER>doc/database-setup-for-oracle-173821.html] has an instruction to grant predefined roles – {{CONNECT}}, {{RESOURCE.}} but these roles are not recommended by Oracle because they should be outdated in near future.  h3. Suggested Solution    Change the instruction to grant   * {{CREATE SESSION}}   (to replace the role in {{CONNECT}})   * {{CREATE CLUSTER}}   * {{CREATE INDEXTYPE}}   * {{CREATE OPERATOR}}   * {{CREATE PROCEDURE}}   * {{CREATE SEQUENCE}}   * {{CREATE TABLE}}   * {{CREATE TRIGGER}}   * {{CREATE TYPE}}   (to replace the role in {{RESOURCE}})    h3. Why this is important    Because these predefined roles may not be created automatically as described in below.   [Configuring Privilege and Role Authorization-- Predefined Roles in an Oracle Database Installation |https://docs.oracle.com/database/121/DBSEG/authorization.htm#DBSEG4414]  {quote}Note: Oracle recommends that you design your own roles for database security rather than relying on this role. This role may not be created automatically by future releases of Oracle Database.  {quote}"""

summary: "Jira issue macro table filter will not display correct information if linked issues field is used and if data comes from two different Jira instances"

Environment  Confluence 6.9.0/6.6.6  Jira 7.9/7.8/7.11  Application link between all applications.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # In Jira 1 create tickets TEST-1, TEST-2, TEST-3  # In Jira 2 create ticket SER-1  # In Jira 1 in ticket TEST-1 make an internal link to tickets  TEST-2, TEST-3, SER-1  # On Confluence page insert Jira issue macro as a table,   ## In filter use TEST-1  ## In Display option add field """"Linked Issues""""  # Save the page and observe the results    h3. summary: "Atlassian Space Blueprint Tutorial breaks space creation"
description: """This bug is created on behalf of a Community post:  [https://community.<USER>t5/Confluence-questions/Unknown-Server-Error-in-Confluence-from-using-Atlassian-s-Space/qaq-p/1001852]  Another community post having a similar issue:  [https://community.<USER>t5/Confluence-questions/Plugin-uninstall-destroys-Confluence/qaq-p/1019918#M135051]  h3. Issue Summary    When following the Confluence Space Blueprint tutorial, once you've created and applied your plugin. You're no longer able to create a Confluence Space.  h3. Environment    Reported on Confluence 6.7.1, 6.12.2, 6.14.0 using Postgres 9.6.    (Optional - If Applicable)  *  *  h3. Steps to Reproduce    Steps to reproduce:   # Install the latest Atlassian SDK as per [https://developer.<USER>server/framework/atlassian-sdk/install-the-atlassian-sdk-on-a-windows-system/]   # Navigate to the instruction set for creating a space blueprint: [https://developer.<USER>server/confluence/write-a-simple-confluence-space-blueprint/]   # Clone the repository located in those instructions that contains the completed space blueprint plugin to a folder on your local machine:    git clone <EMAIL>:atlassian_tutorial/confluence-space-blueprint.git   # Open a command prompt (on windows) and navigate to the folder that you cloned the repository into.   # Build and run the plugin by typing the following command into the command prompt:  atlas-run   # Open up a browser window and navigate to [http://localhost:1990/confluence/] (or whichever link you use to access the local instance of confluence you just started).   # Login using admin/admin as username/password.   # Click on the Confluence Administration cog wheel in the upper right hand corner, and then click """"Manage Apps.""""   # Click on """"Upload App.""""   # Navigate to the directory you cloned the repository into, and click on the """"target"""" folder (if this folder doesn't exist, you need to run atlas-run).   # Select the space-blueprint-1.0-SNAPSHOT.jar file and click """"Upload.""""   # Refresh the page, and select """"Create space"""" under the Spaces dropdown. Note that the Example Space Blueprint is listed as an option, and you can successfully create a space if you wish to test that.   # Go back to Confluence Administration->Manage Apps, and uninstall the plugin you just installed.   # Refresh the page, and select """"Create space"""" under the Spaces dropdown. Note that no error occurs.   # Select the command prompt, and hit Ctrl-C to kill the server instance currently running (you may have to navigate to Task Manager and kill a process named """"Java SE Platform Binary"""" in order to completely kill the server).   # After the server has stopped, type the following command into the command prompt:  atlas-clean   # Open pom.xml in your favorite text editor (or open as a project in IDEA or other IDE).   # Change the confluence versions and data versions in the properties block from this (I also tried modifying my amps.version to 6.21, but it didn't fix anything):  <properties>  <confluence.version>6.7.1</confluence.version>  <confluence.data.version>6.7.1</confluence.data.version>  <amps.version>6.3.15</amps.version>  <plugin.testrunner.version>1.2.3</plugin.testrunner.version>  <atlassian.spring.scanner.version>1.2.13</atlassian.spring.scanner.version>  <!-- This key is used to keep the consistency between the key in atlassian-plugin.xml and the key to generate bundle. -->  <atlassian.plugin.key>${project.groupId}.${project.artifactId}</atlassian.plugin.key>  <project.build.sourceEncoding>UTF-8</project.build.sourceEncoding>  <maven.compiler.source>1.8</maven.compiler.source>  <maven.compiler.target>1.8</maven.compiler.target>  </properties>to this:  <properties>  <confluence.version>6.14.0</confluence.version>  <confluence.data.version>6.14.0</confluence.data.version>  <amps.version>6.3.15</amps.version>  <plugin.testrunner.version>1.2.3</plugin.testrunner.version>  <atlassian.spring.scanner.version>1.2.13</atlassian.spring.scanner.version>  <!-- This key is used to keep the consistency between the key in atlassian-plugin.xml and the key to generate bundle. -->  <atlassian.plugin.key>${project.groupId}.${project.artifactId}</atlassian.plugin.key>  <project.build.sourceEncoding>UTF-8</project.build.sourceEncoding>  <maven.compiler.source>1.8</maven.compiler.source>  <maven.compiler.target>1.8</maven.compiler.target>  </properties>   # Repeat steps 4 through 13.   # Refresh the page.   # Click on """"Create Space"""" under the Spaces tab, and note the """"Unknown Server Error"""" message you receive.   # In the command prompt, look at the log output. You should see an error like the following:   [INFO] [talledLocalContainer] 2019-02-07 15:35:17,015 WARN [http-nio-1990-exec-8] [confluence.impl.hibernate.ConfluenceHibernateTransactionManager] doRollback Performing rollback. Transactions:   [INFO] [talledLocalContainer] ->[PluginReadWriteTx]: PROPAGATION_REQUIRED,ISOLATION_DEFAULT (Session #1567347778)   [INFO] [talledLocalContainer] – referer: [http://localhost:1990/confluence/plugins/servlet/upm] | url: /confluence/rest/create-dialog/1.0/space-blueprint/dialog/web-items | traceId: 04182b73ba117a25 | userName: admin    h3. Expected Results    You should be able to create a new space within Confluence.  h3. Actual Results    When attempting to create a new space within Confluence, you get """"Unknown Server Error""""    h3. Notes    (Optional - If Necessary)  h3. Workaround    No present workaround."""
comments: "This is a duplicate of CONFSRVDEV-10025 except for the minor detail of affecting 6.7.1 as well as newer versions.    I can explain that though. Prior to 6.11, the problematic not-null check was a Java assertion.  I'm somewhat confidently assuming that when following the tutorial, the community member has assertions enabled, and hence they hit this problem.    Thank you very much for the report, it's very helpful extra information for CONFSRVDEV-10025" [SEP] "On reading the community post it seems that the issue explicitly does _not_ occur on 6.7.1"

comments: "This is an intentional change introduced in Confluence 6.13 to allow customers to apply their own styles to headings.  Please refer to the Confluence 6.13 release notes [here|https://confluence.<USER>doc/confluence-6-13-release-notes-959288785.html#Confluence6.13ReleaseNotes-PDFexportimprovements].  [This|https://confluence.<USER>doc/customize-exports-to-pdf-190480945.html#CustomizeExportstoPDF-Headings] article gives a in-depth overview of how you can customise PDF space exports." [SEP] "Hi [~<USER>!    As listed in the release notes, each page PDF is generated separately and therefore we do not inherently provide a way to identify child pages by their heading level.  With that said, headings are not rendered uniformly (i.e. if two pages contain a h5 element, the rendered heading will look the same no matter the hierarchy) - so to achieve this effect one of the only solutions is to actually change the heading sizes on the child pages (or use something like the HTML macro, which we tend to discourage)." description: """AS a user I want to be able to optionally include a file size attribute when adding the Attachments Macro to a page so that I can immediately see what size attachments are before downloading.       {quote}GIVEN I am an editing a Confluence page    WHEN I add an Attachments Macro    THEN I am given the option to include the file size as an attribute.  {quote}     {quote}GIVEN I am viewing a Confluence page using an Attachments Macro    WHEN the file size has been included in the macro parameters    THEN I can see a column identified as """"File size""""    AND the file size for each attachment is displayed in bytes, kilobytes or megabytes.  {quote}"""

summary: "Excel Macro renders graphs over page footer"
description: """h3. Summary    When using the Excel macro on a page, excel sheets with graphs are rendered incorrectly overwriting the texts on the page and the Atlassian Footer.  h3. Steps To Reproduce:   # Create a page.   # Download and attach the [^test_with_Graph.xlsx] to the page.   # Add a Office Excel macro.   # Insert the above attachment in the Excel macro and publish the page.    h3. Expected Result    The macro should render the excel page correctly without overwriting the texts and the footer.  h3. Actual Result    After the page is published, the excel graph overwrites the texts on the page and the footer.  h3. Workaround    None."""

summary: "{include} and {include-exerpt} macros should work for older versions of pages"
description: """In order to snapshot documentation content at a specific point in time for a product point release and allow the content source pages to continue to evolve as future features are developed, the \{import} macros should be able to get content from older versions of the page so that only the pertinent information is included for that product point releases's level of functionality.    Relates to CONFSERVER-1406 and [https://confluence.<USER>pages/viewpage.action?spaceKey=DISC&title=Using+Confluence+for+professional+documentation]     """

summary: "In Confluence, using the JIRA issue macro, if the user is searching for Alphanumeric key, the macro will automatically perform a search in the description and summary fields. "
description: """h3. Issue Summary    In Confluence, using the JIRA issue macro, if the user is searching for Alphanumeric key, the macro will automatically perform a search in the description and summary fields. (bug)    This is not the case for alphabetic keys, however.    # When the project key is alphabetic only(HLPD), and you typed in the key for tasks within the project such as HLPD-3555 the macro will automatically perform the following search:       # When a project key is alphabetic only, and you typed the project key only. For example HLPD, the macro will automatically perform the following search:      Based on the above, it should be the case that for alphanumeric keys, the behavior should be the same as the above.    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # In JIRA, create a project that has alphanumeric key, for example:       # In Jira, create few tasks under the above project, so that the tasks have keys such as NR5GX0A-1, NR5GX0A-2.   # In Confluence, using the Issue Macro, type in the NR5GX0A in the search field.   # In Confluence, using the Issue Macro, type in the NR5GX0A-1 in the search field.    h3. Expected Results   {quote}In Confluence, using the Issue Macro, type in the NR5GX0A in the search field.  {quote}  The result of the above should be that the macro performs the following search in JIRA    {quote}In Confluence, using the Issue Macro, type in the NR5GX0A-1 in the search field.  {quote}  The result of the above should be that the macro performs the following search in JIRA    h3. Actual Results  {quote}In Confluence, using the Issue Macro, type in the NR5GX0A in the search field.  {quote}  The result of the above should be that the macro performs the following search in JIRA    {quote}In Confluence, using the Issue Macro, type in the NR5GX0A-1 in the search field.  {quote}  The result of the above should be that the macro performs the following search in JIRA    h3. Workaround    The only workaround here is to ensure that the user explicitly type the """"key"""" filed in the Jira issue macro search. For example:     """
comments: "In our case, the problem is not only numbers in project key but also underscores ({{_}}). E.g. our project keys are prefixed so issue ID P_XXX-1 can never be searched within Confluence Jira issue macro."

summary: "Users with Delete Page permissions should have rights to delete page versions "
description: """h3. Problem Definition  Currently, only Space Administrators are allowed to delete Page or attachment version as outlined in [Delete or Restore a Page|https://confluence.<USER>doc/delete-or-restore-a-page-139429.html].  This means that even if a user has a full Delete right for Pages or Attachments, they still can't delete versions of that same page.    h3. Suggested Solution  * If a user has Delete Page and Delete Attachments space Permissions, they should be allowed to delete a version of the page too.  * Alternatively, Adding an option to delete Versions could also be added into Space Permissions control page     h3. Workaround  There is no workaround, a user needs to be a Space Administrator as deleting page version is permanent and can not be recovered from trash like a deleted page is."""

summary: "Office Excel macro:Error rendering macro 'viewxls' : Unrecognized date string, please use mm/dd/yyyy"
description: """Regression of CONFSERVER-32468.   Replicated on 6.12.2 and 6.14.1  {quote}We have an attachment called *Business Objectives.xls*, (attached), and when we try to display the content of the file using the Excel macro in a page titled *(3/5/14-4/29/15)*, it fails to render and instead throws the error below, (see the attached _screenshot XLS_bug.jpg_):    ??Error rendering macro 'viewxls' : Unrecognized date string, please use mm/dd/yyyy??    Notice how the macro appears to be picking up the wrong page name, (see previously mentioned screenshot """"_XLS_bug.jpg_""""), when on the page titled *(3/5/14-4/29/15)* it becomes truncated to */5/14-4/29/15)*.    Embedding the same spreadsheet in a page with the title *Test XLS*, (or any other title which doesn't contain date like formatting), allows the content of the spreadsheet to be rendered normally, (see attached screenshot """"_Working_example.jpg_""""), and the naming convention of the file also appears to have no impact on the bug.    This issue is specific to pages which contain date-like formatting in their titles, for example the page title *(3/5/14-4/29/15)*.  {quote}"""
comments: "For information:    Clearing plugin cache and rebuild index did not affect the behavior.     " [SEP] "On [Atlassian Confluence|http://www.<USER>software/confluence] 6.7.1 I also got the same error when trying to attach an excel file through Office Excel macro."

summary: "Unable to add any content to a created field on a blueprint"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    After adding a new field to the Decision blueprint, that field is not editable until you publish the page and then choose to edit.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Navigate to the administration console and edit select the option for *Global Templates and Blueprints*  # Edit the *Decision* blueprint  # Add a new row to the table and add any title to the row and add a new variable type (example - $dec-type)  # Save the blueprint  # Navigate to a space  # Create a new page using the *Decision* Blueprint    h3. You can edit all rows/text within the table  h3. The newly created cell that should house the variable is blank and is unable to be edited or added to      h3. Workaround  Publish the page, edit the page and the entire table should be able to be edited.  """

summary: "Hide the ""Uploaded from Companion app"" comment in the attachments macro for all files edited in the Companion App"
description: """h3. Problem Definition  When adding files to a page with the Attachments Macro and using the Companion App to edit attached files, the macro displays """"Uploaded from Companion app"""" on a line beneath the file name, as shown in the screenshot below:   !attachments macro.png|thumbnail!    The text consumes unnecessary space in the macro, especially when numerous files are edited with the Companion App.    h3. Suggested Solution  Provide the ability to hide this text in the Attachments Macro or remove it entirely.     h3. Why this is important  If users edit numerous files using the Companion App, the Attachments Macro consumes more of the page to make room for the additional text. It makes it more difficult to navigate the file list, as users must scroll through a longer list of text.     h3. Workaround   There is no workaround at this time. """
comments: "Hi everyone, thanks for your patience. I recently posted an update on Community regarding Companion and a resolution to this issue: [File editing in Confluence Server: Atlassian Companion updates are almost here!|https://community.<USER>t5/Confluence-discussions/File-editing-in-Confluence-Server-Atlassian-Companion-updates/td-p/1285578]. Updates for Companion will be available with 7.3." [SEP] "The ""Uploaded from Companion app"" message has been removed and will not longer show for files edited with Companion.    Companion 1.0.0 has been released for Confluence Server and Data Center customers, along with [Confluence 7.3.1 (Release Notes)|https://confluence.<USER>doc/confluence-7-3-release-notes-983794557.html].    Companion will update automatically, unless you are on the MSI. Please check out the [Atlassian Companion app release notes|https://confluence.<USER>doc/atlassian-companion-app-release-notes-958455712.html] for the Mircosoft Installer or to see what other issues have been resolved."

summary: "Search works only with exact words"
description: """{panel:title=This issue has been split into 2 suggestions}  Please read this comment for a detailed explanation. In summary:    If you are typing a *full word* (e.g. """"manage"""" to match """"manager"""") and find that your document does not match, you are likely interested in CONFSERVER-58280    If you are typing a *partial word* (e.g. """"manag"""" to match manage) and find that your document does not match, you are likely interested CONFSERVER-58279  {panel}  h3. Issue Summary    With Confluence 6.14, many improvements where made on search engine, making finding different words easier and faster. We don't need to type all the word to find results. Engine should give suggestions as we type. This was explained in [Search for anything|https://confluence.<USER>doc/confluence-6-14-release-notes-963655609.html]  h3. Environment    Confluence 6.14.0 and 6.14.1  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create multiple pages with the following titles: Management, Managers, Managerial.   # Make suer the index was built   # Search for """"Manage""""    h3. Expected Results    All 3 pages should be returned  h3. Actual Results    Nothing is returned  h3. Workaround    Add * at the end of word you are looking for. This is not ideal as it might return a lot noise.    Alternatively, copy the title of the page into the page body. Then search seems to return it fine.    Note that the site indexing language also needs to be set correctly for Confluence to stem words correctly. i.e. if you select """"French"""" as your indexing language you cannot expect different versions of English words to match."""
comments: "Another example - we've documented our firewalls: Searching for ""firewal"" does not find anything, only fully typing ""firewall"" will show results.    It should work like it previously did, perfectly documented here: [https://confluence.<USER>display/CONF51/Searching+Confluence]    -> even short strings (meaning some sort of type-ahead-search) should match [st matches test], right now in 6.14 you need to fully type out the whole word..." I’d like to take a moment to explain what exactly the issue is and where it comes from.    Prior to Confluence 6.14.0, if you clicked on

summary: "Allow users to add more than 3 sections in Page Layout"
description: """h3. Problem Definition      Currently, Confluence only allows users to add a maximum of 3 sections in the Page Layout.    h3. Suggested Solution      Add the ability to add more sections."""

summary: "Date picker for variables in templates"
description: """Currently the types available for input on a variable in a template include Text, Multi-Line Text and List.    I propose adding another type, which provides a calendar-style date picker. The calendar picker is already a feature in other areas of Atlassian products such as Jira when changing the Completion Date of an issue. This could be named Date.    We have an international team that has different date formats, so inserting in this form will prevent the usual month/day mix-up. The output could be in ISO 8601 i.e. 2019-02-28.         Thank you,    <USER>""

An attacker is able to exploit this issue to achieve path traversal and remote code execution on systems that run a vulnerable version of Confluence Server or Data Center.         *Affected versions:*    All versions of Confluence Server and Confluence Data Center before version 6.6.12, from version 6.7.0 before 6.12.3 (the fixed version for 6.12.x), from version 6.13.0 before 6.13.3 (the fixed version for 6.13.x) and from version 6.14.0 before 6.14.2 (the fixed version for 6.14.x).         *Fix:*   * Confluence Server version 6.15.1 is available for download from [https://www.<USER>software/confluence/download].   * Confluence Server version 6.14.2 is available for download from [https://www.<USER>software/confluence/download-archives].   * Confluence Server version 6.13.3 is available for download from [https://www.<USER>software/confluence/download-archives].   * Confluence Server version 6.12.3 is available for download from [https://www.<USER>software/confluence/download-archives].   * Confluence Server version 6.6.12 is available for download from [https://www.<USER>software/confluence/download-archives].         For additional details, see the full advisory: [https://confluence.<USER>display/DOC/Confluence+Security+Advisory+-+2019-03-20]     """

description: """Pages created from blueprints leave behind a """"create.blueprint.page.draft.request"""" entry in the os_property table after they're published.  These may contain small amounts of PII so it's important to delete them."""

summary: "After migrating from Cloud to Server, the ""x Jira links"" button does not appear on existing pages"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    After migrating from Cloud to Server, the """"x Jira links"""" button does not appear on the header of existing pages that do contain Jira links.    h3. Environment  * Confluence 6.14.1  * Jira 8.0.1    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a Confluence+Jira website in Cloud.   # Create a project and two issues in Jira.   # Create a page in Confluence and link the two issues on it.   # Backup both sites.   # Restore the Confluence backup in a Confluence Server 6.14.1 instance.   # Restore the Jira backup in a Jira Server 8.0.1 instance.   # Create an application link between the Server applications   # Go to the page and click """"Login & Approve"""" if requested.    h3. Expected Results    The header of the page loads a button saying """"2 Jira links"""".    h3. Actual Results    The button is not loaded at all.     !Screen Shot 2019-02-27 at 3.36.00 PM.png|thumbnail!     h3. Workaround    # Create a copy of the page. The copy will work, but the original one won't.  # (Optional) Delete the original page.  # (Optional) Rename the copy with the original name.     !Screen Shot 2019-02-27 at 3.34.47 PM.png|thumbnail! """

summary: "Clicking Dashboard in Breadcrumbs Navigates to Pages and not Dashboard."
description: """h3. Issue Summary  By clicking on *Dashboard* in the top breadcrumbs menu from a page in Confluence, it does not take you to [the Confluence Dashboard|https://confluence.<USER>doc/the-dashboard-139374.html], but rather the view of all pages in the space, the same menu you can reach by clicking *Pages* in the sidebar.    !spacehomepage.png|thumbnail!     h3. Environment  Confluence Server    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Create a space in Confluence.  # From a page in this space, click *Dashboard* at the top in the breadcrumbs menu  # Note you will see one of the two images, depending on how many additional pages you have added to the space:    !pagesview1.png|thumbnail!  !pagesview2.png|thumbnail!      h3. Expected Results  By clicking *Dashboard* I would expect to be taken to the Dashboard.   !dashboard.png|thumbnail!      h3. Actual Results  By clicking *Dashboard*, you are instead brought to *Pages*.    h3. Notes  Note that in the possibility that it's expected behavior to navigate to *Pages* when clicking on the head breadcrumb, a name change of that link might be needed so there is no confusion.    h3. Workaround  No current workaround."""
comments: "Thanks for reporting this, Shannon!      Although this is marked as Low priority, I am hoping for a quick fix from Atlassian, so that the entire Confluence user base has the correct experience when using Confluence." [SEP] "Hello, I'm also experiencing this. Any updates so far?" [SEP] "Hello, any chance this might be changed?   Having a Page overview but calling it Dashboard again is really unnecessary and misleading..."

summary: "Display Application URL in the Whitelist if the Application link is configured with different Display and Application URL (unproxied)"
description: """If you create the [unproxied application link|https://confluence.<USER>kb/how-to-create-an-unproxied-application-link-719095740.html] in Confluence, the *Whitelist* entry for Jira will display the *Display URL* while it is actually pointing to the *Application URL* that the request is coming from.    Confluence administrator had to add a new entry with the same *Display URL* to resolve the issue accessing the link in the page header as it is rendered by the *jira-metadata* plugin that is going through the whitelisted *Application URL*. This causes the *Whitelist* to print the *Display URL* twice and cause confusion to the administrator who maintain the instance.    Therefore, it is best to display the correct *whitelisted Application URL* instead of *Display URL* when we create the [unproxied application link|https://confluence.<USER>kb/how-to-create-an-unproxied-application-link-719095740.html] in Confluence.    8/23 Update:  To clarify the workaround: add another Whitelist rule for <display URL>/* with a Type of _Wildcard expression_."""

summary: "Remove Companion App Upload prompt a pop-up in Firefox to Leave Page"
description: """h4. Issue summary  # When using the Companion App with Firefox, after saving the changes to the attachment, and selecting *Upload*, Firefox will show this prompt.  ** *This page is asking you to confirm that you want to leave - data you have entered may not be saved.*  ** Options: *Leave Page* *Stay in Page*  # We can just click on *Leave Page*, and everything works as expected.  # But this is an extra step.    h4. Suggestion  # If possible, send Firefox the required data so that it does not think we have unsaved data, and does not prompt the message.    h4. Workaround  # Type in the address bar *about:config* and press Enter.  ** (Promise to be careful,  if asked)  # Type in the search bar and look for the preference :  ** *dom.disable_beforeunload*  # Set it's value to *true*."""
comments: "When editing a file directly from the list of attachments to a page, both chrome and firefox produce similar leave/stay prompts." [SEP] "A fix for this issue is available to Server and Data Center customers in Confluence 6.15.7  [Upgrade|https://www.<USER>software/confluence/download?utm_source=confluence_bugfix&utm_campaign=confluence_bugfix&utm_medium=email] now or check out the [Release Notes|https://confluence.<USER>display/DOC/Confluence+6.15+Release+Notes?utm_source=confluence_bugfix&utm_campaign=confluence_bugfix&utm_medium=email] to see what other issues are resolved."

summary: "PDF macro cannot find file if colon (:) is present in the page title."
description: """h3. Issue Summary  # PDF macro cannot find file if colon """":"""" is present in the page title.  # Regression of CONFSERVER-29660    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Create a page with colon in it's title.  ** *Example:* Page: 1  # Add a PDF as an attachment, and add the PDF macro in.  # We will be unable to choose the PDF file in the macro.    h3. Expected Results  # Be able to choose the PDF file even if the page title has a colon.    h3. Actual Results  # Unable to choose the PDF file unless the colon is removed from the page title.    h3. Workaround  # Remove the colon from the page title.  # Add the macro and choose the PDF  # Re-add the colon to the title."""

summary: "Make the Summary field in within the Leave event type should be optional"
description: """When users create a Leave event in the Team Calendar, the Summary field needs to be added. This provides an issue for organizations, that do not require this field to be added. Providing an option to make this field optional would make the feature easier to be used by users. """
comments: "Yes, introducing this feature would really be useful.    We usually do not require our resources to provide an explanation on why they are taking a leave and hence the summary field should be optional."

summary: "Size of Hibernate query cache is initially set to an insufficient value in Data Center"
description: """In Data Center, most heavily used caches have default size limit of 25,000. Due to a typo, Hibernate query cache is set to have just 1000 maximum entries. This might increase pressure on Database in busy instances.    To make matter worse, because of https://jira.<USER>browse/CONFSERVER-54777, the size of this cache cannot be increased via Cache Management UI in Confluence versions prior to 6.7.1  h2. Expected behavior    Hibernate query cache to have default limit of *25,000*  h2. Current behavior    Hibernate query cache has a default limit of just *1000*  h2. Workaround  h3. For Confluence versions prior to 6.7.1   # Modify the cache override file at _<shared-home>/config/cache-settings-overrides.properties_, make sure that this line exists:       # Restart Confluence    h3. For Confluence versions 6.7.1 and later   # Access Cache Management UI at _<confluence-base-url>/admin/cache/showStatistics.action#fullView_, adjust size of _org.hibernate.cache.internal.StandardQueryCache_v5_ to be 25000.     # Restart Confluence"""

summary: "Improper error messages while trying to edit a page with restrictions"
description: """h3. Description  While user A is editing a page and User B restricted the page when User A is still editing the page, Confluence will allow User A to continue to edit the page but will not allow the User A to save the page. The warning message appear is misleading.    *Steps to reproduce*  # Login Confluence with User A  # Edit a page in Confluence  # Open a new browser windows  # Login as User B  # Restrict the page that User A is editing  # Go back to the User A browser and click Save  # Confluence will show error related to the following  {quote}  Looks like your session expired. Log in again to keep working  {quote}  or  {quote}  Collaborative editing is here! That means we need a little help from you to restart your editing session. All you need to do is copy the content, edit this page again, then paste it in and save.  {quote}    Expected Result  A proper warning message is shown to the user    Actual Result  The error message is misleading."""

summary: "Attachments with multibyte file name don't get hit in search dialog for file/image insertion  "
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Attachments with multibyte file name like """"添付ファイル.docx"""" don't get hit in """"Search on other pages"""" dialog for file/image insertion.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Attach a file with name """"添付ファイル.docx"""" in any page and save it.   # Create any other page.   # Click """"Insert files and images""""   # Click """"Search on other pages""""   # Input """"添付ファイル"""" in the inputbox and search.    h3. Expected Results    You can find 添付ファイル.docx attached in step 1 above.  h3. Actual Results    Nothing found in the search dialog.    !Screen Shot 2019-02-27 at 14.59.04.png|thumbnail!  h3. Note    The search dialog internally convert the search query for lucene to """"添付ファイル*"""" . i.e. It adds a wildcard in the end of whole keyword.   Apparently this makes Lucene recognize that """"添付ファイル"""" is a single word, not a sentence.  h3. Workaround    Apparently putting wildcard in front of the keyword as """"*添付ファイル"""" works for this case.   Internally this raises {{LEADING_WILDCARD_NOT_ALLOWED}} , but it retries the search removing all wildcard ending up with a clean search with a keyword """"添付ファイル"""".    !Screen Shot 2019-02-27 at 15.00.35.png|thumbnail!"""

summary: "Atlassian Companion does not Version Check"
description: """With Confluence Version 6.14.1, the attachment editing feature now in the form of Atlassian Companion app still does not do a basic version check.  What this means is that if 2 people are editing the same attachment, and they both save changes without each other knowing, the person that saved their changes later will overwrite all the changes from the person that saved earlier.    The Atlassian Companion application should have access to the version number of the attachment being edited and who edited the version.  When someone tries to save back to Confluence, the Atlassian Companion app should do a version check to see if the current edited Version Number + 1 exists on the attachment.  If it doesn't, then it is safe to save back.  If a version number like that does exist, the Atlassian Companion app should tell the user they are not editing the most recent version of the document and if they save back, they will overwrite past changes done by said person or people.  Then the user can decide whether or not to save the changes.    I am sure there are more than one way to make this feature work, and I am fairly confident this feature can be accomplished without implementing anything new infrastructure wise because the REST endpoint already exists. ([https://developer.<USER>cloud/confluence/rest/#api-content-id-child-attachment-get)]    It would be cool if the Atlassian Companion app could also show what content changed.  But a warning at least would make the companion app much more trustworthy to use."""
comments: "Attachment locking is also available through the the [Cenote Lockpoint|https://marketplace.<USER>apps/36199/cenote-lockpoint-attachment-checkout?hosting=server&tab=overview] app, which is fully integrated with the Atlassian Companion. This helps prevent the type of overwrite scenario mentioned in this issue, and it also provides a number of other tools for managing locked attachments. (Disclaimer: I work for Cenote.)" [SEP] "A fix for this issue is available in Companion 1.1.0. View the [Release Notes|https://confluence.<USER>doc/atlassian-companion-app-release-notes-958455712.html] to see what other issues are resolved.    If Companion is installed via the MSI, then it will need to be manually updated to the new version.    "

summary: "Disabling ""Events"" event type breaks CalDAV synchronisation to mobile and the calendar on TC"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  Disabling """"Events"""" event type on Team Calendars will   * Prevent event creation from mobile CalDAV connection   * Corrupt calendar """"Events"""" event type   * Create duplicate """"Events"""" event types and actual Events for every attempt to create an event from the mobile   * Prevent events from getting created using """"Events"""" event type even after re-enabling the type.    h3. Environment   * Android Mobile with CalDAV two way sync to Team Calendars   * Confluence Team Calendars 6.0.27    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a Calendar in TC   # Create a _test_ event using built-in *Events* event type   # Add calendar to an android mobile device using CalDAV as outlined in [Subscribe to Team Calendars from Android Calendar|https://confluence.<USER>teamcal/subscribe-to-team-calendars-from-android-calendar-946631707.html] instructions   # Synchronize and confirm that you can see new test event in mobile calendars   # On team calendars disable """"Events"""" event type, _test_ event will disappear as expected.   # Synchronize CalDAV on mobile, _test_ event will disappear on *TC* and *Mobile*   # Attempt to create a new event on *Mobile*    h3. Expected Results  The event is created on Mobile Calendar and then syncronised to Confluence Team Calendars    h3. Actual Results   * Test event is created on mobile, but disappears after refresh and never appears on TC   * Re-enabling disabled *Events* event type will result in duplicate *Events* event types and events, one for each """"failed"""" mobile event creation attempt   !multiple event types.png|thumbnail!   * From calendars event type we do not see any custom event types   !no custom event type.png|thumbnail!   * Attempting to create an event with default *Events* event type will result in {{java.lang.NullPointerException}} in WebUI and {{atlassian-confluence.log}}:       * On mobile, none of the duplicates will be visible    h3. Workaround  Exporting calendar as an ical, deleting and re-importing it fixes duplicate event types and allows events creation again"""

summary: "Changing the color of links in Confluence pages does not reflect on the links once the page is published"
description: """h3. Issue Summary In Confluence pages, changing the colour of links to other Confluence pages using the color palette does not get reflected when the page is saved.  h3. Environment Issue observed on Confluence 6.14.0 onwards. Issue does not observed on Confluence 6.13.x and below h3. Steps to Reproduce # Create a new page # Add a link of another Confluence page by: #* Directly pasting a link of another Confluence page #* Go to Insert More Content > Link > Search > Insert for any other Confluence page # Change the link color using the palette to a different color.  # In the Edit mode, the links will seem to have their color changed.  !CONFSERVER-57949-replication steps.png|thumbnail! # Publish. h3. Expected Results The links will have their color changed h3. Actual Results The link will maintain the the blue color regardless of the color that was assigned to it. h3. Notes * The issue seems to occur if a whole link has its color changed. If you attempt to change the color of part of the link. The new color will be reflected after the change.  * The issue does not affect links to outside of Confluence such as {{https://www.google.com}} h3. Workaround #1: Source Editor # Download and install the [Confluence Source Editor|https://marketplace.apps/1210722/confluence-source-editor?hosting=server&tab=overview] app in your Confluence instance. # Edit the problematic page and click the *<>* button to edit its Storage Format. # You will need to adapt the piece of code for the colored links. Starting from this non-functional, green-colored link example: # Add an {{}} tag right after the {{}} node. Also add its closing tag, {{}}. # Inside this new tag, add a _span_ tag with the color settings on its """"style"""" parameter. You can either copy or move the text from the existing code to the new location. Remember to also move or copy the closing tag, {{}} to the proper location. In the example below, I moved the text: # Inside the _span_ tag, add the link text - repeat the same value of the """"ri:content-title"""" attribute: # Save and publish the page. h3. Workaround #2: HTML Macro # Go to !https://confluence.doc/files/590259974/590058056/1/1398739558493/Cog.png! > *Manage Apps* # Click the dropdown menu and select *All Apps*. # Click on """"x of 8 modules enabled"""". # Enable the *html (html-xhtml)* module. # Go to the problematic page and insert an [HTML Macro|https://confluence.doc/html-macro-38273085.html]. # Add some HTML code inside the macro according to your needs. For example: #* (!) Note that this will be a """"hardcoded"""" link and will not inherit some Confluence features like automatic link renaming when the page gets moved or renamed. We suggest using a [Tiny Link|https://confluence.confkb/the-differences-between-various-url-formats-for-a-confluence-page-278692715.html] instead of the default link. # Save the page."""
comments: "We observe the same in version 6.15.4. Changing part of the link sometimes works, but not always. It is sometimes possible to add color to the whole link, a few letters at a time, but I could not reproduce it with all links. This impacts teams that rely upon color-coding links on their pages.    Please let me add that this issue did not occur in version 6.2." [SEP] "My users are very disappointed that this fairly basic functionality has been lost in an upgrade intended to improve user experience. It has effectively removed their ability to consistently format their spaces. I hope this will be fixed ASAP." [SEP] "Does this effect 6.14.3, too?" [SEP] "[~<USER>, yes, Confluence 6.14.3 is affected by this bug.   It seems like this bug is introduced in 6.14.0 and all versions after that will be affected."

summary: "Unable to create spaces in 6.11.x to 6.15.2 when a space blueprint plugin has been removed"
description: """h3. Summary    A bug in 6.11.0 to 6.15.2 leads to the Create Space dialog not loading, and giving an  """"unknown server error"""" in the UI after clicking on the Create Space button.    A pre-exiting KB has almost 100% effectiveness with some exceptions that are environmental, not the application. Here's the KB:   - [https://confluence.<USER>confkb/creating-new-page-or-space-loads-forever-744329880.html]    This was caused by the space blueprints table AO_54C900_SPACE_BLUEPRINT_AO containing a reference to a blueprint that was no longer available.  E.g. the plugin that included the blueprint has been uninstalled.    Another situation is where the blueprint was partially enabled.    This bug is fixed in 6.13.5 and 6.15.3.      h3. Logs    This is the logging by default that is used to identify the problem:    Logs with debug on for AO:    It happens on UPGRADES from 6.x to 6.11.x or later versions and it happens with all databases, no common plugin used, dropping/truncating the tables always resolve the problem, with just one exception.    h3. Steps to reproduce    There are a few ways to reproduce it.  The most common and dangerous is:   * Create a space using the a space blueprint   * Disable the plugin module that provides that space blueprint   * Upgrade to 6.11.x or above   * Try to create a space    The """"it happens on upgrade"""" is because the bug was introduced there, and in previous versions it was fine to disable the space blueprint of a plugin module.   * When using 6.11.x or above   * Create a space using the a space blueprint   * Disable the plugin module that provides that space blueprint   * Try to create a space    Another way is to just disable a space blueprint plugin module without disabling the space blueprint item module, e.g.   * When using 6.11.x or above   * Go to Manage apps   * Turn off the team-space-blueprint module   * Try to create a space    h3. Workarounds    * Upgrade to 6.15.3 or 6.13.5  * Create spaces using the old UI.  You don't get to choose a space type, but you can still create spaces.  Start at http://<baseurl>/<contextpath>/spaces/createspace-start.action  * If you have disabled or uninstalled any space blueprint plugins or plugin modules you used to use, then re-enable them  * If those aren't enough, follow this KB to delete old space blueprint use from the database [https://confluence.<USER>confkb/creating-new-page-or-space-loads-forever-744329880.html]"""
comments: "Important to note - while the work around proscribed in the KB article works....it also deletes all global blueprints created by the user...so the work around is NOT an acceptable solution for us." [SEP] "This is the stacktrace from one of the support cases.  I'm not sure at this stage whether it's common between all of them.        " [SEP] "Some quick notes from my investigation of this:    * This bug can be reproduced by  ** Using a space blueprint to create a space, (e.g. create a documentation space) THEN  ** Disabling or uninstalling the plugin module for that space blueprint  ** Trying to create a space  * The NullPointerException stack trace is swallowed when you try to create the space, but shows up when you view the ""Global Templates and Blueprints"" screen  * The current workaround of deleting everything from AO_54C900_SPACE_BLUEPRINT_AO will fix it, but so will   ** deleting *just the row for the disabled space blueprint* OR  ** re-enabling/re-installing the disabled space blueprint  * I don't ha

summary: "Adding panel,excerpt,warning,note,tip or info macro to the page in edit mode will trigger mixed content action in browser"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Editing page with a panel, warning, note, tip or info macro over https in the browser will trigger mix content action, it will break the certificate trust on request of _plugins/servlet/confluence/placeholder/macro-heading?definition=e25vdGV9&locale=en_US&version=2_.  h3. Environment    Confluence 6.14.x  Confluence 6.15.x  Confluence 7.0.x    Connector with SSL  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a page and add an excerpt, warning, note, tip or info macro   # Open inspect tools/network tab and edit the page once again.    h3. Expected Results    The certificate will stay trusted (green) and all request will be resolved over HTTPS  h3. Actual Results    At least one request is resolved over HTTP and certificate is not trusted because of mixed content.   [^localhost_6.14.1.har.zip]  h3. Notes    The issue does not exist in 6.13.x  h3. Workaround    Some users have found the below custom HTML resolves the issue in Chrome:    Copy text/code below into *BODY* section of *Configuration --> Custom HTML*       """

summary: "Exporting a page to PDF does not include internal images when they are added as URL"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  Exporting a page to a PDF file doesn't render a Confluence image when the following conditions are met:   * The image is added to the exported page by using its link, not as an attachment.   * The Space holding the above image is *not* accessible to anonymous user.    ** Either by Space or Global permissions.   * The image is still rendered in the exported page.    h3. Environment  This issue was validated on multiple versions from *6.1.0* to *6.13.2*.  The described use case was still working as expected on Confluence *6.0.7*.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Fresh install of Confluence *6.13.2*.  # Enable _Anonymous Access_ on [Global Permissions|https://confluence.<USER>doc/global-permissions-overview-138709.html].   #* Enable both _Use Confluence_ and _View User Profiles_.   !global_permissions_img001.png|thumbnail!   # Create a Space named _Space Anonymous Access Enabled_ with Space Key _SAAE_.  # In Space _SAAE_ [enable the _View_ permission|https://confluence.<USER>doc/space-permissions-overview-139521.html] to anonymous users.   !space_permissions_img001.png|thumbnail!   # In Space _SAAE_, create an unrestricted page named _Page with Image_ and attach an image to it.   !sample_page_img001.png|thumbnail!   # Create a Space named _PDF Export Test_ with Space Key _PET_.   #* This space should have regular access to confluence users, but *no* access to anonymous.  # Create an unrestricted page named _Export Page_ in Space _PET_.   #* This page should contain 3 images: 1) an image *attached* to the page; 2) an image as a *link* to the image attached to the same page; and 3) an image as a *link* to the image in _Page with Image_.   #* The storage format of this page will look similar to the one below.     !sample_page_img002.png|thumbnail!   # [Export page _Export Page_ as a PDF|https://confluence.<USER>doc/export-content-to-word-pdf-html-and-xml-139475.html#ExportContenttoWord,PDF,HTMLandXML-ExportsinglepagestoPDF].      h3. Expected Results  The exported PDF file should have all 3 images rendered, since all of them are accessible to the user when accessing the page from the web UI.  The PDF file should look like this:  [^PET-ExportPage-607.pdf]     h3. Actual Results  The exported PDF file does not render the image that uses the link to the image attached to _Export Page_.  The PDF file looks like the following:  [^PET-ExportPage-610.pdf]      h3. Notes  The problem is *not* related to a link to an image in the same page, but the fact that its Space does not allow anonymous access.  However, this should not be a problem, since the user exporting the page has view access to the image.    The below query may help the Confluence administrator to find the pages that are potentially affected by this bug and notify the page creators by e-mail:    (!) This query was validated on PostgreSQL; you may need to change it according to your database flavor.  (!) Change _CONFLUENCE_BASE_URL_ to your Base URL to get accurate links to the affected pages.    h3. Workaround  Instead of using a web link to the image, use the [_Insert a file attached to another page_|https://confluence.<USER>doc/display-files-and-images-245827130.html#DisplayFilesandImages-FromAnotherPageInsertafileattachedtoanotherpage] functionality.    You may also use the Marketplace App [_Scroll PDF Exporter for Confluence_|https://marketplace.<USER>apps/7019/scroll-pdf-exporter-for-confluence?hosting=server&tab=overview], since it deals well with the scenario described in this bug report.    As an alternative, the user can rely on browser's functionality to save the current web page as a PDF file."""

summary: "Repair ancestor table scheduled job DB transaction is stuck"
description: """h4. Description    On confluence 6.14.x onward, a scheduled job was implemented called """"Repair the Ancestors Table"""". This job was created, as the name implies, to search for corrupt entries in the ancestor table and correct them at 3AM each day.  h4. Expected results    The scheduled job was supposed to run, check the data, perform the correction and close itself.  h4. Actual result    The Scheduled job runs however it does not close itself and remain in memory as a open transaction.  h4. How to replicate and behavior observed    Install a Confluence 6.14 or newer. Go to Confluence administration -> scheduled jobs and run manually the """"Repair the Ancestors Table"""". Or wait for it to run at 3AM each day.    On a MSSQL database, run this query which will show the open transactions:    This transaction won't be removed unless killed manually.    This was tested on a MYSQL database with the same result. The transaction was not finished after the scheduled job ended. Using this query will show the transaction opened:    The other databases, Postgres and Oracle, should be affected as well.    This appears in the confluence logs using SQL logging enabled:     * Another behavior observed within a customer environment, which might increase the severity of this bug, is that the transaction opened consumed disk space. For one case, it consumed 1GB per day and over the week it reached 6.4GB so this bug could cause a out of memory on the server if the transaction is not killed.    h4. Suggested Workaround    Disable the scheduled job """"Repair the Ancestors Table"""" so it won't run during 3AM. OR kill the transaction manually in the database."""
comments: "This issue prevents reindexing the database, at least with PostgreSql: Once {{reindex database <...>}} is issued, it hangs forever due to the locks not being released by this scheduler job, which in turn blocks Confluence." [SEP] "A fix for this issue is available to Server and Data Center customers in Confluence 6.14.2  [Upgrade|https://www.<USER>software/confluence/download?utm_source=confluence_bugfix&utm_campaign=confluence_bugfix&utm_medium=email] now or check out the [Release Notes|http://confluence.<USER>display/DOC/Confluence+6.14+Release+Notes?utm_source=confluence_bugfix&utm_campaign=confluence_bugfix&utm_medium=email] to see what other issues are resolved." [SEP] "Backport to 6.13.x Enterprise release?" [SEP] "[~<USER>, this fix is in 6.13.6 and above in the 6.13.x series. The fix version list has been updated."

summary: "As a Confluence user I would like to add a version comment when creating a new page"
description: """h3. Problem Definition  The Confluence Editor has a feature to add comments when publishing a new version of an existing page. This feature is not available when creating a new page, which would be important to record the reasoning of creating that page.  For example of a use case, when a page is migrated from another Wiki solution to Confluence, it would be interesting to add a version comment when the page is created (through the REST API in this use case).    h3. """

summary: "Fix existing stale drafts (for CONFSERVER-57458) with an upgrade task"
description: """When CONFSERVER-57458 was implemented, we did a fix to prevent stale drafts from becoming valid drafts during a content refactoring action (which can be triggered by actions such as page moves). But existing content can still have stale drafts that have had their modification date updated. The scope of this ticket is to try and provide an upgrading task which can identify all invalid data and correct it as well as all needed condition to run that upgrading task.    Ex:   - Find all drafts which have version < current version and lastmoddate > current lastmoddate then update the draft's lastmoddate < current lastmoddate   - Make sure we only run if Collaborative Editing (CE) is off (upgrade task might be not suitable for the CE on case because we can't delay running the upgrading task until we turn off CE)"""
