Suggested Solution  Implement a similar method/endpoint for the Confluence Server API.    h3. Why this is important  If administrators need to update a large number of page restrictions, the API would be helpful for automating some of this work.     h3. Workaround   The [<USER>Confluence CLI|https://bobswift.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/CSOAP/overview] can achieve this objective.     """

summary: "Cluster Monitoring panel shows that nodes are unable to reach each other due to mismatched ""serialVersionUID"" values for in the ""RemoteModuleCallable"" class"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  In a Data Center environment, an administrator may find that within <USER>> Clustering, the user interface shows that nodes are unable to reach one another other with errors like:  {quote}  The node [xxxxxx] is temporarily not reachable. Please check the server logs.  {quote}    The actual cluster is up and running, despite what the UI is suggesting. However, the message in the UI is alarming to administrators and should be corrected.    h3. Environment  This issue was first observed in a 2-node Confluence Data Center 6.6.3 cluster using AWS cluster join method. Both nodes were using Java 8 update 162:      h3. Steps to Reproduce  Unknown, the issue may be intermittent as {{serialVersionUID}} looks to be auto-generated by the JVM at run time.    h3. Expected Results  Cluster Monitoring UI shows that nodes are able to communicate with each other.    h3. Actual Results  Cluster Monitoring UI shows that nodes cannot reach one another. However, the actual cluster itself is up and running, despite what this UI is saying.    Logs the following corresponding warnings:        h3. Notes  Some notes from Development review:  {quote}  We should manually set the `serialVersionUID` in the class `RemoteModuleCallable`, instead of having it autogenerate. Usually, that generated ID is going to be the same in both nodes, but because this is done by the JVM internally, any minute differences in the environment or just due to sheer luck, a different ID gets generated by different nodes.  {quote}    h3. Workaround  Restarting Confluence _may_ help, but due to the random nature of this problem, it is not 100% guaranteed to resolve the issue."""
comments: "I just faced this issue, was revolved with a rolling restart of all nodes. I also dropped the plugin-cache directories on the first node which was restarted."

description: """h3. +Summary+    In [Restore Passwords To Recover <USER>Rights|https://confluence.<USER>doc/restore-passwords-to-recover-admin-user-rights-158390.html], instead of redirecting users to [Configuring System Properties|https://confluence.<USER>doc/configuring-system-properties-168002854.html], it is better if we add a 'Expand/Collapse' section and all the steps from Configuring System Properties under the step 2 :   {quote}Edit {{<installation-directory>/bin/setenv.sh}} or {{setenv.bat}} and add the following system property, replacing {{<your-password>}} with a unique, temporary password.     See [Configuring System Properties|https://confluence.<USER>doc/configuring-system-properties-168002854.html] for more information on using system properties.  {quote}  Also, for the step 3 :   {quote}[Start Confluence|https://confluence.<USER>doc/start-and-stop-confluence-838416264.html] *manually* (don't start Confluence as a service).  {quote}  Again, instead of redirecting user to [Start and Stop Confluence, |https://confluence.<USER>doc/start-and-stop-confluence-838416264.html].    It will be easier for the user when all the information is on one page."""

A floating table of contents would be a huge help for our readers to keep track of which step of the procedure they are in. I'd be very grateful to see this implemented!" [SEP] "My vote as well, this would be a fantastic feature"

Also, the user will only be able to preview other images if they are clicked on, otherwise, we can't navigate through them ( <  >).  h3. description: """h3. Summary    The Spaces List Macro provides a link to each space's home page on the list, however the URL on the link is not a direct link to the page itself, which stops any macro/add-on that uses that URL from working correctly. This occurs regardless of whether the Space home page is the default one or not.  h3. summary: "Installing Confluence in OpenSUSE Tumbleweed through the installer fails"
description: """h3. Summary      * Is is not possible to install Confluence using the Linux installer (.bin format) in OpenSUSE Tumbleweed.    h3. Environment   * Linux OpenSUSE Tumbleweed  * Fedora29    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Download the Linux installer (.bin format)  # Make the installer executable  # Run the installer    h3. Expected Results      * You'll be able to proceed with the installation normally, following the steps described in [Installing Confluence on Linux|https://confluence.<USER>doc/installing-confluence-on-<USER>143556824.html]    h3. Actual Results     * In Confluence 6.14, as soon as you execute the .bin file, the following message is shown:      or     {code:borderStyle=solid}  Unpacking JRE ...  Starting Installer ...  java.lang.NullPointerException  at sun.awt.FontConfiguration.getVersion(FontConfiguration.java:1264)  at sun.awt.FontConfiguration.readFontConfigFile(FontConfiguration.java:219)  at sun.awt.FontConfiguration.init(FontConfiguration.java:107)  at sun.awt.X11FontManager.createFontConfiguration(X11FontManager.java:774)  {code}    h3. Notes   * In OpenSUSE Tumbleweed, the _fontconfig_ is already installed, but its dependency, _fontconfig2_, is replaced by the _libfreetype6_ package.  * A similar issue was reported in [Confluence Server 6.13 or later fails with library not loaded error when starting up on MacOS|https://confluence.<USER>confkb/confluence-server-6-13-or-later-fails-with-library-not-loaded-error-when-starting-up-on-macos-961781427.html], but the workaround proposed by it isn't possible because the installer doesn't start at all.  * Also, it seems like this issue is related to AdoptOpenJDK 8 having a broken dependency on _libfreetype.6.dylib_, but trying to install Confluence 6.12 through the installer also fails.    h3. Workaround   * Install Confluence using the _tar.gz_ file."""

summary: "Page comparison in Confluence has a high heap usage and might contribute to OOM errors"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  [Comparing two versions of a page|https://confluence.<USER>doc/page-history-and-page-comparison-views-139379.html#PageHistoryandPageComparisonViews-Comparetwoversions] consumes a high amount of memory and might contribute to out of memory errors.    This is intensified when the _Send batched notifications_ scheduled job runs and needs to send e-mails about changed content.    How to identify if the bug is affecting your instance during periods of high load:  # High heap usage.  # High GC activity, including concurrent and Full GC.  # If a heap dump is taken, you will notice a significant number of instances (reachable or unreachable) of any of the below classes.   #* {{org.eclipse.compare.rangedifferencer}}   #* {{com.atlassian.confluence.diff}}   #* {{org.outerj.daisy.diff}}      h3. Environment  This was validated on Confluence Server on version *6.13.2*.  It can definitely impact 6.14.1 since there were no improvements on that area, but wasn't tested on that version.  This affects Confluence Server or Data Center on any version prior (and including) to the highlighted above.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Fresh install of Confluence.  # Start your preferred [live monitoring tool|https://confluence.<USER>doc/live-monitoring-using-the-jmx-interface-150274182.html] and start to monitor your Confluence instance.   #* _Jconsole_ is used throughout this test.  # Create a test space named _Test Page Comparison_.  # Create a sample page named _Sample Page Comparison_ and publish the 1st version with no contents.  # Edit and publish a new version of the page with the contents from [^sample_page_v1.txt] storage format.   #* You need to use the [Source Editor|https://confluence.<USER>conf613/confluence-storage-format-964960676.html] to edit the storage format of a page.  # Edit and publish a new version of the page with the contents from [^sample_page_v2.txt] storage format.  # Access the _Page History_ of _Sample Page Comparison_ and at this point you should have 3 versions as the image below.   !page_history_img001.png|thumbnail!    #* Keep this page opened in a tab.  # In a new tab, access the [Cache Statistics admin page|https://confluence.<USER>doc/cache-statistics-150472.html] and flush *all* caches.  # Go back to the tab with the _Page History_ of _Sample Page Comparison_ opened.  # Select _v. 2_ and _CURRENT (v. 3)_ and click on _Compare selected versions_.    h3. Expected Results  Confluence performs page comparison using minimum server resources.    h3. Actual Results  Confluence uses a lot of heap to perform page comparison.   !jmx_comparing_sample_page_img001.png|thumbnail!     h3. Notes  The situation may get worse on large Confluence instances, such as on Enterprise deployments with Confluence Data Centers, on which users perform multiple changes on pages of different complexity.  When the _Send batched notifications_ scheduled job runs it will compare versions of watched pages and send e-mails to users.  This requires more heap and CPU resources as shown in the below graph after 5 pages were edited with the same contents as the example above.   !jmx_batch_notification_5pages_img002.png|thumbnail!     h4. Performance on Scale  There are some situations on which the performance is impacted depending on the scale:  # Heap usage increases depending on the number of watchers on the page -- for cases triggered by the batch notifications job.  # Heap usage increases depending on the number of pages to be processed -- for cases triggered by the batch notifications job.  # Heap usage increases depending on the complexity of the page and the changes between versions.    h5. Number of watchers of the page   !jconsole_img001.png|thumbnail!   *1 watcher on a sample page*     !jconsole_img002.png|thumbnail!   *1,000 watchers on the same page*      h5. Number of pages to process   !jconsole_img005.png|thumbnail!   *10 pages with 1 watcher per page*     !jconsole_img006.png|thumbnail!   *250 pages with 1 watcher per page*    h3. Workaround  There's no workaround when the user needs to compare page versions from the web UI.  To minimize the impact that could be caused by the _Send batched notifications_ scheduled job, users must disable the _Show changed content_ option in their [Email Notifications|https://confluence.<USER>doc/email-notifications-145162.html] option.  There's an enhancement request to globally disable this functionality, as described in [CONFSERVER-43671|https://jira.<USER>browse/CONFSERVER-43671]. You may want to check one of the workarounds described in [this suggestion report|https://jira.<USER>browse/CONFSERVER-43671].    As a practical example of the benefits of disabling this functionality on all users, the screenshot below shows heap and CPU usage while running the _Send batched notifications_ on the same scenario of the _Notes Section_, but all users watching all pages had the _Show changed content_ option disabled.   !jmx_batch_notification_5pages_noContent_img001.png|thumbnail!    Users will still receive an e-mail notifying changes on the watched page, but no page comparison is run.    h3. Further Reading  [Page History and Page Comparison|https://confluence.<USER>doc/page-history-and-page-comparison-views-139379.html]  [Email Notifications|https://confluence.<USER>doc/email-notifications-145162.html]  [How to globally disable e-mail notifications with page content|https://confluence.<USER>confkb/how-to-globally-disable-e-mail-notifications-with-page-content-718670714.html]  [CONFSERVER-43671|https://jira.<USER>browse/CONFSERVER-43671] -- _Ability to disable """"Show Changed Content"""" site-wide_  """
comments: "A fix for this issue is available to Server and Data Center customers in Confluence 7.0.1  [Upgrade|https://www.<USER>software/confluence/download?utm_source=confluence_bugfix&utm_campaign=confluence_bugfix&utm_medium=email] now or check out the [Release Notes|https://confluence.<USER>doc/confluence-7-0-release-notes-973479448.html?utm_source=confluence_bugfix&utm_campaign=confluence_bugfix&utm_medium=email] to see what other issues are resolved.  "

summary: "Jira attachment not showing in PDF export even after logging into Jira"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  # When a Jira issue is added into Confluence , using the *Table* display option, we are able to view attachment, if it's in one of the table fields.  # But if we were to export the page as PDF, it does not export out.  # *Note:* We will have to be logged into Jira in the same browser session.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Create an issue in Jira, and add an attachment to the *Description* field.  # Add the issue in Confluence, using the Jira Issue macro, and change the display option to *Table*.  # Add the *Description* to the list of columns to display.  # The attachment will be displayed, but if we were to export the page to PDF, the image will not be exported out.    h3. Expected Results  # As long as the user is able to view the attachment on the page, the exported PDF should have the attachment as well.    The below exception is thrown in the atlassian-confluence.log file:       Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""
comments: "Note that this is functionality that worked previously in our environment.  Thus, something has changed recently to break this functionality.  My Confluence document was relying on the ability to render an attachment (in my case, a .png attachment) within a JIRA issue/table.  I would like to see the priority of this issue raised. "

summary: "Path problems with the REST API Browser under some Confluence versions"
description: """h3. Summary    Have a plugin with a REST API deployed that specifies a package: *applinks-oauth*:        # Open the *REST API Browser version 3.2.2* on a *Confluence 6.13.2*   # Deselect """"Show only public APIs""""  # Type """"applinks-oauth"""" in the browser  # Notice that the  path is duplicated:   !image2019-2-8_15-42-1.jpg|thumbnail!     {{applinks-oauth/1.0/applinks-oauth/1.0}}    instead of     {{applinks-oauth/1.0}}    Since the path is wrong a call of the service via the REST API Browser results in a HTTP 404.    *Expected Result*  Incorrect paths resulting in a 404 when called with the REST API Browser.    *Actual Result*  Proper URLs to the REST services of a add-on that specifies a <package> element.    h3. Notes    * When {{package}} element is present in the rest module then you get a duplicate path  * On a *Confluence 5.10.9* this is not a problem. All URLs are correctly shown with *REST API Browser version 3.2.2*.  * Add-ons that do not specify the <package> element work fine.  * If the {{package}} element is removed, then the REST API Browser works fine even with *Confluence 6.13.2*.   * The REST API service is usable outside of the REST API Browser. However, the REST API Browser happens to display incorrect URLs (with the duplication) and each call will results in a 404.  """
comments: "This issue also affects Jira v8 with REST API Browser 3.2.2. ""api/2"" are duplicated in path, for example: api/2/api/2/field    Any possibility that there will be created a ticket for Jira environment as well?" [SEP] "We are also seeing this after upgrading to Jira 8" [SEP] "Also seeing this on Bitbucket 6.8.0, and Jira 8.5.0" [SEP] "Created an issue on the plugin's repo: https://bitbucket.org/atlassian/rest-api-browser/issues/11/path-problems-with-the-rest-api-browser" [SEP] "Still seeing this in Jira 8.6.1" [SEP] "This is now resolved - https://ecosystem.atlassian.net/browse/RAB-31 - Please upgrade to the latest version."

summary: "Implement concurrent editing of documents using Companion App"
description: """h3. Problem Definition  If more than one user is editing the same document in Companion App, there is no warning, and the last person to save the document will overwrite any other changes made by previous users.  h3. Suggested Solution  Implement concurrent editing or warn when another user is editing the document to prevent overwrites.    h3. Why this is important  Currently, a user could lose a lot of work they've done if they are unaware that another user is editing.     h3. Workaround   No current workaround."""
comments: "At minimum there should be a warning at upload time if the version on the server has changed since the initial download.  While I understand that this same issue exists with users manually editing attachments the companion app is a great place to address that problem." [SEP] "Attachment locking is also available through the the [Cenote Lockpoint|https://marketplace.<USER>apps/36199/cenote-lockpoint-attachment-checkout?hosting=server&tab=overview] app, which is fully integrated with the Atlassian Companion. This helps prevent the type of overwrite scenario mentioned in this issue, and it also provides a number of other tools for managing locked attachments. (Disclaimer: I work for Cenote.)" [SEP] "It would be very nice if this was solved. Right now you are forcing users to use other tools when they need to do do collaboration such as google, dropbox, sharepoint <USER> Please take Confluence to the next level and also start to support collaboration much better so Confluence could be our number 1 go-to-softwar

summary: "Companion app leaks memory if the process is started twice"
description: """h3. Summary    The Companion app leaks memory if the process is started twice.      {panel:title=Issue fixed on Atlassian Companion App version 0.6.0|borderStyle=dashed|borderColor=#cccccc|titleBGColor=#f7d6c1|bgColor=#ffffce}   # There will still be *two processes* running for the Companion app even on 0.6.0.   # But there should be only *one instance* of the app, which can be see with there being only one Companion App icon on the task-bar.{panel}  h3. Environment   * Only observed on Windows   * App installed with *.msi* file    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Install the Companion app with the *.msi* file using the following script   ** For 32-bit OS:       ** For 64-bit OS:       # Reboot the computer    h3. Expected Results    The app is started normally and no duplicate processes or memory issues are observed.  h3. Actual Results    Duplicate processes for the app are observed, as we can see here:    !task-manager-view.png|thumbnail!    One of the processes will *leak memory at a rate of around 20 bytes per second*. We have some cases where the memory usage reached more than 1GB before the app crashed after running in this situation for a few hours.  h3. Notes    The installation method that triggers this situation is a workaround for the following bugs:   # https://jira.<USER>browse/CONFSERVER-57792   # https://jira.<USER>browse/CONFSERVER-57541    After running this installation method and rebooting the computer, we end up having two entries on the registry that trigger the app startup, one under the key *HKEY_CURRENT_USER* and the other under *HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE*.    Even though this is not triggered under normal circumstances, the idea of this bug is that one of the following situations must be avoided:   # The app should check if it is already running before starting a new process, or   # If the possibility of having two processes is expected, the secondary process should not leak memory    If the app has one of those protection mechanisms bundled, we exclude the possibility of compromising the end-user computer resources.  h3. Workaround 1    Remove one of the registry entries (either from HKLM or HKCU tree), which can be done by an administrator. Only one of the following entries must exist in the registry:      (!) For a 64 bit OS, the registry tree looks like this:    h3. Workaround 2    Uncheck the *Launch at startup* box on the app itself, which can be done by the end user. This will effectively remove the registry entry from *HKEY_CURRENT_USER*, but the app will still start automatically on reboot due to the entry under *HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE*."""
comments: "Why did you close this issue?    I don't believe anything has be done to resolved this issue.    I'm a paying customer.     " [SEP] "Hey [~logistics], have you upgraded your Companion version to 0.6.0? " [SEP] "I'm trying, but the .msi packaging is still not very practical    That being said, what I find bad here is that Atlassian closed this bug without any explanation: The original submitter wrote a very clear explanation of his understanding of the bug:    _""the idea of this bug is that one of the following situations must be avoided:_   # _The app should check if it is already running before starting a new process, or_   # _If the possibility of having two processes is expected, the secondary process should not leak memory""_    Atlassian didn't answer if the possibility of having 2 processes is expected. So, we can't figure out what has been fixed here.     " [SEP] "[~logistics]     Multiple instances of Companion are not expected. As of Companion 0.6.0, only one Companion main process can run on t

summary: "JIRA links fail to auto-convert to JIRA macros"
description: """h3. Summary   * When a user created a new page on Confluence and pasted a Jira issue link, Confluence wouldn't auto-create Jira macro for this link. Once the page is published and we edit and pasted the Jira issue link again, Confluence would auto-create the Jira Macro.    h3. Environment  * Confluence 6.6.x  * Jira 7.6.9, 7.10.x, 8.5.0  * Application Link    h3. Steps to Reproduce  * Create a new page  * Paste a link to a Jira issue  * Save the page    h3. Expected Results  * Jira Issue should auto-convert to Jira Issue Macro.    h3. Actual Results  * Confluence wouldn't auto-create Jira macro for this link. Once the page is published and we edit and pasted the Jira issue link again, Confluence would auto-create the Jira Macro.    h3. Notes  * Once we edited the page and paste the Jira issue link again, Confluence would auto-convert the link to a Jira Issue macro  * The issue is reproducible in Confluence 6.6.8 and 6.6.10 version and Jira version 7.6.9  * This issue is also reproducible if above-mentioned Confluence version is linked with Jira version above 7.10 as well    h3. Workarounds  * When creating a new page, save and then edit before pasting Jira issue links  * Use the Insert option from the top right and choose Jira Issue/Filter"""

summary: "Support the Merging Confluence Server Spaces into Single Instance for Confluence Server"
description: """h3. Problem Definition  When two large instances of Confluence need to be merged the process of exporting and importing spaces individually becomes very cumbersome    h3. Suggested Solution  It would be useful to have support for support importing multiple Confluence Server spaces.  A tool similar to the [Cloud Migration Assistance for Confluence|https://confluence.<USER>confcloud/migrate-from-confluence-server-to-cloud-724765529.html] could work    h3. Workaround   # Export spaces by either:  ## Individually [Export the desired space as a full XML backup|https://confluence.<USER>doc/export-content-to-word-pdf-html-and-xml-139475.html]  ## Or per this [community post|https://community.<USER>t5/Questions/Is-there-a-way-to-mass-export-spaces/qaq-p/244098], utilize [<USER>s Confluence Command Line Interface (CLI)'s|https://marketplace.<USER>apps/284/confluence-command-line-interface-cli] and its [exportSpace|https://bobswift.atlassian.net/wiki/display/CSOAP/Reference#Reference-exportSpace] action to bulk export spaces to XML  # [Restore each of the spaces to target Confluence instance|https://confluence.<USER>doc/restoring-a-space-152036.html]  * (!) You can't import into an earlier version of Confluence. So if the Confluence instances are on different versions, you may be limited by this and need to upgrade  ** For example, if you export a space from Confluence 5.9, you can't import it into Confluence 5.5.  * (!) If the space to be imported is very large, you may need to temporarily increase the memory available to Confluence. See [How to fix out of memory errors by increasing available memory|https://confluence.<USER>confkb/how-to-fix-out-of-memory-errors-by-increasing-available-memory-154071.html]"""
comments: "We are merging the Confluence instances from two companies. It is going to take a couple of weeks to move the 74 spaces from one production system to another. Based on 5 spaces a day accounting for possible space key conflicts. With a tool to import just the spaces we want to move from Confluence system to another in a bulk function. It would save us a ton of time and therefore money." [SEP] "Totally agree with this request. Is there any update about this?"

summary: "Warning regarding the performance impact of index rebuilding is shown after user commits"
description: """Warning regarding the performance impact of index rebuilding is shown after user commits    """"The search index allows searching of Confluence content. If you are having troubles with search, you may need to rebuild the search index. Please note, can severely affect the performance of your instance - it can take hours for some large instances"""".         There should be a confirmation dialog allowing the user to postpone or abort the rebuild."""

summary: "Disabling the Delete Own permission still allows the users to delete their own content"
description: """h3. Summary    * The *Delete Own* space permission is meant to give you permission to delete any pages, blogs, attachments and comments you've created in this space (regardless of whether other users have subsequently edited the content), according to our documentation ([Space Permissions Overview|https://confluence.<USER>conf613/space-permissions-overview-964960242.html]). This means that in case this permission is disabled, it shouldn't allow a user to delete the own user's content created, however, even though this permission is disabled, we are still able to delete our own comments/pages/<USER>    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a user who is a member of the _confluence-users_ group only (user doesn't have administration permissions)   # In a specific space, go to *Space Tools >> Permissions >> Edit Permissions*  ## Remove all group permissions, by deselecting all the permissions applied to each group  ## Add the user individually and grant this user the following permissions:  ##* *All - View*  ##* *Pages - Add*  ##* *Blog - Add*  ##* *Attachments - Add*  ##* *Comments - Add*  ## Save the changes made  ## Have this user comment on a page and refresh the page    h3. Expected Results    * The user is able to add a comment, but cannot delete it.    h3. Actual Results    * The user adds a comment and not only the *Delete* option is available, but is also functional, allowing the user to delete his own content (even though the *Delete Own* permission is disabled).    h3. Workaround  N/A"""
comments: "This issue is affecting our instance of Confluence. Please add Server 6.14.2 as having this issue.     Adding a vote to get visibility on this issue. "

summary: "Customize Global and Space Activity"
description: """h3. Problem Definition    * Currently, the Global and Space Activity of Confluence shows pre-defined information about an instance, without the possibility of customizing it.    h3. Suggested Solution    * Add the ability for our customers to customize what information they would like to have displayed in their Global and Space Activity.    h3. Why this is important    * Each company has its own necessary information for creating reports and auditing purposes. Some information shown in the Global and Space Activity might not be very relevant to display, so customizing this area would reduce the unnecessary content shown."""
comments: "This is a very important and useful feature. Please release it soon."

summary: "Read-only administrator permission"
description: """h3. Problem Definition   * Currently, there isn't an option to give a user access to the General Configuration screen or the Space Tools unless the user is a Confluence administrator or a space administrator.    h3. Suggested Solution   * Create a middle permission role, where a user can access this information available on the Confluence's administration screen (as well as the space's administration area) but cannot edit any information of it.    h3. Why this is important   * This is important to companies who wish to give some users access to their instance's information for auditing purposes, without risking having these users edit any settings of the instance."""

summary: "instructional sections in templates"
description: """In our templates we need to include detailed instructions and examples how to fill them with content. The existing instructional text-feature is not enough, as it    * can only hold one line   * can't accept formatting   * is indexed and leads to misleading matches in search.   * is copied on creating a page from the template.    We suggest a new macro *""""instructional section""""*.   * Shown only in edit mode   * <USER>formatting   * Content can't be changed by the page editor   * Page-Editor can hide/unhide  all instructional sections    * Content is not indexed.   * Multiple instructional sections can be interspersed with normal content, so that the instructions can be freely placed where  needed.   * Instructional sections should be clearly discernible, for example by special background color or some other adornment   * Content is NOT copied to the page on creation, but rather is referencing the text from the template. Changes to an instructional section in the template are reflected while editing existing pages based on the template.    Of course, the last point means the page has to maintain a link to the template it was created from. If that is too much of a breaking change, an alternative approach would be to have the instructional section macro reference a section in a helper-page. But this reference has to be rendered in edit-mode, which normal macros can't do.    *This enables teams to create and maintain consistent documentation.*"""

summary: "Enforce TLS v1.2 for the Confluence Mail Server SMTP Protocol"
description: """Currently, TLS 1.0 is used when *Use TLS* option is checked in *Confluence Mail Server Configuration* while TLS 1.2 is used in Jira Mail Server SMTP Protocol.    Therefore, it would be more convenient to *enforce TLS v1.2 for the Confluence Mail Server SMTP Protocol*.  h4.  Workaround    Add the following parameter in Confluence system properties:    (i) Please refer to [Configuring System properties|https://confluence.<USER>doc/configuring-system-properties-168002854.html] to add the parameter.     """
comments: "Some users reported that the issue is fixed with Confluence 7.1. With local tests, I could confirm this." [SEP] "I'm on Confluence 7.1.2, have the Use TLS checkbox on and have NOT configured the proposed workaround in Confluence system properties. The system does not have a problem sending email via SMTP server that does not accept TLS 1.0.    Prior to upgrading to 7.1, it only worked with the workaround and would throw an error without it."

summary: "Confluence Mobile Server MDM (unsigned binaries)"
description: """Hi,    I contacted customer support with this request and they recommend I open an issue here.    I am a developer for a government contractor attempting to bring Confluence Mobile to our company. One of our requirements for mobile applications is they must run in Blackberry UEM, an encryption service that protects sensitive data.    The team managing Blackberry UEM has requested of me unsigned binaries for Confluence Mobile so we can repackage them and run them in our environment. My understanding is these are .ipa and .apk files which have not yet been signed with a distributor's certificate.    Is there a process in place for acquiring this? We noticed Infosysta offers these files for Mobile for Jira and are hoping we can find the same with Confluence.    Thank you,    Mitch Dyer  Software Developer  General Dynamics Mission Systems"""

summary: "Would like to limit attachment size in Confluence per file type"
description: """h3. Problem Definition    We have in the past seen issues with the Content Indexing processing and application degradation due to large mp4 video attachments being viewed or downloaded. We attempted to alleviate this by decreasing the attachment size from default to 526.84 MB. This seemed to work for some time until recently where we have had two occurrences of a user viewing an mp4 attachment and it subsequently causing a block on our content indexing queue.   For each occurrence once the video was closed out the Content Indexing started processing again.  h3. Suggested Solution    We actually have 2 suggestions to improve the way we work with attachments that would help in this case:   # Would like to limit the attachments size per file type, so we can limit the size of videos being attached.   # Would like to be able to choose which file types can be uploaded or which should be restricted without using a third-party plugin for that.    h3. Why this is important    To avoid issues with the Content Indexing processing and application degradation due to large mp4 video attachments being viewed or downloaded.  h3. Workaround   * Use third-party plugins to restrict file types from being attached."""

summary: "As a user I want Page Index Macro in Confluence to render the pages until it reaches the page.index.macro.max.pages threshold"
description: """h3. Problem Definition  When using the [Page Index Macro|https://confluence.<USER>doc/page-index-macro-182682092.html] in a large Space with more pages than the configured {{page.index.macro.max.pages}} threshold, the macro won't be rendered in the page showing a message as below.   !space_index_message_img001.png|thumbnail!     h3. Suggested Solution  The macro could render the pages until it reaches the threshold, providing the user with some information instead of none.  Also, it could simply provide the summary count of pages starting with each letter and dynamically render the pages' title when a letter is selected by the user.    h3. Why this is important  This is important for a better user experience.  While the Confluence administrator can limit how many pages are rendered/processed by the Macro, the user can still see content, even on a large Space.     h3. Workaround   None so far."""

summary: "Confluence DB Connection Pool Startup Check"
description: """Confluence appears to ignore Tomcat Datasource DB config for this check. I get the nag screen """"Your database connection pool size is not configured. The recommended minimum size is 60"""" after changing to DS config following these instructions - [https://confluence.<USER>conf64/configuring-a-datasource-connection-936511522.html#Configuringadatasourceconnection-3.ConfigurethedatasourceinTomcat]    According to  [https://confluence.<USER>confkb/startup-check-database-connection-pool-size-960713815.html] this shouldn't be the case.    I'll attach server.xml, web.xml and confluence.cfg.xml files. They clearly show maxTotal size is 70 with HTTP maxThreads of 56.    It just looks like the check assumes you have configured c3p0 and is probbaly using the default value for it since it's not configured.         h3. Workaround  Use C3P0.   """

summary: "Space Wizard fails after deactivating server app with space blueprint"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  Space Wizard fails with """"An unknown server error has occurred"""" after deactivating server app with space blueprint    h3. Steps to Reproduce (You may need to shift-reload the page before launching the space wizard)   # Have an add-on installed that provides at least on space blueprint  # Deactivate this add-on  # Try to create a new space with the space wizard (Spaces -> Create Space)    h3. Expected Results  The wizard shows the space blueprints to select from.    h3. Actual Results  The space wizard shows """"An unknown server error has occurred""""   !space-wizard.png|thumbnail!   The logfile shows this:  {code}  [INFO] [talledLocalContainer] ..... WARN [http-nio-1990-exec-7] [confluence.impl.hibernate.ConfluenceHibernateTransactionManager] doRollback Performing rollback. Transactions:  [INFO] [talledLocalContainer] ->[PluginReadWriteTx]: PROPAGATION_REQUIRED,ISOLATION_DEFAULT (Session #.....)  [INFO] [talledLocalContainer] – referer: ...../confluence/pages/viewpage.action?spaceKey=PP&title=x | url: /confluence/rest/create-dialog/1.0/space-blueprint/dialog/web-items | traceId: ..... | userName: .....  {code}    Checking the dev console logs:  The call {{GET /rest/create-dialog/1.0/space-blueprint/dialog/web-items}} results in {code}{""""errorCode"""":500,""""errorType"""":""""UNKNOWN""""}{code}    h3. Notes  This problem occurs on Confluence 6.11.2 and above (although we did not test every single released version of Confluence), but not on 6.11.1 and below (again, we tested some 6.x and 5.10.9 which did not show the problem). The release notes to 6.11.2 seem not to relate to this issue ...    If the add-on is reactivated, everything works fine again.    The problem shows on MySQL, PostgreSQL, and H2. It occurs in the Atlassian SDK as well as in production.    h3. Workaround  The workaround mentioned in https://community.<USER>t5/Confluence-questions/Confluence-add-Space-fails-An-unknown-server-error-has-occurred/qaq-p/893077 fixes the problem. In fact, dropping the AO_54C900_SPACE_BLUEPRINT_AO after server stop, removing the plugin cache directories, and restarting the server also fixes the problem: new spaces can again be created.    {code:sql}  DROP TABLE AO_54C900_SPACE_BLUEPRINT_AO;  {code}    There is a minor difference in the table between the 6.11.1 and 6.14.0: The name column contains label keys instead of labels in the NAME column. But replacing these with arbitrary strings did not fix the problem. Even if the server is restarted and the plugin cache dirs are cleared.  """

summary: "Using the Page Index Macro in a Space with thousands of pages takes a long time to complete and may overload the Confluence server"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  When loading a page with the [Page Index Macro|https://confluence.<USER>doc/page-index-macro-182682092.html], the page takes a long time to render.  If a timeout is configured (i.e. from a proxy), then the page is not loaded because of it.    Depending on how many times the macro is loaded at the same time, it may overload the Confluence server and eventually make it unusable.      h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Have a fresh install of Confluence.  # Login to Confluence as an _admin_ (part of the {{confluence-administrators}} group).  # Create a sample Space and add thousands of pages to it.   #* 1k pages is enough as a proof of concept.   #* If you want to add more complexity to the issue, create 4.5k pages.  # Create a page in the same space and add the Page Index Macro.   #* When the page is loaded after saving it, the macro will render fast -- this occurs because the _admin_ bypass the page restrictions validation.  # Login to Confluence as a regular user (*not* part of the {{confluence-administrators}} group).  # Access the page with the Page Index Macro.      h3. Expected Results  A page with the Page Index Macro will load within an acceptable time and the user won't experience a delay on rendering the macro.    h3. Actual Results  The page takes a long time to load, but will eventually.  In a simple computer, this is the time the macro takes to render depending on the number of pages within the Space:  ||Number\\of pages||Time (s)||  |100|0|  |250|0|  |500|8|  |1,000|32|  |2,500|252|  |4,998|961|    The results may differ depending on the HW used to run the tests, but it is clear the time to render the macro doesn't scale linearly with the number of pages.    h3. Notes  The situation may worsen because the macro is rendered synchronously. That means Confluence must fetch the macro content before retrieving any content to the user's client (browser).  The user might think the request was not processed and may try to refresh the page or try to access it in other browser windows, triggering new threads that will add more overhead to the Confluence _backend_.    Depending on several parameters (number of users trying to access the same page, an user refreshing the page multiple times, complexity of the Space), this macro may create a situation on which the HW resources (CPU, memory) will be highly consumed and eventually bring the instance down (or make it unusable to the users).    h5. How to identify if affected by this issue    Taking thread dumps during periods of high load, or before restarting Confluence on the event of an incident, shows one or more HTTP threads stuck with a similar stack trace as below.      Checking what Confluence does on the database, most of the time it is performing queries to validate if the current user has enough permissions to view the pages within the target Space.  Thousands of queries similar to the ones below may be found in the DB transaction log.      With [log profiling enabled|https://confluence.<USER>doc/troubleshooting-slow-performance-using-page-request-profiling-200987.html] you will notice most of the time is spent while doing permission management tasks.      h3. Workaround  The Confluence administrator may add the below JVM argument to restrict the macro from running on large spaces:    _Check [this KB|https://confluence.<USER>doc/configuring-system-properties-168002854.html] to know how to configure system properties._    Users will still be able to add the macro to pages, but when loading the page, the macro will render a message as the one below without running its background job:   !space_index_message_img001.png|thumbnail!     (!) _Note that you must be in one of the versions that fix bug [CONFSERVER-55450|https://jira.<USER>browse/CONFSERVER-55450] to have this functionality working as expected._  """

summary: "As a user I want Page Index Macro in Confluence to be rendered asynchronously to improve overal page loading time"
description: """h3. Problem Definition  Depending on the number of pages of the Space, the [Page Index Macro|https://confluence.<USER>doc/page-index-macro-182682092.html] in Confluence may take a long time render the content.  The task takes a long time on the server side (_backend_), delaying the load of the remaining of the page.    h3. Suggested Solution  The Page Index Macro should be rendered asynchronously, allowing the whole page to be loaded to the user even if the contents to render the macro have not been fetched.  While the macro is fetching content from the _backend_, a friendly message should appear in the page where the macro is placed so the user knows it is still running.    h3. Why this is important  The Confluence user may not have the understanding this is a time consuming macro, which would delay the rendering of the whole page.  Because of this, they may refresh the page or try to open it again in a new browser window, which may worsen the situation in the _backend_.     h3. Workaround   If the [Cache for Confluence|https://marketplace.<USER>apps/152/cache-for-confluence?hosting=server&tab=overview] App by <USER>is available, then the user can wrap the Page Index Macro with the [Future Macro|https://bobswift.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/CACHE/pages/42631201/Future+Macro]."""

summary: "ToC anchors don't work when the headings are from the 'Office Word' macro"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  If you insert a Table of Content macro above an Office Word macro that displays the contents of a Word file with headings, the anchors in the ToC macro just brings you to the top of the page instead of the section of the page where the correct heading is.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Create a new page.  # Attach a Word file that contains multiple headings.  # Insert a ToC macro to the page.  # Insert an Office Word macro to the page and choose to display the file you've just attached in Step #2.  # Go to the page and click on any of the headings that appear in the ToC macro.    h3. Expected Results  The anchor should bring you to the part of the page where the heading is at.    h3. Actual Results  You either remain at the top of the page or you get brought to the top of the page, if you already weren't.    h3. Workaround  Currently there is no known workaround for this behaviour. A workaround will be added here when available."""

summary: "Using a JQL for 'assignee was X during (date1, date2)' doesn't work in Confluence's Jira Issue Filter macro"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    When using the Jira Issue Filter macro in Confluence, you can't enter a JQL as shown below.      This JQL works fine in Jira and will display the results as expected, but Confluence throws an error message instead.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Link Confluence to Jira via application links.  # In Jira, run this JQL to confirm that it works and results are returned (change accordingly based on your instance).     # Create a new page and insert the Jira Issue Filter macro.  # Add in the following JQL to the macro.       h3. Expected Results  The same results should be displayed in Confluence as it did in Jira.    h3. Actual Results  Confluence displays the following error message.  {quote}The Jira server didn't understand your search query. If you entered JQL, please ensure that it's correctly formed. If you entered an issue key, ensure that it exists and you have permission to view it.{quote}    h3. Workaround  Add in another condition to the JQL. Any of these examples will work, so long as the condition you define is valid (i.e. a project by key 'test' and a user called 'foo' exists).    """

summary: "Provide the functionality to perform bulk delete for unsynced users"
description: """h3. Problem Definition  Starting from Confluence 6.13, admins can delete users that are unsynced from Confluence. Unsynced users are users that are not synced any more when an external user directory is being synchronized. This could be because the user has been removed from the external directory or it is because the user directory filters no longer include that user <USER>  While this feature is useful, the current implementation only allows the deletion of these users individually and there is no option to perform bulk delete.    h3. Suggested Solution  Having the bulk delete feature would greatly help."""
comments: "Hello,    As you are ""gathering interrest"" of this, this is one: because of GDPR laws in Europe, we are requested to delete personal information about people who left the company (this is why this ""unsync users"" feature has been introduced in Confluence 6.13.    As we are using Confluence since 6 years now, with a current population of 14.000 users, we have *hundreds* of people who left the company and we will now need to be removed. We can't humanely do it manually.    It's a good thing to have provided a way to keep the thing clean in the future, but you have to think about the past too.    In addition to that, if we would like to develop an automated process to cleanup people information in sync with their termination in the company, a REST API is needed.      " [SEP] "Please make it happen. This seems to be an easy enhancement.  Hao" [SEP] "Our instance has 18257 unsynced users atm.    What am i supposed to do? Run selenium scripts on it?" [SEP] "Well, I have around 90,000 unsynced users right now.

summary: "Big images does not resize well in Internet Explorer when Column and Section macro are used"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  Images and attachments will not resize well in Internet Explorer, when having Column and Section macros    h3. Environment  * Internet Explorer, IE 11.253    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Create a Confluence page with a section macro and 3 column macros inside it  # Add an image with a very high resolution in the 2nd column. Example:   #* Example page: !screenshot-1.png|thumbnail!   #* Example image with high resolution:   [^1.jpg]   #* Example page's storage format:    # Save the page    h3. Expected Results  The image will resize to fit the browser window.  The following screenshot is from Chrome where this is expected:   !screenshot-2.png|thumbnail!     h3. Actual Results  In Internet Explorer, The image will not resized to fit the browser window.   !screenshot-3.png|thumbnail!     h3. Workaround  Edit the page and change the image resolution to small  # Edit the page  # Resize the image to a smaller resolution   !screenshot-4.png|thumbnail!   # Save the page"""

summary: "Multimedia macro doesn't work properly with mp4 and mov videos"
description: """h3. Summary  When using the Multimedia macro to view videos, it might work or not. The video start but stops at a certain time saying that they are either corrupted or run into a decoding problem. It's unclear what causes the problem reported here since it's not consistent.    h3. Steps to replicate    # Install Confluence 6.12.x with either Oracle/Adopt java  # Create a test space/page  # Remain on the page Editor mode and Drag'n drop a mp4 or mov file (it will automatically become a multimedia macro), best if it's around 30mb or more.  # Execute the video with the browser dev console + network open to monitor the error.  # Firefox and Chrome were used and had different results depending on the file, not its type.  # We're going to see the below errors:  h4. In Chrome    h4. In Firefox      h3. Expected results    Video will run without problems.    h3. Actual results    Video suddenly stops and needs to be started again from where it stopped.    h3. Bug variation  The problem reported here is very similar to an older bug and looks like it's derived from:  - [Multimedia macro does not render mp4 videos|https://jira.<USER>browse/CONFSERVER-42759]    h3. Workaround    May use the user macro from:  - https://community.<USER>t5/Confluence-questions/What-are-you-using-for-streaming-video-to-confluence/qaq-p/614294  """
comments: "Thank you Artur for helping with this and creating the bug report! I am the initial user who brought this issue to support, and can perhaps add some context and additional information to users experiencing this issue.    * I can confirm we updated to Ver. 6.14.0 and the issue remained.    * If you don't use the MultiMedia macro,  and the MP4 just attaches to the page (you can tell the difference because instead of showing the video player it will show a generic thumbnail with the file name) then you also will see the ""corrupted..."" error shows when the video stops.   * The specific error I was receiving on Chrome was: *""The media playback was aborted due to a corruption problem or because the media used features your browser did not support.""*   * This appears to have something to do with the way Confluence serves the file to the browser (through the confluence page code, macro, <USER>..). It occurs if the MP4 being streamed is attached to the page.    * The MP4 file size does appear to play a factor

summary: "Meeting Notes blueprints should create new page under index page"
description: """When I first use the Meeting Notes template it creates the index page and the new meeting notes page at the same level i.e. root. It would be more helpful if it created the specific meeting notes pages beneath the index page. And if you create subsequent meeting notes pages it will only put them beneath the index page if you're on that page and use the blueprint again or use the macro on the index page. Blueprints are meant to be a way to help you neatly organize your space but this behavior seems to go against that.    fyi there is an old bug on this https://jira.<USER>browse/CONFSERVER-31035 that was closed as not a bug. So I'm entering this as a suggestion."""

summary: "Bulk update question's tags in Questions for Confluence"
description: """h3. Problem Definition      * If a user has a lot of questions in Questions for Confluence it is hard to update its tags since this has to be done individually for each task.    h3. Suggested Solution      * Add the ability of bulk updating question's tags in Questions for Confluence."""

summary: "I would like to order Drafts in Confluence by last updated date so I can find most recently worked on drafts"
description: """h3. Problem Definition  Currently we organized the *'User Profile' > Drafts* page by Space name, and only under each Space it's organized by last updated date or time.    h3. Suggested Solution  Would like to have an option to order all pages in *'User Profile' > Drafts* by the time of the last update instead of only organizing by Space name.  e.g.    !Screen Shot 2019-02-04 at 13.55.06.png|thumbnail!     h3. Why this is important  It would be helpful to see the most recently worked on Drafts to be able to get back to working on it faster.    h3. Workaround   For now we have two options  # Scroll down until you find the Space and page you want to work on again.  OR  # Use the browser search to find the Page by name.        """

summary: "Embedded database unsupported for upgrades"
description: """h3. Problem Definition  The [embedded database|https://confluence.<USER>doc/embedded-h2-database-145098285.html] in Confluence is ONLY supported during evaluation per the above linked documentation.    {quote}The embedded H2 database is only supported while you are evaluating Confluence. You must migrate to a supported external database before using Confluence as a production system.   {quote}    However, it may not be clear from this that the embedded database is also not supported when upgrading the system. This can cause many issues such as the one from this forum post:    http://h2-database.66688.n3.nabble.com/TimeNanos-out-of-range-when-using-different-version-of-jar-No-check-when-DB-upgraded-td4033636.html    In general, the solution for this is to migrate to a supported database.    h3. Suggested Solution  Clarify on the documentation that the H2 database is only supported within evaluation, including when upgrading or migrating a Confluence instance.  """

summary: "Usability issues with  recently-updated-dashboard macro"
description: """h3. Problem Description    If the macro is used in small frames or with smaller screen sizes the view is not useful and some basic functionality is lacking.    If not all tabs are visible, three dots are shown instead and if clicked a sub-menu will be shown. in the background of the sub-menu when loading a large loading circle is shown and only a part of that is inside the visible space.     h3. Additional points:    In the same setup clicking on the network tab: If the user does not have a network configured a hint will be show. The hint will be shown off screen in the centre of the page and one must scroll to read it. There is also no link to network setup in this area, and it is not clear to users where to find it.    Note: The above appears in all browsers. Tested in latest versions of Chrome, Firefox and Safari."""
comments: "In case of clicking one item of the ""three dots sub-menu"", a reload of the page will be initiated. Without the sub-menu there is no reload necessary.     I prefer the selection without a reload also for the sub-menu items."

Relevant information as the title of the result or the highlighted search term differ only slightly from its surrounding."""

summary: "Team Calendar Custom Event display the Event description and Event Title in the Month View"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  Team Calendar Custom Event display the Event description and Event Title in the Month View compared to the Default Events that only display the Event Title.    h3. Environment  Reproducible with Confluence Team Calendar version 6.0.25    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Install Team calendar plugin in Confluence.  # Add a Calendar and create a *Custom Event*  # Create an Event in the Calendar by choosing the custom event and fill in the *What*, *Who*, *When* and *Description*.  !customfield.png|thumbnail!  # Preview the Calendar in *Month* View.    h3. Expected Results  The Time (specified in *When*), User (specified in *Who*) and Title (specified in *What*) will be display in the Month* View similar to the Default Events.  !default.png|thumbnail!    h3. Actual Results  The Time (specified in *When*), User (specified in *Who*), Description (specified in *Descriptions*) and Title (specified in *What*) will be display making the *Month* View looks messy with a long event description.  !custom.png|thumbnail!    h3. Workaround  Leave the *Description* empty when adding the custom event."""

summary: "Page Up and Page Down key issues in Editor mode"
description: """h3. Issue summary    Page up and Page down key in editor mode does not work as expected:   * When there are no sections, page/up down key moves to the top/bottom of the page   * When the page contains multiple sections, page up/down key moves to the top/bottom of that section users are currently viewing    h4. How to replicate - No sections   # Create a page with long content   # On Editor mode, use 'Page Up' or 'Page Down'   # Expected results: The Page up/down button acts as a scroll    h4. Actual results    Pressing Page Up brought you to the Top of the page   Pressing Page Down brought you to the End of the page  h4. How to replicate #2 - when the page *that contain multiple sections*.    When pressing on the 'Page Up' key, the user is normally brought one page up with every press of the button. However, when the page contains multiple sections and the user is currently viewing content from a lower section, the 'Page Up' key brings them to the top of the first section in the page. This only affects *Google Chrome* and pages *that contain multiple sections*.   # Create a page with 5 sections.   # Insert long content in sections 3 and 4.   # Save the page.   # Edit the page again.   # Use 'Page Down' so your view is around section 3 or 4.   # Press on 'Page Up'.   # Expected results: Your page view only goes one page up.    h4. Actual results    You get brought to the top of the page where the first section is at.  h3. Environments   # Multiple sections issue observed in Chrome, on Confluence version 6.13.1   # Both issues observed in Firefox 68.0.1, on Confluence version 6.15.7    h4. Workaround / Notes   * Issue not observed in view mode   * Use a different browser (e.g. IE).   * If you're using a keyboard with no 'Page Up/Down' keys (i.e. MacBook), the issue is not reproducible using the shortcut for those keys (FN + Arrow key up/down)."""

summary: "Mouse Cursor displayed incorrectly when hovering over attached Image in Confluence Page"
description: """h3. Summary    Mouse Cursor displayed incorrectly when hovering over attached Image in Confluence Page    In Confluence 6.14.0 when hovering the mouse cursor to an attached image in the page, the mouse cursor display as a """"Move"""" cursor instead of the """"Hand"""" cursor that can be seen in the older versions of Confluence  h3. Environment    Confluence 6.14.0   Chrome Version 71.0.3578.98 (Official Build) (64-bit)   Internet Explorer 11.523.17134.0   Firefox 64.0.2 (64-bit)  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Upload an image attachment to a Page and save it   # In view mode, hover the mouse over to the image    h3. Expected Results    Mouse cursor changed to the """"Hand"""" cursor   !hand cursor.jpg|thumbnail!  h3. Actual Results    Mouse cursor changed to the """"Move"""" cursor   !move cursor.jpg|thumbnail!  h3. Information    When the page is refreshed, hovering mouse over to the image actually shows the """"Hand"""" cursor correctly until the page has finished loaded   [^6.14 image cursor.mp4]  h3. Workaround    Disable TinyMCE 4 editor and rollback to v3:   # Visit http://<conf-URL>/admin/darkfeatures.action   # Add frontend.editor.v4.disable to the list at the bottom   ** We noticed that adding *frontend.editor.v4.disable* in dark feature can have adverse affects to the color picker in edit mode (tested in Confluence 7.5.2). The color picker may not render as expected for the text color option or the table color.    OR    The workaround that has been shared in CONFCLOUD-57252:   # Go to *General Configuration* *>* [*Stylesheet*|https://confluence.<USER>doc/styling-confluence-with-css-166528400.html#StylingConfluencewithCSS-GettingStarted]   # Click *Edit* and paste in the custom CSS below:       # Click *Save*"""

Your workaround had fixed my Confluence.           Hi, Boon    Curtis's workaround is useful.  I think it will be nice if it was written as a workaround in the description.    Best Regards.     " [SEP] "affects 6.14.2, 6.14.3" [SEP] "Affects 6.15.7" [SEP] "Dear All,    I have discovered  another interesting  behavior,    In Linux(Ubuntu 18.04.3 LTS),  the  move cursor (CSS value) is not  executed  instead the Grab cursor  is executed after some inspection in  the developer view Firefox browser    Browsers(Firefox, Google chrome, Opera)    In Windows(10 in particular),  the  move cursor (CSS value) is  executed  instead  of the  hand cursor(pointer)   after some inspection in  the developer view Firefox browser.    Browsers(Firefox, IE, Google chrome, Opera)    _The  Confluence versions  6.15.8 , 7.1.0

summary: "Chrome freezes because of an endless loop in 'adjustHeaderSize' if Confluence is displayed in an iframe"
description: """In our web based inhouse application we are displaying certain Confluence pages via iframe - an apache httpd takes care of x-frame-options. The pages are displayed as idented.    Issue:  If a user invokes an ajax-request in our web app after scrolling within the confluence page/iframe and not returning to its top, the Chrome tab freezes and must be restarted. This is caused by an endless loop in a javascript provided by Confluence.     !adjustHeader-bug-public.gif!     Bug:  The file `atlassian\confluence\confluence\WEB-INF\atlassian-bundled-plugins\confluence-fixed-headers-1.4.0.jar\js\utils\dom.js` contains a function called `adjustMainHeaderSize` which takes care of positioning the breadcrumb and sidebar within Confluence. This function is called by a `MutationObserver` which itself is initialized in `js\confluence-fixed-headers.js`. The observer listens on changes for the style-attribute. If a change occurs the observer is called and therefore `adjustMainHeaderSize`, which itself changes the style-attribute resulting in a new observer call and so forth.    h3. Workaround    The function `adjustMainHeaderSize` uses jQuery's `outerWidth` to calculate new values. But `outerWidth` relies on changing style attributes. This issue is already solved by Atlassian for the function `updateTableStickyHeaderOption`, which is located just below `adjustMainHeaderSize`      Therefore we applied a similiar approach for our Confluence instance and succeeded.        The function `adjustMainHeaderSize` should take care of this situation just like `updateTableStickyHeaderOption`.     !adjustHeader-fix-public.gif! """
comments: "We confirm that this is a bug and that it can be fixed as described above. Many thanks for the detailed description."

summary: "Jira Issues Macro in Confluence sometimes loses sorting ability on some columns"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    The Jira Issues Macro in Confluence will sometimes lose the ability to sort some columns. Some of the columns will remain sortable, others will not.  h3. Environment   * Confluence   * Jira (connected via Application Links)    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Link Confluence and Jira   # Create a Page   # Insert a Jira Issues Macro   ## Choose a project of filter   ## Make no other changes   ## Save the macro   # Save the page    h3. Expected Results    Most, if not all, fields should be sortable  h3. Actual Results    Some fields cannot be sorted.    The {{/rest/jiraanywhere/1.0/jira/clientIds/}} call returns the macro. In order for a field to be sortable, the header must have {{<span class=""""jim-table-header-content"""">}} wrapped around it. The non-sortable fields are missing this span.    !source.jpg|thumbnail!  _(Above, the 'Key' field can be sorted, but the 'Summary' field cannot)_    h3. Notes    This seems to not happen after a recent Confluence start-up, but after running for some time, it happens. It can be observed across pages, spaces, filters, projects, browsers, and users. Clearing the browser cache or using another browser does not fix it.  h3. Workaround    Disable, then re-enable the *Confluence Jira Plugin* in Confluence.    or    Re-start Confluence"""
comments: "This bug is observed in 6.13.4 version too." [SEP] "this a duplicate of https://jira.<USER>browse/CONFSERVER-60129"

summary: "Code Block Macro Empty Lines Are Duplicated For Typed Content"
description: """h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Create a new Confluence page  # Create a new Code Block Macro (whilst not required, enabling """"show line numbers"""" makes the issue easier to see)  # Type the following code into the Code Box Macro  {code:title=Sample Text}  // comment    // comment    method A  {  }    method B  {  }      h3. Actual Results  The following output  {code:JAVA|title=Actual Output}  // comment      // comment      method A  {  }      method B  {  }  {code}    Each new empty line added results in double empty lines in the output.    These empty lines are reflected in the editor when the page is edited.     h3. Notes  Copy and pasting bypasses this issue.    h3. Work Around  Create the code in a text editor and copy it into the text box.    Alternately, the code can be created Code Box Macro, copied into a text editor, and then copied back from the text editor into the Code Box Macro.    Simply copying the Code Box Macro content to clipboard and pasting it back into the macro will not suffice."""

summary: "Moving pages between spaces breaks links"
description: """h3. Summary    When a page tree is moved to another space, some of the links between pages are broken.  h3. Environment   * Confluence Server 6.5.0    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create some pages. I.e. following the page tree below:       # Copy the links to all 4 child pages and insert them into the Parent page;   # Move the parent page to a different space    h3. Expected Results    The links should change to point to their new location  h3. Actual Results    Links are changed from working links to links that redirect the Page Editor, with the page title filled in but nothing else. If you check the broken link, you are also unable to edit it:      !broken-link.png|thumbnail!           h3. Notes    The broken links were changed from    [https://<BASE-URL>/wiki/spaces/TS/pages/5505026/Child+page+1|https://instance.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/TS/pages/5505026/Child+page+1]   to    [https://<BASE-URL>/wiki/pages/createpage.action?spaceKey=TS&title=Child+page+1|https://instance.atlassian.net/wiki/pages/createpage.action?spaceKey=TS&title=Child+page+1]  h3. Workaround    No other workaround besides editing the page and changing the links."""
comments: "We ran into this problem with a little difference.  Part of our solution may be the work around for this issue.  We are using 7.4 but believe this would work for some older versions of Confluence.  We <USER>links when we moved pages and at the same time change the name of the page titles. We had to do two main things to fix this. First was just a SQL script and second was a program script to make string manipulation easier.    1) Update links table to the correct space and in our case title    UPDATE L  SET DESTSPACEKEY = '<<SpaceKeyPagesWhereMovedTo>>',   LOWERDESTSPACEKEY = '<<SpaceKeyPageWhereMovedTolowercase',   DESTPAGETITLE = U.DESTPAGETITLE + ' <<added title text>>',   LOWERDESTPAGETITLE = U.LOWERDESTPAGETITLE + ' <<added title text>>'  FROM LINKS L inner join  (  SELECT L.*  FROM SPACES S  inner join CONTENT C ON S.SPACEID = C.SPACEID  inner join LINKS L ON L.CONTENTID = C.CONTENTID  WHERE S.SPACENAME = '<<SpaceNamePagesWhereMovedTo>>'  and L.DESTSPACEKEY = '<<SpaceKeyPagesWhereMovedFrom>>'  and C

summary: "Improve Granularity of Event Reminders in Team Calendars"
description: """If we create *two or more events* of the same event type, Team Calendars is likely to send a *single email notification* for them.    Requirements for this to happen:   * Start time must have a *difference of less than 5 minutes*   * The events must be on the same Calendar   * The event types *must be the same*   * The event must not be of the *Event* event type    Example:   # Create one event of the type Travel set to 3:30 PM   # Create *another Travel event* set to 3:33 PM   # Create the last travel event set to 3:35 PM   # Set reminders to *5 minutes before*    *Results* :The notification for the events set at 3:30 and 3:33 PM is sent on the same email. The event set for 3:35 PM has its own email notification.         This is possibly related to this issue:    https://jira.<USER>browse/CONFSERVER-48422         *Suggested solution*:    Improve the calendar job execution to be more granular so the reminders actually correspond to the settings. Or at least *split the notifications* in different emails."""

summary: """Purge All"" space trash fails with ""org.hibernate.StaleStateException"" due to Confluence's handling of CustomContentEntity"
description: """h2. Problem summary  Due to an issue with Confluence's handling of {{CustomContentEntity}} within the {{ContentPropertyService}} API, using the """"Purge All"""" option in the Space Trash can fail with a {{org.hibernate.StaleStateException}}. This can affect spaces which contains a custom entity created by {{ContentPropertyService}}. An example is the Numbered Headings plugin, which uses this API.    h2. Environment  # Confluence 6.13.0 Server  # Numbered Headings 5.2.2 for Server ([link|https://marketplace.<USER>apps/16063/numbered-headings/version-history]) -- this is just an example, there may be other plugins impacted by this bug    h2. Steps to reproduce  # Create a page  # In Edit mode, press the """"Numbered Headings"""" button in the tool bar (screenshot: [^press_this.png])  ** Note: Whenever the """"Numbered Headings"""" button is pressed, a row will be inserted in the CONTENT table which looks like this:    # Save the page (the page content doesn't matter)  # Delete the page  # Go to the space trash, and try to """"Purge All"""".    h2. Expected behavior  The space trash is purged without issue    h2. Actual behavior  Stacktrace will be reproduced in the UI and in the logs. Snippet below, and Full stacktrace here: [^full_stacktrace.txt]).      h3. Workaround    As a workaround, purge the offending page(s) *individually* from the space trash, which will then allow the """"Purge All"""" option to work again.    h3. *Numbered Headings*    In the case of *Numbered Headings* specifically, you can use this query to identify spaces/pages that may be affected:        h3. *Comala Workflows*    In the case of *Comala Workflows* specifically, you can use this query to identify spaces/pages that may be affected:        and then to manually delete the above pages, we need to first delete out the child dependent pages before we can delete the above pages using these steps:    # Shutdown Confluence  # (!) Backup the Confluence database  # Run these manual SQL to delete the affected pages (and their child pages)    # Startup Confluence  #* Navigate to *Confluence Administration* » *General Configuration* » *Content Indexing*  #** Rebuild the indexes to realign the Lucene index files from disk with the manual DB changes we made above  # Navigate to any Space and attempt to purge out the Trash and it should succeed  """
comments: "Dear Team,    Since this is given as an workaround so, everytime end users having a dependency with confluence admin user. They are not happy with this solution to resolve the issues.    Expecting a fix to resolve from the end user end itself (i.e., space admin level) it should be completed.    So, kindly take an action to resolve as early as possible.    Regards,    Yoga." [SEP] "kindly take an action to resolve as early as possible." [SEP] "Could you please provide statement 4 and 5 also for mysql working.    ""You can't specify target table 'CONTENT' for update in FROM clause""    Thanks in advance.    <USER> [SEP] "We are reaching hundreds of pages in trash now and the error expanded to more than 1 space already. Some CQL Scripts also seem to count trash pages to statistics which is undesireable (aside from issues such as stale attachments and hard drive space use). Workaround manual deletion is not really feasible, especially when the frontend-assertion doesn't really give us a Page ID to work with to i

summary: "Add ability to change Confluence's full search default parameters"
description: """h3. Problem Definition      * Sometimes it may be helpful to set a different default search parameter in Confluence's full search, such as leaving the *Personal search* enabled in the *In space* option of it.    h3. Suggested Solution      * Add the ability to change the default settings of Confluence's full search.    h3. Why this is important       * If a determined search with pre-defined parameters is made often, it will save some time and be of more assistance if the parameters are set to be the default search in Confluence's full search.    h3. Workaround   * Create a direct link to the search that is frequently made, with the URL being in the following format:   ** <Confluence_base_ URL>/dosearchsite.action?cql=space.type+in+(%22favourite%22%2C%22global%22%2C%22personal%22)     (!) *Please remember to change the base URL to match your Confluence's instance*"""

summary: "New search UI: Move loading icon to avoid vertical result relocation after search finishes"
description: """Hi,    in the new search UI, start a search for something that can actually be found in your instance.    Then quickly select a result while the search is actually still ongoing.    Depending on your timing, your will actually click the SECOND result because the result list has been moved up a few pixels because the search finished in the meantime, and the space the search-ongoing icon took has collapsed on you.    Example video for Atlassian staff: CSP-243965    With the old UI I only searched via the small top bar in the navigation so I assume the old search showed the loading icon inline with the words I searched for?    I don't actually remember it that well to be honest, even if it's just a gone few days now :D    I dig the new search though, and just thought relocating the loading icon would be a nice improvement, because to me that seems to be the easiest fix to stop the results moving after the search has finished.    As usual, once stuff changes everything gets looked at more closely than before ;)    Best regards    <USER>""

summary: "Confluence Search return ""Something went wrong"" after session timed out instead of redirecting user to Login page."
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Confluence Search return """"Something went wrong"""" after session timed out instead of redirecting user to Login page.  h3. Environment    Confluence version 6.14.0  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # *Login* into Confluence.   # Open a *new browser tab* and access any Confluence page in the new tab.   # *Logout* from Confluence in the first browser tab (in *Step 1*).   # In the *new browser tab*, Click the search box and search for any keyword.    h3. Expected Results    Confluence will redirect user to a Login page since the session is terminated/expired when user logout in the other tab.  h3. Actual Results    Confluence does not redirect user to a Login page.    The following message return in the Search box when user tried to search after session timed out:    h3. Notes    The issue can be replicated when the default session timed out (60 minutes) without manually Logout from Confluence in other tab.  h3. Workaround    Logout then re login into Confluence to perform the search"""
comments: "A fix for this issue is available to Server and Data Center customers in Confluence 6.15.2.    [Upgrade|https://www.<USER>software/confluence/download?utm_source=confluence_bugfix&utm_campaign=confluence_bugfix&utm_medium=email] now or check out the [Confluence 6.15 Release Notes|https://confluence.<USER>doc/confluence-6-15-release-notes-965554120.html?utm_source=confluence_bugfix&utm_campaign=confluence_bugfix&utm_medium=email] to see what other issues are resolved.  " [SEP] "This just happened with our users on 7.4.1 Enterprise Release. Clearing cookies on the browser helped them avoid this error. So, this issues do not seem to have resolved. "

summary: "Width values for adjusted responsive table columns should be modifiable "
description: """h3. Problem Definition    When adjusting responsive table columns, a width value is added.  This is not able to be seen or adjusted without modifying the source code (using an unsupported Source Code Editor).    h3. Suggested Solution  For the purpose of having greater control over table columns, allow for these values to either be modified or removed/turned off completely within the page editor to return full responsive table functionality.     h3. Workaround  Modify the source code using Source Editor manually."""

summary: "Allow Confluence Administrators to Export Restricted Pages to PDF and HTML"
description: """h3. Problem Definition  In some disaster recovery plans it is necessary to have immediate access to content as an HTML or PDF export. However, restricted pages not being exportable in PDF and HTML formats leaves content dependent on Confluence's restore to be accessible.    h3. Suggested Solution  Allow option for system administrators to be able to export restricted pages to PDF and HTML.  """

summary: "Creating a Jira Issue Macro will send hundreds of requests to Jira"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  A Confluence user that has no login permission in Jira tries to create/use Jira Issue Macro while editing a Confluence page will send hundreds of requests to Jira application.    h3. Environment  * Confluence integrated with Jira  * Confluence user has no login permission in Jira  * Jira <USER>installed  * Context path configured in Jira    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # In Confluence, create a new Page   # Add Jira Issue Macro    h3. Expected Results  * No spam of requests to Jira application.  * No timeout error in the UI, instead show a login failure.    h3. Actual Results    The below exception is thrown in the *Jira's access_log* file:    (i) There are 1400+ requests of the same on Jira by just a single action in Confluence  (!) Note the duplicate (doubled) of Jira's context path {{/j7130/j7130}}    The below error is seen in the UI of Confluence:  !confluence.png|thumbnail!      h3. Workaround  Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""
comments: "Does Jira <USER>require to be installed in order to replicate this behavior? This is essential information to determine if this bug would be applicable for our instance when we upgrade. Thank you in advance!"

description: """Atlassian Confluence Server from version 6.12.0 (or earlier), and before version 6.13.1, or before version 6.14.0 allows an authenticated user to download a deleted page via the word export feature."""
