summary: "Page Tree Search macro and Page Tree Macro search should be consistent"
description: """The Page Tree Search macro ought to have the same options as the Search used for the Page Tree Macro (name be able to use relative references like @self and @parent)"""

summary: "Search Box Macros should take a list of spaces to include in search."
description: """I have a set of related spaces.   I would like search macros placed on pages in any of these paces to search all the related spaces (and only those spaces).     """

summary: "Changing part of bullet list to numbers will change the whole list to numbers."
description: """h3. Issue Summary  Changing the part of bullet list to numbers list will change the whole list to numbers list.    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a 4 line bullet list of text   # Select middle 2 lines and change them to numbers list    h3. Expected Results  Only middle 2 lines will change to numbers list.    h3. Actual Results  All lines change to numbers list.    h3. Notes  In Confluence 6.12 and bellow we could mix bullet lists and numbers list in the same list.    h3. Workaround   Currently, there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Add the possibility to Watch this Blog button to the Blog Posts macro"
description: """(i) We would like to reuse the link to the *Watch this Blog* and add it directly to a Confluence page (Homepage).    !image-2019-01-28-08-26-32-153.png!    We are currently using the macro 'Blog Posts' to show the blogs of the last 7 days on a lot of Confluence spaces.    (+) It would help us in our *user adoption* if this 'Blog Posts' macro could be extended by the Watch this Blog or if we get an easy way to embed the *Watch this Blog* as a link.         (x) It is currently not possible because the *Watch this Blog* contains a user specific token and can not be reused.     """

summary: "Excerpt Include macro does not take you to included page when in edit mode"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    When in Edit mode on a page that has the “Excerpt Include” macro, clicking “Go to Included Page” merely loads the current page.  h3. Environment   * Confluence 6.11.2    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a page   #  Insert the “Excerpt Include” macro to the page and link it to another page on the site   # Click *Save*   # When taken to the editor, click on the macro to load the options bar   # Click “Go to Included page”    h3. Expected Results    You should be taken to the page you included in the macro in a new tab.  h3. Actual Results    The *current* page is loaded in a new tab, as opposed to the page that was included in the macro.  h3. Workaround    Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available."""
comments: "This bug looks like a duplicate of [CONFSERVER-57378 - The Include Page Macro's "Go to Included Page" button redirects to the current page|https://jira.<USER>browse/CONFSERVER-57378] which has been fixed in Confluence 6.12.1"

summary: "Ability to disable automatically inherited Page Restrictions"
description: """h4. Problem Definition  Currently, there is no way to disable the automatic permissions that are inherited on child pages, from the configured page restrictions on parent pages. This is a limitation in select cases where a user might not want those permissions to be automatically applied in the first place.    h4. Suggested Solution  The ability to simply select whether restrictions from a parent page should be applied to its child pages would already solve this issue, and provide greater freedom to Confluence admins."""
comments: "Once a space has already become large and relatively complicated, moving some pages to a separate space to accommodate separate permissions is not efficient.      Ideally, I would want the ability to _DENY_ access to a group or individual, so I could override the inherited permissions _just for that group/individual and just for that page._    Being able to disinherit parent permissions for that page (would this affect the sub-pages?) at least gives us the ability to virtually ""deny"" by only adding groups/individuals we want to ALLOW.  However, that is also not efficient if you have an environment that has dozens of groups and you only want to exclude _one_ of them on that page. :-/     "

summary: "Include versioning of REST API commands"
description: """In [Confluence Server REST API|https://developer.<USER>server/confluence/confluence-server-rest-api/], it indicates that the REST API is applicable from Confluence version 5.5 and higher:  {quote}  Applicable Confluence Server 5.5 and later  {quote}  However, there are some commands like {{<your-domain>/rest/api/user?username=xyz}} which do not work in older versions of Confluence like 5.9.     Could the [REST API Reference guide|https://docs.<USER>ConfluenceServer/rest/6.14.0/] be updated to include the version in which a particular command is supported?"""

summary: "Jira Issue unable to display issue summary in Personal Task intermittently"
description: """h3. Summary  Jira Issue unable to display issue summary in Personal Task intermittently    Jira Issue Macro may not load the *Issue Summary* when navigating to the [Tasks|https://confluence.<USER>doc/add-assign-and-view-tasks-590260030.html#Add,Assign,andViewTasks-Viewtasks] page from user profile and this happens intermittently.    h3. Environment  * Application:  ** Confluence 6.13.0  ** Jira 7.13.0  * Browser:  ** Chrome Version 71.0.3578.98 (Official Build) (64-bit)  ** Firefox 64.0.2 (64-bit)  ** IE      h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Create a new page and insert a task that includes:  #* Mentioning a user  #* Jira Issue Macro  # Go to the profile page of the mentioned user and click on the *Tasks* tab to view all the tasks that is assigned    h3. Expected Results  Jira Issue Summary can be loaded correctly   !expected result.png|thumbnail!     h3. Actual Results  Jira Issue Summary sometimes loads indefinitely but never return with a result   !actual result.png|thumbnail!     h3. Information  (i) Possibly related to CONFSERVER-56588  In the successful attempt, log below can be observed after enabling *HTTP Dump Log* in Jira and {{*org.apache.http*}} DEBUG logging in Confluence:  {code:title=atlassian.confluence.log}  2019-01-25 10:03:32,298 DEBUG [JIM Marshaller:thread-14] [http.impl.execchain.MainClientExec] execute Executing request GET /j7130/sr/jira.issueviews:searchrequest-xml/temp/SearchRequest.xml?tempMax=1000&returnMax=true&validateQuery=false&jqlQuery=KEY+IN+%28TP-1%29&field=type&field=summary&field=status&field=resolution&field=statuscategory&field=link HTTP/1.1      However, in the attempt when the Jira Issue Summary is not loading, no log is observed in both Confluence and Jira.    h3. Workaround  Refresh the same page since this is an intermittent issue"""
comments: "This is not intermittent on the actual customer environment (using the Confluence 6.10.1 and Jira 7.11.1). This is happening every single time when a user is trying to check her task using the 'Tasks' menu on the profile. So the *'workaround' specified here is not working*."

summary: "Image cannot be displayed when exporting a page with more than 50 images into Word file"
description: """h3. Summary  Image cannot be displayed when exporting a page with more than 50 images into Word file    When using the [Export to Word|https://confluence.<USER>confcloud/export-content-to-word-pdf-html-and-xml-724764824.html#ExportContenttoWord,PDF,HTMLandXML-ExportsinglepagestoWordorPDF] feature on a Confluence page that contains more than 50 images, the exported Word file cannot display all of the images properly or appears to be broken.    h3. Environment  Confluence 6.11.2  Confluence 6.13.0      h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Create a new page in Confluence and upload 55 different image files into the page  #* (i) They can be the same image, but they all must be *different files*  # Save the Page and make sure all 55 images are displaying properly in the Confluence Page  # Export the page into Word file      h3. Expected Results  The exported Word file is able to display all 55 images properly    h3. Actual Results  Any images that are after the *50th* image will be broken.  * Broken Image exported from Confluence 6.11.2 viewed in Microsoft Word 2016:   !6.11.2 export.png|thumbnail!   * Broken Image exported from Confluence 6.13.0 viewed in Microsoft Word 2016:   !6.13.0 export.png|thumbnail!   * Broken Image is not displaying any error message in Office 365 Subscription on Mac OS X  * Exported result:   [^Much+More+Image+Here.doc]       h3. Information  * This is the image file that is being used to test (file size around 2KB):   !atlassian logo.png|thumbnail!   * Also performed the test with a larger image at around 2MB and same outcome is observed (images after the 50th starts to break)  * Turning the [Logging and Profiling|https://confluence.<USER>doc/configuring-logging-181535215.html] for Confluence to *Diagnostic* mode during the Word Export but no error messages are logged.    (i) As the reason behind why the image breaks after the 50th image is not known yet, this bug might be encountered before or after 50 images depends on the environment."""

summary: "Bundle AdoptOpenJDK with Confluence"
description: """Confluence 6.13 and later supports both Oracle JDK and AdoptOpenJDK. Previously we bundled the Oracle JRE.     This change will bundle AdoptOpenJDK 8u192-b12, which will be used when installing Confluence using the Windows and Linux installer.     Admins can still choose to switch to Oracle JDK if they prefer. See [Change the Java vendor or version Confluence uses|https://confluence.<USER>display/DOC/Change+the+Java+vendor+or+version+Confluence+uses]    *Note:* Confluence 6.13.0, 6.13.1, and 6.14.0 all bundled Oracle JRE.   6.13.2+ and 6.14.1+ will have AdoptOpenJDK.    """
comments: "So I am a little confused. I upgraded our installation from 6.13.1 to 6.13.2 (Standalone Server, Linux x64, Centos 7) expecting that either one of two things would have occurred.    1) the new version would be utilising AdoptOpenJDK     2) AdoptOpenJDK would be installed on the server and a manual change be required for the setjre.sh as per ([https://confluence.<USER>doc/change-the-java-vendor-or-version-confluence-uses-962342397.html)]         I have searched for various incantations of *adopt*, *jdk8u* but nothing returns.     There is no indication that AdoptOpenJDK needs to be manually installed, as I associated ""Bundled"" to be included in the install. Especially with the note on Supported Platforms         *Good to know:*   * You don't need to install Java if you plan to use the installer to install Confluence, as a JRE is bundled with Confluence (provided by AdoptOpenJDK).         I would understand if installing AOJDK manually is a requirement given that there are many different distributions o

summary: "Links in Team Calendar email reminders should bring you to the specific calendar month"
description: """h3. Problem Definition    Links in Team Calendar email notifications, reminding you of a future event (a month or more ahead) at some time before it occurs (for example reminding you 4 weeks, or 5 minutes before) do not bring you to the month mentioned in the calendar itself.  When clicking on any of the links, they instead bring you to the current date and month.     !Screen Shot 2019-01-24 at 12.10.24.png|thumbnail!     The link in the email is similar to <confluenceurl>/calendar/previewcalendar.action?subCalendarId=729f1c21-52c6-40bb-8d1f-dd191d0866f1.  There is nothing in the link that would reference a future date, only the correct calendar.    h3. Suggested Solution    Links in event notification emails should bring you to the exact date mentioned.     h3. Workaround    No workaround at the moment."""

summary: "Cannot add inline comments to words that exist inside a macro body"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  Cannot add inline comments to similar words that exist inside a macro body. For example, in a page, a macro body contains the text """"This is inside a macro"""" and the text """"I am not inside"""" lives outside the macro.    !Edit_-_Inline_Comment_issue_-_Demonstration_Space_-_Confluence.jpg|thumbnail!     Adding an inline comment to the word """"inside"""" results in this error      h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Create a plugin with macro (or use https://bitbucket.org/atlassian_tutorial/confluence-macro-plugin-tutorial/src/macro-master/)  # Use body type _RICH_TEXT_ and output type _INLINE_  # Run the server app  # Create a page and include the macro  # Write words inside the macro body with similar words outside the body  # Add inline comment to the words outside the macro    (i) This issue is also replicable when the text is in [Page Properties Macro|https://confluence.<USER>doc/page-properties-macro-184550024.html] that is marked as *hidden*   !hidden page properties.png|thumbnail!     h3. Expected Results  Inline comments will be placed successfully.    h3. Actual Results      h3. Workaround  Currently, there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available    h3. Notes  Seems like _matchIndex_ and _numMatches_ values are incorrect due to the texts inside the macro body being matched."""
comments: "Workarround: Just select more words, so that there is no match with the words inside the macro." [SEP] "It is very easy to reproduce:    # Start editing a page, insert a macro, which is NOT visible. For example, ""Page properties"" with the option ""Hidden"" switched ON.  # Type some text inside the macro.  # Type the same text outside the macro.  # Save the page.    From now on you won't be able to make inline comment for any substring, which matches the text inside the macro.    * The bug is in the plugin: {{confluence-highlight-actions}}  * The method {{com.atlassian.confluence.plugins.highlight.xml.TextMatcher#match}} returns null because it finds selected text twice on the page, while provided {{TextSearch}} object contains only one match (which is correct, since only one occurrence of the text is visible).  * The {{TextMatcher#match}} method should not blindly compare all text matches. It has to take into account the fact that some text portions are not actually visible.  * Eventually the {{com.atlassi

summary: "Windows Installer for Data Center Upgrades"
description: """Currently, in order to upgrade a Confluence Data Center instance, it is necessary to manually complete the process found in [Upgrading Confluence Data Center|https://confluence.<USER>doc/upgrading-confluence-data-center-1507377.html].  If Confluence could use an executable installer file (like we use for installation), it would make the upgrade process easier for those admins who are only used to using the executable method."""

summary: "Companion App throws System clock error if it starts up before the computer has synced with the NTP server"
description: """h3. Summary  # When connected to the internet, the Companion App checks and compares the computer's time with the NTP time.  # If the time is out of sync, it will throw the error saying """"System clock is incorrect""""  # Sometimes the Companion App will start before the computer has time to sync with the NTP server, which will them prompt the error message as well.    h3. Steps to replicate  # Set the Companion App to auto start on OS boot.  # Start the computer in another location where the local time is different.  # If the computer boots up, but has not synced with the NTP server to update it's time, the Companion App will throw the """"System clock is incorrect"""" error.    # The issue can also be reproducible by simply changing the local time of the machine, and then setting it to update using the NTP server.  # Start the Atlassian Companion app, and it will throw the error, since it takes some time for the computer to sync with the NTP server time.    h3. Expected behavior  # When starting up, the Companion app should wait awhile for the computer time to sync with the network time, to prevent the error message from appearing.  # Or, there should be an option to disable the checking.    h3. Current behavior   # Companion App throws """"System clock is incorrect"""" error message, as the computer has have the time to sync with the network time.   # In some cases, the app will crash on boot, and but works fine if started manually after."""
comments: "Affects Version Confluence 7.1.0" [SEP] "Affecting Confluence 6.13" [SEP] "Issue solved for us after upgrading to Confluence version 7.3.1 on Monday, March 16, 2020." [SEP] "Affects Version Confluence v6.15.2" [SEP] "Affects version 7.4.0" [SEP] "Hi folks, this issue is in the Companion App itself and is not related to the Confluence version. " [SEP] "A fix for this issue is available in Companion 1.2.0. View the [Release Notes|https://confluence.<USER>display/DOC/Atlassian+Companion+app+release+notes] to see what other issues are resolved.    If Companion is installed via the MSI, then it will need to be manually updated to the [new version|https://update-nucleus.<USER>Atlassian-Companion/291cb34fe2296e5fb82b83a04704c9b4/latest/win32/ia32/Atlassian%20Companion.msi?_ga=2.261166863.323564345.1563926916-1578275846.1563926916]."

description: """Hi,  We'd like to have the option to add custom HTML to the head section of confluence mobile view (not the app), similarly to the option to add custom HTML to the desktop version.  The main motivation is for scripts like google analytics."""

summary: "Macro parameters referencing pages in current space break when copying to new space"
description: """h3. Summary    The bug was found when vendor was testing their macro but this issue affects any macro using """"confluence-content"""" parameters. This affects both Cloud and Server.     _All details here are provided by the vendor_    Replication steps:    Preparation (in space SRC):   # Add a macro that takes a page as a parameter to a page A   # Set page C in the current space (SRC) as the value of the parameter    Different variants of the bug:   # Copy-paste the macro:   ## Open editor for the page A   ## Select the macro placeholder in editor and choose copy   ## Open editor for a page B in another space DEST   ## Paste the macro from page A to the editor for page B   ## Save page B   -> the macro does not point to the page SRC:C anymore   -> unless the DEST space has a page with title C the macro breaks (eg """"unable to render \{include}  The page could not be found"""" is shown)     # Copy page and change location in editor to new space   ## Open the page A   ## Choose copy   ## In the editor opened for copy of page A, click 'location' (next to breadcrumb)   ## Choose a page in another space (eg DEST) as the new parent   ## Save   -> same problems as in variant A   # Copy page directly to new space (without children)   ## same as B but choose a parent page in different space already in the """"pick location"""" dialog opened after clicking on """"copy""""   ## the """"pick location"""" dialog is not shown at all on Cloud, so 3rd affects only Server    h3. Expected    When macro is copied to new space the relative link to a page in its original space is qualified with the original space key (turned into an absolute link)  h3. Actual    The link breaks  h3. Affects/Impact    This affects any macro saving a page reference to a macro parameter with type """"confluence-content"""" (for example """"Include page"""" or """"Excerpt include"""")    *Customer Installation Impacts: 1,215*  h3. Notes  - in all cases using a Link to page A works correctly but macro parameters break  - the macro user (or developer) cannot choose to use an absolute link to a page in current space by qualifying with the space key when setting the parameter value. Confluence will automatically (in some cases) turn links to pages in current space to be relative (always using absolute references would also solve the problems with page titles including ':'...)  - the copy operations seem to work correctly when the editor is not involved, so the bug is in handling the macro parameters in """"editor format"""" (ie the data-macro-* attributes on the macro placeholder)  - have not tested if this happens also with macros with body, it happens at least for any bodiless macro  - tested on Confluence Cloud, and Confluence 6.0 + 6.12 on Server    h3. Workaround  No current workaround. This will be change once a workaround is possible"""

summary: "Companion app MSI doesn't start automatically"
description: """Currently, when deploying Companion App on Windows OS using the MSI, it will not start automatically.    If the MSI is distributed across the organization by the system administrator, if the user is not aware and doesn't reboot the machine afterward, they will still get prompted to install the Companion app upon trying to edit the attachment.    This will result in a new installation of the Companion app on top of the one that is already installed.    The solution for this is to make the Application start automatically after it is installed.  ----  h5. Current scenario:    After user runs the App for the first time, the registry key will be added in the following path    h5. The Workaround    One of our customers came up with a solution to add the registry key automatically in the HKLM during the deployment to ensure that the app is started automatically without having the user to start it manually.       {panel:title=Custom script for Companion App MSI|titleBGColor=#d3dded|bgColor=#d3dded}  msiexec /qn /i """"Atlassian Companion.msi""""   reg add """"HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\Software\WOW6432Node\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Run"""" /v """"electron.app.Atlassian Companion"""" /d """"\""""C:\Program Files (x86)\Atlassian Companion\Atlassian Companion.exe\"""""""" /f  {panel}   """
comments: "This is a duplicate of CONFSERVER-57541. Thus I am closing this ticket. "

summary: "Calendar creator cannot receive notification about events which were created by another user for the first time"
description: """h3. Summary    Calendar creator cannot receive notification about events which were created by another user *for the first time*.  h3. Environment   * Team Calendar v. 6.0.25   * Confluence needed to be integrated with SMTP server    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Login Confluence with User A   # Create a space (Space 1) and unwatch it   ** This step is required for isolating this problem from CONFSERVER-57698   # Create a calendar and associate it with Space 1   ** Confirm if User A is watching the newly create calendar   # Create an event """"Birthday"""" on the calendar   ** Please don't set a user *other than User A or User B* to _Who_ field to prevent any unwanted notification   ** User A shouldn't receive any notification as it was own update   # Logout Confluence   # Login Confluence with User B   # Create an event """"Birthday"""" on the calendar   # Confirm if User A receives a notification about the event   # Create an event """"Leave"""" on the calendar   # Confirm if User A doesn't receive any notification about the event    h3. Expected Results    User A should receive a notification in *Step 10* as well as it received in the *Step 8*.  h3. Actual Results    User A didn't receive a notification in *Step 10*.  h3. Notes   * An even """"Birthday"""" was created by User A *for the first time* but the other one """"Leave"""" wasn't.   * The event types themselves don't seem important to reproduce this bug   -- You can reproduce this bug with any two event types    h3. Workaround    There seems to be no workaround at this moment."""

summary: "Inaccurate link for Confluence Data Center Azure setup"
description: """[Getting started with Confluence Data Center on Azure|https://confluence.<USER>doc/getting-started-with-confluence-data-center-on-azure-937176452.html/#GettingstartedwithConfluenceDataCenteronAzure-ExtraSSL] suggests in the *Additional steps required for SSL* section that you need to set up SSL between the application gateway and Synchrony by linking to [Overview of end to end SSL with Application Gateway|https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/application-gateway/application-gateway-backend-ssl], which specifically addresses configuring backend SSL.    However, this is inaccurate, and configuring SSL on between the load balancer and Synchrony can actually break the connection, since Synchrony does not support SSL natively (see [Possible Confluence and Synchrony Configurations|https://confluence.<USER>doc/possible-confluence-and-synchrony-configurations-958779064.html]). This link should be removed, or updated to a different Azure doc that is not about configuring SSL behind the load balancer."""

summary: "Confluence to Confluence integration (different instances)"
description: """h1. Use case    As I user working in a company with multiple distinct confluence instances, I would like to do perform:   * Cross instance linking   * Cross instance attachment use (i.e. for images)   * Cross instance macro usage   ** Task reporting (_Task report_ macro)   ** Page properties reporting (_Page properties report_ macro)   ** _Page tree_ macro   ** Cross instance page excerpts (_Excerpt Include_ macro)   ** Basically all macros that do reporting    h1. Precedence    This use case for Jira to Jira, Confluence to Jira and Jira to Confluence is already supported as all this cases support multiple instances with regards to integration  h1. User interest    I have collected already existing questions for this requirement on atlassian forum under the label [confluence-to-confluence-integration|https://community.<USER>t5/tag/confluence-to-confluence-integration/tg-p]    Also, CONFSERVER-24425 relates to this very same use case, but it was closed as answered, and the linked issue that is a follow up is a broken link"""
comments: "The feature would be extremely helpful for our organization. We have several server based Confluence instances and need a possibility to embed content from one instance to another one. Since there is no possibility to use iframes for this without security implications described in [https://confluence.<USER>confkb/confluence-page-does-not-display-in-an-iframe-827335781.html?_ga=2.50003430.1537957449.1588688839-2131708736.1551689076] , we are currently getting by by using simple web links to refer to the required content, which is not an optimal solution.    What we need is something similar to the Jira macro in Confluence, which can embed issues from an integrated Jira. Something like an include page macro from another Confluence, where an application link has been established.    We a thrilled to find out if we could use this feature in future!" [SEP] "I would add the following to the above list:    Allow excerps to be replicated or used between instances    Allow page inclusions as well."

summary: "Confluence Cache statistics page shows old value"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    The cache statistics page of Confluence doesn't have HTTP Cache-Control headers. This can cause old values to display, if the size of Cache is adjusted through the User interface (Cache Management) and there is an intervening caching proxy.    h3. Environment    * Confluence v6.13  * Any middle-ware proxies like Nginx with caching capabilities    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Update the cache size of any any entity.  # Refresh the page.  # Capture a HAR file.    h3. Expected Results    Note the response headers for the following request,      The response should have the following HTTP header with some values to decide how caching proxies should handle this.      h3. Actual Results    The response doesn't contain the Cache control values for the body.    h3. Workaround    Flush cache in proxies.   """

description: """h2. Summary    Currently, the Jira Issues Macro will return up to the predefined 'Maximum Issues' limit, but displays them all in one page.  If you have, for example, 300 issues being returned, you either need to view all 300, or you set the 'Maximum Issues' limit to something less (such as 50), and then Confluence will make you click a link to see the rest of the issues in Jira.  It would be nice to have an option to display the issues in Confluence via pagination.  h2. We were able to workaround this in Confluence's Stylesheet using the custom CSS below.    {code:css}  h1[id]:not(.with-breadcrumbs)::before,    h2[id]:not(.with-breadcrumbs)::before,    h3[id]:not(.with-breadcrumbs)::before,    h4[id]:not(.with-breadcrumbs)::before,    h5[id]:not(.with-breadcrumbs)::before,    h6[id]:not(.with-breadcrumbs)::before {      content: """";      display: block;      height: 50px;      margin: -50px 0 0;    }   {code}  *Source:* https://stackoverflow.com/questions/4086107/fixed-page-header-overlaps-in-page-anchors (the solution using {{:target::before}} didn't work for us)." [SEP] "A fix for this issue is available to Server and Data Center customers in Confluence 7.8.0  [Upgrade|https://www.<USER>software/confluence/download] now or check out the [Release Notes|https://confluence.<USER>display/DOC/Confluence+7

summary: "Configure a Server for Outgoing Email documentation has conflicting information"
description: """h3. Problem Definition  The information from [Configuring a Server for Outgoing Mail|https://confluence.<USER>doc/configuring-a-server-for-outgoing-mail-151078.html] is quite unclear, and it needs to be clarified.    {quote}You can personalize email notifications by configuring the 'From' field to include the name and email address of the Confluence user who made the change.{quote}    h3. Suggested Solution  There are missing words here. We need to understand there's a difference between the 'From name' and 'From address' fields.    Users see this sentence, and they are lead to believe that it means you can customize the 'From' field to equal that of the user who made the change, and after this change, can therefore simply reply to the email and the message will go to the user who initiated the change. This is not the case here, as the information below this is conflicting:    {quote}From Address: Enter the email address that will be displayed in the 'from' field for email messages originating from this server.  This field is mandatory. This must be an ordinary email address, you can't enter variables in this field.{quote}    In order to set the 'From' Name and Address fields to the initiator of the change, you would need to use variables, as this changes constantly.    It needs to be more clear that this is a cosmetic change, and the 'From Address' can not be changed in this way due to the fact that it does not accept variables. In the 'From Address' field you can only enter an email address.    h3. Why this is important  Documentation should be clear and easy to follow, and even I had a hard time understanding what it was trying to convey."""

summary: "Improve timezone recognition for Team Calendars"
description: """Under some circumstances, Team Calendars will need to locate a timezone (such as during an import of an .ics file). If the file does not contain a valid timezone, TC will attempt to obtain the system's timezone in this order:    # _atlassian.confluence.settings/defaultTimeZoneId_ in Confluence's bandana table.  # _user.timezone_ Java sysprop, if set.  # Platform-specific default timezone id. JRE identifies this differently in MacOs, Linux and Windows.  # The default value: GMT    However, some of the valid values for Java's {{user.timezone}} such as """"UTC"""" or """"GMT"""" are not considered valid for Team Calendars, since TC's supported timezone values are based on an intersection of Joda and Ical4j timezones: [^ teamcal_supported_tz.txt]    Because TC does not consider values like """"UTC"""" to be valid, this can cause problems during certain scenarios, like the import calendar problem described in CONFSERVER-57756"""

summary: "Team Calendars cannot import .ics files if no valid timezone can be found"
description: """h2. Steps to Reproduce  # Install Confluence and Team Calendars  # Set Confluence's timezone to a value that is not considered valid by Team Calendars, using a JVM parameter. For example, """"UTC"""":    # Try to import this .ics file into Team Calendars: [^example.ics]    h2. Expected Results  The calendar either imports or displays a reason in the UI why it cannot be imported.    h2. Actual Results  The UI shows no action when the Import button is pressed, and the dialog remains up.  Application logs show that a particular VM file cannot be found:      h2. Notes  From the error, we can see that the VM file does not actually exist where plugin is expecting it. In the Team Calendars JAR file, it is located in a slightly different path:  ||Context||Path||  |In the exception message|/com/atlassian/confluence/extra/*templates/velocity/calendar3*/calendar-import-subcalendar-timeZoneRequired.vm|  |Within the Jar file|/com/atlassian/confluence/extra/*calendar3/templates/velocity*/calendar-import-subcalendar-timeZoneRequired.vm|    A related ticket to improve Team Calendars timezone detection has been raised here: CONFSERVER-57757    h3. Workaround  Ensure that the .ics file contains a valid timezone. Definitions and examples below:  * https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc5545#section-3.6.5  * https://stackoverflow.com/questions/35645402/how-to-specify-timezone-in-ics-file-which-will-work-efficiently-with-google-outl"""

summary: "Administrator cannot export a Space to PDF when the Root Is Restricted"
description: """h3. Summary  When a Confluence administrator attempts to export a space that has restrictions at the root page a vague error is encountered in the logs:  {code}  Caused by: java.lang.IndexOutOfBoundsException: Index: 0, Size: 0  {code}    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Create a new space  # Create a child page  # At the root of the Space restrict the page to a user or group not containing the Confluence administrator  # Go to *Space Admin* > *Content Tools* > *Export*  # Select *PDF*  #  Select *Normal Export* and hit *Export*    h3. Expected Results  A PDF will export without content as the root page is restricted    h3. Actual Results  # You'll receive an error in the UI:  {code}  Failed during PDF export. Exception message - Exception while rendering the PDF document /confluence-home/atlassian-confluence-6.2.3/temp/pdfexport-20190109-090119-1417-7/RS-090119-1417-8.pdf  {code}  # You'll see the following error in the {{atlassian-confluence.log}} file:      h3. Notes  * Our [Export to HTML, XML, or PDF|https://confluence.<USER>confcloud/export-content-to-word-pdf-html-and-xml-724764824.html#ExportContenttoWord,PDF,HTMLandXML-ExporttoHTML,XML,orPDF] article notes that only visible pages would be exported, so this is the expected behavior  {quote}  *Normal Export (HTML and PDF)* - to produce an HTML file containing all the pages that you have permission to view.  {quote}  * If you select *Custom Export* you will see no pages are loaded in the tree    h3.Workaround  Explicitly grant the user performing the export view permissions to the root of the space:  # As a Confluence administrator browse to the Space  # On the root page/home page of the space click the !https://confluence.<USER>doc/files/139414/945098808/1/1517274860024/restriction-icon-red-locked.png!  # Add the user who is attempting to export the page  #* If it is your own account, you may already see yourself listed, but still move forward with searching for your username and adding the permission again  # Save the permissions  # Attempt the export again"""

summary: "Update health check with correct encoding requirements for Windows users on installation with PostgreSQL "
description: """h3. Problem Definition    When upgrading Confluence to 6.14 or later, admins see the following message that English_United States.1252 is not UTF-8    !Capture.JPG|thumbnail!    This is a false warning and can be ignored, *provided the client encoding* is set correctly.    h3. Background    I've had several users on Community reach out to me regarding this confusion, for example:   * [Confluence on PostgreSQL what collation is recommended|https://community.<USER>t5/Confluence-questions/Confluence-on-PostgreSQL-what-collation-is-recommended/qaq-p/738405]    Until September 2019, the [Database Setup for Postgresql|https://confluence.<USER>doc/database-setup-for-postgresql-173244522.html] doc specified that:  {quote}Collation must also be set to utf8. Other collations, such as """"C"""", are known to cause issues with Confluence.  {quote}  This is clear to Linux users, but for Windows users, their options might look like the following:   * C   * POSIX   * English_United States.1252    During setup of the database in Windows, users are to select a locale, and are presented with the following selections:    !image.png|thumbnail!  h3. Expected behavior  Confluence health check does not give the false warning when using .1252 collations    h3. Suggested Solution  We should update the health check to accept the Windows equivalent of UTF-8.    h3. Notes  [According to Postgres|https://www.postgresql.org/docs/9.3/static/multibyte.html]:  {quote}UTF-8 encoding can be used with any locale.  {quote}  In addition, I reviewed this blog post, [Locale in Windows|http://www.g-loaded.eu/2011/02/27/locale-windows/], and it appears that you should be able to use English_United States.1252.    Although I believe this is addressed with the following line:  {quote}Choose the locale that best matches your geographic location.  {quote}  """
comments: "The new 6.14 confluence complains post migration, on windows, to be on collation ""English_United States.1252"", stop the migration with a warning, but let the user continue anyway.    Never did in the previous version installations.    The stock postgres installer on windows set encoding UTF8 and collation English_United States.1252     " [SEP] "I had the same problem. Installed PostgreSQL on Windows, and upgraded Confluence to the newest version.   Still using UTF-8 encoding, but Danish locale. And Confluence warns me that its not supported. " [SEP] "So what is recommended? Can we still (for now) start Confluence regardless of this problem?    Will it work? Or could we experience malfunctions or damage?    To fix the collation a complete copy of the database is required.    This will be a rather lengthy and difficult procedure which I would like to avoid at the moment and postpone to next weekend, for example.    Can I run with the problem until then or do I need to rollback the VM snapshot?  h5. Databa

summary: "Root pages should not disappear in the Page Tree in the sidebar"
description: """At times, pages in a space will be moved into the Root of the space (not under any parent page in a space). Examples:  * After restoring a previously deleted page, pages will be restored to the root of the space - [Reference|https://confluence.<USER>doc/delete-or-restore-a-page-139429.html]  * Pages are reordered manually by going to Space Tools > Reorder Pages > Move pages to the same position as the Space Home Page    When this happens, these pages will not be shown in the Page Tree sidebar.     This is misleading to users as the pages appears to be """"missing"""".    Suggestion behavior:  * Display all pages in the Page Tree sidebar, regardless if they are at the Root of the space or not.  """
comments: "This just happened to us the other day, though not related to restoring deleted page, just simply happened as a result of reordering pages.     To recreate this buggy behavior of hiding pages in page tree mode (defeating the purpose of page tree mode)    1. Create new confluence space and create additional pages  2. In Reorder pages, rearrange so that new pages become parent pages at a level at or above the home page   3. Click on space link and in page tree sidebar configuration the same or higher parents relative to home page no longer appear in the view making it very difficult to navigate to those pages since it appears as if they don't exist    Seems like it would be better behavior in page tree mode to always display all parents regardless of where the home page lives within the hierarchy."

summary: "Article meta information - Friendly name and hidden tags"
description: """It would be useful to be able to store a configurable friendly name and a list of friendly tags for each article.    This information should only be accessible and configurable by space admins and above.    The goal is to be able to use this 'meta' information about the articles in the design and structure of information across our wikis.    The friendly name and friendly tags would be added to an article's Friendly Details screen, accessed through the standard page menu, and only available to those with space admin privileges or higher.    *Friendly name*    In particularly large spaces, there is often the requirement to name article with over-long names to in order to ensure uniqueness across the space and also to heavily contextualise the info, such as a single space containing the details for multiple tools used on a single project. Each article pertaining to a single tool requires the tool's name prepended to ensure uniqueness. However, in the body of the relevant tool's articles, this extra naming is actual inconvenient, unnecessary, and occassionally intrusive.    A 'friendly name' would allow information designers to dispense with the extra naming baggage, and create articles that read better.    I'm aware of the ability to set custom text while including links in articles, but that text once changed breaks the logical link with the article and can forever be missed when updates are made to the article directly.    The friendly name allows for a single point of update, while options to use the article's title or the article's friendly name in links, children displays, content by label, and other macros give us many more options.    *Friendly tags*    Labels and tags are a great way to categorise and gather articles together, not least of all with the plethora of macros available but also in searches.    However, labels as currently implemented have two drawbacks:   # They are permanently visible on the page, which may not be appropriate for certain categories (secret projects, keywords, <USER>).   # There is no specific permission to gate access to labels. Access to the page is all that is needed to add labels or even remove labels, some of which are invaluable to the organisation of information.    Friendly tags, added and maintained in the same screen as the friendly name, would be hidden to all other than space admins and above. They would remain unmolested from accidental deletion or unintentional hijacking by others who innocently reuse a 'restricted' or administrative label on their own content.         Both these friendly features give content managers the confidence to arrange their content free from fear of their efforts being interfered with, however innocently, by other wiki users."""

summary: "Saving a newly created page changes the pageID when collaborative editing is turned off"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  When collaborative editing is turned on, third party app is able to get the appropriate pageId on create or save. Unfortunately, when turned off, the pageId changes.    h3. Environment  Confluence Server versions 6.8.2, 6.9.0, 6.9.3    h3. Steps to Reproduce  1. Create a new page, observe the pageId:  At this point the app does a GET request to check the page properties, finding none (normal behaviour) as this is a new page.    2. Save the page (publish)  The app applies numbered headings to the page, which results in a PUT request in the following form:       Note that at this point, the app pushes the numbered heading config via put to the app, receiving it in the following block:         Since the page is saved now, we can observe that numbered headings are not applied to the page, this is because the pageID changed upon saving the page.     !screenshot-2.png|thumbnail!     3. Click on edit:  At this point we do a GET request just like in step 1 to check the page config:     Retrieving information from the following resource:     Vendor observes that the pageID did indeed change.     !screenshot-3.png|thumbnail!     4. Nothing happens when the numbered headings is applied  In vendor's debugger they added breakpoint in this code to check the pageID at those points, they added screenshots of those to show that indeed the pageID changes when creating a new page and saving it.    *_Video recording_*:  [^Screen Recording 2018-12-19 at 15.04.07.mov] or can be found here: [DEVHELP-1947|https://ecosystem.atlassian.net/browse/DEVHELP-1947]    * Every load of a page, the app does an init by GETing the page-configuration. Initially, there's nothing yet but when app is used then changes should be pushed on save    * The pageID is requested via a get in the following method:        h3. Expected Results  pageID stays the same when saving page    h3. Actual Results  pageID changed after saving the newly created page    h3. Notes  Vendor added an interesting note - when the page is eventually saved, the pageID seems to be stable and doesn't change. This is after everything saving it initially, so:    1. Create page  2. Save page   3. Open page  4. Save page (at this point, the pageID is the same when opened in action 3)    This is why a lot of vendor's customers don't experience this issue as they apply the app on already saved pages.    * I'm guesssing the main problem is the contentId returned by: AJS.params.contentId    h3. Impact  There are *_7,830_* possible affected customer active installs    h3. Workaround    Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Macro parameters containing '=' or '|' are escaped incorrectly when CustomHtmlEditorPlaceholder is used"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    When macro parameters contains any or combination of characters: [""""="""", """"|"""", """"<""""], values are escaped incorrectly when CustomHtmlEditorPlaceholder is used.  h3. Environment    Confluence *5.10 to 6.12*  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Define a macro implementing CustomHtmlEditorPlaceholder   # Add some parameters to it   # Try to save the macro with parameters containing '=' or '|'    -> the value of data-macro-parameters attribute on the placeholder is wrongly escaped   -> trying to get the macro parameters to custom macro editor fails because there are unescaped '=' and '|' characters inside individual macro parameter values   -> these values are interpreted as separators for macro parameters and the macro parameters are parsed wrongly    -> some parameters end up missing altogether (so probably some error happens somewhere)  h3. Expected Results    Macro parameters are escaped correctly  h3. Actual Results    Macro parameters are escaped incorrectly and lost when trying to further edit the macro  h3. Notes   * '<' fails but for a different reason   (CustomPlaceholderEditorMarshaller.generateCommonMacroAttributes does a substringBetween call that does not work as expected if the macro parameter value contains '>' characters)   * Root Cause - The bug is in {{CustomPlaceholderEditorMarshaller.decorateCustomHtml}}:     # It first uses {{generateCommonAttributes()}} to get a string that contains correctly escaped data-macro-parameters attribute   # Then it uses the escaped string as 'replacement' argument when calling {{matcher.replaceAll()}}   # the code does not take into account that also the 'replacement' argument would need to escape the '\' characters by doubling them   [https://docs.oracle.com/javase/7/docs/api/java/util/regex/Matcher.html#replaceAll(java.lang.String])    h3. Workaround    This can be fixed by pre-escaping the parameter values inside the getCustomPlaceholder() call and mutating the macro parameters map received as an argument  h3. Impact    Possibly affects *_1,219_* app installs"""

summary: "not error message when user with restricted doamin sign up for an account"
description: """h3. Description  The user tried to sign up via {{User Signup Option}} with a not allowed domain. However, when the user submits the information, Confluence does not show the error that the user is not able to signup and only refreshed the page.    *Steps to reproduce*  1. Administration > User Management > User Signup Options  2. Select the option 'Allow people to sign up to create their account'  3. <USER>the option 'Restricted by Domain(s)' and add domains of your choice  4. Save the configuration  5. Log out of the instance  6. Click on the 'Sign up here' link on the login page  7. Fill in the form and use an email address that is not from the domains you chose  8. Click on the 'Sign up' button    *Expectation*  Display a message stating that the user cannot sign up with the email address desired and the sign up has failed.     *Actual Behavior*  Nothing happens, the page just refreshes.    h3. Workaround  Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Database configuration is far too complicated to deliver with confidence"
description: """In the Confluence server, the confluence.cfg.xml file contains all the necessary additions to point the server at an alternative database, HOWEVER these items are not labelled in a human readable way.    For instance;  To modify address for the server it would be more customer friendly to label the 'hibernate.connection.url' field would be more communicably labelled as the more concise 'database.url',    A scripted option would not be complicated either, though it would not be platform agnostic and is beyond my own needs;  read -p """"Please enter the database url: """" database.url  read -p """"Please enter the database port: """" database.port  read -p """"Please enter the type of database being used: """" database.type  read -p """"Please enter the database name: """" database.name  {IF database.type=""""psql"""", database.type=""""jdbc\:postgresql""""}  sed 's/ORIGINAL_VALUE_HERE/<property name=""""hibernate.connection.url"""">${database.type}://${database.url}:${database.port}/${database.name}</property>' ${CONFLUENCE_HOME}/confluence.cfg.xml"""

summary: "Default Confluence search to Basic to avoid error message Oops something went wrong with your search"
description: """We have Atlassian Confluence 6.12.1 server installed and sometimes we see the search shows an error     *Oops, something went wrong with your search.*   We could not successfully complete the search. No field exists with the name: 'test' Did you mean one of : text, type, title    Then we found out, it due to *Advanced CQL search*    h3. Steps to reproduce    1. In Confluence, switch to *Advanced CQL search* and search for something, like: *space=HR* or *text~test*. This works fine.   2. Then, in the *Quick Search* (top right menu navigator), search for something like: *test*, then search page flash for a second with basic search results, but quickly show an error     We believe the error was due to in the search box has *test* as a text, but since we were using CQL search previously, it defaults to the advance search and *test* is of course an invalid CQL search term hence the error shown.    h3. Suggestion:    a. To always default to basic search when using Quick Search   b. OR to add a toggle in settings page to allow item a. above   c. OR to always add *text*~SEARCH_TEXT or a smart function in back-end to determine what users are searching for (like in JIRA Quick Search)"""
comments: "Same issue over here on our large instance." [SEP] "This appears to NOT be a Confluence native feature, but rather a feature of the plugin 'Scriptrunner for Confluence'. The 'Advanced Search' toggle is NOT natively available in Confluence, but only with Scriptrunner. You can disable BOTH the ability to use the Advanced search mode AND the defaulting user-preference, but there is no way to augment this behavior.    This should instead be raised as a Feature Request with the Scriptrunner vendor 'Adaptavist'.    Disable the module labeled {{Cql Functions Search Button (cql-functions-search-button)}} to remove this feature."

summary: "Create Jira Issue from Confluence failed if the Jira Project contains fields made required through workflow Validators in Jira"
description: """*Steps To Replicate*   # In *JIRA* side, go to a project workflow and edit it   # Select the 'Create' transition   # Select the *Validators* option   # Click 'Add validator'   # Select the 'Fields Required' option   # Select a system field (which is *Locked* by default) (e.g. *Epic Link*)   # Publish workflow   # In *Confluence*, try to create a new Jira Issue by following the steps below:   # Highlight some text on your page and choose the !https://confluence.<USER>doc/files/427623543/704578381/1/1422394769659/Creat+JIRA+Issue.png|width=19! *Create Jira issue* icon that appears above the highlighted text.   ## Enter your *server* (if you have multiple Jira sites connected to Confluence), *project* that contain the required field, *issue type* and *description*.   ## Choose *Create*.     *Expected Behaviour*  The required field listed in the form to create the Jira Issue.    *Actual Behaviour*  (i) The required field cannot be filled out because it is not listed in the form to create the Jira Issue and the following ERROR appear in the UI:      *Workaround*  Create the issue directly in JIRA"""

summary: "Companion: jwt token not passed in a standard way"
description: """the Companion app gets a link from the Confluence server which includes the JWT.    The link has the JWT as value of an query parameter named """"jwt"""". Please change that to """"access_token"""", as this is a common way to pass a JWT in a request (RFC 6749). """

Confluence Server code doesn't expect spaceid to be null in drafts and fails.    Example query to Confluence DB:  """

summary: "Add SEO-oriented tools for managing poorly accessed documentation"
description: """As a documentation maintainer, I want to know what searches are failing to find good results in the confluence Search tool.    I would like to see some information on:   # Pages that have not been accessed for a very long time.   # Searches that turned up no results.   # Searches that resulted in a high bounce rate. Eg: User clicks on a lot of pages for a search, but can't seem to find a good result.   # Search results that result in a high bounce rate. Eg: Users often click on this page, but it's not what they were searching for (regardless of whether they were different searches).    This will help me find pages that have been abandoned (1), or search results that are under documented (2, 3), or that show up in misleading search results (4)."""
comments: "My company uses Confluence for maintaining and publishing our documentation website to the public. We want to be able to easily create a sitemap and be able to monitor our site's performance. Currently, Google is not indexing our Confluence pages, which was very discouraging given Google has been doing that for  two years until a few months ago. It'll be great if Confluence can implement an integrated SEO tool to assist with these tasks. Thanks for your consideration.    <USER> Infoblox, Inc."

summary: "Add a note in the HTML macro, letting users know that the use of <body> tags will replace the page's default <body> tag"
description: """h4. Issue summary  # When the HTML macro is used, users are allowed to use<body> tags.  # The use of this tag will override the default <body> tag of the page, causing Confluence not to load the rest of the content on the page after the HTML macro.  # It would good if we have some sort of note to inform the user about this to prevent confusion.  #* CONFSERVER-18486    h4. Current behavior   # No indication to the user that the use of <body> tag in a <body> tag will cause Confluence to stop rendering the rest of the page.    h4. Suggested behavior  # Add a note to inform the user about the behavior with the <body> tag."""
