summary: "Create an scheduled job to cleanup the versioned user information as it is not used."
description: """Each change of user information (profile) like description, will create a new record in the CONTENT table and in BODYCONTENT table.  Since we can not revert back changes like this and having in ming big instances (10 000 users or more), data can accumulate quickly to millions of records.    It would be beneficial to have either API call that we can use to delete this data or to have a scheduled job that we can use to remove this data.    This data can be located with this query:  """
comments: "Doesn't this pose a GDPR problem to system owners?" [SEP] "In the meantime we wrote a tiny little plugin that cleans this old data out.     It seems really easy and straight forward to do this but since we have no idea for what profile data are versionable at all we are not sure if it is really without risk to clean that data. Is there a point we miss? We would like to avoid doing destructive changes that will cause negative effects we don't have any idea about :)" [SEP] "Hi all,    you may have a look on this two GDPR-AddOns. With this AddOns you will be able to be *fully compliant* to the GDPR-Policies.   * JIRA: [https://marketplace.<USER>1218962 6|https://marketplace.<USER>1218962]   * Confluence: [https://marketplace.<USER>1219041 3|https://marketplace.<USER>1219041]    Best Regards   Domenico"

summary: "Edit in Office with Office 365 generates ""Upload Failed Error"""
description: """h3. Issue Summary  When saving changes to a file in Office 365 you'll receive an error:      h3. Environment  Office 365    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # On a machine with Office 365 open a document from Confluence via """"Edit in Office""""  # Make changes to the file  # Save the file    h3. Expected Results  The file is saved and uploaded to Confluence. A new version of the file is available    h3. Actual Results  You'll run into the error in Office:      There are no errors in the Confluence logs    h3. Workaround  To workaround this bug you have two options:  # Select *Save a Copy*  ## Save a copy to the location designated  ## The file will be properly uploaded to Confluence with a new version  # Select *Discard Changes*  ## Any changes you've just made will be removed  ## Make the desired changes again  ## Save the file  ## This time the file will be properly uploaded with a new version  ## Note, the Office document will not close automatically after the upload in this scenario"""
comments: "Hi, we are badly affected by this bug. The workaround provided is not a good one, as we lose data by doing so." [SEP] "We are also having this issue? Can this be looked into?" [SEP] "Hi,    I am facing the same issue,    I understood that Atlassian is not supporting ""Edit-In-Office"" anymore but as we are not moving to Companion app immediately , we would like to have a fix for this issue temporarily until we migrate. (Note: provided workaround leads to loss of data when user forgets to perform it)    I would like to know the below:   # Root cause of the actual issue,    ** *Reason*: If we get to know the actual root cause, we can also try checking the temporary solution from our side.       # Is there any chance of fixing this issue ? or Is issue issue is fixed already in any of the confluence version ?   ** As there are many users facing this issue, Atlassian might have planned to fix this issue or already fixed the issue ,but not seeing any update anywhere.         Regards    PraKasH          " [SEP]

summary: "Saving a PDF file in Adobe Reader DC via the companion app in Windows does not update the file in Confluence"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  Trying to use the companion app in Confluence to edit a PDF file in Adobe Reader DC does not update Confluence on the first save.    Could reproduce in Windows 10, but not in Mac OS.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  _Sample pdf for the steps below: [^test_pdf.pdf]_  # Preview a PDF on the page and open in Adobe Reader DC using the Companion App (*Edit with* > *Edit with Acrobat Reader*)  # Make a change to the pdf (e.g. highlight some text)  # Go to *File* > *Save*.  # In the """"Save as"""" prompt, overwrite the file in the correlating companion app folder.  !reader_saveas.png|thumbnail! !replaceit_prompt.png|thumbnail!    h3. Expected Results  With the new file saved, Confluence will detect the change and activate the """"Upload"""" button to upload the new version of the file.  !active_button.png|thumbnail!    h3. Actual Results  The change is not detected, the button is inactive, and the updated file cannot be uploaded.  !inactive_button.png|thumbnail!    h3. Notes  * Could reproduce in Windows 10, but not in Mac OS.  * Reader opens the """"Save as"""" prompt on all first saves, even for existing files.    h3. Workaround  Two known workarounds:  * Make another edit in Adobe Reader and save. All saves after the initial """"Save as"""" prompt should activate the """"Upload"""" button in Confluence.  * Download the file and upload the new attachment version to the page (not using the companion app, [Upload Files|https://confluence.<USER>doc/upload-files-139513.html])"""
comments: "Closing this ticket as this is not a bug. We have publicly documented this limitation here ([https://confluence.<USER>doc/edit-files-170494553.html]):    !image-2019-01-30-14-54-48-655.png|width=739,height=207!" [SEP] "This issue is still a bug.    The previous comment is wrong, we don´t save a new version, also the PDF ist still a PDF.         We tried it out and were able to get more information:    It seems that small changes in the PDF with Adobe Reader are not recognized by the Companion App. With larger changes everything works fine.    So it seems there is a listener for the filedate and filesize. Only if the filesize was changed the Companion App starts with the upload process." [SEP] "A fix for this issue is available in Companion 1.1.0. View the [Release Notes|https://confluence.<USER>doc/atlassian-companion-app-release-notes-958455712.html] to see what other issues are resolved.    If Companion is installed via the MSI, then it will need to be manually updated to the new version.    "

summary: "Clicking on the left side of Confluence's editor adds an extra white space in the page"
description: """h3. Summary      * When you edit a page with sections in it and click on the left side of the editor, a white space is added at the beginning of the section. If you look at the storage format, you can see that the following tag was added in the page:      h3. Steps to Reproduce     # Create a new page with sections.   !1.png|thumbnail!   # Publish the page.  # Edit the page.  # On the editor, click on the left side of it.   !2.png|thumbnail!     h3. Expected Results      * No changes are seen in the editor.    h3. Actual Results     * A white space is added in the section and is not editable. The following tag is added in the page:      h3. Workaround     # Publish the page with the extra white space in it.  # Edit the page again.  # The white space is now editable and you can remove it."""
comments: "{panel:title=Atlassian Update - September 2019|borderStyle=solid|borderColor=#6554c0|titleBGColor=#6554c0|bgColor=#eae6ff|titleColor=#ffffff}    Thank you for your interest in this ticket. The Confluence Server and Data Center team released a major upgrade to the editor in 6.14. Due to the age of this ticket and/or lack of updates, we suspect that this issue is no longer relevant or was resolved as a result of the upgrade and are now closing this ticket.    If this issue is still a problem in your Confluence instance in version 6.15 or later, please tag me in your comment and we can reopen this ticket for review.    You can read more about the editor upgrade in the [6.14 Release Notes|https://confluence.<USER>doc/confluence-6-14-release-notes-963655609.html].      Thanks    <USER>| Senior Product Manager, Confluence Server and Data Center    {panel}"

summary: "Custom fields not displayed in Jira issue macro when changing the language"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    When changing the language in Jira, the custom fields that have added translation will disappear from Jira Issue Macro.  h3. Steps to Reproduce    Create a custom field in Jira and add a translation in the desired language.   Add Jira Issue Macro in Confluence Page and navigate to macro display options to include this custom field   Change the language in Jira  h3. Expected Results    The custom field should show the translated name along with the assigned value  h3. Actual Results    The custom field remains the same, but the value is missing.    !Screen Shot 2018-12-28 at 2.22.00 PM.png|width=534,height=245!    When you edit the macro, you will notice that the field is not on the list anymore.  h3. Workaround    Create a new custom field without translation  h3. Workaround 2    Manually add the translated custom field from the list in Macro settings"""
comments: "Hi,    It also affects my clients, is it possible to get a fix for that?    Even if this ticket is open for more than a year now, I think it could really affect users from different country, so probably big company around the world :).    Thx"

summary: "No submit button on support.atlassian.com when selecting Questions for Confluence under product Marketplace Apps"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  No submit button on support.<USER>when selecting Questions for Confluence under product Marketplace Apps, see the attached screenshot.    h3. Environment  * Confluence Cloud  * Marketplace Apps  * Questions for Confluence    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Go to support.<USER> # Select *Technical Issues and Bugs* under *What can we help you with?*  # Then select *Marketplace Apps* under *Which product is this for?*  # And select *Questions for Confluence* under *Which Marketplace app?*    h3. Expected Results  There should be a submit button or something which should tell the next step.      h3. Actual Results  No submit button appears     h3. Notes  The customer can create ticket related to Questions for Confluence by selecting Confluence as a product in spite of Marketplace Apps but the point is why this option is even given if it takes to nothing.    h3. Workaround  The customer can create ticket related to Questions for Confluence by selecting Confluence as a product in spite of Marketplace Apps"""

summary: "Race condition in atlassian-ical4j causes high CPU usage in Confluence"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  The latest version of _atlassian-ical4j_'s method {{getCachedOnset}} is susceptible to trigger race condition, in which may lead to high CPU usage in Confluence.    (i) Team Calendar make use of _atlassian-ical4j_ library to support the implementation of timezones in an iCalendar format.  In this case, the race condition was observed specifically when the library was trying to determine whether the retrieved Calendar events' time is in daylight savings time.     h3. Evidence in Thread Dumps  Example thread:      h3. Workaround  None at the time being."""
comments: "A fix for this issue is available to Server and Data Center customers in Confluence 6.13.3  [Upgrade|https://www.<USER>software/confluence/download-archives?utm_source=confluence_bugfix&utm_campaign=confluence_bugfix&utm_medium=email] now or check out the [Release Notes|http://confluence.<USER>display/DOC/Confluence+6.13+Release+Notes?utm_source=confluence_bugfix&utm_campaign=confluence_bugfix&utm_medium=email] to see what other issues are resolved."

summary: "Admin UI should warn when mail disabled by JVM argument atlassian.mail.senddisabled "
description: """h3. Problem Definition  Mail is disabled when {{-Datlassian.mail.senddisable=true}} is added in JVM argument, but administrator cannot know it from UI easily.     h3. Suggested Solution  Add a warning as Jira does in a mail configuration page.   !Screen Shot 2018-12-27 at 18.17.36.png|thumbnail!       h3. Workaround   Go to [System Information|https://confluence.<USER>doc/viewing-system-information-150499.html] and check if  {{-Datlassian.mail.senddisable=true}} is configured in JVM argument. """

summary: "Incorrect ""Execution behavior"" described in document ""Scheduled Jobs"""
description: """h3. Problem Definition    The description of """"Execution behavior"""" for the following jobs in the document """"[Scheduled Jobs|https://confluence.<USER>doc/scheduled-jobs-96567525.html]"""" is incorrect.   * JournalCleaner   * MailQueueFlushJob    h3. Suggested Solution  ||Job Id||Execution Behavior(Wrong)||Execution Behavior(Correct)||  |JournalCleaner|Once per node|Once per cluster|  |MailQueueFlushJob|Once per cluster|Once per node|"""
comments: "Doc has been updated. Changes will be publicly available later today. "

summary: "Let primary node execute ""per cluster"" scheduled jobs keeping other nodes standby on DC"
description: """h3. Problem Definition    Administrator cannot know the """"per cluster"""" [scheduled jobs|https://confluence.<USER>doc/scheduled-jobs-96567525.html] are executed at which node in Data Center.     h3. Suggested Solution  * Add an option to configure primary node to execute """"per cluster"""" jobs.    h3. Why this is important  * Because the log of scheduled job execution will be dispersed to all nodes without primary-secondary configuration.   * To prevent the """"Clean Journal Entries"""" job to recreate index snapshot on a node that has broken index. ([Administer your Data Center search index|https://confluence.<USER>doc/administer-your-data-center-search-index-879956107.html])      h3. Workaround   Login to the expected node and run the scheduled job manually."""

summary: "Subscribe Calendar button is missing if restriction is applied"
description: """h4. Problem Statement  If a calendar has edit restriction applied to it, an unrestricted user will not be able to see the """"Subscribe"""" button.    h4. Steps to Replicate  # Create a test calendar with no restrictions  # Create two test user (user1, user2)  # Both users are able to see the """"Subscribe"""" button on the top right-hand corner (next to the """"Add Event"""" button)  # Apply edit restriction to only user1  # Log in as user2 and check if the button is still present    user1 will see:   !Screen Shot 2018-12-25 at 8.08.55 PM.png|thumbnail!     user2 will see:   !Screen Shot 2018-12-25 at 8.09.31 PM.png|thumbnail!     h4. Workaround  The 'subscribe' button can still be found in the options (...) drop-down list"""
comments: "I've also forced the subscribe button to appear using CSS but using it doesn't actually work. I'm guessing this has to do with the fact that on macOS and iOS you have many ""URLs"" you can subscribe to and that button only provides one path.    If that is the case, please make the default path use the ""iCal"" generated link for those on a Mac, iPhone, or iPad. Thank you!"

summary: "Completely remove the Confluence usage stats add-ons "
description: """We know Confluence usage stats add-ons cause the performance issue in the system, and in the new Confluence versions, some customers still using it. Could we completely remove the add-on in the new version?"""

summary: "Thumbnails should display ""Click to enlarge"" or a magnifier icon on top of images automatically "
description: """{panel:bgColor=#e7f4fa}   *NOTE:* This suggestion is for *Confluence Server*. Using *Confluence Cloud*? [See the corresponding suggestion|http://jira.<USER>browse/CONFCLOUD-23006].   {panel}  *Problem:*  With the small size of thumbnails, when there's only one single thumbnail image in the middle of text on a page, it's often hard to tell it's an image. And the visual presentation is somewhat strange.     *What we do currently:*  Add the text 'click to enlarge' manually as part of an image title as shown in the screen capture.    *Improvements:*  * Thumbnails are displayed with a magnifier on top automatically.   * One step further: Users can view the original image by hovering the mouse over thumbnails after a certain amount of time, say 0.5 or 1 sec, instead of having to click them. """

summary: "Ability to unlink in the code block macro"
description: """We use the code block macro to show example configuration file contents. But regardless of the syntax used (even no syntax), """"x.y"""", for example, is auto-formatted to a URL. When text is auto-converted to a link outside the macro, you can mouse over the link and click unlink. It would be great to have this functionality in the code block macro. Our users will often copy and paste content but accidentally click a non-existent link."""
comments: "Closing as this is a duplicate of     https://jira.<USER>browse/CONFSERVER-57659"

summary: "Customization of Confluence Interface Text Broken"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    According to [Modify Confluence Interface Text|https://confluence.<USER>doc/modify-confluence-interface-text-197745.html], you should be able to modify certain textual elements in the UI by updating the ConfluenceActionSupport.properties file contained in the confluence-x.x.x.jar file.  However, this doesn't appear to work now.  h3. Environment   * Confluence 6.10.0   * Confluence 6.6.0    h3. Steps to Reproduce    To reproduce this problem:   # Follow the steps listed under the """"*Steps For Modification*"""" section of [Modify Confluence Interface Text|https://confluence.<USER>doc/modify-confluence-interface-text-197745.html].   # Modify a given property using guidance from the above page.    h3. Expected Results    You should see the UI change based on the modified property.  h3. Actual Results    There are no changes to the UI.  h3. Notes    Through testing, the feature appears to have been broken in a release between version 6.6.0 and 6.10.0.  h3. Workaround    Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior."""

summary: "Specifying directory outside of <confluence-home> for webwork.multipart.saveDir in confluence.cfg.xml causes PDF export to show ""Page Not Found"" error in UI"
description: """h3. Environment  - This is found on any Confluence version with the PDF exporter bundled plugin running at version 5 or later.    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Specify the following path in {{confluence.cfg.xml}}:    # Start (or restart) Confluence to accept changes  # Generate a PDF export    h3. Expected Results  - On completion of a PDF export the file will download and you will be able to continue working on the page you exported from. URI path looks like this:      h3. Actual Results  - On completion of a PDF export the file will be available in the specified temp directory, but in the UI you will be brought to a friendly Confluence 404 page (""""Page Not Found"""". URI path looks like this:      h3. Notes    This came about while researching PDF exporter issues and was exposed as a new bug in the 5.0 addon branch.    h3. Workaround    There is no workaround other than changing the setting to point to its default location or somewhere else in the Confluence home path."""

summary: "Subscriptions to JustWorks iCal URLs are failing"
description: """h3. Summary      * If you try to subscribe to a JustWorks iCal URL, the following error is thrown and you can't subscribe to it:        h3. Steps to Reproduce   # In a Team Calendar, click on *... >> Subscribe by URL*  # Enter the calendar's name, the JustWorks iCal URL and the related space  # Click on the *Subscribe* button    h3. Expected Results      * You can successfully subscribe to the calendar and see its events.    h3. Actual Results     * The subscription fails and the following error is thrown:        * In Confluence's logs, the following is shown:        h3. Notes   * To create an ICS file from the iCal URL, you can enter in your browser the iCal URL followed by a *.ics* extension in the end.    h3. Workaround   * Please follow the steps described in [Subscriptions to iCal URLs of JustWorks applications into Team Calendars are failing|https://confluence.<USER>confkb/subscriptions-to-ical-urls-of-justworks-applications-into-team-calendars-are-failing-962977564.html]"""
comments: "This issue occurs because some of the events from the Justworks calendar export doesn't include the {{DTEND}} attribute, a condition that is not covered by Team Calendars.  This is described in [CONFSERVER-48870|https://jira.<USER>browse/CONFSERVER-48870].    Closing this bug as a duplicate of [CONFSERVER-48870|https://jira.<USER>browse/CONFSERVER-48870]."

summary: "Team Calendars doesn't send notifications to space watchers"
description: """h3. Summary      * If a user is watching the space where a Team Calendar is in and an event is created/edited in it, the user won't be notified about these changes.    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a calendar  # Have _userA_ to be a watcher of the space where this calendar is at  # Have _userB_ to add an event or edit it without adding _userA_ in the event  # Save the changes    h3. Expected Results      * The watchers will receive a notification of the changes made to the calendar's events.    h3. Actual Results     * No notification is sent. In the logs, the following error is seen:        h3. Notes     * This seems to be related to [CONFSERVER-54550|https://jira.<USER>browse/CONFSERVER-54550].    h3.Workaround     * _userA_ will have to stop watching the space and start watching the calendar to receive the event change's notifications."""
comments: "Similarly, a new version of a document in a space does not trigger a notification.  I'm depending on this notification to indicate someone has answered questions in a document and added the new version. Notification works if they add the new version as a second file, but not as an uploaded new version to the existing file.  "

summary: "Confluence shared drafts are exposed as a regular page using the viewpage.action"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  Confluence allows users to access the _view mode_ of a shared draft as a regular page.  As shared drafts should be accessible by the user only when _editing_ a page, exposing it from the _view mode_ may create inconsistencies in the database, such as when a user clicks on the _like_ button when viewing a draft.  When such inconsistence is created, it can then lead to other problems, such as preventing _Collaborative Editing_ from being enabled.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Have a fresh installation of Confluence.  # Create a new page and publish it.  # Edit this page and save a draft without publishing it.   #* At this point you a have a published version + a draft with _unpublished changes_.   !confserver57697_img001.png|thumbnail!   {code:title=Content Table}  65545;25;""""PAGE"""";""""A quick look at the editor (step 2 of 9)"""";""""a quick look at the editor (step 2 of 9)"""";1;"""""""";""""2017-05-10 14:51:51.826"""";"""""""";""""2018-12-21 11:38:11.465"""";""""''"""";;""""current"""";;98305;1;65547;"""""""";"""""""";"""""""";;"""""""";"""""""";"""""""";;;""""""""  65593;2;""""PAGE"""";""""A quick look at the editor (step 2 of 9)"""";""""a quick look at the editor (step 2 of 9)"""";1;""""402806b267d0fabd0167d0fdf69c0000"""";""""2018-12-21 11:42:43.205"""";""""402806b267d0fabd0167d0fdf69c0000"""";""""2018-12-21 11:42:43.205"""";""""''"""";65545;""""draft"""";;98305;;;"""""""";"""""""";"""""""";;"""""""";"""""""";"""""""";;;""""""""  ;;"""""""";"""""""";"""""""";;"""""""";"""""""";"""""""";"""""""";"""""""";;"""""""";;;;;"""""""";"""""""";"""""""";;"""""""";"""""""";"""""""";;;""""""""    # Access the {{<Confluence Base URL>/pages/viewpage.action?pageId=<draftId>}} URL.   #* Confluence will load the draft as a regular page, with a message _You are viewing an old version of this page_.   !confserver57697_img004.png|thumbnail!   # Click on the _Like_ button associated with the draft and a record in the {{likes}} table is created.  # Go to *Cog Icon* > *General Configuration* > *Collaborative Editing*.  # In the _Collaborative editing_ (CE) administration page, turn *off* CE.  # Turn *on* CE and monitor {{atlassian-confluence.log}} log file for the entry below.        h3. Expected Results  A shared draft should not be exposed to the user on the same way as a regular page.  When {{<Confluence Base URL>/pages/viewpage.action?pageId=<draftId>}} URL is accessed, it should retrieve a 404 response page, even when the draft exists in the database.  Confluence REST API protects the user from this bug, providing a 404 message when trying to access the draft using the {{/rest/api/content}} method.   !confserver57697_img003.png|thumbnail!     When Collaborative Editing is enabled and the [{{removeStaleSharedDrafts}}|https://docs.<USER>atlassian-confluence/6.2.0/com/atlassian/confluence/pages/PageManager.html#removeStaleSharedDrafts--] method is executed, it should consider the {{likes}} table -- and any other with a constraint related to the {{content}} table.    h3. Actual Results  A shared draft can be viewed through {{<Confluence Base URL>/pages/viewpage.action?pageId=<draftId>}} URL.  Then a user can _like_ the draft, what may create a problem if CE is turned off and on.    h3. Workaround  When enabling Collaborative Editing after a long time with it disabled, consider [manually removing stale drafts|https://confluence.<USER>confkb/how-to-manually-remove-stale-drafts-from-confluence-database-951392345.html]."""

summary: "Confluence can degrade if Jira is unresponsive due to gadget feed retrieval requests"
description: """h3. Summary    If Confluence has been integrated with a Jira instance and also contains gadgets on pages, Confluence performance can degrade if Jira has an outage or performance issue. This is due to how Confluence currently stores and fetches gadget data from Jira.  h3. Environment   * Confluence   * Jira   * Confluence Gadgets    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Integrate Confluence & Jira   # Set up some Jira Gadgets in Confluence   # Make Jira unresponsive to Confluence   # Visit a page with a Jira gadget on it    h3. Expected Results   # Confluence may not render the gadget (because Jira is unresponsive) but performance across the instance will remain stable    h3. Actual Results   # Confluence threads can get stuck in a RUNNABLE state attempting to read gadget data from Jira. This has the unfortunate affect of bottlenecking other HTTP threads as those threads take forever to process and can cause a performance issue.    h3. Workaround    Unknown  h3. Diagnosis:    You may see just a few long running *{{RUNNABLE}}* threads with other threads stacking up behind these threads causing a bottleneck.    From *{{RUNNABLE}}* threads, you may see some like the following:    Or Stuck Threads:    (!) Fetching gadget spec doesn't currently have a timeout threshold which explains why these threads can get stuck in a running state."""
comments: "Any update on this bug team? We have a lot of people who are very concerned about this codependency between two of our business critical applications." [SEP] "A fix for this issue is available to Server and Data Center customers in Confluence 7.5.0  [Upgrade|https://www.<USER>software/confluence/download?utm_source=confluence_bugfix&utm_campaign=confluence_bugfix&utm_medium=email] now or check out the [Release Notes|https://confluence.<USER>display/DOC/Confluence+7.5+Release+Notes?utm_source=confluence_bugfix&utm_campaign=confluence_bugfix&utm_medium=email] to see what other issues are resolved." summary: "Team Calendars sending notifications for deleted events"
description: """h3. Summary      * If you have an event which repeats daily and has a reminder and you remove one instance of it of your calendar, you'll still receive the email notification reminding of the deleted event.    h3. Environment   * Windows 7  * Team Calendar 6.0.25    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # In a calendar, create an event that is repeating daily and it has a reminder one hour before the event starts.   # Save the event.   # In the calendar UI, navigate to and click on a future occurrence of this event, choose """"delete – This instance only"""".    h3. Expected Results      * The deleted event won't trigger any reminder notification.    h3. Actual Results     * The deleted event triggers an email notification reminding of its occurrence (even though it is deleted from the UI).    h3. Notes   * On the images 1 and 3, it is shown the calendars with its events and the deleted occurrences.     !1.png|thumbnail!      !3.png|thumbnail!     * On the images 2 and 4, it is shown the notifications received even though some of the events were already deleted.     !2.png|thumbnail!      !4.png|thumbnail!     * On the images 5 and 6, it is shown that the deleted events have been excluded from the calendar (EXDATE field); the image 7 complements these two, showing that the events *35* and *36* are related to the *Test number 5* and *Test number 6* events.     !5.png|thumbnail!      !6.png|thumbnail!      !7.png|thumbnail!     h3.Workaround   N/A"""
comments: "Hi Team,    Do we have any updates on this issue? Is there an ETA or fix version?" [SEP] "Also interested to find out if this issue is being looked at.   An ETA for a fix would be good to know."

summary: "Add Companion App support for iOS"
description: """Prior to Confluence 6.11.0, users were able to edit Office documents in their iOS devices if they access the Desktop version of the site from their browsers.     This however is not possible since the introduction of the Companion App for editing files. Please consider creating a Companion App for iOS."""
comments: "It's very important. I hope this feature will come soon. Please add a high priority." [SEP] "Hello - thank you for voting and watching this suggestion. We recently made changes to Atlassian Companion, which allows you to edit any type of file, if you have a compatible application installed. This requires you to [install Companion|https://confluence.<USER>doc/edit-files-170494553.html] on your computer. You can also read more about these recent changes on Community: [File editing in Confluence Server: Atlassian Companion updates are almost here!|https://community.<USER>t5/Confluence-discussions/File-editing-in-Confluence-Server-Atlassian-Companion-updates/td-p/1285578]. summary: "On error at /rest/ stack-trace is publicly visible"
description: """h3. Summary  On Confluence server 6.12.2 requesting wrong REST URL /rest/cql/contenttypes?category=test we will see full stack-trace.    The same we can see at https://confluence.<USER>rest/cql/contenttypes?category=test    On production, a regular user should not see the stack-trace when an error happens.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Go to an incorrect REST URL    h3. Expected Results  An error message without stack-trace    h3. Actual Results  An error message with stack-trace  """
comments: "Just wanted to note, that I see two bugs here:    1. application should not return error 500 on wrong arguments - it should return  [422|https://httpstatuses.com/422] or [400|https://httpstatuses.com/400]  2. application in production should never returns stack-trace or any other internal details. It should log those things and return en error message."

summary: "Table editing functionality"
description: """Moving a selection of cells to a different position is two mouse clicks in Google Tables/Docs or Excel. In Confluence, this is not possible. Adding or removing rows or columns to achieve the same goal is not helping, as it will affect other content. People often resort to copying the table in another application, making the needed changes there, and copying it back to Confluence (...and then adding the lost formatting).    Please consider adding this functionality - thank you."""

summary: "PDF export: Links are only converted to ""in-file"" links if they do not contain a hashtag"
description: """h3. Summary  When linking to another page in the same PDF export, a plain link with a URL in the format """"/confluence/ds/MyPage"""" will be converted to an anchor link that will go to that location in the PDF (which is expected). However, if the link contains an anchor link such as """"/confluence/ds/MyPage#MyPage-MyAnchor"""" then the link will not be converted and instead, it will link to the actual confluence instance.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Create PageOne  # Add an anchor in PageOne called MyAnchor  # Create PageTwo in the same space  # Add a link to PageOne#MyAnchor  # Export Space (Space tools > Content Tools > Export > PDF)  # Check the exported PDF and click the link in PageTwo    h3. Expected Results  Link within pdf file    h3. Actual Results  Link to confluence instance  """
comments: "Hi, this is something we will also need. It's one of the main feature we would expect to have in the PDF." [SEP] "Hi, we also need this issue to be fixed! We get the same problem with Confluence 6.15.9"

summary: "Jira issues are not converted to macros when adding to a new page"
description: """h3. Summary    When using a newly created page, inserting a Jira issue via URL does not convert issue to a Jira Issue Macro, but turns the link into a HTML link instead.  h3. Environment   * Confluence Server on Windows or Linux   * Confluence with Application Link to Jira (with or without impersonation)   * Relevant Jira version with sample project as documented in  [Application links version matrix|https://confluence.<USER>applinks/application-links-version-matrix-779174762.html]   * Confirm macros are activated as documented in   ** [JIRA links fail to auto-convert to JIRA macros in Confluence editor|https://confluence.<USER>confkb/jira-links-fail-to-auto-convert-to-jira-macros-in-confluence-editor-800862617.html]    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Click Create in Confluence to create a new page   # Copy a URL of an issue from Jira and paste directly into the Confluence editor    h3. Expected Results    The link is converted into a Jira Issue Macro.  h3. Actual Results    The link is converted into a HTML link.  h3. Notes    When you paste a Jira link like   * [http://mycompany.com/jira/browse/TIC-1]    The editor makes a POST request to Confluence with URL of   * [http://mycompany.com/confluence/rest/tinymce/1/macro/placeholder]    and payload of    the return from the POST request is the grey image box which represents the Jira Issue Macro    The original POST request is not being sent on newly created pages.  h3. Workaround    Save the page, and re-edit it before adding Jira issues. Newly added Jira issue URLs are converted."""

I don't have the time right now to do a full upgrade once again, but there seems to be something about an upgraded page and attempting to use the Jira issue macro automatically the first time.  I have confirmed that going to other pre-upgrade pages and editing them now (after the testing done above), the Jira issue macro func

summary: "Ability to configure a limit on the number of Space Administrators by Space on Confluence"
description: """h3. Problem Definition  Current Space Administrators are free to add the [Space Admin permission|https://confluence.<USER>doc/space-permissions-overview-139521.html] to any user or group, without any configurable limit on the number of space administrators.    h3. Suggested Solution  As a Confluence administrator, I would like to globally limit the number of space administrators on each Space.  When a Space admin is adding the space admin permission to new users or groups and if that limit is surpassed, then the admin would receive a friendly notification saying no more admins could be added due to the limit.  This functionality must be able to check the number of users under a group (consider nested groups as well).    h3. Why this is important  Limiting the maximum number of space administrators on all spaces would help to avoid current administrators adding large security groups, without knowing who's part of that group.  As Uncle <USER>said, _with great power comes great responsibility_; and great is the power assigned to a Space administrator, even being able to delete the full space.  Limiting the number of space admins by space is a way to safeguard content, insuring this permissions is wisely assigned.     h3. Workaround  Periodically review the administrators of each space and perform manual _curation_ of space admins.  The following KB articles may help the Confluence administrator to determine the users and groups with Space admin permissions:   * [How do I view a list of all space administrators for all spaces|https://confluence.<USER>confkb/how-do-i-view-a-list-of-all-space-administrators-for-all-spaces-346325879.html]   * [How to list which spaces a group can access|https://confluence.<USER>confkb/how-to-list-which-spaces-a-group-can-access-720636535.html]   * [How to Check Permissions for a Space via SQL Queries|https://confluence.<USER>confkb/how-to-check-permissions-for-a-space-via-sql-queries-717062216.html]"""

summary: "Password reset messages are misleading"
description: """h4. Background  CWD-3484 underlined a misleading wording that was displayed as password reset message. While it apparently has been fixed for Crowd, it is still an issue in Confluence:    {quote}  The message that is displayed can be very misleading as it always informs users that an email was sent even though this is not always true. If there are no plans to make the message conditional, it would certainly make sense to at least change the wording of the generic message. Perhaps something like the following would be more appropriate:  """"Thank you. If we find an account matching the username you have entered you will receive an email with further instructions and a reset password link. The link will lead to a page where you can choose your new password.""""  {quote}  !screenshot-1.png|thumbnail!     h4. Steps to reproduce    # Go to the login page and click on _Forgot your password?_  # Type in any string as username which is either existing or not  # The following misleading message is displayed:  !screenshot-2.png|thumbnail!   !screenshot-3.png|thumbnail!   !screenshot-1.png|thumbnail!     As pointed out in CWD-2457, not revealing whether or not the user exists in the DB is a cautious design decision, which is fine.    h4. Suggestion    However, in line with CWD-3484, a more generic wording should be used in Confluence as well:  {quote}  Thanks! If we recognize that email address, you should receive a link to reset your password via email soon. If you don't receive an email in the next five minutes, check your spam folder or try again with a different email address.  {quote}    Our additional suggestion:  {quote}  Please also make sure to not include a leading or trailing whitespace (e.g. by copying and pasting your username.  {quote}"""

summary: "Site reindex should reset journal entry pointer upon successful completion to prevent  the reindex run after each restart when the journal entry queue is empty"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  As documented in [How to Rebuild the Content Indexes From Scratch on Confluence Data Center|https://confluence.<USER>confkb/how-to-rebuild-the-content-indexes-from-scratch-on-confluence-data-center-833941594.html], _journal_ directory must be deleted before reindexing. This however may cause that node to run full site reindexing at every restart afterward if there is no new index journal (content updates that require an index update e.g. new page, page update, <USER>..) during that reindexing. The reason is Confluence expects there must always be some entries in [journal directory|https://confluence.<USER>confkb/deleting-the-journal-ids-on-nodes-will-cause-indexing-to-run-on-restart-945123939.html].  h3. Steps to Reproduce    Just follow the instructions described in [How to Rebuild the Content Indexes From Scratch on Confluence Data Center|https://confluence.<USER>confkb/how-to-rebuild-the-content-indexes-from-scratch-on-confluence-data-center-833941594.html]    h3. Expected Results  {{<confluence-home>/journal/main_index}} is created when the index is rebuilt.    h3. Actual Results  {{<confluence-home>/journal/main_index}} is not created.    h3. Notes  When you do some page operation (e.g. new page, page update, <USER>..) after rebuilding index, you can see that the file is created for the first time.    h3. Workaround  When you do [How to Rebuild the Content Indexes From Scratch on Confluence Data Center|https://confluence.<USER>confkb/how-to-rebuild-the-content-indexes-from-scratch-on-confluence-data-center-833941594.html], add a step to make page operation on Confluence as  {quote}  Start Node 1 *and make any page operation on Confluence (this will recreate journal/main_index directory)* . This node will rebuild its index from the database automatically on startup. Log files will print lines like the below to signify the reindex has been kicked off:  {quote}"""

summary: "Content must not be null error thrown for missing attachment"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Navigating to a URL of a non-existent attachment leads to a stack track in the browser, as follows:     {code}  Cause  java.lang.IllegalArgumentException: Content must not be null      at org.springframework.util.Assert.notNull(Assert.java:115)    Stack Trace:[hide]  java.lang.IllegalArgumentException: Content must not be null   at org.springframework.util.Assert.notNull(Assert.java:115)   at com.atlassian.confluence.pages.attachments.CachingAttachmentDao.getLatestAttachment(CachingAttachmentDao.java:69)   at com.atlassian.confluence.pages.DefaultAttachmentManager.getAttachment(DefaultAttachmentManager.java:143)   at com.atlassian.confluence.pages.DefaultAttachmentManager.getAttachment(DefaultAttachmentManager.java:109)   at com.atlassian.confluence.pages.DelegatorAttachmentManager.getAttachment(DelegatorAttachmentManager.java:104)   at com.atlassian.confluence.pages.CachingAttachmentManager.getAttachment(CachingAttachmentManager.java:175)   at sun.reflect.GeneratedMethodAccessor833.invoke(Unknown Source)   at sun.reflect.DelegatingMethodAccessorImpl.invoke(DelegatingMethodAccessorImpl.java:43)   at java.lang.reflect.Method.invoke(Method.java:498)   at org.springframework.aop.support.AopUtils.invokeJoinpointUsingReflection(AopUtils.java:302)  {code}    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Navigate to a page in Confluence where an attachment doesn't exist. For example: https://confluence.<USER>download/attachments/189015999/not_exist.jpg.    h3. Expected Results    An error message like 'This attachment doesn't exist or you don't have permissions to view it' is displayed, vs. an unreadable stack trace.     h3. Actual Results  The below exception is thrown in the browser and in the logs:        See screen shot: https://cl.ly/663b43cc4e8c    h3. Workaround  None"""

summary: "Include Attachment Properties link in search results"
description: """h2. Request  Include a """"properties"""" link in Confluence search results, when searching for attachments.    The """"properties"""" link refers to the one displayed in the attachments macro, or attachments page at: {{<base-url>/pages/viewpageattachments.action?pageId=<pageId>}}    h4. Use Case  In large environments where attachments need to be managed or manipulated, the search function is a much easier method to locate attachments, than having to go page by page or use an attachments macro to display a large list of attachments.     h2. Details  This is what the attachments page looks like, which includes the """"properties"""" link for each attachment:   !attachments.png|thumbnail!     This is what the current search results look like, which do not include the """"properties"""" link for attachments:   !search-results.png|thumbnail!   """

description: """h4. Summary   # Search does not find certain pages when the Indexing language is set to the default English and the page name contains a dot, """"."""".   # Search works as expected when using Indexing language like """"Other"""".    h4. Example and Steps to replicate   # Create the following pages, and search using the following terms.   # Found: (/) Not Found: (x)   # *Search term:* """"bj""""   ## *Page name:* bj (/)   ## *Page name:* 1.bj (x)   ## *Page name:* a.bj (x)   ## *Page name:* localhost.bj (x)   ## *Page name:* localhost1.bj (x)   # *Search term:* """"bx""""   ## *Page name:* bx (/)   ## *Page name:* 1.bx (/)   ## *Page name:* a.bx (x)   ## *Page name:* localhost.bx (x)   ## *Page name:* localhost1.bx (/)   # *Search term:* """"bl""""   ## *Page name:* bl (/)   ## *Page name:* 1.bl (/)   ## *Page name:* a.bl (x)   ## *Page name:* localhost.bl (x)   ## *Page name:* localhost1.bl (/)   # *Search term:* """"bm""""   ## *Page name:* bm (/)   ## *Page name:* 1.bm (x)   ## *Page name:* a.bm (x)   ## *Page name:* localhost.bm (x)   ## *Page name:* localhost1.bm (x)   # If we were were to switch the Indexing language to """"other"""", the search for """"bj"""" and """"bm"""" returns the pages for the other pages as well.   # If we were to use wildcard search, like below, all pages are returned, but this also depends on the name of the page before the dot. where pages like *serv0.bjl01*, will not return even with wilcard search is used.   ##        ##       h4. Current Behavior   # Search does not work as expected for certain characters like """"bj"""" and """"bm"""",after a dot in the page name, when the indexing language is the defaut English.    h4. summary: "Confluence iOS App users unable to login after updating to version 1.2.0"
description: """h3. User Scenario:    Error while attempting to log in to Confluence iOS app post upgrade to version 1.2.0  *""""Unauthorised error. Your session timed out. Log in again to keep working""""*  h3. Expected Result :    Login successfully  h3. Actual Result:    Users end up with the above mentioned error  h3. Cause     During the login process cookies sent from the server are stored in the """"pre-authentication cookie store"""" but post login certain cookies are not transferred from the """"pre-authentication cookie store"""" to the """"post authenticated cookie store"""". The absence of these special cookies that are needed for authenticated calls is the reason for the app's session expiry.  h3. Workaround    As of today, developers have shipped a beta version, Confluence iOS app v1.3.0.142 to TestFlight, which can be downloaded using the link [https://testflight.apple.com/join/BP8yM5kF]   h3. Note    This TestFlight build may not be released into production till the end of Jan next year as we are currently in a release freeze.  The issue seems to affect Crowd SSO users as well."""
comments: "I downloaded the beta version, but I have the same problem." [SEP] "The beta v1.3.0.142 works for us with Crowd SSO." [SEP] "Fixed in Version 1.3.0 of the Confluence Server iOS app"

summary: "Allow disabling of automatic Hyperlinks in Code Block Macro"
description: """h3. Problem Definition    When using the [Code Block Macro|https://confluence.<USER>doc/code-block-macro-139390.html], should the sample code placed in the macro contain a URL, it will automatically become a clickable link once saved. This can be an issue when a placeholder URL, such as [https://example.com/], is being used, which will take users to a 404 page or an unwanted area.  h3. Suggested Solution    Provide an option to disable the automatic hyperlink that is created.  h3. Why this is important    Having active links in a code block appears to be extremely undesirable at many companies, especially when documenting any internal APIs, in which there can be a number of links that trigger actions such as tracking counts, trigger jobs, <USER>  h3. Workaround    A current workaround would be to use the no format macro, which unfortunately loses syntax highlighting.    Upgrading to Confluence version 6.13.0 also prevents the automatic creation of links within the code block macro."""

summary: "Clicking the Unpublished Changes lozenge refreshes the page."
description: """h3. Summary  When a page has unpublished content, the Unpublished Changes lozenge appear at the top of the page. This is clickable, and it refreshes the page. This behavior appears in Firefox, Chrome and Safari. In IE, however, it redirects to the home page of the space.     Ideally, Unpublished Changes lozenge should *not* be clickable as the current behavior causes confusion to users.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Create a new Confluence page and add some content to it and then publish it.   # Edit the newly saved page and then add some extra content, this time do not publish. Click on *close*  # The Unpublished Changes lozenge will appear on the top.  # Click on it.    h3. Expected Results  The Unpublished Changes lozenge should not be clickable, or if it is clickable, it should redirect to the edit mode.    h3. Actual Results  The page is refreshed in Firefox, Chrome and Safari. In IE, however, it redirects to the home page of the space.   In Confluence 6.12 onwards, clicking on the """"Unpublished Changes"""" rectangle sends the user to the _#all-updates_ dashboard, which is even worse from user experience point of view.    h3. Workaround  Confluence administrator may choose to change the behaviour of the lozenge, removing the link underneath it.  This can be accomplished with a [custom JavaScript|https://confluence.<USER>confkb/how-to-use-javascript-in-confluence-313458839.html].  When the bug is resolved, you may need to remove this customisation as a best practice.  # Go to *Cog Icon* > *General Configuration* > *Custom HTML*.   #*  Or directly access {{<Confluence Base URL>/admin/viewcustomhtml.action}}  # Click on the _Edit_ button.  # In the _At end of the HEAD_ box, add the below JS.    # Click on _Save_."""
comments: "+500 user" [SEP] "This is really annoying.  I get calls from my 8000 users often about how this isn't working." [SEP] "7.1.2 is affected, too." [SEP] "How could I remove the onmouseover tooltip of the lozenge(""Edit this page to view and publish the changes""), too? It is a little bit annoying to have a tooltip for a not-working-function :(     "

summary: "The request /rest/api/content/ returns No content found with id for drafts"
description: """h3. Summary    Fetching draft data using REST will return a 404 error code.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Create a page and publish it  # Edit the page and exit the editor, choose keep draft if necessary  # Get the *contentid* of the draft  # Execute the following REST call to fetch the draft data:      h3. Expected Results    Draft content is returned as expected.    h3. Actual Results    Draft content is not returned. Instead, this is what we see:        h3.Workaround    It is not possible to access the content ID for drafts directly. Instead, if you need to fetch draft data, use the content ID of the published version of the page and add *status=draft* to the request. Example:        In this request *101112* is the content ID of the last published version of this page."""
comments: "Hi [~<USER>, this looks an expected behavior. After the page is published, the draft id is not expected to be used like this. You can use the page id to access the draft. Remember to add the parameter ""status=draft"" in the rest request. " [SEP] "Closing as ""Not a bug"". "

summary: """Events"" type does not export Who field into ICS summary"

Summary    When exporting to an ICS / iCal file, most event types (Leaves, Birthdays, <USER> in Team Calendars concatenate the """"Who"""" and """"Summary"""" Team Calendar fields into the SUMMARY field in the ICS file. The """"Events"""" event type only includes the """"What"""" Team Calendar field and not the """"Who"""" field in the ics SUMMARY field. description: """h3. Issue Summary    In current versions of Confluence, users are unable to add Flickr URL links utilizing the *Widget Connector Macro*, which we [specifically mentioned|https://confluence.<USER>doc/widget-connector-macro-171180449.html] should work with Flickr. When users add the Flickr URL with the macro, it does not render the content and instead displays a gear icon on the page.    h3. Environment    * Confluence 6.11.0    h3. Steps to Reproduce    # Edit any page and add the *Widget Connector Macro*  # In the *Web Site's Widget URL* parameter, paste the URL provided by Flickr for sharing an image.    h3. Expected Results    When adding the Flickr URL through the macro, it should display the image on the page.    h3. Actual Results    hen users add the Flickr URL with the macro, it does not render the content and instead displays a gear icon on the page:     !gear-icon.png|thumbnail!      h3. Notes    I was able to replicate the issue in Confluence 6.13, as well as trying different Flickr URLs, such as the direct Image URL itself with no luck.    h3. Workaround    The current workaround would be to get the Embed HTML code for an image provided by Flickr and embed it to a page using the [HTML Macro|https://confluence.<USER>doc/html-macro-38273085.html]."""

summary: "PPTX File Conversion Service causes high CPU and/or memory usage because of Aspose Slides InterruptMonitor is not implemented"
description: """h3. Summary  Uploading some PPTX files to Confluence can cause a high CPU/Memory usage which causes the instance to crash or become unavailable. Although this bug was fixed [CONFSERVER-52279|https://jira.<USER>browse/CONFSERVER-52279], the Aspose Library for Powerpoint Slides being used doesn't implement an InterruptMonitor to stop that thread from leaking memory or CPU resources    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Upload a large Powerpoint file  # Wait for the preview to generate  # At the same time monitor the CPU and memory usage on your instance    h3. Expected Results  Confluence should be able to handle the conversion of a PPTX file and if it reaches a certain threshold it should kills/quit the conversion.    h3. Actual Results  Confluence becomes unresponsive. High CPU/Memory usage starts showing and we see this in the thread dumps:  h5. Thread Dumps  In the thread dumps, you would see a *long running thread* that looks like this      h5. Heap Dumps  In the heap dump, you would see the following pattern   !HeapLeakSuspect.png|thumbnail!     h3. Workaround  The current workaround is to upgrade to Confluence 6.10 or newer to benefit from the Sandbox feature for *Confluence Data Center*. You can read more about this in this documentation [Document Conversion Sandbox for Confluence Data Center|https://confluence.<USER>conf610/document-conversion-sandbox-for-confluence-data-center-952623449.html]    If you are running a server instance that is affected by the bug, you can disable the file conversions service as follows until it is possible to upgrade:    * [Disable the file conversions service|https://confluence.<USER>confkb/file-conversion-service-causes-out-of-memory-errors-in-confluence-5-7-or-above-703398523.html#:~:text=Disable%20the%20file%20conversions%20service&text=To%20disable%20the%20file%20conversion%20service%3A,Conversions%20(file%2Dconversions)%20module]    h3. Mitigation Plan  If you are unable to upgrade to Confluence 6.10 and above and you would like to limit the impact of this issue on your instance, you can limit the number of conversion threads in your instance by adding the following JVM argument    (!) *Please note that this JVM argument doesn't remove the risk of one thread causing this issue again, but it reduces the possibility of this issue having a bigger impact on your instance.*"""
comments: "this bug causes in the recent weeks repeated crushes (several times a week Confluence stops responding due to Out of Memory) in production instance of Confluence in a large Server instance (about 1,000 users). the Customer is very frustrated!     For server instances there is no good workaround. You can use an add-on to limit the size of uploaded PPT attachments.     For Data center the workaround is to activate the External Process Pool feature, which makes the file conversion to run in the a separate process and thus preventing the crash of Confluence." [SEP] "A fix for this issue is available to Server and Data Center customers in Confluence 7.0.1  [Upgrade|https://www.<USER>software/confluence/download?utm_source=confluence_bugfix&utm_campaign=confluence_bugfix&utm_medium=email] now or check out the [Release Notes|https://confluence.<USER>doc/confluence-7-0-release-notes-973479448.html?utm_source=confluence_bugfix&utm_campaign=confluence_bugfix&utm_medium=email] to see what other issues are resolved.

summary: "Improving the performance on Adding Team Calendar Macro into Page"
description: """h3. Problem  Adding new Calendar in Page using Team Calendar Macro might fail if the user belongs to too many groups and the space has a lot of calendars within itself.  The macro will search for all group memberships and eventually hit the threshold that is set and the warning message below is thrown in the log:  {code}   [http-nio-8081-exec-4] [confluence.util.profiling.DefaultActivityMonitor] close Exceeded the threshold of 60000 ms   {code}  (i) This threshold is not able to be extended due to the block at CONFSERVER-36883     When creating a new calendar through the Team Calendar Macro, Confluence do a bunch of permission checks for each of the existing subcalendars within the space. The server returns a complete object with all of the sub calendars within the space which is necessary for processing on the front-end. This can be seen below (""""AbstractSubCalendarResource#toExtendedSubCalendar), this is performed when the user clicks """"Add new Calendar""""  {code:java}  private SubCalendarsResponseEntity.ExtendedSubCalendar toExtendedSubCalendar(PersistedSubCalendar subCalendar, ConfluenceUser forUser)   {   UtilTimerStack.push(""""CalendarResource.toExtendedSubCalendar()"""");   try   {   return new SubCalendarsResponseEntity.ExtendedSubCalendar(   subCalendar,   calendarPermissionManager.hasViewEventPrivilege(subCalendar, forUser),   calendarPermissionManager.hasReloadEventsPrivilege(subCalendar, forUser),   calendarPermissionManager.hasEditSubCalendarPrivilege(forUser),   calendarPermissionManager.hasEditEventPrivilege(subCalendar, forUser),   false,   false,   false,   calendarManager.isEventsOfSubCalendarHidden(subCalendar, forUser),   calendarPermissionManager.hasDeleteSubCalendarPrivilege(subCalendar, forUser),   calendarPermissionManager.hasAdminSubCalendarPrivilege(subCalendar, forUser),   new HashSet<SubCalendarsResponseEntity.ExtendedSubCalendar.PermittedUser>(   Collections2.transform(   calendarPermissionManager.getEventViewUserRestrictions(subCalendar),   new UserToPermittedUserTransformer(userAccessor, settingsManager)   )   ),   calendarPermissionManager.getEventViewGroupRestrictions(subCalendar),   new HashSet<SubCalendarsResponseEntity.ExtendedSubCalendar.PermittedUser>(   Collections2.transform(   calendarPermissionManager.getEventEditUserRestrictions(subCalendar),   new UserToPermittedUserTransformer(userAccessor, settingsManager)   )   ),   calendarPermissionManager.getEventEditGroupRestrictions(subCalendar),  // getSubCalendarWarnings(subCalendar),   new HashSet<String>(),   false //reminder   );   }  {code}    Within a few of these """"hasPrivilege"""" methods, if the user is not the creator of the subcalendar, Confluence check Admin group membership for the user {{isUserMemberOfOneGroup(user, CONFLUENCE_ADMINISTRATORS_GROUP_NAMES)}} which gets all of the user's groups and checks if the Admin group is contained within that list. This uses the method    """"DefaultUserAccessor#getGroupNames"""", which calls the following method to performs the permissions check:  {code:java}  public List<String> getGroupNamesForUserName(String userName) {   return (List)(userName == null ? Collections.emptyList() : ImmutableList.copyOf(this.crowdService.search(QueryBuilder.queryFor(String.class, EntityDescriptor.group()).parentsOf(EntityDescriptor.user()).withName(userName).returningAtMost(-1))));   }  {code}    So in summary, for every subcalendar, Confluence may have to retrieve all of the groups that the user belongs to *multiple times*.       h3. Suggestion  Since it will be retrieving the same groups every time, we should be able to improve the performance by adding some simple caching.     h3. Workaround  # Add a new Calendar from *My Calendar* View and link it to the desire space  #* This method can still allow user to create calendar without hitting the issue above  # Search and insert the calendar in a space that has no calendar (in order to avoid this issue) and examine the [Storage Format|https://confluence.<USER>doc/confluence-storage-format-790796544.html] to get the calendar macro that look like below:  {code}   <ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id=""""f1360258-9d29-4828-b510-ba63bdfc51e3"""" ac:name=""""calendar"""" ac:schema-version=""""1"""">      <ac:parameter ac:name=""""id"""">859956f5-0eb8-4169-9c5b-24a034e40578</ac:parameter>    </ac:structured-macro>  {code}  # From the storage format above, either copy it and add it into the affected page using [Source Editor|https://marketplace.<USER>apps/1210722/confluence-source-editor?hosting=server&tab=overview] or type the wiki markup like below to insert the calendar macro:  {code}  {calendar:id=859956f5-0eb8-4169-9c5b-24a034e40578}  {code}"""
comments: "Hi There,    I appreciate the workaround provided to us, it works fine.   However it does not really make sense that why only the macro will have to repeatedly check on the user permission and caused timeout and the wiki markup work just like magic.  We have near to 10K users in our Confluence site, it definitely confuse them when they have to use different method to add team calendar to the page in different confluence page.   I hope you can include the fix of this bug into you nearest release as this is really affecting our users experience using Confluence.    Thanks and Regards,  <USER>    "

summary: "Hitting the base Content API endpoint can cause performance problem for large instances"
description: """h3. Summary  The content API endpoint is available to retrieve content from a Confluence instance. While most users pass arguments that help to filter down tp specific pieces of content, it is possible to hit the generic endpoint which fetches the entire contents of a given Confluence instance.  When this happens, the threads that are tasked to run the operation can run longer than expected, especially with larger Confluence instances. In addition, this is especially true for calls to this endpoint that are made anonymously (as the endpoint contains the @AnonymousAllowed designation) and a Confluence instance is not set up for anonymous access. When this occurs, the operation can still run for a longer than expected time and may ultimately return an empty set to the user.     h3. Environment  * Confluence    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Fill a Confluence with several hundred thousand ->  million pages  # run the following command *{{curl -X GET http://<baseURL>/rest/api/content | python -mjson.tool}}*    h3. Expected Results  # the operation will run and will not impact performance on the instance    h3. Actual Results  # the operation can be expensive and cause performance to degrade    h3.Workaround  Prevent calls to the base end point via networking/proxy rules"""
comments: "Dear Atlassian partner,    For large enterprise, this can cause thread to run for over 12 hours (impacting the DB). "

summary: "Allow changing create from template macro creation link when using excerpt + include macros"
description: """h3. Use Case    If I'd like to host templates under a certain page for better tracking and to help my users to easily create them from just clicking a button anywhere, it should be allowed to change the creation link of the Create from Template macro.    h3. Situation    # I have a Space A and Page A to keep DACI templates  # Under Space A, I have thousand of pages, for my users to find where to create any DACI, currently, they need to navigate to Page A and create the template there.  # This proposal is to use the Excerpt on Page A, inside it have the Create from Template button for the DACI template.  # Now, on any of the thousand pages, I can simply add the Excerpt Include and add Page A with the Excerpt macro. Doing this, I have the create from template button and with just one click my users should be able to create the DACI template under Page A instead of needing to find the respective page.    h3. Current Situation  The create from template button is just a shortcut for a specific template so, it will create under the page you are currently in.    h3. Workaround  It might be possible to do this by [editing the macros|https://developer.<USER>cloud/confluence/modules/macro-editor/] in question."""

summary: "Community Forums: Topic Author Column macro : real username without picture"
description: """At the moment, the author column in """"Community Forums""""  shows either   * User Link (user ID with link to user)   * Plain Text (user ID without link)   * User Link/Picture (real user name with picture)    In our company, the user ID differs from the real user name as it's an anonymous number.  But in a forum, we like to show the real name of the topic author.    This would work with """"User Link/Picture"""" - the real user name is shown - but the picture takes space and extends (doubles !) a topic line's height.    My suggestion is a fourth possibility in the """"Forum - Topic Author Column"""" macro, that shows the real user name in the same way """"User Link/Picture"""" but without the user's picture ."""

summary: "Custom Jira fields with type ""Text Field"" do not appear on Jira Charts macro"
description: """h4. Issue summary   # In Jira, we can create custom fields for issues with different types, like """"List"""", """"Text Field"""", and """"Label"""", each with their own format.  # In Confluence, with the *Jira Chart* macro, when using the *Two Dimensional* chart's *Display Option*, custom *Text Field* from Jira do not appear as selection for the *XAxis* and *YAxis*.  # Other custom fields with type """"List"""" and type """"Label"""" appear fine.  # There is a difference between the text and label however, where for the text custom fields, I could only filter them between null or not null, and could not use the equals sign *=* in my query, but I could do so with labels, querying the exact value like * = 2.4.3* for example.    h4. Expected behavior  # All custom fields should be able to appear in the Jira Charts macro    h4. Current Behavior   # Custom field with type """"Text Field"""" do not appear.    h4. Workaround  # Use type """"Label"""" instead."""

summary: "'Find' option in Editor only finds first instance of text when using new version of Firefox browser"
description: """h3. Summary    'Find' option in Editor only finds first instance of text when using new version of Firefox browser.    h3. Environment   - Firefox Quantum 63.0.3   - Tested in Confluence 6.6.3, Confluence 6.6.9 and Confluence 6.12.1   - Also tested in different OS (Mac OSX Mojave and CentOS 7)    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # In Confluence, create a new page and insert multiple times the same word, for example: 'Content'.   !01_create_page.png|thumbnail!   # In Edit mode, hit ctrl+F, cmmd+F, or click the 'Find/Replace' icon on the top right corner.   !02_find.png|thumbnail!   # In the 'Find' box type the written word 'Content' (or whichever word used) and hit 'enter' or click 'Next'.   !03_type_next.png|thumbnail!   # After finding the first instance of the search, click 'Next' again.   !04_next_again.png|thumbnail!    (!) Notice that nothing changes and it doesn't highlight the next word.    h3. Expected Results    It is expected to see the next instance of the word highlighted in the same way we see the first instance.    Exemplo screenshots taken from Google Chrome:   # Type 'Content' in the 'Find' box and click 'Next'   !01_from_chrome.png|thumbnail!   # Click 'Next' highlight moves to next word   !02_from_chrome.png|thumbnail!   # Click 'Next' highlight moves to next word, so on and so on...   !03_from_chrome.png|thumbnail!    h3. Actual Results    Only the first instance of the word is highlighted even after clicking 'Next'  h3. Workaround   - Use older versions of Firefox, version 62.0.3 works fine   - Use different browsers, tested with Safari and Google Chrome and it works fine."""

summary: "Confluence Supported Platforms states that AdoptOpenJDK is required"
description: """The Confluence Server [Supported Platforms|https://confluence.<USER>doc/supported-platforms-207488198.html] page states that Confluence can only be used with Oracle JRE/JDK or AdoptOpenJDK.  However, AdoptOpenJDK *is* OpenJDK...just a specific packaging of OpenJDK.    Requiring AdoptOpenJDK for the platform-native installers of Confluence Server might make sense from a packaging perspective.  However, for those of us using the """"TAR.GZ Archive"""" distribution and configuring our own installation, why is AdoptOpenJDK required?    Will Confluence Server actually fail with OpenJDK packaged in other ways, e.g. the Fedora distribution package?  If so, what are the impacts and when will they be resolved?  And if not, is this simply a documentation error?    Note that the Jira Server [Supported Platforms|https://confluence.<USER>adminjiraserver/supported-platforms-938846830.html] page states that OpenJDK is supported, and that testing has also occurred with AdoptOpenJDK.  Is this a more accurate phrasing of the Confluence Server requirement?"""

summary: "Plans to end support for Confluence gadgets"
description: """{panel:title=Atlassian status as of September 2019|borderStyle=solid|borderColor=#B2D4FF | titleBGColor=#B2D4FF | bgColor=#EAF1FC}  [Gadgets|https://confluence.<USER>display/DOC/Gadgets] were designed to allow you to display information dynamically from sources like iGoogle or Jira, for example, in Confluence. The first gadgets were introduced in Confluence 3.1, and much of the technology they were based on is now superseded or obsolete. Since then we have also implemented a number of better ways to display dynamic information using macros and other integration points.    We ended support for the following Gadgets in Confluence 7.0, and hid them from the macro browser. Any existing instances of these gadgets will still work, but you won't be able to insert these gadgets into the editor using the macro browser:    * Activity Stream  * Confluence Page Gadget  * Confluence Quick Nav Gadget    The News gadget was removed in Confluence 7.0 as it was not currently functional.     End of support means Atlassian will not fix bugs related to these gadgets in Confluence 7.0 or later versions. We will remove these gadgets entirely in a future Confluence release, and will provide more information at that time.  {panel}    Feel free to comment on this issue if this change will have a significant impact on your Confluence site.     Find out more about our other [planned changes in Confluence 7.0|https://confluence.<USER>display/DOC/Planned+changes+in+Confluence+7.0]"""

summary: "Plans to end support for Usage Stats"
description: """{panel:title=Atlassian status as of March 2019|borderStyle=solid|borderColor=#B2D4FF | titleBGColor=#B2D4FF | bgColor=#EAF1FC}  The [Confluence Usage Stats plugin|https://confluence.<USER>display/DOC/View+Space+Activity] provides basic page view tracking in Confluence in the Space Activity tab. We know that usage tracking is important to you, however the current implementation is disabled by default as it can have a noticeable impact on your site's performance.    We had planned to end support for the Confluence Usage Stats plugin in Confluence 7.0, but we've heard your feedback, and will not end support at this time.   {panel}    Feel free to comment on this issue if this change will have a significant impact on your Confluence site. description: """{panel:title=Atlassian status as of September 2019|borderStyle=solid|borderColor=#B2D4FF | titleBGColor=#B2D4FF | bgColor=#EAF1FC}  [Trackback|https://confluence.<USER>display/DOC/Trackback+and+External+Referrers] enables Confluence to send and receive trackback pings when pages are linked to. [Referrers|https://confluence.<USER>display/DOC/Trackback+and+External+Referrers] appear on the Page Information view of a page, and list clicks from external websites to the page. Trackback is no longer widely used in modern websites, and because it relies on accepting unauthenticated requests to a particular URL, is a spam vector.    We removed the trackback and referrers features completely in Confluence 7.0.  {panel}    Feel free to comment on this issue if this change will have a significant impact on your Confluence site.     Find out more about our other [planned changes in Confluence 7.0|https://confluence.<USER>display/DOC/Planned+changes+in+Confluence+7.0]"""

summary: "Ability to track a space or page email notification and whether it was triggered by auto-watch or manual watch"
description: """h3. Problem Definition  Confluence currently provides the ability to watch a specific page or an entire Space. You can do that either via auto-watch or a manual watch.  At the moment, it is not possible to track whether an email notification was generated through Space or Page watch and whether this was triggered through auto-watch or a manual watch      h3. Suggested Solution  It would be good if we are able to distinguish those scenarios which will allow the Confluence admin to determine where the bulk of the email notifications is coming from."""

summary: "Disable Watch functionality per Space including email notifications of that Space"
description: """h3. Problem Definition  As usage grows, Confluence can end up sending a lot more notifications than desired due to page or space level watches.    Currently, a Confluence/Space Administrator needs to manually remove watchers at the page and space level one-by-one via View Page > Manage Watchers. This can be cumbersome if there are a lot of pages and individual watchers. Additionally, there is no way to turn off notifications for a given space in general.    h3. Suggested Solution  The suggestion is to have the following capabilities in a configuration page:  # As an admin, disable watching an entire Space in bulk (for individual page watches, space watches, or both)  # Turn off email notifications (or, prevent watching) for a specific Space entirely"""

summary: "Improve Atlassian Companion to allow multiple saves"
description: """Currently, The Atlassian Companion only registers the first save operation in a desktop application, after which the file remains open and editable. Further saves are not registered, which can leave to data loss in cases where users are unaware of this flow.    The Companion user experience would benefit greatly from allowing multiple saves or providing a way for the user to signal when the changes are complete and an upload can be made.  """
