summary: "Plans to disable the Hipchat integration plugins"
description: """{panel:title=Atlassian status as of September 2019|borderStyle=solid|borderColor=#B2D4FF | titleBGColor=#B2D4FF | bgColor=#EAF1FC}  Confluence provides a number of [integration points with Hipchat|https://confluence.<USER>display/DOC/Use+Hipchat+and+Confluence+together], including the ability to send page notifications to a Hipchat room, and use Hipchat emoji in Confluence pages. As you may be aware, we have discontinued development on all chat products. Hipchat Cloud services will be shut down in February 2019, and HipChat Data Center and Server will both reach end of life during the next 18 months.    We plan to take a phased approach in Confluence, so that any organizations still using Hipchat Server or Data Center can continue to use this integration.    We ended support for all bundled Hipchat plugins in Confluence 7.0. These are disabled by default for new installations. This will have no impact on existing installations, and can be easily enabled if required.    End of support means that Atlassian will not fix bugs related to Hipchat integration in Confluence 7.0 or later versions.   {panel}    Feel free to comment on this issue if this change will have a significant impact on your Confluence site.     Find out more about our other [planned changes in Confluence 7.0|https://confluence.<USER>display/DOC/Planned+changes+in+Confluence+7.0]    If don't use Hipchat, or have migrated to Slack already, find out about [Disabling the HipChat Integration with the Atlassian products|https://confluence.<USER>display/KB/Disabling+the+HipChat+Integration+with+the+Atlassian+products] to reduce clutter in Confluence."""

summary: "Plans to hide the orphaned pages screen"
description: """{panel:title=Atlassian status as of September 2019|borderStyle=solid|borderColor=#B2D4FF | titleBGColor=#B2D4FF | bgColor=#EAF1FC}  In Confluence 6.15 and earlier you can see a list of all pages that Confluence considers [orphaned pages|https://confluence.<USER>display/DOC/Orphaned+Pages] (not a child of a space homepage, and not linked to by any other page). Since the introduction of the Confluence 5 default theme, the orphaned pages screen has been less useful because it's always possible to see all pages in a space via Space Tools > Reorder pages.    We removed the Orphaned pages screen in the default theme in Confluence 7.0.  {panel}    Feel free to comment on this issue if this change will have a significant impact on your Confluence site.     Find out more about our other [planned changes in Confluence 7.0|https://confluence.<USER>display/DOC/Planned+changes+in+Confluence+7.0]"""
comments: "I understand the motivation to making the UI cleaner, and makes sense for new users. But you are penalizing power-users with years of Confluence experience, who may use this feature infrequently, but then when they do, they have to hunt to figure out what happened to that tab. So much of what I do in Confluence is muscle memory.    So when streamlining your product, put as much effort in to not penalizing your power users as you do in attracting new users, please." [SEP] "I concur with <USER> I haven't needed this feature so far, but it seems like something that might be sorely missed one of these days.    What about a compromise – replacing it with a ""Highlight orphaned pages"" button on the ""Reorder pages"" screen, next to the ""Sort alphabetically"" button?" [SEP] "The justification for removal is a logical fallacy.  In complex and dense spaces, reorder pages on its own has no indication as to which pages are orphans. It would be far more useful for the feature to remain and expanded to show all true

summary: "Plans to hide the attachment storage configuration screen"
description: """{panel:title=Atlassian status as of Septemver 2019|borderStyle=solid|borderColor=#B2D4FF | titleBGColor=#B2D4FF | bgColor=#EAF1FC}  Since Confluence 5.5 the only supported method for storing attachments is in the file system. The [attachment storage configuration|https://confluence.<USER>display/DOC/Attachment+Storage+Configuration] screen previously provided options for customers to change where attachments are stored. This screen has no purpose in any site where attachments are already stored in the file system.    We hid this screen in Confluence 7.0 for all sites that are already storing attachments in the file system.     If you still store attachments in the database, we recommend considering migrating to the file system, as storing attachments in the database can make backing up and restoring your database more difficult.  {panel}    Feel free to comment on this issue if this change will have a significant impact on your Confluence site.     Find out more about our other [planned changes in Confluence 7.0|https://confluence.<USER>display/DOC/Planned+changes+in+Confluence+7.0]"""
comments: "If you plan to remove dedicated screen (which is logical since it can't be changed) information about configured attachments path must be displayed in System Information. It is very helpful for troubleshooting and sometimes engineers supporting installation can be limited with host access thus can't poke in confluence.cfg.xml"

summary: "Edit in App does not work when Confluence is integrated transparent SSO (and 2FA)"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  If Confluence uses Two Factor Authentication, the Companion App cannot edit/save Office files when using the *Edit With* functionality. The error below is thrown when trying to open an Office file with the Companion app:   {quote}  Excel cannot open the file XXXXX.xlsx because the file format or file extension is not valid. Verify that the file has not been corrupted and that the file extension matches the format of the file.   {quote}    h3. Environment  * Confluence 6.11.2  * Third party Two Factor Authentication     h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Install the Atlassian Companion App  # Create a page  # Upload an Excel file (or any Office file) to the page  # Click on the file to show in Preview  # Click *Edit with Excel*    h3. Expected Results  The file opens in Excel and the user is able to edit the file and save it.     h3. Actual Results  The Companion App tries to retrieve the attachment from the Confluence URL, but since the instance is behind 2FA, it cannot authenticate the request.     The Excel file becomes corrupt, and its contents contain tagging from the 2FA application denying the Companion App access to the Trusted URL.        h3. Workaround  For the time being, the only way to work around this error is to disable the Atlassian Companion App. This can be done manually, but it requires downgrading a plugin which may not be compatible with newer versions of Confluence and is not supported. However, this has been tested and it works with up to Confluence 6.12 so far:   * Shutdown Confluence   * Copy the *OfficeConnector-6.0.0.jar* from an older version of Confluence (i.e. 6.10)   * Place it in the *confluence_install/confluence/WEB-INF/atlassian-bundled-plugins/* folder   * From the same folder, move the *OfficeConnector-7.0.3.jar* to another folder as backup   * [Clear the plugin cache|https://confluence.<USER>confkb/how-to-clear-confluence-plugins-cache-297664846.html]   * Restart Confluence    You can then [disable the Atlassian companion app|https://confluence.<USER>doc/administering-the-atlassian-companion-app-958456281.html#AdministeringtheAtlassianCompanionApp-Disablefileediting] functionality, and use 'Edit in office' moving forward.    (!) Note this is only recommended if there is an SSO issue or if there is something in your environment that blocks the use of the companion app. For companion app troubleshooting, please reference this guide: [Administering the Atlassian Companion App|https://confluence.<USER>doc/administering-the-atlassian-companion-app-958456281.html].  """
comments: "Hello, FYI I see people are still reporting this issue in confluence versions where it was reported to have been fixed, see CONFSERVER-56444 :       [~<USER> added a comment - 11/Jan/2019 11:47 AM  Still doesnot work after upgrade to 6.13, content is still the login html." [SEP] "Hi everybody,    I disagree with the impact level. 2FA or even MFA is rapidly becoming a mandatory security measure for enterprises, and if they have to choose between state of the art security or Confluence, guess who wins?" [SEP] "please update affected versions." [SEP] "Hi,    It is happening in version 6.15.2 as well.    I would ask to increase priority because it is a problem related security topics.    Thank you" [SEP] "This issue is happening in Confluence 6.15.6 version as well.    Could you please prioritize this issue for resolution in upcoming versions." summary: "Make Calendar Reminder Text Customizable"
description: """h2. Current Situation    Confluence Team Calendars can send out reminders for events.  For some event types (Birthday, Travel, Leave), the 'Who' field from the event is included with the notification.  For other event types, including custom events, it is not.      For Travel, Birthday, and Leave events, the reminder will say:  *<Username>'s travel*  For any other event, the reminder will just use the 'What' field from the event     !reminders.jpg|thumbnail!     h2. Suggestion    Make the calendar reminders customizable so that users or admins can choose which fields to include in the reminders."""
comments: "I do not understand why Ralted page field that usually points to the web page with more details of the event is not part of that e-mail body so to get there I have to open the full blown calendar, wait until it loads all events form all users (because you can not filter the events by user) and then find the event and find the related web page. Having Related page, Description and the Assignee field of the event as a part of the reminder e-mail would make much more sense."

summary: "Disabled Internal Directory User appears in the Individual Users section of Space Permission without the disabled tag"
description: """h3. Summary  Given that an internal user is:  # Disabled.   # Has been specifically added in the Individual Users section of a space's Space Permission.   This user will still be visible in the list of Individual Users space permissions without any visual indication that it is disabled.     h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Add a user that belongs to the Internal User Directory.  # Ensure the user is enabled.  # Head over to a space and then to Space Permissions.  # Under individual Users, add the above user and setup some permissions for that user.   # Head over to *General Configuration* > *Users* and then disable the user.   # Head over back to the above space permissions.    h3. Expected Results  The user should be shown as disabled or is not shown under the Individual Users.    h3. Actual Results  The user is displayed without any visual indication that it is disabled.      """

summary: "Multibyte characters don't get rendered when conversion sandbox enabled"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Multibyte characters don't get rendered when conversion sandbox enabled even when appropriate font is installed with the following steps.   [https://confluence.<USER>confkb/non-ascii-characters-display-as-square-characters-in-confluence-317948797.html]  h3. Environment   * Confluence Data Center   * document.conversion.sandbox.disable=false (same as default)   * Multibyte font installed on the server   ** """"4 fonts package"""" of Japanese IPA fonts ([https://ipafont.ipa.go.jp/old/ipafont/download.html#en]) installed in   *** /usr/share/fonts   *** /opt/atlassian/confluence/jre/lib/fonts/fallback   ** Following JVM argument is specified in setenv.sh  {code:none}  CATALINA_OPTS=""""-Dconfluence.document.conversion.fontpath=/usr/share/fonts/ -Dconfluence.document.conversion.slides.defaultfontname.regular=IPAGothic -Dconfluence.document.conversion.slides.defaultfontname.asian=IPAGothic -Dconfluence.document.conversion.slides.defaultfontname.symbol=IPAGothic ${CATALINA_OPTS}""""  CATALINA_OPTS=""""-Dconfluence.document.conversion.words.defaultfontname=IPAGothic ${CATALINA_OPTS}""""  {code}    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Install Japanese fonts on the hosting server as described in above.   # Start a Confluence Data Center instance   # Attach [^テスト.pptx] in an editor    h3. Expected Results    """"テストプレゼンテーション"""" is shown in thumbnail and preview.  h3. Actual Results    Square characters are shown for thumbnail and preview.   * Thumbnail   !Screen Shot 2018-11-29 at 18.11.04.png|thumbnail!     * Preview   !Screen Shot 2018-11-29 at 18.11.21.png|thumbnail!    h3. Notes    Characters are rendered correctly for some file type.    As far as I confirmed in 6.12.2,  ||file type||thumbnail||preview||  |pptx|(x)|(x)|  |docx|(/)|(x)|  |xlsx|(/)|(/)|  |txt|(/)|(x)|  h3. Workaround    Disable the conversion sandbox adding following option in setenv.sh. This is not recommended as it will affect the stability of the instance.  {code:java}  CATALINA_OPTS=""""-Ddocument.conversion.sandbox.disable=true ${CATALINA_OPTS}""""  {code}    h3. Solution    Add the following to setenv.sh so that system settings are correctly propagated to the sandbox:  {code:java}  CATALINA_OPTS=""""-Dconversion.sandbox.java.options=-Dconfluence.document.conversion.slides.defaultfontname.regular=IPAGothic,-Dconfluence.document.conversion.slides.defaultfontname.asian=IPAGothic,-Dconfluence.document.conversion.slides.defaultfontname.symbol=IPAGothic,-Dconfluence.document.conversion.words.defaultfontname=IPAGothic  ${CATALINA_OPTS}""""  {code}"""

summary: "Allow content to be dynamically inserted across multiple pages"
description: """h4. Problem statement  Currently, a page can display content from another page using the """"Excerpt Include Macro"""" or """"Page Properties Report Macro"""".     However, this content will be *read-only* in the target page where you insert the """"Excerpt Include Macro"""" or """"Page Properties Report Macro"""" to. The change will be reflected in the target page only when the change is done in the source page (containing """"Excerpt Macro"""" and """"Page Properties Report Macro"""".    A user won't be able to update the content from the target page and have the change reflected in the source page.    h4. Suggestion  Allow the content to be dynamically updated across pages. This helps to centralise data across different pages and ensure that all copies of that data are updated when only one copy is updated."""
comments: "As a more visual representation of what this feature is looking to achieve, please refer to the graphic attached.     [atlassian-dynamic-widget.jpg|https://i.imgur.com/paE2qBp.jpg]"

summary: "Poll or survey capability"
description: """Thanks for looking at my suggestion.    I suggest you add a poll or survey capability to Confluence without needing a plug-in.  Polling is a basic, basic component of a collaboration suite – which is the basic premise of using Confluence – and that _should_ be part of the regular package. summary: "Later version of Tomcat hit into ""Invalid character found in the request target"" when subscribing to RSS feeds"
description: """h3. Summary    Doing certain actions on newer versions of Confluence which contains newer versions of tomcat, will results with {{400 - Bad Request Invalid character found in the request target}} error message  h3. Steps to replicate #1 - Subscribing to RSS feeds   # On any space, go to Space Tools > Content Tools > RSS Feed   ** Alternatively, go to <base_URL>/spaces/listrssfeeds.action?key=<space_key>   # Click on any links: Pages, Blog, Mail, Comments, Attachments, All Content   # Expected Results: Prompts for RSS file    h3. Steps to replicate #2 - Clicking the """"Go to included page"""" option in Excerpt Include macro when page contains square bracket   # Create page A which contains square brackets in the page title for example: """"Page with [square] brackets""""   # Create page B.   # Add Excerpt Include macro and point it to the page A   # Save the Page B, Edit mode for page B   # Click on Excerpt macro and then navigate to """"Go to Included Page"""" button   # Expected Results: Page A """"Page with [square] brackets"""" will be opened    h3. Actual Results    Following error is returned in the UI:    h3. Notes    The Apache Tomcat server, which is a web server used by Confluence, is filtering out requests that contain special characters. That’s because Tomcat is using a different encoding and URI standard than most browsers.    See also Apache [Tomcat 8.5|https://tomcat.apache.org/tomcat-8.5-doc/config/http.html] http docs for more details:  {quote}relaxedPathChars:    The [HTTP/1.1 specification|https://tools.ietf.org/<USER>rfc7230.txt] requires that certain characters are %nn encoded when used in URI paths. Unfortunately, many user agents including all the major browsers are not compliant with this specification and use these characters in unencoded form. To prevent Tomcat rejecting such requests, this attribute may be used to specify the additional characters to allow. If not specified, no additional characters will be allowed. The value may be any combination of the following characters:    . Any other characters present in the value will be ignored.  {quote}  h3. Workaround    Specify tomcat to allow the special characters. For the above issues, we only need to allow the comma """","""" character.   # Go to <confluence_install>/conf, backup, and edit the server.xml file.   # Find all connectors your application is using. Just search for Connector in the file, or look at the example below. You're interested only in connectors whose protocol is set to HTTP and HTTPS (not AJP).   # Add {{relaxedQueryChars=""""[,]""""}} to the connector properties in server.xml. For example:    #* If the above workaround does not work, try specifying the other characters by adding the following in server.xml instead:     # Restart Confluence"""
comments: "I can confirm this issue and the workaround works for me." [SEP] "problem remains with  *Message* Invalid character found in the request target. The valid characters are defined in RFC 7230 and RFC 3986    my server.xml     <Connector port=""8210""    connectionTimeout=""20000""    redirectPort=""8443""   maxThreads=""48""    minSpareThreads=""10""   enableLookups=""false""    acceptCount=""10""    debug=""0""    URIEncoding=""UTF-8""   protocol=""org.apache.coyote.http11.Http11NioProtocol""    relaxedQueryChars=""[,]""   scheme=""https""    secure=""true""    proxyName=""dev""    proxyPort=""443""/>" [SEP] "Same here:    {{Apache Tomcat/9.0.12}}    {{Confluence 6.12.2}}     " [SEP] "Months later.  No change in issue status.  Doesn't work for me either.    And the popup says ""Get updates, inspiration and best practices from the team behind Jira.""  Seriously?  You must be kidding" [SEP] "Also affected:    Apache Tomcat/9.0.12    Confluence 6.14.2    Workaround according to CSP-255199 which I opened yes

summary: "Whisper Plugin performs several Full Table Scans/Joins in the Database"
description: """h2. Issue Description    The Atlassian Notifications plugin is used by Atlassian to send in-app notifications and messages in the format of pop-ups. In big instances (>2K concurrent users), this plugin doesn't scale properly and can be accountable for around 100K queries executed against the database in the period of an hour.   h2. Steps to Replicate    There are no known steps to replicate, as this plugin doesn't have a logic set of steps to be triggered.  h2. Sample Queries    Queries being triggered by the plugin includes but are not necessarily limited to below examples:  h4. Expensive Join  {code:sql}  SELECT message.""""ID"""", message.""""CONTENT"""" FROM """"AO_21F425_MESSAGE_AO"""" message JOIN """"AO_21F425_MESSAGE_MAPPING_AO"""" message_mapping ON message.""""ID"""" = message_mapping.""""MESSAGE_ID""""  {code}  h4. Full Table Scan  {code:sql}  SELECT * FROM ( SELECT """"ID"""", """"CONTENT"""" FROM """"AO_21F425_MESSAGE_AO"""" ) WHERE ROWNUM <= 1  {code}  h2. Example of Plugin Usage    The plugin is used to send messages in-app like the ones below:   !image.png|thumbnail!  h2. Diagnose Steps & Workaround    It is not easy to spot problems caused by this plugin unless you have a good way of creating reports from your database, like [Oracle AWR Reports|https://oracle-base.com/articles/10g/automatic-workload-repository-10g]. Usually, you will see spikes in Database Connections and probably Long Running Threads. If that's a symptom you have along with slowness in the application, look into your query execution history for the queries above. If you find them, you are them affected by this bug.    The workaround by now is to disable the plugin through a Dark Feature. To do so:   # Access <Confluence-Base-URL>/admin/darkfeatures.action   # Add the following Dark Feature to your list:  {code:java}  whisper.disabled  {code}"""
comments: "Upon further investigation, we found that the Whisper plugin queries should not be expensive/degrade application performance as the plugin queries an empty database table. I've updated the description of the ticket to reflect this. "

summary: "Macro translated to the language of the user who last edited the page"
description: """h4. Issue summary  # Issue only happens on Confluence 6.9.3, and not on Confluence 6.12.2.  # When a users edits a page, and uses a macro, the macro will be translated to the language that the user sets in their Profile.  # But if we were to change the user's language in the profile to another language, the macro is not translated to the newly updated language, until the page is edited and saved.  # If another user were to edit the page as well, the macro will be translated to the language of the user who last edited the page.    h4. Steps to replicate  # Change the language of the user in their profile page to German for example.  # Create a page and add a macro.  # The macro will be in German.  # Now change the language of the user in their Profile to another language, like English.  # Edit the page from before, and the macro will still be in German.  # If another user were to open the same page, on another machine, even if their language for their profile is set to English, the macro will still be in German, unless they were to save the page and edit it again.    h4 Current behavior  # Macro translated to the language of the user who last edited the page.    h4. Expected behavior  # Macro should display in the language of the user set in their profile."""
comments: "Marking this issue as resolved in 6.12.2."

summary: "Confluence Unresponsive due to stuck Workbox Notifications threads"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    It was observed when Confluence is configured to link with other applications via Application Link, such as JIRA, if that application becomes unresponsive, Confluence can also gradually become unresponsive as well (Confluence is returning HTTP 503 errors when accessed from the Load Balancer).    (i) The issue continues to occur even after Confluence restart.    In addition, the following stuck threads detections messages are also being thrown in the {{catalina.out}} log:    Looking at the thread dumps taken during the incident window, a vast number of long-running HTTP threads are shown and most of them are stuck waiting on SSL connection.   In specific, these {{RUNNABLE}} threads are for Confluence's [Workbox Notifications|https://confluence.<USER>display/DOC/Workbox+Notifications]:      The issue occurs as each request coming from the Confluence's Workbox notifications is waiting to connect to the linked application (e.g. Jira). This then results in Confluence not having enough threads free in order to service users, leading to performance degradation.  h3. Workaround    Effort should be made to address the linked application's unavailability problem as that is what's triggering this bug.   We may do so by performing the following diagnosis steps:   - Please make sure that the linked application has been configured correctly in the Application Link page.   - Please ensure that Confluence can reach all linked instances (via Application Link) over https.   (i) Check this by using [httpclienttest|https://bitbucket.org/atlassianlabs/httpclienttest/src].   - If the other end (e.g. Jira) is down or no longer in service, remove the application link    (i) After resolving the issue above, you'll need to restart Confluence for the threads to be released and available to users again."""

summary: "The Space sidebar contains a Create Page link even for users without the Add Page permission"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  The Space sidebar contains a _Create page_ link even when the _current user_ doesn't have the _Add page_ permission.  If that user clicks on that link, then a _No Permission_ page is loaded with the wrong warning message.  Even if a mail server is configured, this page won't load the _Request Access_ button.      h3. Environment  Fresh install of Confluence *6.12.0*.  It was confirmed on *6.6.3* as well.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  Create a fresh install of Confluence Server and do the following as the _admin_:  # Add a new user (_user001_) to Confluence and make sure it is added only to the _confluence-users_ group.  # Create a new space named _Space A_.   #* Add the _View_ Space Permission and *remove* any other related to the _confluence-users_ group.    Access Confluence as _user001_:  # Access _Space A_ home page and you will see the _Create page_ link in the side bar.  # Click on the _Create page_ link and the _No Permission_ page is loaded with the wrong warning message.    h3. Expected Results  # If the user doesn't have the _Add page_ permission, the _Create page_ link doesn't appear in the sidebar.  # If the link must be there, then the _No permission_ page should give a meaningful message saying the user doesn't have permission to create pages on that Space and should render the _Request Access_ button if the mail server is enabled.    h3. Actual Results  # The user has access to a _Create page_ link even when the _Add page_ permission is not granted.   !new_img_001.png|thumbnail!   # Access the _createpage.action_ link to a Space on which the user doesn't have the _Add page_ permission shows a confusing message.   #* If the mail server is enabled, there's no _Request Access_ button.   !create_page_img001.png|thumbnail!      h3. Workaround  _None at this moment_"""

summary: "Companion App MSI should automatically startup on next login after install"
description: """h2. Problem Definition    Currently when the Companion App MSI file is deployed by admins wishing to roll out this feature to multiple Windows machines the app still needs to be manually started before it can make the system call to add it to launch at startup. Customers have run into this issue and see that only after running the app manually the registry entry is placed here: {{HKEY_CURRENT_USER\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Run}}    This differs from the normal installer behaviour (.exe) which does launch the Companion App after installing. However, MSIs are usually installed via an admin account, so having the executable launch automatically after installation will not solve the problem (as this will only add the registry entry for the admin user).   h3. Current scenario:    After user runs the App for the first time, the registry key will be added in the following path    h2. Workaround:    The registry key can be added to HKCU (for all users that should have Companion running) during the deployment to ensure that the app is started automatically without having the user to start it manually.    For 32-bit OS:    For 64-bit OS:         As of Companion 0.6.0, a registry key can alternatively be manually added to HKLM (instead of HKCU) with no side effects. This will allow Companion to automatically launch for all users on the machine. The process will be the same as above, but adding the registry key to HKLM:     {{HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\Software\WOW6432Node\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Run}}     """
comments: "In LexisNexis we have a similar issue:     When pushed from Microsoft Configuration Manager (SCCM), Companion App does the install under the SYSTEM account, *not* the logged in user's account. Because of this, the install has no idea of what profile to install under.    These are our requirements to have the Companion app start automatically:    1. Install the companion application under all the C:\Users\Public profile so all users have the installation.  2. As part of the install, have it place the shortcut in the HKLM\Software\Microsoft\CurrentVersion\Run key so that the app will start automatically for all logged in users.  This would solve the problems.     " [SEP] "Note that there's also an issue when you want to update Atlassian Companion via SCCM.    Scenario:   # Atlassian Companion 0.5.3 is running   # Admin pushes Atlassian Companion 0.6.0  => Atlassian Companion 0.5.3 must be stopped before the update can take place   # Atlassian Companion is updated but stopped  => User cannot edit attachments

summary: "Users mentioned in inline tasks who aren't the assignee (i.e. the first mention) aren't indexed as a mention"
description: """Mentions in inline tasks are ignored and not indexed in lucene as mentions. This is a change made in CONFDEV-8546 , which fixes a bug where the assignee of a task is being sent two emails (one for the mention, one for the task assignment). The fix for the bug CONFDEV-8546 made the first mention in the inline task an assignee, and ignored the other mentions in the task (i.e., not indexed). Therefore, searches for a user's mention will not return any pages where the mention exists as an assignee or as a secondary mention in the task.    GDPR removal of a user's mentions in the search index does not pick up those secondary mentions due to the above reason, and therefore the search results will continue to display the username in the excerpt.    *Workaround*:  Edit the page with the secondary mention in tasks, which will force a reindex of the page, and fix the issue."""
comments: "Hi all,    you may have a look on this two GDPR-AddOns. With this AddOns you will be able to be *fully compliant* to the GDPR-Policies.   * JIRA: [https://marketplace.<USER>1218962 6|https://marketplace.<USER>1218962]   * Confluence: [https://marketplace.<USER>1219041 3|https://marketplace.<USER>1219041]    Best Regards   Domenico" [SEP] "Also, if you are the second mention in a task and you aren't mentioned elsewhere on the page, you won't get an email notification about it."

summary: "Rendering of create button in Confluence header bar is very slow due to permissions checks"
description: """h3. Problem Definition  In code, deciding if the _Create_ button should be rendered in the header bar (a.k.a. top bar) is an expensive operation due to permissions validation.    When the user access one of the following dashboards, Confluence uses an expensive operation to decide whether or not the _Create_ button should be rendered in the header bar:   * All updates ({{<Confluence Base URL>/#all-updates}}).   * Popular ({{<Confluence Base URL>/#popular}}).   * Recently worked on ({{<Confluence Base URL>/#recently-worked}}).   * Recently visited ({{<Confluence Base URL>/#recently-viewed}}).   * Saved for later ({{<Confluence Base URL>/#starred}}).    The above mentioned _Create_ button is highlighted in the image below, from the user perspective:   !create_button_dashboard_img001.png|thumbnail!     On the background, Confluence will check if the current user has permission to create a page or a blog post on any Space.  These are assigned by _Add page_ and _Add blog_ [Space Permissions|https://confluence.<USER>doc/space-permissions-overview-139521.html].  If more than one Space is retrieved from the above operation, then Confluence will use only one of them as a reference, which is sorted by the _Space Name_ and the _Space Key_.  No previous usage of the selected space is taken into account, meaning that it won't check for Spaces users visited before or created/edited content.      h3. Suggested Solution  Confluence could render the _Create_ button on any dashboard page, whether the user has create page permission to any Space or not.  The permission validation should be made after the user has clicked on the button.  If the user doesn't have enough permissions to create a page in any Space, then a graceful message should be displayed.    h3. Performance Impact on Scale  Looking at a single operation, the permission validation may appear as a _simple_ task.  However, when we think of an enterprise deployment of Confluence, with thousands of users accessing Confluence on a single day, then deciding when rendering the _Create_ button becomes an expensive operation.    On an enterprise instance, Confluence would look through over 250k rows in the database to retrieve a single one to complete this permission validation.    h3. Workaround  _None at this moment_"""
comments: "Part of the problem will be fixed in https://jira.<USER>browse/CONFSERVER-58065: optimizing permission queries for MySQL" [SEP] "A possible mitigation for anyone experiencing this is to bump the size of the StandardQueryCache.  Some of the queries involved in searching for permitted space are cached in just in the query cache, which could easily be thrashing when the system is under load."

summary: "Limit which groups users can share content with"
description: """h3. Problem Definition    AD groups inherited through a user directory connection such as the """"Everyone"""" group will have all users as members. It is desired to block a group such as this from being used to share content with, i.e. the group does not show up as a sharing recipient and therefore cannot be used to spam all users.   h3. Suggested Solution    Create the ability to blacklist groups which will suppress their ability to be used for sharing purposes, but will leave them available for user security/permissions purposes. The could also be done via group attribute that is either """"can be used in sharing"""" = on or off  h3. Workaround    An LDAP filter can be put in place to prevent the Everyone group from being inherent through the user directory connection, but this takes away the groups usefulness as a security/permissions group in Confluence."""

summary: "Send a custom 404 page when the user agent is an e-mail client"
description: """h3. Problem Definition  Confluence will retrieve a full 404 page when a restricted content is requested from a mail client, which is an inefficient operation since the content retrieved will not be rendered by the client.    The problem is detailed below:  Users may add Confluence content (such as images) into an e-mail.  This content will be fetched by the e-mail client, such as MS Outlook, and Confluence will retrieve it.  One can simulate the e-mail client request by running a {{GET}} operation with curl similar to the one below:      If the user that received the e-mail doesn't have access to this content, Confluence will retrieve a full 404 page, on the same way as accessing it from the browser.  See below an example of the response:   !request_curl_img001.png|thumbnail!     This isn't efficient, since Confluence will go through the whole process to build all components of the 404 page, which will not be used by the client.  As an example, Confluence will build the top bar, which requires permissions validation to determine which components will be rendered to the user.  These permissions validation are costly operations to Confluence and could be avoided in the above scenario.    h3. Suggested Solution  If the user agent of the current request is MS Outlook ([contains the string {{Microsoft Outlook}}|https://udger.com/resources/ua-list/browser-detail?browser=Outlook%202016]), then Confluence should send only a 404 status without retrieving any page content.  The same could be applied when the user agent is any other email client.    h3. Performance Impact on Scale  Fixing this bug would help Confluence to be more efficient, retrieving a full 404 page only when needed -- i.e. user accessing it from a browser.  On scale, an e-mail with a restricted image can be sent to several recipients that don't have access to it. Serving a full 404 page to thousands of users is costly to Confluence and to the Database.  In depth:   * Building the top bar requires Confluence to check for permissions, which is a costly operation.   * Running thousands of queries in the database may consume too much of RAM, provided that these queries will use a {{join}} operation.    Depending on the size of your Confluence instance, this could create a situation on which around 10,000 of GET requests would be sent to Confluence from e-mail clients.    h3. Workaround  If you are running Confluence behind a proxy, you could create a filter to drop requests from an e-mail client when a burst is detected.  The configuration below is based on [Nginx Rate Limiting|https://www.nginx.com/blog/rate-limiting-nginx/] to drop requests for images from an MS Outlook client after a burst.  You may need to change the threshold according to your environment. Also, other proxy technologies may offer similar capabilities.  {code:title=Nginx Rate Limiting example}  ### filter for requests coming from an Outlook client  map $http_user_agent $bad_outlook {      default """""""";      ~*(Outlook) 1;  }  limit_req_zone $bad_outlook zone=outlook:10m rate=10r/m;  limit_req_status 429;  server {      ### your standard Confluence configuration          ### use Outlook filter on thumbnails      location ^~ /c663/download/thumbnails {          limit_req zone=outlook;          proxy_pass http://localhost:6663/c663/download/thumbnails;      }      ### use Outlook filter on attachments      location ^~ /c663/download/attachments {          limit_req zone=outlook;          proxy_pass http://localhost:6663/c663/download/attachments;      }  }  {code}"""

summary: "Allow Content Report Table Macro to be able to exclude space in result"
description: """h3. Problem Definition  Allow Content Report Table Macro to be able to exclude space in result    Currently, the [Content Report Table Macro|https://confluence.<USER>doc/content-report-table-macro-317194676.html] is able specific one or multiple spaces to display the matching results, but not able to *exclude* a space.    In order to exclude a space, a tedious workaround wound be to include _every other spaces_ into the filter list except for one that is intended to exclude. It would be good if the macro can configured to exclude specific space(s)    h3. Suggested Solution  Implement the Space filter similar to [Recently Updated Macro|https://confluence.<USER>doc/recently-updated-macro-139519.html] which is able to exclude content from a specific space using the minus sign  """

summary: "Editor scrolls back to the top when editing instead of maintaining scroll position from when viewing page"
description: """h5. Issue Summary    In older versions of Confluence (tested on 6.0.1), when we click on *Edit* in a page, the editor loads in the same position the page was when it was being viewed.   In newer versions (tested on 6.11 and 6.12.2). the editor loads from the top of the page, instead of wherever the vertical scroll was when viewing the page.    Issue persist on latest version of Chrome, IE, and Firefox.  h5. Steps to reproduce   # Create a new page in Confluence, and add [a large amount of text|https://loremipsum.io/generator/?n=100&t=p].   # Save the page.   # Scroll down, to the middle or to the bottom of the page, and scroll up a little to get the *Edit* option, or use the *e* shortcut to nring up the editor.   # Notice that the editor will load the page from the top.   # In Confluence 6.0.1, the editor will load from where the page was last.    h5. Expected behavior   # Editor should load in the same positon of the page when it was being viewed.    h5. Current behavior   # Editor loads from the top of the page, even if the page was lower before *Edit* was clicked."""

summary: "Add missing indexing languages to Confluence"
description: """In Confluence 5.10, and 6.2, new languages were added to Confluence. However, indexing support for those languages were not added and so instances generating content in those languages would not be able to index and search them properly. We should add support for indexing these languages:   * Danish   * Spanish   * Italian   * Hungarian   * Dutch   * Norwegian   * Romanian   * Finish   * Swedish    If I have time remaining I'm going to look at strategies to support multiple languages in the same instance.    Note: Icelandic, Slovenian and Estonian are out of scope as there are no official analyzers from the Lucene project.  Polish is out of scope because parts of the Stempel analyzer from Lucene uses a non-standard BSD based license which our legal team is still evaluating.  h2. Workaround    Change the """"Indexing Language"""" of the site to """"Other"""" in """"General configuration"""" administration settings. This setting should work reasonably well, but not perfectly, for most languages."""

summary: "Allow network redirection in iOS mobile app"
description: """h3. Problem Definition    Confluence Mobile App(*iOS) does not work with network redirection. The APP will try to connect directly to the address added so in case a user inserts the hostname expecting to be redirected(HTTP 302), the APP will say that the version of Confluence isn't compatible.    For instance:    * A proxy in front of Confluence redirecting from https://hostname.example.com to https://hostname.example.com/confluence(Base URL).    The mobile view will redirect the URL properly, but the iOS app will try to connect directly to https://hostname.example.com, while the correct Base URL uses a context path at the end(/confluence).    h3. Suggested Solution    Improve the iOS mobile app to work with redirections, like regular browsers, or update our documentation saying that in case it exists a network redirection, use directly Confluence's Base URL.    """

Summary  When a weekly repeated event is created in Team Calendar, editing a single event affects nearby events as well, in some cases moving the event to the previous day, or if an event is moved to the next day, the event that's on the next day gets shifted as well.  h4. Steps to reproduce   # Create a repeated event like the example below.   ** !1_create_event.PNG|thumbnail!   # The event should be look like something below.   ** !2_event_created.PNG|thumbnail!   # Now if we were to edit one of the event, by extending it like below.   ** !3_event_Extended.PNG|thumbnail!   # Refreshing the page will show that the event from the next day is moved to the edited day.   ** !4_next_day_event_moved.PNG|thumbnail!   # If we were to instead move the event to the next day like below   ** !5_event_moved_to_next_day.PNG|thumbnail!   # Refreshing the page will cause the event to move like this.   ** !6_event_moved_down.PNG|thumbnail!   # There can be more variations of this issue if we were to move the events around more for example, once the events are as they are in Step 6 above, moving the event from the second day back up and refreshing the page will bring the event back down to how it was in the screenshot again, unless we were to move the event from the next day over, and move the event from the second day to the first day instead, and only then can it be returned to how it was in the beginning, like the screenshot in Step 2.     h4. summary: "JPG Image Caching Not Working for IE, Edge and Firefox - ETag is missing from resource"
description: """h2. Problem Statement  Image caching is crucial for reducing load times to improving overall user experience. However, JPG caching is not functioning well with JPG images on certain browsers i.e. IE, Edge and Firefox. Looking closer into the header attributes, we see that the ETag attribute is not being added to the resource during reload. Hence, the JPG images have to be requested to the server on every page reload.    h2. Expected Behavior  All image files should be cached and retrieved from the cache resource instead of having to requesting from the server directly every page reload.    h2. Actual Behavior  JPG files are requested from the server on every page reload."""

summary: "Page Properties Report needs CQL operator support for Including Text"
description: """The Page Properties Report currently supports only the following:  |*Including text***|Include items that contain this text.|CONTAINS (single word or phrase)|    ** You can add these filters in CQL-powered macros but in search they're part of the standard search filters, so they don't appear in the *Add a filter* menu.    Ideally you would want to apply CQL to the text to exclude certain words and not just include.  |*Including text*|Include or exclude items that contain this text.|CONTAINS (single word or phrase), NOT(single word or phrase), OR(single word or phrase)|"""

summary: "duplicate item in dashboard all-updates after deleting a user"
description: """h3. Summary    After deleting a user, it's observed that mentions contained in comments in the #all-updates view still exist with the original user name intact and there is an additional comment created that contains a key instead of the anonymised username:     h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Comment on a page and mention a user  # Delete that user  # Comment in Dashboard > All updates view contains user name and duplicate comment     !comment_mentions.png|thumbnail!     h3. Context  This bug only appears when the content in #all-updates belongs to the user being deleted. All-updates relies on change documents in lucene. As part of reindexing content with user mentions, we also reindex change documents, which involves first looking up the original change document for a given content object, deleting it, and then adding a new change document for the same content object. Unfortunately, looking up the original change document relies on the *username*. By the time, we try and look up the original change document, the user has already been renamed, so we don't find the original, but we do add a new one, resulting in duplication.    See {{com.atlassian.confluence.search.lucene.tasks.AddChangeDocumentIndexTask#perform}}    h3. Workaround  Perform a site reindex to remove the duplicates. Or, create more than 40 new changes, which will push the duplicate entry out of the all-updates tab."""

summary: "Clarify database setup for SQL Server permissions documentation"
description: """h3. Problem Definition  In our [Database Setup for SQL Server|https://confluence.<USER>doc/database-setup-for-sql-server-9258.html] documentation, step 2 item 5 is a bit unclear:    Give this user full create, read and write permissions for the database tables. Confluence must be able to create its own schema. Refer to the SQL Server documentation for how to do this.     For many users this is done by adding the user to the *db_owner* role.    A lot of users may not be aware of this, and thus will run into permissions issues. I've personally seen this in many support tickets and on Community.    h3. Suggested Solution  Clarify in the documentation that in many cases it can be accomplished by adding the user to the *db_owner* role"""

Findings  From the HAR file analysis, it is found that the photos that are being rotated have the following additional header:   !screenshot-1.png|thumbnail!     h4. Workaround  * Remove the individual photo and add it back  * Upload 3 photos or below at a time"""

summary: "Options to clean up EXTRNLNKS table"
description: """h2. Summary  The EXTRNLNKS table is responsible for tracking external pages which link to inbound Confluence pages (Referrers). This feature is enabled by default and is documented in https://confluence.<USER>doc/trackback-and-external-referrers-147487.html    However, this table grows unbounded, and the only time rows are removed is when the associated Confluence page has been deleted/purged. This can be especially problematic in larger Confluence environments over time, causing a large database footprint. In one observed environment, this table contained 25 million rows.    It would be good to be able to set a limit on this table whether by number of records or by date. The table contains """"lastmoddate"""" column; perhaps this can be used to set a limit on retaining only records within a certain timeframe."""
comments: "This bug won't be fixed because referrers and trackbacks are going to be removed in Confluence 7.0: https://jira.<USER>browse/CONFSERVER-57611"

summary: "Import space with pages that contains JIRA issue does not auto trigger Rest API to create the mentioned link in JIRA."
description: """h3. Description  When importing a space with pages that contain JIRA issue, Confluence will not trigger the Rest API to create the mentioned link in JIRA.  Example: Space X with Key X should have Key Y. Space X has a page with a linked jira issue JIRA-1. The issue itself has a link with """"mentioned in"""" and links to confluence page in space X.  When user export Space X and rename it and it's key to Y, now the space Y also has a page with the same linked Jira issue JIRA-1. But the jira issue """"mentioned in"""" still links to space X.  There is no link created to space Y.    *Steps to reproduce*  # Create a JIRA ticket name {{Bug-1}} in JIRA.  # Create a new space name {{ABC}} in Confluence  # Create a new page name {{Page 1}} in space {{ABC}}  # Add the {{Bug-1}} JIRA ticket to {{Page 1}}  (i) You will see that Page 1 contains JIRA link beside the page title. In {{Bug-1}}, we can see a {{mentioned in Page 1}} link is created.  # Export the Space {{ABC}}  # Rename the Space {{ABC}} to {{XYZ}} according to [How to copy or rename a space in Confluence|https://confluence.<USER>confkb/how-to-copy-or-rename-a-space-in-confluence-169578.html] documentation  # Import the Space after changed the spacekey to {{XYZ}}  # We can see that the {{Page 1}} in Space {{XYZ}} contians JIRA issue link {{Bug-1}} but there is no {{mentioned in}} link to Space {{XYZ}} create in the {{Bug-1}} ticket    *Expected behavior*  A new {{mentioned in}} link is created in JIRA ticket    *Actual Result*  No {{mentioned in}} link is created in the JIRA ticket after imported the space that contains the link to the JIRA."""

summary: "Don't Close Sidebar while in edit mode"
description: """I found this suggestion, but it was closed after only 2 months this year.  I would have voted for it.  Please leave open for longer to see if there is more interest.  I have seen some items open for several years.  Great Product, Thanks    When changing to edit mode the side bar gets hidden. From my experience this is counterproductive:   * If I want to change at a specific position of a larger page, I lose track of that position while change from view to edit mode – because of the changed width (with/without the side bar) the paragraphs become reformated. At least the text position last shown in the view will be scrolled to automatically in edit mode. But due to the heavy reordering in the paragraphs it's hard to relocate the position which shall be changed…   * Sometimes while editing I need to look back in some other pages, which's links normally would be shown in the side bar. If the side bar is missing, I have to open a new Confluence tab, go there to the page I am editing, to have the same side bar content, and only then I am able to click the relevant link. If it was still there while editing, a click with the middle mouse button had opened it directly in a new tab.     So, please provide an option to keep side bars enabled even while edit mode is active. """
comments: "Duplicate, please comment on the closed ticket with additional information to help us in considering if it should be re-opened. "

summary: "Inline comments can't be added on words that use <b> bold tags"
description: """h4. Issue summary    Confluence by default uses {{<strong>}} to wrap around bold words in a page. However, if the content of a page uses {{<b>}} instead, users can't add inline comments on those words.    This affects pages that are created *using the Confluence Mobile App* because it uses the {{<b>}} tag.  h4. Steps to replicate   # Download the Confluence Mobile App (reproduced using *v0.1.40* on Android and *v1.1.0.110* on iOS).   # Connect it to Confluence.   # Create a page on the mobile app and add some bold text to the page.   # On a web browser, try to add an inline comment on the bolded text.    (i) It can also be reproduced if you directly add the problematic storage format to the database in the {{bodycontent}} table.  h4. Expected results    The inline comment should be successfully created.  h4. Actual results    The inline comment can't be created due to the error below.  {quote}We can't add your inline comment; the section you highlighted may contain a macro or user mention. Try highlighting plain text.  {quote}  !image.png|thumbnail!  h4. Workaround    Edit the page on the web browser without making any changes and save it. Confluence will automatically switch the {{<b>}} tags to {{<strong>}}.    """

summary: "Duplicates in confancestors table appear if editor.ajax.save.disable dark feature is enabled"
description: """h1. Background    Duplicates in confancestors appear if collaborative editing is enabled, and dark feature editor.ajax.save.disable is enabled as well.  h1. How to reproduce   # Enable collaborative editing   # Enable editor.ajax.save.disable dark-feature   # Create a new page and save it   # Check that duplicates appeared in confancestor table    h1.  Technical details    When editor.ajax.save.disable is enabled and user saves a page, docreatepage.action is called. Method AbstractCreatePageAction.doAdd calls assignParentPage(draft, getParentPage()) method which adds a child, and adds ancestors to the child.    But the child already had ancestors before, so assignParentPage adds duplicates.    The method """"assignParentPage"""" is not called if collaborative editing is disabled.    To check if the dark-feature is enabled, go to <CONFLUENCE_URL>/admin/darkfeatures.action"""

summary: "Duplicate atlassian-synchrony.log files are created when running in Data Center mode"
description: """h3. Summary    Two *atlassian-synchrony.log* files are created and populated when running in DC and Synchrony is in self-managed mode.    h3. Environment    * Confluence 6.12.0 or up in DC mode  * Synchrony in self-managed mode (started and administrated by Confluence)    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Start the DC node  # Check the following directories:  #* *<Confluence-Home>/logs/*  #* *<Confluence-Home>/*    h3. Expected Results    The *atlassian-synchrony.log* file can only be found under *<Confluence-Home>/logs/*.    h3. Actual Results    We can see two *atlassian-synchrony.log*, one in *<Confluence-Home>/logs/* and the other under *<Confluence-Home>/*.     h3. Notes    Both files log the same information, so it is just a problem of redundancy. The only difference is the formatting:    # *<Confluence-Home>/logs/atlassian-synchrony.log* uses log4j formatting  # *<Confluence-Home>/atlassian-synchrony.log* uses JSON formatting    By inspecting the Synchrony source code, we notice that the second log file above is created here:         Based on this snippet, it seems that the flag *external-log4jconfig-provided* is not getting set to true, even though we have log4j settings for Synchrony inside *<Confluence-Install>/confluence/WEB-INF/classes/log4j.properties*. This ends up triggering the extra log appender.    h3.Workaround    # Edit the file  *<Confluence-Home>/synchrony-args.properties*  # Add the following line to this file:    (!) somefile is just a dummy name, the file does not need to actually exist on the file system.  # Go to *Cog Icon > General Configuration > Collaborative Editing* and *Restart Synchrony*    Once those steps are executed, the file *<Confluence-Home>/atlassian-synchrony.log* will no longer be populated and can be safely deleted. The only Synchrony log that will be used after that is *<Confluence-Home>/logs/atlassian-synchrony.log*."""

summary: "Editing a page with Collaborative Editing enabled might show an older version of that page"
description: """{panel:title=Issue Partially Fixed}  The root cause of this issue has been addressed in the following versions of Confluence:   * Enterprise release 6.6.x stream: Confluence 6.6.12+   * Enterprise release 6.13.x stream: Confluence 6.13.1+   * Regular release stream: Confluence 6.14.0+    Please do note, however, that this is a data corruption issue. If you are on one of the releases mentioned above, new data corruption will not occur, but existing stale drafts that have had their modification date wrongly updated will continue to show. We are currently working on an upgrade task to fix this. Please see CONFSERVER-58285. In the mean time, the workaround  mentioned below can be applied on a page by page basis.  {panel}  h4. Issue Summary    In some cases, After clicking on the _Edit_ button of a page, the user has the impression of editing an old version of it.   Checking the content of the current published page and the content that appears while editing it, they are different, but seems to match exactly what it was in one of the previous versions.    For example, this is the content of the latest published version of a page while on the _view mode_:   !current-page.png|thumbnail!    When clicking on the _Edit_ button, this is the content you get while on the _editing mode_:   !editing-page.png|thumbnail!    This only occurs when Collaborative Editing (CE) is enabled.   If you *disable* CE, and edit the same page, then you get the current content in the _edit mode_, such as the image below:   !editing-page-full.png|thumbnail!    This only affects some pages on a very specific situation (which is described in the next session), meaning that you don't see this behavior on all pages of the Confluence instance.    This issue was confirmed in versions *6.6.3* and *6.12.1*, meaning that it may affect any version with Collaborative Editing before and including *6.12.1*.  h4. How to Reproduce the Issue   # Have a Confluence instance configured with CE enabled.   # Create a blank space.   ** Space Name: _Space A_.   ** Space Key: _SPACEA_.   # Create another blank space.   ** Space Name: _Space 1_.   ** Space Key: _SPACE1_.   # In _Space 1_, create a blank page with title _Page 1_.   !page1_img001.png|thumbnail!   # In _Space A_, create a page named _Page A_.   ** Add any content you like, but make sure to include a [link|https://confluence.<USER>doc/links-776656293.html] to _Page 1_.   !pageA_img001.png|thumbnail!   # Start editing _Page A_, but don't publish the contents, save it for later.   !pageA_img002.png|thumbnail!   # For now we have the following scenario:   ** Collaborative Editing enabled.   ** _Space 1_ with page _Page 1_.   ** _Space A_ with page _Page A_ with a link to _Page 1_ as part of its contents.   ** A draft of _Page A_ with _unpublished changes_.   ** If we check the {{CONTENT}} table for _Page A_, there are 2 records, one for the page and the other related to its draft.   !pageA_img003.png|thumbnail!   # [Turn CE *off*|https://confluence.<USER>doc/administering-collaborative-editing-858772086.html#AdministeringCollaborativeEditing-Changetheeditingmode] using the Confluence UI.   # Start editing _Page A_ again and publish the modifications.   ** Since CE is off, it will not use the modifications that were in the shared draft.   !pageA_img004.png|thumbnail!   # If we check the {{CONTENT}} table for _Page A_, now we have 3 records.   !pageA_img005.png|thumbnail!   # Move _Page 1_ from _Space 1_ to any other Space, such as _Space A_.   !page1_img002.png|thumbnail!   # If you check the storage format of _Page A_, the source related to the link to _Page 1_ has changed.   !pageA_img006.png|thumbnail!   # If you check the {{lastmoddate}} value of _Page A_'s draft, it was changed.   ** Note that the {{lastmoddate}} value of the current version hasn't changed.   !pageA_img007.png|thumbnail!   # [Turn CE *on*|https://confluence.<USER>doc/administering-collaborative-editing-858772086.html#AdministeringCollaborativeEditing-Changetheeditingmode] using the Confluence UI.   # Start to edit _Page A_ and it will use the shared draft of the old version, prior to disabling CE.   !pageA_img008.png|thumbnail!    h4. Expected Behavior   # When editing a page with CE enabled, Confluence gives you a draft based on the last version of the page.   # Move page actions doesn't update the shared draft if the {{lastmoddate}} of the shared draft is older than the published page.   ** No matter if CE is off or on, Confluence should compare the timestamp of the current page version and the draft.    h4. Workaround    If you identify a page is using the wrong shared draft, you can discard the draft as follows:   # Click on the _Edit_ button of the page.   # Add any modification to the page and click on the _Close_ button at the bottom right of the editor.   # In the _Close the editor_ pop-up, select _Discard all changes_.   ** (!) If you discard changes, your unpublished changes will be deleted. You can't undo this action."""

summary: "Recently worked on dashboard tab can be slow on SQL Server 2014"
description: """h2. Steps to reproduce   * Set up Confluence 6.6.7 with a SQL Server 2014 v12.0.5589 database (may affect other versions of SQL Server as well)   * Populate the database with data with dimensions similar to this:   ** Over 2 million rows in the content table   ** At least one user with ~10,000 documents or versions of documents modified   * Visit the recently worked on tab in the dashboard    h2. Expected results    Dashboard tab loads in a timely manner.  h2. Actual results    Dashboard tab never loads. The database CPU spikes for a long time and the query takes about 30 minutes to return. Due to the database being so busy this can cause stability issues causing the rest of Confluence to stop working as well.  h2. Workaround    The best workaround at the moment is to disable the feature. summary: "Roadmap planner overlaps other content in PDF export"
description: """*Steps to reproduce:*    1. Create a page with a multiple column layout   2. Add a roadmap planner macro in the left column, and some other content in the right column.   3. Export the page to PDF.     *Expected result*    Roadmap planner displays correctly, beside the content in the next column.     *Actual result*    Content in the right hand column, overlaps the roadmap planner macro in the left column     *Workaround*  Place roadmap planner macro in its own, full width section (no columns).       """

summary: "Allow search feature to ignore accent for German and Czech"
description: """We have an issue here with confluence that prevent us to adopt it fully in our company .    In the French language, we use accents, such as é,à,é,<USER>. and we need Confluence to ignore those accents for searchs, like google does.    For instance the search text: """"majore"""", should give us pages with """"majoré"""" or """"majore"""" or """"majorè""""    It would be very useful if we could configure the search feature to be able to ignore accents.    Languages that already ignore accents for searches as of Confluence 6.10 are:   * English   * French   * Spanish   * Portuguese    However, German and Czech do not currently support ignoring accents due to limitations in Confluence's version of Lucene."""
comments: "Add support for Slovak to the list, please.    Slovak language is very similar to Czech in regard of used accents and pronunciation." [SEP] "Please add Slovak language as well. PS: If you don't know how, ask Microsoft, they have it since 2000 ;-)" [SEP] "Please, for Danish language do NOT allow danish letters æ,ø,å / Æ,Ø,Å to be treated as accents (as it does now in 6.15). It messes up our search results." [SEP] "Hi, I am voting for CZ language." summary: "Consistency check for Index health"
description: """h4. Problem Statment  There is no background job that runs to check the health of Index on Confluence. Several customers have faced corrupt Index issue many times.     h4. Suggestion  It would be beneficial to write a job that would run as a background process and perform a consistency check on the index on a periodic basis to determine the index health. If the index was deemed unhealthy it could alert the admin that a reindex was needed or attempt to correct the index.    """
comments: "I would also suggest to build an easy button to reindex datacenter without console access. "

summary: "Allow restriction of user results from showing up in quick search and rest API searches for certain users or groups"
description: """h3. Problem Definition  Even after [restricting a user's ability to use the people directory or perform a quick search|https://confluence.<USER>confkb/how-to-restrict-users-from-accessing-people-directory-or-search-users-from-quick-search-688883545.html], a user can still use the REST API to locate another user's information:      h3. Suggested Solution  We cannot restrict the API entirely as Confluence uses the same REST API for it's processes as noted in [CONFSERVER\-7913: Need ability to limit use of remote API to certain users, or a certain group|https://jira.<USER>browse/CONFSERVER-7913]. However, it would be useful to be able to restrict certain users or groups from receiving user results.    We have the [CONFSERVER\-7837: People Directory and """"controlled"""" privacy / configure access to People Directory depending on Group membership|https://jira.<USER>browse/CONFSERVER-7837] feature request, however, this feature doesn't include results that can be pulled from the REST API."""

summary: "Create Jira Issue from Team Calendar's event"
description: """h3. Problem Definition      Currently, there isn't an option of creating Jira Issues directly from Team Calendar's interface, based on an event of it.    h3. Suggested Solution      Add the functionality of creating a Jira Issue based on a Team Calendar event.    h3. Why this is important       This is important to make a better integration between Confluence and Jira since this would make it easier to create a Jira Issue based on an already existing event, instead of creating an event in Confluence for this issue only after it is created in Jira."""

summary: "Page numbering does not work for sandboxed PDF space exports"
description: """Trying to apply the page numbering customisations mentioned in the [KB article|https://confluence.<USER>doc/customize-exports-to-pdf-190480945.html#CustomizeExportstoPDF-PageNumbering] will not work for DC instances that use the external process pool (sandbox) to export PDFs.    Specifically:      Server customers and DC customers who have it disabled are not affected.  h2. How to Reproduce    1) Apply any of the above recommendations to add page numbering to PDF exports  2) Export a space as PDF  h2. Workarounds      None at the moment. """
comments: "A fix for this issue is available to Server and Data Center customers in Confluence 6.14.0  [Upgrade|https://www.<USER>software/confluence/download?utm_source=confluence_bugfix&utm_campaign=confluence_bugfix&utm_medium=email] now or check out the [Release Notes|https://confluence.<USER>doc/confluence-6-14-release-notes-963655609.html] to see what other issues are resolved."

summary: "Disable update check for Companion App MSI"
description: """The Page [Administering the Atlassian Companion App|https://confluence.<USER>doc/administering-the-atlassian-companion-app-958456281.html] states that by using the MSI, the app won't automatically get the updates.  {quote}If you deploy using the Microsoft Installer, the Companion app won’t automatically get the latest updates, including security and bug fixes, so some maintenance is required.  {quote}  While this is true, the logs are flooded with update checks on every few minutes      Please remove the update check for the MSI version of the App."""
