comments: "This is caused by a combination of bad luck and unclear UI.  h3. Bad luck    There are 4 possible combinations of config to link Confluence and Jira: 2 directions (Confluence -> Jira and vice versa) x 2 auth modes (OAuth and OAuth with impersonation). This issue happens only if users choose this specific config:   * Confluence -> Jira: OAuth with impersonation.   * Jira -> Confluene: OAuth.    h3. Unclear UI    While the Manage Application Links UI gives an impression that users can choose whatever mode of authentication for whatever direction they want, [our public document|https://confluence.<USER>applinks/oauth-security-for-application-links-774045732.html] clearly states that _*Make sure that that the authentication matches for the local and remote ends of both the incoming and outgoing directions.*_ We should've done better to not allow customers to choose one mode for a direction but another for the other way around."

summary: "Image URL has been truncated when saving the page "
description: """h3. Step to reproduce the issue   # Create a new page   # Use the Image insert button on the menu.  # Copy / parse the image URL in the example text file. https://jira.<USER>secure/attachment/329901/image_url.txt  # Insert the URL  # Save the page     h3. Expected behavior   The image should render as the same right after insert on the page.     h3. Actual behavior   It is showing a broken image macro."""

summary: "View employee face in calendar timeline view"
description: """We use the team calendar to plan our support duty which is a scheduled with rolling on-duty (two people are on duty at all times).    So we create events in the team calendar to reflect this. Here I would really like the timeline to show the avatar of all the invited people for the events. This would the timeline view a lot more helpful for quickly getting an overview of who might be busy for the near future.     """

summary: "Regex for TOC macro work differently between in preview and in page itself"
description: """h3. Summary    Regex for TOC macro works differently between in the preview and in the page itself.    h3. Steps to Reproduce    # Open a page in edit mode  # Insert headings which have the names below  #* ABC  #* BCD  # Insert [Table of Contents Macro|https://confluence.<USER>doc/table-of-contents-macro-182682099.html] in the page  # Open a configuration dialog of the macro  # Set _Include Headings_ option with using a regex {{B.+}}  # Hit _Preview_ button  # Hip _Save_ button    h3. Expected Results    The preview and the page itself shows same headings with the macro.    h3. Actual Results    The preview and the page itself shows different results. These behaviors are quite unintuitive for users.    The preview shows only the heading """"BCD"""".    !screenshot-1.png|thumbnail!     The page itself shows both """"ABC"""" and """"BCD"""".    !screenshot-2.png|thumbnail!      h3. Notes    From my investigation, the regex of _Include Headings_ works as follows.    h4. In preview:  Confluence uses the regex to match *substring* of each heading    h4. In page itself:  Confluence uses the regex to match *head* of each heading    It means, if we would like to match only """"BCD"""" in the page itself, we need to use {{^B.+}} or {{^B}} instead.    h3. Workaround    Please try to use regex to match *head* of each heading."""

summary: "Jira display options duplicate when filtering on the same issue"
description: """h3. Summary  If a Jira issues macro are on the same page use the same JQL query, the display options may duplicate even though one of the macros has been configured to display unique options than what is displayed.     h3. Environment  * Confluence  * Jira    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Set up Confluence  # Integrate with Jira 7.6.7 with application links  # In JIra, create a test project with some issues in the project   # Create a page in Confluence  # Add a Jira Issues Macro  # Add JQL to select a specific issue in Jira by typing *key = <insert your JIRA issue key value here>* - do not save yet  # Select *Table*  and leave the default display options checked  # Return to the page and add an additional Jira Issues Macro with the same JQL as the previous maco  *key = <insert your JIRA issue key value here>* - do not save yet  # Select *Table*  and this time, remove a few of the default options to only leave a few and save    h3. Expected Results  The display options you chose for the second macro will display as configured    h3. Actual Results  The options from the first macro are duplicated     h3.Workaround  Resave the macro by editing it and resaving the displaying options"""

summary: "Poor drawing in the User Directories screen"
description: """h3. Summary    The *Order* column in the """"User Directories"""" administration screen is poorly drawn.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Go to !https://confluence.<USER>doc/files/590259974/590058056/1/1398739558493/Cog.png! > General Administration  # On the left-hand panel, click on *User Directories*  # The poor drawing can be already seen here   !screenshot-3.png|thumbnail!   # Add and configure valid external directory, in order to have two entries in the list  # Go back to the *User Directories* screen    h3. Expected Results    The list of directories is properly drawn.    h3. Actual Results    The """"Order"""" column is misaligned as shown below:     !screenshot-1.png|thumbnail!    !screenshot-2.png|thumbnail!      h3. Notes  Noticed on Firefox 62.0.3 (64-bit) and Chrome 70.0.3538.77 (64-bit)    h3.Workaround  No workaround so far."""
comments: "Also seen on 6.13.0"

summary: "link between a confluence document created with a template an the template"
description: """So we create templates for our communities however, when those templates are changed, the already created documents wont match the updated template. This could cause a document to miss important newly requested information. So the owner of those documents could receive a notification that the document(s) using this template had been modified, that will help ensure that document(s) is/are up to date. The other concern is how to import what is already created don't have to be copy and pasted in the newer template.    thank you"""

summary: "Add the link to download Companion App for Mac on the Administering the Atlassian Companion App page"
description: """We have the Windows version to download there but, the mac option we need to go to the [release notes|https://confluence.<USER>doc/atlassian-companion-app-release-notes-958455712.html] page to download, this is a quick fix.    Referred page:  - https://confluence.<USER>doc/administering-the-atlassian-companion-app-958456281.html"""
comments: "The documentation has been updated to include download links for the app."

summary: "Knowledge base articles are not searchable on Jira Service Desk with Japanese (non-Latin) keyword"
description: """h3. Summary    h4. Scenario 1  *Related knowledge base articles* are not shown in issue pages on Jira <USER>if the issue summary is written only with multibyte character.    h4. Scenario 2  Knowledge base articles are not searchable on Jira <USER>with multibyte characters.    h3. Environment    * Confluence 6.12.0  * Jira <USER>3.15.3    h3. Steps to Reproduce    h4. Scenario 1  # Integrate Confluence with Jira <USER> # Create a service desk project  # Create an issue with Japanese summary in the project  # Navigate to the issue page and see *Related knowledge base articles* section    h4. Scenario 2  # Integrate Confluence with Jira <USER> # Create a service desk project  # Create a knowledge base article which contains both Japanese and English  # Navigate to customer portal and insert Japanese keyword in the search bar    h3. Expected Results    h4. Scenario 1  No error was shown.    h4. Scenario 2  The knowledge base article should be suggested on the portal.    h3. Actual Results    h4. Scenario 1    The following error was shown on the UI.  {code}  The knowledge base server returned an error response.  {code}     !screenshot-1.png|thumbnail!     We can see the error below in {{atlassian-confluence.log}} as well when reproducing this bug.    {code:java}  2018-10-29 08:35:37,057 ERROR [http-nio-8090-exec-2] [common.error.jersey.ThrowableExceptionMapper] toResponse Uncaught exception thrown by REST service: Raw query is required.   -- url: /rest/knowledge-base/1.0/search/SER | traceId: 4de6e1e060d9ea2a | userName: admin  java.lang.IllegalArgumentException: Raw query is required.   at com.atlassian.confluence.search.v2.query.TextFieldQuery.<init>(TextFieldQuery.java:41)   at com.atlassian.confluence.plugins.search.api.model.SearchQueryParameters$SearchV2Query.<init>(SearchQueryParameters.java:151)   at com.atlassian.confluence.plugins.search.api.model.SearchQueryParameters.toSearchV2Query(SearchQueryParameters.java:123)   at com.atlassian.confluence.plugins.confluence_kb_space_blueprint.rest.SearchResource.publishKbSearchPerformedEvent(SearchResource.java:291)   at com.atlassian.confluence.plugins.confluence_kb_space_blueprint.rest.SearchResource.lambda$executeSearch$0(SearchResource.java:272)   at com.atlassian.sal.core.transaction.HostContextTransactionTemplate$1.doInTransaction(HostContextTransactionTemplate.java:21)   at com.atlassian.sal.spring.component.SpringHostContextAccessor$1.doInTransaction(SpringHostContextAccessor.java:71)   at org.springframework.transaction.support.TransactionTemplate.execute(TransactionTemplate.java:133)  ...  {code}    h4. Scenario 2    The knowledge base article was not suggested.  The same knowledge base was suggested as expected with English keyword.    English:   !screenshot-2.png|thumbnail!     Japanese:   !screenshot-3.png|thumbnail!     The same exception was shown in the log when reproducing this problem.     h3. Notes    h4. (!) This is not a Jira <USER>bug but a Confluence bug.    * This problem couldn't reproduce with Confluence 6.10.x  * When opening a Jira issue page (*Scenario 1*), JSD sent the following request to Confluence but there seems to be nothing wrong with it  {code:java}  http(s)://<confluence-base-url>/knowledge-base/1.0/search/<SPACE-KEY>?type=page&queryString=<ENCODED-ISSUE-SUMMARY>&permissionCheckedUser=<USER>&highlight=false&xoauth_requestor_id=<USER>  {code}    h4. (!) As described in the above, this problem could be reproduced if the issue summary is written *only with* multibyte characters.    || Issue Summary || Is reproduced? ((/) means reproducible) ||  | テスト | (/) |  | test | (x) |  | テストtest | (x) |  | testテスト | (x) |    h4. (!) This is also reproducible with other non-Latin character-sets and has been verified to occur with Cyrillic characters as well     h3.Workaround    Please make the issue summary contain one or more English characters."""
comments: "This problem was reproduced also in the following two.  · Jira <USER>3.15.3  · Confluence 6.12.1    It is inconvenient that the Jira issue can not display the knowledge base link, so I want you to fix it soon.    Also, although the relevance to this problem is unknown, even searching with double byte code in the customer portal can not search the knowledge base." [SEP] "This problem was reproduced also for:  · Jira <USER>3.15.3  · Confluence 6.12.2    for Russian language.    Interestingly, search from the title ServiceDesk portal page is working fine, but once clicking on some specific <USER>and try to search within that SD - search fails." [SEP] "Also reproduced in JIRA <USER>3.16.0 and Confluence 6.13.0" [SEP] "Hi all,    The problem was caused by Confluence KB plugin which stripped all non-latin letters from JSD search query. This mistaken replacement has been performed by a regexp operation which removed all {{[^\w]}} characters. This is fixed right now and is going to be shipped in 6.13.1." [SEP] "

summary: "Support for PostgreSQL 11"
description: """{panel:title=Atlassian Update - Oct 2020}  Hi All, I am one of the Product Managers on the Confluence Server/DC team. We do have Postgres 11 on our short term roadmap and are aiming to have it released within the next 6 months. We are also actively looking at support for Postgres 12 and 13. I apologise for the lack of visibility on this issue. We will add another update once we have an expected release version.  {panel}  PostgreSQL 11 was [released|https://www.postgresql.org/about/news/1894/] in October 2018. It would be desirable to have this supported to keep up to date with the latest PostgreSQL database versions."""
comments: "Confluence only supports PostgreSQL 9.6, which in turn only supports Windows 2012 R2.  So if anyone wants to have a fully supported installation of Confluence, they have to stick with the outdated Windows Server 2012 R2 operating system.  PostgreSQL 10 and 11 support Windows Server 2016, so Atlassian moving support to PostgreSQL 10 and 11 is important to maintaining support for current Windows operating systems as well as database versions.  " [SEP] "Debian Buster only supports PostgreSQL 11. Pleaseschedule support with upcoming Buster release" [SEP] "I have been running a copy of our Confluence install on PostgreSQL 11 and have found no issues so far. Are there any known compatibility problems running PostgreSQL 11? If not supporting it should be trivial." [SEP] "Debian 10 Buster is out with PostgresSQL 11.4. Must have feature in my opinion. Honestly I do not understand why it is not supported already. :)    Running Confluence since Release of Buster on PostgresSQL 11 - well it works." [SEP] "Because At

description: """I'm inserting multiple Jira issue filters into my confluence pages and they are making the overall page quite lengthy.  Each of these embedded filters should return just 0-5 issues, but because each 'Jira issue/filter' table has a header row (column names) and a footer row (issue count + refresh) the overall asthetic and usability of the result page is impaired.    Ideally I would like a pair of simple checkboxes in the 'Display Options' to enable/disable the header and footer respectively.    I'm currently on Confluence 6.8.0"""

summary: "Page restrictions dialog has misleading language regarding permissions"
description: """h3. Summary  The restrictions dialogue has confusing language that indicates users can view and edit pages, even if they don't have space permissions. This can be confusing and misleading, even if the user understands the relationship between space permissions and restrictions.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Create a new space with space permissions to only be viewable by one user (user1, in this example)  # As user1, attempt to add any other user to the restrictions on a page in the new space.    h3. Expected Results  Either one of the following:  * The dialogue would give a clear indication space permissions are required before restrictions.  * Users who do not have the appropriate space permissions would not be allowed to be added to the restrictions on a page.    h3. Actual Results  Users without space permissions can be added to the restrictions, and the dialogue indicates users can view and edit the page, even if they don't have space permissions.    The screenshot below shows the restrictions for a page in a space that only <USER>can view. However, the dialogue indicates """"Everyone can view"""" and <USER>Adams """"Can edit"""".  !restrictions_screenshot.png|thumbnail!"""
comments: "fyi - we are running v6.8.2 and have this issue.    Thanks for bringing this up"

description: """If someone sends a smiley face in Google Hangouts, and you copy this message and place it into a Confluence page, the editor will successfully render the smiley face as a span (not Confluence's native emoticon). When the user attempts to save the page, they will get the following message:   """"Unable to communicate with server"""". I'd expect Confluence to:   # Not allow you to paste in characters that it won't be able to save   # Convert these emojis to Confluence's native emoticons if there is an equivalent so the page can be saved"""

summary: """Insert files and images"" dialog breaks on new pages when collaborative editing is disabled"
description: """h3. Summary  When a user attempts to upload attachments to a new page, the attachment is unusable from the """"Insert Files and Images"""" dialog until the page is saved.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Disable collaborative editing  # Click *Create* to create a new page  # In the editor toolbar, click the *Insert files and images* button to open the dialog.  # Click *Upload files* and select a file to upload.    h3. Expected Results  The file will be added as an attachment on the page and be selectable from the """"Insert Files and Images Dialog""""    h3. Actual Results  # During the upload, the loading bar shows it uploads 100%, then freezes on the filled loading bar.  !ss_loading-bar.png|thumbnail!  # If you close and re-open the dialog, an error shows: """"{{Error retrieving attachments from Confluence. The server may be unavailable.}}""""""""  !ss_error-retrieving.png|thumbnail!  # If you save the page, then re-open to edit, the """"Insert files and images"""" dialog shows the attachment and functions normally.  !ss_normal-dialog.png|thumbnail!    Alternatively:  If you upload an image via drag-and-drop, it will show on the page, but the """"Insert files and images"""" dialog will still show an error until the page is saved for the first time.  !ss_drag-and-drop.png|thumbnail!    _(i) There are similar bugs where this issue happens to anonymous users and large images (CONFSERVER-38871, CONFSERVER-45746). However, this issue happens for any file for logged in users._    h3.Workarounds  * Only use the drag-and-drop functionality to insert files when creating a new page.  * Save the page and edit to have access to the full """"Insert files and images"""" functionality"""
comments: "The issue seems to be a duplicate of https://jira.<USER>browse/CONFSERVER-56252, which is fixed in 6.11.1. "

summary: "Limit the number of document conversion threads by default"
description: """h3. Problem Definition  Confluence uses document conversion service to convert Office file and PDF files for preview and thumbnail generation. Currently, this service has a queue of tasks to run through and the file conversion service spins up background threads to convert those documents. Unfortunately, if one document is taking a bit more time to convert, Confluence might spin too many concurrent threads which might add too much pressure on the CPU and memory.    h3. Suggested Solution  The suggested solution is to limit the number of concurrent conversion threads by default.      h3. Workaround  The current workaround is to add the following JVM argument to limit the number of conversion threads.  """

summary: "Add an Edit (or Preview) button to the View Files macro"
description: """The customers have many pages which display the document content inline, e.g. through """"View Excel"""", """"View Word"""" or """"View Powerpoint"""" macros.  In these cases, there is no way to get into the attachment previewer.  A workaround would be to add an """"Edit"""" link to the page manually. Not user-friendly.  An additional request would be to add an """"Edit"""" button to the View Files macros. Remember there are many users which have already expressed the request to not obsolete this macro!  Sites which have the lock-point plugin installed, to make sure that an attachment is not concurrently edited by several people (to address the """"last upload wins"""" issue).  For such sites, this flow is being enforced at the moment:  * go to the attachment page  * lock the document  * go to view mode  * view the document in previewer -> Edit with ...   * upload the change  * exit previewer  * go back to the attachment page  * unlock the document.  This requires to navigate between the attachment page and view page 2 times. Productivity loss. By adding the """"Edit"""" link to the attachment page, this would not be necessary."""

summary: "Standalone Synchrony in Data Center starts with no error if DB credentials are wrong"
description: """h2. Issue Summary  On Confluence Data Center, Synchrony is configured as a separated process, which requires a connection to the Confluence database.  If the database credentials is wrongly configured in [Synchrony startup script|https://confluence.<USER>conf66/configuring-synchrony-for-data-center-943962921.html], no error is logged in {{atlassian-synchrony.log}} during startup.  Also, [checking Synchrony status|https://confluence.<USER>confkb/how-to-check-the-status-of-synchrony-for-confluence-data-center-953651062.html] shows it is working properly.      h2. Environment Specifics  * Confluence Data Center  * Standalone Synchrony cluster    h2. Current State  Running {{start-synchrony.sh}} script with a wrong DB password will successfully complete without any error.  Also, the script exit status will be 0.  {code:title=start-synchrony.sh STDOUT and exit status with a wrong DB password}  $ ./start-synchrony.sh  To run Synchrony in the foreground, start the server with start-synchrony.sh -fg  Starting Synchrony with PID 73455...  Binding: 127.0.0.1  Please wait 30 seconds, then check this heartbeat URL in your browser for an 'OK': http://127.0.0.1:8091/synchrony/heartbeat  ecrmac-100728:synchrony root# echo $?  0      Testing Synchrony websocket upgrade will successfully complete.  {code:title=Testing Synchrony websocket upgrade with a wrong DB password}  $ curl -i -N -H """"Connection: Upgrade"""" -H """"Upgrade: websocket"""" -H """"Sec-WebSocket-Key: 33xyqDvzAXTYgsjjbaYD5A=="""" --header """"Sec-WebSocket-Version: 13"""" http://127.0.0.1:8091/synchrony/sockjs/v1/018/u4q4rohu/websocket  HTTP/1.1 101 Switching Protocols  upgrade: websocket  connection: upgrade  sec-websocket-accept: RV68WHXjNM0H+IFE5/W1ioLmMHI=    �o�a[""""[39,1,256,20000]""""]^C  {code}    Checking {{atlassian-synchrony.log}} shows no error: [^atlassian-synchrony.log_excerpt_wrong_pwd.txt]     This is a misleading behavior, since it indicates Synchrony is running properly, even though the DB password was wrongly configured in the Synchrony startup script.    h2. Expected Behavior  Synchrony startup script must perform a DB credential check on startup and fail if it was unable to connect to the database.  If that's not the case, Synchrony should log an error about not being able to connect to the database. In addition to this, Synchrony heartbeat should fail.    h2. Impact  Confluence administrator will take some time to learn that the DB credential is wrong when configuring Synchrony for the first time.  We may waste time troubleshooting the problem when it should log the error information during the process startup.    h2. Workaround  None so far.  """

summary: "Require 2FA Challenge for Designated Spaces"
description: """h3. Problem Definition  Currently Two Factor Authentication (2FA) can be enabled at an account level, requiring a user to use a secondary authenticator when logging in.    It would be useful to be able to prompt for 2FA when a user attempts to access particular spaces in order to ensure that the user who authenticated is still in control of the session.    This is a common function in many systems in order to access more sensitive information.    h3. Suggested Solution  Implement Space level 2FA for users who are already authenticated.    This 2FA should allow access to the resources with a controllable timeout.    h3. Workaround  None currently."""

summary: "Deleting a focused comment does not trigger a page refresh"
description: """Navigating directly to a comment and then deleting it does not trigger a page reload showing that the comment was removed.     This can cause confusion because it appears that the comment was not deleted, and can trigger further SQL errors if a user tries to delete the comment again.      h3. Steps to reproduce   * Add a comment to a page   * Right click the date of the comment to obtain the direct link to the comment   * Navigate to the link to go to the focused comment   * Delete the comment    h3. Expected results    The page will refresh and the comment no longer appears    h3. Actual results    Nothing seems to happen. The page does not refresh, and the comment still appears on the page. However, the comment has been deleted in the database.    h3. Workaround    Instead of trying to delete the comment again, refresh the page manually. """

summary: "Corrupted issue links on a page break correct issue links"
description: """h3. Problem description    Each Application Link in the system has customizable ServerName and generated ServerId. When a link to Jira issue is posted on the page, it automatically creates issue link in the following storage format    {code:none}  <ac:structured-macro ac:name=""""jira"""" ac:schema-version=""""1"""" ac:macro-id=""""638accfe-f49a-48c6-94dd-6923005b2ad8"""">      <ac:parameter ac:name=""""server"""">Your Company JIRA</ac:parameter>      <ac:parameter ac:name=""""serverId"""">986c17b5-1e77-3526-a338-fd866f770849</ac:parameter>      <ac:parameter ac:name=""""key"""">MEOW-1</ac:parameter>  </ac:structured-macro>  {code}    On different Confluence installations the ServerId and ServerName might be different for the same external Jira. And after exporting and importing page content, the system might end up in the state when the page has issue link to some system that is not configured as an application link.    When such page is opened, there's an error macro placeholder in the place of issue links, which is logical because the system doesn't know anything about the external instance. But it also causes another problem, which is the subject of this issue. If you have correct issue link in the beginning of the page and then broken issue link somewhere in the middle, correct issue link will not work as well and display macro placeholder.    Please see the attached screenshot.    h3. Steps to reproduce  * create a page with two correctly set issue links in it  * go to database and change server id and server name for the second link to invalid values (alternatively you can import pages from another system with issue links to unknown Jira location)  * flush all caches  * refresh the page and check issue links    _Expected_: first normal issue link works as expected, while the second throws an error  _Actual_: both issues don't work    h3. How the problem should be fixed    The problem reproduces because Jira macro tries to resolve multiple issues in single batch. When it fails on invalid issue because can't find a server, it will also corrupt macro representation for correct one. This also explains why only the first application link was corrupted.    The problem happens in {{confluence-jira-plugin}}:  {code:java}  // Collect all possible server IDs from the macro definitions  for (MacroDefinition singleIssueMacroDefinition : singleIssueMacroDefinitions) {      String key = singleIssueMacroDefinition.getParameter(JiraIssuesMacro.KEY);      String serverId = getServerIdFromKey(singleIssueMacroDefinition.getParameters(), key, new DefaultConversionContext(entity.toPageContext()));        if (serverId != null) {          macroDefinitionByServer.put(serverId, singleIssueMacroDefinition);      }  }  {code}     If there's an issue link with invalid server info in the system, it shouldn't affect other correct issue links. This is not the case right now because of batch nature of confluence-jira-plugin. We need to add correct error handling.    h3. Workaround    The easiest way to fix broken links is to create application links to missing system. Luckily, when Jira macro resolves server information, it first tries to find application link with given ServerName and only in the case of failure fallbacks to ServerId. Because of this you can create application link with exactly the same name as you see in a page storage format parameter {{<ac:parameter ac:name=""""server"""">}} pointing to the correct Jira location."""

summary: "Emphasize warning for Unknown plugins on UPM Upgrade Checker"
description: """h4. Summary  The UPM Add-ons update checker should put more emphasis on """"Unknown"""" plugins, or those which are not registered with the Atlassian Marketplace.    As an example, this is a screenshot of a Confluence 6.0.4 environment performing a compatibility check to update to Confluence 6.6.7:   !findings.png|thumbnail!     h4. Recommendation  The description for unknown should be updated with stronger wording to reflect the importance of uninstalling or removing these plugins prior to a production upgrade. Plugins should be evaluated in a lower environment first, or a newer version of the plugin should be installed.    Plugins have the ability to heavily affect both the performance and reliability of the environment, it is *critical* that they are thoroughly tested prior to an upgrade.    """

summary: "New URL format proposal (permanent URLs that include meta-data)"
description: """Today a page (not counting shortened urls) can have two types of URLs:   # [https://confluence.example.com/display/SPACEKEY/Page+Title]   # [https://confluence.example.com/pages/viewpage.action?pageId=123456]    *The former format (1)* gives hints the user about the content of the page. This is extra great when you send several links to a user, since it often removes the need to write a description for each of the links.    However, since the URL can change at any given moment. Making the format useless for long term usage.    *The latter format (2)* is permanent,** but extremely user unfriendly. When sending links of this kind, the sender must always include description of the links.    It is also not clear for the vast majority of the end users why a certain URL is generated in a certain way (some power users may know, but it not common).    I propose a *new default URL format* for pages on confluence. Allow a middle chunk in the URL to contain meta-data that *_is not used_* when retrieving the page:  {quote}{{[https://confluence.example.com/p/Page+Title/123456|https://confluence.example.com/pages/viewpage.action?pageId=123456]}}  {quote}  Since the only parts used for page retrieval would be /p/*/123456 you would not risk the URL to stop working, but the receiver could still figure out what it is about by reading the link.    *Page titles with special characters could now be sanitised and included in the generated URLs. The retrieval would still work.*    Looking at other services, this is a quite common way to solve this exact issue, and as an extra bonus, the URLs are far more SEO friendly."""
comments: "I'd suggest a third URL scheme which looks like this: [https://confluence.example.com/123456.html|https://confluence.example.com/pages/viewpage.action?pageId=123456]    Short URLs are actually user friendly since URLs are completely visible on small screens. Description of the URL can be in front the URL as plain text or can be the link text. Since Confluence runs on top of Apache Tomcat, mod_rewrite and .htaccess are standard features.    [http://magicmonster.com/kb/app/apache/neat_urls.html]    Once I get my Confluence server running, I'll make an attempt and write a guide if there's enough interest in setting up a mod_rewrite."

summary: "Confluence Export to Word functionality results in missing web images in Word document"
description: """*Steps to Replicate*  # Create a page in Confluence.  # In the Editor Toolbar, choose """"insert files and images"""".   # In the """"insert files and images"""" form, choose """"Images from the web"""" and then add an image from an external URL  # *Save* the page and hit *Export to Word*.    *Expected Behaviour*  The image appear in the Word document.    *Actual Behaviour*  The generated Word documented doesn't have the image.    *Notes*  Export to PDF display the generate the image correctly.    *Workaround*  *One: Save the Generated Image as an attachment*  # Save the generated image to your computer  # Attach the image you saved to the page  # Insert the image using the editor  # Export the page to Word    *Two: Export as PDF, and then convert to Word*  You can also export the file as a PDF, and then use Adobe Acrobat (the full version of Acrobat Reader) to convert the file back to a Microsoft Word compatbile format.  """

"""

[SEP] "Ah, good point Brendan. From a technical standpoint, I think we could fix it though. We could get the hash via frontend javascript code, and propagate it to the backend by having javascript transcode it as a URL param. Would understand if that kind of change is not feasible though" [SEP] "Yeah, I don't think we will be able to undertake this anytime soon.  For the Confluence Previewer we ended up using query params instead of URL fragments to transfer the state between requests.  Would you be able to do

summary: "File Conversion Services causes High CPU/Memory Usage"
description: """h2. Summary    Previewing large Excel files in Confluence can cause Out of Memory Errors as well as High CPU Usage, leading the application to freeze until the document is converted or, in worst scenarios, to get unresponsive until it has been restarted.  h2. Previous Bugs    There has been fixed bugs related to File Conversion Services in the past:   * CONFSERVER-46542   * CONFSERVER-52279    This specific bug is an edge case that can still happen even if you are in one of the versions flagged as fixed by above bugs or if you have manually applied the workaround listed on them.  h2. Steps to Replicate   # Attach an excel file with more than 100000 cells to Confluence.   # Try to preview it.   # CPU usage will increase and so will the Heap consumption.   # Confluence will freeze for other requests such as page view or space view.   # It may eventually return to normal operation if the file is converted successfully, but, there are higher chances that the application will need to be restarted than that the file will be converted.    h2. Workaround    You will need to apply a manual workaround or to upgrade your application accordingly. Please rely on the table below in order to confirm which approach you should take and contact support in case of any questions.  ||Confluence version ||Document Library  Version||  |h4. Before 6.4|Upgrade to Confluence 6.6.9 once possible, the manual patch is not available for these versions.|  |h4. 6.4 and newer versions|Download Document Conversion Services version [2.0.2.1|https://packages.<USER>maven-closedsource-local/com/atlassian/plugins/document-conversion-library/2.0.2.1/document-conversion-library-2.0.2.1.jar] and apply the workaround below - Or you can also update your application to version [6.6.9|https://confluence.<USER>doc/issues-resolved-in-6-6-9-959785937.html]. |   # Access Confluence as an Administrator.   # Click on the Cog Icon in your Right Upper Corner and then click on Add-ons.   # Click on Upload Add-on.   # Upload the file that you have previously downloaded to Confluence. It will do the rest automatically.   # Once that's done, just confirm that *all* modules for both {color:#f79232}Confluence Document Conversion Library{color} and {color:#f79232}Document Conversion Library{color} are enabled.   # Also, please confirm that {color:#f79232}Document Conversion Library{color} has version 2.0.2.1 on it.   # With that completed, go ahead and create a new page in any space.   # Attach a problematic file to the page.   # Click on it. The Preview window will be rendered. The file may take a couple seconds to be rendered, but, it does render just fine."""

summary: "After index rebuild on a node, automatically replicate the updated index to other cluster nodes in the background"
description: """h3. Summary  In instances where nodes have *not had downtime* or required a restart, but the index has been rebuilt on one node, automatically replicating the index in the background to the other nodes is useful to administrators who wish to maximize uptime.     If a manual index rebuild is triggered, it should be sufficient to flag that node's index as the most current copy for replication to the other nodes without downtime.    In this scenario, only the node with the already-rebuilt index will have a current index, and thus it would be recommended to load balance users to only that node during said index replication process, to ensure their search results are current and complete.    h3. Environment  Confluence Data Center 6.x      h3. Current State  Currently, the index replication only occurs on startup of a node, thus requiring a node to be restarted in order to either automatically pull a fresh copy of the index. Manually copying the index to other nodes is another option but does require downtime.    h3. Reference Documentation  * [Administer Your Data Center Search Index|https://confluence.<USER>doc/administer-your-data-center-search-index-879956107.html]  * [How To Rebuild The Content Indexes From Scratch On Confluence Data Center|https://confluence.<USER>confkb/how-to-rebuild-the-content-indexes-from-scratch-on-confluence-data-center-833941594.html]  * [Confluence Data Center Technical Overview - Indexes|https://confluence.<USER>doc/confluence-data-center-technical-overview-790795847.html#ConfluenceDataCenterTechnicalOverview-Indexes]    Related to: CONFSERVER-54760"""
comments: "[https://getsupport.<USER>browse/CSP-261503]" [SEP] "This has been implemented in Confluence 7.7: https://confluence.<USER>doc/confluence-7-7-release-notes-1004960930.html#Confluence7.7ReleaseNotes-Reindexyourclusterwithoutdowntime"

summary: "Cannot Update User Information When Connected to Jira or Crowd with Read and Write Permission"
description: """h3. Summary  When attempting to add information to a user's profile, it's not possible to save the changes if there's no write permission all the way through the origin of the user (the final directory that this user comes from).    An example of a scenario in which the problem happens:  # Confluence is set with Read/Write permissions to Crowd;  # Crowd is connected to the internal or to an external directory in order to provide users to Confluence;  # The confluence application inside Crowd does not have permissions to write to the directory that the users come from.    In this scenario, if we update the *About Me* section of a user's profile in Confluence, the application throws an error related to a lack of permissions as Crowd does not allow updates in the directory. However, the profile details are only stored locally in Confluence, so the application should never try to update Crowd.    If we set Confluence to connect to Crowd/Jira with Read Only permissions, Confluence will not try to update the external directory and will successfully update the user's profile.    h3. Environment  * Confluence connected to Crowd or Jira    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Connect Confluence to a Crowd with Read/Write permissions;  # Create an application in Crowd for Confluence and add the internal directory as a source of users in that application;  # Into the created application in Crowd, go to the *Permissions* tab and uncheck all permissions there, then save;  # Now go to Confluence, log in with a user from Crowd, go to the Profile of this user and try to add anything in the *About Me* field, for example, then save the changes.    h3. Expected Results  Confluence will update the information as it's stored locally only.    h3. Actual Results  The below exception is thrown in the atlassian-confluence.log file:        h3.Workaround  There are two possible workarounds for this issue:  # Set Crowd/Jira to Read Only in Confluence; or  # Give write permissions in Crowd/Jira for the directory that the user comes from."""
comments: "We have a policy requiring new users to update certain non-crowd profile data such as 'about me', but this bug causes a conflict with another policy to disallow users from editing crowd profile data such as email address."

summary: "Anonymous Access to Space with Team Calendar installed causes Safari in iOS to prompt authentication request"
description: """h3. Problem Definition  Anonymous Access to Space with Team Calendar installed causes Safari in iOS to prompt authentication request    h3. Environment  Confluence 6.11.2  Team Calendar 6.0.20    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Install Team Calendar to Confluence  # Enable Anonymous Access for the Confluence  # Browse to a publicly accessible space _without logged in_ using browsers below:  #* Google Chrome  #* Safari from iOS    h3. Expected Result  All browsers should be able to load and display the page properly    h3. Actual Result  Safari from iOS specifically, will prompt an authentication request   !iOS Safari.png|thumbnail!   Meanwhile, other browsers such as Chrome and Firefox, even Safari from MacOS X loads the page just fine    h3. Information  This is becuase browsing the space when Team Calendar is installed will always result in a REST API call that returns with *401 Unauthorized* response:    * /rest/calendar-services/1.0/tips?_=1539931039888  (i) The string of numbers can be different every time the space is loaded    In the case if the user is logged in, this REST call will return with *200 OK* but there won't be aren't any useful information being returned      Due to the *401* response it will trigger the authentication prompt on Safari from iOS which can be annoying to the public user since it is not necessary and doesn't provide any useful information after authenticating    h3. Suggested Solution  If this is behavior is not intended, perhaps it can be skipped entirely.  A temporary workaround can be to have JavaScript to detect the type of browser and strip this result from the responses"""

summary: "User macro with no parameter, no ""$noparams"" doesn't show up in macro browser"
description: """h3. Summary    User macros that have no parameter and no """"$noparams"""" don't show up in macro browser.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Create a user macro as follows    # Create a new page  # Open macro browser and search the created macro    h3. Expected Results    The test macro shows up in the macro browser, and behave as follows --   {quote}  If the user macro contains no parameters and does not specify @noparams, then the macro browser will display a free-format text box allowing users to enter undefined parameters.  {quote}  as described in https://confluence.<USER>doc/user-macro-template-syntax-223906130.html    h3. Actual Results  The test macro doesn't show up in the macro browser.       h3.Workaround  * Add """"@noparams"""" in your macro.    Or,  * Insert the macro directly typing """"{<macro-name>}"""" in Confluence editor."""

summary: "Atlassian Companion: Provide Option to Choose the Application to Edit / View Attachment (Images)"
description: """h3. Problem Definition  * The current *Edit With* feature goes hand to hand with the [Atlassian Companion|https://confluence.<USER>doc/administering-the-atlassian-companion-app-958456281.html] application that allow users to dynamically edit an attached file using its native desktop application, then automatically save the file back to Confluence.   !Screen Shot 2018-10-19 at 09.23.26.png|thumbnail!   * There are certain scenarios where the users utilize different applications to view and edit images    h3. Suggested Solution  * Provide an option to choose either *edit* or *view* the image and pull the application list from the system so users can choose  * Additionally, allow the users to set default edit / view application within Confluence (or maybe Atlassian Companion app) so they don't have to get prompted every time they open the attachment   !Screen Shot 2018-10-19 at 09.23.27.png|thumbnail!     h3. Why this is important  * Unless you are editing images often, previewing image in graphics editor will consume more resources.    h3. Workaround   * Change the default application to edit / preview the image on the OS level. For example, [How to set default apps on Mac|https://www.imore.com/how-set-mac-app-default-when-opening-file]"""

summary: "Confluence: Support multiple page versions per language"
description: """(related to I18n, Internationalization, Localization)    *Requirements*   * Pages should be able to have multiple versions per language.   * Search results should display the versions of articles in user's logged-in language. This should apply when using the search from the <USER>portals as well.   * When viewing a page, a user should see links to the different language versions (like Wikipedia does.)   * The translated pages should know which language should be considered the """"primary language"""". The primary language should have a Space-level default and be switchable per page.   * When a user views a secondary language version, the page should display a warning indicating when the primary version has been updated more recently (i.e. """"This page may no longer be current"""").   * Admins should be able to see a list of pages which have either not yet been translated into a given locale (e.g. """"How to Cook Tacos"""" is available in English but not Japanese) or have had updates to the primary version.   * Provide API hooks when a translated page was updated, along with change list (e.g. to facilitate marketplace integration of translation services.)   * Admins should be able to define locale fallbacks at the space-level, for example, """"Simplified Chinese"""" falls back to """"Traditional Chinese"""" if available, otherwise falls back to """"English"""" (meaning that Chinese users will see English content if the Chinese version isn't available.)   * Ideally, URLs for other language versions would use the same slug as the primary version, could be something like /my-blog-post?lang=en and /my-blog-post?lang=fr    Wikipedia should be considered a reference for this functionality. There is also a requirement doc from 2006 here [https://confluence.<USER>pages/viewpage.action?spaceKey=DISC&title=Translation+of+content] which is still relevant.    *Current Workarounds*    Currently, the recommended solution is to make multiple Confluence Spaces, one for each language. This approach is fraught with problems, for example, since one <USER>can only be linked to one Confluence space, you effectively can't use Service Desks in multiple languages with Confluence.    *Note about Marketplace*    The related tickets CONFCLOUD-1076 / CONFSERVER-1076 were closed 5 years ago with a note saying this would be handled by the marketplace, However, in 5 years the marketplace has not produced a tenable solution in either the cloud or server versions.   There are some translation management solutions such as Appfusions ([http://www.appfusions.com/display/LNGOTKC/Home]) however these are not substitute for the core issue here, which is that Confluence lacks the proper datastructure. If Atlassian makes the proper datastructure (described above) then it will facilitate more translation solutions (e.g. human, machine, <USER> being made available on the marketplace, especially if API hooks are made when content changes."""
comments: "This was meant for CONFCLOUD (but applies to both server and cloud)"

summary: "Cache statistics page does not display custom values after upgrade"
description: """If you customize cache settings in one version of Confluence and then subsequently upgrade to a new version, the cache statistics page does not display the custom values. Instead, it displays default values.    Here are the steps taken to create the problem:   * installed 5.10.8   * customized cache settings cache-settings-overrides.txt file   * verified that these settings were in the file   * manually upgraded to 6.10.2   * immediately after upgrade, checked cache-settings-overrides.txt file and verified that it contained the customized settings from 5.10.8   * opened cache settings screen in 6.10.2, but custom settings did not display on screen    h4. Workarounds   * Content reindex *does not* help   * Clearing plugin cache *does not* help   * Instead, added one new customized setting in 6.10.2. Though this wasn't immediately reflected in the UI, it and all other cache customizations appeared after a reload of the page.    h4. Other Notes    For a newly installed 6.10 instance, custom cache changes appear to refresh with no issue.  Also, in the cache-settings-overrides file, some values are stored with a """"_v5"""" suffix while others are not."""

summary: "The (view change) style is not applied to the open bracket ""("""
description: """In the *All updates* page in Confluence, it shows all the recent updates, and in some of the updates, there will be an option to view the changes.    The font of the *View Change* is slightly darker and bigger, but it is not applied to the opening bracket, """"("""".    In Firefox, it's even worse, with some spacing after the bracket.  h3. """

summary: "Confluence modifies tables with active objects on every restart"
description: """h1. Background    When Confluence starts up, it verifies schemas for all tables with active objects (AO), and modifies / adds fields if required. For example, if a new version of a plugin is installed, and this plugin introduces a new field in AO class, the corresponding table will be updated and new column will be created.  h1. The problem    For MySQL, migration procedure has a bug and updates several tables on every boot like this:    It is not a serious issue for Confluence with one node (it only increases boot time), but in case of 2+ nodes it could lead Confluence to deadlock. Example:   # The first node is executing slow query (which takes a lot of time) on AO_BAF3AA_AOINLINE_TASK table   # The second node is being restarted and is trying to modify AO_BAF3AA_AOINLINE_TASK. But this operation is blocked while the first node does not finish all SQL queries on AO_BAF3AA_AOINLINE_TASK   # The first node tries to run more SQL queries on AO_BAF3AA_AOINLINE_TASK, but all of them are blocked by DDL operation on the second node.    As a result, all threads could be blocked.  h1. Expected behaviour    Confluence should not run any DDL operations on boot if tables do not require changes.  h1. Technical details    It happens in SchemaGenerator.migrate"""
comments: "Hi,         Today we had an outage and this bug led us to lose 30 minutes of investigation since our second node lock the DB. While I understand this could be limited to some specific circumstance, the impact can be huge for an organization. Could we consider this in the LTS bug fix ? This is typically the type of bug that enterprise pay to avoid ( I mean come on, it leads to all threads being blocked !!! ... )" [SEP] "As of today, we faced another outage due to this bug. A simple restart of one node of our cluster triggered the bug and the entire outage." [SEP] "A fix for this issue is available to Server and Data Center customers in Confluence 6.15.5   [Upgrade|https://www.<USER>software/confluence/download?utm_source=confluence_bugfix&utm_campaign=confluence_bugfix&utm_medium=email] now or check out the [Release Notes|https://confluence.<USER>doc/confluence-6-15-release-notes-965554120.html?utm_source=confluence_bugfix&utm_campaign=confluence_bugfix&utm_medium=email] to see what other issues are resolved

summary: "Internal directory user's password needs to match the one in Crowd if SSO is enabled"
description: """h3. Summary    Internal directory user's password needs to match the one in Crowd in the following conditions.    * SSO is enabled  * Confluence Internal Directory is in a higher position than Crowd Directory in [User Directories|https://confluence.<USER>doc/configuring-user-directories-229838212.html] setting    h3. Steps to Reproduce    # [Integrating Crowd with Atlassian Confluence|https://ja.confluence.<USER>crowd/integrating-crowd-with-atlassian-confluence-198573.html]  # Create a user who has same username in in each directory as follows  #* Confluence Internal Directory:  #** username: testuser  #** password: *password1*  #* Crowd Directory:  #** username: testuser  #** password: *password2*  # Move Confluence Internal Directory in a higher position than Crowd Directory in _Confluence administrations > User Directories_  # Enable SSO with following instruction in the above page  # Try to login Confluence with the latter one (in Crowd Directory) credential  #* Please login Confluence from Confluence's login page and do not login from Crowd or the other SSO enabled applications    h3. Expected Results    As [the document|https://ja.confluence.<USER>crowd/integrating-crowd-with-atlassian-confluence-198573.html] said,    bq. It is possible to define multiple user directories in Confluence. However, if you enable Crowd SSO integration, you will only be able to authenticate as users from the Crowd server defined in the crowd.properties file.    the credential in Crowd Directory should be used for the authentication and the login should succeed.    h3. Actual Results    The login failed. Even if we try to login with the credential in Confluence Internal Directory, it failed as well. It means both of the passwords need to match each other.     h3. Notes    The user seemed to be authenticated twice by both Jira Internal Directory and Crowd Directory in this situation.    There was also [a suggestion|https://jira.<USER>browse/CONFSERVER-25060] which asked for disabling other directories than Crowd if SSO was enabled but it was already closed as """"Won't Fix"""".    h3.Workaround    We can still login Confluence from other SSO enabled applications' login page."""

summary: "Embedded Crowd to have a checker for user creation "
description: """h3. Problem Definition  If a user is deactivated and down the road, a new user comes along with the same username. The user will have access to the old username.     However, this is an expected behavior. Where the old Username access is preserved as it is being deactivated. That being said, this causes a security risk and also confusion in big companies. As Confluence and LDAP admins might not be in the same department when the new LDAP is being created.     h3. Suggested Solution  A checker or an alert to the admin in Atlassian Product end should be notified before the user is being created automatically if for example a name or an email is mismatched from the old set of LDAP details to determine if the user is activated back correctly or this is a separate user altogether.     h3. Why this is important  If the previous user is a Manager position and a new user with the same name come along. The new user might have access to certain information that he/she should not.      h3. Workaround   Do not use the same username for now. """

summary: "Confluence should not hold a database connection when it is not needed"
description: """h3. Problem Definition  On a super high-level description, Confluence occupies a database connection from the connection pool throughout the entirety of the HTTP request. Once that HTTP request returns, the database connection is returned to the pool.  This is a very complex topic on how Confluence handles database connections. Several enhancements and optimizations were done throughout the years to make sure we don't hold up a database connection for long. However, the first statement still holds.   When an HTTP request is required to interact with an external system, that database connection will still be held until a response is received from the remote server. In the case where you have a slowness with getting the response, the new incoming HTTP requests will open more database connections in Confluence.    h3. Suggested Solution  It would be nice to have Confluence use a database connection only when it is needed within the lifespan of an HTTP request.    h3. Workaround  Unfortunately, there are no workarounds at the moment."""

summary: "Users are duplicated in the search results due to duplicated USERINFO records in the database"
description: """h3. Symptoms   * Users appear twice in the search result   * Users have two USERINFO records with no 'prevver' value (ie two current records), eg:    (i) USERINFO records that do *not* have an entry in prevver are the currently active records. This is a little counter-intuitive given the name of the field, however older records that are no longer active contain the id of the superseding record in prevver. If prevver is null, the record is considered active.   * If duplicated users appears anywhere else, refer to [CONFSERVER-30050|https://jira.<USER>browse/CONFSERVER-30050].    h3. Diagnosis - is this affecting you?    First, try a content [reindex|https://confluence.<USER>doc/content-index-administration-148844.html], and a [reindex from scratch|https://confluence.<USER>confkb/how-to-rebuild-the-content-indexes-from-scratch-on-confluence-server-110035351.html]. If this doesn't help, run this query (on any version). If it returns any results, this bug is the cause of your duplicates.    If this query returns nothing, have a look at CONF-36018 as well.  h3. Work Around    Follow the *Workaround* provided in the [CONFSERVER-30050|https://jira.<USER>browse/CONFSERVER-30050]"""

summary: "Static content is truncated if Compress HTTP Responses is active"
description: """h3. Summary    Static content served by Tomcat is truncated if """"Compress HTTP Responses"""" is active.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Create a .txt file under *Confluence-Install/confluence* directory (let's call it static-content.txt in this example)  # Put this content in the file:    # Try to access it using a browser by reaching the corresponding URL: *http://<Base-URL>/static-content.txt*    h3. Expected Results    The following output is presented:     !static-content-correct.png|thumbnail!     h3. Actual Results    The output is truncated as we can see here (only the first three 'a' characters are shown for the second line):     !static-content-truncated.png|thumbnail!      h3. Notes    The cause of the problem seems to be related with GZIP compression, which is enabled by default in Confluence. Based on that, the problem only manifests when accessing the file through a browser. If you curl the same URL, the output is correct:        This could be a serious problem if you are using static content for external applications, like monitoring tools. If the content is truncated, the tool may not be able to recognise it and fail to run.    h3.Workaround    # The first workaround is to change the extension of the file from .txt to something else (e.g. .aaa)  # Second workaround is to disable the *Compress HTTP Responses* option in the *General Configuration* menu"""

summary: "Allow Companion app to connect to Confluence without requiring infrastructure changes"
description: """Some of our customers are facing an issue where they cannot use the Companion App to edit files. They run Confluence in managed environments where proxies are employed and ports are often blocked. Their infrastructure teams may also have security protocols or policies they must abide by that forbid some of the access that Companion App requires to function.    This access is often something both the users and the infrastructure teams have no ability to change in their environment leading to an impaired experience and no ability to edit documents in this manner.    Since most browsers do not allow an insecure websocket connection (from the browser to Companion) when Confluence is hosted on a secure connection, in order to make a secure websocket connection between the browser and companion, we've created a DNS on the public internet for atlassian-domain-for-localhost-connections-only.com that maps to the IP address 127.0.0.1. It is imperative that this domain is excluded from the Browser's forward proxies, otherwise the Browser will never find its way from the forward proxy back to the Companion App on the local machine. If the connection is not allowed we do fall back to trying to connect to 127.0.0.1 directly if the domain connections failed, however depending on the cause of failure this could take additional time before a connection is made.     """
comments: "It would be helpful to have at least a fallback to 127.0.0.1 although I don't understand why this IP or ""localhost"" is not being used directly instead of the dummy hostname mapping to 127.0.0.1.    I wasn't able to see requests to 127.0.0.1 but requests to the dummy hostname only. After five requests resulting in 403 errors (that is after about 3 minutes) Confluence shows the installation dialogue for the Companion App although the app has already been installed. Even after that Confluence keeps creating requests to the dummy hostname. I was not able to observe any fallback to 127.0.0.1.    Kind Regards,    <USER>    " [SEP] "As Enterprise Company we will NOT add the DNS name atlassian-domain-for-localhost-connections-only.com to our company wide proxyconf Configuration. As users are not allowed to change their company wide proxy settings (that uses the company wide proxyconf) the companion app is not working for us, as the browser could not connect to the companion app.    Who in the hell at Atlassian h

summary: "Searching with CQL for space with number at the beginning of space name will throw an error."
description: """h3. Summary    Searching for a Space with name that has numbers at the beginning will throw an error in CQL.  h3. Environment    Confluence 6.9.3  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create spaces with names: 1TES, 1, TEST, T1E, TE1   # In terminal execute command to retrive CQL results:      h3. Expected Results    Result will be retrived, like in this case where name is TEST    h3. Actual Results    h3. Notes    Other combinations work (1, T1E, TEST, TE1)  h3. Resolution    Put quotes around the space key for CQL to consider it a string. e.g. instead of space=1TES, use space=""""1TES"""""""
comments: "Why has this been prioritised as low?  A basic functionality (create a space with a name which contains only allowed characters) breaks another basic functionality (searching for content).  Shouldn't this be a high prio issue?" [SEP] "I agree. Prio should be higher than LOW." [SEP] "This is due to CQL not seeing something like 1TES as a string. Instead it is seen as a number followed by something unknown. It is only for convenience that we can write something like space=TES and it works, but the better way to do it would be to surround it in double quotes, i.e. space=""1TES"" and then it is sure to work. Hope this helps all of you!    Regards,  <USER> Senior Team Lead, Confluence Server  Atlassian"

summary: "Ability to disable the share page with groups feature"
description: """Currently, the [Page Sharing|https://confluence.<USER>doc/share-a-page-or-blog-post-16482511.html] option in Confluence is controlled by the Share Page add-on {{Manage add-ons > System > Share Page}}, however, it doesn't seem to be possible to disable the sharing with users, groups or mail.     It would be great to split those sharing options, so if needed, we can disabled them individually. """
comments: "For us, the issue is allowing page sharing with groups.  We need the ability to disable that functionality based on the following scenario:    If Confluence is sync'd with an LDAP server such as Active Directory, mailing lists are pulled into Confluence as groups, with the members of the group being the emails that are part of the mailing list.  AND, if a user shares a page (possibly unintentionally) with a group that represents a LARGE mailing list, they could end up spamming thousands of users.  Many of those users may not even use Confluence, and thus have no idea of the context, and that could result in a significant number reporting the email as spam and even a possible phishing scam.    Email servers can have restrictions in place to stop this type of wide-spread email, but this scenario in Confluence would bypass those restrictions because it sends thousands of emails to individual users, instead of one email to the mailing list that represents the group the page is being shared with.    So, at the

summary: "Changed By in Page History does not show all users who edited that version"
description: """There seems to be an issue with the *Changed By* in the Page History page.    h4. Issue summary  # When multiple users are editing a page, and someone saves the page, a new version is created, and we can see that version in the *Page History*.  # The Page History will also show the *Changed By*, which shows all the users who contributed to that version.  # The issue appears that in some version, certain users are missing from the *Changed By*, even-though you can see that their changes are there when we use the *Page History* to compare versions.    h4. Steps to reproduce  # We have been unable to replicate the issue in our test instance, but we are seeing the issue in our customer's production instance.  # 3(or more) users join a page to edit it.  # All 3 users add something, and one of them then publish the changes.  # A new version of the page is created, and we can see if in the *Page History*.  # Instead of showing all the contributors, one or two users might be missing from the list of *Changed By*.    h4. Expected result  # All contributors of the version should show up in the *Changed By*.    h4. Current result  # Some users are missing from the *Changed By*.   """

summary: "Create an option that makes the creation of a comment required when a page update is made"
description: """Please consider adding an option that requires the creation of a comment when a page is updated by a user.  Also, when enabled, ensure that this option can't be bypassed by any keyboard shortcuts (eg. ctrl+s)."""

summary: "Setting DEBUG on the root class of Confluence can cause the instance to crash"
description: """h3. Summary    Currently in Confluence Administation *Logging & Profiling*, we expose the {{root}} class logger at the bottom of the page. While this may be needed when certain scenarios exist, admins can change this setting easily from the UI without understanding the scale of the change they are making. We would ask that a warning flag is present or for the option to be removed and reserved for a manual change in {{log4j}} since changing this setting can cause Confluence to crash.  h3. Environment   * Confluence    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Set up a vanilla confluence instance   # Visit the Administration section > Logging & Profiling   # At the bottom of the page, set the {{root}} class to DEBUG and save    h3. Expected Results   # Confluence will log debug messages at the root level    h3. Actual Results   # Depending on size of instance and current load, this change can crash the instance.    h3. Workaround    Don't do this. """

summary: "Allow our REST API to retrieve who did a change in a space"
description: """Currently, Our [CQL feild|https://developer.<USER>server/confluence/cql-field-reference/] does not have a method to retrieve who make changes to a space exp: space title    The activity stream uses the public search API of Confluence which at this time don't supply the correct user when listing recently updated entries. We only get the user responsible for the latest change on the space homepage, which at the time of creation would be User A in our example, causing them to be listed.     Reference:   https://issues.refinedwiki.com/browse/RWOT-1201"""
comments: "For more information, see my question on the dev community here: [https://community.developer.<USER>t/get-last-modifier-of-space-details-via-cql/23686.]         Basically we use the following CQL:               on the public search API *_/rest/api/search_*    with the following expanded in the expand field: _content.space,content.history.lastUpdated,content.history.previousVersion,space.homepage.history_         Our issue is that entities with contentType: ""space"" returned from this API doesn't contain any reference to lastUpdated such as the other contentTypes does. There's seemingly no expand params to use to access this information either. As such, there's no way of getting the information on who changes a space last through CQL. "

summary: "Standard header for Word Exports"
description: """It would be amazing if the Word export out of the box included a standard header or footer.  Many standard exports include the following:    | [Page #] | [Date] | [Source] |    This would help my team determine when they exported the document for comparison purposes, the page to reference when in meetings that don't have access to our instance, and the source for allowing others to know where the data was pulled from, even if it was just the parent instance level URL."""

summary: "Creating two new lines after code macro will render only one line"
description:
"""h3. Summary Creating two new lines after code macro will render only one line in the preview of the page. With 3 lines it will render 2. h3. Environment - Confluence 6.10.2 h3. Steps to Reproduce # Create code block macro, and add some text. # Create 2 empty lines (with enter) after macro. # On the third line write TEST. # Publish/Save page. h3. Expected Results - Using development tools of browser we can see only 2 """"spacer"""" element (

) is between TEST and code macro. h3. Actual Results - Using development tools of browser we can see only 1 """"spacer"""" element (

) is between TEST and code macro."""
comments:
"Cannot reproduce on bulldog (tinymce 4.7.11 + collab editing) or EAC (3.4.9-atlassian-11 + collab editing). Stripping empty paragraphs sounds like a feature (where any number of empty paragraphs are treated as a single paragraph), so it's surprising when 3 paragraphs are rendered as 2 in the bug description. Perhaps this only occurs when collab editing is disabled?" [SEP] "Confluence strips empty paragraphs with {{auto-cursor-target}} class on them: Such elements are not rendered in view page / preview, unlike regular empty paragraphs which are preserved. Auto-cursor-targets are added before and after the macro automatically but only in case there's no other space already. Although I cannot reproduce this issue using steps from the description, I can assume that there's one auto-cursor-target in the page and the other one is an empty paragraph without any class ({{


}}), resulting in described behaviour. We can make this more consistent by collapsing all consecutive empty paragraphs regardless

summary: "Tables generated by macros and have two <thead> HTML tags are not sortable when they are added to Expand Macro"
description: """h3. Summary  Tables that are generated by macros and have <thead> HTML tag are not sortable when they are added to to Expand macro.     This issue seems to be related to [CONFSERVER-55253|https://jira.<USER>browse/CONFSERVER-55253] where tables generated by the Page Property Report macro are not sortable when added to the expand macro. However, it seems that this issue is more common and happens to other tables that are generated by macro.     Another example of this is Content Report Table. A table that is generated by this macro and placed inside the Expand macro will not be sortable.    A common characteristic of both of the above tables are that they   * Have two <thead> tags where the first is wrapped around the header *and* the  second <thead> tag is wrapped around the first element of the table from <tbody>.   * The table macros above when not added to an expand macro will only have one <thead> tag and it is wrapped around the header of the table only      h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Create a new Confluence page  # Add a PPR macro or Content Report Table.   # Place these macros inside the Expand macro  # Attempt to sort the table    h3. Expected Results  The tables are sortable    h3. Actual Results  The tables are not sortable   """
comments: "This bug affects Table Filter and Charts app with over 6000 customers. Please, fix it." [SEP] "This bug affects Smart Table. Can you please fix it? Shouldn't be something big."

summary: "REST API needs ability to remove specific versions of a page"
description: """h3. Problem statement  The REST API methods in our [documentation|https://docs.<USER>ConfluenceServer/rest/6.11.1/] doesn't have a method for deleting a specific version of a piece of content.    If we run this command to remove an older version of a page, it returns 404 although the page exists and I have permission to delete it.      If we change the ID to the latest version of the page, then the content is removed.    h3. Suggestion  Provide a new method for removing specific versions of a content or modify the existing one so that it doesn't return a 404 for a valid content ID."""
comments: "This also affects our Confluence instance with more than 90.000 users.    We could not agree more with this request.    Why does the method return 404 for valid content ids?    It is such a big housekeeping use-case to delete old versions (and not the newest) of content.(e.g. older than x years getting easily from the database)    Would be really great if this does not return 404 for valid content id's:  [https://docs.<USER>ConfluenceServer/rest/6.11.1/#content-delete]     "

summary: "Log spams ""mIMPORT Base URL is unknown"" WARN messages when a mail notification is sent"
description: """h3. Summary    The class """"web.csskit.antlr.CSSLexer"""" spams a WARN message in the Confluence logs when a mail notification is sent.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Install a fresh Confluence instance  # Create two users with valid email addresses  # Configure a [Mail Server|https://confluence.<USER>confkb/how-do-i-configure-confluence-to-use-gmail-as-the-mail-server-42795030.html]  # Log in as User1, create a page and mention User2 on it, in order to trigger a mail notification    h3. Expected Results    An email is sent successfully without any warnings in the logs.    h3. Actual Results    The email is sent successfully, but the following messages are shown in the {{atlassian-confluence.log}}:         h3. Notes    * Other scenarios that trigger a mail notification would be enough to reproduce the issue.  * The messages can appear at regular intervals depending on the frequency of the Scheduled Job that flushes the mail queue.    h3.Workaround    Although there is no workaround, the WARN messages are harmless and can be safely ignored.    If the warnings needs to be suppressed, the following package can be configured to have a [logging level|https://confluence.<USER>doc/configuring-logging-181535215.html] of *ERROR*.    """
comments: "Thanks for finding out...    I experienced the same and had no clue, where these message originate from. My Log files are cluttered with those messages." [SEP] "How is it not possible to suppress this in the logging settings?" [SEP] "A fix for this issue is available to Server and Data Center customers in Confluence 6.13.0  [Upgrade|https://www.<USER>software/confluence/download?utm_source=confluence_bugfix&utm_campaign=confluence_bugfix&utm_medium=email] now or check out the [Release Notes|https://confluence.<USER>doc/confluence-6-13-release-notes-959288785.html?utm_source=confluence_bugfix&utm_campaign=confluence_bugfix&utm_medium=email] to see what other issues are resolved."

Step to reproduce  # a blank new space I'm create a new page 'A'. This page is basically empty.   # I'm adding a new child page 'A.1' which contains a """"Children Display"""" macro only.  # After that I'm adding a new child page 'A.1.I' which contains no content (blank page).  # Setting viewing and editing restrictions to page A.1 which allows access only to my account.  # Editing page A.1 by adding some text.    h4. WARN in *atlassian-confluence.log*      h4. esult  The WARN message is thrown in the atlassian-confluence.log    h4. Expected  No WARN message."""

summary: "Question not removed from Questions List macro after label is removed from question"
description: """h4. Issue summary:    When a label is removed from a question that has a comment, or an answer, the question still appears on the """"Questions List"""" macro that matches the label that's been removed.  h4. Steps to reproduce:  # Install Questions for Confluence Plugin.  # Create a question, and give it at least 2 labels. (Example: Label1, Label2)  # Now, add a comment, or an """"Answer"""" to the question. (Issue does not occur when there are no comment or """"Answer"""" in the question)  # In a new page, add the """"Questions List"""" macro, and add one of the label to filter. (Example: Label2)  # Now go and remove the label, """"Label2"""" from the question.  ## *Note:* We need to *edit* the question and remove the label, and not remove the label directly by clicking on the *Pencil* icon.  ## Issue does not occur if we remove the label using the *Pencil* icon, without *Editing* the question.  # You will see that the question still shows up in the """"Questions List"""" macro.    h4. Expected behavior   # Question gets removed from the """"Questions List"""" macro when the matching label is removed.    h4. Current behavior   # Question does not get removed from the """"Questions List"""" macro when the matching label is removed.    h4. Workaround  # Use the *Pencil* icon to remove the label in the fist place, without editing the question.  # If the question is edited and removed, add back the label and remove it again using the *Pencil* icon.  # Clear the plugin cache and re-index Confluence from scratch.     """
comments: "Thanks"
