summary: """Content by label"" with Rich Content prevents page loading"
description: """h3. Summary    A page with the """"Content by label"""" macro, combined with an Excerpt Display option of """"<USER>content"""" will prevent a page from loading given enough pages with the label. Rather than the stack overflow seen in CONFSERVER-40154 the browser displays a blank page whilst it tries to load.  h3. Environment    Confirmed in Confluence 6.4.2 and 6.6.7  Content by Label macro with Excerpt Display set to <USER>Content  h3. Steps to Reproduce   * Create a page (Test Parent Page for example) that contains the Content by Label macro. Click edit and set """"Excerpt Display"""" to <USER>Content. Add a label to this page e.g. """"test""""   * Create child pages that all contain the Content by Label macro with the rich content option set and the test label (In testing, performance starts to noticeably decrease with 4 child pages. The more pages you add in this configuration the longer the loading takes)   * Attempt to load any of the pages    h3. Expected Results    Page renders in a reasonable amount of time or an error is logged/displayed  h3. Actual Results    Nothing logged whilst page is being loaded, however, if you keep your page count low whilst still triggering the issue, you get a stack like this just before the load eventually completes. If you have too many pages, you may never see the stack as it seems to indicate the page is about to display:  """

summary: "Have an available sorting mechanism for the recentlyViewedSpaces() CQL in REST API"
description: """h3. Summary    Currently, we can sort or order the response of recentlyViewedSpaces() by fields available in the response. However, it would be great if we could order the response by timestamp of user viewed either asc or desc :    Sample REST API  """
comments: "Hi,    Thank you for raising the feature request. It might be that the APIs are aligned but for clarification I needed this feature on Confluence Server, so maybe it would be best to move the ticket to that project?     I'll also include my additional information from the DEVHELP ticket:  {quote}As an interesting side note, you can specify the limit of the response to 1 =>   ""space in recentlyViewedSpaces(1) and type = space”   This will fetch the correct space (the last viewed space). but as soon as you get more the order seems lost. So somehow the system will know which is the most recent one internally, it’s just not exposed through the API. Similarly the internal API used to populate the spaces dropdown in the global menu (/rest/ia/1.0/spacesmenu) does return the list sorted in the correct order.  {quote}  Best regards,    <USER>" [SEP] "Hi [~<USER>,    Thanks for the heads up! Moved the ticket to Confluence server.    Cheers,  <USER>

summary: "Store Confluence plugins in the file system instead of database"
description: """Storing Confluence plugins in the database makes database dumps painful, due to the size of the table. All the other applications - as far as we're aware - store their plugin JAR data in the filesystem.    Is there some justification for storing Confluence's plugins in the database and then pulling them down to the file system on startup?"""

summary: "Large XML Imports in Confluence Data Center Can Fail Due to Hazelcast"
description: """h2. Problem    When importing large spaces in Confluence, while running multiple nodes, imports can fail with the below error:    h2. Workaround    Bring down any additional nodes until just 1 node is running.  Run the import.  It should succeed now."""
comments: "Hi,    This bug describes symptoms, but not the root cause of the problem.    One of the root causes was fixed in Hazelcast 3.7: [https://github.com/hazelcast/hazelcast/issues/8730]    Hazelcast was upgraded from 3.5.2 to 3.8.6 in Confluence 6.6.6, 6.8.0 and 6.9.1, so this root cause was eliminated. But Hazelcast could have other bugs with the same symptoms, so if you experience this issue in Confluence with upgraded Hazelcast, please feel free to post details about the your errors here.    Best regards,   <USER> [SEP] "@George,         We are facing same issue in Confluence 6.6.11 DC. Can you helps us?         Log printing      " [SEP] "Hi [~<USER>, thank you for noticing it.    Can you please raise a support ticket request and provide your support zip file (and some details about when it happens).    Thank you! "

Steps to Reproduce  # Install Confluence 6.10.1 (using Installer .exe)  # Create a new space  # Uncheck the 'Favourite' for the space  # Create a page and insert """"Space List"""" macro  # Save the page    h3. Notes  HAR file for the page with 'Space list' macro is attached   [^test.har]     h3.Workaround  * Download an older version of Confluence (Zipped version)  * Unzip the binary  * Go to atlassian-confluence-version-number/confluence/images/icons/emoticons/  * Copy star_grey.png and star_grey.svg file into your binary i.e. atlassian-confluence-6.10.1/confluence/images/icons/emoticons/  * There is no need to start/stop Confluence for this. """

summary: "Slow performance for non-administrator users on embedded database"
description: """h3. Summary  Users who are not in the confluence-administrators group experience 20-60 second page loads when using the H2 embedded database without a connection to the internet.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Install Confluence on the embedded database  # Go to !https://confluence.<USER>doc/files/590259974/590058056/1/1398739558493/Cog.png! > *User Management* and create a new non-admin user (""""user2, in this example"""")  # Turn off any network access on the application server.  # Attempt to log in as user2 and navigate Confluence.    h3. Expected Results  user2 will be able to navigate Confluence normally with no delay.    h3. Actual Results  user2 experiences 20-60 second load times on initial login and every subsequent page load.     h3. Notes  * This only affects non-admin users on a server without internet or with certain network configurations (see final note in this list). user2 can navigate normally if either of the following occurs:  ** user2 is added to the confluence-administrators group  ** reliable internet connection is established to the server  * Behavior appears tied to the confluence-administrators group, not the permissions; user2 is still affected when """"Confluence Administrator"""" and """"<USER>""" permissions are added to the confluence-users group.  * This issue was also reproduced in instances with an internet connection. In one instance, the issue was reproduced when connected via VPN (though it functioned normally with the VPN disabled). The speeds were not slower on the VPN connection, and it's not clear what factor in the VPN connection caused the slowness.    h3.Workarounds  * Establish an internet connection on the server.  * Add all users to the confluence-administrators group during evaluation.  * Install confluence on a [supported database|https://confluence.<USER>doc/supported-platforms-207488198.html]."""
comments: "I have experienced this on local development Confluence instances 6.6 through 6.15 (haven't test 6.5). I'm not using the Atlassian SDK to start them, just a tomcat instance started via Codehaus Cargo that I deploy the confluence webapp to.     I think the network access is a red herring, as all of my instances have been internet connected.    In 6.7 and later, you can get deadlock errors. These may be distinct from the slowdown, as that still happens in 6.6, without the deadlock.        The example above has 24 threads, but this happens even with the default 3 threads.    Notably, if the instance stays up for a couple of hours, the problem seems to go away. That may be because the instance has some time to build some indexes & caches which prevent database queries, which create the problem."

summary: "Exceeding license limit should not render the server Read-Only for Data Center"
description: """h3. Problem Definition:    Data Center customers have an expectation of availability for their server instances. Often in these large data center environments, users are programmatically created or provisioned from directory systems. However, when the license count is exceeded, users can still continually be created. This is a problem, because once the license count is exceeded the server becomes Read-Only and is no longer available for any real use. This action negates the feature of high availability, since no work can be done on it.  h3. Suggested Solution:    No longer allow the creation of any new users that exceed the license limit, and keep the server from being made Read-Only.  h3. Why this is needed:    Keeping the license count from being exceeded in order to maintain high availability of the server for all existing users requires constant vigilance for some teams."""

summary: "New Macro Button to Add / Remove Labels to a Page (Applications within Confluence)"
description: """I have a comment and some votes on a page about the Create from Template button and labels. But what I am proposing is so easy for you to do but also so powerful that I decided to create a ticket for it to pitch the idea to you.     I'd like to see the ability to have a configurable button on a Confluence page that you can have the option to only add a label to the page, only remove a label to a page or choose that it toggles the said label on/off of the page.          This functionality would allow for Application like functionality within Confluence. Here is a few examples below.          Case Scenario 1:  I have a page that I use the create from template button to make pages that are storyboards for eLearning training development lessons. The new storyboard page inherits the labels from my template page. I have a work flow main page based on labels and what labels are on the storyboard pages. I am using the macro Content by label to show the storyboards in each state. To advance a storyboard from planned, to in production, to editing, <USER> I simply remove a label to move it from one step in the work flow to the next. With buttons that can add or remove labels then I can have a toolbar in my page template to move a page through the workflow. This would be powerful for the end user.          Case Scenario 2:  I have a knowledge base page with a create from template button to create a new how-to article. The new pages inherit the label """"draft"""" from my template page. I now have draft how-to articles that myself and my tech writer can vet out and polish up and then remove the draft label and consider the page as production.          Theoretical Case Scenarios: Using the concept I am employing above you can have a page for """"ToDo"""". You can create your tasks for yourself and merely press a button on that tasks page as """"Done"""" and you're done. That main page could easily be copied and setup for other people for their own task list without much explanation of what labels do what for the status of your task list. Also it would be customization for power users obviously.     Also, you could have a team of people creating new projects and then have them go through a work flow to completion. End users would have to just click a button on a page that is in plain English. This would be such an amazing and powerful feature for your company to have. Companies don't want to have to purchase very expensive line of business applications for the various project management or process management that they do. For most uses this would serve the purpose needed.          A complementary macro to the button or perhaps the macro could have an alternate option of a drop down that could be configurable so you can present 3 labels (statuses) for your particular workflow to your end users.         You guys have nothing to lose and everything to gain from this. Can I get it next week? : )"""
comments: "Some people were asking for this feature along with me on these other tickets but these other tickets are really for Create from Template feature to have the option to add a different label than what is being inherited from the template. (Which needs to be done too FYI because we that will make our templates for multiple uses).  https://jira.<USER>browse/CONFSERVER-30716  https://jira.<USER>browse/CONFSERVER-29581"

summary: "Confluence Mail Archiving Plugin is recording attachments from a random user and inadvertently sending notifications that they made an attachment (that they didn't really make)"
description: """h2. Summary of Issue    When the *Confluence Mail Archiving Plugin* is creating pages from Emails, it is:    * Recording attachments by a random user (i.e. that user is not the email sender)  * Users watching the Space are then sent an email notification that an attachment was added to a page (however, a notification was never sent about the original page created from the email so we shouldn't get an email notification from the attachments processing)      h2. How to reproduce    # Install fresh Confluence 6.9.1  # Add two additional users outside of the Admin account with valid external email addresses (e.g. USER1, USER2)  # Login as USER1 and USER2 and watch the Space that the Confluence Mail Archiving will be enabled on (e.g. Product Development Space)  # To activate the Email Archive Add-on:  #* Enable *Confluence Mail Archiving Plugin* system add-on in *Confluence Administration* » *Add-Ons* (click Show All)  # Navigate to *Space Administration* of the Product Development space » *Integration* » Setup a new *Pop* or *Imap* to your incoming Mail Box  # Send an email to the Pop server _with an attachment_  # Wait for the email to be processed into Confluence and check the _Email turned into a Page_  # In my local instance:  #* I will only see attachments attached by Anonymous  #* Confluence notifies all users watching the Space of an _attachment made to the Email Generated Page_ (but Confluence did not send an email when the Email was converted to a Page in the first place)    h2. Investigation conducted    # Attachments by the *Confluence Mail Archiving Plugin* are added to the Confluence page as such:  {quote}      {quote}  #* So in summary, the creator of the attachment is set to the *{{AuthenticatedUserThreadLocal.get()}}* - it should be set to the current login user  # However, as it is a """"scheduled job"""" that polls for emails - there is no current user on the """"Scheduled job"""" thread *unless something is setting it*    Suspicion is that a third party add-on is setting a user into *AuthenticatedUserThreadLocal* so when the scheduler thread retrieves the User, it is pulling back a random user each time.    Add-on responsible for setting the *AuthenticatedUserThreadLocal* at this stage has been unconfirmed.    h2. Expected results    # The attachments should either be Anonymous or at least match the Email Sender  # As we don't send email notifications when the Email is converted to a Confluence page, Confluence shouldn't be sending an email notification that emails were added to the page"""

summary: "Modifying Additional User Details on User Profile Page Triggers Application Permission Validation  "
description: """h3. Summary  Currently, Confluence and Jira will only synchronize the main user attributes, such as user full name, email address, usernames, <USER> Other additional user attributes, such as user's phone number, position, department and location will not be synced over to Confluence and Jira accordingly, and vice versa.     As the additional user attributes are not synced over from LDAP, specifically in Confluence, users are able to add or modify their additional user attributes manually from the _User Profile_ page. This data would then be stored in Confluence's Database - {{os_propertyentry}} table in specific, whereas the synced user attributes/details synchronized over from LDAP is stored in the tables prefixes with {{cwd}}, e.g. {{cwd_user}} table.      h3. Steps to Reproduce  # First, set up an LDAP User Directory in Jira *with _Read Only_* settings.  # Then in Confluence, please set up a Jira User Directory  (i) Both Jira and Confluence users would be pulled from LDAP  # Once you've done this, please try to modify your phone number *only* from the _User Profile_ page in Confluence and save the changes.    h3. Expected Results  By right Confluence should then allow the user to edit their additional user attributes from the _User Profile_ page as this data is managed locally in Confluence and will not be synced over to other external user directories (LDAP or Jira).     h3. Actual Results  Modifying only an additional user attribute (e.g. phone number) from the _User Profile_ page, Confluence will actually  perform the LDAP application permission validation which then will then cause the _APPLICATION_PERMISSION_DENIED_ that we're seeing as LDAP user directory is configured to _Read Only_. By right, on additional user attribute edit Confluence does not have to validate the LDAP directory again as the additional user attribute is stored locally. It should only do this when the user modify their full name or email address from the _User Profile_ page.    The below exception is thrown in the {{<Confluence-Home>/logs/atlassian-confluence.log}} file:      With the following error message thrown in the Confluence UI:   !Screen Shot 2018-09-19 at 6.08.01 PM.png|thumbnail! """

summary: "Jira Issues Macro Displaying Number is Incorrect After Limiting the Number of Issues in Jira"
description: """h3. Summary    The Jira Issues Macro displays the incorrect number of issues that the macro shows when we set a *jira.search.views.max.limit* in Jira. The limit works, but the macro shows a message like this:  {quote}*Showing 166 out of 139 issues*  {quote} * In this case, 166 is the amount set in the *Maximum issues* field of the Jira Issues Macro in Confluence    h3. Environment   * Jira 7.10.1   * Confluence 6   * Application links properly configured between them    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Have an application links working between Confluence and Jira   # In the Jira Home Directory, create a file called *jira-config.properties* files (or edit it in case it already exists)   # Add the following properties there:   ** jira.search.views.default.max = 5   ** jira.search.views.max.limit = 5   # Now restart Jira   # Create a page in Confluence with the Jira issues macro in there   # Set a filter that will retrieve more than 5 issues from Jira   # Set the *Maximum issues* to any number between 1 and 1000   # Save the macro and access the page    h3. Expected Results    The Jira Issues Macro will show only 5 issues, which is the max limit number we set in Jira.  h3. Actual Results    The Jira Issues Macro shows the *Maximum issues* number we set in the macro, but it actually only shows the limit of issues we set (5 in this case), which means that the number that the macro says that is showing is wrong.    Here's a screenshot with the limit set to 5 in Jira, and the *Maximum issues* set to 50 and 166 in the Jira Issues Macro. Note that we still have only 5 issues showing there, which is expected:     !bugJiraIssues.png|thumbnail!"""

summary: "Page Properties Report - Create option to include pages without properties in the report"
description: """Use case:    Eventually one may want to introduce properties on demand to a selection of pages and perhaps replace existing instances of the """"Content Report Table"""" or """"Content by Label"""" macro by the """"Page Properties Report"""" macro.    The problem for this use case is that the """"Page Properties Report"""" will only show pages matching the selection criteria that contain the """"Page Properties"""" macro, which makes an on demand switch impossible, and requires the user to add the properties macro to all pages to be listed.    The idea is to add an option to the """"Page Properties Report"""" macro that will allow the inclusion of pages without properties to be listed as well, being shown with empty property columns."""
comments: "Thank you for providing valuable feedback to our team. At this stage, we do not have plans to address this suggestion. We are closing this as it has not received as much interest/feedback as other suggested feature improvements.    Thanks  Makisa | Confluence Server and Data Center"

summary: "Clicking View next to attachments opens in page rather than in Preview"
description: """h3. Summary    Clicking *view* from the attachments summary page opens attachment in a Confluence page rather than in a preview light box.    It can be very confusing especially if the user is attempting to edit an office document with Companion App.  h3. Environment   * Confluence Server 6.11.0 or 6.11.1    h3. Steps to Reproduce    Navigate to page containing attachment   Select *. . . > Attachments*   Click *View* next to your desired attachment.    !Screen Shot 2018-09-17 at 1.02.05 PM.png|thumbnail!  h3. Expected Results    !Screen Shot 2018-09-17 at 1.00.47 PM.png|thumbnail!  h3. Actual Results    !Screen Shot 2018-09-17 at 1.00.34 PM.png|thumbnail!  h3. Notes    Confirmed it does work this way in the Cloud version. Is this what is also intended for Server?  h3. Workaround    Navigate to the page itself, insert the Attachments macro and click *Preview* from there, or click on the thumbnail of the document already attached on a page.    Or upgrade to Confluence 6.12 or newer and click on the filename instead of the """"View"""" link."""
comments: "We worked on this as part of some improvements we made in Confluence 6.12.0 which will be released very soon :)    In Confluence 6.12.0 clicking on the View link will still open the file as shown here. But clicking on the file name will open the file in the file previewer." [SEP] "A fix for this issue is available to Server and Data Center customers in Confluence 6.12.0  [Upgrade|https://www.<USER>software/confluence/download?utm_source=confluence_bugfix&utm_campaign=confluence_bugfix&utm_medium=email] now or check out the [Release Notes|https://confluence.<USER>doc/confluence-6-12-release-notes-957143056.html?utm_source=confluence_bugfix&utm_campaign=confluence_bugfix&utm_medium=email] to see what other issues are resolved."

summary: "User @ mention pulls old entries from browser cache "
description: """h3. Summary    Old deleted users are listed with @ mention selection menu after 2 characters typed, even users from previous instances  h3. Environment   * Chrome 69.0   * Firefox 62.0   * Safari 11.1   * Confluence 6.5.1    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a Test instace running Confluence 6.5.1   # Add Several Users to instace and test if you can @ mention them   # Delete most of those users   # Try to @ mention deleted users    h3. Expected Results   # Deleted users no longer appear in @ mention menu    h3. Actual Results   * Deleted users will be listed when only 2 letters after @ mention are used, even users that are not matching the letters   !one user.png|thumbnail!  !old users 2.png|thumbnail!   * At the same time, newly added users might not get listed at all   * Using more than 2 characters after @ might return correct existing users   * Clearing Confluence plug-ins cache and rebuilding indexes from scratch does not fix the issue   * Deleting entire test Confluence 6.5.1 instace and creating a new Confluence 6.5.1 with no users will still result in old users from the previous instace being listed in @ mention    h3. Workaround    The issue is not observed when using the *Incognito/Safe/In Private* mode of a browser or after clearing entire browser cache and history, clearing cookies has no impact on the problem  The issue is not observed on Confluence 6.6.*"""

summary: "Blogposts are listed and sorted based on the blog's draft creation time and not publish time"
description: """h3. Summary    If you publish a blogpost which has been created and kept as a draft for a very long time, it won't appear at the top of the list of """"recent blogs"""". This is because it's """"creationDate"""" is marked as the date/time you first *drafted* the blog and not the date/time you *published* the blog.    This results in the blogpost to be near the bottom of the """"Recent blogposts"""" list if a large number of blogposts have been published since that """"creationDate"""".    In the {{content}} table, you will also see the the """"creationDate"""" column for the blog stores the time you first """"created"""" the draft and not the time you published the blog.  h3. How to replicate   # Make sure Collaborative Editing is enabled.   # Create a blog post called """"Blog 1"""".   # Add some content.   # Close the editor and save it as a draft.   # Wait a couple of minutes.   # Create another blog post and publish it as """"Blog 2"""".   # Create another blog post and publish it as """"Blog 3"""".   # Wait a couple of minutes.   # Go back to the """"Blog 1"""" draft and publish it.    h3. Expected results    On {{<base-URL>/pages/viewrecentblogposts.action?key=<spacekey>}}, we should see the blogposts in this order (top to bottom):   * Blog 1   * Blog 3   * Blog 2    h3. Actual results    The blogposts are instead listed in this order (top to bottom):   * Blog 3   * Blog 2   * Blog 1    h3. Note    The behaviour persists only if Collaborative Editing is enabled. The results underneath """"Expected results"""" occurs when Collaborative Editing is disabled."""

summary: "Valid drafts are being removed as a stale draft when we restart Collaborative Editing"
description: """h3. Summary  Based on the [removeStaleSharedDrafts class|https://docs.<USER>atlassian-confluence/6.2.0/com/atlassian/confluence/pages/PageManager.html#removeStaleSharedDrafts--], a stale draft is defined as:  {quote}Pages with a {{content_status}} '*draft*' and which have a last modified date that is earlier than or equal to the last modified date on the published page.{quote}  However, it is possible that a draft is marked as stale only by definition but in theory, it is still a valid draft. Refer to the 'Steps to reproduce' section for more information.    h3. Steps to reproduce  # Create a new page and insert a task to the page.  # Publish the page.  # Edit the page again to add some text, then close the editor while opting to save it as a draft.  # Run the query in [How to manually remove Stale Drafts from Confluence Database|https://confluence.<USER>confkb/how-to-manually-remove-stale-drafts-from-confluence-database-951392345.html] to confirm that a stale draft doesn't exist yet in the DB.     # On the page in 'View' mode, <USER>the task as completed. This creates a new version of the page without the draft being published.  # Run the query in step #4 again and we will see that there is one stale draft.  # Set Collaborative Editing to 'Off' or 'Limited' and then to 'On' to trigger the stale draft removal process.    h3. Expected results  The draft should not be removed despite it fitting the definition/criteria of a stale draft.    h3. Actual results  The draft is removed because the draft's {{lastmoddate}} is earlier than the page's {{lastmoddate}}, which fits the definition of a stale draft.    h3. Workaround  Before turning Collaborative Editing ON you can retrieve information from possibly affected pages by running the query below.  With that information, Confluence administrators will be able to share the draft content if a user asks for it.    This query will list the following attributes:   * Draft ID   * Draft last modify date   * Draft storage format   * Current version pageID   * Link to the current page   * Creator username   * Last modifier username   * Last modify date    {code:language=SQL}  SELECT  DISTINCT(d.contentid) AS DRAFT_ID, d.lastmoddate AS DRAFT_LAST_MOD_DATE, bd.body AS DRAFT_STORAGE_FORMAT, c.contentid AS CURRENT_VERSION_ID, '<Confluence Base URL>/viewpage.action?pageId='||c.contentid AS CURRENT_VERSION_LINK, umc.lower_username AS CURRENT_VERSION_CREATOR, umcm.lower_username AS CURRENT_VERSION_LAST_MODIFIER, c.lastmoddate AS CURRENT_VERSION_LASTMODDATE  FROM CONTENT d  JOIN CONTENT c ON d.prevver = c.contentid  JOIN """"AO_BAF3AA_AOINLINE_TASK"""" i ON d.prevver = i.""""CONTENT_ID""""  JOIN BODYCONTENT bd ON bd.contentid = d.contentid  JOIN USER_MAPPING umc ON umc.user_key = c.creator  JOIN USER_MAPPING umcm ON umcm.user_key = c.lastmodifier  WHERE d.content_status = 'draft'    AND d.prevver IS NOT NULL    AND c.lastmoddate > d.lastmoddate    AND (i.""""COMPLETE_DATE"""" > d.lastmoddate OR i.""""UPDATE_DATE"""" > d.lastmoddate)  ;  {code}"""

summary: "Default 'Description' field is not marked as required in the Jira issue creation dialog in Confluence"
description: """h4. Summary    When the default 'Description' field in Jira is marked as 'Required', Confluence does not show an asterisk symbol in the issue creation dialog when trying to create an issue by highlighting words.    If we proceed with the issue creation with this field blank, it uses the value of the 'Summary' field as the description.  h4. How to replicate   # Create an application link between Jira/Confluence.   # In Jira, <USER>the default 'Description' field as required.   # Confirm that creating an issue in Jira shows the asterisk * symbol next to the 'Description' field.     !Jira issue creation 2.png|thumbnail!    # In a Confluence page, highlight some words and choose to create a Jira issue from it.    h4. Expected results   * The 'Description' field should appear and be marked as a required field using an asterisk.   * If the field was left blank, it should also prevent the issue from being created and prompt an error message.    h4. Actual results   * The 'Description' field is not marked as required.   * If the field was left blank, the issue creation goes through and it uses the value of the 'Summary' field as the description.     !Jira issue creation 3.png|thumbnail!     h4. Workaround  If needed, use a custom field called 'Description' to replace the default one. If the custom field is marked as required, it will be reflected as such in the issue creation dialog in Confluence.     !Jira issue creation.png|thumbnail!  !Jira issue creation 4.png|thumbnail! """

summary: "Adding Last-Modified to the response header information"
description: """Adding [Last-Modified information to request header|https://www.w3.org/Protocols/rfc2616/rfc2616-sec14.html#sec14.29] will ensure that search engine fetches the correct information.    If this header is not there, search engines (the crawler) will get the entire page to check, whether or not the page has changed. This produces a lot of load on the instance (avoidable traffic), which is not really necessary if the last-modified header is there."""

summary: "Changing the page restrictions from the UI should not compulsorily add yourself to the restriction"
description: """h2. Current Scenario  When any user is managing the [Page Restrictions|https://confluence.<USER>doc/page-restrictions-139414.html] from the web UI they are forcibly included in the page restriction before making any modification.    See the example below:   * Logged user: admin002   * Current page restrictions:    !page_restriction_img008.png|thumbnail!    * *admin002* clicks on the padlock in the page view mode.    !page_restriction_img009.png|thumbnail!    * *admin002* is compulsorily added to the restrictions of that page and cannot remove himself.    !page_restriction_img010.png|thumbnail!     This may be fine for regular users of Confluence, but should not be applied to administrators that are part of the [confluence-administrators group|https://confluence.<USER>doc/confluence-groups-for-administrators-138706.html].    Consider the following example:  * *admin002* is hired as a Confluence administrator and added to {{confluence-administrators}} group.  * There is a Space which is opened to every user.   ** Within this Space, there is a *page* that is restricted to a few users.    !page_restriction_img001.png|thumbnail!   * *admin002* receives a request to add *user001* as an editor of the above page.   ** *admin002* should not be an editor of this page, but will have access to it, since he is included in the {{confluence-administrators}} group.  * While adding *user001* as and editor of that page, restrictions to *admin002* is automatically added too.   ** *admin002* can't remove himself from the restrictions.   !page_restriction_img002.png|thumbnail! !page_restriction_img003.png|thumbnail!   * *admin002* moves to another role within the organization and is removed from the {{confluence-administrators}} group.   ** Because of the previous task, *admin002* has access to a page he shouldn't have.   !page_restriction_img006.png|thumbnail!   * *admin* discovers about the above and removes *admin002* access to that page.  * Now *admin* has direct access to the restricted page.  !page_restriction_img007.png|thumbnail!     In the above example, the Confluence administrator should have the option to change the page restrictions without being forcibly added to it.    The same problem occurs if trying the experimental REST API method {{/rest/experimental/content/<page ID>/restriction/byOperation/read/user?userName=<user name>}} as a Confluence administrator, but without direct access to the content, then you cannot change the restrictions.  See the REST API error below.      This also doesn't take into consideration if the user performing a change on the page restriction is part of a group that is already added in the restriction.  Consider the following example:   * *admin002* is part of group {{confluence-users}}.   * {{confluence-users}} has a view/edit restriction to a certain page.   !page_restriction_img011.png|thumbnail!   * To make any modification on the page restriction, *admin002* will be compulsorily added to it, without taking into consideration the group based restriction.   !page_restriction_img011.png|thumbnail!       h2. Expected Scenario  Based on the current scenario described above, this is what is expected as a Confluence design:   # If a user is part of the {{confluence-administrators}}, they should not be compulsorily added to the page restrictions when modifying it from the web UI.    #* In addition to this, it should not require a direct restriction to be in place when using the REST API.   # If a user is part of a group that is already in the page restrictions, the user shouldn't need a direct restriction to make modifications to it.    #* The same should apply to REST API calls, effective group restrictions should be taken into account.    h2. Workaround  There are two known workarounds to this issue, which are described below.    h4. Page Information  This only works if you need to remove a restriction. It doesn't work if you need to include one.  As Confluence or Space administrator do the following:   # Access the Page Information page.   !page_restriction_img013.png|thumbnail!   # In the _Page Permissions_ section, click on the padlock on the side of the restriction you want to remove.   !page_restriction_img014.png|thumbnail!    h4. JSON RPC API   While the REST API doesn't have all the necessary methods implemented, the deprecated RPC API is still bundled with Confluence.  Before using it, make sure [it is enabled|https://confluence.<USER>doc/enabling-the-remote-api-150460.html].  Consider using these two JSON-RPC API methods to change the restrictions of a certain page:   * {{getContentPermissionSet(String token, String contentId, String permissionType)}}  {quote}  returns the set of permissions on a page as a map of type to a list of ContentPermission, for the type of permission which is either ‘View’ or ‘Edit’  {quote}   * {{setContentPermissions(String token, String contentId, String permissionType, Vector permissions)}}  {quote}  sets the page-level permissions for a particular permission type (either ‘View’ or ‘Edit’) to the provided vector of ContentPermissions. If an empty list of permissions are passed, all page permissions for the given type are removed. If the existing list of permissions are passed, this method does nothing.  {quote}    Setting page restriction with method {{setContentPermissions}} does not require to explicitly add yourself in the restriction and can be made by the Confluence administrator."""

summary: """Upgrade completed successfully"" line missing when performing manual upgrade"
description: """h3. Summary    When performing a manual upgrade to Confluence version *6.6.8* from an earlier version, the line *Upgrade completed successfully* does not show up in the *atlassian-confluence.log* file. This can cause confusion as to whether or not the upgrade was successful as users will not see the setup wizard when performing a manual upgrade.    h3. Environment    * Confluence *6.6.8*     h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Install an earlier version of Confluence, such as *6.5.0*.  # Perform a [manual upgrade|https://confluence.<USER>conf66/upgrading-confluence-manually-943962904.html] to Confluence *6.6.8*    h3. Expected Results    After taking the manual upgrade steps and restarting Confluence, the following *info* line should show up in the *atlassian-confluence.log* file:        h3. Actual Results    The *Upgrade completed successfully* line is missing from the log files.     h3. Notes    This issue does not appear to affect the actual upgrade process but can be confusing for users that rely on this information to ensure that their upgrade was successful.    h3.Workaround    While the *Upgrade completed successfully* line is missing from the logs, the user should still be able to run the upgrade process without any major issues. Users should look for the following line in their logs:      After that, to ensure that their instance is on the new version, they will want to log into Confluence and check the footer of their page, which should show the following info:    !https://i.gyazo.com/aaca7466a96e9661834323f5519d5def.png!"""

Additionally, both 6.5.0 and 6.6.8 are ""Build 7502."" Perhaps this is why the app can't tell anything has been upgraded?     " [SEP] "Note: Also found that this message does not appear if the installer is used during the upgrade process." summary: "Unintended draft and uncommitted changes created with with Collaborative Editing"
description: """h2. Problem statement    When a user attempts to edit a page but then changes their mind (by hitting the back button or by closing a tab), now when another user attempts to edit the same page; they're unable to save their own change without saving the previous user's """"uncommitted"""" change. There doesn't appear to be any option to dismiss any previous pending/uncommitted changes and just apply your own i.e. it's an all or nothing situation    h2. Suggestion    1) To have a dialog pop-up to tell the user to save/discard their change upon closing the tab or pressing back while in edit mode (just like Facebook).  2) To have a dialog pop-up when entering edit mode to save/discard a previous change that was neither saved or discarded before being allowed to continue.  3) While editing a page, if another user attempts to edit that same page to have a dialog pop-up to grant/deny consensual editing of that page by the third party.  """

summary: "Let admins boot users from editing a specific page"
description: """h3. Problem Definition  If the maximum number of editors are editing a page, these users will block other users from editing the page indefinitely, as long as those users have the editor open. This can lock pages for an unreasonable amount of time if editors are left open (e.g. if a user leaves an editor open overnight).    (In addition, the session will not time out, as noted in CONFSERVER-56344 & [How to adjust the session timeout for Confluence|https://confluence.<USER>confkb/how-to-adjust-the-session-timeout-for-confluence-126910597.html])    h3. Suggested Solution  Give admins functionality to boot certain users from the editor.    This way, if a page is locked at the max editors for an extended period of time, and end user can contact the admin to boot a user from the page.     h3. Workaround  Restart Confluence to end all editor sessions."""

summary: "Native Linux Companion App"
description: """h3. Problem Definition  Since Confluence 6.11 the [Edit Files|https://confluence.<USER>conf613/edit-files-964960723.html] feature works with the [Atlassian Companion app|https://confluence.<USER>doc/atlassian-companion-app-release-notes-958455712.html].    The Companion App is available for Mac and Windows, but we don't have a release for Linux distributions.    h3. Suggested Solution  Provide a release of the Companion App to Linux distributions.    h3. Why this is important  Confluence users may choose to run their personal computers on Linux. This may be true for many developers, which might be the heavy users of Atlassian tools and Linux distributions.  The [Edit in Office|https://confluence.<USER>conf65/office-connector-prerequisites-939701141.html] functionality used to support Linux with Open Office.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # In Confluence Server, make sure the user timezone is not set / has been set to other than UTC  # Perform a space export  # Import the backup into Confluence Cloud  # Compare the """"Published"""" time in Page History between Server and Cloud    h3. summary: "Loading the ""Reorder Pages"" space tool page for very large spaces as a non site-admin user causes CPU spike"
description: """If you have a space that is large (tested and confirmed with a single space with 20,000 pages), going to the """"Reorder Pages"""" space tool page causes a sustained CPU spike. This can cause performance issues on the Confluence node that you're on.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   - Generate a space with 20,000 pages or more (testing notes are placed into an internal comment)   - As an site admin user, restrict view/edit of an arbitrary set of pages in the space   - Create a user with basic permissions   - In a new browser session, login to Confluence with this new user's credentials and go to the space and go to the Space Tools cog > """"Reorder Pages"""".   - The expected page tree won't be displayed on the page, but a spinning wheel icon will be shown on the _Reorder Pages_ tab instead.  (i) The same issue is also being observed should the space-admin user performs the same steps.     h3. Diagnosis   - In thread dumps, calls to the """"children.action"""" endpoint will show up in thread dumps if you have Thready installed. This corresponds to the same process IDs that have the highest CPU usage.   - The threads will eventually clear and complete the action (unclear if the action times out and the end user gives up or if it actually renders). Thus, it will show as a big spike that can cause alerting platforms to send out warning alarms outside of normal baseline.   - It's not clear if this can actually cause an outage or not. All tests thus far have been on an AWS m4.4xlarge instance with 16 GB of JVM heap and no active users.   - We saw this take place multiple times for an Enterprise customer that caused concern, but no outages resulted as part of this action.    h3. Performance Points   - Accessing the """"Reorder Pages"""" space tool can be done by any user that has access to the space. Even users who are unable to edit pages in a space and have only View rights can still go to the page and trigger the same calls.   - These calls appear to go to the database directly instead of to cache or an index of some kind. In other words, it uses an expensive method to retrieve the data it needs.   - By itself, this likely will not cause outages, but if there are several large spaces or specific users attempting to access the same tool at the same time, it could potentially kick off multiple threads.   - This seems to be more of a scaling issue than anything - smaller spaces don't actually seem to encounter this issue at all."""

summary: "Editing a Page broken links are not shown in red"
description: """h3. Summary    Customer reports that broken links to confluence pages are not shown in red color in a Page, while in Edit Mode, they are shown in blue instead.    On Read mode they are shown correctly.   The behavior is reproducible all the times, with any user.  h3. Environment    Tested in several browsers (Firefox, Safari, Cloud), and several Confluence versions (6.6.5, 6.6.6, 6.10.0), same behavior   Tested 5.8.18, and it worked correctly: broken links were shown in red, in Edit Mode.    Collaborative Editing: On.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a new page in any given space, name it """"Page 1"""", type any text, publish it.   # Create another page, name it """"Page 2"""", and in its text, add a link to the Page 1, publish it.   # Delete the Page 1   # Access the Page 2, notice that in Read Mode, it shows the link to Page 1 as broken (red color, underlined)   # Click the Edit button   # Notice that in Edit Mode, it shows the link to Page 1 as valid (blue)    h3. Expected Results    Broken links to be shown in red color, even in Edit Mode  h3. Actual Results    Broken links are shown in blue color in Edit Mode  h3. Notes    It has worked as expected at some point, however, in newer versions that functionality was lost.   https://jira.<USER>browse/CONFCLOUD-53848 shows it worked before.  {quote}Working in Confluence Server: 5.9.4  {quote}     h3. Workaround    Publish the affected page and the link will change to red."""

summary: "Table editor no longer functional after keypress while resizing"

Summary    If a key is pressed while the cursor is in a cell while resizing a row or column of a table in the page editor, the cursor icon becomes stuck on the resize handle icon and editing the table is no longer functionally possible. No other key or button press will exit this state.    h3. Environment  Tested in the following browsers:  OSX - Chrome (Occurs)  OSX - Safari  (Occurs)  -OSX - Firefox- (works as expected)  Windows - IE11  (Occurs)  Windows - EDGE  (Occurs)    h3. Actual Results  Editing the table is no longer functionally possible until the page is saved or exited.     h3. Notes  The behavior in Windows is slightly different as there is also a different bug in play with IE 11 and Edge where the correct cursor icon is not showing, but the result is the same. See: [CONFSERVER-53364|https://jira.<USER>browse/CONFSERVER-53364]    h3.Workaround  Reload the page. Confluence will then show you a message about a recent draft being saved and you can choose “resume editing”."""

How to replicate  # Create a user group in Confluence """"*testgroup""""*  # Give """"*testgroup""""* Confluence can use permission, and permission to a new space, *""""testspace""""*  # In Jira for example, create a new user group called """"*Testgroup""""*  # Create a user and add them into this group in Jira.  # Connect Jira to Confluence for user management and synchronize.  # The user will now be found in Confluence, in the """"*testgroup""""* user group.  # They will be able to view all pages and content in the space, but if they were to search, there will be no results at all.  # If given the link to the page directly, they can view it.    h4. Expected scenario  # User is able to search    h4. Expected scenario  # User is unable to search anything"""

summary: "Ability to compress images in Confluence"
description: """Some users are experiencing performance issues with their Confluence instance, especially due to the latency transfering content from the server to the clients.     What would be useful to avoid performance issue is an option to have Confluence do the compression for an the image that is being uploaded.      As mentioned in CONFSERVER-23720  {quote}An analogous situation would be how a smart phone will reduce the resolution of a picture before being sent in a text message. Below are potential features we would like to see.    -The option to set a maximum KB size for images and automatically edit images to fit this requirement upon upload   -The option to have the compressed image displayed on the page but still have the original image available for download    The ultimate goal being to reduce the complexity of making sure images on pages do not slow the Confluence end user experience.  {quote}  Another customer input in the previous ticket that is valid for this situation :  {quote}Most of our confluence users are in developing countries with very low bandwith.   Large images slow the loading of pages significantly and sometimes pages can't be even loaded or there is a timeout.    An automatic compression can move us forward...   We have tried to approach the users informing and instructing to upload smaller images but that represents more work and it's just easier to drag and drop. The result: hundreds of pages with very large photo galleries that can be only loaded in a couple of countries, instead of the 33 in Asia, Africa and Latin America.  {quote}"""
comments: "All modern content management systems provide image optimization as a standard service offering. At Airbnb we are experiencing adoption issues in countries where bandwidth is a concern. Would love some advice on plugins or this feature request becoming part of a future enhancement."

However, if the group exists in AD or in Crowd, Confluence will import the group without issues.  # Create a Confluence page.  # Add a User List Macro  # In the Macro's dialog box add the groups name Group,1     h3. Expected Results  The list of users belonging to Group,1 shows.     h3. Actual Results  No users are displayed.       !Screen Shot 2018-08-30 at 11.02.40 PM.png|thumbnail! """
comments: "The User List macro (from the userlister plugin) uses commas as a separator between different group names, so a comma in a name will break the macro. The macro needs to get updated to use a better group selector, so that it can support group names with spaces and commas." [SEP] "[~<USER> That's right.    I think the best way would be, if you have a search-box and there you can add the groups like a space in the task-report macro.  Then you don't need a seperator.    !image-2018-08-31-10-33-13-471.png!"

(!) summary: "Allow users with view only permissions to check off tasks that they are assigned"
description: """Sometimes teams have a space or page(s) which are view only to extended team members.    These teams sometimes need to assign tasks to these view only users, however they are unable to <USER>their task as complete.    It would be convenient to allow view only users to check off tasks which they are assigned."""
comments: "This is a very common scenario: Every team/group has it's own space. Each space is open for edit for the team, and for view for all the employees.  Most of the meeting minutes are cross-team. So a team member that called the meeting is writing a meeting summary in his space, and assigns tasks to other people form other teams. Those people have only view permission of the space, but they were allocated a task. they cannot <USER>this task as complete.    This is impacting for example a large customer of ours.    Atlassian - please do something to support this scenario     "

summary: "Workbox notification - Watching all content in a space does not notify watchers when a comment is added"
description: """h3. Summary  According to the [Workbox Notification Documentation|https://confluence.<USER>doc/workbox-notifications-284365963.html#WorkboxNotifications-Whichnotificationsareincluded?]   {quote}  The workbox displays a notification when someone does one of the following in Confluence:  ...  Comments on a page or blog post that you are watching.  ...  {quote}  However, we also mention the following  {quote}  The workbox does *not* show notifications triggered because you are watching a space. Only watches on pages and blog posts are relevant here.  {quote}    Although you are watching a page through watching all content in a space, you will not be notified of a comment on that page until one of the following happens:  # Comment on the page itself and have the *Watch this page* checbox checked  # You stop watching the content in that space and specifically watch this page      h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Install a Confluence vanilla instance  # Create 2 users (user1 and user2)  # Login as user 1, watch the entire content of a space  # Login as user 2, create a new page  # Comment on the page as user 2     h3. Expected Results  According to the documentation, user 1 should be notified of the new comment added by user 2    h3. Actual Results  user 1 will not be notified of this comment unless:  # user 2 comments on the page and checks the *Watch this page*  # user 2 stops watching the entire content of this space and specifically watches this page"""

summary: "Workbox notification - User is not notified of a like on a page or blogpost when they are watching that page or blogpost"
description: """h3. Summary  According to the [Workbox Notification Documentation|https://confluence.<USER>doc/workbox-notifications-284365963.html#WorkboxNotifications-Whichnotificationsareincluded?]   {quote}  The workbox displays a notification when someone does one of the following in Confluence:  ...  Likes a page or blog post that you are watching.  ...  {quote}    However, this is not the case. Only the creator of the page gets notified when there is a like on that page.      h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Install a Confluence vanilla instance  # Create 3 users (user1, user2 and user 3)  # Login as user 1, create a new page  # Login as user 2, watch the page that was just created by user 1  # Login as user 3, like the page that was just created by user 1    h3. Expected Results  According to the documentation, user 2 should be notified that user 3 liked the page    h3. Actual Results  Only user 1 is notified of the like because they are the owner of the page     h3. Workaround  No workaround at the moment"""

summary: "Improve Data Center license system"
description: """Currently Confluence stores the license details in the {{confluence.cfg.xml}} file, which is stored locally on each node.    When ever a license is installed on a node, the license details need to be distributed to each of the other nodes to be installed and stored in the {{confluence.cfg.xml}} file.    Prior to Confluence 5.6, license changes were not propagated across nodes (a bug). In Confluence 5.6, license changes are propagated to other nodes using the cluster event {{InternalLicenceUpdatedEvent}}.    The problem is that if a node is not active when a license is installed, it will not receive the cluster event {{InternalLicenceUpdatedEvent}}.    *Workaround*  * update the license key manually on the newly joined nodes which license key hasn't updated  * Alternatively, we strongly recommend you update the respective AMI every time a new license key is applied to the active node within the existing cluster."""

summary: "Unable to purge trash from personal space with a white space in the space key"
description: """h3. Summary    Unable to purge trash from personal space with a white space in the space key  h3. Environment  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Start Confluence instance   # Connect to a Jira directory or LDAP Directory   # Create a user in Jira or LDAP with a white space in the username, example """"atlassian charlie""""   # Sync the user to Confluence   # Login as """"atlassian charlie"""" and create a personal space   # Note that the space key is """"~atlassian charlie""""   # Create couple of pages in the personal space   # Navigate to space trash and try to purge all pages or specific ones    h3. Expected Results    Pages should be purged  h3. Actual Results    You get permission denied and pages are not purged   !Screen Shot 2018-08-29 at 16.22.47.png|thumbnail!  h3. Notes   # The issue is related that the space key has a white space which is not allowed.   # Confluence internal directory doesn't allow usernames with white space, however it's allowed in external directory.    h3. Workaround    Contents can be deleted via REST API.   # Find the ID of contents in trash     # Issue {{curl}} command for the content obtained in step 1  {code:bash}  curl -X DELETE -u <user>:<password> http://<confluence-url>/rest/api/content/<content-id>?status=trashed  {code}"""

summary: "Allow users to mark a page for deletion"
description: """It would help us curate our wiki if people could <USER>pages for deletion. Currently, we use labels for this purpose. The advantage of an explicit """"This page should be deleted"""" option for users would be to more easily track who suggested the page should be deleted and to include a workflow to remove incoming links prior to deletion.    For example, as a basic user of the wiki (without the privilege to delete pages) who spots a page that is outdated and should be deleted, I could click on a """"This page should be deleted"""" link/button and would then see the page marked for deletion with a warning that it has two incoming links that should be addressed. I could help remove those incoming links while waiting for an editor to come along later and delete the page.    It might be helpful to include this """"<USER>page for deletion"""" feature for editors who have the privilege to delete pages, giving them the option to queue up pages for deletion as well."""
comments: "Allowing users to <USER>pages for deletion and, as a result, giving editors a way to see the list of pages marked for deletion, would obviate the need for CONFSERVER-56347."

summary: "Add ""incoming links"" as filter for Content by Label macro"

This feature would allow us to call out any pages labeled for deletion that still have incoming links to them (for additional curation)."""

summary: "Session timeout not respected when user has an editor open"
description: """h3. Summary    Confluence won't respect the session timeout in web.xml if the user has an editor open. Essentially, you can set the session timeout to 1 minute, and an inactive user viewing a page will be kicked out after one minute. However, if that user has the editor open for a page, that user's session seems to stay active as long as the editor is open. Happens with Collaborative editing off and on.    May be related to CONFSERVER-54142 (though the seraph-config.xml workaround does not fix this issue)  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Set session timeout to 1 minute in web.xml and restart Confluence ([How to adjust the session timeout for Confluence|https://confluence.<USER>confkb/how-to-adjust-the-session-timeout-for-confluence-126910597.html])   # Log in and open the editor on a page and wait.    h3. Expected Results    User is logged out after 1 minute.  h3. Actual Results    The user is not logged out and remains logged in beyond 20 minutes longer. The same user is logged out after 1 minute if the editor is not open."""
comments: "We've had several cases of accidentally extending the session in the past - https://jira.<USER>browse/CONFSERVER-26796 is an example. That said, having an open editor has always been expected to extend the session. This is not a bug, and working as designed."

summary: "Allow customizing what is displayed in the events from the calendar view"
description: """h3. Problem Definition  Allow customizing what is displayed in the events from the calendar view    Currently, fixed fields will be displayed for each calendar event type in the calendar view and the information being displayed are always following the same sequence and this may not fit different use case of the customers depends on how the calendar is being used or the language that is used.    ||Event Type || Information Displayed (In Order) ||  | Travel | Who:Summary |  | Leave | Who:Summary |  | Birthdays | Who:Summary |  | Events | Who:What |  | Custom Event | Who:Description What|     !text position.png|thumbnail!     h3. summary: "Text Limit for event details displayed in calendar view is not standardized"
description: """h3. Problem Definition  Text Limit for event details displayed in calendar view is not standardized    When adding events for *Travel*, *Leave* and *Birthdays* type, the required fields to tell other users about _what is happening in this event_ will be *Summary*:   !Event Summary.png|thumbnail!     However, when adding events for *Events* and *Custom Event* type, there is no *Summary* fiend, but it has *What* field instead:   !Custom Event What.png|thumbnail!     When they are lined up together in the calendar view, they seem to serve the same purpose but the *Summary* fields only have a text limit of *30 characters* and anything after the 30th characters will be displayed as _3 dots_ like below:   !Summary Limit.png|thumbnail!     h3. Suggested Solution  Standardize the limit of text that should be displayed in the calendar view and possibly allow admins to change the limit to fit different use case.      h3. Note  Currently, the *What* field that is shown in the calendar view has no text limit so this could be a potential problem for a very long text entered in the *What* field as well:   !calendar unlimited text.png|thumbnail! """

The official project management system is not Atlassian and will not be integrated with Confluence. However, each project has a project ID number and 1:many reviews (but the review numbers are not related to the project number). If I tag my notes with ""review_details"" and the project number, it would be nice to get an index of all other reviews also associated with that project number. Also, if I tag with, say, OAUTH2, would be nice to get a list of the other reviews associated with OAUTH2 as well.    So talking it out, what it sounds like I really want is the capability to require  label1 AND \{other_label1 OR other_label2 OR ... other_label n} WHERE page IN SPACE my_notes" [SEP] "This is way similar to what has been asked back in 2015 (CONFSERVER-38753) and still not being taken in consideration..    Having the possibility to list/propose contents to the users based on the same labels applied to the c

summary: "Page Properties Report Missing Column With nbsp"
description: """h3. Summary    If the Page Properties field contains a non-breaking space between words, then it will prevent the Page Properties Macro from displaying that field.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   * Create a page, and use this storage format:       * Give that page a label   * Create a new page with a Page Properties Report macro, using the same label. Choose the 'Current Status' field to display    h3. Expected Results    The field will be displayed, and the content of 'Not Working' should also be displayed  h3. Observed Results    The field is displayed, but blank.  h3. Regression    The existing bug [CONFSERVER-31518|https://jira.<USER>browse/CONFSERVER-31518] fixes this issue if the nbsp appears at the end of the field title, but not if it appears in between words of the title    h3. Workaround  Use the Confluence Source Editor to remove the nbsp, or just remove the column and re-create it in the normal editor."""

summary: "Improve default css .innercell for better page view"
description: """Have found it a continual bugbear, and it would seem to be an oversight, that your core programming seems to default a variety of images, text, boxes, <USER>, into scrollbars when it deems them to big for the page or field.    This is poor programming as far as I can tell and is non-standard web design. The reason seems to be down to a class called .innercell.    This class would be best automated to 'overflow visible' as I believe that most users would like their input to resize with their page rather than go into a scroll bar.    Can we suggest  that the default css is .innercell \{overflow: visible;} which would overcome this headache?    I may be wrong but I've found this to be the solution on a regular basis. Alternatively it could be added as an option (scroll or show oversized images) rather than forcing users to implement an html fix manually.    Thanks"""

summary: "Editing with companion app leaves garbled comment on attachment when multibyte language is used"
description: """h3. Summary    Garbled comment is added when an attachment is edited with companion app in an environment of multibyte language as Japanese or Chinese.  h3. Environment   * Companion application for both Windows/Mac   * Multibyte language like Japanese or Chinese is set for the profile on Confluence    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Set the language to multibyte language as Japanese or Chinese   # Upload an office file   # Preview the file and edit with companion app   !Screen Shot 2018-08-24 at 14.37.01.png|thumbnail!   # Save it and upload   # !Screen Shot 2018-08-24 at 14.37.40.png|thumbnail!   # Then go to the attachment screen on the page in Confluence    h3. Expected Results    Right expression that means """"Uploaded from Companion app"""" should be added in comment.  h3. Actual Results    The comment is garbled.   !Screen Shot 2018-08-24 at 14.38.01.png|thumbnail!  h3. Note   * At least, this garbled character was confirmed when attachment is edited with Japanese and Chinese.    h3. Workaround    Change the profile language to English."""

summary: "E3 provisioning fails when using the Confluence CloudFormation template from atlassian-aws-deployment"
description: """h3. Issue  E3 currently uses the Atlassian internal templates in the [atlassian-aws-deployment|https://bitbucket.org/atlassian/atlassian-aws-deployment] repo for provisioning. However, it seems like the templates have recently undergone major changes, i.e. naming and parameters which E3 depends on. Out-of-the-box, E3 will fail to access the templates because of naming changes and """"fall back"""" to trying to pull the templates from S3, which shouldn't happen. If E3 is forced to point to the atlassian-aws-deployment templates, provisioning will be unsuccessful.    h3. Workaround  Update {{e3.yaml}} to use the stable public AWS CloudFormation repo for provisioning:    """

summary: "Confluence is using anonymous user when linking a page to a sprint "
description: """h1. ISSUE    When creating a page from a sprint in JIRA Confluence uses an anonymous user instead of the USER from the OAUTH transaction, IF JIRA is behind a load balancer due to it being Data Center, it's impossible to route the call back to the node that created it, this results in a 404    h2. Steps to replicate:    # set up an applink between JIRA DC and Confluence   # link a sprint to a page  # intermittently it will succeed     h2. Expected Results:    Confluence would use the same user as it is logged in as to make the PUT call therefor allowing the call to be returned to the node of origin     h2. Actual results:  Unless you're really lucky you get a 404 and the JIRA link does not show on the page:      API call in question:  """"PUT /jira/rest/greenhopper/1.0/api/sprints/1/remotelinkchecked HTTP/1.1[\r][\n]""""     [^connieissue.mov]       h2. Workaround:    There is no current workaround """

summary: "Automatically add label when creating a backlog item for specific board"
description: """I have several Scrum boards with accompanying backlogs in a single project - and while I know I could technically create separate projects for this, I'd like them to have some shared issues.    So, my request is that when adding an item to the backlog while on a specific board, give the possibility to automatically add a label (and epic?) to the item.    That means when adding to board A, it will automatically label with label A so that you can set up your filter to use that, while when you create an issue through Issues it will be shown on all boards.    I hope this makes sense - and I realize it might not be possible or a priority, but no fault in trying :)"""

summary: "Direct link to ""edit with"" for attachments without going through preview"
description: """Starting Confluence 6.11, the *View File* macros, which were depreciated since [2015|https://confluence.<USER>conf69/end-of-support-announcements-for-confluence-950285781.html#EndofSupportAnnouncementsforConfluence-DeprecatedmacrosforConfluence(13November2015)], have lost the the *Edit Document* link.   !Capture.PNG|thumbnail! summary: "Confluence section/columns macros display incorrectly on mobile"
description: """h3. Summary    There appears to be some layout problems with Confluence native macros on mobile devices. This seems to be specific to Confluence pages that have sectioned layouts or use the    {column}    and    {section}    macros. Pages that only have text and do not specify out sections/layouts appear normally and in an expected way. Landscape nor Portrait mode appear to have an effect on how the page is rendered.   h3. Environment   * Confluence 6   * Mobile device   * Non-desktop version of Confluence    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Start Confluence   # Install the Confluence source editor plugin   # Create a page and add the following markup in the source editor       # Visit your site from a mobile device OR using your browser developer tools:   *{{<confluence-base-URL>/plugins/servlet/mobile#dashboard}}*   # Navigate to each test page from the main menu    h3. Expected Results    The pages will display relatively similiar to how they appear on the desktop version  h3. Actual Results    The formats of the pages do not look the same and are a bit harder to follow given that they do not respect the layout.  h3. Workaround    Implement custom CSS to work around this issue.  h3. Notes    Testing was conducted on a browser emulator and actual hardware. The results were the same.     [^mobileTest2] - this contains a page with sections - one left skinny column and a larger right hand column. """
comments: "Thank you for raising this.    We recognise this bug with mobile layouts here however we don't plan to work on this for the foreseeable future. That’s not to say that we’ll never work on this, just that we have higher priority items that we need to focus on.     For this particular issue, we’ve made the practical design decision to ensure that users are able to view content on mobile without the need to pinch and scroll. This comes at the expense of maintaining the original layout and the layout differences.     Please follow this ticket for any future updates.    Cheers,     Server Product Management "

summary: "Provide a more informative error message for unknown macros"
description: """h3. Problem Statement  When a plugin or plugin module is disabled, or the plugin is not yet installed in Confluence, existing macros will appear as """"Unknown macro"""" with little context on why this has happened.    Users will also tend to think that the data within those macros are completely gone and can't be recovered as there's no indication that it is otherwise.    h3. Suggestion  Provide a more informative error message for these unknown macros so users know what to do when they hit into this issue."""

summary: "Expand Confluence's ""attribute_value"" column of the ""cwd_directory_attribute"" table to match that of Crowd 3.3+"

summary: "Macros nested inside other macros are corrupted if original macro is disabled or not installed"
description: """Confluence will corrupt nested macros inside a unknown macro, leading to data loss in the page storage. The bug lies in the XhtmlContent's implementation and what the class does when a macro definition does not exists or it's not available.    When the xhtmlContent.handleMacroDefinitions method is called with a block of xhtml content which contains a reference to a macro module that has been disabled it will return invalid data - such that it ignores the macros that the disabled macro contains and will conflate the parameters of those macros into the disable MacroDefinition.    xhtmlContent.handleMacroDefinitions will generates invalid data leading to data loss.    In the page we will see this image:   !image1.png|thumbnail!   And if we edit the page, nothing will appear inside the macro:   !image2.png|thumbnail!     The storage format shows that the data exist in the page and the following error appears in the logs:      h4. Replicating the issue    The """"info"""" above is the standard info macro and simple macro was a test macro created to just return the body.    When this situation arises, a call to xhtmlContent.handleMacroDefinitions will result in the first MacroDefinition passed to the MacroDefinitionHandler.handle method being corrupted - the MacroDefinition will have no body, and all of the parameters of the child macros will be conflated into the corrupt definition.    On the event, this bug caused the F4C (Forms for Confluence) migrator to attempt to migrate the content based on invalid data, and thus corrupted the pages.    In the test, if we used the following storage format:    With both """"Simple-macro"""" and """"other-macro"""" enabled, we get these results from the test plugin (which iterates across the macro definitions found by XhtmlContent and outputs the parameters and values)        However, if the """"other-macro"""" have it's module disabled, we see that the """"warn"""" macro content disappears and the arguments are conflated ito the """"other-macro"""" definition.    This will cause data loss on all pages that uses the disabled macro and a separate macro migration tries to use the XhtmlContent to convert markup to MacroDefinition objects so that they can be adjusted and then reformatted out to migrate the content to the new format.    h4. Workaround    Reverting to the page last version fixes the problem, however not all pages have a previous version so it might require a manual intervention to restore an old backup into a staging instance and manually restore lost page contents to production."""
comments: "I don't have permission to add attachments, but I have a test plugin which demonstrates the issue in a simple, clean manner - it could be useful for debugging.    EDIT: Support kindly added the attachment :)    Please unzip the tar file into a folder and run atlas-mvn clean verify to make the error happen." summary: "Companion app's ""Launch on startup"" does not work if Windows user has username with spaces"
description: """The Atlassian Companion app has an option to """"Launch on startup"""", which creates a registry entry in Windows, for just that.   There is an issue with Windows however, where sometimes, it's unable to launch a program if there's space in between a user name.    For example,   *Does not work:* C:\Users\user name\AppData\Local\atlassian-desktop-companion\Atlassian Companion.exe --was-opened-at-login   *Works:* C:\Users\username\AppData\Local\atlassian-desktop-companion\Atlassian Companion.exe --was-opened-at-login    This seems to be a Windows issue, since it should be able to launch the app, even if the path has spaces in it, but it just does not work on certain Windows install for some reason.   [https://answers.microsoft.com/en-us/windows/forum/all/on-startup-windows-tries-to-open-a-file-but-cant/420aae7f-be93-4f6b-9bc5-b75d01c0fca0]   [https://stackoverflow.com/questions/2919286/getting-the-username-from-the-hkey-users-values]   [https://superuser.com/questions/479105/how-can-i-format-this-path-to-work-as-registry-key-value]   [https://answers.microsoft.com/en-us/windows/forum/windows_10-other_settings/windows-10-upgrade-issue-with-space-in-username/3ea1a411-2c59-4d2a-bda1-d48b9793231f]    *Workaround:*   We can workaround the issue by editing the registry entry, and add quotes around the whole path.   *HKEY_CURRENT_USER\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Run\com.squirrel.atlassian-desktop-companion.AtlassianCompanion*""""C:\Users\user name\AppData\Local\atlassian-desktop-companion\Atlassian Companion.exe --was-opened-at-login"""".    This works, but once the App starts, it updates the path again, and removed the quotes, and so we have to do this again before restart, after the app starts.    *Suggestion:*   We should add quotes around the path as a preventative measure, so that users with this issue are able to have the app launch on startup."""
comments: "This is an upgrade blocker for me at the moment. This will cause many (if not all) of my users an issue and I'm not willing to support this, especially as there's no viable workaround as the app overwrites whatever registry setting is changed to try and fix it.    Note: the line in the Works example appears to be missing the double quotes around the executable path. summary: "Mobile servlet can time out on first load post start up"
description: """h3. Summary    Confluence lazily loads classes. As such, after start up, it's possible that Confluence won't have certain classes loaded in memory and this can lead to slower load times and timeouts. In this specific case, we are seeing a timeout on first load when attempting to view the mobile version of a Confluence instance.   h3. Environment   * Confluence   * Mobile device    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Start Confluence   # From a mobile device, access the base url for Confluence    h3. Expected Results    The page will render as expected.  h3. Actual Results    The user receives a timeout. A reload of the page renders the page as expected.  h3. Workaround    N/A  h3. Notes    At the minimum, it would be helpful if there was some sort of message that said that loading the classes was happening in the background and to be patient. But a timeout leads the user to believe that there may be a problem occurring."""

summary: "Improve log, or error message for when Confluence is unable to access itself, when set behind a reverse proxy"
description: """When Confluence is set behind a reverse proxy, and incoming traffic is not allowed, using any internal gadgets opens up a HTTP thread, and clicking on the internal gadgets multiple times will eventually exhaust all HTTP threads, causing Confluence to hang, or crash.    This behavior is similar to CONFSERVER-39393, where when Confluence is unable to access external gadgets, opening up the gadgets causes Confluence to use up it's HTTP threads.    In this case, the issue is fixed by allowing incoming traffic from Confluence server to the load balancer.    The issue is that the errors and symptoms of this issue is similar to CONFSERVER-39393, and as such, it's not immediately apparent what the issue is.    *Steps to Reproduce:*   # Add a proxy configuration for tomcat in server.xml (e.g. proxyName confluence.xyz where confluence.xyz is a load balancer or webserver).   # On the confluence.xyz loadbalancer, do not permit incoming traffic from the Confluence application server. Confluence will (mostly) still work, except for gadgets.   # On Confluence, click on Insert > Macro. This will cause the page to spin and timeout. Do this 48 times (or whatever your maxThreads is set to).    *Result:*    # Confluence is no longer accessible and needs reboot. There are errors in the log hinting at what may have happened but it is not immediately clear.    *Expected:*   # There should be a clearer log message or perhaps add a health check that would fails. The current situation where there is a time-delayed crash after some use is not too easy to debug.          """
comments: "Just to clarify, 'incoming traffic is not allowed' here specifically refers to incoming traffic sourced from the application server on 80/443.    We ran into this when setting up Confluence in AWS; initially the only incoming traffic permitted on 80/443 in the ELB security group was from the subnets that user traffic originates from.    Happy to provide thread dumps, logs or any further clarification as needed. Affected version was 6.3.3." [SEP] "As a bit of assistance for anyone that might be hunting on google, you'll see a few error messages like the following in the logs:  "
