summary: "The word 'was' is not searchable."
description: """h3. Summary    When you create a Space and the title/spacekey contains 'was', it is not searchable to other users. Pages that contain the word 'was' is also not searchable. For example, when you search for 'I was', only i is highlighted in the search.    !highlighted i.png|width=564,height=265!  h3. Steps to replicate  h4. Space   # Create a space called 'WAS' and set the permission to 'Everyone can view and edit"""".   # Search for space called 'WAS'. While typing for 'was' in the searchbox, you will see an error 'The query could not be parsed'.   # Click 'Enter'. Then, user will get an error :  {panel}  No results found for *was*. Please try one of the following suggestions:   *    ** Use more general search terms or [consult the online documentation|https://confluence.<USER>display/DOC/Confluence+Search+Syntax] for tips.   ** If you are filtering by space, content, last modification date or contributor, try removing or adjusting the filter{panel}    h4. Page   # Create a page called 'WAS' and set the permission to 'Everyone can view and edit"""".   # Search for a page called 'Was', or page that contains the word 'was'. While typing for 'was' in the searchbox, you will see an error 'The query could not be parsed'.   # Click 'Enter'. Then, same error is observed.    h3. Expected results    User should be able to search for the space and pages containing 'was'.  h3. Actual results    User is not able to search for the space containing 'was'.     """
comments: "This is not a bug. In most search systems it is normal to have [stop words|https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Stop_words]. These are removed from content before content is indexed and also removed from search terms when a search is performed. ""Was"" is one such stop word in English. Here is the complete list from the Lucene source code:    One possible workaround is to change the indexing language to ""Other"" but that may introduce other issues, for example [stemming|https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Stemming] might not work properly."

summary: "Lots of SQL queries issued for REST GET Content "
description: """h3. Problem Definition    Lot of Content search queries as below are issued when accessed via REST content api.  {code:sql}  select abstractpa0_.CONTENTID as CONTENTI1_12_, .... from CONTENT abstractpa0_   ... limit 500, 501;  select abstractpa0_.CONTENTID as CONTENTI1_12_, .... from CONTENT abstractpa0_   ... limit 1000, 501;  select abstractpa0_.CONTENTID as CONTENTI1_12_, .... from CONTENT abstractpa0_   ... limit 1500, 501;  .....  select abstractpa0_.CONTENTID as CONTENTI1_12_, .... from CONTENT abstractpa0_   ... limit 10000, 501;  {code}  h3. How to replicate this problem    There are at least two types of scenario to cause this problem –   *Scenario 1*   # Create a Confluence Instance with many content. e.g. 20,000 content.   # Create a user who has access permission to the contents created in step 1. e.g. testuser   # Clear cache   # Send a GET request with {{rest/api/content?start=<any number over 500>}} to the instance. e.g. start from 10,000  {code:java}  curl -u testuser:testuser http://<confluence-base-url>/rest/api/content?start=10000  {code}    i.e. Confluence tries to fetch all 10,500 contents to obtain just 500 contents from 10,001st to 10,500th items.    *Scenario 2*   # Create a Confluence Instance with many content. e.g. 20,000 content.   # Create a user who *doesn't* have access permission to the contents created in step 1. e.g. testuser   # Clear cache   # Send a GET request with {{rest/api/content}} to the instance.  {code:java}  curl -u testuser:testuser http://<confluence-base-url>/rest/api/content  {code}    i.e. Confluence tries to fetch all 20,000 contents to filter the contents on which the testuser has permission.  h3. Suggested Solution    For scenario 1, to obtain data for {{rest/api/content?start=10000}} , *only* the query  {code:java}  select abstractpa0_.CONTENTID as CONTENTI1_12_, .... from CONTENT abstractpa0_   ... limit 10000, 501;  {code}  should be issued.    For scenario 2, filter the CONTENT first with permission and fetch the items later.  h3. Why this is important    Because this can cause a performance problem on database server processing a lot of queries at the same time."""

summary: "When undoing a paste, the text is appended at the end of the page"
description: """This has been reproduced on Windows 7 and Windows 10, using Firefox and Chrome.   *Confluence versions tested*: 5.10.2 , 6.0.5, 6.3.2, 6.10.1    When using *Ditto* or *AutoHotkey* (clipboard manager applications), if you paste some text in the Page, and then hit the Undo button (or Ctrl+Z), the text will still be published upon saving the Page.  The only difference is whether the Collaborative mode is turned ON or OFF.    If the *Collaborative Editing is ON*, the text will break into multiple lines and will not disappear from the screen at all. Upon Saving the Page, the text will be appended at the end of the Page and published as if the """"Undo"""" button was never used.  Video demonstration: [https://screencast-o-matic.com/watch/cFjo6fqIYq]    If the *Collaborative Editing is OFF*, the text will initially disappear when hitting the Undo button, but once the Page is saved/published, it will be appended on the bottom of the Page.  Video demonstration: [https://screencast-o-matic.com/watch/cFjo6QqIYa]    This behavior is reproducible using two different 3rd Party Apps, [Ditto|https://ditto-cp.sourceforge.io/] and [AutoHotkey|https://autohotkey.com/]    Thanks,  <USER>Radenovic | Atlassian Support"""

summary: "Hibernate Adapter Map is not Thread Safe"
description: """h2. Summary  There's an internal Hibernate Adapter Map used to support Hibernate API Versions 2 and 5. The purpose of the map is to translate exceptions and other tasks from one version to another. This map can get corrupted, which can cause threads to get stuck when executing the WeakHashMap method, which is not thread safe and can lock the entire application.    h2. Symptoms  Application tasks that rely on achieving connections from the database, such as the Index, may stop working, since the WeakHashMap locks all threads who doesn't have a connection to the database yet.  There may be other operations that can be impacted too.    The way to identify this scenario is by taking thread dumps, where we see the majority of the threads in either Blocked or Waiting statuses, while there's only one thread in Runnable status, executing a WeakHashMap:      h2. Steps to Replicate  It is unclear on how the application would get into this state.    h2. Workaround  Unfortunately, the only way to clear the stuck thread is to restart the application."""
comments: "The HibernateExceptionAdapter in hibernate.adapter-0.101 is using a WeakHashMap to keep track of Hibernate 2 <-> 5 exception mappings. Sadly the way it's using this map is not atomic, meaning multiple threads updating the map could lead to map corruption. We need to update to the release of hibernate.adapter that uses synchronization around this map.    Note that may look like the entry populated into the exceptionMap is _backwards_, and that should have been {{exceptionMap.put(ex, result);}} This is not the case, as we use the map to try to ensure that adapt(adapt(ex)) == ex - avoiding generating more adapted exceptions in that case.    Longer term: we should replace this map with a tunable cache, so that memory pressure doesn't immediately cause more problems with exception creation, especially around populating stack traces (which is a relatively expensive operation by itself).    Note that the relevant library repo has moved to [https://bitbucket.org/atlassian/hibernate-upgrade-adapter] and is now open

summary: "JIRA Issues Macro does not display issues when ""jira.search.views.max.limit"" is set to ""-1"" (unlimited) on the JIRA side"
description: """h2. Problem Summary  On the JIRA side, there is a configurable parameter called [{{jira.search.views.max.limit}}|https://confluence.<USER>jirakb/how-to-increase-the-jira-search-results-limit-on-the-issue-navigator-357696242.html] which limits the number of issues returned within a JIRA search. If this value is set to """"-1"""", which denotes """"unlimited"""", then the JIRA Issues Macro will fail to render issues inside of Confluence. The max number of issues will continue to be displayed, but not the listing of issues themselves.    h2. Steps to reproduce  # In JIRA, set {{jira.search.views.max.limit}} to """"-1"""" in jira-config.properties  # Start up Confluence and JIRA  # Create an applink so Confluence can pull in issues from JIRA  # In JIRA, create a project and a couple of test issues  # In Confluence, insert a JIRA Issues Macro and use an issue filter which returns multiple results  # Save the Confluence page    h2. Expected results  Confluence displays JIRA Issues in the JIRA Issues Macro    h2. Actual results  The number of issues found are returned in the macro, but not the listing of issues themselves:     !jim_table.png!     h2. Workaround  * On the JIRA side, set """"jira.search.views.max.limit"""" to a non-unlimited value (the default is 1000).  * Related KB: https://confluence.<USER>confkb/jira-issues-macro-shows-an-empty-table-with-no-issues-but-shows-the-total-number-of-issues-when-displaying-in-table-mode-690849922.html"""

summary: "e-mail notifications are not sent when page is deleted. "
description: """h5. Environment:  Confluence 6.10 (Linux 64bit), postgreSQL 9.5, SMTP server (gmail)    h5. Bug description  * Email notifications are not sent when page is deleted.   * Recommended Updates email doesn't contain deleted pages actions    h5. Steps to replicate  # Configure SMTP server with Confluence according to [Configuring a Server for Outgoing Mail|https://confluence.<USER>doc/configuring-a-server-for-outgoing-mail-151078.html]  # Create user account with actual email address  # Newly created user should watch testing space """"TEST""""  # Login as another user and perform following actions on space """"TEST""""  * Add page  * Add comment   * Update existing page  * Remove existing page   * Remove comment    h5. Expected results  * In all scenarios email notifications will be sent.   * Above actions will be included in """"Recommended Updates"""" list    h5. [SEP] "I'm closing this as a duplicate as this bug is already covered by these 2 different bug reports:   # CONFSERVER-52687 covers no notifications sent when the page is deleted or trash is purged.   # CONFSERVER-37898 covers no notifications sent on the daily email report. The recommended updates email is based on popularity and will not contain page deletions.    Please watch/vote on one of those instead."

summary: "Alloe admin to bulk update the page watcher"
description: """h3. Summary  Currently, Confluence does not have the feature to bulk adding user or replace using to watch pages in Confluence. Admin will need to open the page and add the user as a watcher of the page. It will be efficient if the admin is allowed to bulk adding user to watcher pages."""
comments: "To give some context to this.    Say a user ""employee.123"" watches 80 pages and spaces.    That employee changes role or leaves the company. A new user account ""employee.456"" is made for a new user.    As an admin, I wish to make ""employee.456"" a watcher of all the spaces and pages that ""employee.123"" watches. I wish to do this without having to select each page individually and add the new user as a watcher." [SEP] "Sounds like it could be solved with CONFSERVER-44953 which is about allowing groups to be defined as a watcher. Simply defining a user group for the role and then changing the member(s) of the group would solve this use case. But then, CONFSERVER-44953 is not (yet) implemented either... "

summary: "Maximum call stack size exceeded"
description: """h3. Summary    When creating a page with both Cache for Confluence (7.8.0) and Refined Themes for Confluence (6.0.6) apps installed, page is not loaded properly and a """"Maximum call stack size exceeded"""" error is thrown.    Steps to reproduce:  # Spawn a Confluence Server 6.2.3  # Install Cache for Confluence (7.8.0) and Refined Themes for Confluence (6.0.6) apps  # Set Refined Themes as Site theme (General Configuration -> Site Theme -> Confirm setting Refined Theme)  # Create a page with Future macro, and inside it, a Code block macro with Java as highlight (as per screenshot)  # Publish the page and it's an eternal """"Loading app.."""" with error:      Notes:  - Issue does not occur for Confluence 6.10.0"""
comments: "Hi Support,    Is there any ETA to address this?    Thanks    RK" [SEP] "[~<USER>, this problem has to do with babel-polyfill and syntax highlighter both trying to polyfill String methods. It appears that Refined Theme is loading babel-polyfill. When the code macro code is executed before the babel-polyfill is loaded, this infinite recursion is triggered. This is resolved in Confluence 6.7 where the babel-polyfill is loaded early from Confluence Core. A possible workaround is to load the babel-polyfill in atl.general context, for example. This could be done inside any plugin. I have tested it by loading babel-polyfill within code macro plugin as follows:    The polyfill comes from here: [https://babeljs.io/docs/en/babel-polyfill.html]    You could try this inside Cache Macro and have that polyfill loaded in the general context all the time. This is only a workaround for now as, unless you are using the polyfill, you should have no reason to load it.    As we're not likely to release further Confluence versio

summary: "Allow restore to use the upgradeRecoveryFile format"
description: """The """"import from XML"""" function works only with the {{entities.xml}} file (along with its additional properties file). It does not work with the upgradeRecoveryFile format XML file, which appears to be a database dump in XML rather than SQL. There is apparently no way to restore the database from the upgradeRecoveryFile. The import function in the administration panel should be able to use this format file as an alternative to the {{entities.xml}} file."""

summary: "Fixed Width table displays strange behaviors in the editor for Blog post"
description: """h5. Description:  # When inserting a table with """"Fixed Width"""" into a blog post, in editor mode, there a few strange behaviors  # First, after inserting the table and setting it to """"Fixed Width"""" and adjusting it, we are unable to type anything unless we click around a little.  # Next, if we were to add another table with """"Fixed Width"""", just moving the cursor around seems to add line breaks into the page.  # Publishing the page makes the space goes away.    h5. Steps to reproduce issue:  # Add a new blogpost  # add a table with 2 lines three columns (or whatever you feel like i tried different numbers out)  # set it to fixed width (changing the width is not necessary but I did it in the screencast I took and attached to the bug ticket)  # add another table underneath it  # move the mouse curser over the first table and just move it around there a little  # watch in awe when line breaks mysteriously are added to the page    h5. Expected:  # Fixed width table in blog post should work like normal, like on a standard page.    h5. Actual:  # Same as issue description.    h5. Video from customer   [^screencast-wiki.com-2018.08.08-16-17-40.webm] """
comments: "Hi guys, so quick use case description of how we came about to stumble upon this gem:  Our clients use blog posts in that specific space mainly for company wide annoucnements such as new hires, anniversaries and that sort of stuff.  Whenever they have a new hire they use the same page structure where the new colleagues name, image and department are entered in a table with fixed width (I've suggested using Confluence Profile Macro for this, but with new people a lot of times they don't have their picture uploaded yet, plus the client likes the structured look of tables and that the images can be displayed bigger and so on and so forth).  Often it happens that they announce more than one new person so they add another table below the first one (sometimes there is more description or a little selfwritten paragraph by the person underneath the table, which is why they don't simply add another row) and that is obviously where the trouble begins and magic line breaks appear.  They initially just ignored it, think

summary: "Drafts can't save attachments via insert option, with Collaborative Editing Off"
description: """h3. *Summary*    With Collaborative Editing *Off* the following situation exists:  * New drafts can't save attachments via upload attachment option.  * Drafts of existing pages can save attachments, as usual.    h3. *Steps to Reproduce*    # Ensure Collaborative Editing is Off.  # Create a new page, give it a title and a few line.  # Select *Close*.  # From the profile picture navigate to Drafts.  # Open the draft for editing again.  # Upload attachment via *Insert files and images* option.    h3. *Expected Results*    The attachment should upload to the draft.    h3. *Actual Results*    The attachment pop-up never gives the option to *Insert*. The only option is to *Close*.    h3. *Workaround*  Try one of the following two:  # Drag and Drop the attachment on to the editor.  # Enable Collaborative Editing.    h3. *Diagnosis*    Confluence returns a 400 bad request to the request from the browser.      The symptoms are similar to CONFSERVER-38719.    If we try an upload again, we see the following error.   !server_ unavailable.png|thumbnail!     In application logs, we see something like following.     h3. *Additional Notes*    Upon testing this in Confluence v6.6.7, this issue doesn't happen. This version actually uses a different request method to upload attachments.  """

summary: "Confluence should not allow another action while one is already been executed on a Page"
description: """h3. Description:  Confluence application allows Copy Page and Copy Page Tree actions to be executed while Move page is already in process on the same page. This creates a race condition and can cause attachments to go missing on both source and destination pages. Due to the timing required to reproduce this condition, this tends to affect larger page trees which are being copied/moved simultaneously     h3. Steps to reproduce:   # Create a page with subpages and few attachments.    # Click on """"..."""" and select Move page to another space.   # Immediately click <- (Back) on the browser or refresh the browser.   # Now click """"..."""" again and select Copy action on the same page.   # Select to copy the page tree and choose a different destination from the move   # Now check attachments on the source and destination pages for the move and the copy actions    h3. Expected behavior:  Confluence should prevent running Copy action while Move action is in process and ideally inform the user in the UI of what's going on behind the scenes     h3. Actual behavior:  Copy actions trigger even when Move is already running. This causes many attachments to go missing from the filesystem on both source and destination pages    h4. Details:  In local testing, it appears that the page move will move the attachments to a new location on the filesystem. However, the page moves themselves will fail due to a copy being done at the same time. This results in the pages not being actually moved, but attachments are all moved (and can no longer be found in the UI).    At the same time, the copy tree is successful, but none of the physical attachments are copied over since they're now """"missing"""" from the expected original places in the filesystem.    Finally, the original source pages are now also missing attachments since they've been moved despite the move page action failing. Trying to access the attachments from various pages will lead to the following errors in the UI:    !Null-pointer.png|thumbnail! !Attachment-not-found.png|thumbnail! """

summary: "Improve node startup time for datacenter deployment"
description: """h3. Summary  Node startup time takes more than 30 minutes for data center deployment    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Install data center via de AWS cloudformation template  # Load a lot of data (https://developer.<USER>platform/marketplace/testing-your-app-with-a-large-data-set/)  # Scale up the cluster with an extra node    h3. Notes  The delay is caused by a {{chown -R in /<USER>rc.d/init.d/atl-init-confluence}}, specifically in function {{configureSharedHome}}. This chown -R takes about 30 minutes on the mounted storage and while it might make sense in certain setups, in this case it seems totally superfluous.  """
comments: "We noticed this too in our DC testing. We generated a ""large"" instance size with the right number of attachments, which were each backed by a separate small text file. The chown -R had booting up a new node hanging for a loooong time.    I realise it might be hard to detect what node number is being booted, and thus if the chown -R is required. Maybe having the right ownership set on the shared-home could be a documented pre-condition instead? (It seems it would be unusual for it to be set incorrectly anyway?)"

summary: "The HttpClient library used in confluence-collaborative-editor-plugin can't parse the Load Balancer's cookie having the Expires attribute in RFC 6265 format"
description: """WARN messages printed in logs:    This may prevent some requests from getting the correct cookie (which may break session affinity). summary: "Users restricted from pages due to invalid data in CONTENT_PERM_SET and CONTENT_PERM tables"
description: """h2. Summary  The steps to reproduce this problem is unknown. However, the Support team has observed that in multiple customer environments, page restrictions can get into a state which prevents all users from viewing/editing a page (except site admins in the """"confluence-administrators"""" group). An examination of the database confirms that the page contains a restriction, but no user or group is named to be part of that restriction. This effectively blocks *all* users from viewing or editing the page.    h2. Symptoms from the UI  A red lock appears at the top of the affected page, indicating that there is some sort of page restriction. However, when clicking on the lock, the restrictions dialog appear blank and says that there are no page restrictions    h2. Diagnosis via the database  The *CONTENT_PERM_SET* table will contain a row which references on the affected page, indicating a View and/or Edit restriction is indeed on the page. This causes the red lock to appear in the UI. However, there is no corresponding row in the *CONTENT_PERM* table, which keeps track of which users/groups are applied to the restrictions in the first table. This causes the restrictions dialog to come up blank.    In short, the bug is that there is a row in CONTENT_PERM_SET which locks down the page, but no corresponding row in CONTENT_PERM to complete that restriction.    h2. Workaround  # The following query can be used to identify pages affected by this condition:    # Using a site admin/super user (i.e. someone in the """"confluence-administrators"""" group), visit the pages returned by the above query  # Explicitly add a View restriction and save. This has the effect of overwriting the bad data with an actual page restriction.  # Finally, remove the View restriction and save. The page will now be unlocked"""
comments: "Hi There,    Is there a fix for this as of yet. IF so can you kindly let us know which version carries the fix. This is impacting a larger audience in our customer base (which by the way has several thousands of them).     --Ajay "

That UI need to be updated to clearly show the progress bar instead of <USER>"""

summary: "Anchors Non-Functional in Templates"
description: """When creating a template for a page structure I plan to use frequently, the anchors macro feature is non-functional.  This creates an issue, in that I cannot fully build out a template for page that would require anchors.  I typically write procedures, so many of my pages need anchors to make it easier to navigate throughout the page.  Can this be something that is fixed?  I am not sure if it is a bug."""

summary: "Text doesn't wrap around file preview (ex. Word Document previews) macro in Confluence"
description: """h3. Summary  When attempting to align file previews in a Page and wrap text, it displays correctly in the editor but doesn't display as expected in the page.      h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Create a new page  # Attach a word document and insert a preview in the page  # Add text to the page  # Align the preview to the right  # Save the apge    h3. Expected Results  The preview aligns to the right and text wraps around a shown in editor mode   !works-in-editor.png|thumbnail!     h3. Actual Results  The page saves and the preview is neither aligned to the right nor wrapped with text   !formatting-not-applied.png|thumbnail!      h3. Notes  Looking at the storage format for the same steps but for an image, we see the following added to the image element:  {code:borderStyle=solid}  ac:align=""""right"""" ac:style=""""float: right;""""  {code}    But these don't appear on file preview macros. It looks like the formatting is stripped."""

summary: "HTML Export shows page layout sections on new lines"
description: """h3. Summary  Side-by-side sections on a Confluence page will show as on top of each other when exported to HTML.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Create a page that has a [page layout|https://confluence.<USER>doc/page-layouts-columns-and-sections-275188613.html] section with multiple columns.  !page-layout-editor.png|thumbnail!  # Save the page  !page-layout-saved.png|thumbnail!  # Export the space to HTML  # View the page    h3. Expected Results  The sections will show side-by-side, as they do on the page.    h3. Actual Results  The sections are stacked on top of each other.  !page-layout-html-export.png|thumbnail!    h3.Workaround  Add the following at the end of the styles/site.css file in the export:  {code:css}  .columnLayout {      display: flex;  }    .cell {      flex: auto;  }  {code}"""

summary: "Control +Arrow does not skip words in Firefox on Windows in Editor"
description: """h3. Summary    Using the Ctrl + Arrow key to skip words in the Collaborative Editor does not work.    When using this keyboard shortcut, it will skip words, however when you reach the end of the paragraph or line, the cursor gets lost. This requires the user to take the cursor and place it in the location they want to type.    h3. Environment  This issue is reported following environment:   * Firefox """"Quantum"""" 61.0.1 or above   * Windows   * Confluence 6.x and above    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Open Confluence 6.x or above in Firefox on Windows   # Open the editor   # Type a line of words   # Click Control+ Right Arrow to move the cursor over words   # Continue to do so till you get to the end of the line   # Hit Enter to create a new line beneath   # Try to use Control + Left Arrow to navigate back to the end of the first line    h3. Expected Results  The cursor returns to the end of the first line.    h3. Actual Results  The cursor is lost.      h3. Notes  This appears to be related to Collaborative Editing, since this behavior is not seen in versions before Confluence 6.x  h3. Workaround    Use the cursor to return to the end of the first line."""
comments: "Hi there, I'm the one who reported this issue.  I was asked to describe how it's affecting us, so I will say that it's negatively changing the way I work in all other keyboard-based applications.  Trying to avoid the trapped cursor by slowing down and inching forward one character at a time as I approach the end of a line is becoming a muscle memory that I find myself doing in other applications.  Being a rapid/touch-type, keyboard-shortcut-using person this diminishes my overall speed and productivity and is frustrating every time it happens.  I implore you to address this as soon as possible."

summary: "Links in emails stop working after changing a page's title"
description: """h4. Issue Description  As the link generated in the email is a static page URL, if there is changes in the page title after the email notification is sent, the page won't be accessible by the link directly anymore.    h4. Expected Result  User get redirect to the correct page    h4. Actual Result  User received a """"Page Not Found"""" message as the title of the page is no longer the same.    h4. Suggested Resolution  Using a *tiny URL* or *pageid* will be a better approach."""

summary: "Provide REST API methods to manage users and groups"
description: """h3. Summary    As a Confluence <USER>I would like to be able to manage users and groups directly from REST API endpoints. Automation and scripting of user management can be made easier with REST API.  h3. Problem statement    User and groups management is currently not possible via REST API only. Bulk operations, automation or scripting of management actions is not currently possible when using REST API. User management was previously possible with SOAP, which has been deprecated.  h3. Suggested Resolution    Provide methods akin to what as present with SOAP that would allow Admins to manage users and groups via REST API.                   _Previous Ticket Description_   As Confluence is popular tool used by many users, it should provide the API to add users to groups, similar to what Jira Cloud has."""
comments: "Hi Team,         We can we expect this feature to be present in the REST API ?         Thanks,    Harshit" [SEP] "Does it mean that there is no way to deactivate users in Confluence via rest api too?" [SEP] "We need this function urgently.  It would be very good for all customers if this were implemented in this way." [SEP] "You can add and remove users from groups through the internal JSON-RPC APIs, albeit not public or guaranteed to be the same (or even still there) release to release." [SEP] "We need this functionality!" [SEP] "This feature would be really appreciated" [SEP] "feature upvote +1" [SEP] "Hi,    we also very much would like to see the possibility of administrating users and groups via REST, since SOAP is deprecated." [SEP] "I hope to see this feature soon too!" summary: "A collaborator relation is added when two users save their drafts with collab editing off"
description: """h2. Issue Summary    It is currently possible to get into a state where Collaborative Editing is off and the page history still shows multiple contributors.  h2. Steps to reproduce   # Turn off Collaborative Editing.   # Publish a new page (Page History will show it as V1 - Version 1).   # Edit this page as User A and User B at the same time.   # User A and User B commit their changes to the page, creating two new versions.   # Page History shows a version (V3) *Changed By* both users:  !Screen Shot 2018-07-30 at 16.57.25.png|thumbnail!    h2. What happened?    An entry was added to the DB with users A and B as collaborators of v3.  h2. What should have happened?    Only one of the users should have contributed to version 2 and 3."""

summary: "CalDAV URL not able to select different calendars"
description: """h3. Summary    When you select the """"CalDAV"""" option on the """"Subscribe to calendar"""" panel, the URL is not changed when you select a different calendar. According to [Subscribe to and from Team Calendars|https://confluence.<USER>teamcal/subscribe-to-and-from-team-calendars-273285883.html], the *root* URL will be used.    This implies that the application that will subscribe to Team Calendar will have a calendar discovery. However, if the application doesn't have a calendar discovery, it will not be able to fetch the events from Team Calendars from a specific calendar.  h3. Environment   * Team Calendar 6.0.17   * Confluence 6.9.1    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Install Team Calendars.   # Create two calendars.   # Click on the """"Subscribe"""" option.   # Select """"CalDAV"""" and the calendars created.    h3. Expected Results   * The URL given by Confluence will have different endpoints for each calendar.  or   * Gray out the """"calendar"""" selection and display a warning saying that the application must have calendar discovery, as it happens with Outlook.    h3. Actual Results    The same URL is given for different calendars.    !Screen Shot 2018-08-01 at 1.49.12 PM.png|thumbnail!     !Screen Shot 2018-08-01 at 1.49.20 PM.png|thumbnail!  h3. Notes    If you select Thunderbird, it will have a url {{/caldav/}} and different endpoint for each individual calendar.  h3. Workaround    No workarounds"""
comments: "Confluence 6.12.2 and TC 6.0.25 is also affected." [SEP] "Hi there    Thanks for raising this issue. We've investigated and deemed this as *Not a bug*.     The Description of this ticket implies that an incorrect URL is being used - it should at least be an https link.    Thanks    Makisa | Senior Product Manager, Confluence Server and Data Center"

The bug means that we will not be able to upgrade to 6.10 or newer until this is fixed. " [SEP] "Hi [~<USER> and [~<EMAIL>],    This is actually expected behaviour now as of Confluence 6.10 and newer. Cheers,  <USER> Senior <USER>Lead - Confluence Server" [SEP] "Dear Niraj. summary: "Quick Search results are not ordered with the most recently updated first"
description: """h3. Summary    Quick Search does not list pages in recently updated first order, despite documentation stating so, it appears that quick search is heavily influenced by last accessed and ignores recently modified. In fact, the most recently modified page goes to the bottom of the search.  h3. Steps to Reproduce    On Confluence 6.10.0   # Created 5 pages in sequence *1, 2, 3, 4, 5*, so *5*th is the last to be modified and *1* is the oldest   # Accessed them one by one in the same order: *1, 2, 3, 4, 5* and back to space homepage   # Click on QuickSearch and see pages in reverse order from top to bottom, *5, 4, 3, 2, 1*   # Type in """"page"""" into quick search, pages are displayed in *5, 4, 1, 2, 3*   # Go back to page tree, and access pages in reverse order: *5, 4, 3, 2, 1* and then home   # In QuickSearch, pages listed as *1, 2, 3, 4, 5*   # Type in """"page"""", pages are still listed *1, 2, 3, 4, 5*   # Logged in as another user and edited page *4*   # Switched back to the original user and refreshed the page, QuickSearch recent page list remained the same: *1, 2, 3, 4, 5*   # Typing """"page"""" changed list to *1, 2, 3, 5, 4*    h3. Expected Results    Defined and documented order in which quick search results are meant to be sorted as if it's last accessed or last modified.   If there are additional algorithms that can influence quick search results, those should be documented too.    According to [Search|https://confluence.<USER>doc/search-139528.html] documentation:  {quote} * Items are ordered with the most recently updated first{quote}  Above is clearly not respected.  h3. Actual Results    It is unclear as to what logic or order quick search results are displayed in. The same scenario was tested on 5.10.8 and behaviour is identical."""

summary: "Provide Email alert for Deleting Custom EvenT Type"
description: """h3. Problem Definition  When a """"Custom Event Type"""" completely deleted from a Calendar notifications are not sent out via e-mail like they are for singular events nor is a backup of those events sent like when one deletes the whole calendar.    This causes problems as we do not have restrictions nor audit trails for Team Calendars. A user can come in and delete an entire calendar with no record.    h3. Suggested Solution  Provide an e-mail notification and backup of the calendar during deletion.  """

summary: "Make XML backup attachment hierarchy path the same as from original data directory"
description: """When we create a XML backup with attachments, the XML file is created with a folder called attachments, however the [attachments hierarchical structure|https://confluence.<USER>doc/hierarchical-file-system-attachment-storage-704578486.html] is changed completely and most of the path is removed, leaving only page IDs.   !attachment_structure_difference.JPG|thumbnail!    !attachment_structure_difference_2.JPG|thumbnail!     In case the XML does not import some attachments, we cannot manually import the entire attachment folder from the zip as workaround.    This is specially problematic for Cloud migrations to Server instances, since missing attachments during the import cannot be manually imported by copying de attachments folder from the XML zip to the data_directory/attachments.    Suggestion:    We should create the XML zip file with the complete and unchanged attachment hierarchical path that its used within a Confluence data directory. This will avoid confusion when checking for missing attachments and at the same time should make the attachments manual copy possible when the import procedure doesn't work correctly for some attachments."""

summary: "Clearing the format of a text will remove the inline comment added to the text."
description: """h3. Summary    If the Clear Formatting option is used on selected on text, the inline comments added to the text will be cleared.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # On a new page, type text """"Hello World"""". Change Text Formatting (as an example choose """"Monospace"""").    # Save the page.   # Add an inline comment to the text """"Hello World"""".   # Edit the page. Then, select the Monospaced text and Select """"Clear Formatting"""" from the Editor Toolbar. Save page.   # It is observed that the Inline comment will be gone once the page is saved.     h3. Expected Results    Inline comment will still be there until the user clicked 'Resolve'.  h3. Actual Results    Inline comment is gone and will be automatically moved to 'Resolved'.  h3. Notes    Appears to affect all Text Formatting options, Superscript, Subscript, and Strikethrough  h3. Workaround    Select Formatting to be removed from Editor Toolbar rather than selecting 'Clear Formatting'."""

summary: "Confluence doesn't get installed as a service even when the installer is run as root in Ubuntu"
description: """h2. *Summary*    According to the [guide|https://confluence.<USER>doc/installing-confluence-on-<USER>143556824.html] of installing Confluence on Linux Server, if the installer is run as root, Confluence will be installed as a service. Although, it doesn't get installed as a service on Ubuntu.  h2. *Testing Environment*    Ubuntu 18.04 LTS   Confluence installer 6.10.1  h2. *Steps to reproduce*   # Download installer of Confluence.   # Add the execute flag to the installer.   # Run the installer as root.    h2. *Expected Results*    Confluence should be installed as a service.  h2. *Actual Result*    Confluence doesn't get installed as a service.  h2. *Workaround*    Installer does create the {{/<USER>init.d/confluence}} file, though it doesn't have the run levels in it.  So, if we will directly _enable_ the service, Ubuntu throws an error.  {code}  update-rc.d: error: confluence Default-Start contains no runlevels, aborting.  {code}  We need to add the following lines on the top of {{/<USER>init.d/confluence}} file, but after the shebang.  {code:java}  ### BEGIN INIT INFO  # Required-Start:    $all  # Required-Stop:  # Default-Start:     2 3 4 5  # Default-Stop:  ### END INIT INFO  {code}  After the above change, run the following command again to install Confluence as a service.  {code:java}  systemctl enable confluence  {code}    h2. *Diagnosis*    This issue does not happen in CentOS, which means it might be an issue with {{update-rc.d}} scripts in Ubuntu.      """
comments: "A fix for this issue is available to Server and Data Center customers in Confluence 6.11.2  [Upgrade|https://www.<USER>software/confluence/download?utm_source=confluence_bugfix&utm_campaign=confluence_bugfix&utm_medium=email] now or check out the [Release Notes|https://confluence.<USER>doc/confluence-6-11-release-notes-953678666.html?utm_source=confluence_bugfix&utm_campaign=confluence_bugfix&utm_medium=email] to see what other issues are resolved." [SEP] "I just upgraded to Confluence 6.11.2 and did not see any LSB headers in the initscript.    The following lines (or similar) are what I expected to see in /<USER>init.d/confluence.  The status function is copied from the Bitbucket Server initscript.  {code:bash}  #!/bin/bash    ### BEGIN INIT INFO  # Provides:          confluence  # Required-Start:    $network $local_fs $syslog  # Required-Stop:     $network $local_fs $syslog  # Default-Start:     2 3 4 5  # Default-Stop:      0 1 6  # Short-Description: Start Confluence daemon at boot time  # Description:

summary: "Multiple collaborators shown even when collaborative editing is off"
description: """h2. Issue Summary    It is currently possible to get into a state where Collaborative Editing is off and the page history still shows multiple contributors.  h2. Steps to reproduce   # Turn off Collaborative Editing.   # Publish a new page (Page History will show it as V1 - Version 1).   # Edit this page as User A and User B at the same time.   # User A and User B commit their changes to the page, creating two new versions.   # Page History shows a version (V3) *Changed By* both users:   !Screen Shot 2018-07-30 at 16.57.25.png|thumbnail!    h2. What happened?    The history shows that both User A and User B contributed to version 3.  h2. What should have happened?    Only one of the users should have contributed to version 2 and 3.  h2. Impact    It is not possible to know through the UI which changes have been introduced by User A and which changes have been introduced by User B.  h2. Workaround    Login as a system administrator, go to the dark features page at <your Confluence url>/admin/darkfeatures.action and add the following dark feature: *collaborative-audit-log.disable*    Page history will now only show the user that published the page in the history."""
comments: "A fix for this issue is available to Server and Data Center customers in Confluence 6.12.0  [Upgrade|https://www.<USER>software/confluence/download?utm_source=confluence_bugfix&utm_campaign=confluence_bugfix&utm_medium=email] now or check out the [Release Notes|https://confluence.<USER>doc/confluence-6-12-release-notes-957143056.html?utm_source=confluence_bugfix&utm_campaign=confluence_bugfix&utm_medium=email] to see what other issues are resolved."

summary: "Add Feature to Send Replies from Notifications Back to Page as Comments"
description: """This suggestion is more toward mobile users who want to be able to respond to an email and have it sent back to the page sending the notification and applied as a comment."""

summary: "Numbered List Microsoft Edge"
description: """Environment   * Windows 10 Version 1803   * Microsoft Edge 42.17134.1.0   * Confluence 6.9.1    Description    When creating a numbered list with a second level, the count does not behave as expected.    Steps to reproduce   # Create a new page   # Add a numbered list   # Add any number of lines to the list   # Add a second level for the list   # Keep adding lines to the second level    Expected behaviour    The line count for the second level is coherent, follows a logical order    Actual Results    The count is not correct, never leaving the first value         Example    Instead of:   # My list   # Second value   ## second level   ## second level value 2    We get:   # My list   # Second Value  1. second level   1. second level value 2       Workaround    Every time a new item is inserted (before typing anything else), clicking the outdent and then reindent fixes the behaviour.    Notes:  This problem seems to have been introduced with version 42 of MS Edge as version 41 works as expected."""
comments: "[~<USER> I can't reproduce it with v4. Even more strange, it doesn't happen with v3 as well.  I used Microsoft Edge, Win 10 running on VirtualBox" [SEP] "This problem seems to have been introduced with version 42 of MS Edge as version 41 works as expected." [SEP] "Please add version 6.6.11 to this as well.  Version 6.6 is an enterprise release.  Please look at fixing this.  It is also much worse than the description indicates.  The results are much more convoluted and confusing where the second indented line ends up being on it's own line without a number and subsequent new lines result in no numbering at all."

summary: "Exporting a page to PDF/Word should render Widgets as URLs"
description: """Scenario:    I have an embedded YouTube video for a training guide. I export the guide as a PDF to print and share at a live training with non-Confluence users. The YouTube video link should appear when I export the PDF (ideally the video thumbnail should as well) so that when I email the PDF it is available to follow."""

summary: "Code block macro has different colour highlighted on number and pane when ""show line numbers"" is checked."
description: """h3. Summary    When you add a code block macro to a page, then click the checkbox for """"show line numbers"""" and select a theme, the code block will have a different colour highlighted on the number/pane.  h3. Steps to replicate   # Go to a page and add """"Code Block"""" macro.   # Select """"C++"""" for syntax highlighting.   # Click on the checkbox for """"show line numbers"""".   # Select """"Django"""" theme and click Save.   # Update page. Then, edit the page again.   # Repeat steps 1-4 with different syntax highlighting and theme.    h3. Expected results    Code block will have the same colour selected in the theme box. Both in the number and code pane.  h3. Actual results    Code block has different colours in number and code pane.  h3. Note"""
comments: "I've noticed the bug appears when there are multiple code blocks on a page with different themes.  A page with a single code block does not manifest the issue.    It would also appear that certain themes override the gutter css from other themes.    Example: Django will override Confluence theme, but not the other way around regardless of code block order"

summary: "Asynchronously Render Jira Charts Macro "
description: """For a page consisting of many Jira chart macros the page constantly times out or takes a long time to load as the rendering is synchronous.  Render the Jira chart macros asynchronously similar to Jira issues macros."""

summary: "Pages with broken macros will experience slower load times when gadget feed is present on the system"
description: """h3. Summary    Pages with broken macros will experience slower load times if Confluence has individual gadgets or gadget feed added from a system with network latency.  h3. Environment   * Confluence 6.8 and 6.9   * Gadget feed or individual gadgets added to confluence from another system, such as Jira.    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a blank Confluence page   # Add a broken macro to the instance by one of two methods:   # *Via Source Editor*   #* Install [Confluence Source Editor|https://marketplace.<USER>apps/1210722/confluence-source-editor?hosting=server&tab=overview]   #* Edit existing page and add a non-existing macro to it via *Source Editor*:     #* Save page   # *Via third-party plug-in*   #* Install a Third-Party plug-in, for example, *view tracker*   #* Add a view tracker macro to a page   #* Go to add-on manager and disable the plug-in   # Macro will appear as *""""Unknown macro: macro name""""* on the page   # Go to *General Configuration > External Gadgets*   # Either add a large number of gadgets individually or add gadgets feed with a large number of gadgets in it   # Go back and load the page with broken macro    h3. Expected Results    Page loads quickly, considering there is no other content except for a broken gadget  h3. Actual Results    Page takes a long time to load, the more gadgets exist in the feed and the slower the connection to them, the longer page will be loading.  h3. Notes   * It appears that gadgets metadata is being pulled in the background by a macro browser on pages with broken macros.   * The issue is caused by a CONFSERVER-55620 bug   * On consecutive re-load, page load is significantly faster, but clearing Gadget Metadata Cache from *General Configuration > Cache Management* will result in page taking a long time to load again.   * On our test instance with 48 gadgets in gadgets feed, the page with a broken macro took about 40 seconds to load, when it took milliseconds without the feed.   * Loading times are proportional to the number of gadgets, so although connection might seem fast to individual gadgets, each new gadget will stack and increase loading times:   ** Baseline with no feed or individual gadgets on a page with broken macro and no other content   !gadget test baseline.png|thumbnail!   ** 10 gadgets added   !gadget test 10.png|thumbnail!   ** 24 gadgets added   !gadget test 24.png|thumbnail!   ** 48 gadgets added or feed with 48 was added   !gadget test 48 (feed).png|thumbnail!    h3. Workaround   * The issue is fixed with 6.10.0, 6.6.7, 6.8.5, 6.9.3   * Make sure no broken macros are present on a page   * Removing Gadget feed and only adding individual gadgets that are required should help reduce load times"""

summary: "Data center upgrade from 6.0 to 6.10 gives an error that confluence.cfg.xml is not found in the shared home "
description: """h2. *Summary*    When data centre upgrade is done from version 6.0.2 to v6.10, the following error is thrown.      h2. *Testing Environment*    Confluence v6.0.2, v6.1.0, v6.3.1 and v6.10.1      h2. *Steps to Reproduce*    # Install a CDC v6.0.2  # Once its up and running,  upgrade it to v6.10.1    h2. *Expected Result*    The upgrade should be smooth and without errors.    h2. *Actual Result*    The above error appears, because {{confluence.cfg.xml}} doesn't exist in the shared home.    The error is not causing any upgrade issues until now. The read-only mode works fine as well.    h2. *Resolution*    This issue is further documented in the following article and can be resolved by following the *Resolution* section in it.    * [Could not save access.mode into the shared confluence.cfg.xml file error after upgrading to Confluence Data Center 6.10|https://confluence.<USER>confkb/could-not-save-access-mode-into-the-shared-confluence-cfg-xml-file-error-after-upgrading-to-confluence-data-center-6-10-955171246.html]    h2. *Diagnosis*    Confluence installations above v6.1.0 have {{confluence.cfg.xml}} file in the shared-home and will avoid this issue. Upon testing an upgrade from Confluence v6.1.0 or v6.3.1, this issue was not found, because these version have {{confluence.cfg.xml}} file in the shared-home.    Another thing to note is, a new installation of Confluence v6.10 creates {{confluence.cfg.xml}} file in the shared-home, though an upgrade doesn't.  """

summary: "Clear or highlight the editor ""Find"" field on subsequent uses"
description: """h3. Problem Definition  When a user performs subsequent searches with the following steps...    # Open the editor for a page.  # ⌘ + F (or ctrl + F) to open the """"Find and replace"""" toolbar.  # Leave the 'Find and replace' toolbar.  # ⌘ + F again to search a different term.    ...the """"Find"""" text box is selected with the cursor at the end of the previous search term:  !find_screenshot.png|thumbnail!    With this, the user has to delete the entire word before performing the next search. This is extra work, and an unexpected user experience based on how the ⌘ + F functionality typically highlights the previous search term in many browsers and text editors.    h3. Suggested Solution  On subsequent searches, either...  * Highlight-select the previous search word  * Auto-delete the previous search word    Both these options would improve the user experience by reducing unnecessary work. summary: "crowd.token_key not removed if Crowd session is expired"
description: """h3. Summary    When utilizing Crowd SSO auth to Confluence, the crowd.token_key cookie is not removed if a user's Crowd session is expired.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Configure a Crowd Server   # Integrate Confluence with Crowd using the SSO Seraph authenticator   # Authenticate to Confluence as a Crowd user, view browser cookies and observe presence of *crowd.token_key* cookie   # Forcibly expire the user's session in Crowd   # Wait for the Crowd *session.validationinterval* value to expire (2 minutes by default)   # Try to load a Confluence page, you should be logged out and redirected to login page   # Refresh and view cookies, observe that the *crowd.token_key* cookie is still present.    h3. Expected Results    The cookie should be removed from subsequent requests, as it is with Bitbucket.  h3. Actual Results    Cookie persists, and each attempt to load the page before re-authenticating will perform a POST call to the Crowd server for a no-longer extant Crowd session, e.g.:    Confluence will retry the request 4x+ times before user is redirected to a login page.    Confluence should also remove the cookie when it performs the redirect."""
comments: "We are running Confluence 6.12.3 and after investigating so unusual load on our Crowd server, I found this bug report.    Indeed, I confirm the description of the reporter. What is even worse is that a lot of end-users will not close their Confluence session by logout and will just it a tab in their browser.    Then their Confluence sessions expire at some point, but because of the notification box for example, their browsers still interact with Confluence which sends requests to Crowd just to get 404 as a returned response, and always by multiple of 4, sometimes, 5 or 6. All those requests per the number of users with expired sessions and we now have a lot of useless requests Crowd needs to answer to and which consumes in our case between 1/3 and 1/2 of the server load.    Regards"

summary: "Intermittent NPE errors when using one or more Page Property Macro or any other macro"
description: """h3. Summary    Seeing intermittent NPE and System Errors on a Page Properties Report Macro page. The page doesn't load and redirects to a 500 error page.   (!) It is also possible to encounter this bug through a different macro that uses the same code path and throws the same underlying error.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Download and restore [^PPM1space.xml.zip] to a Confluence instance   ** This space XML provide 900 pages that contains Page Properties Macro with label """"discussions""""   ** One of the page (titled """"1"""") contains an image that will be fetched by the Page Properties Report Macro   # Download and restore [^PPM2space.xml.zip]   ** This space contains a page with Page Properties Report Macro that fetches all pages in this site, labeled """"decisions""""   # Go to PPM2 > Page Properties Report Macro page    h3. Expected Results    All pages are fetched as expected  h3. Actual Results    Seeing Intermittent errors on this page, on each reload.    The errors initially doesn't give us more details as the JVM might me ommitting the stacktrace as part of    In order to inspect the full stack trace, we need to disable the JVM optimization by adding the following JVM argument    The full stacktrace looks like this:    The same behavior could cause the following stacktrace as well:    or    or    Watch the gif for more info:   * [^z0Qt3emhop.gif]    h3. Notes    The following points are observed:   # Issue is more pertinent when the PPRM filter is above 200   # Issue is *observed* when the PPRM is calling any sort of rich content (images, plugins, macros,<USER>   # Issue is *not observed yet* with PPRM located in the same space as the pages with the PPM   # Issue is *not observed* when the image/rich content is removed   # Page Properties Report Caches are already on 50% utilization    h3. Cause    This issue is caused by a hibernate bug tracked here [https://hibernate.atlassian.net/browse/HHH-7867]. Some work has been done to alleviate and fix a part of this which can be found here [https://hibernate.atlassian.net/browse/HHH-10562].  h3. Workaround    There is nothing much we can do on our side until this bug is fixed on hibernate side. However, some users were able to avoid this by reducing the amount of Page Properties Macro fetched to 50 or below."""

summary: "License countdown is incorrect"
description: """On 'License' admin screen Confluence does not show expiration date. Instead it shows count down time to license expiration in form '3 month 23 days 8 hours'.    This countdown is calculated incorrectly. If you add current time with countdown value, you will not get license expiration time. The root cause of the problem is the way how Connie calculates the difference:    If subtracts current timestamp from license expiration timestamp and tries to convert this to month assuming there's always 31 days in month. The problematic method is {{com.atlassian.core.util.DateUtils#dateDifference}} is used in {{ViewOrUpdateLicenseAction#getExpiryTime}}.      """
comments: "A fix for this issue is available to Server and Data Center customers in Confluence 6.12.0  [Upgrade|https://www.<USER>software/confluence/download?utm_source=confluence_bugfix&utm_campaign=confluence_bugfix&utm_medium=email] now or check out the [Release Notes|https://confluence.<USER>doc/confluence-6-12-release-notes-957143056.html?utm_source=confluence_bugfix&utm_campaign=confluence_bugfix&utm_medium=email] to see what other issues are resolved."

summary: "GDPR Compliance - Option to add a privacy notice in the footer"
description: """An integral part of the EU General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR) compliance is producing appropriate documentation, which includes a privacy notice / privacy statement.     This privacy notice has to be easy accessible for any user.  The preferred way to display this notice is to have a permanent menu item, normally in the footer of the page. After clicking the user is shown a page with the privacy notice.    At the moment Atlassian Confluence does not provide such a mechanism out of the box.    You can add a custom html for your footer, but that kinds of breaks the design of the standard footer. Furthermore, if your wiki does not allow anonymous usage, you have to host that privacy statement on an external website outside the wiki, as users who just visit the login page are not able to see privacy statement on the wiki page.    To make life easy,    * Add a Menu item """"Privacy notice"""" in the the Administrator menu   * There you can add the text for your privacy notice   * When activated the a privacy notice menu item is shown in the standard footer of the wiki where it says """"Powered by [Atlassian Confluence|http://www.<USER>software/confluence] 6.10.0 ...""""   * After clicking the item It will show a page with the text of the privacy notice to any user, regardless if logged in or not.   * Optional (but highly recommended): The privacy notice is shown to new users at the first login and the users have to accept it, before they can start using the wiki."""
comments: "Hi all,    you may have a look on this two GDPR-AddOns. With this AddOns you will be able to be *fully compliant* to the GDPR-Policies.   * JIRA: [https://marketplace.<USER>1218962 6|https://marketplace.<USER>1218962]   * Confluence: [https://marketplace.<USER>1219041 3|https://marketplace.<USER>1219041]    Best Regards   Domenico"

Steps to Reproduce  # Install Confluence Questions  # Create a question   # Select a space from """"Ask in a space""""  # Enter a question title, description, and select a topic  # Before clicking save, a draft will appear in *User Profile > Drafts*  # Now uninstall the plugin  # Try to access *User Profile > Drafts* again    h3. Expected Results  * The current drafts the user has will show in the list.     h3. Actual Results  The page shows an error like the one below:  {code}  org.apache.velocity.exception.MethodInvocationException: Invocation of method 'getBodyAsString' in class com.atlassian.confluence.content.CustomContentEntityObject threw exception java.lang.UnsupportedOperationException: The body of this ContentEntityObject ('') was 'BodyType:XHTML' but was expected to be 'BodyType:RAW' at /users/viewmydrafts.vm[line 47, column 29]  {code}    h3.Workaround    A temporary workaround is to delete the draft created for the Confluence Questions plugin from the database.  # Make sure to take a backup copy of the database before running any queries.  # Run the queries below to delete the drafts related to the Confluence Questions plugin:  {code:sql}  DELETE  FROM contentproperties  WHERE contentid IN      (SELECT contentid       FROM CONTENT       WHERE CONTENT_STATUS='draft'         AND pluginkey = 'com.atlassian.confluence.plugins.confluence-questions:answer-draft');      DELETE  FROM bodycontent  WHERE contentid IN      (SELECT contentid       FROM CONTENT       WHERE CONTENT_STATUS='draft'         AND pluginkey = 'com.atlassian.confluence.plugins.confluence-questions:answer-draft');      DELETE  FROM notifications  WHERE contentid IN      (SELECT contentid       FROM CONTENT       WHERE CONTENT_STATUS='draft'         AND pluginkey = 'com.atlassian.confluence.plugins.confluence-questions:answer-draft');      DELETE  FROM imagedetails  WHERE attachmentid IN      (SELECT contentid       FROM content       WHERE pageid IN           (SELECT contentid            FROM CONTENT            WHERE CONTENT_STATUS='draft'              AND pluginkey = 'com.atlassian.confluence.plugins.confluence-questions:answer-draft'));      DELETE  FROM contentproperties  WHERE contentid IN      (SELECT contentid       FROM content       WHERE pageid IN           (SELECT contentid            FROM CONTENT            WHERE CONTENT_STATUS='draft'              AND pluginkey = 'com.atlassian.confluence.plugins.confluence-questions:answer-draft'));      DELETE  FROM content  WHERE pageid IN      (SELECT xid       FROM         (SELECT c.contentid AS xid          FROM content c          WHERE CONTENT_STATUS='draft'            AND pluginkey = 'com.atlassian.confluence.plugins.confluence-questions:answer-draft') AS x);  {code}  # Restart Confluence.    (i) In case you face any constraint problems while running the queries above, please raise a support ticket providing the latest logs and the queries that worked and failed so we can help you."""

"""

summary: "Macro parameters of type ""full_attachment"" cannot be used as optional (required=false)"
description: """h3. Summary    When specifying the """"full_attachment"""" property as required=false:      And did not use the """"full_attachment"""" property  and saved the storage format as such:      The macro is rendered broken due to an NPE in {{StorageMacroV2Unmarshaller#computeDerivedParamFromFullAttachment}}, which assumes that {{attachmentRI}} will never be empty.     Getting NPE in {{attachmentRI.getAttachmentContainerResourceIdentifier()}}:       h3. Background  Vendor would want to specify an optional (required=""""false"""") macro parameter of type """"full_attachment"""", as well as the needed """"derived"""" parameters for page and space.    However if there is no value for the main attachment parameter, parsing of the """"derived"""" parameters fails and 'Broken macro' is rendered in the view    (following is based on source of Confluence 6.0.1)    This is caused by a missing null check in com.atlassian.confluence.content.render.xhtml.storage.macro.StorageMacroV2Unmarshaller"""

The issue is normally created in Jira.   This is not present in other browsers    h3. Steps to reproduce  # Open Microsoft Edge  # Create a new page (or you can use an old one)  # Select text within the table  # Create an issue based on that text  # Check that the issue is created in Jira but the text is not updated accordingly    h3. Workaround  Use another browser     Windows and edge version are on the screenshots"""

summary: "Create Confluence space via REST API call w/ specific blueprint"
description: """As a Confluence administrator, I'd like to be able to create spaces with a specified blueprint through REST API calls. Ultimately, I'd like to create my own UI which will allow a user to log in to Confluence, specify his space name, key, description, and type of space (Knowledge Base/Team/Documentation/<USER>, click submit, and have the space created without any intervention from myself as the administrator.    Creating a blank space through REST API is simple enough. Why can't I specify what  {color:#FF0000}*type*{color} of space I want as well - I found nothing in the request <USER>to modify so that I'm able to do this.    Are there any workarounds or solutions to this? Otherwise any helpful advice would be very welcome - thanks."""

summary: "Creating Multiple Issues from Table does not allow users to set Epic Names individually "
description: """h3. Summary    When creating issues by highlighting text on a table in Confluence the user is given the option to *create multiple* *issues*. If the user selects *Epic* as the issue type, it's only allowed to select one *Epic Name*, which will be applied to all Epics created.     h3. Steps to Reproduce   # In a Confluence page, add a table and some content on it;   # Select some text on the table and click on the *Create Jira issue* option;   # Select *Epic* as the issue type;   # Enter the _Epic Name_ and hit *create*.     h3. Expected Results   * The user should be given the option to name the Epics individually.    h3. Actual Results   * The _Epic Name_ selected is applied to all Epics created    Workaround   * Create Epics individually."""
comments: "Hi,    Users in my company let me know that this functionnality is usefull for them because they want to list in a table all epics for a project and generate in one click elements in Jira Software project. The workaround is not enough. It would be appreciable to take in consideration this problem.    The expected result is to use a column in the table for identifying the Epic Name like it is done for title and description.    Furthermore, this issue has to be linked with the ones which deal with creation of epics by difference between 2 bulk adds, and with creation for selected lines (CONFSERVER-52357 )    Regards / Cordialement    Jérôme" [SEP] "We have the same issue as per the comment above – if we want to create our product requirements in a table in Confluence, and then use the ""create multiple issues"" option to create our Epics in Jira, we do not have the ability to set the Epic Name field (we wanted to set it based on a column selection as per summary & description).     "

summary: "Improvement for Show Changes functionality when multiple users edit a page"
description: """Currently, when a page has multiple updates and a user clicks Update on the page, Confluence notifies the user that other users have made updates to the page.  Additionally, there's an option to """"Show changes"""" which displays a color code for each user and their changes.  In instances where there are many changes to a lengthy page, this approach can make it difficult to easily discern and navigate to changes made by any particular user.  This request would be for the addition of a mechanism that would allow you to click from one update to another, ignoring white space differences, for any one particular user."""
comments: "Further, if the ""show changes"" could attribute the change to who did it." [SEP] "We've just hit another problem related to this.  User A was making some edits then User B came and made a bunch more.  User A then realizes he needs to withdraw his edits, they're wrong and bad.   There's no way to do so.  He would have to remove all of user B's work and then ask them to do it all again (data loss).  The above idea of keeping track what is A vs B is critical." [SEP] "Has any movement happened in the area of collaborative editing?  The idea of a single shared draft for everyone has a number of issues and I'm hoping you have some thoughts/roadmap to make it better:   # hard to discard just your edits if you dont recall every one of them   # cannot rollout your edit without pulling partial work from others   # cannot see what person made the which change which are gathered unpublished   # cannot opt-out on a per-page basis (exclusive edit bit on a page maybe?)   # the 'view changes' during edit gives a pan

summary: "Refreshing ""Backup Successful"" page triggers new backup creation"
description: """h3. Summary    Refreshing """"Backup Successful"""" page results in new backup getting generated  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Go to  !https://confluence.<USER>doc/files/747833194/747833195/2/1517274813281/cog.png! > *General Configuration* > *Backup and Restore*   # Put a tick box on """"Archive to backups folder""""   # Click *Back Up* button   # After the backup is completed you are shown """"Backup Successful"""" page with two paths to backup locations:   ** Copy of archive in the _Temporary_ folder   ** Copy of archive in the _Backups_ folder   # Refresh the page, either with f5 key or hitting browser Refresh button    h3. Expected Results    The same successful backup page is displayed as page represents backup job result  h3. Actual Results    A new backup is initiated in the background and created in both temp and backup directory  h3. Notes   * Tick box on """"Archive to backups folder"""" is optional, a new file will be created every time refresh is done on """"Backup Successful"""" page   * The issue is observed in all major browsers   * In certain cases, where the browser does not cache pages, clicking on another page and then hitting the *Back* button to come back to """"Backup Successful"""" page will also result in the backup creation    h3. Workaround    No"""

summary: "External Storage Integration"
description: """Confluence is widely used within our organisation and has become critical that we find a solution to attaching files in Confluence. We have limited our users to uploading X MB at a time however we would prefer that all attachments (including images embedded in pages) are saved automatically into our Document Management System. We have tested the SharePoint Connector for Confluence as advertised on the Atlassian marketplace however the functionality does not meet our requirements and it seems the vendor will not be releasing an update soon.         We understand the previous versions of Confluence catered for managing files using WebDav functionality and DB storage vs file system that we currently use. What is the roadmap for the use of external storage locations such as SharePoint?         We would really appreciate your assistance & guidance in determining how we can overcome this challenge in our organisation."""

summary: "Temp directory clean up job is not actually removing contents of /temp"
description: """h3. Summary    The Clean Temporary Directory job is located under Scheduled Jobs from Confluence administration. Currently this job does not seem to be running nor throwing errors when attempting to run manually or via schedule.   h3. Environment   * Confluence    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Start a test Confluence instance   # Head to *General Configuration -> Scheduled Jobs*   # Next to *Back Up Confluence*, select Run (this will generate a .zip file in the <confluence-home>/temp directory)   # From terminal, visit *<confluence-home>/temp* and note that the zip file for the back up are there   # Return to the Scheduled Jobs screen and manually Run the Clean Temporary Directory job    (!) Also tested scheduling the job to see if it would complete correctly but it also failed to remove the contents of the directory.    h3. Expected Results    The zip file(s) will be removed from the directory  h3. Actual Results    The files remain in /temp and the time stamp is updated in the Schedule Jobs UI showing that the job was ran but no contents were actually removed from the directory.     h3. Workaround    Manually remove the contents of the /temp directory"""

Steps to reproduce   # Create a calendar;   # Add an event with a participant (in the _Who_ field);   # Export the calendar to .ics;   # Import the .ics file in another instance.    h2. Expected behavior    The calendar is imported with no errors.  h2. Actual behavior    NullPointerException appears in the importing window. Confluence logs show the following:    In spite of that, all the events are imported correctly with no participants - which is expected since the related user identifier (user_key) doesn't exist in the new instance.  ----  h2. Additional details    This bug seems to be caused by a user checker introduced in TC 6 to fix CONFSERVER-55887  h2. Workaround   # Uninstall Team Calendars 6.0.x in the Confluence instance   # Download Team Calendars version [5.3.20|https://marketplace.<USER>download/apps/72307/version/2320]   # Navigate to the *Manage apps* Page   # Select *Upload Apps*   # Choose to upload the *team-calendars-5.3.20.obr* file that was just downloaded in step 2   # Create a new Team Calendar   # Import the ICS file into the newly created Team Calendar   # Once the import is successful, Update the Team calendar to TC 6.0.x (the latest version)"""

The backtrace in Confluence logs looks similar to the one shown in the description.         *EDIT:*  After removing ORGANIZER and ATTENDEE entries from VEVENTs we were able to import the calendar data, but then we ran into this problem: CONFSERVER-49097" [SEP] "Martti – Yes, the events are added but it's not obvious. You have to close out the operation, and manually add the calendar that's now added to Team Calendars memory, but was NOT added to the actual calendar. It adds 3-5 clicks for every cal

summary: "Option to make Preview the default behaviour for attachments"
description: """The default behaviour for the attachments macro is to download a file rather than open it.  This draws the user out of the Atlassian ecosystem and into the PDF viewer application.  For a smoother and more Confluence-centric experience it would be good to have an option on the macro to make previewing default behaviour."""

summary: "Different users moving overlapping pages concurrently within the same space causes the move to fail but the attachments to be moved"
description: """h3. Summary  When you have different users moving overlapping pages concurrently within the same space, the move is stopped and rolled back by Confluence but the attachments are moved anyways.  This will cause the attachments to be lost      h3. Steps to Reproduce  # User 1 moves a specific page tree in a space  # User 2 moves a subtree in the same space    h3. Expected Results  The move should handle this scenario by having a global lock so we don't execute multiple moves at the same time that target the same space or page tree    h3. Actual Results  The page move fails and the transaction is rolled back, but the attachments on the filesystem are not rolled back.  h6. Successful Move  {code:borderStyle=solid}  2018-06-19 11:08:41,905 WARN [http-nio-8090-exec-312 url:/confluence/pages/movepage.action username:user1 ] [confluence.util.profiling.DurationThresholdWarningTimingHelperFactory] logMessage Execution time for publishing event com.atlassian.confluence.event.events.content.page.PageMoveEvent@65f5a6ec[theMover=ConfluenceUserImpl{name='user1', key=e4c2a0b94a5414b1014a58601f77001f},oldSpace=Space{key='TEST'},oldParentPage=page: Test Page v.10 (12345678),oldPosition=<null>,movedBecauseOfParent=false,movedPageList=[page: Subpage Test v.1 (123456788),   ... SNIPPED  ,suppressNotifications=false,timestamp=1529372833643] took 1288257 ms (warning threshold is 5000 ms)   -- referer: https://confluence.example.com/confluence/display/TEST/Subpage+Test | url: /confluence/pages/movepage.action | traceId: 8f0cf5869e35c7ff | userName: user1 | action: movepage      h3.Workaround  Unfortunately, the only workaround available is to fix the attachments using the [Fix Missing Attachments Script|https://bitbucket.org/atlassianlabs/confluence-support-fix-missing-attachments/src/master/] and instruct the space owners and collaborators to coordinate between each other when a page tree move is happening."""

summary: "Confluence listundefinedpages.action causes CPU Spike on a large space containining huge number of Undefined Pages"
description: """*Steps to replicate the issue*  # Create *200 to 500* [Undefined Pages|https://confluence.<USER>doc/undefined-page-links-139580.html] in a few Confluence Page in within a Space.  # Navigate to *Space Tools* > *Content Tools*  # Click on *Undefined Pages* which should list up to *1k* of Undefined Pages.    *Expected Behaviour*  The Undefined Pages list should load immediately and does not cause CPU spike    *Actual Behaviour*  The Undefined Pages list took sometime to load (~5 seconds) and causes CPU spike (from 7% to 26% in my test) until the Undefined Pages list loaded.    Clicking on the *Undefined Pages* tab multiple time in a short period (5 times in about 3 seconds) causes the CPU Spike as per the following:  ||Number of Undefined Pages||CPU Usage to load Undefined Pages||  |250|49.2%|  |540|64.0%|  |700|78.0%|  (i) With only one click, there is a small spike (from 7% to 26%) until the undefined pages list loaded.    *Notes*  With 200K of Undefined Pages:  * Time taken to get the undefined pages from one space took *2m36.777s (0:02:36)*  * CPU usage for Confluence process went from ~0% to ~60%, spiked at 150-200% - on 24 cores machine.  """

summary: "Attempting to import an iCal exported from Team Calendars fails"
description: """h3. Summary  Trying to import an iCal previously exported from Confluence Team Calendars, in Outlook, fails with the message:   !capture 2018-07-16 17.31.54.png|thumbnail!     h3. Environment  * Confluence 6.3.2  * Team Calendars 6.0.12  * Outlook 2013, 2016 (Outlook 2010 not affected)    h3. How to Replicate and Instance Details  (i) The following scenario was tested on Confluence 6.3.2 with Team Calendar version 6.0.12.  # Add a recurring leave event to a particular Confluence calendar (12th July 2018) with the configuration as follows (recurring once every 4 days and ended exactly on the following day when it first recurs):   !Screen Shot 2018-07-10 at 4.25.00 PM.png|thumbnail!   # Then move the main event from 12th July 2018 to 13th July 2018  # Once you've done that, remove the first recurring event (16th July 2018) by choosing the _Delete this instance only_ option.  # Lastly, remove the main event itself (16th July 2018) by choosing the _Delete this instance only_ option.    h3. Actual Results  This error message is received when you attempt to import into Outlook 2016:  {code:borderStyle=solid}  Task 'Internet Calendar Subscriptions reported error (0x000710D2) : 'The VEVENT, """"<username>"""", defined near line 3580, contains a recurrence pattern that has no instances.'    select * from """"AO_950DC3_TC_EVENTS"""" where """"DESCRIPTION"""" like '%Recurring%';  {code}    h3. Possible Workaround  Remove the event *completely* when no instances are left."""
