summary: "Admin's name will be shown in Restrictions with 'Can Edit' even when editing is restricted to certain users."
description: """h3. Summary    When editing is restricted to certain users, Space Admin will not see the 'Edit' button on the page. However, when an admin clicks on the  !https://confluence.<USER>doc/files/139414/945098807/24/1531452826214/restriction-icon-grey.png|width=16!  icon at the top of the page, they will see their name as 'Can Edit', even when they are restricted from editing.  h3. Steps to replicate   # Create a new page in a space.   # Restrict the permissions for editing.   # Grant only certain people editing rights (except user A).   # Give user A full admin rights on that space.   # Login as user A. User A will not be able to see an edit button on the page.    # Click on the  !https://confluence.<USER>doc/files/139414/945098807/24/1531452826214/restriction-icon-grey.png|width=16!  icon at the top of the page, user A will be listed with a 'Can Edit' permission on that page.    h3. Expected results    User should not be able to see their name on the list of people with 'Can Edit' permission since they are restricted from editing the page.  h3. Actual results    User will see their name on the list of people with 'Can Edit' permissions."""

summary: "Stop Jira Gadgets from refreshing on browser reloads "
description: """h3. *Problem Definition*     When Jira gadgets are added to a page in Confluence, gadgets gets refreshed when the page is reloaded.   Currently the _refresh_ setting of Jira gadgets are for controlling the refresh of data on a static page/dashboard. So, when a reload of browser is done, a new set of data is plotted onto macro, irrespective of the refresh setting of the gadget.    h3. *Suggest Solution*   Gadgets should have an option of saving the snapshot at a particular time or till the next refresh interval and should not get refreshed on page reloads.      h3. *Why this is important*  This functionality can be used when multiple users in a company are using a gadget for a report on different browser sessions.    """

summary: "Confluence Questions reputation check is not working for Space questions"
description: """h2. Background    It is possible to enable [Reputation based permissions|https://confluence.<USER>questions/permissions-407724476.html#Permissions-RepBasedPermissions] for Confluence Questions (CQ). One of the examples of such permissions at work is that the user is allowed to downvote the answer only if user has at least 15 reputation.    While this works OK for global questions, this particular thing is not implemented properly in questions inside space.  h2. Scenario    1. Install CQ   2. Switch on Reputation Based Permissions at General Configuration → Questions for Confluence → Customisation   3. Create question an an answer linked to any space   4. Downvote an answer (with non-admin user that has <15 reputation)    *Expected*: error message is shown similar to ones for global questions   *Actual*: answer is successfully downvoted  h2. Additional information   * This works as expected for global questions (not linked to a space)   * There are other things broken (except downvoting). We need to find them all and fix properly."""
comments: "Is there a release date for the fix?         Thanks,    Damien     " [SEP] "This is a functional defect and it affects the very purpose of why it's there. Would be great to have this fixed soon."

summary: "Unable to add page comments, inline comments and tasks in page view but not in edit mode"
description: """h1. Symptoms  While in the *View Page* mode, the following operations fail:   * Adding a page comment fails with """"*Failed to save the comment. Please try again later.*"""" error in a pop-up.   * Checking or unchecking an inline task fails with """"*Oops! Your task change couldn't be saved. There could be a few reasons for this.*"""" error in a pop-up.   * Adding an inline comment fails with """"*Could not save the comment*"""" error in a pop-up.    The error logged in {{atlassian-confluence.log}} will be similar to the one below:      Additional examples of errors:  {code:title=Error related to inline tasks}  2018-08-07 00:45:35,078 WARN [http-nio-8090-exec-8] [confluence.impl.hibernate.ConfluenceHibernateTransactionManager] doRollback Performing rollback. Transactions:    ->[com.atlassian.confluence.pages.DefaultPageManager.saveContentEntity]: PROPAGATION_REQUIRED,ISOLATION_DEFAULT (Session #487036433)   -- referer: http://localhost:8090/confluence/display/TS1/new+page+12345 | url: /confluence/rest/inlinetasks/1/task/12877825/3/ | traceId: b9509185a649694d | userName: admin      {code:title=Error related to page comments}  2018-08-07 01:02:55,672 WARN [http-nio-8090-exec-1] [confluence.impl.hibernate.ConfluenceHibernateTransactionManager] doRollback Performing rollback. Transactions:    ->[com.atlassian.confluence.content.service.comment.CreateCommentFromEditorCommand.execute]: PROPAGATION_REQUIRED,ISOLATION_DEFAULT (Session #1011990537)   -- referer: http://localhost:8090/confluence/display/TS1/new+page+12345 | url: /confluence/rest/tinymce/1/content/12877825/comment | traceId: 6fadb580902edbb0 | userName: admin  {code}    h1. Cause  This issue occurs because contents are out of sync in sequences and tables related to *Confluence Content Notifications Plugin* and to *Confluence Batch Notifications Plugin*.  One of the reasons is if the sequence is reset to use values that already exist in their related table.    Below are the sequences that play a role on this.  The complete name may vary on different DB types, so they are presented as a _regex_:   * {{AO_88BB94_BATCH_NOT%}}   * {{AO_9412A1_AONOT%}}    Below are the tables that play a role on this:   * {{AO_9412A1_AONOTIFICATION}}   * {{AO_88BB94_BATCH_NOTIFICATION}}    h1. How to Reproduce the Issue  The instructions below are to reproduce the issue on Confluence 6.6.4 running with PostgreSQL database.    # Reset the sequence {{AO_88BB94_BATCH_NOTIFICATION_ID_seq}} to a value that contains in table {{AO_88BB94_BATCH_NOTIFICATION}}.  # Reset the sequence {{AO_9412A1_AONOTIFICATION_ID_seq}} to a value that contains in table {{AO_9412A1_AONOTIFICATION}}.  # Access Confluence and create a sample page with a single inline task and publish it.  # Try to check the inline task.  # Try to add an inline comment.  # Try to add a page comment.    h1. Workaround  For each set of sequence and table:  # Get the maximum value of the id attribute in the related table.  # Set the current value of the sequence equals to what you identified in the previous step.    Since the procedure might differ on each database type, pair up with your DBA to get this done.    Please check [this KB|https://confluence.<USER>confkb/unable-to-add-comment-due-to-sql-server-error-aonotification-table-321854221.html] if you encountered similar problem on Confluence running with MS SQL Server.    h1. Expected Behavior  If there is anything wrong on the database side specifically with the notification plugin, this should *not* impact on other areas such as comments, inline comments and inline tasks.  Also, the logging should be more meaningful regarding this issue. Checking all sequences and their related tables could be a step of Confluence initialization process."""

Steps to Reproduce  # Create a question or answer in Confluence Questions  # Comment on a question or answer using an @mention    h3. Expected Results  User should receive a notification as stated in the notification drop down:  (New notifications will appear here when other users:  Mention you within a comment, page or blog)    h3. Notes  I found two other DEV issues that are seemingly related:    [CONFSRVDEV-4163|https://jira.<USER>browse/CONFSRVDEV-4163]  [CONFSRVDEV-4203|https://jira.<USER>browse/CONFSRVDEV-4203]    It's also not clear anywhere if this is an expected behaviour or feature of the Confluence Questions add-on.     h3.Workaround  No known workaround.   """

summary: "NPE in TeamCalendars if part of the UID group has recurrence rule"
description: """Implementing https://jira.<USER>browse/CONFSERVER-55506 introduced a regression to TC.    Suppose event table contains more than one event with the same UID. At least one event has recurring rule and at least one don't. Please see example below    {code:none}  mysql> select * from AO_950DC3_TC_EVENTS where vevent_uid='5dafe179-f4c2-4e4f-a99c-1a37e5625633'\G  *************************** 1. row ***************************                  ALL_DAY: 0                  CREATED: 1531458320051              DESCRIPTION:                          END: 1535547600000                       ID: 33            LAST_MODIFIED: 1531458320051                 LOCATION: ABC                ORGANISER: NULL  RECURRENCE_ID_TIMESTAMP: NULL          RECURRENCE_RULE: NULL      REMINDER_SETTING_ID: NULL                 SEQUENCE: 0                    START: 1535544000000          SUB_CALENDAR_ID: 7d76b884-ab0f-43ec-a70e-025ed6002ef9                  SUMMARY: Some summary 1                      URL: NULL                  UTC_END: 1535547600000                UTC_START: 1535544000000               VEVENT_UID: 5dafe179-f4c2-4e4f-a99c-1a37e5625633  *************************** 2. row ***************************                  ALL_DAY: 0                  CREATED: 1531458320052              DESCRIPTION:                          END: 1535547600000                       ID: 34            LAST_MODIFIED: 1531458320052                 LOCATION: NULL                ORGANISER: NULL  RECURRENCE_ID_TIMESTAMP: 1535490000000          RECURRENCE_RULE: NULL      REMINDER_SETTING_ID: NULL                 SEQUENCE: 0                    START: 1535544000000          SUB_CALENDAR_ID: 7d76b884-ab0f-43ec-a70e-025ed6002ef9                  SUMMARY: Some summary 2                      URL: NULL                  UTC_END: 1535547600000                UTC_START: 1535544000000               VEVENT_UID: 5dafe179-f4c2-4e4f-a99c-1a37e5625633  2 rows in set (0.00 sec)  {code}    This will led to NPE at SubCalendarEvent#compareWith in the line  {code:java}  final int periodInDays = Math.abs(Days.daysBetween(originalStartTime, that.originalStartTime).getDays());  {code}    due to the fact that originalStartTime is null.    I'm not sure how valid this iCal configuration is. I wasn't able to reproduce the problem using only TC because I failed to create two events with the same UID but with valid recurrence status. In any case, this problem can be reproduced by using iCal file from outlook. """

summary: "Page Properties Report Macro pagination not working for Confluence 6.4.1 in Linux environment"
description: """h3. Summary  Page Properties Report Macro pagination not working for Confluence 6.4.1 in Linux environment    h3. Environment  * Linux  * Confluence version 6.4.1    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Create Page with title *Page 1* and label the page as 'Test'.   # Add a """"Page Properties"""" Macro and create a table within the macro and save the page.  # Create second page with title *Page 2* and label the page as 'Test'  # Add a """"Page Properties"""" Macro and create a table within the macro and save the page.  # Create third page with title *Page Report* and add the """"Page Properties Report"""" macro. Insert the label 'Test' in the macro and expand the *Options* by clicking show. Limit the *Number of items to display* to """"1"""" and save it. Publish the page.    !screenshot-2.png|thumbnail!     h3. Expected Results  * Users should be able to click on *Next* and *Prev* to toggle between pages """"1"""" and """"2"""".    h3. Actual Results  * Users able to click on *Next* but unable to toggle back to *Prev* page.      h3. Notes  * This works fine in Confluence 6.4.1 within Windows environment    h3.Workaround  * Upgrading to Confluence version 6.6.1 and above (It was tested and verified working)"""

summary: "JIRA Charts are not available for your version of JIRA. Upgrade to JIRA 6.1.1 or later to use this macro."
description: """h3. Summary    The error message """"{{JIRA Charts are not available for your version of JIRA. Upgrade to JIRA 6.1.1 or later to use this macro}}"""" can be presented to users already running versions of Jira higher than 6.1.1.    There appear to be multiple causes for this issue, but any connection failure to Jira appears to result in a value of {{-1}} being stored in cache for the remote Jira version number.    This causes the version check above to fail as {{-1}} is explicitly listed as an unsupported build version.    Additionally, the cache holds this value for 4 hours, so any subsequent requests for the next 4 hours will also fail with this error.  h3. Environment   * Confluence 6.3.2   * Jira 7.3.9    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create an AppLink between Confluence and Jira, both behind a Reverse Proxy   # Create a Jira Charts macro (JCM) in Confluence   # Shutdown Jira   # Restart Confluence (with Jira still down)   # Load the page with the JCM   # The Jira macro will fail to Preview   # Start Jira - other JIM macros will work except the Jira Chart will still fail    h3. Expected Results    The JCM should be displayed    h3. Actual Results    The below exception is thrown in the atlassian-confluence.log file:      h3. Validation    To inspect the *Jira build number* that Confluence has in it's cache, open up this URL to the Confluence Server:    * http://<confluence-base-url>/rest/jiraanywhere/1.0/servers    The JSON that returns will show the buildNumber for each of the Jira application link and the failing one should show -1 as the stored *buildNumber*. e.g.        h3. Notes    This issue often presents as a single occurrence that is resolved when the cache is cleared 4 hours later. In some cases, it can be persistent.  h3. Workaround    Re-indexing appears to correct this issue as in the process, the relevant cache is cleared in the process.    This may not be an acceptable workaround as if connectivity issues persist, the incorrect value will be stored in cache on the next version request.    Additionally, reindexing can be a time and resource expensive process that may rule this out as a fix in production."""
comments: "How is this minor? wow" [SEP] "A fix for this issue is available to Server and Data Center customers in Confluence 6.13.1  [Upgrade|https://www.<USER>software/confluence/download?utm_source=confluence_bugfix&utm_campaign=confluence_bugfix&utm_medium=email] now or check out the [Release Notes|https://confluence.<USER>doc/confluence-6-13-release-notes-959288785.htmll?utm_source=confluence_bugfix&utm_campaign=confluence_bugfix&utm_medium=email] to see what other issues are resolved.     "

summary: "Add bulk search and replace"
description: """h3. Problem Definition  Currently there is no way to bulk update content across multiple pages/spaces. An example scenario would be:  Imagine you create documentation for a bike. You've got 40 pages, in which you mention a helm 15 times. At some time, you decide to rename """"helm"""" to """"rudder"""". It would be great if there was a way to track the number of occurrences of “helm” and update all of them to “rudder”.  h3. Suggested Solution  Create a tool or macro to track those changes and allow admins to implement the search and replace when necessary.   h3. Why this is important  This is really helpful for technical documentation, since certain technical components change over time and it’s difficult to update the documentation in every location. """
comments: "Considering that huge content needs maintenance *without* potentially harming the database I strongly vote for this feature to be included. Extensions to this functionality could be:    * Limiting replacements to a certain page tree and manual selection of pages   * Limiting replacements by pages having a certain label   * Adding a preview of the content (excerpt)    * Renaming Page Titles as well as the certain words in there are often mentioned as part of the content inside "

summary: "Roadmap planner not allowing user to edit new lane's label"
description: """h3. Summary  Roadmap planner not allowing user to edit new lane's label after it has been added.    h3. Steps to reproduce  # Go to any page in Confluence and edit it.  # Add Roadmap Planner macro.  # Click on *Add <USER>.  # Click on the new added lane's label to edit it and click on _pencil_ icon.  # Try to edit the label.    h3. Expected result  The label should be editable.    h3. Current result  Any of the lanes(including existing lanes) is not editable after new lane added.    h3. Workaround  After a new lane is added, click at any white space to release the focus on *Add Label* button. The lanes' label should be editable again after that.     !roadmap_planner_lane_name.gif! """

summary: "Non-CE Edits become jumbled on save when merging multiple edits"
description:
"""h3. Summary Merging multiple drafts can create inconsistent or jumbled inserts when editing in Confluence Non-CE mode (Synchrony NOT enabled). h3. Steps to Reproduce - Use Case A: Resuming Draft # Create a page as an admin # Log in as User A # Log in as User B # Edit the page as User A # Edit the same page as User B # As User A, make edits to the page and save it. # As user B, begin to make edits to the empty page in a different area of the page than user A edits. summary: "Page Move feature should be more stable"
description: """There are a lot of problems with moving large page trees via 'Move' feature. It usually takes a lot of time and can easily fail if any concurrent modifications from users are happening. The reason of time complexity if the fact that the whole move is executed in single DB transaction. Due to hibernate autoflush it can take O(N^2) operation because of persistence context scanning.    Another problem is that move feature emits a ton of PageMovedEvents that are used to update various stuff (links, attachments, index). If the listener of the event fails, the system prints the error in log and continues move. These failed event listeners lead to system inconsistencies. For example links between pages might be corrupted or attachments not available.    Basically, it is impossible right now to execute large move operation (tens of thousands of pages) and get successful results from the first run. This feature should be redesigned so that it becomes more stable on large customer instances.    What workarounds are used right now by big customers:  * unlink the instances from load balancer to ensure no concurrent modifications are hapenning  * restrict affected spaces so that no one modifies pages there  * use API instead of UI because UI timeouts after couple of minutes (with backend operation still running)  * split initial move to batches to make it more managable  * go to safe mode so that plugins don't fail on listening PageMove events  """
comments: "6.5.2 seems affected, too"

summary: "Description of <confluence-base-url> for synchrony.service.url and SYNCHRONY_URL doesn't match"
description: """h3. Problem Definition    There're two pages that describe Confluence Data Center configuration, but the descriptions in these documents are not synchronized especially in the Synchrony configuration.   * [Moving to Confluence Data Center|https://confluence.<USER>doc/moving-to-confluence-data-center-203651.html]  {quote}-Dsynchrony.service.url=http://<confluence-base-url>/synchrony/v1   For example [http://yoursite.example.com/synchrony/v1]. You must include /v1 on the end of the URL.  {quote}   * [Configuring Synchrony for Data Center|https://confluence.<USER>doc/configuring-synchrony-for-data-center-858772125.html]  {quote}SYNCHRONY_URL    This is the URL that the browser uses to contact Synchrony. Generally this will be the full URL of the load balancer Synchrony will run behind plus the Synchrony context path, for example [http://yoursite.com:8091/synchrony].    Note that it does not end with /v1 , unlike the synchrony.service.url system property passed to Confluence. If this URL doesn't match the URL coming from a users' browser, Synchrony will fail.  {quote}         Especially, the description in the former page,  {quote}-Dsynchrony.service.url=http://<confluence-base-url>/synchrony/v1  {quote}  is not strictly true.    For example, _<confluence-base-url>_ can be like _yoursite.example.com/confluence_ .   Then the following description would result in   {quote}-Dsynchrony.service.url=[http://yoursite.example.com/confluence/synchrony/v1]  {quote}  and this is incorrect.         The latter is not referring to _<confluence-base-url>_ , so the former description should synchronized with the latter.       h3. Suggested Solution    _<confluence-base-url>_ description should be replaced with _yoursite.example.com_ <USER> in [Moving to Confluence Data Center|https://confluence.<USER>doc/moving-to-confluence-data-center-203651.html] as below.   * [Moving to Confluence Data Center|https://confluence.<USER>doc/moving-to-confluence-data-center-203651.html]  {quote}*-Dsynchrony.service.url=*[*http://yoursite.example.com/synchrony/v1*|http://yoursite.example.com/synchrony/v1*]   You must include /v1 on the end of the URL.  {quote}   * [Configuring Synchrony for Data Center|https://confluence.<USER>doc/configuring-synchrony-for-data-center-858772125.html]  {quote}SYNCHRONY_URL    This is the URL that the browser uses to contact Synchrony. Generally this will be the full URL of the load balancer Synchrony will run behind plus the Synchrony context path, for example *[http://yoursite.example.com/synchrony|http://yoursite.example.com/synchrony*].*    Note that it does not end with /v1 , unlike the synchrony.service.url system property passed to Confluence. If this URL doesn't match the URL coming from a users' browser, Synchrony will fail.  {quote}               """
comments: "Thanks for passing on this feedback. We’ve updated the URL in the following docs to fix this issue:    https://confluence.<USER>doc/configuring-synchrony-for-data-center-858772125.html    [https://confluence.<USER>doc/moving-to-confluence-data-center-203651.html#MovingtoConfluenceDataCenter-6.StartConfluenceonNode1]"

Environment   * Collaborative editing is ON   * Disabling question plugin   * Questions plugin version 2.5.5 , 2.6.6    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Install Confluence questions plugin   # Create a test question   # Disable confluence questions plugin   # Navigate to profile>drafts page    h3. Expected Results  h3. Actual Results    The below exception is thrown in the UI as attachment and below the confluence.log file:    !Screen Shot 2018-07-05 at 18.06.08.png|thumbnail!     {code}  org.apache.velocity.exception.MethodInvocationException: Invocation of method 'getBodyAsString' in class com.atlassian.confluence.content.CustomContentEntityObject threw exception java.lang.UnsupportedOperationException: The body of this ContentEntityObject \(''\) was 'BodyType:XHTML' but was expected to be 'BodyType:RAW' at /users/viewmydrafts.vm[line 47, column 29]\\     at org.apache.velocity.runtime.parser.node.ASTIdentifier.execute(ASTIdentifier.java:223\)\\ \\ caused by: java.lang.UnsupportedOperationException: The body of this ContentEntityObject \(''\) was 'BodyType:XHTML' but was expected to be 'BodyType:RAW'\\ at com.atlassian.confluence.core.ContentEntityObject.getBodyContent(ContentEntityObject.java:290\)  {code}  h3. Workaround options   # First option: Disabling collaborative editing, user should be able to navigate to drafts page normally.   # Second option: Re-enabling question plugin then clear drafts.      h3. Workaround via Database    # Shutdown Confluence.  # Backup database.  # Identify all the drafts created by Questions plugin.  {code:sql}  select * from content c where pluginkey='com.atlassian.confluence.plugins.confluence-questions:answer-draft'  {code}  # Change the status of all these drafts to _deleted_.  {code:sql}  update content set content_status = 'deleted' where contentid in (select c.contentid from content c where pluginkey='com.atlassian.confluence.plugins.confluence-questions:answer-draft');  {code}  # Restart Confluence."""

summary: "Importing content relations from shadowed users fails"
description: """When we try to import a space which have shadowed users in entities.xml, this might result to an error.    Suppose we have two users with the same username (one of them is shadowed).      In reality, the system won't import both of them. It will treat those users as the same because they have the same username. The piece of code responsible for it:        This is a problem because if we had entries for both these users in the old system (for example in user relations)        The system will try to insert both of them, but with the same user_key in sourceEntity (because it treats users with equal username as the same users). This will lead to unique key violation in usercontent_relations table:    {code:none}  UNIQUE KEY `u2c_relation_unique` (`TARGETCONTENTID`,`SOURCEUSER`,`RELATIONNAME`),  {code}    The import should not treat users with the same username as the same users because they are not. We have a notion of shadowed usernames and currently it fails the import, which should be fixed.  """

summary: "Troubleshooting Collaborative Editing - Modify Documentation"

You may want to use a third party monitoring tool to help you monitor your Synchrony cluster.    {quote}  However, the documentation does not explain why this screen doesn't work for Data Center users, so people reading it have to go figure. :)    h2. Request  Reason why it doesn't work for Data Center is a bug. Can we update the documents to reflect that and have a more precise information to people using it? Bug listed below:  * Collaborative Editing - Status Screen doesn't work for Data Center - [CONFSERVER-56052|https://jira.<USER>browse/CONFSERVER-56052]"""

summary: "Collaborative Editing - Status Screen doesn't work for Data Center"
description: """h2. Issue Summary  Confluence has a screen where Collaborative Editing/Synchrony statuses can be checked. This screen doesn't work properly for Data Center implementations, only for Standalone implementations. URL is listed below:  * <Confluence_Base_URL>/admin/confluence-collaborative-editor-plugin/configure.action    h2. Environment Specifics  * Confluence Data Center  * Synchrony Running in its own cluster    h2. Current State  Assuming we have Confluence Data Center running with Synchrony on its own cluster, in order to terminate Synchrony we need to execute Stop scripts in Synchrony nodes or kill all Synchrony processes (not recommended).    Once we do that, Collaborative Editing/Synchrony status screen looks like below - Observe it still says {{On/Running}}:  !Screen Shot 2018-07-04 at 16.47.29.png|thumbnail!    h2. Expected Behaviour  Status screen should have changed to {{Off/Not Required}} as it does for Server implementations:  !Screen Shot 2018-07-04 at 16.49.10.png|thumbnail!    h2. Impact  There's no reliable way of monitoring Collaborative Editing/Synchrony through Confluence native screens if in a Data Center implementation.    h2. Workaround  If you want to check Synchrony status, hit its cluster URL through below endpoint and check the response:  * <Synchrony_Base_URL>/synchrony/heartbeat    In case response is different than 200, then Synchrony is not running properly.    Several additional checks can be found here: [How To Check The Status Of Synchrony For Confluence Data Center|https://confluence.<USER>confkb/how-to-check-the-status-of-synchrony-for-confluence-data-center-953651062.html]"""
comments: "{panel:borderStyle=solid|borderColor=#97A0AF|bgColor=#EBECF0}  Thanks for your interest in this issue.    The reported behaviours is somewhat by design, in that (with a Data Center instance) so long as a given Confluence node can communicate with a any Synchrony node then Synchrony will be reported a 'running' in the collaborative editing screen.    Please see [CONFSERVER\-53380: Improve Clustering page by adding Synchrony cluster status|https://jira.<USER>browse/CONFSERVER-53380] regarding the suggestion on improving this messaging to make it more Data Center friendly.    Cheers,    Confluence Product Management  {panel}"

summary: "Not able to move, delete, insert a person and update event type in Outlook via CalDAV"
description: """h3. Summary    When trying to subscribe to Team Calendars from Microsoft Outlook via CalDAV following this [documentation|https://confluence.<USER>teamcal/subscribe-to-team-calendars-from-microsoft-outlook-251005307.html/], the user is unable to update the calendar by moving, deleting or changing an event type in Outlook, changes are not be reflected in the team calendars.    Also, when a user created an event and add a user in the subject, it will not be updated as a 'Who' in Team Calendar, instead it will be added in the subject of the event.     The only thing that will be updated when changed is the subject.  h3. Environment    Confluence 6.7.1, T.C 6.0.12  Confluence 6.10.1, T.C 6.0.20    h3. Steps to Reproduce    *Moving an event*   # Go to Event in Outlook.   # Change the Start time and End Time.   # Save and Close.   # Click Synchronise Now.   # Event will not be moved in Team Calendars.    *Delete an event*   # Go to Event.   # Click Delete.   # Click Synchronise Now.   # Event will not be deleted in Team Calendars.    *Change an Event Type*   # Right-click on any calendar entry in the list and choose any 'Purple' to change its color.   # Event in Team Calendar will not be updated and will change to blue colored 'Event'.    *Add a user in the subject*   # From your Calendar folder, select New Appointment   # Enter subject as *admin;AL* . Then enter the location, start time, and end time.   # Select Save & Close to save your appointment   # Click Synchronise Now.   # The event in Team Calendar will appear with a subject """"admin;AL"""". Instead of updating admin in the 'Who' column.    h3. Expected Results    Changes will be synced in Team Calendars.  h3. Actual Results    Changes are not synced in Team Calendars.  h3. Workaround    There is currently no workaround for this issue."""
comments: "Did some local testing and noticed that only the *Events* type works in term of 2-way synchronizing such as modifying the event date:  | Event Type | Outlook Read from Confluence | Outlook Write to Confluence | Outlook Delete to Confluence |  | Travel | (/) | (x) | (/) |  | Leave | (/) | (x) | (/) |  | Birthday | (/) | (x) | (/) |  | Events | (/) | (/) | (/) |  | Custom Event | (/) | (x) | (/) |    Tested locally with the environment below:  * Confluence 6.6.3 and 6.11.2  * Team Calendar 6.0.20  * Outlook 2016" [SEP] "+1"

summary: "Warning messages when deleting a Group is not accurate."
description: """h3. Summary  Warning messages when deleting a Group is not accurate.    When trying to delete a group, a warning message below is shown before confirming the group delete:  {quote}  *Note*: All the above permissions will be removed.    _<group_name>_ currently has members. The memberships will be deleted.  Deleting a group will remove all permission restrictions associated with it. Content that is only restricted to this group will become available to all confluence users. Are you sure you want to delete _<group_name>_?  {quote}  Based on the message above, pages that have View or Edit restriction only for this group would become _no restriction_ and can be viewed/edited by everyone.  However, after deleting the group. Pages that are formally restricted to only this group would still be restricted and other users that never had the permission to view/edit would still have the restriction applied.    h3. Environment  Confluence 6.7.2    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Create a new Group and add in any group member  # Go to Space Permission and grant permission to this group for a specific space  # Create a page in the space above and apply View/Edit restriction to only the newly created group in Step 1  # Delete the group created in Step 1  # Login using other user and try to view the new page that is created in Step 3    h3. Expected Results  Any user who has appropriate Space Permission can view/edit the page    h3. Actual Results  Restrictions still apply in the page created in Step 3. Other users that are not specifically allowed to view/edit the page won't be able to perform said action.  """
comments: "Perhaps it is due to the Confluence would always add the specific user to the restriction list so a page would not have the situation of ""restriction to only a certain group"". Thus deleting the group will not open up the content for all.    {panel:title=Example}  # UserA is part of GroupA  # When UserA is setting Edit Restriction to Page1, you will see the restriction as below:  | Everyone | can view |  | UserA | can edit |  | GroupA | can edit |  # When GroupA is deleted, the page is still restricted to UserA  {panel}  This means that there will _always_ be at least one user left in the restriction of the page, thus deleting any groups wouldn't cause the page lost its restriction and open to everyone else.  However, this need to be further confirmed  " [SEP] "HI [~<EMAIL>], per your comment, it is actually not possible to only have a group in the restrictions of a page via the UI. Therefore i don't believe your steps to reproduce are valid as UserA will still have permission (restriction) to that page. "

summary: "Set First Day of The Week as Sunday for English (US)"
description: """As an administrator, I would like to have the first day of the week on the date picker to be set as *Sunday when English (US) is the default instance language*.    According to ISO-8601 the first day of the week is Monday. However, for North America and also other countries outside of it, the first day of the week is *Sunday*  - [*Week Numbering*|https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Week#Week_numbering]    h3. Why is it important  Having a different first day on the calendar can lead to incorrectly scheduled appointments and meetings. This can cause major drawbacks for businesses where time is the biggest constraint. """
comments: "This was recently fixed for European languages as part of https://jira.<USER>browse/CONFSERVER-43385.    It would be great if it could also be fixed for U.S. English so that we don't need to hack a solution by modifying the .jar files." [SEP] "Hi [~<USER>, you may be experiencing [CONFSERVER\-55041: Confluence does not respect language settings from browser|https://jira.<USER>browse/CONFSERVER-55041]. Please check that your site's setting for Global Default Language is set to 'English (US)' via (<yoursite>/admin/configurelanguage.action).  I can confirm that the first day of the week for the US language pack is in fact Sunday."

summary: "Clarify Task Report Macro documentation regarding use of Labels"
description: """h3. Problem Definition  Using [Task Report Macro|https://confluence.<USER>doc/task-report-macro-590260009.html?_ga=2.176581733.1122300989.1530604979-1293891407.1527167282], when a user enters multiple labels separated by a comma, it will treat them like an OR statement, and show all tasks that have either one of those labels.     This is not clear from the documentation.    There's a feature request to be able to toggle: CONFSERVER-45518    h3. Suggested Solution  A customer in Community has suggested the following update to our documentation:   {quote}The documentation currently only says:     Filter by Label. The macro will only display tasks on pages with this label. You can enter multiple labels, separated by a comma.  I suggest changing it to:    Filter by Label. The macro will display tasks on all pages with any of the labels specified in this list, separated by a comma. Confluence Search Syntax is not supported.{quote}      """
comments: "Yes please clarify that it is not possible to use AND.  We have a space with around 50 users and want to have several meeting notes but it is not possible to handle meetings in Confluence in a good way. The whole meeting idea in Confluence does not work because of the limitations of presenting the meeting notes in a good way as well as making it easy for multiple users to keep a good structure. A lot of this would be solved by doing ""AND"" on labels. The other solution is to create multiple blueprints. One for each type of meeting where the labels are added in the blueprint." [SEP] "Documentation has been updated to be clearer that this field uses an OR operator.  Changes will be visible in docs later today. " [SEP] "Please also update _CLOUD_ documentation ([https://confluence.<USER>confcloud/task-report-macro-724765319.html]) ...or fix https://jira.<USER>browse/CONFCLOUD-45518 ;)"

summary: "c_si_ct_pv_cs_cd_idx recreated with wrong definition when upgraded under MySQL"
description: """h3. Summary    The index c_si_ct_pv_cs_cd_idx on CONTENT table is recreated with wrong definition when confluence is upgraded under MySQL.    h3. Environment  * MySQL 5.6.32    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Install any older Confluence in a <USER>instance. e.g. Confluence 5.10.8    # Upgrade the Confluence to latest version e.g. Confluence 6.10.0  # Then issue the following command  {code:sql}  show create table CONTENT;  {code}    h3. Expected Results  The definition of the c_si_ct_pv_cs_cd_idx shall be as below.  {code:xml}    KEY `c_si_ct_pv_cs_cd_idx` (`SPACEID`,`CONTENTTYPE`,`PREVVER`,`CONTENT_STATUS`,`CREATIONDATE`),  {code}  Note: Expected as above because this is the definition on the clean install.    h3. Actual Results  The definition of the c_si_ct_pv_cs_cd_idx is as below.  {code:xml}    KEY `c_si_ct_pv_cs_cd_idx` (`CREATIONDATE`,`PREVVER`,`SPACEID`,`CONTENTTYPE`,`CONTENT_STATUS`),  {code}       h3. Notes  This issue is critical because this can trigger CONFSERVER-52429 letting MySQL optimizer to wrongly choose this multi-column index, c_si_ct_pv_cs_cd_idx.  It is assumed that queries that has _order by CREATIONDATE_  is looked up by the index specifies _CREATIONDATE_ in a first column because of the description in https://dev.mysql.com/doc/refman/5.6/en/multiple-column-indexes.html      h3.Workaround  Manually recreate the index.   {code:sql}  -- Drop the index  ALTER TABLE CONTENT DROP INDEX c_si_ct_pv_cs_cd_idx ;    -- Create the index manually  CREATE INDEX      `c_si_ct_pv_cs_cd_idx`  ON      `<Database Name>`.`CONTENT`      (          `SPACEID`,          `CONTENTTYPE`,          `PREVVER`,          `CONTENT_STATUS`,          `CREATIONDATE`      )  {code}"""
comments: "If this is not expected, then it is also happening on PostgreSQL 9.6. We are testing upgrading from Confluence 5.10.4 to 6.10.0.    In the logs upon a restart it says:  "

summary: "Update Synchrony for Data Center Documentation"
description: """h2. Description  In order to install Synchrony in a Data Center environment, administrators often follow [Configuring Synchrony for Data Center|https://confluence.<USER>doc/configuring-synchrony-for-data-center-858772125.html] documentation.    This document can lead to mistakes, since it looks more like a collection of information regarding Synchrony than like a step-by-step guide on how to get it implemented. Although it is possible to get Synchrony installed in DC with this collection, it would be better to enhance the documentation to avoid mistakes and ensure customers are properly installing Synchrony.    h2. Suggested Changes  The document, ideally, should look like below - If needed, we can split below tasks in different documents and link them into the main one:  # Step-by-Step guide to deploy Synchrony to its own node(s).  ## Which configuration should I do in the node receiving Synchrony?  ## Do I need to have open ports in there? Which ones?  ## Which configuration do I need to do in Synchrony start-up scripts?  ## Should I change anything on Confluence side? URL information perhaps? Service URL?  ## Should I change anything at my Load Balancer?  ## If Confluence runs over HTTPS, should I have Synchrony URL working as HTTPS in a browser level and being terminated at the Load Balancer or not?  ## How many nodes do I need? Is there a balance in the sense of spinning one Synchrony node for each two Confluence nodes, for example?  ## How can I validate if my installation is correct? I mean, any testing steps to ensure that Confluence can connect to my nodes properly?  # Step-by-Step guide to deploy Synchrony in the same node as Confluence is running.  ## What's the difference in between choosing this option and choosing to deploy Synchrony in its own nodes?  ## Do I need to spin Synchrony process in one Confluence node only or in all of them? For example, if my DC has four nodes, shall I spin Synchrony in all of them or in a single node only? What's recommended for this scenario?  # Specific Documentation about Parameters  ## What's the meaning of each parameter inside my script.  ## Which parameters inside Confluence start-up script I should pay attention to in order to run Synchrony properly and what do they mean.  ## Does Synchrony and Confluence URL need to share the same address but with different context paths? Or can I run Confluence as www.confluence.com and have Synchrony URL as www.synchrony.com as long as my Load Balancer knows how to find the right place to direct traffic to?"""
comments: "The documentation for how to configure Synchrony was rewritten when Confluence 6.12 was released.     See [https://confluence.<USER>display/DOC/Possible+Confluence+and+Synchrony+Configurations] for a summary of the options.     See [https://confluence.<USER>display/DOC/Set+up+a+Synchrony+cluster+for+Confluence+Data+Center] for specific steps to set up your Synchrony cluster.     See [https://confluence.<USER>display/DOC/Migrate+from+a+standalone+Synchrony+cluster+to+managed+Synchrony] to find out how to switch to managed Synchrony, and let Confluence take care of all the configuration for you (available from Confluence 6.12 and later)"

summary: "Confluence Whitelist is case sensitive for domains"
description: """h3. Problem Definition    Confluence whitelist is case sensitive for domains.    h3. Suggested Solution    The Whitelist could parse the domains to lower-case automatically, replicating what browsers do when you type a domain with upper-case letters. Preferably, it would be case insensitive for domains to match the [RFC 4343|https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc4343] specification.    h3. Why this is important    If a domain with upper-case letters is added to the whitelist, requests with the same domain but in all lower-case letters will be blocked. Example:    # Create an application link to the domain *Company.com*  # An automatic entry for *Company.com* will be added to the whitelist  # Trying to add an RSS feed with *company.com* will fail as the source is not allowed  # Changing the feed to use *Company.com* will work as expected    This can cause confusion as users are familiarized to the browser's behavior of parsing the domain to lower-case.     h3. Workaround     Add a separate entry to the whitelist with all lower-case letters."""

summary: "Convert TinyLinks (Shared Links) and pageID links pasted into the Confluence editor"

This not only makes for ugly links, but deprives readers of the page of a useful bit of information when viewing the page – namely, it does not display the target URL, with the targets space-key and page title, in the link URL.    Just as the Editor can auto-convert a pasted Confluence URL, it should not be difficult to do the same for TinyLinks and pageID links.      This would vastly improve both the editing experience, and the viewing experience."""

It gives the impression it isn't working properly.    *Steps to reproduce:*  # Create a P2 plugin that uses a hybrid cache (see example config below)  # Perform an action that uses the cache  # Open """"Cache Management"""" in Confluence administration, and observe that the stats remain at 0        *Expected Result*  Cache statistics reflect the usage of the cache    *Actual Result*  They remain at 0    https://community.developer.<USER>t/missing-confluence-hybrid-cache-statistics/19455"""

summary: "Make attachments move operation reliable"
description: """When moving a page from one space to another, attachments linked to that page have to change location on disk to follow [storage hierarchy|https://confluence.<USER>doc/hierarchical-file-system-attachment-storage-704578486.html].    This process is not reliable according to following references:   - [https://confluence.<USER>confkb/pages-can-not-be-copied-or-images-not-loaded-on-a-page-738722951.html/]   - [https://confluence.<USER>confkb/how-to-resolve-missing-attachments-in-confluence-201761.html]    [Tool proposed to fix situation|https://bitbucket.org/atlassianlabs/confluence-support-fix-missing-attachments] requires service downtime.    My suggestion consists in improving attachment move on disk storage in a reliable way.    The following sequence may be implemented to avoid timeout and keep Confluence internal state (DB and attachment storage) consistent in case of failure at any steps:   # Create copies of page's attachment files on disk storage to new space expected target locations, if possible with a progress bar reported to user (in case of large, many attachments). Recommendation for performances: take benefits of file system hard links instead of full content copy, if available on current operating system.   # Apply page move with attachments in database (single transaction)   # If success, delete or unlink old attachment files at previous space locations    In case of failures, some attachment files (copies) in disk storage may be """"orphan"""" with in new space storage (if failure in step 1) or in old space storage (if failure in step 3).    In Confluence, a health check can be implemented to detect such """"orphaned"""" attachments. A tool to clean up situation does not require to service down-time - it only consists in deleting/unlinking files with reference in database.     """

summary: "Anchor link that holds ""id-"" for multi-byte title wrongly converted when pasted in another page"
description:
"""h3. Summary Confluence uses following format to express an anchor link in non-English(multi-byte) page title {code:none} pages/viewpage.action?pageId=#id--{code} while it's expressed as below when the title is all in English. {code:none} pages/viewpage.action?pageId=#-{code} When you paste the former link then Confluence auto-convert the url wrongly and it doesn't navigate correctly to anchor. {code:none} pages/viewpage.action?pageId=#id---{code} h3. Steps to Reproduce # Create a page with page title """"あ"""" and following contents. {code:xml}

Heading1

{code} # Copy the URL from the page above. It will be like {code:none} http:///pages/viewpage.action?pageId=#id-あ-Heading1{code} # Create a new page and paste the above in it. h3. Expected Results The page in step 3 has a proper anchor link to """"Heading1"""" and user can go to the page in step 1. h3. Actual Results It doesn't navigate to """"Heading1"""". Just go to the page in step 1. This is because the link is wrong format as below. {code:none} http:///pages/viewpage.action?pageId=#id-あ-あ-Heading1{code} h3. Note Regular format link URL as below works fine. It work as expected result. {code:none} pages/viewpage.action?pageId=#- {code} h3. Workaround Use just English characters for page title."""

summary: "Don't force transition to Data Center with Data Center license"
description: """h3. Problem Definition    When admins upgrade to a data center license after their previous standalone license expires, applying the license to Confluence forces a migration to Data Center, whether the admin is ready to perform this transition or not.  h3. Suggested Solution    Allow the option to apply a Data Center license without migrating the instance to Data Center. This would allow admins to use a valid data center license on a standalone instance while taking time to plan for a transition to data center if needed.  h3. Workaround    If the previous license is expired, and the admin is not ready to transition to Data Center, the admin can generate a 30-day evaluation license at [https://my.<USER>https://my.<USER>] to buy time before the transition."""

summary: "Space's TinyURL not working for Service Desk users(unlicensed)"
description: """h3. Summary  When a <USER>user access a TinyLink of a """"<USER>Knowledge Base"""", the user gets a """"Not Permitted"""" page, however, if this same user access the full URL of the space, he is able to access that specific space.    According to [CONFSERVER-45631|https://jira.<USER>browse/CONFSERVER-45631], """"Unlicensed users can only access the spaces if they have the direct links"""", however, the link of the space is working, only its TinyURL that isn't.    We can see that it appears that there's a layer when Confluence is redirecting the URL that is not accessible for <USER>Users, but this layer should not be active when the space is linked to <USER>    h3. Environment    * Confluence   * <USER>   h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Connect Confluence with <USER>  # Create a test space.  # Link this new space to a <USER>Project(<USER>Knowledge Base).  # Create a <USER>User.  # Access the full URL of the space just to confirm that the unlicensed user can view it.  # Go back to the space, get its TinyURL and share with the <USER>user.    h3. Expected Results  The user will be able to view the space through its TinyURL.    h3. Actual Results  The user gets a """"Not Permitted"""" page.     h3. Notes  * Same problem doesn't happen with Anonymous Users, only with <USER>users. Ensure that Anonymous access is disabled in order to confirm the problem.  * This can be a blocker if customers are sharing TinyLinks to their customers.    h3.Workaround  If you are able to share the full link, use it instead of the space's TinyURL. If you are not able to do that, then there is no other workaround.    """

summary: "Limit the number of messages that are used for thread building in Mail Archiving Plugin"
description: """Each time message is opened from Mail Archive, it builds thread by searching related messages and organising them into tree. This searching/tree building process takes quadratic time and memory relative to how many related messages have been found. Currently we treat related messages as either  * Having reference between each other using 'References' email field  * Having similar subject to each other    The problem here is that there's no upper limit on number of related messages. If their number is about 20K, it takes several minutes for thread building process to finish. As this operation is Lucene and CPU heavy, it can bring Confluence down.    We should at least provide some way to limit the number of messages in thread in order to save application from OOMs and CPU spikes.    *System variable*  """

summary: "Confluence - Link Report/Link Validator"
description: """h4. Problem Description  Administrators may need to verify if links inside their Confluence pages are working properly. summary: "Wrong URL in email notifications(attachments)"
description: """h3. Summary    When you share or create a page with special characters(like forward and back slashes), the embedded URL's will not be transformed to its page ID or will not replace the characters.    For example, a page named """"Morning Report 06/13/18"""" will have its URL as http://localhost:8090/confluence/pages/viewpage.action?pageId=2195460. However, this doesn't happen when the page is shared.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Setup an SMTP Mail server.  # Create a page named """"Morning Report 06/13/18"""".  # Insert an image to the body of the page.  # Ensure that you user subscribed the space and is a watcher.  # See your mail box.  # Inspect the broken image icon.    h3. Expected Results    The embedded URL will be transformed and you will be able to see the image while accessing its URL.    h3. Actual Results  The URL will point to a non-existing URL and it will show a """"Page not Found"""":    - http://localhost:8090/confluence/download/attachments/embedded-blogpost/TES/2018/06/18/Morning%20Report%2006/13/18/happy.png?api=v2    As you can see, the page's title(url) is now """"Morning%20Report%2006/13/18"""". It is transforming blank spaces but not special characters.     h3. Notes  If you don't use special characters, the embedded url will show the attachment, for example http://localhost:8090/confluence/download/attachments/embedded-blogpost/TES/2018/06/18/Morning%20Report%20/happy.png?api=v2    h3.Workaround  No workaround.  """

summary: "Synchrony - 404 When Creating New Pages"
description: """h2. Summary  When creating a new page in Confluence, Synchrony will throw a 404 response in the background since it doesn't know about the newly created content yet. Although this is an expected behaviour, it doesn't work well with [Custom Error Response|https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonCloudFront/latest/DeveloperGuide/custom-error-pages.html] software, like Amazon CloudFront, for instance, since it can't be set to avoid checking specific URLs.    The same behavior occurs when pages were created while Collaborative Editing (CE) was OFF and they are updated for the first time after CE is turned ON.    h2. Suggestion  Gracefully handle this situation and have Synchrony returning a different status (ideally, 200) instead of 404. Synchrony may be configured to know that it doesn't know the content yet because it is new and just acknowledge it instead of throwing an error.    h2. Impact  For administrators who wish to display friendly error pages instead of a 404 page to users through [Custom Error Response|https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonCloudFront/latest/DeveloperGuide/custom-error-pages.html] software like CloudFront, this is a showstopper. The editor will be locked spinning and won't allow users to edit pages, since CloudFront will grab the 404 and understand it as an error.    h2. Steps to Replicate  # Setup a Custom Error Response in CloudFront or any other software/provider you may be using. Screenshot below:  !Screen Shot 2018-06-25 at 12.54.49.png|thumbnail!  # Access Confluence.  # Create a new page. Editor will be busted unless you disable the Custom Error Response.  # This happens because in the background, 404 is throw as can be seen in the screenshot below:  !Screen Shot 2018-06-18 at 12.40.54.png|thumbnail!  # URL that throws the problem is:      *Scenario 2*:   # Turn Collaborative Editing (CE) OFF.   # Create and publish a sample page.   # Turn CE ON.   # Edit the same page.    h2. Workaround  Disable Custom Error Response for 404 responses or disable Synchrony (worst case scenario).    h2. Related Information  This has been previously raised as a bug ([CONFSERVER-43777|https://jira.<USER>browse/CONFSERVER-43777]) and closed due to expect behaviour. It is now being raised as a Suggestion instead due to the impact listed above. :)"""

summary: "Inconsistent behavior with adding a new row while there are merged cells"
description: """h2. Problem summary  While adding a new row to a table when there are merged cells present, the new row's merge behavior is inconsistent.    ||Cell merge type on current row||New row position||Result||  |Cells merged across columns|Insert above|Cells in new row copies the current row's merges (/)|  |Cells merged across columns|Insert below|Cells in new row copies the current row's merges (/)|  |Cells merged across rows|Insert above|Cells in new row does not extend the current row's merged cells (x)|  |Cells merged across rows|Insert below|Cells in new row extends the current row's merged cells (/)|    The third scenario stands out from the rest as it does not not *extend* the merged cells, and instead inserts a standalone cell above the merged cells. It appears to duplicate the row formatting from the row *above* instead of using the current row's formatting.    The fourth scenario inserts a row below, and it does correctly extend the merged cells so that now the merge is one cell longer."""

summary: "Attachments become 'Unknown Attachment' in the page editor with Collaborative Editing turned on"
description: """{panel:title=Atlassian Update - Sep 2020}  Hi All,    Thanks for all your feedback after we have closed this bug. I want to apologise again for all the pain it has caused you and your organisation. What follows is some answers to your questions:    *Will we backport this fix to the last LTS (7.4)?* Yes, this is a high priority bug that we do plan to backport to 7.4. As with all backported bugfixes, we aim to soak the change on the most recent version before we backport. For this reason, you can expect this fix to make it to 7.4 over the coming months. We will update the fix version of this bug once that happens.     *How do we fix already broken pages?* Unfortunately, this bug results in Confluence losing the association of the editor reference to the specific file. For this reason, it isn't possible for us to safely automatically re-associate the editor reference to a specific file. However, the underlying file is still attached to the page and the only thing that has been lost is the specific editor association. The best way to fix this is for a subject matter expert of the page to re-associate the reference with the actual file.    We understand that this is frustrating, and again, we apologise. We are ensuring that we learn from this mistake and will aim to ensure that issues like this do not happen again in the future.     Regards,    <USER>   Product Manager | Confluence Server & DC  {panel}  h4. Summary    When the storage format of a page embeds an image that is _not yet_ attached to the page, the image will still be rendered as """"Unknown attachment"""" in the page editor although the image has been added to the page beforehand.  h4. Steps to replicate   # Ensure Collaborative Editing is switched on.   # Create a page with this storage format.       # The page displays """"Unknown attachment"""".   # Via '{{...}}' > '*Attachments*', just insert an attachment 'test.jpg' into the page. You'll see that the page now contains 1 attachment, which is 'test.jpg'.   # View the page you first created and the image is now there.   # Edit the page.    h4. Expected results    The image would still be displayed.  h4. Actual results    The image is now replaced by the initial """"Unknown attachment"""" message.    !CONFSERVER-55928 - Unknown attachment.png|thumbnail!  h4. Notes    If a script that uses REST API to automate the process of creating/copying pages and uploading attachments to it is being used, please remember to follow the order below in the creation of the page to avoid being affected by this bug:   # create a page in Confluence   # add the attachments to this created page   # update the page with the expected content, in the storage format, using REST API    h4. Workaround  h5. Option 1 (preferred)    Leave an inline comment on any text of the page. You can delete or resolve it immediately afterwards. Attachment upload doesn't change page storage format, so the code path that triggers sending view page updates to synchrony is not used. Leaving an inline comment triggers the path, pushing updated editor format data into synchrony.       h5. Option 2 - For Confluence 6.9 and up   # Open the editor for the affected page   # Click on the ellipsis button on the bottom right corner of the screen (besides the Close button)   # Click on *revert back to the last published version*   # Check if the attachment appears on the editor now    The problem is caused by the draft entry on the DB being created before the attachment exists. The application replaces the attachment name with *plugins/servlet/confluence/placeholder/unknown-attachment?locale=en_US&version=2* in that situation. Following the steps from Option 2 creates a new draft entry for this page, so it is able to recognize the attachment.  h5. Option 3    The issue can be resolved if Collaborative Editing is toggled from *On* to *Off* and back to *On*.   note from [~<USER>: Please never use this workaround!     """

summary: "'Epic Link' column causes longer load time for Jira Issue Macro"
description: """h4. Summary  Jira Issue Table with 'Epic Link' column will take much longer to render compared to a table that doesn't have 'Epic Link' column in it.    h4. Environment  Tested on Confluence 6.8 + Jira Software 7.8    h4. Steps to Replicate  # Create a test project in Jira (eg. *TEST*)  # Create ~1000 Jira Issue in Jira with Epic Link in *TEST*  # In Confluence, insert a Jira Issue Macro with {{project=TEST}} as the search filter  # Include """"*Epic Link*"""" in the *Columns to display* option  # Set *Maximum issues* to 1000  # Insert  # Observe the amount of time taken to load the table  # Edit the Jira Issue Macro and remove """"*Epic Link*"""" in the *Columns to display* option (leave the rest as it is)  # Insert  # Observe the amount of time taken to load the table again    h4. Expected Behavior  The table loads quickly with or without """"Epic Link"""" column    h4. Actual Behavior  According to test results, it took ~2 seconds for the table to populate *without Epic Link*. On the other hand, it took ~15 seconds for the table to populate *with Epic Link*.     ||Condition||Load Time||HAR File||  |With """"Epic Link""""| ~15 seconds|[^with epic link.har]|  |Without """"Epic Link""""| ~2 seconds|[^without epic link.har]|    h4. Note  * Jira Issue Macro starts showing notable slowness while *Maximum issues* is set to 500 or above.   * If Confluence took longer than the expected timeframe, you'll notice the following message:   !timeout.png|thumbnail!   * The same issue is also observed when 'Epic Name' column is added to the filter as well.    h4. Workaround  * Increase socket timeout as suggested in [Timeouts in the JIRA Issues Macro or Highlighting Plugin|https://confluence.<USER>confkb/timeouts-in-the-jira-issues-macro-or-highlighting-plugin-726108967.html]  * Lower the amount of issue display in the table."""

summary: "Unable to create Jira issue from Confluence page due to unintended cache"
description: """h3. Summary    Unable to create Jira issue from Confluence page due to unintended cache.    This problem is reproducible when integrating Confluence with more than one Jira instances.    h3. Environment    * More than one Jira instances (I used Jira 7.10.1)    h3. Steps to Reproduce    # Prepare one Confluence instance and more than one Jira instances (e.g. Jira1 and Jira2)  # Make all the applications (Confluence, Jira1 and Jira2) use a same user base  #- e.g. I set up Jira 1 as Jira user server and configured Confluence and Jira2 to use it as user directory  # Create the following users in the user base  #- A user (named """"User A"""") who has permissions to access (including create issue permission) all the applications  #- A user (named """"User B"""") who has permissions to access only Confluence and Jira1 (no access of Jira2)  # Create application links using OAuth (with impersonation) as follows  #- Confluence <-> Jira1  #- Confluence <-> Jira2  # Logout from Confluence  # Login Confluence as """"User B""""  # Try to create an issue in Jira2 from a Confluence page  #- """"User B"""" cannot create an issue as he/she doesn't have access of Jira2  # Logout Confluence  # Login Confluence as """"User A""""  # Try to create an issue in Jira2 from the Confluence page    h3. Expected Results    """"User A"""" should be able to create the issue as he/she has access of Jira2.    h3. Actual Results    """"User A"""" was not able to create the issue with an error """"The version of selected Jira server is not supported. You may want to upgrade to the newer version or create issue in Jira."""" even though he/she has access of Jira2. (Of course, the Jira instance is supported version as well.)     !screenshot-1.png|thumbnail!      In case that """"User A"""" tries to create the issue *before* """"User B"""" do it, the issue creation continues to work without any problem for a while. (Technically, it seems to work until the cache is refreshed.)    h3. Notes    The above behavior is due to _Jira Resources Manager_ cache.    h3.Workaround    h4. Never use the caches  Configure Confluence not to use the caches.  # Go to _Confluence administrations > Cache Statistics > Jira Resources Manager_  and flush the cache  # Change the cache size to """"1"""" with following the information in [Cache Performance Tuning|https://confluence.<USER>doc/cache-performance-tuning-169119133.html]    (i) Please be careful to use this workaround. This workaround might cause some performance problem.    h4. Delete the wrong caches and create appropriate ones hourly  The caches are refreshed if they are not accessed for an hour. That's why, executing following commands hourly (by cron or something like that) could be the workaround.    (i) Please replace {{<user>}}, {{<pass>}} with credentials of a Confluence admin user who has accesses of all the integrated Jira instances."""

Install Confluence using the installer (don't install as a service)  3. Complete the setup wizard.   4. Stop Confluence.   5. Attempt to start confluence using {{start-confluence.bat}}    *Expected result:*    Confluence starts, using the bundled JRE.    *Actual result:*    Window closes immediatley, and Confluence does not start.    *Solution*    {panel}  Start Confluence using the options in Windows' Start menu, or run {{<install-directory>/startup-bundled-jre.bat}}.     The {{start-confluence.bat}} command is only used when you've installed Confluence manually, and are not using the bundled JRE{panel}  """

summary: "Reword ""Write a comment..."" hint"
description: """Some of our users keep clicking the generic comment box at the bottom, when they actually try to reply to an existing comment - even if they are trying to respond to a nested existing comment.    While I can agree that we can blame those users, I still think it is not productive to make them feel bad... especially since this will not solve the issue for other new users who will have the same problem until corrected.    Therefore, I believe a different wording would probably help the users at least pause before clicking, for example something like """"Start a new comment thread..."""" or whatever else you guys may deem more suitable.    Alternatively, this could maybe be changed into a button with wording that clearly informs the user that it will be a whole new comment thread, and not a correct way to reply to an existing comment?"""

summary: "Spring scanner library should be updated to v.2 in all plugins"
description: """When Confluence is starting up, it scans spring annotations for plugin classes. As part of this scanning process, it tries to ensure that these spring annotation classes are contained only in single jar. Responsible class for this action is AtlassianScannerBeanDefinitionParser.    If for some reason plugin can see spring annotations from another plugin that bundled them, plugin initialisation fails with error    The problem here is that plugins from error message have nothing to do with bundling spring scanner. Because of OSGI non-deterministic wiring, these bundles see spring annotation from 'wrong' bundle.    What is causing these errors is the fact that some of the plugins use old 1.x version of atlassian-spring-scanner-annotation dependency. Here is the list of bundled plugins that do it:   * atlassian-authentication-plugin   * ImageEffectsPlugin   * atlassian-webhooks-plugin   * chart-plugin   * confluence-collaborative-editor-plugin   * troubleshooting:plugin-confluence   * atlassian-playbook-blueprints   * synchrony-interop    All of the plugins above (and maybe some more) should be upgraded to use 2.x version of spring scanner annotations. They should also use <provided> scope instead of <compile> or <runtime>. After new versions of these plugins are released, they should be used in Confluence."""

summary: "Maximum width for sections on a page"
description: """Freely flowing text becomes harder to read as the width of the browser increases.  I want to limit how much a section on a page can grow in width.    I want to specify a maximum width for sections, and have those sections center justified in the browser window.  If the browser window width is shorter that the maximum width specified for the section, the section must honor the browser width.    There may be diagrams on the page that may need to be wider.  These can be put in a section of their own, and that section allowed to match the width of the browser at all times, i.e. maximum_width set to 'ANY'.    I am not referring to Section & Column macros here.  I am referring to the page layout tool and the sections that can be added with it.    I was able to achieve the maximum width setting using HTML macro by using {{<style>}}, and setting it for {{.columnMacro.conf-macro.output-block}}, {{.cell.normal.innerCell}}, and {{.columnLayout.single}}.  But it is just a hack.  A Confluence native solution is desirable.    Using stylesheets is not an option for me because creating a new space to apply it is not something I decide or control in my company. I can only control individual pages. In fact, my company has actively refused to create separate spaces for projects for various reasons.     """

summary: "Excerpt-include macro loses Space name"
description: """*SUMMARY*    If a page which has a title containing a colon has an excerpt macro, the corresponding excerpt-include page can """"forget"""" the Space that contains the excerpt macro.    *Steps to Reproduce*   # Create a page with a colon in the title, such as """"Excerpt: testing"""" and add an excerpt macro which contains some content.   # Create another page in the same Space and add an excerpt-include macro and select the """"Excerpt: testing"""" page created in step one.   # Create another page in the same Space and add an excerpt macro which contains some content.   # Add a new excerpt-include macro to the same page created in step 2 which references the excerpt macro created in step 3.   # Edit the title for the page created in step 3 so that it is different in some way.   # Go to the page which contains the two excerpt-include macros which was created in step 2, and click Edit and then Save.   # Observe that the excerpt-include macro for the page which contains the colon in the title is now broken. If you click edit, you will see that the Space name that had been present is now missing.    *Observations*    I noticed that when you add an excerpt-include macro which references a page with a colon in the title that the Space key is always automatically added even when the page with the excerpt macro is in the same Space. When you add a page in the same Space whose title does not contain a colon, the Space key is not automatically included."""

description: """h3. Summary  When a user wants to share a Confluence page to a group, the user needs to know the exact name of the group/user. If a user types """"group"""" in the Add People field of the search dialog, Confluence will not retrieve the above groups as options the user can choose from. """

summary: "Extension point to filter information about created pages on Confluence dashboard"
description: """Hi Team!    My name's <USER>and I'm a product owner of [Smart Attachments for Confluence|https://marketplace.<USER>apps/1219123/smart-attachments-for-confluence?hosting=server&tab=overview] app.    It will be nice to have an extension point to filter appearance of pages on the Confluence dashboard. So that we will have an option to block appearance of newly created pages (created by some third-party app) on the dashboard.    At the moment we are setting the old dates to pages, so they do not appear. But this is not convenient and is not good in general from the architecture perspective.    *Use Case*    We are using your native Confluence pages to render our own entities (folders) in the space storage for keeping documents and files. The problem is that a newly created page is displayed on Confluence dashboard. We would greatly appreciate if you could provide/implement some extension point to filter information about created pages on Confluence dashboard. summary: "Init scripts for lsb applications should have supported databases under Should-Start"
description: """h3. Problem Definition    Currently init scripts for LSB applications do not contain LSB dependency info, which means Confluence may attempt to start before or at the same time as its database backend. It fails to access its database, and therefore fails to start.    h3. Suggested Solution    For LSB applications, put all the supported databases under Should-Start instead of Required-Start. Then Confluence after whatever databases are installed on that host.     h3. Workaround    You can add a customized [LSB comment block|https://wiki.debian.org/LSBInitScripts]."""

summary: "Restore this Version option does not work when using Page History Macro"
description: """h3. Summary  When users access an old version of a page via the Page History Macro the old version is displayed. However the *Restore this Version* link on the top of that page does not work. Clicking on that link will redirect the user to the most recent version of the page.      h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Navigate to a Confluence page that has old versions.  # Click *Edit*  # Add a *Page History Macro* in the page and click *Update*.   # A list of the versions of the page will be displayed in the page.   # Access an old version of the page through one of the links in the list. This will redirect you to the old version.  #  In the top of the page you will see *Restore this Version*.    h3. Expected Results  Confluence will redirect the user to a page where he needs to confirm that he wants to restore the page to this old version.    h3. Actual Results   Clicking on that link will redirect the user to the most recent version of the page.    h3.Workaround  From the Confluence page in question. Head over to the *...* > Page History > Click restore on the version of your choice."""

summary: "LaasPerformanceLoggingJob log spam while LaaS performance logging is turned off by default"
description: """h3. Problem Definition    A WARN level event is logged every 5 min due to failed scheduled *LaasPerformanceLoggingJob* job.  The job is not required on the server version of Confluence and is used in Cloud. The job is failing because LaaS performance logging is turned off by default.  Warn is getting logged because scheduled job log level was raised as a result of CONFSERVER-54226 feature request.    The below exception is thrown in the atlassian-confluence.log file:    h3. Suggested Solution    The job should be removed or disabled on startup to avoid confusion, log spam and conserve resources allocated to running the job.  h3. Workaround    Disable the """"scheduledjob.desc.LaasPerformanceLoggingJob"""" from  !https://confluence.<USER>doc/files/747833194/747833195/2/1517274813281/cog.png! > *General Configuration* > *Scheduled jobs*"""
comments: "Yes, please remove the job from Confluence Server installations as we don't need it anyway. These messages only flood the application log." summary: "Confluence mentions do not find users if search term is preceded by punctuation in the display_name"
description: """h3. Summary  Confluence mentions do not find users if search term is preceded by punctuation in the display_name. (e.g. searching """"<USER>""" for someone who has a display_name of """"Mays,<USER>""" does not return any results). This is an issue in recent versions (6.8, 6.7), but did not happen in 6.4 or 6.2 (In these versions, """"Mays,<USER>""" would come up in a mention search for """"<USER>"""). This is an issue that appeared somewhere between 6.5 and 6.7.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Create a user with punctionation-separated strings in the display_name (e.g. """"Mays,<USER>""")  # Do an @-mention search for """"<USER>"""    h3. Expected Results  """"Mays,<USER>""" will appear in search results.    h3. Actual Results  """"Mays,<USER>""" does not appear in search results.    h3. Workaround  Search the user by their username or a string that is not preceded by punctuation in the display_name."""

summary: "Update version information of ""Confluence Attachment Extractors"" in Atlassian Marketplace"
